title: A Simulation-Based Framework for Fatigue-Aware Task Reallocation in Human-Robot Collaboration: Software Verification with 1000-Episode Digital Twin
authors: Claudio Urrea
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Collaborative robotics in Industry 4.0 manufacturing faces persistent challenges in balancing productivity with operator safety, particularly when human fatigue accumulates during extended work shifts. While physical validation remains the gold standard, computational simulation offers advantages for systematic algorithm development: perfect ground truth unavailable from self-reported fatigue measures, parameter sweeps across physiological ranges infeasible with human subjects, and controlled testing of extreme scenarios unsafe for physical trials. A simulation framework is presented for developing fatigue-aware task reallocation algorithms before deployment on actual collaborative robots. The framework implements a tricolor semaphore system (Green/Orange/Red thresholds at 30%/40% fatigue) with dynamic sampling rates (5.0 min baseline, 0.1 min critical monitoring) and automated robotic intervention when operator fatigue exceeds safe thresholds. Software verification across 1000 simulated episodes demonstrates 99.30% collision-free operation—approaching but not yet achieving the ISO/TS 15066:2016 safety target of 99.85%, with a 0.55 percentage point gap for future algorithm refinement. Friedman analysis confirms statistically significant fatigue progression (chi-squared=3000.0, p<0.001) from 28.06% at baseline to 68.04% at 45 minutes, with all pairwise comparisons significant (p<0.001, Cohen’s d=1.18–2.34). The framework achieves 100% intervention triggering when needed, with robots active during 50.28% of episode duration. Analysis reveals strong temporal coupling between skill and fatigue (r=+0.970): operators advance from Intermediate to Advanced competence while simultaneously accumulating physiological depletion. This correlation arises naturally from the model structure where both variables increase with time, demonstrating that growing expertise does not offset fatigue-related safety risks. Results establish computational feasibility of the approach and provide baseline metrics for subsequent physical implementation with actual collaborative robots and physiological sensors. The complete simulation environment, statistical analysis code, and 4000-point dataset are publicly available at https://doi.org/10.6084/m9.figshare.30983713 (GitHub: https://github.com/ClaudioUrrea/TM14X) to support reproducible research and algorithm benchmarking in human-robot collaboration safety systems.

title: Real-Time Emulator of Robust Speed Controller for PMSM Wind Energy Conversion via a FATA Morgana Algorithm using OPAL-RT
authors: Walid S. E. Abdellatif, Saad A. Mohamed Abdelwahab, Manal M. Emara, Mohamed A. Amar, Noura A. Nouraldin
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This study investigates advancements in modern wind power systems, focusing on rapid wind speed tracking, efficient maximum power point tracking (MPPT), and enhanced performance under uncertain wind conditions. A novel MPPT method is proposed, based on the FATA Morgana Algorithm-based PI controller FATA-PIC. To evaluate its effectiveness, the FATA-PIC is compared with two techniques: the Harris Hawks algorithm-based PI controller (HHA-PIC) and the conventional PI controller (CPIC). Performance metrics include power efficiency, algorithmic complexity, and adaptability to varying wind conditions. Extensive simulations are conducted in MATLAB/SIMULINK under various wind speed scenarios-including step changes, ramp variations, and real-world wind fluctuations. The results indicate that the FATA-PIC controller consistently delivers superior performance, reaching an efficiency of 90.03%, which slightly surpasses the 88.57% achieved by HHA-PIC and the 87.20% recorded with CPIC. Additionally, the root mean square error (RMSE) analysis confirms the high accuracy of the FATA-PIC in estimating wind speed and maintaining performance stability under dynamic conditions. To further validate these findings, the proposed control system is implemented in real time using OPAL-RT 4610 platform. This advanced real-time simulator enables precise hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) integration of detailed MATLAB/Simulink models, facilitating accurate emulation of real-world operating conditions. The real-time simulations confirm that the FATA-PIC offers better dynamic response and control quality when compared to HHA-PIC and CPIC controllers, especially during critical transition periods and under rapidly.

title: SIRE-ST: A Swin Transformer-Based Cascaded Model for Urban Rainfall Estimation in Complex Environments
authors: Guanyu Hou, Zeming Zhao, Tianlong Bao, Hengyi Zhang, Xinjie Zhuo, Chulei Zhang, You Zhou
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Sudden changes in rainfall may cause serious potential threats to human life and property such as traffic accidents, landslides, and urban floods. Therefore, timely and accurate rainfall amount estimation plays an important role in preventing and mitigating disasters caused by these events. However, due to the complexity and variability of rainfall, rainfall estimation methods still face many challenges in complex environments. In this paper, we propose Swin Transformer for Single-Image Rainfall Estimation（SIRE-ST), which is cascaded architecture consisting of two kinds of sub-networks: a raindrop extraction network and a rainfall estimation network based on Swin Transformer. We use raindrop extraction network to generate raindrop streak from images, Then the raindrop streak image will be the inputs of rainfall estimation network which enables efficient rainfall estimation based on the extracted raindrop streak information.To explore the impact of number of backgrounds in the dataset on the generalization ability of the model, we synthesized a dataset based on real raindrop streaks and real backgrounds. SIRE-ST was shown to be effective on three publicly-available datasets, according to experimental results. Our model also has mean absolute percentage errors in the range of 5.68% on the RD3-night2 dataset to 19.78% on the more challenging RD2-nightdataset which are better than current methods in all cases. Especially, the generalization performance of the model is very strong when dealing with daytime rainfall events.In summary, our research not only makes new advances in rainfall estimation based on innovative methods, but also provides a new idea for the production of datasets.

title: Comparing Patent Network Approaches for Extracting Technology Intelligence
authors: Leehee Kim, Sungjoo Lee
publish: IEEE Transactions on Engineering Management
abstract: Patent network offers advantages for extracting technology intelligence, such as analyzing technology trend and identifying emerging technologies. Despite the significant value of patent network methods, few studies compare similarity measures used to construct a network, which can have a substantial impact on analysis results. Because technology intelligence extraction depends on contextual relevance, understanding the unique characteristics of each similarity measure enables more appropriate and effective insights. This study first conducts a network structure analysis using multiple patent similarity measures. Basic network statistics and comparisons of network similarity are performed, along with sensitivity analyses that vary similarity measurement levels and threshold settings to examine how network characteristics change under different analytical conditions. The study then compared three similarity measures—citation-, classification-, and text-based approaches—across two perspectives: patent clustering and key patent identification. For clustering, we further evaluate robustness, assessing stability across hyperparameter configurations and consistency of pairwise assignments. The findings provide guidance on the selection of patent network construction strategies for analytical objectives.

title: U-NEXT: A User-Centered New Prototype for Walking Excavator Simulator
authors: Long Cheng, Gilberto Mazzola, Samuel Knüsel, Michael Schreiner, Andreas Kunz
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: The walking excavator is a highly complex construction machine, characterized by challenges in control, instruction, and operational safety. In this work, we implemented a virtual reality (VR) walking excavator simulator that integrates real hardware components with kinematic physics simulation. Using the simulator, we conducted two user studies. The first involved 45 participants and examined the effects of user perspectives on performance and preference. The second involved 12 participants and investigated the influence of video passthrough of the controllers. Results show that the choice of perspective should be task-dependent. Third-person view outperformed first-person view in the undercarriage task in which complex leg movements are needed. It supported better planning and allowed complete body visualization. However, it did not reduce motion sickness and was less preferred than first-person view for training. From the second user study, passthrough significantly improved controller learning compared to animated controllers, with participants making fewer errors when using the passthrough design. Based on these findings, we provide design recommendations for user perspectives and controller representation in VR-based training simulators.

title: Interaction Under Whole-Body User Rotations in VR Space_supp1-3680596.pdf
authors: Filip Skola
publish: N/A
abstract: Virtual reality (VR) enables interactions beyond real-world limitations, yet many such capabilities remain underexplored due to concerns about user comfort and increased disorientation and cybersickness. This study examined how altering the user's virtual pitch orientation influences interaction performance and subjective ratings. In a within-subject design, 30 seated participants experienced 12 virtual tilts from moderate to extreme (±180°). We assessed comfort, simulator sickness, perceptual responses, and performance. Our results revealed no significant increases in nausea, disorientation, or decreased comfort, with mild tilts performing equivalently to baseline. Notably, even extreme tilts maintained low nausea levels. Performance effects were mixed: forward tilts yielded similar or slightly improved performance, whereas backward tilts modestly impaired performance, though not significantly. These findings suggest that VR experiences featuring virtual body orientations distinct from the user's physical posture are feasible without compromising comfort or performance, enabling novel opportunities in simulation, interface design, visualization, creative content creation, and VR gaming.

title: Analyzing Deviations from Monotonic Trends through Database Repair
authors: Shunit Agmon, Jonathan Gal, Amir Gilad, Ester Livshits, Or Mutay, Brit Youngmann, Benny Kimelfeld
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
abstract: None

title: Tailoring the Sensitivity-Range Tradeoff in Monolithic InGaN/GaN Temperature Sensing Devices via Indium Content Engineering
authors: Hongying Yang, Xiaoshuai An, Kwai Hei Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: Monolithic integration of InGaN-based optoelectronics offers a promising platform for compact sensing devices, yet the influence of key material parameters such as indium content on temperature-sensing performance remains unexplored. This work systematically studies the effect of the indium concentration in InGaN/GaN multiquantum wells (MQWs) on the performance of monolithically integrated light-emitting diode (LED)–photodetector (PD) devices. Devices with indium fractions from 0.137 to 0.302, yielding room-temperature emission from 431 to 540 nm, are fabricated and characterized from <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$220~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C. The sensing mechanism is driven by the temperature-dependent spectral overlap between the LED emission and PD responsivity, influenced by thermal bandgap narrowing, thermal depolarization, and carrier dynamics. Experimental results demonstrate that higher indium content extends the linear operational range (from 0–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$140~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C to 0–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$220~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C) but reduces sensitivity (from 0.441%/°C to 124%/°C), revealing a direct design tradeoff. All devices exhibit high reversibility, stability, and linearity (R<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{{2}} \gt 0.98$</tex-math> </inline-formula>). This study identifies indium concentration as a critical parameter for tailoring the sensitivity-range compromise, providing essential design guidelines for optimizing monolithic III-nitride optoelectronic devices.

title: Towards Robot Skill Learning and Adaptation with Gaussian Processes_supp1-3681188.mp4
authors: A K M Nadimul Haque
publish: N/A
abstract: General robot skill adaptation requires expressive representations robust to varying task configurations. While recent learning-based skill adaptation methods refined via Reinforcement learning (RL) have shown success, existing skill models often lack sufficient representational capacity for anything beyond minor environmental changes. In contrast, Gaussian process (GP)-based skill modelling provides an expressive representation with useful analytical properties; however, adaptation of GP-based skills remains underexplored. This paper proposes a novel, robust skill adaptation framework that utilises GPs with sparse via-points for compact and expressive modelling. The model leverages the demonstrated trajectory's first and second analytical derivatives to preserve the skill's kinematic profile. We present three adaptation methods to cater for the variability between initial and observed configurations. Firstly, an optimisation agent that adjusts the path's via-points while preserving the demonstration velocity. Second, a behaviour cloning agent trained to replicate output trajectories from the optimisation agent. Lastly, an RL agent that has learnt to modify via-points whilst maintaining the kinematic profile and faster feed-forward adaptation. Evaluated across three tasks (drawer opening, cube-pushing and bar manipulation) in both simulation and hardware, our proposed methods outperform every benchmark in success rates. Furthermore, the results demonstrate that the GP-based representation enables all three methods to attain high cosine similarity and low velocity magnitude errors, indicating strong preservation of the kinematic profile. Overall, our formulation provides a compact representation capable of adapting to large deviations from a single demonstrated skill.

title: A Two-Stage RF-SVM Model Against SSDF Attack in Cooperative Spectrum Sensing
authors: Renjie Yu, Jun Wu, Hongru Zhang, Rui Zhao, Chenpu Li, Yanrong Zhai, Fan Li, Xiaorong Xu, Jianrong Bao
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: With the increasing use of wireless devices, available spectrum resources are becoming increasingly precious. Cooperative spectrum sensing (CSS) is a key technology in cognitive radio (CR) networks. It enhances spectrum utilization efficiency by enabling multiple secondary users (SUs) to jointly detect the spectrum occupancy of the primary user (PU). However, its inherent data fusion mechanism makes it vulnerable to spectrum sensing data falsification (SSDF) attack. To enhance the system’s SSDF fault tolerance, this paper proposes a novel two-stage hybrid malicious user (MU) detection model integrating random forest (RF) and particle swarm optimization-support vector machine (PSO-SVM), denoted as RF-SVM. This model first employs an RF for preliminary screening of SUs, followed by support vector machine (SVM) for fine-grained classification, thereby combining the strengths of both algorithms. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed RF-SVM model outperforms both the PSO-SVM and standalone RF models, as well as advanced methods such as the enhanced support vector machine (ESVM) and isolation forest-spectral clustering (IFSC), in terms of both detection accuracy and false alarm control. This model maintains stable performance even under scenarios with high-intensity attacks and limited training samples, while also achieving efficient computation. These advantages fully verify its effectiveness, robustness and practical application value.

title: Wideband Low-RCS Phased Array Antenna Based on Hybrid Reduction Mechanism
authors: Xin Yue, Jinbo Liu, Qingxin Guo, Fei Liu, Zengrui Li
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: A novel integrated phased array is proposed to achieve wideband low radar cross section (RCS) and maintain its high-gain scannable beam, addressing the trade-off between RCS reduction and radiation performance. It adopts a hybrid reduction mechanism that combines phase cancellation and wave absorption. Each element comprises a resistive metasurface (RM), an antenna, and a stripline-based optimum load impedance network. The structural-mode and the antenna-mode scattering fields of the element have nearly equal amplitudes and are 180° out of phase, which lead to phase cancellation and thereby achieves in-band RCS reduction. The RM exhibits an absorption–transmission–absorption frequency characteristic to reduce out-of-band RCS. An 8×8 array is designed and fabricated, with a 30.77% 10-dB impedance matching bandwidth (5.5–7.5 GHz). Measured results show a peak gain of 22.4 dBi and a scanning capability of ±45° in both xoz- and yoz-planes, demonstrating no degradation in key radiation performance. Meanwhile, under normal incidence, the 7-dB RCS reduction bandwidth is 96.1% (3.3–9.4 GHz) for x-polarization and 47.6% (3.7–4.9 GHz and 7.8–9.5 GHz) for y-polarization.

title: Interleaved Luo Converter-Fed Electric Vehicle Battery Charger Integrated with Renewable Energy
authors: K.Sharmela, D.Velmurugan, G.BANU, Sivaranjani S., Sujitha K, Janani K.
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The Proposed system aims to develop an advanced EV charger using an Interleaved Luo Converter, a special DC-DC converter that improves power quality, reduces harmonic distortion, and maximizes charging efficiency. With electric vehicles growing globally spurred by the demand for renewable energy and lower carbon emissions, efficient and reliable charging stations are more in demand. The proposed system contributes to environmental sustainability by reduces transportation-related emissions In discontinuous conduction mode, the system is effective, splits the input current, and lowers switching losses thanks to its two interleaved Luo converter cells. The design also reduces the input and output current ripple. Until a certain state of charge is reached, the charger runs in continuous current mode. A PIC microcontroller provides precise control and safe operation, while a flyback converter creates electrical isolation between the input and output.

title: Intelligent Air Quality Forecasting and Pollution Prediction using Hybrid Machine Learning Algorithm
authors: Rajarajeshwari B, Sankari S, Harivel GK, Muthupandi G, S. Pavithra
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Urban air pollution is the next global emerging problem thrown in by rapid industrialization, urbanization, and booming vehicular emissions. A novel machine learning-based model for the estimation of air quality and prediction of pollution in support of sustainable environmental management for smart city infrastructure is proposed. The model performs temporal dependency analyses and multi-horizon forecasting of pollutant levels using the Temporal Fusion Transformer (TFT). The model first applies Random Forest algorithms to identify significant environmental drivers and secondly uses CatBoost to determine pollution severity class levels with a superior rationale regarding complex interactions of features. As a multi-algorithm model, it deals better with noisy, incomplete, and non-linear IoT sensor network data with reasonably high prediction accuracy with regard to traditional models. It provides policymakers and city planners with real-time actionable inferences from which they may decide most efficiently for pollution abatement. Experimental results show substantial improvements in forecast accuracy of R2=0.9809. By integrating advanced machine learning techniques to real-time data streams, this study takes strides toward envisioning more intelligent and healthy cities and thus advocates sustainable policies that will attempt to stem the environmental and health implications of air pollution.

title: YOLOv8 with Integrated Attention Mechanism and ASFF for Brain Tumor Detection and Classification in MRI Images
authors: Shuang Zhang, Yang Chen
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Brain tumors pose a significant threat to human health, making their early and precise detection critical for improving clinical outcomes and patient survival. Traditional manual diagnosis of brain tumor MRI images is inefficient for large datasets and susceptible to subjective errors. The YOLOv8 algorithm, known for its high speed and accuracy, shows strong potential in medical image analysis. This study proposes an enhanced YOLOv8 framework that integrates the CBAM and SE attention mechanisms to improve feature extraction and the ASFF module for optimized multi-scale feature fusion, addressing challenges such as complex tumor morphology and small lesion detection. Experimental results demonstrate notable improvements: mAP50 reached 0.951 (a 6.4% increase) and mAP50-95 rose to 0.806 (a 23.1% gain). For Meningioma and No Tumor classes, mAP50 achieved 0.986, indicating high classification accuracy. Although the introduced attention and feature fusion modules increase computational overhead, the complexity analysis clarifies the source of the performance improvements. This study provides an effective baseline model for subsequent model lightweighting, offering a clear pathway for advancing high-precision models toward real-time clinical applications.

title: Comparative Analysis of Sample-and-Hold Circuits for Low-Voltage and Low-Frequency Biomedical Applications
authors: Anoop Garg, Ankit Kumar, Hrishikesh Das, Gaurav Kaushal, Manisha Pattanaik
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Sample-and-hold (S/H) circuits are critical building blocks in analog-to-digital converters (ADCs), especially for biomedical signal acquisition systems that operate under lowvoltage, low-frequency, and low-power constraints. This paper presents a comparative transient-analysis-based evaluation of NMOS-based, PMOS-based, transmission-gate-based, and bootstrapped S/H circuits. All architectures are designed and simulated using 180 - nm CMOS technology in Cadence Virtuoso. The comparison focuses on key performance metrics extracted from transient simulations, namely power consumption and signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), along with an analysis of the trade-off between linearity and circuit complexity. Simulation results show that basic MOS-based S/H circuits offer minimal complexity and low power consumption but suffer from poor linearity and reduced SNR. Transmission-gate-based S/H circuits provide improved linearity and SNR at the expense of increased circuit complexity. Bootstrapped S/H circuits achieve the highest SNR and best linearity; however, this performance improvement comes with significantly higher design complexity and dynamic power consumption. For low-frequency biomedical signals such as electrocardiogram (ECG) and electroencephalogram (EEG), passive op-amp-free S/H architectures demonstrate an effective balance between power efficiency, linearity, and implementation complexity.

title: Comparative Numerical Analysis of Lead-Free Perovskite Solar Cells Using SCAPS-1D
authors: Md Shazarul Islam, Md Abdul Kuddus Sheikh, Hasina Huq
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: A comprehensive numerical study of lead-free perovskite solar cells was conducted using the SCAPS-1D simulation framework with the device architecture ITO/SnO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>/Perovskites/NiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</inf>/Au. The work investigates the replacement of the central Pb cation with Sn, Ge, and Bi, followed by absorber-layer thickness optimization to enhance device performance. The impact of systematic Pb substitution on key photovoltaic parameters was first evaluated. Among the candidates, FASnI<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>-based devices exhibited the most promising performance, achieving a power conversion efficiency (PCE) of 26.48%, with a short-circuit current density (J<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">sc</inf>) of 19.31 mAcm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-2</sup>, an open -circuit voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">oc</inf>) of 1.57 V, and a fill factor (FF) of 87.29%. Further optimization of absorber thickness revealed an optimal value of 1600 nm, yielding an enhanced PCE of 27.15%. These results highlight the strong potential of Pb-free perovskites for next-generation eco-friendly photovoltaic technologies.

title: A Wide Depletion Region Single Photon Avalanche Diode
authors: Guilan Feng, Jichen Zhang, Tianqi Zhao, Junjie Hou
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Single photon avalanche diode (SPAD) has achieved a wide range of applications in the field of single-photon detection by virtue of their single-photon level sensitivity, ultra-high speed, and other characteristics. Front-illuminated SPAD has gained widespread attention in recent years due to their advantages of low fabrication cost, simple process, and compatibility with mature CMOS. However, the photon detection performance of conventional front-illuminated SPAD is often limited by the issues such as narrow depletion region of planar avalanche junction. In this paper, a wide depletion region silicon-based SPAD with a pixel radius of 2.55 μm is designed and simulated using TCAD software and several electrical parameters and optoelectronic characteristics has been obtained. The SPAD creates several high values of electric field through several semicircular N-type regions. With a reverse bias above the breakdown voltage, several semicircular areas with high electron breakdown probability appear near the top surface of the device. These areas help improve the photon detection probability. Finally, according to the simulation results, the device has a peak photon detection probability of 45% and a DCR value of 1180cps while Vex=5V, wavelength = 450nm.

title: Deep Reinforcement and Federated Learning-Based Joint Spectrum and Power Allocation in D2D-Enabled Cellular Networks
authors: Srinivasarao Yarlagadda, Telaprolu Dhanushyasri, Kolakani Hansika Bose, Lebaka Kyathi, Thati Jaya Sarayu, Ajay Bhardwaj
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: In underlay cellular networks supporting Device-to-Device (D2D) communication, efficient <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r$</tex> esource allocation remains a key challenge due to spectrum sharing and crosstier interference. This work proposes a Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL)-based framework for joint spectrum and power allocation aimed at maximizing system throughput while limiting interference to cellular users (CUs). The network environment is modeled as a Markov Decision Process (MDP) that captures essential wireless characteristics, including path loss, interference, and signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR). Two DRL algorithms, namely Deep Q-Networks (DQN) and Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO), are implemented using the Stable Baselines3 framework to dynamically assign resource blocks and transmission power levels to D2D pairs in a shared spectrum setting. Simulation results demonstrate that the PPO-based agent consistently outperforms DQN and baseline allocation schemes in terms of average throughput, SINR distribution, energy efficiency, and convergence stability. Overall, the proposed DRL-based approach achieves an effective trade-off between throughput maximization and interference mitigation under dynamic network conditions. While federated learning is discussed as a promising direction for distributed and privacy-preserving optimization, this study focuses on centralized DRL-based resource allocation and leaves federated extensions for future investigation.

title: Deep Learning-Driven Framework for Robust Cybersecurity Threat Detection and Classification
authors: Narsing Rao Dyavani, Bhagath Singh Jayaprakasam, Charles Ubagaram, Rohith Reddy Mandala, Ignatious K Pious, S.Jayanthi
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Network Intrusion detection Network intrusion is an important factor in safeguarding network infrastructures against increasing cyberattacks. Conventional intrusion detection systems are characterized by the existence of a lot of false positives and false negatives particularly when the network is large and dynamic. In this paper, a new model is suggested that combines feature-classification Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) with Ant Colony Optimization (ACO) to learn the model and optimize hyperparameters. The capability of LSTM to learn time-related patterns in network traffic and the efficiency of ACO in the feature selection and hyperparameter optimization allow the proposed model to be much better regarding the accuracy and the complexity of computations. The framework is assessed using the Network Traffic Data for Intrusion Detection dataset, with an accuracy of 99.78, precision of 98.56, recall of 97.78 and F1-score of 98.87. These findings prove the possibility of the framework to be deployed in the real world to protect networks. In this paper, the synergy between deep learning and optimization plans is highlighted to improve intrusion detection, which proves to be an inspiring control mechanism to effective cybersecurity in large-scale networks. The originality of the work is the fact that LSTM is used along with the ACO to classify, as well as to select the features and establish the hyperparameters, reducing the number of computing resources, the number of false positives, and maximizing the accuracy, which makes the framework highly relevant to the real-world cybersecurity problem.

title: Intelligent Control Strategies for EV Motor Drives: A Comparative Study of BLDC and PMSM Systems
authors: Sujatha Banka, Chava Sunil Kumar, Arakala Harshitha, Theegala Anupriya
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Electric Vehicles (EVs) mainly use Brushless DC (BLDC) motors and Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors (PMSM) because of their features like efficient, reliable, and compact. Motor drives that use Fuzzy logic controllers (FLCs) can make sure optimal energy use. They provide smooth acceleration and under various operating conditions improves efficiency. EVs use FLCs to control BLDC and PMSMs. FLC offers an optimised solution with different principles, which removes the need for complex mathematical models. In BLDC motors, FLC improves speed control and reduces torque ripples, which will make the operation smoother and more stable. For PMSM, FLC works together with direct torque control (DTC) to reduce torque ripples and improving response times and overall efficiency. MATLAB/Simulink software supports modelling and simulation. Simulations and real-time tests show that the FLC-based control system is more efficient than traditional Proportional-Integra (PI) controllers. It responds very fastly to changes in motor behavior, ensuring reliable, flexible, and energy-efficient performance. This makes FLC the best choice for the future of electric vehicles, ensuring the best motor performance in various conditions and also improves overall vehicle efficiency and durability

title: Hierarchical Feature Integration Framework for Software Defect Prediction: Combining Syntactic Structure Mining with Neural Code Representations
authors: Cheng Yuan, Ziyang Liu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Software defect prediction plays a critical role in improving software quality and optimizing the allocation of testing resources during software development lifecycle. Existing approaches typically rely on either handcrafted software metrics or neural semantic representations extracted from pre-trained models, both of which exhibit inherent limitations in comprehensively capturing the multidimensional characteristics of defective code. Handcrafted metrics lack semantic understanding while pure neural approaches often ignore explicit structural information. This paper proposes a hierarchical feature integration framework that synergistically fuses Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) structural patterns with pre-trained code model representations to achieve enhanced defect prediction performance. Our framework introduces three key components: (1) Syntactic-Semantic Hybrid Encoding (SSHE), which serializes AST hierarchical structures into sequential format suitable for pre-trained model processing, enabling the joint capture of syntactic hierarchies and lexical semantics within unified contextual embeddings; (2) Multi-Scale Feature Aggregation (MSFA), which integrates high-dimensional neural embeddings with low-dimensional traditional software metrics through SMOTE-based class balancing and L2-normalized concatenation, effectively addressing both class imbalance and feature scale disparity issues; (3) Random Forest ensemble classifier for robust and stable prediction. Comprehensive experiments conducted on six widely used open-source Java projects under both within-project (cross-version) and cross-project settings demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach. Our framework achieves F1-scores of 0.752 and 0.691, AUC values of 0.813 and 0.752, and MCC values of 0.412 and 0.347 for within-project and cross-project prediction, respectively. These results correspond to a relative improvement of 9.1% to 14.8% in F1-score and 6.8% to 9.1% in AUC over state-of-the-art baseline methods. Ablation studies rigorously validate the contribution of each component, revealing that syntactic-semantic encoding provides the most significant benefit, yielding a relative F1-score improvement of 10.6%.

title: Full-Wave Simulation and Experimental Validation of Antennas Using Complex RF Components
authors: Anthony Eldridge, Alastair Wiegelmann, Samuel Drake, Shengjian Jammy Chen
publish: 2026 Australasian Symposium on Antennas (ASA)
abstract: Full-wave antenna simulations typically exclude complex Radio Frequency (RF) components because accurately modeling their intricate performance is challenging. However, this work demonstrates an approach to include accurate modeling of complex RF components in full-wave antenna simulation using both Touchstone<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">®</sup> data (S-parameters) provided by the supplier and a Computer Assisted Drawing (CAD) model with material properties which can provide a more comprehensive antenna performance prediction. As an example, we illustrate the process using a half-wave dipole antenna fed by a transformer-based balun, where the manufacturer's multi-port Touchstone<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">®</sup> file (S-parameters of a 4 port network) for the transformer is incorporated into a full-wave simulation workflow in conjunction with a CAD file of the transformer with appropriate material properties applied. Good agreement between simulation and the measured results of an antenna prototype proves that the proposed approach efficiently predicts complex antenna behavior and supports reliable design of advanced antenna structures.

title: Software Reliability Growth Across Multiple Releases Considering Fault Removal Efficiency, and Error Generation
authors: Shivani Kushwaha, Umashankar Samal, Rangan Kumar Guha
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: With technology becoming a part of our daily lives, there is a growing demand on adaptable software. Nevertheless, such software is complicated and time-intensive to develop, and therefore difficult to keep a breast with a mong companies. Companies have to keep their products upgraded to keep abreast with the competition and meet the requirements of the users. The single release model can not support this continuous process and this is why multi release software is required. Software reliability is affected by multiple factors throughout the development process, such as inefficiency in f ault removal, the disparity between fault-removal and actual faults, and the occurrence of more faults. That's why fault removal efficiency (FRE), fault reduction factor (FRF), and error generation, must be considered while modeling software reliability growth. This study presents a software reliability growth model (SRGM) for multi-release software that integrates FRE, FRF, and error generation. Fault reduction is modeled with the help of generalized modified Weibull d istribution (GMWD), which p rovides a more realistic model of failure behavior. This model provides a robust framework for evaluating debugging efficiency a nd optimizing reliability across multiple releases.

title: On the Problem of Interpretability of Neural Algorithms for Predicting Microscopic Traffic Flow Data
authors: Mikhail Gorodnichev
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: This paper develops and experimentally validates a method for interpreting driver behavior based on inertial sensor data. The relevance of the study stems from increasing road safety requirements and the development of telematics services and driver monitoring systems. A dataset consisting of multidimensional accelerometer and gyroscope time series was generated. Preprocessing was performed, including feature standardization, sequence segmentation into observation windows, and preparation of training samples. An interpretable algorithm for aggregating classifier predictions is proposed. Based on the distribution of recognized maneuvers, it generates a final driving style assessment based on threshold rules. The developed approach can be applied in telematics, driver monitoring, and behavioral insurance, and can also serve as a basis for further expansion with additional data sources and adaptation to various operating conditions.

title: A Survey on Privacy-Preserving Clustering Techniques: From K-Anonymity to Federated Learning
authors: Sachidanand, Sreemoyee Biswas, Pragati Agrawal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Privacy-preserving data publishing has become increasingly important due to concerns regarding data misuse, re-identification <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r$</tex> isks, a nd <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r$</tex> egulatory c onstraints s uch as GDPR and HIPAA. This survey reviews recent advances in privacy-preserving clustering, focusing on three methodological streams: differentially private hierarchical clustering, clustering-based k-anonymization, and privacy-preserving clustering in federated and distributed settings. We synthesize theoretical foundations, algorithmic developments, evaluation methodologies, and privacy–utility trade-offs across over twenty peer-reviewed studies. A unified taxonomy is proposed, followed by a discussion of open challenges and future research directions.

title: DualFusion SwinConv-UNet++: A Hybrid CNN–Transformer Framework for Thyroid Ultrasound Image Segmentation
authors: Smitirekha Behuria, Sujata Swain, Kriti Raj, Anjan Bandyopadhyay
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Segmentation of thyroid ultrasound image is a critical step to automating and improving the diagnosis and treatment planning of thyroid disease. Nevertheless, the natural imaging issues, including the speckle noise, low contrast, and non-uniform nodule morphology, make the accuracy of segmentation of traditional techniques more challenging. We address these challenges by proposing the state-of-the-art hybrid deep learning architecture, DualFusion SwinConv-UNet++ that consists of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and Swin Transformer designs with a new attention-based fusion mechanism and a nested U-Net++ decoder to improve feature interaction and reconstruction. Extensive tests on publicly available benchmark datasets show that the segmentation performance is substantially improved, and the mean Dice coefficient is 0.90, the pixel accuracy is above 92 percent, and the quantitative results are better than the state-of-the-art baselines. The work has provided new possibilities to create strong and clinically applicable thyroid ultrasound analysis tools.

title: Machine Learning Approaches for EMG Signal Classification to Predict Hand Gesture
authors: Yogendra Narayan, Fayzullo Omonov, Deepak Vasant Ulape, Charul Jain, Manu Kumari, Deepak Juneja
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: In this study, the hand gestures prediction for electromyography (EMG) signals classification is provided. EMG signals which are electrical stimulations of muscles were measured, processed and analyzed to achieve discriminative features in time, frequency and time-frequency domain. To improve a better result of the classification, a number of classifiers namely, Support Vector Machine (SVM), k-Nearest Neighbors (k-NN), Random Forest (RF) and Artificial Neural Network (ANN) have been compared with each other with the objective of hybrid feature combination to enhance the accuracy of the result of the classification. As it turned out, ANN proved to be the most precise in classification (96.2 percent), followed by SVM (94.6 percent), RF (92.7 percent) and k-NN (89.4 percent). These findings suggest that hybrid feature extraction works better in terms of discriminative power and nonlinear learning capacity of ANN can be employed to extract the complicated and non-stationary nature of EMG signals. Overall, this research paper indicates the potential of hybrid feature extraction in conjunction with sophisticated learning architectures to enhance performance, robustness and flexibility of EMG-based hand gesture recognition systems to be applied in the field of prosthetic control, rehabilitation robotics as well as human-machine interfaces.

title: Wideband Metamaterial Absorber Based on Polylactic Acid for Stealth Technology
authors: Kushagra Pratap, Shiven Shiv Senkumar, Tanishq Kapoor, Dilip Kumar Choudhary
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This work aims to present a PLA (Polylactic Acid) based microwave absorber aimed for wideband applications, with best performance in X-band <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(8-12 \text{GHz})$</tex>. The absorber structure consists of two hexagonal resonator rings coated with resistive rings, positioned over a concentric split ring column structure. This structure is backed by a copper sheet. There is a frame like PLA stripe along the perimeter of the copper sheet for improved structural integrity. The use of presented geometry instead of using a slab of similar dimensions also results in achieving of a light form factor. The resistive ink used on the horizontal resonator has bulk conductivity of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2500 \mathrm{S} / \mathrm{m}$</tex>. The dimensions are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 1. 6 m m} \times \mathbf{1 1. 6 m m} \times \mathbf{5 m m}$</tex>. The structure resonates at the frequency of 9.56 GHz. The −10 dB working band is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6.88 \text{GHz}-19.88 \text{GHz}$</tex>, thus the proposed absorber can be of utility in operations in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{C}, \mathrm{X}, \text{Ku}$</tex> and K bands. Further, fractional bandwidth of 135.9% is offered by the structure. Absorptivity of greater than 88% is accomplished in the occupied band.

title: Passive Fingerprinting of ICS Devices Using Aggregates of Modbus Packets
authors: Andres Galiasso, Peter J. Hawrylak
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: Industrial Control Systems (ICS) serve as the foundation for the operation of industrial processes by monitoring and controlling physical systems. Fingerprinting of ICS devices is a crucial first step in maintaining the security of industrial networks. Active scanning may have adverse effects on an industrial network, so using passive fingerprinting is important. In this paper, we propose a technique for fingerprinting ICS devices that utilize the Modbus protocol. Our technique is based on analyzing an aggregate of Modbus query and response packets within a fifteen minute window. The Modbus registers accessed are recorded for each Modbus query, and the Shannon Entropy and hexadecimal sums of chosen segments of the message are calculated for each Modbus response packet. The distribution of these values are used to create 17 statistical features that a multinomial logistic regression machine learning algorithm uses to fingerprint the ICS devices. Two experiments were performed to evaluate the fingerprinting model. The model achieved an accuracy score of 90.55% in classifying a set of five devices during our most challenging experiment.

title: Quantum-Inspired Sparse Bayesian Learning Framework for Ultra-Reliable Spectrum Sensing in Low-SNR Cognitive Radio Networks
authors: Anandh Nagarajan, Hema V, S Punitha, B.Senthil Kumar, S.Lavanya, V.Brinda
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Cognitive radio networks using spectrum sensing technology are prone to problems at low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) conditions, where traditional schemes of detection are characterised by low probability of accurate detection and high false alarms. In this paper, a framework of the Quantum-Inspired Sparse Bayesian Learning is proposed that aims to attain the ultra-reliable spectrum sensing under such unfavorable conditions. The method utilizes ideas in quantum probability theory to formulate signal presence uncertainty, and combines sparse Bayesian learning to make use of signal sparsity inherent in the frequency domain. The method allows better discrimination between primary user signals and noise even under low SNR (SNR < -20 dB), because the sensing problem is expressed in a quantum-inspired Hilbert space. The framework also includes an adaptive prior updating scheme where the algorithm would respond to different spectral occupancy patterns in a dynamic manner and hence increase the robustness of the algorithm to environmental changes. The results of simulations indicate better detection probability, low computational complexity, and noise uncertainty resilience than the conventional energy detection, matched filtering, and cyclostationary methods. This paper offers a promising route toward next-generation, ultra-reliable spectrum awareness of cognitive radio and emerging 6G wireless systems.

title: Relai — AI for Disaster Response and Relief Using Random Forest Regression for Faster Response and Alerting
authors: Mohith Mohan, Mokshitha Garapati, Goutham Y. K, Manoranjan S, Abdul Haq Nalband, Bahubali Shiragapur
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Disaster response is increasingly relying on artificial intelligence, which uses a range of data-how it saves lives and the challenges it faces. AI has become a powerful new tool for disaster response, and the Department of Homeland Security is investigating how to best use it. In concert with a team of scientists from Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, we produced a very preliminary AI model to demonstrate the potential of this new technology. This model was trained with a combination of human-generated and machine-generated datasets, using the XGBoost Classifier-a machine learning algorithm that Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory scientists have found works well with the kinds of data we have and the types of problems we're trying to solve. The model can be used to predict where and when a disaster will occur so that maps of the projected disaster zones can be generated and displayed on a monitor.

title: High Performance Convolutional Neural Network for Brain Tumour Detection from MRI Images
authors: Sangita Hirani, Niti Khetra
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This study presents an automated framework for the detection and classification of brain tumours using deep learning methodologies, incorporating Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Artificial Neural Networks (ANN), and transfer learning (TL) strategies. The proposed system aims to accurately identify tumour presence and categorise tumour types from Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) scans, thereby assisting radiologists and clinicians in clinical decision making. By improving diagnostic accuracy and reducing analysis time, the proposed approach has the potential to enhance early detection, optimise treatment planning, and ultimately improve patient outcomes in real world healthcare. The training dataset comprises 1635 brain MRI images with tumours and 1584 images of normal brains. The developed CNN model includes three convolutional layers with max-pooling operations, supplemented by dropout layers and fully connected dense layers for classification. Comparative analysis indicates that the proposed model outperforms existing CNN-based techniques, achieving an accuracy of 97 %, precision of 96 %, recall of 97 %, specificity of 97 %, and an F1-score of 97 %, demonstrating its suitability for brain tumour detection tasks.

title: H-OTFS: An Anti-Jamming Transmission Scheme for OTFS via Hadamard Spreading
authors: Yongning Zhuo, Yuhan Qiu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Aimed to enhance the ability of robust transmission in jamming environment for OTFS system, a novel scheme termed H-OTFS is proposed, which spreads the sparse user data using Hadamard transformation to Delay-Doppler (DD)domain. At the receiver, after converting the received signal into the DD domain, a screening process is employed to excise DD domain bins contaminated by jamming signals whose power exceeds a predefined threshold. The original user data are subsequently recovered from the partial observation using a multi-constraint compressive sensing (CS) with Alternating bidirectional multiplier algorithm (ADMM). Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheme significantly outperforms conventional OTFS systems under strong dense jamming conditions, achieving a substantial reduction in bit error rate.

title: Hello-Id: Honeypot Enabled Deep Learning Framework for Intrusion Detection
authors: Sangyam Rohith Shailaja, Ushanadhini
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: An Intrusion Detection System (IDS) is designed to monitor network traffic for signs of suspicious activity and generate alerts when threats are detected. IDS play a critical role in identifying and mitigating cybersecurity risks by differentiating between legitimate and malicious network traffic. However, traditional IDS frameworks often face challenges in accurately detecting complex attack patterns and distinguishing them from normal activity. This leads to a high rate of false positives and hampers the system's ability to adapt to the rapidly evolving landscape of cyber threats. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a Honeypot EnabLed deep Learning framewOrk for Intrusion Detection (HELLO-ID) approach for enhancing network security and detecting attacks accurately. Initially, the system utilizes a honeypot server for data collection. The system employs Dragonfly Optimization for feature selection and a Stacked Convolutional Neural Network (SCNN) and Bidirectional Gated Recurrent Unit (BiGRU) approach for attack classification. Experiments on the NSL-KDD and IDS2018 datasets show that the suggested HELLO-ID outperforms other existing methods. The suggested technique efficiency is assessed utilizing accuracy, precision, recall, f1score, and MSE. Compared to existing techniques the proposed approach offered enhanced results for two datasets with 97.84%, and 97.59% accuracy, respectively.

title: Mitigation of Crosstalk of On-Chip Antenna in RFIC Using Dual Guard Band Method
authors: Harshavardhan Singh, Vipin Singh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: On-chip antennas (OCAs) provide a solution for designing a truly wireless and compact Radio Frequency Integrated Circuit (RFIC) for system on chip applications. However, crosstalk between OCA and other RFIC components limits the application. This study analyzes the crosstalk between two components (here, two OCA) on a single chip placed side by side and then provides a solution to mitigate it. Initially, a miniaturized on-chip patch antenna is designed at 10.5 GHz. To analyze crosstalk <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(S_{12}\right)$</tex>, two similar antennas on the chip were placed adjacent. With the 6 mm optimized separation between two patches, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S_{12}$</tex> is obtained as −20.1 dB. Furthermore, the placement of two parallel guard bands between patches reduces the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S_{12}$</tex> to −32.5 dB. In addition, a parametric study was performed to decide the optimal number of guard bands. The simulated results, along with the comparative analysis, validate the potential of the proposed method in crosstalk reduction in the RFIC environment.

title: Design and Analysis of a Stack-Enhanced LECTOR-Based Power Gating Scheme with PMOS Sleep Transistors
authors: Mahesh Bidarkote, M. Suresh, Deep Chandra Upadhyay, Ankur Beohar, Pallabi Sarkar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This paper presents a novel hybrid leakage reduction scheme named SL-PG (Stack-Enhanced LECTOR with PMOS-only Power Gating) for CMOS digital circuits. By synergistically combining Forced Stack, LECTOR, and PMOS sleep transistor techniques, the proposed approach achieves superior leakage suppression in deep-submicron regimes. Through comprehensive SPICE simulations on 2 -input NOR gates and 4:1 multiplexer in 90nm technology, we analyze static/dynamic power, total dissipation, and Power-Delay Product (PDP). Results demonstrate that SL-PG achieves 99.47% leakage reduction <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.00369 \mu ~\mathrm{W})$</tex> for NOR gates with optimal PDP of 1.78 fJ for multiplexers. Forced Stack offers 99.1 % leakage suppression with moderate overhead, while LECTOR increases power by 167 % in NOR gates. Based on extensive analysis, we provide design guidelines for selecting appropriate techniques across applications.

title: A Deep Reinforcement Learning-Based Dynamic Queue Management Approach for Network Traffic Scheduling
authors: Xiaoliang Zhang, Yinan Dou, Mei Yan, Shuyu Han, Chenyang Xu, Jian Wu, Bei Ou, Yitong Liu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: With the growing scale and heterogeneity of network traffic, efficient network traffic scheduling has become a critical challenge in modern communication systems. Existing methods often show limited adaptability under resource constraints and dynamic traffic conditions. This paper proposes a deep reinforcement learning-based dynamic queue management approach for network traffic scheduling, termed DRL-DQM, which formulates traffic scheduling as a queue-level sequential decision-making problem to achieve multi-dimensional performance optimization. Extensive simulations under diverse traffic loads and bandwidth configurations demonstrate that DRL-DQM significantly outperforms baseline methods in queueing latency and packet loss rate. Moreover, the proposed approach exhibits strong stability and robustness across different network scenarios.

title: A Fused Ensemble Learning Approach for Early Heart Disease Prediction
authors: Narayan Jee, Gesu Thakur, Sumit Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The correct early diagnosis of heart illness ensures effective therapy and improves patient outcomes. This article presents a new method using fused ensemble learning that combines the abilities of different machine learning models to increase the accuracy and robustness of heart disease diagnosis. The framework uses basic classifiers comprising Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Gradient Boosting (GBM), which are later enhanced through meta-level fusion. Based on all-round testing on benchmark datasets, ensemble models outperform both individual classifiers and standard classification techniques in precision, recall, and classification accuracy. The results emphasize the mixed ensemble approach's effectiveness as a consistent decision-support system for the early detection of heart disease in clinical settings.

title: Athercare: Unified AI System for Health Intelligence in Real Time
authors: Pandurang, V Mareeswari, Snigdha Kesh, Navyashree N, Nandana P T, S M Siddalingaswamy
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Quality healthcare access is a worldwide issue, with disjointed systems, slow diagnostic processes, and complex medical documentation posing significant barriers for many. Medical AI introduces an innovative digital health ecosystem that simplifies and improves the delivery of care. this sophisticated platform integrates a range of advanced features, such as geolocation-based tools to locate nearby medical facilities in real time, AI-driven assessments for detecting acne and other skin conditions, and intelligent processing of blood test results from PDF reports. An interactive chatbot delivers tailored health advice, making it easier for users to navigate their health concerns. the platform leverages sophisticated image processing and machine learning-driven natural language processing to interpret intricate medical records effortlessly. Mediconnect AI is particularly impactful in rural and underserved communities, where access to specialized medical expertise is often scarce. By improving diagnostic precision and simplifying medical data for patients, it empowers individuals to better understand their health. mediconnect AI marks a fundamental advance in creating a more equitable, intuitive and affordable health system, offering immediate assistance and allowing users to be in charge of their health journey.

title: Universal Onboard Complex of an Unmanned Ground Vehicle
authors: I. A. Pestova, A. A. Gorbenko, V. M. Zuev
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: The article provides a detailed analysis of the onboard systems of autonomous transport vehicles. The architecture “sensors-computing-actuators” is examined, and the operating principles as well as the trade-offs in selecting various types of sensors (lidars, radars, cameras) are analyzed in detail. Special attention is paid to AI-based computing platforms and software stacks for perception, prediction, and path planning. Key technological challenges are identified, such as ensuring fault tolerance and handling “edge cases”.

title: Enhancing Earthquake Prediction with Ensemble Learning: A Hybrid XGBoost and LightGBM Approach
authors: Manni Kumar, Nivedita Sharma, Mahatava Saxena, Samaira Handa, Tamanna Sarawag, Tamanna Kashyap
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Anticipated prediction of earthquakes is also a critical component of disaster management that requires accurate and timely forecasts to minimize the possible damages. The study adopts a two-machine learning system that comprises of XGBoost and LightGBM to attain better accuracy in the process of predicting earthquakes. The data of historical seismic activity is merged with seismic parameters such as magnitude and depth and location-based information which was acquired at Kaggle. The combination of XGBoost and LightGBM functions in an ensemble model allows more sophisticated seismic patterns to be detected that eventually adds value to the prediction quality. When ensemble learning approaches are applied, the results of improved generalization and reduced overfitting issues are achieved, which in turn allows the application in the real world. The mixture of XGBoost and LightGBM works in the ensemble model permits to rent finer seismic patterns and is more enhanced than individual models in prediction. Ensemble learning through the assistance of a special preprocessing pipeline of unequal seismic data and strong hyperparameter optimization that improve generalization and reduce overfitting. The quick detection of earthquakes that will be evident through the assistance of this strategy will assist the authorities in charge of disaster management in undertaking pre-emptive preventive measures in order to minimize the number of victims and safeguard infrastructure. As has been shown, the proposed ensemble outperforms the classic machine learning baselines, and it means that the methods of AI- based ensemble are applicable in the real world when it comes to earthquake prediction.

title: A Hybrid Machine Learning Approach for Fraud Prevention and Health Insurance Claim Verification
authors: Talari Srinivasa Rao, Myneedi Chetan Viswa Kumar, Janapati Malathi, Shaik Mujahid
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Insurance fraud is a loss for both the insurers and the honest insured. To counter this, a hybrid model was created combining XGBoost and SVM, supported by Optical Character Recognition (OCR) and Natural Language Processing (NLP) for extracting key information from claim documents and accelerating the verification process. In order to deal with the class imbalance problem, both SMOTE and random undersampling were implemented which in turn improved the performance of the model. The overall accuracy was 98.48% in the case of the developed system. For the legitimate claims, it achieved 98% precision, 99% recall, and a 99% F1-score, while for the fraudulent claims the scores were 99% precision, 97% recall, and 98% F1-score. The AUC and ROC score of 0.98 indicates the robustness of the model and reveals its strong potential for real-time, scalable fraud detection.

title: Robot Learning Framework using Behavioral Cloning and Gaussian Mixture Model (BC-GMM) for Sorting Tasks
authors: Hambal Tella, Prajyot Patil, Maria Kyrarini
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: As collaborative robots (cobots) become available in industry, it is crucial to enable non-coders to program cobots easily. For example, in sorting tasks that may require adjustments over time, workers could easily adjust the cobot’s behavior by utilizing learning from demonstrations (LfD) approaches. This paper presents an LfD approach for enabling a cobot to learn sorting tasks by integrating the Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM), validated in Mujoco for policy training. Quantitative analysis shows that the improved BC-GMM achieves the lowest mean squared error (MSE = 0.00207) and highest R-squared score (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>=0.89), outperforming other methods, such as standard BC, diffusion policy, and implicit behavioral cloning (IBC).

title: Fusing Camera and Electromyography Data for Enhanced Range of Motion Assessment
authors: Xuke Yan, Bo Liu, Jinzhao He, Guangzhi Qu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Accurate and automated Range of Motion (ROM) assessment is essential for rehabilitation, physical therapy, and post-surgical recovery. Traditional manual goniometer-based evaluations suffer from subjectivity, inter-rater variability, and reliance on trained professionals. Although RGB-based computer vision enables markerless ROM estimation, it remains susceptible to occlusions, pose estimation errors, and difficulties detecting subtle joint movements. Furthermore, vision alone cannot capture neuromuscular activation, which is crucial for understanding joint dynamics. To address these challenges, we propose a multi-modal deep learning framework that integrates RGB-based motion tracking with electromyography (EMG) signals. EMG provides neuromuscular activation data, enhancing the robustness against visual occlusions and improving sensitivity to subtle joint displacements. Our method employs an Hourglass-based convolutional neural network (CNN) for spatial feature extraction and a gated recurrent unit (GRU)-based model for temporal EMG processing. To further enhance performance, we introduce feature-level and modality-level attention modules, dynamically emphasizing the most informative features and modality contributions. Experimental results demonstrate that our proposed model achieves an overall RMSE of 2.55, and improvements via the feature and modality attention mechanisms, respectively. Moreover, the fully fused RGB-EMG model outperforms RGB-only approaches, particularly in accurately predicting subtle ROM movements.

title: Design and Validation of a YOLOv8 Synthetic Data Generation Pipeline for Surface Defect Detection in Manufacturing
authors: Armin Moghadam, Sahib Bhatia, Fatemeh Davoudi Kakhki, Hiroyuki Ichikawa
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Surface defect detection is essential for maintaining quality in manufacturing as it helps ensure product reliability and reduces material waste. While AI-driven visual inspection in manufacturing offers promising automation, to achieve high performance, it demands large, diverse and annotated datasets, which are often impractical to collect specially in low-volume or additive manufacturing contexts. Traditional defect detection methods relying on manual inspection and physical sensors can be labor-intensive, costly, and subject to variability. This study introduces a validated scalable pipeline for generating synthetic image datasets tailored for AI-based surface defect detection. Our pipeline integrates 3D modeling in Blender, procedural defect simulation, randomized rendering, and annotation generation to produce synthetic datasets with controlled variations in lighting, camera angles, and defect morphology. Cylindrical components were selected as the test geometry due to their industrial relevance and predictable curvature for defect propagation. To validate the pipeline, synthetic images were used to train a YOLOv8-based instance segmentation model, which was then applied, without retraining, to images of 3D printed physical parts. The trained model achieved 88% accuracy and 90% precision on the real object test dataset, demonstrating strong generalization and validating the realism and utility of the generated synthetic data. This research provides a scalable, low-cost approach for developing AI inspection systems without the need for labor-intensive real-world data collection. By bridging simulation and deployment through 3D printed physical validation, this pipeline contributes a practical foundation for synthetic-data-driven quality assurance in smart manufacturing environments.

title: An Efficient Technique to Implement Individual Temperature Control of Dynamic Burn-in Tests for High Power Integrated Circuits
authors: Yuansheng Li, Zewei Wang, Lili Teng, Xu Zhang, Jun Luo, Zhizhe Wang, Guoguang Lu, Pengfei Hu, Shuanzhu Fang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: This paper presents an efficient technique to achieve individual temperature control during dynamic burn-in of high-power integrated circuits (ICs). A CPU dynamic burn-in board prototype was developed featuring dual, independently temperature-controlled stations, each incorporating multi-source heating, a thermal management system, real-time temperature acquisition, and FPGA-implemented PID regulation. Specifically, an efficient vapor-chamber-based cooling methodology is proposed to support burn-in for 200 W TDP chips. Furthermore, the system provides flexible digital I/O and DPS configurations so that it can accommodate a wide range of test conditions. The efficacy of this technique is validated by experimental results, which show that the proposed method achieves temperature control accuracy within ±1 °C and maintains a relative error below 5%.

title: Real-Time Plastic Waste Detection and Region Density Mapping using Optimized YOLOv5 and Heatmaps Overlays
authors: Semmathi G S, R Mathesh, Samvrutha S B, G Muthupandi, S Pavithra
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Plastic waste in coastal and marine environments threatens ecosystems and biodiversity. This calls for rapid detection systems to detect and prioritize zones of high-density waste for effective monitoring and removal strategies. Limited computational power in field conditions and the absence of actionable visual insights pose hurdles in the practical deployment of these systems. This work seeds these gaps by building a two-step plastic waste detection and density mapping system. Essentially, we first put lightweight YOLOv5 variants (YOLOv5n, s, and m) to test on a filtered TACO dataset in an attempt to find the optimal accuracy-time trade-off. Then the selected model is used for the generation of zone-based heatmaps by spatial accumulation of detection densities followed by Gaussian smoothing. The proposed system maintains real-time detection while visually highlighting the waste accumulation zones for cleanup planning. Experimental results show that YOLOv5s yields the best compromise between speed and accuracy; and the heatmaps provide meaningful and interpretable localization of prioritization for environmental cleanup.

title: Hybrid Clinical–Fundus Model for Diabetes Classification
authors: Devnihil S, Sri Venkada Krishnan S, S Ajay Vikram, S. Manimaran
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Diabetes prediction models have traditionally relied either on structured clinical measurements or on retinal fundus image analysis, which limits their ability to fully exploit visual biomarkers while remaining lightweight and deployable. Traditional diabetes prediction relies on isolated clinical or imaging modalities, limiting holistic risk assessment. In this work, we propose a hybrid multimodal framework that fuses deep convolutional embeddings from EfficientNet-B3 with structured clinical variables (e.g., BMI, Glucose) and handcrafted Haralick texture features. By concatenating these diverse signals into a unified vector, we capture both metabolic risk factors and subtle microvascular retinal changes. The proposed pipeline achieves 97 % accuracy and 0.97 AUC, significantly outperforming an image-only baseline <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(84 \%)$</tex>. Statistical validation via McNemar's test <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(p<0.001)$</tex> confirms that integrating clinical metadata with deep and handcrafted visual biomarkers yields robust, statistically superior predictions for non-invasive screening.

title: Wireless Electric Vehicle Charging Powered by Solar Monitoring
authors: Malathika A, Dharun P.K, M. Shyamalagowri
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The fast adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) has created an urgent need for more efficient, available, and sustainable charging solutions. This paper proposes a novel prototype system that integrates Dynamic Wireless Electric Vehicle Charging with a Solar-Powered Monitoring Mechanism, aiming to revolutionize how EVs are powered in real-time, in particular in urban and highway environments. A microcontroller-primarily based transmission unit is powered thru a solar-assisted battery financial institution, ensuring that the strength supply remains smooth, renewable, and impartial of traditional grid systems. The middle charging mechanism employs switching circuits to manipulate the inductive energy drift correctly, with system parameters which includes voltage, modern, and strength switch fee being monitored continuously. A real-time comments machine and show interface enhance visibility and manipulate, at the same time as a secondary embedded unit within the car manages reception and safety. Furthermore, remote monitoring competencies are included via Wi-Fi conversation protocols to ensure clever diagnostics, device indicators, and performance analytics. This prototype addresses key boundaries in conventional plug-in and static wireless charging systems by presenting flexibility, sustainability, and scalability. Field-stage validation of the prototype demonstrates solid performance and promising energy switch performance, marking a huge step towards clever transportation ecosystems. The integration of sun electricity with dynamic Wi-Fi charging affords a feasible and eco-friendly course forward for the future of electric mobility.

title: SkewFuse: Unsupervised Confidence-Guided Rank Fusion for Multi-View Image Retrieval
authors: Enis Teper, Mustafa Keskin, Emre Rençberoğlu, Yusuf Hüseyin Şahin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: In image retrieval systems, the use of multiple embedding models to capture diverse visual features often improves retrieval performance. However, traditional rank aggregation methods generally assume uniform reliability across retrieval models, neglecting the variability in model confidence across different queries. This paper introduces SkewFuse, an unsupervised rank fusion approach that dynamically weights retrieval results based on statistical skewness of similarity score distributions, effectively capturing query-specific confidence. We extend the Dowdall positional voting method by integrating these confidence weights, achieving adaptive and robust fusion. Evaluations conducted on the In-Shop Clothes Retrieval dataset demonstrate that SkewFuse consistently outperforms existing unsupervised and supervised fusion methods, notably improving early precision metrics, including a 4.7% relative gain in Recall@1 over the strongest baseline. SkewFuse offers an efficient, parameter-light solution ideal for real-world applications where top-ranked retrieval precision is critical.

title: Deep Learning-Based Sentiment Analysis in English Literature
authors: Santhi P, Rajya Lakshmi Yamala, Renu Sree Pokala, Shabeena Shaik, S Ram, K Krishnam Raju, Jhansi Vazram Bolla
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Thought analysis (sometimes referred to opinion extracting) is a specialty of NLP. It is applied to find out the sentiments in text information. The operations of classical machine knowledge are based on the manually constructed features and dictionary-based point space. Automated erudite analysis is more accessible via the then system, learn more about what is emotional, and obtain information that is potentially useful to get an anatomy of erudite factory and place that into training. The literary would be disposed to collect the fined heartstrings which would influence the interest and critical appreciation of the florilegium. The research process may be assisted by the methodology in objective erudite reading and pedagogic protocols to empower researchers to perform a superior analysis of the emotive writing and thematic compounding in the text through relating and trying identical emotive variables offered by NLP.
