title: Duck Swarm Optimization-Enhanced Contextual Graph Attention Networks for Power-Optimized 10T2C Capacitive SRAM Near-Memory Computing Macros
authors: Sudha P, G. LakshmiNarayanan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Current computing-in-memory (CIM) computer architecture designs based on conventional 6T and 8T SRAM technologies have been highly challenged to balance the storage density, power, and computational stability thus restricting their scaling to AI-driven near-memory computing. To address these shortcomings, this work suggests a 10T2C capacitive SRAM near-memory computing (NMC) macro with Duck Swarm Optimization (DSO)-based Contextual Graph Attention Networks (CGATs). The macro has been proposed using charge-sharing capacitors to enhance the stability of read/write and leakage and the DSO-CGAT framework to offer adaptive multiobjective optimization based on power, delay, and reliability measures. Circuit- and architecture- Level analysis indicates that the current approach provides a 34.7 % lower average power consumption, 27.5 % higher read/write stability margin and up to 22.1 % faster inference throughput than traditional PSO- and GA-assisted optimization frameworks. In addition, DSO-CGAT model has <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 9. 6 \%}$</tex> reduced power-delay product (PDP) compared to the baseline graph-based optimizers, which emphasizes its applicability in edge-AI applications. These findings position the 10T2C capacitive SRAM macro as scalable, power-saving NMC solution to next-generation low-power computing systems.

title: MediVault - Your Medical Wallet
authors: R. Jegatha, Jayani Nirupa V, Saivarshaa S, Krithika G
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: In critical care scenarios, timely access to accurate patient data is paramount. This paper introduces MediVault, a comprehensive, end-to-end digital solution designed to securely consolidate and manage electronic health records (EHRs). Developed as a cross-platform mobile application using Flutter with a Supabase backend, MediVault centralizes a patient's medical history, including diagnoses, prescriptions, and reports–enabling healthcare professionals to retrieve essential information swiftly. The system employs device-based biometric authentication to uphold stringent privacy standards. By providing a unified platform with role-based access for patients, doctors, and administrators, MediVault reduces data fragmentation and improves continuity of care. The core objective is to support medical personnel with immediate access to vital health data, thereby minimizing clinical errors and improving patient outcomes in both emergency and routine healthcare settings.

title: SnapClean: Garbage Violation Reporting and Monitoring System
authors: Aniket Amte, Poonam Gupta, Harsh Desai, Jay Baraskar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Solid waste management has become one of the most critical challenges in rapidly urbanizing societies due to the increase in population density, unregulated waste disposal practices, and the inability of municipal authorities to effectively monitor and respond to violations. Manual reporting methods for garbage violations are often unreliable, delayed, and inconsistent, resulting in public dissatisfaction and environmental hazards. This paper presents an AI-powered Garbage Violation Reporting and Monitoring System that integrates deep learning-based image classification, severity prediction, and geo-located citizen reporting to enhance municipal waste management efficiency. A ResNet-50 convolutional neural network (CNN) is trained on a custom garbage image dataset to classify waste images into four severity classes: High, Moderate, Low, and Invalid. The Invalid class enables automatic rejection of non-garbage or irrelevant submissions, ensuring data reliability. Citizens can upload geotagged waste images through a web-based platform, which are processed by the trained model to generate actionable insights for municipal dashboards. The system provides real-time visualization of violation hotspots, severity-based prioritization, and report analytics. Experimental evaluation on the custom dataset demonstrates an overall classification accuracy of approximately 76%, reflecting robust performance under real-world lighting and background conditions. The proposed system promotes civic participation, supports data-driven decision-making, and improves the responsiveness of urban waste management operations.

title: Multi-Resolution Fall Transformer: A Dual-Branch Transformer for Wrist-IMU Fall Detection Using WEDA-FALL and FallAllD Datasets
authors: Milan Mondal, Yadunath Pathak
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The importance of accurate fall detection is crucial for promoting the safety of seniors and allowing for remote, independent health monitoring. However, the inconsistency in behavior of models across datasets due to variations in sensor sampling rate and individual movement characteristics makes it difficult to develop reliable models. In our study, we propose the concept of the Multi-Resolution FallTransformer (MR-FT). This is a dual-branch Transformer architecture that takes into account wrist IMU data sampled at two different rates (WEDA-FALL samples at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 ~ H z}$</tex>; FallAllD samples at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 3 8 ~ H z}$</tex>), without requiring any downsampling. Both branches learn their respective rhythmic motion behaviors, yet the cross-attention block acts as some form of communication mechanism to allow both branches to “communicate” with each other, to ensure that slow changes in posture are identified along with rapid impacts that are detected by both branches. We demonstrated that the MR-FT achieved an AUC score of approximately 0.979 and an accuracy score of approximately 0.889 when tested using paired testing, outperforming typical CNN, BiLSTM, and single-branch Transformer configurations. We developed MR-FT to maintain the original timing information and allow attention to fuse the data, creating an extremely robust and transparent method to combine data recorded at multiple sensor sampling rates. Therefore, MR-FT will allow us to better design systems capable of adapting to various applications including, but not limited to fall detection, rehabilitation tracking, and daily health monitoring.

title: Electricity Rate Analysis for Remote Alaskan Microgrid Communities: Impact of Renewable Energy Integration and Electrification
authors: J. R. Yadav, I. Mathiasson, B. Panikkar, M. Almassalkhi
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: Alaska’s remote communities face some of the nation’s highest electricity rates due to reliance on diesel generation, prompting state through the Power Cost Equalization (PCE) program and growing interest in renewable energy and tribal Independent Power Produce (IPP) models. This paper presents an analysis of the complex dynamics of electricity rates within this context, focusing on how renewable penetration (α), IPP sales rate (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">IPP</inf>), electrification, and fixed costs influence retail rates (R) and stakeholder outcomes. This analysis identifies combinations of α and R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">IPP</inf> that keep retail rates within the PCE threshold ($/kWh) and demonstrates strategies to bring the retail rate back into the eligible range when it exceeds the PCE threshold. The key finding is that when renewable energy is procured at a price lower than fossil-fuel generation, it benefits both the utility and the state by reducing overall production costs and lowering the use of PCE funds, while simultaneously generating revenue for tribally owned IPPs. This collaborative framework promotes cleaner operations, enhances system efficiency, and stabilizes retail rates against fluctuations in diesel fuel prices.

title: Reducing Tail Latency in Storage-Disaggregated Database Systems
authors: Xi Pang, Jianguo Wang
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
abstract: None

title: Emerging Approaches for Integrating Passive Balancing and Charging Strategies in Electric Vehicle Batteries
authors: Shrusti Girimath, Anupama R. Itagi, Rakhee Kallimani
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The growing pace of electric vehicle (EV) adoption and renewable energy storage has increased the need for safe, efficient and long-life lithium-ion battery systems. Among the various strategies used in Battery Management Systems (BMS), cell balancing and charging algorithms are important for maintaining battery health and operational safety. In this work, the operation of a passive cell balancing mechanism combined with the Constant Current-Constant Voltage (CC-CV) charging algorithm is studied. The focus is on the relationship between SoC equalization and charging efficiency, and the associated trade-off for energy loss and control complexity. A complete literature survey is performed to synthesize recent advances in balancing strategies from traditional resistive mechanisms to AI-enhanced hybrid methods, and their interactions with new charging algorithms. By collating relevant findings from the literature review, the work highlights the need for adjustable, intelligent, and low-cost balancing-charging paradigms to improve the safety, reliability, and durability of lithium-ion batteries with strong applicability for EVs. This comprehensive literature review establishes that the work is grounded in the current literature and will proceed to address meaningful gaps in the literature.

title: Multimodal FactCheckNet on Low Resource Social Media Data
authors: Vani Malagar, Aditi Sharma, Shailendra Tiwari
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Social media, as a medium for news consumption, offers both positive and negative aspects. With the advent of its affordability, accessibility, and speedy dissemination of information, individuals can pursue and engage with news via social media, which, conversely, facilitates the prevalence of “fake news,” defined as low-quality news containing deliberately incorrect information that creates a significant social threat involving the manipulated content. The categorization of multimodal misinformation presents an intricate challenge of complexity, especially under conditions of restricted data availability and resource constraints. This study addressed the classification challenge of differentiating between fake and legitimate news employing a multimodal framework that integrates text and visual data. In this paper, our analysis was carried on a sizable social media dataset due to limited computational resources and the restricted availability of complete datasets as a result of content removal or platform-based restrictions and proposed a Fact_Check_Net architecture by comparing the lightweight and heavier based deep learning configurations for multimodal data by evaluating under the fusion strategies to understand how the encoder depth influences the classification performance.

title: The Future of VLSI Testing: Deep Learning Models for Accurate Fault Detection – Review
authors: Balkees Marliyya S, Saumiya S, Kirubasankar K
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The growth in digital VLSI circuits has increased the complexities in fault detection. Traditional methodology such as ATPGs and SAT-based algorithms suffer from space explosion and limited scalability fails to detect the faults such as hardware Trojans. Enhancement in machine learning especially in deep learning algorithms such as SSAE (Stacked sparse autoencoder), and DSAE promises effective fault detection. These models guarantee efficient feature extraction, test pattern recognition and efficient fault classification in sequential and combinational circuits. The main objective of this paper is to review and compare machine learning-based fault detection, deep learning-based fault detection with conventional method in digital VLSI. It highlights their comparative strength, limitation, scalability, research gaps and methodology used in each method. The novelty of the paper lies in its structured comparison of unsupervised, semi-supervised and supervised models for fault detection.

title: Design of Camouflage Military Robot using Raspberry Pi and Machine Learning
authors: T A Mohanaprakash, D R Swathi Kumari, Savija. J, M.Geetha, K.M. Gopinath, S.Kaviarasan
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Globally speaking, India is a country that is growing quickly. Crop diseases pose a severe danger to food security, yet they are still hard to identify. Due to their reliance on a manually created feature extraction process, these systems' accuracy has reached its peak. This approach classification method needs to incorporate CNN to surpass grading accuracy for tomato leaf pestilence. To properly describe and categorize tomato infections, the Deep Learning algorithm is used. Using a sample of 3000 frames of tomato leaves with nine various pestilences and a better and healthier leaf, the full simulation was carried out using Google Colab. The targeted area of the input photographs is first segregated from the genuine snaps after preprocessing the input images. Second, the frames are further processed using various CNN model hyper-parameters. CNN also extracts additional qualities from frames, such as colours, borders, and textures. The results reveal that the predictions made by the demonstrated replica are 98.49% precise.

title: SmartLib: Next-Generation IoT Access Control for Libraries
authors: Sushant Naik, Swarup Magar, Sneha More, Sakshi Murade, Tejas Pandure, N.S. Patankar
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: In response to the limitations of the traditional library system, which is still supervised manually, this paper presents SmartLib, an IoT architecture for an automated library management system. The system’s primary feature is an automated access control system which is secure and RFID enabled and uses a solenoid lock to free the access control system from manual entry logs. To assist the administrators, SmartLib provides an analytics dashboard which is robust and presents real-time visitor information and provides usage reports on resources. Also, the framework enables students to access an online portal to browse the library catalogue and remotely reserve books which are still on the library stack. The effectiveness of SmartLib shows a large improvement in operational efficiency, security and resource management in academic libraries.

title: Dispersion and Bandgap Analysis of Miniaturized Meander-Lined EBG for Sub-GHz Communication
authors: Siva, Crasencia Mary, Divya Lakshmi, Mohan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This paper offers a dispersion and bandgap analysis of a compact square-shaped electromagnetic bandgap (EBG) structure constructed on a 1.6mm thick FR-4 substrate. In order to achieve sub-wavelength functioning inside a 10 mm × 10 mm footprint, the unit cell uses a downsized meander-lined architecture with many turns that effectively extend the current route length. In sub-GHz and lower-microwave wireless systems, the structure's prominent bandgap of 0.45 GHz, which covers the 4.45-4.85 GHz range, makes it appropriate for reducing surface-wave propagation and enhancing antenna isolation. To assess the propagation behavior and verify the stopband's existence, dispersion characteristics are retrieved. The findings show that for small RF platforms needing improved electromagnetic performance, the suggested miniaturized EBG unit cell offers an effective, affordable option.

title: Ensuring Multi-Microgrids Emergency Resilience and User-Side Demand via Vehicle-to-X: A Transfer Hierarchical Safe Reinforcement Learning
authors: Peixiao Fan, Runzhuo Ma, Siqi Bu, Song Ke, Yuxin Wen
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: Existing long-timescale dispatch strategies cannot handle instantaneous power shortages in microgrids, undermining resilience and user supply. Vehicle-to-everything (V2X) enables rapid energy and information exchange for emergency support, but its potential is limited by a lack of real-time scheduling and effective user participation. This paper proposes a V2X-based emergency resilience enhancement strategy for multi-microgrids using transfer hierarchical safe reinforcement learning (TH-SRL). First, key resilience metrics covering microgrid operation and user demand in the early post-event stage are defined, and a hierarchical scheduling framework for emergency response is established. The framework coordinates microgrids, distributed resources, and loads (including charging stations), embeds a user participation mechanism, and exploits V2X to orchestrate energy and information flows. TH-SRL employs transfer learning to handle heterogeneous electric vehicles, coordinates power control via the hierarchy, and enforces battery and grid safety constraints through safe reinforcement learning. Simulations show that TH-SRL improves multi-microgrid resilience while safeguarding user demand.

title: Joint Radio-Computing Resource Allocation Modeling for Next-Generation Networks
authors: Tammam Z. Dawood, Hanin N. Karhili, Mikhail S. Stepanov, Ghanshyam Singh, Artwell R. Muzata
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: Next-generation networks face the challenge of efficiently managing both radio and computational resources, especially under the demands of bandwidth-intensive services such as $\mathbf{4 K}$ video streaming. This paper presents a joint radio and computational resource allocation model for a single $\mathbf{5 G}$ access node equipped with Multi-access Edge Computing (MEC), serving homogeneous 4K-video streaming sessions. The model describes the interplay between finite radio and computing resources, deriving expressions for steady-state probabilities, blocking probability, and average resource utilization. A recursive algorithm is proposed to determine the minimal radio and computing resources required to meet a target quality of service (QoS). Numerical experiments illustrate the applicability of the model, showing how blocking probability varies with traffic load and resource availability. The results provide a practical framework for planning next-generation access nodes with joint resource constraints.

title: Multi-Regional Neighborhood Spatio-Temporal Dynamics for Crop Yield Prediction
authors: Meenakshi, Deepa Bura
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: For agricultural planning and food security, accurate crop yield prediction is essential, but it is still difficult because of intricate inter-district spatial dependencies and extremely erratic temporal variations caused by agroclimatic factors. The ability of current deep learning techniques to capture higher-order regional interactions and non-linear temporal behaviour present in agricultural systems is limited because they frequently rely on local neighbourhood assumptions for spatial modelling and smooth temporal evolution. A spatiotemporal framework for modelling multi-regional neighbourhood dynamics in district-level crop yield prediction is presented in this paper. The suggested method creates a series of annual spatial graphs to depict inter-district relationships that go beyond close neighbours, making it possible to examine more extensive regional influences. A multi-neighbor graph convolutional mechanism based on MixHop is used to learn multi-regional spatial representations by aggregating data from several neighbourhood ranges. A chaos-aware Long Short-Term Memory network is used to capture irregular and non-linear yield fluctuations resulting from climatic uncertainty in order to model temporal evolution. Superior predictive performance is achieved by explicitly modelling multi-regional spatial dependencies along with irregular temporal dynamics, according to experimental evaluation on district-level crop yield data. The suggested framework consistently outperforms cutting-edge baselines, achieving an RMSE of 0.103, a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> score of 0.790, and a correlation coefficient of 0.890. These findings demonstrate how crucial multi-regional spatiotemporal modelling is for reliable and accurate crop yield forecasting.

title: Federated Learning for Early Detection of Diabetic Retinopathy in Distributed Healthcare Systems
authors: S. Sathya, R. Anandha Lakshmi, R.Bagavathi Lakshmi, Vishwa Priya V, D. Narayani, N. Anandakrishnan
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is one of the leading causes of preventable vision loss in the world, and early detection is critical for an effective intervention. However, the sensitive nature of patient data, together with regulatory considerations, restricts the use of centralized model training of healthcare data across institutions. This study presents a federated learning (FL) framework, to train a deep convolutional neural network(CNN) (EfficientNet-B0), to provide remote, early detection of DR type using retinal fundus images collected from multiple clinics that are distributed geographically, without sharing un-identified raw health data. The performance of the FL training-and-test scheme was evaluated on a range of AUC, accuracy, sensitivity, and specificity against a central, local only, ensemble, and pre-trained model. It is found that the FL model achieved an AUC of 0.91, and accuracy of 88.3%. Federated learning is chosen for this study to ensure patient privacy to handle distributed non-IID data, and achieve near-centralized diagnostic accuracy. This work also explores the future research opportunities for federated learning, and suggests that federated learning represents an advanced, scalable and privacy-respecting avenue for the implementation of AI-supported diagnostic imaging tools in the healthcare distributed healthcare systems.

title: Label-Guided Imputation via Forest-Based Proximities for Improved Time Series Classification
authors: Jake S. Rhodes, Adam G. Rustad, Sofia Pelagalli Maia, Evan Thacker, Hyunmi Choi, Jose Gutierrez, Tatjana Rundek, Ben Shaw
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Missing data is a common problem in time series data. Most methods for imputation ignore label information pertaining to the time series even if that information exists. In this paper, we provide a framework for missing data imputation in the context of time series classification, where each time series is associated with a categorical label. We define a means of imputing missing values conditional upon labels, the method being guided by powerful, existing supervised models designed for high accuracy in this task. From each model, we extract a tree-based proximity measure from which imputation can be applied. We show that imputation using this method generally provides richer information leading to higher classification accuracies, despite the imputed values differing from the true values.

title: MADE-PI: Framework for Effective Anomaly Detection in Federated Learning Applications
authors: Raman Zatsarenko, Sergei Chuprov, Dmitrii Korobeinikov, Leon Reznik, Adrian Peña
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: In this research, we address a critical vulnerability of Federated Learning (FL) in practical applications: their susceptibility to adversarial attacks from malicious participants, which can severely degrade a global model’s performance. We propose MADE-PI, a novel defense mechanism designed to identify and exclude malicious clients early in the training process. Our method introduces a pre-aggregation step where the server evaluates each client update using a Proportional-Integral (PI) score. This score is a unique distance-based metric inspired by the PID controller, where a proportional term P measures the immediate deviation of a client’s update, and an integral term I tracks its behavior over time. By analyzing the distribution of these scores, our technique effectively flags and removes malicious contributions. We validate our approach through an empirical study on three datasets representing various applications of FL, including intelligent transportation, medical image analysis, and handwriting recognition. We concentrate on data poisoning as a highly relevant class of attacks. The results demonstrate that MADE-PI outperforms conventional defenses by providing superior malicious client detection and improving both learning efficiency and final model accuracy.

title: Comparing Neural Architectures for English-Spanish Machine Translation: From LSTM to Transformer
authors: Jingyuan Zhu, Anbang Chen, Bowen Wang, Sining Huang, Yukun Song, Yixiao Kang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: This paper presents a systematic comparison of neural architectures for English-to-Spanish machine translation. We implement and evaluate five model configurations ranging from vanilla LSTM encoder-decoders to Transformer models. Using the OPUS-100 corpus (1M training pairs) and FLORES+ benchmark (2,009 test pairs), we evaluate translation quality using BLEU, chrF, and COMET metrics. To ensure rigor, we employ a unified preprocessing pipeline with a shared BPE vocabulary across all models. Our best Transformer model achieves a BLEU score of 20.26, closing approximately 65% of the performance gap between our strongest LSTM baseline (BLEU 10.66) and the state-of-the-art Helsinki-NLP model (BLEU 26.60). We further analyze the impact of frozen pretrained FastText embeddings on recurrent architectures and provide a detailed breakdown of metric discrepancies.

title: Suicide Prediction Using Machine Learning on Assamese Language
authors: Manabjyoti Choudhury, Aniruddha Deka, Gunikhan Sonowal, Soraisam Gobinkumar Singh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Sentiment analysis in low-resource Indian languages remains a challenging research area due to limited annotated corpora, complex linguistic morphology and script-specific processing requirements. This study presents a vigorous machine learning channel for Assamese sentiment arrangement using a stratified TF-IDF feature representation combined with a stacking ensemble classifier. The proposed framework integrates Random Forest, XGBoost, Gradient Boosting and Decision Tree as base learners with meta-level soft voting to enhance generalization. Experiments were conducted on a manually curated three-class Assamese sentiment dataset and the model performance of 87.58 % of accuracy and 94.45 % of macro-AUC value in ensemble stacking. The projected method accomplishes good performance compared to existing deep learning and transformer-based models reported in recent Assamese and Indic sentiment literature, demonstrating that optimized ensemble learning remains a strong contender for resource-constrained languages where pretrained language models or large-scale datasets may not be accessible.

title: High Performance 3D Stacked Packaging of SiC Power Module Enabled by Direct Plated Copper Ceramic Interposer
authors: Ben Tian, Xinyuan Wang, Ruijie Liu, Deyi Chen, Yiqian Huang, Siyu Chen, Heping Yu, Quan Jiang, Zelian Lai, Yun Mou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: Aiming to solve high parasitic inductance, uneven thermal stress, and excessive junction temperature of power module, a novel 3D stacked packaging architecture incorporating a direct plated copper (DPC) ceramic interposer has been proposed. Attributed to high thermal conductivity and suitable CTE of DPC interposer, The DPC-based 3D stacked SiC power module demonstrates an internal interconnection parasitic inductance as low as 4.74 nH, a maximum thermal stress reduction of 28.14 MPa, and a junction temperature decrease of 49.03°C compared to conventional PCB-based 3D stacked packaging. Moreover, the maximum stress difference between high-side and low-side SiC MOSFET chips decreases by 79.5%, the stress imbalance between SiC diode chips is improved by 84.3%, while the junction temperature difference is minimized to only 7.42°C. The fabricated 3D stacked SiC power module also exhibits robust static and dynamic performances, confirming its suitability for high power density and high frequency applications.

title: Modeling and Stability Assessment of Grid Forming Converters in Islanded AC Microgrids
authors: Ravi Kumar Gaddala, Kaushik Rajashekara
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: In practical microgrid implementations, feeder impedance mismatches necessitate additional control mechanisms to achieve equal and proportional reactive power sharing among parallel converters. In addition, the literature review reveals the need for detailed model formulation and stability assessment of parallel grid-forming converters under practical deployment scenarios. To address this, droop control is employed for active and reactive power sharing, while virtual impedance and line impedance compensation loops are integrated to improve reactive power distribution. In this paper, a comprehensive small-signal state-space modeling for assessment of grid-forming converters in islanded AC microgrids is proposed. The proposed model includes droop control dynamics, virtual resistance and reactance loops, and line impedance compensation dynamics. Using the developed small-signal model, the influence of control and system parameters including active (mp) and reactive power droop coefficients (nq), virtual resistance (rv) and reactance (xv), line compensation filter cutoff frequencies (ωv), active and reactive proportional power-sharing coefficients, and filter inductances (Lf) on system stability is thoroughly analyzed. Based on Eigen-value analysis, the stability boundaries of critical control and system parameters are identified, providing valuable insights for the design of robust and stable control strategies, while simultaneously ensuring accurate equal and proportional sharing of both active and reactive power among parallel grid forming converters under practical impedance mismatch conditions. The proposed models and stability analysis are validated through the Typhoon hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) testbed.

title: MixWT-Net: A MixStyle and Wavelet-Guided Transformer Network for Generalized Glaucoma Classification
authors: Dipankar Das, Deepak Ranjan Nayak, Ram Bilas Pachori
publish: IEEE Sensors Letters
abstract: Early diagnosis of glaucoma is crucial to prevent permanent blindness. However, it remains challenging due to subtle structural changes in the lesion regions, overlapping features, and variation in imaging conditions. While convolutional neural networks (CNNs) achieve significant improvements for automated glaucoma screening through fundus images, they exhibit limited capability in extracting critical lesion features and correlations among them, leading to suboptimal generalization performance. To address these issues, in this paper, we propose a MixStyle and wavelet transform guided transformer network, MixWT-Net, for effective glaucoma classification. Specifically, we introduce a wavelet-based efficient self-attention module (W-ESAM) to capture relevant global contextual dependencies across the spatial and channel dimensions, thereby facilitating the capture of salient lesion cues. The W-ESAM leverages wavelet transform to obtain low-dimensional feature map while preserving contextual information and ensuring efficient attention computation. A MixStyle module is adopted to mix feature statistics and enhance adaptation and generalization ability. We perform extensive experiments on four benchmark datasets: LAG, Drishti-GS, REFUGE, and ORIGA, and the results demonstrate that our method achieves superior classification and generalization performance compared to other state-of-the-art approaches, making the MixWT-Net a method of choice for real-time glaucoma screening.

title: A PVDF-based Flexible Keyboard Device Exploiting the Piezoelectric Effect
authors: Rui Shao, Yichun Peng, Ba Qin, Tao Lu, Zhong Li, Weiwei Zhu, Weimin Xia, Shaobo Tan, Zhicheng Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Interconnected devices, characterized by intelligence, portability, and adaptability, have garnered substantial attention and are progressively being integrated into internet of things (IoT) technology to foster innovation and improve human-machine interaction interfaces. This study presents a polymer-based flexible keyboard that utilizes the piezoelectric effect of poly(vinylidene fluoride) (PVDF). The device is equipped with an array of flexible electrodes, with a thickness of merely 0.13 mm, enabling the press units to respond within 50 ms. Notably, it demonstrates exceptional flexibility and adhesiveness, allowing it to be attached to any desired surface without signal crosstalk. Moreover, the sensitivity and sensing performance of this keyboard device can be further enhanced through encapsulation techniques, rendering it highly suitable for low-frequency trigger scenarios such as device interaction.

title: Highly Dense Capacitor SRAM Computation-In-Memory With Dynamic Range Calibrated Column-by-Column ADCs
authors: Yong-Jun Jo, Chufeng Yang, Sen Cao, Dong-Hyun Yoon, Yuanjin Zheng, Tony Tae-Hyoung Kim
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: This paper presents a highly dense SRAM-based computation-in-memory (CIM) architecture designed for area-efficient AI acceleration. The proposed architecture leverages charge redistribution in a capacitor-based CIM design to enhance linearity, effectively mitigating the impact of variable parasitic capacitance inherent in FETs. To address the dynamic range mismatch between the CIM array and the ADC, a dynamic range calibration scheme is introduced. Recognizing the significant area overhead of ADCs in CIM, this work also proposes an area-efficient SR-latch-based cap-DAC driver. Furthermore, this work compares and analyzes various ADC comparator reference types, ultimately proposing individual reference columns with global drivers to achieve both ADC symmetry and improved area/power efficiency. Fabricated using a 65nm LP process, the prototype occupies a core area of 0.265 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, comprising a 108kb (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$432\times 256$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) SRAM-CIM array and 256 column-by-column ADCs with peripherals. This design achieves a weight density of 415.4 kb/mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. By employing column-by-column ADCs, which avoid the throughput limitations of ADC sharing, the prototype achieves an energy efficiency of 95.4 TOPS/W and a throughput of 813.6 GOPS. The resulting area and energy-efficient architecture achieves a SWaP (space, watt, and performance) figure-of-merit of 39.63 TOPS/W <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> Mb/mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>.

title: Full-Waveform-Accurate 3D Inversion for Transient Electromagnetics via Parallel Spectral Methods
authors: Yao Wang, Jianhui Li, Yinjia Zhu, Hongzhu Cai, Junjie Wu, Xingchun Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Conventional three-dimensional (3D) inversion of transient electromagnetic (TEM) data predominantly employs time-stepping forward modeling, which suffers from inherent limitations in parallelization efficiency and high computational costs. These drawbacks become particularly pronounced when incorporating full transmitter current waveforms, especially those involving waveform repetitions. To address these challenges, this study proposes a 3D inversion method based on spectral forward modeling. By employing MPI+OpenMP hybrid parallel programming technology, efficient parallel computation of magnetic field responses and Jacobian matrices along the frequency dimension is achieved. Additionally, a convolution algorithm is incorporated to integrate the effects of waveform repetition into the forward modeling of full-waveform transmitting current. Validation with synthetic data examples demonstrates that, compared to traditional time-stepping methods, the spectral method exhibits significant advantages in both forward modeling and inversion efficiency. The study also reveals that full-waveform inversion can more accurately reconstruct subsurface electrical structures, with magnetic field (B-field) data inversion showing markedly higher sensitivity to identifying good conductors in low-resistivity environments compared to induced electromotive force (EMF) data. Furthermore, the inclusion of full-waveform information not only improves inversion accuracy but also significantly enhances overall computational efficiency by accelerating residual convergence and reducing iteration counts. In practical applications, inversion results from exploration data in the Qing-chengzi ore concentration area, Liaoning Province, indicate that while the overall geoelectric structures derived from EMF and B-field data inversion are consistent, the B-field data provide more accurate delineation of the vertical extent of deep good conductors. These findings align closely with previous research, fully demonstrating the unique advantages of magnetic field data in deep exploration.

title: A 220 GHz Filtering Slot Array Antenna Based on Micro-Metal Additive Manufacturing
authors: Xianzhe Ma, Qinlong Li, Guanghua Shi, Sifan Wu, Cheng Guo, Zhen Wang, Zhou Chen, Xiaoming Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Terahertz Science and Technology
abstract: A 220 GHz slot array antenna with an integrated filtering response is proposed based on micro-metal additive manufacturing (M-MAM) technology. The bottom layer of the antenna structure is a seventh-order all-resonator 1-to-16 filtering power divider. This waveguide feed network can transfer energy to the cavity through coupling slots with the same amplitude and in phase. Then, each cavity excites a 2×2 radiation slot subarray at the top layer. The 2×2 slot subarray unit can be used to form a larger array, providing flexibility in the size of the array antenna. By using the proposed subarrays, an 8×8 slot array antenna is designed and fabricated in accordance with the fabrication process of M-MAM. The surface roughness using M-MAM is measured, and the effects of surface roughness on the feed network as well as array antenna have also been analyzed. Due to the low surface roughness and all metal structure, the array antenna achieves high gain, high aperture efficiency and good filtering response. The filtering array antenna using M-MAM technology is promising for sub-terahertz (sub-THz) applications.

title: Independent SAR System Calibration of Sentinel-1 C
authors: Patrick Klenk, Kersten Schmidt, Jakob Giez, Andrea Pulella, Matteo Nannini, Pau Prats-Iraola, Marco Schwerdt
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Sentinel-1 C is the third satellite of the Sentinel-1 mission. Launched in late 2024, it ensures seamless continuity of C-band SAR data for global monitoring of the Earth surface in the framework of the Copernicus program. In parallel to the commissioning of Sentinel-1 C by the European Space Agency (ESA), an independent verification of the system calibration has been performed by the German Aerospace Center (DLR) under an ESA contract. Based on an efficient calibration strategy, this paper describes the different activities performed and discusses in detail the results and main findings of DLR during the commissioning phase (CP) of Sentinel-1 C. The main goal is to provide a comprehensive compendium of the SAR calibration and performance status of S1C at the beginning of its operational mission: At the end of the commissioning phase we find that S1C achieves or even exceeds all SAR performance requirements put forth and is ready for its nominal mission operations.

title: Recovering Sewing Patterns from 3D Garment Surfaces via BoxMesh Bridging_supp1-3680930.mp4
authors: Xiaoguang Han
publish: N/A
abstract: Recovering sewing patterns from draped 3D garments is a challenging problem in human digitization research. Unlike draping from designed sewing pattern with mature physical simulation engines, the inverse process requires mapping complex garment surfaces back to parametric 2D patterns, which existing methods still struggle with. To this end, we propose a two-stage methodology that recovers sewing patterns from 3D garments via an intermediate representation, BoxMesh, which inherently encodes garment-level geometry and panel-level details in 3D space. In Stage I, a geometry-oriented auto-regressive model predicts BoxMesh from the input 3D garment. In Stage II, a semantic-aware auto-regressive model parses BoxMesh into parametric sewing patterns. This decomposition separately tackles geometric inversion and numerical reasoning, enabling more accurate recovery. Experiments have demonstrated that our method not only achieves state-of-the-art performance on the GarmentCodeData benchmark but also can be applied effectively to real scans and single-view images.

title: Corrigendum: Attribute Filtering in Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search: An In-depth Experimental Study: [Experiments & Analysis]
authors: Mocheng Li, Xiao Yan, Baotong Lu, Yue Zhang, James Cheng, Chenhao Ma
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
abstract: None

title: On Efficient Approximate Aggregate Nearest Neighbor Queries over Learned Representations
authors: Carrie Wang, Sihem Amer-Yahia, Laks V.S. Lakshmanan, Reynold Cheng
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
abstract: None

title: A New Federated Learning Approach for Imbalanced Medical Image Datasets
authors: Aniruddha Tiwari, Zhen Ni, Xiangnan Zhong, Ahmed Imteaj
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Medical image classification plays a vital role in disease diagnosis, but often suffers from class imbalance and privacy concerns, particularly in rare disease categories. Such an imbalance can bias machine learning models toward majority classes, reducing the diagnostic accuracy for minority conditions across geographically diverse populations. To address these challenges, we propose integrating the synthetic minority oversampling technique (SMOTE) into the federated learning (FL) framework for 2D medical image analysis, enabling privacy-preserving and balanced learning. The SMOTE technique enhances neural networks’ generalization by generating informative synthetic samples, outperforming raw duplication, and reducing overfitting. In our approach, by operating on features obtained from deep models like VGG19, we avoid pixel-level interpolation artifacts and retain critical semantic information. The SMOTE technique is applied to the extracted features at the local client level, where it generates new samples for the minority class by interpolating between existing samples to achieve the local data balance. The proposed approach allows each client to balance its local data without compromising privacy, promoting fair learning across all classes in disease classification tasks. We evaluate our approach on two publicly available chest X-ray datasets for binary and multi-class classifications. Our method achieves 83% accuracy and 97% recall in binary classification and 50% accuracy in multiclass settings, highlighting its potential to contribute to diagnostic fairness and robustness in healthcare applications.

title: Comparative Analysis using Power-Aware Design of Traditional and Low-Power ALUs
authors: Sai Mani Deepika S, Asritha Venkata Naga Hasini B, Saikeerthi Abburi, Swaminadhan Rajula, Kiranmai P
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: This work presents a comparative study between a traditional Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU) and a low-power ALU implementing operand isolation, approximate arithmetic, and power gating. Both designs were implemented in Verilog HDL and synthesized using Vivado 2023.1 targeting a Xilinx FPGA. SAIF-driven post place-and-route analysis shows that the proposed low-power ALU achieves 38.46% reduction in slice LUTs, 41.67% reduction in slices, and 58.01% reduction in total on-chip power compared to the traditional design. Dynamic power reduces by 58.42%, and junction temperature drops by 23.5 °C, indicating significant improvement in thermal efficiency. The design achieves these gains without compromising functional correctness for all tested operations, making it suitable for energy-sensitive digital systems.

title: Classification and Detection of Lung Cancer Using VGG-DeepCNN with GRAD-CAM Interpretability
authors: Priyanka Rawat, Shilpi Harnal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: To identify and categorize lung cancer from CT scans into four classes, this research introduced the VGG-DCNN classification model. Loss functions weighted for classes have been utilized for handling class imbalance, and the model was trained using early stopping and adaptive learning rate adjustments. The proposed model employs a VGG as an extractor of features composed of 5 convolutional blocks to learn hierarchical features, and it has a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$224 \times 224$</tex> RGB image as an input. There are several convolutional layers in each block to extract image patterns. For efficient dimensionality reduction, a GAP layer transforms the last feature maps into a small 512-dimensional vector after feature extraction. The fourth dense layer of the fully connected layers of the classification head-Dense, Batch Normalization, Dropout Dense, and Dropout and Dense layer-outputs probabilities for four output classes. With a classification accuracy of 97 % and an AUC-ROC score of 100 %, our model showed exceptional ability to classify between cancer subtypes and normal lung tissue. Besides, Grad-CAM has been applied to provide visual interpretability, ensuring that the model appropriately targeted tumor areas for every type of cancer.

title: Progressive Information Flow Non-Linear Dark Image Restoration Method
authors: Shatakshi Saxena, Shivam Taneja, Kriti Singhal, Arnav Sen, Naman Chaudhary, Kartik Koul, Taanisha Jain, Suresh Raikwar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Dark image restoration presents a significant challenge due to its inherently ill-posed nature. Existing methods often yield results with insufficient brightness and loss of fine details, limiting their practical applicability. In response, we introduce a novel framework designed to enhance brightness while preserving critical image information. This is achieved through a progressive non-linear flow of information that maintains finegrained details and produces perceptually brighter outcomes compared to existing state-of-the-art techniques. The effectiveness of the proposed approach is validated through comprehensive evaluation on benchmark datasets.

title: Energy-Efficient Machine Learning Models for Real-Time Anomaly Detection in IoT Sensor Networks
authors: K Sathish
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: ML models used to detect anomalies in IoT sensor networks in real-time require more energy-efficient methods as such networks have seen full deployment and reduced power availability. In this study, a combination of methods of more sophisticated pre-processing combined with a Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) model and optimized using Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) was also proposed. An evaluation was done based on the popular and commonly known Numenta Anomaly Benchmark (NAB) dataset. Findings show that likelihood of accuracy, F1-score, recall and precision of the GRU+PSO model was 92%, 0.93, 0.92 and 0.94 respectively compared to the conventional models such as Random Forest (84%), K-Means (83%) that tend to perform poorly. The suggested framework was also highly energy-efficient with no more than 30% of the reduced energy consumption and 35 percent less data redundancy when compared to conventional approaches. This means that the optimized deep learning method is scalable and feasible to use to increase the level of IoT security and reliability and reduce resource utilization. Finally, the study provides strong and interpretable large-scale real-time anomaly detection in IoT sensor networks.

title: Evaluation of Deformable Medical Image Registration Techniques for Improved Analysis
authors: Ashish Singh Ramola, Prabhjot Kaur
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Deformable Medical Image Registration (DMIR) is a fundamental process for aligning medical images through nonrigid transformations. It plays a crucial role in applications such as multi-modal image fusion, longitudinal studies, tumor tracking, and image-guided interventions. This study presents a comprehensive comparative evaluation of six learning-based DMIR techniques: a generic convolutional neural network (CNN)-based registration pipeline, VoxelMorph, Multilevel Transformation Progressive Registration (MTPR), Fast Deformable Registration Network (FDRN), Affine and Deformable Medical Image Registration (ADMIR), and XMorpher. All methods were evaluated on Magnetic Resonance (MR) image slices from the OASIS benchmark dataset. Performance is assessed using Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Dice Coefficient (DC), Normalized CrossCorrelation (NCC), Mean Squared Error (MSE), and computational time. Experimental results demonstrate that ADMIR achieves the highest Dice score (0.9854), indicating superior structural alignment, while MTPR excels in intensity preservation and pixel-level accuracy, achieving the highest NCC (0.9524) and the lowest MSE (0.003557) and MAE (0.03123). Overall, the findings highlight trade-offs between accuracy and efficiency, suggesting ADMIR for precision-critical tasks, MTPR for intensity-sensitive alignment, and VoxelMorph for large-scale or real-time clinical workflows.

title: Performance Analysis of a Race Reduced Data Flip Flop Under Wide Thermal Variations for Future High-Speed Counters and Prescalers
authors: Shilagani Shivaprasad, Daddanala Nagavarun, Janamanchi Rathna Koushal, J Ajayan, Ponnaboina Charan Sai, MD Badhesha
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The minimal building blocks of digital integrated circuits are flip-flops (FFs) although ensuring they are highly effective (as measured by performance), low power, and extremely reliable is a major challenge. The results of this paper consider the validity of high-speed, dynamic, D-flip-flop (DFF), which is another model of a flip-flop called the Race-Reduced Flip-Flop (RRFF). RRFF design assumes a master-slave kind of arrangement with dynamic pre-charge-evaluate logic to enhance speed with fixed keeper latches to enhance robustness and race conditions. The circuit was also tested in terms of its performance and reliability by simulation at the 16 nm technology node. Performance factors involved assessment of the transient characteristics of the circuit, Fault (Short Circuit) power, total power, delay, and the switching energy (PDP) and determination of the Delay-Power Ratio (DPR) at the range of temperatures 27° C and 107° C and intervals of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.7 ~\mathrm{V}, 0.8 ~\mathrm{V}$</tex> and 0.9 V. The super-threshold performance of the circuit was established to be constant at 0.7 V and 0.8 V when subjected to the transient performance, as well as the conclusion was made that movement at 0.6 V was not consistent. The most alarming was that the propulsion delay is also increasing 0.395%, as the ambient temperature increases, more unexpectedly, the overall power dissipated decreases 14.19% at 0.7 V which previously was believed to be the most energy consuming. The temperature rise with this prompted an increase in temperature- power product (PDP) by 13.88% at 0.7 V. The motivation was mainly the Reduction of SC power 35.3% at 0.7 V within the temperature route examined. 0.7 V was found within the analysis as the most energy effective operating voltage and accurately represented the thermal power conventional and operational dependability.

title: Dynamic Sign Language Identification Using Mediapipe Landmarks and Random Forest Classifier
authors: Shrivathsa TV, Amritha UR, Panchami Pai, Vishrutha, Ananya Amin
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Communication is an important tool for effective information exchange over people and it ensures messages are clear and as intended, which fosters relationships. Communication between hearing and non-hearing individuals remains limited due to the lack of efficient a utomated sign language recognition systems. The paper outlines a machine learning framework for real-time gesture and sign language detection based on custom dataset. The dataset used includes all 26 letters of the American Sign Language alphabet, each with around 300 samples for training, testing, and validation of the classifiers. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$O$</tex> penCV and MediaPipe were used for annotation and accurate landmark feature extraction from the frames captured through the camera interface. These features are then used by a Random Forest Classifier to detect the gestures correctly. The combined approach of random forest along with the MediaPipe model provides a promising precision in the identification o fg estures and, in combination with OpenCV provides robustness in dynamic identification o ver a set of variations in gestures and conditions.

title: A Compact Frequency-Reconfigurable Monopole Antenna Using PIN-Diode-Controlled Parasitic Stubs for IoT Applications
authors: Anvesh Pandey, Arun Kumar Saurabh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This paper presents a frequency-reconfigurable compact monopole antenna designed for Internet of Things (IoT) and next-generation communication systems. The antenna is exceptionally low-profile <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.01 \lambda \mathrm{g})$</tex>, with a compact footprint of 16 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\times 20 \text{mm}^{2}\left(0.21 \lambda_{\mathrm{g}} \times 0.26 \lambda_{\mathrm{g}}\right)$</tex>, where <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\lambda_{\mathrm{g}}$</tex> corresponds to the guided wavelength at 3.28 GHz. The antenna integrates a PIN diode that connects a semi-circular monopole radiator to precisely tuned arc-shaped parasitic stubs, enabling multiple resonant modes and wide multi-band operation through an integrated biasing network. Full-wave simulations validate a reflection coefficient <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\left\vert\mathbf{S}_{\mathbf{1 1}}\right\vert)$</tex> below −10 dB across multiple frequency bands: 3.18-3.37 GHz, 5.65-5.90 GHz, 6.387-7.78 GHz, and 8.88-11.48 GHz. The antenna maintains stable omnidirectional radiation characteristics and achieves a total radiation efficiency of up to 87.4% across its operating frequency range. The compact form factor, frequency reconfigurability, and stable radiation performance of the proposed antenna enables its use in IoT, 5G NR, and emerging wireless applications.

title: Spatio-Temporal Registration Method for Sea Surface Infrared and X-Band Radar Image
authors: Qun Yang, Haipeng Yu, Xiaoliang Chu, Dabo Guo, Guang Yuan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Radar systems and infrared cameras are commonly employed to observe nearshore ocean surface phenomena. Owing to their distinct viewing perspectives and spatial resolutions, the images acquired by these two sensors are typically analyzed independently. However, joint observations can provide more comprehensive information about sea-surface dynamics. In this study, radar–infrared image registration is achieved through combined spatial and temporal alignment. Radar data are transformed into images from the infrared camera perspective and associated with infrared imagery to accomplish spatial alignment, while temporal alignment is performed using timestamps recorded in the data files. The effectiveness of the proposed approach is validated using field data collected in a nearshore environment.

title: PLER: A Novel Full-Reference Metric for Evaluating the Geometric Fidelity of 3D Assets
authors: Gleb A. Voronkov, Andrei V. Balobanov
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: This paper presents an enhancement of the previously proposed PLER (Peak Length to Error Ratio) metric for objective quality assessment of 3D models. The original PLER metric, which compares a reference and test 3D model by analyzing the distances of rays cast from a bounding sphere, suffered from non-uniform ray distribution. To overcome this limitation, a comprehensive study of methods for generating uniformly distributed points on a sphere was conducted. The Fibonacci (golden) spiral method was identified as optimal, leading to the development of PLER-2.0. The paper details the comparative analysis of point distribution methods, presents experimental results demonstrating the superiority of the Fibonacci spiral, and validates the improved metric on test 3D models, showing increased accuracy and stability.

title: A Novel Transformer-CNN Hybrid Framework for Early Alzheimer’s Diagnosis using Multimodal MRI and PET
authors: Sowgandh Krishnaa Nandamuri, V.D.Ambethkumar
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Early proper diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease remains one of the biggest challenges in medical imaging. This study introduces a new hybrid neural network that blends Transformer architecture with Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) to effectively integrate multimodal MRI and PET brain images. The model leverages the power of CNN's capturing local spatial information and the Transformer's ability to capture long-range dependencies, allowing for better fusion of structural and functional information. Established tests Alzheimer's imaging findings reveal that the hybrid method that significantly enhances feature representation and classification accuracy relative to benchmark single-modality and unimodal models. The results show that the hybrid architecture offers a promising avenue to the production computerized, preclinical diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease.

title: Interpretable Transfer Learning System for Multi-Class Lung Cancer Identification Using Enhanced CT Imaging
authors: Savitha N J, Uday Raj, Sathvik T Soged, Sandeep Jadhav
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Lung cancer continues to be the leading cause of cancer mortality worldwide, making early identification crucial for improving patient survival. We introduce a transparent deep learning system for categorizing lung cancer into multiple classes using CT scan imagery. Our approach employs transfer learning with ResNet-50 as its foundation, strengthened through extensive data enhancement and Grad-CAM visualization for improved model transparency. The system utilizes the publicly accessible IQ-OTHNCCD dataset containing three distinct categories: benign conditions, malignant tumors, and healthy lung scans. Our methodology achieves 92.8% validation accuracy, with precision at 91.5%, recall of 90.9%, and F1-score reaching <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 1. 2 \%}$</tex>, demonstrating superior results compared to standard models. The inclusion of explainable AI methods offers clinical transparency, positioning this approach as suitable for computerassisted diagnosis in pulmonary radiology.

title: DefenSys: An Integrated Platform for Malware Detection and Containerized Attack Simulation using Deep Learning
authors: Elvis Vincent, Prabu Jayant, Mohana, Abhilash Chakkan
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The increased growth of cyber threats, with attacks happening every 11 seconds and global losses exceeding $6 trillion annually, creates a lot of challenges for enterprise security. Standard cybersecurity solutions remain costly and very complex for small enterprises, creating substantial security gaps. This paper presents DefenSys, a comprehensive cyber defense platform integrating intelligent malware detection with dynamic network attack simulation to help increase cybersecurity across organizations of all scales. DefenSys employs a containerized Docker architecture for attack simulation environments, enabling secure execution of real-world cyberattacks including DDoS attacks using Apache Benchmarking tools. The malware detection service uses deep learning techniques using Residual Neural Networks and Convolutional Neural Networks for binary classification. Malware files undergo binary-to-grayscale image conversion, achieving 98.7% binary classification accuracy and 76% multi-class classification accuracy across 25 malware categories including worms, trojans, ransomware, and botnets, with macro-averaged F1-score of 0.76 and weighted F1-score of 0.71. The system architecture incorporates Flask-based web services with Apache HTTP Server for maintaining reduced deployment overhead. Experimental testing demonstrates DefenSys's performance in real life threat simulation and detection with reduced computational resource utilization while also maintaining high accuracy and faster response capabilities through the dual stage classification approach. DefenSys addresses the cybersecurity gap by providing business level protection through a cost effective and intuitive platform that reduces technical barriers for small organizations with little to no cybersecurity expertise.

title: Joint Power and Subcarrier Allocation for SCMA-Assisted ISAC Systems
authors: Zhuoshu Li
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Sparse code multiple access (SCMA), with its flexible non-orthogonal structure, is a promising candidate for integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) systems. In this paper, we study the joint power and subcarrier allocation in SCMA-assisted ISAC downlink systems, aiming to maximize the sum rate subject to sensing accuracy, user quality-of-service (QoS) constraints, and a total power budget. To tackle the nonconvex and combinatorial problem, a decoupled framework is developed. Specifically, the power allocation subproblem is handled via successive convex approximation (SCA) for a given subcarrier assignment, while dedicated subcarrier allocation strategies are developed for the fully-loaded and under-loaded scenarios, respectively. Numerical results show that the proposed scheme consistently outperforms orthogonal frequency-division multiple access (OFDMA) and random subcarrier allocation, with clearer performance gains observed in under-loaded systems.

title: A Machine Learning-Based Traffic Prediction Framework for Smart Cities
authors: Chetan Ghadi, Akanksha Hake, Sara Khare, Harshil Kanakia, Renuka Pawar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The rapid expansion of urban transportation systems has intensified the need for intelligent traffic prediction models that can operate reliably under dynamic real-world conditions. Traditional rules-based approaches often fail to account for fluctuating factors, such as weather variations, public events, infrastructure constraints, and transit disruptions, resulting in limited forecast accuracy and delayed decision-making. This research presents a machine learning–based traffic prediction framework that leverages historical traffic weather data along with real-time environmental inputs obtained from the OpenWeather API. A Random Forest regression model is trained to estimate vehicle volume and is improved through contextual modifiers, including road construction, transit outages, and event-driven surges. The system further evaluates congestion severity, estimates effective road capacity, and generates risk assessments to support proactive decision-making. Additionally, it provides actionable recommendations for traffic authorities and police departments through automated alerts and deployment strategies. The proposed framework is modular, scalable, and designed for real-time operational use, contributing towards safer, smarter, and more sustainable traffic management in modern urban environments.

title: Sustainable Hydrogen Through Biological Systems: Current Trends in Production and Storage Solutions
authors: Sarbani Ganguly, Dhananjay Kr. Tripathi, Kamalika Banerjee, Sreyasree Basu, Indrajit Bhattacharjee, Mainak Debnath
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)
abstract: Hydrogen is becoming popular globally as a clean energy source. Green hydrogen from renewable-powered electrolysis considered as the most sustainable option though it faces challenges of high energy demand, water purity requirements, and cost. Biomass-based biological routes particularly dark fermentation offer a sustainable for converting agro-waste biomass into hydrogen under mild conditions with lower energy input. Pretreatment of lignocellulosic residues like rice straw, bagasse, and corn straw is essential to enhance microbial accessibility. Hybrid approaches where dark fermentation techniques are combined with either photo fermentation, microbial electrolysis, or biorefinery platforms ensured improve hydrogen yields, reduce carbon footprints and generate valuable co-products. For large-scale adoption, effective hydrogen storage is essential. Developments in nanostructured metal hydrides, liquid organic hydrogen carriers, and porous nanomaterials are effectively addressing the limitations associated with physical and chemical storage.

title: Intelligent Vehicle Damage Inspection: Detection, Multi-View Reasoning, and VLM-Based Report Generation
authors: Andrei Sergeev, Sergei Gaponenko, Ksenia Polyantseva
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: This paper presents a general framework for automated vehicle handover inspection based on multi-view image analysis and a staged machine-learning pipeline. The system concept combines a backend layer for request orchestration and lifecycle control, a dedicated inference service for vehicle-presence validation and initial damage detection, and a VLM component for context-aware defect interpretation with structured assessment output. The driver uses a mobile application to create the report by initializing a request, capturing four mandatory views, and submitting the images for processing. The architecture is designed for practical deployment and supports decision traceability, reprocessing workflows, and operator-assisted final verification. The main contribution is the formalization of an end-to-end pipeline from image upload to final resolution with a unified JSON representation suitable for auditing and integration into fleet-management operations. Target system properties include accuracy, interpretability, scalability, and acceptable response time, establishing a basis for subsequent comparative evaluation and further development.

title: Multimodal Visible - Infrared Fusion for Robust Human Action Recognition
authors: Aditya Balodi, Manoj Diwakar, Neeraj Kumar Pandey, Aditya Joshi, Sanjay Roka, Prabhishek Singh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Human activity recognition that is truly robust to a wide variety of environments will have to depend on the effective use of the different modalities in a complementary way. On one hand, visible-spectrum imagery gives texture and appearance details; on the other hand, infrared imaging provides motion cues that are not dependent on lighting, while 3D skeleton data captures the movement of the human body. But when these modalities are individually modelled, the performance under difficult conditions is usually low. To overcome these problems, the paper at hand introduces a threefusion framework capable of bringing together RGB, infrared (IR), and 3D skeleton modalities for human activity recognition in one seamless flow. The architecture involves a Star Transformer, which captures long-range spatiotemporal dependencies in RGB video sequences, a Swin Transformer, which captures hierarchical local-to-global representations from IR frames, and a Spatial-Temporal Graph Convolutional Network (ST-GCN), which learns structured joint-level dynamics from skeleton sequences. First, the features that belong to each modality are obtained separately and then at the level of representation, they are fused to keep the characteristics that are discriminative while making the cross-modal integration complementary. The experimental results show that the proposed three-fusion strategy achieves better accuracy and is also more robust and reliable under varying environmental conditions, hence it consistently outperforms unimodal and bimodal baselines.

title: Enhancing Decision-Making Strategies in Autonomous Vehicles Under Challenging Conditions
authors: Ramu K, C. Sivakumar, Priyadharshini J, K Mohammed Ameen Sharrif, Archana Shakya
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Decision-making under challenging road conditions remains a primary barrier to safe autonomous vehicle deployment. Existing studies often relied on simulationonly evaluation, lacked geographic diversity, or omitted formal statistical validation. This work proposed a hybrid decisionmaking framework that combined a rule-based safety supervisory layer with a multi-agent reinforcement learning policy and a post-hoc explainability module. Training used extensive CARLA episodes with domain randomization; evaluation included two independent Indian real-world datasets and decision-level replay validation. On held-out CARLA tests the approach achieved a collision rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3. 0 \%} (\pm \mathbf{1. 0 \%})$</tex> and a success rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 3. 0 \%} \boldsymbol{(} \boldsymbol{\pm} \mathbf{1. 5 \%} \boldsymbol{)}$</tex>, yielding a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 \%}$</tex> relative reduction in collisions versus a pure MARL baseline <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(6.0 \% \pm 1.8 \%, p$</tex> = 0.003, paired test). Cross-dataset testing produced collision rates of 6.0% (India A) and 8.0% (India B), both significantly higher than CARLA (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}<0.05$</tex>), highlighting residual sim-to-real gaps. Contributions comprised the hybrid architecture, cross-domain evaluation with Indian road data, formal statistical analysis, and real-world decision-level validation with ablation studies.

title: Physics-Informed Smart Maintenance for Offshore Wind Power: Prospects and Research Directions*
authors: Xinxing Wang, Jiahao Chen, Qianwei Zhang, Jiewu Leng
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: The transition of offshore wind to deep-water environments requires advanced O&M strategies to manage coupled loads. Harsh marine conditions and data scarcity limit current reliability management. While TinyML supports monitoring, traditional models still face latency and interpretability challenges. This paper reviews Physics-Informed Smart Maintenance and proposes four future research prospects: edge-native intelligence for real-time simulation, physics-consistent data fusion, generative intelligence to address data scarcity, and cognitive decision-making enabled by Large Language Models (LLMs). These prospects support a resilient, autonomous ecosystem for next-generation wind turbines.

title: Research for Garbage Classification and Detection Based on HWM-YOLO
authors: Xu Gao, Chen Dong, Hanyang He, Xiaozhuo Bi, Huiyanhua Dai
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: For four common types of garbage, namely metal, cardboard, glass, and plastic, existing garbage classification and detection models had suffered from garbage missed detections in complex scenes, insufficient bounding box regression accuracy, and poor adaptability. To address these challenges, this paper proposed an HWM-YOLO garbage classification and detection model. Built upon YOLOv11, the proposed model replaced the original PANet-style neck with a dynamically cross-scale fused Hyper Neck module, which effectively reduced garbage missed detections of small-scale and occluded garbage objects in complex environments. Moreover, a WIoUv3 loss function incorporating a dynamic non-monotonic focusing mechanism was introduced to handle stacked and irregularly shaped garbage, which suppressed gradient anomalies caused by low-quality samples during bounding box regression and significantly improved regression accuracy in complex scenes. In addition, a Mixed Aggregation Network (MANet) that integrates three convolutional variants was employed to enhance the representation capability for garbage objects with diverse shapes, thereby improving the model’s adaptability to complex scenarios. Experimental results on a public dataset demonstrated that the proposed model had achieved a precision of 90.8%, a recall of 87.3%, an mAP50 of 91.7%, and an mAP50–95 of 76.6%, providing a robust solution for garbage classification and detection in complex scenes.

title: Implementation of Smart Doorbell for Hearing Impaired Using FPGA, ModelSim and MATLAB
authors: Chejarla Venkata Surya Sai Anish, Rajesh Kannan Megalingam
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Hearing-impaired individuals often encounter significant difficulties responding to conventional audio doorbell notifications, which may result in missed visitors and a higher security risk. Current solutions either are not reliable or do not support multi-modal feedback for varying environmental conditions. To counter these challenges, we propose a smart doorbell system particularly for the hearing-impaired individuals. The system incorporates a push-button interface, a knock sensor, and a microphone sensor to identify visitor presence by physical contact or voice. A camera module offers real-time video surveillance of the entrance zone, and an LDR controls LED brightness in relation to ambient lighting to maintain visibility. A vibration motor offers haptic feedback, and visual signals are presented by adaptive LEDs, which are controlled by LDR. An FPGA acts as the core control unit, allowing for effective realtime processing. For added reliability, FIR filtering is used to remove environmental noise from the knock and microphone sensors so that they trigger exactly, mitigating fake alerts. The system provides double feedback-visual and tactile, assuring the users are immediately alerted to a visitor's presence. Future development will concentrate on integrating image recognition techniques with mobile app development to alert the user about the visitors using the live feed from the camera module.

title: Impact of AI Integration on Downsizing Intentions in Entrepreneurial Enterprises
authors: Smita Deshpande, Vimal Bhatt
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
