abstract: The current article seeks to explore the relationship between: 1) entrepreneurial digital literacy / learning (EDL), 2) usage of AI (UAI), 3) entrepreneurial mindset (EM), 4) entrepreneurial strategic intent (ESI), 5) competitive psychology (CP) and 6) entrepreneurial attitude (EA). The impact of these constructs on downsizing intention (DSI) is studied. DSI is a concept that is frequently used when there are large layoffs of employees in an organisation. While analysing the literature, it was found that there exists a research gap in exploring the impact of DSI in AI-driven enterprises. This relationship established among the six independent variables and the dependent variable is well-knitted with the help of two intents of the theory of planned behaviour (TPB). To conduct this empirical study, a questionnaire was sent to those entrepreneurs who have adopted AI techniques in their enterprises. The data collected was cleaned and a regression analysis was run in Excel. The results revealed that there is significant variance in EA as explained by the five independent variables (EDL, UAI, EM, ESI and CP). Further, the second regression analysis revealed that there is significant variance in DSI too as explained by two variables (EA and SN). The study can be used to frame a favourable yet knowledge-provoking syllabus for the new generation entrepreneurs, and help the AIenabled entrepreneurs to brainstorm remedial ideas to reduce the downsizing intentions in their respective enterprises. This study is an original work to add value to the new generation and existing entrepreneurs who have adopted AI technology in either way.

title: EEG Signal Analysis for Epileptic Seizure Detection using Principal Component Analysis and Gradient Boosting
authors: C. Gethara Gowri, M Amanullah, G. Kavitha, Lakshminarayanan
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: In order to diagnose and treat epilepsy, a neurological disorder characterized by abnormal brain activity, it is crucial to first identify epileptic episodes. Because of their high dimensionality and noise, EEG data can provide valuable information on brain electrical patterns, but accurate classification is challenging. Using Principal Component Analysis (PCA) for dimensionality reduction and Gradient Boosting for classification, this research proposes an efficient paradigm. After preprocessing EEG recordings to remove artifacts, both statistical and spectral properties were recovered. PCA preserved the most informative aspects while lowering computational complexity. This was followed by training the Gradient Boosting Classifier to distinguish between seizure-free and non-seizures. Experimental findings show that the proposed method achieves 93.8% accuracy, 92.5% precision, 94.1% recall, and a 93.3% F1-score. exceeding several baseline models. The method’s exceptional robustness makes it a feasible choice for real-time seizure monitoring systems.

title: Intelligent Medical Decision Support System Using IoT and CNN-LSTM-Based Predictive Modeling
authors: Ravinder L C, Mohammed Ali Shaik
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The emergence of illness, especially severe illnesses like chronic kidney disease (CKD) and heart disease (HD), is precisely forecasted, but this is a formidable and frequently unattainable endeavor. This paper presents a hybrid deep learning architecture that combines long short-term memory (LSTM) networks with convolutional neural networks (CNN) to enhance feature extraction and temporal pattern recognition, thereby improving diagnostic accuracy. We evaluate the proposed model against established benchmarks, including DLMNN, EO-LWAMCNet, and XGBoost Bi-LSTM, using HD and CKD datasets. The CNN+LSTM model outperforms the most advanced approaches across various metrics, including precision, recall, accuracy, and F1-score. It achieves maximum accuracy values of 95% for CKD and 94.5% for HD, surpassing the established diagnostic benchmarks. The model is consistent with a wide range of dataset characteristics, making it suitable for implementation in real clinical decision support systems.

title: Intelligent Hybrid Deep Learning System for Automated Retinal Disease Diagnosis
authors: D. Chandra Prakash, Yerram Sneha, Nayani Sateesh, Bathula Balnarsaiah, Ashok Kumar Vootla, Kokkula Shiva Prasad
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is a main cause of acquired blindness and a leading cause of preventable blindness in the world. Individual detection of DR on the embodiment of a retina is critical to timely intervention and save sight. This proposed project will utilize a Hybrid Deep Learning Model (resnet50 plus vision transformer (ViT)) with the aim of discriminating Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) and Non-Diabetic (Normal) cases. The ResNet50 element is efficient in obtaining local retinal specific characteristics including microaneurysms and hemorrhages, whereas the ViT element assesses overall structural details of the retina which allows full image insights. The combination of these two feature extractors promotes the discriminatory capability and strength of the model. The proposed model performed at 99.49% overall classification accuracy using the given dataset of more than 500 well-crafted images per class with the best precision, recall, and F1-score. The system is convenient to the clinical screening and tele-ophthalmology uses because of a Flask-based web interface that permits real-time image upload and instant disease detection.

title: Temperature Dynamic Analysis of PEMFC Characteristics and Its Implications for MPPT
authors: Nageswarappa N, V. Naga Bhaskar Reddy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This paper presents a novel voltage temperature feedforward MPPT method for Proton Exchange Membrane Fuel Cell (PEMFC). First, the performance of a PEMFC was investigated under dynamic operating conditions. Based on this analysis, we analysed the impact of temperature variations on the voltage-current and power-current characteristics. Sensitivity analysis <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\frac{\partial P_{M P P}}{\partial T}, \frac{\partial V_{M P P}}{\partial T}\right)$</tex> of PEMFC with dynamic temperature shows shifts in the maximum power point (MPP). This sensitivity analysis was used to design the proposed MPPT. The proposed method effectively determines the value of the voltage (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{V}_{\text{mpp}}$</tex>) at the MPP with varying temperatures.

title: Active Authentication via Korean Keystrokes Under Varying LLM Assistance and Cognitive Contexts
authors: Dong Hyun Roh, Rajesh Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Keystroke dynamics is a promising modality for active user authentication, but its effectiveness under varying LLM-assisted typing and cognitive conditions remains understudied. Using data from 50 users and cognitive labels from Bloom’s Taxonomy, we evaluate keystroke-based authentication in Korean across three realistic typing scenarios: bona fide composition, LLM content paraphrasing, and transcription. Our pipeline incorporates continuity-aware segmentation, feature extraction, and classification via SVM, MLP, and XGB. Results show that the system maintains reliable performance across varying LLM usages and cognitive contexts, with Equal Error Rates ranging from 5.1% to 10.4%. These findings demonstrate the feasibility of behavioral authentication under modern writing conditions and offer insights into designing more context-resilient models.

title: PEPS: Quantum-Inspired Reinforcement Learning for Coherent Reasoning Traces in LLMs
authors: Venkat Margapuri, Garik Kazanjian, Naren Kosaraju
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) often struggle with maintaining coherent multi-step reasoning traces, particularly in tasks that require a structured logical flow. This work introduces a quantum-inspired approach to address the challenge by incorporating a fidelity-based reward derived from Projected Entangled Pair States (PEPS) into Proximal Policy Optimization. Unlike prior approaches that use direct supervision or contrastive objectives, the proposed method guides learning through structural consistency, offering a novel approach to enforce global coherence in generated reasoning traces. The proposed framework is evaluated using multiple coherence-determining metrics on diverse datasets such as GSM8K, StrategyQA, and EntailmentBank spanning arithmetic, intuitive, and entailment-based reasoning. Results show that the proposed quantum-inspired approach offers significant improvements over supervised, contrastive, and pretrained baseline approaches, highlighting the effectiveness of quantum-inspired fidelity as a foundation to improve reasoning trace coherence in LLMs.

title: A Generalizable Energy Management System for Quantified Thermal Virtual Energy Storage Control
authors: Peidong Li, Yue Cao
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: The thermal mass of a building offers a significant resource for virtual energy storage (VES). However, its grid integration is hindered by the lack of generalizable modeling and grid-compatible control metrics. This paper proposes a comprehensive energy management framework to address these limitations. The framework introduces a generalizable, adaptive gray-box model for building thermal mass using equivalent thermal parameters (ETP) which employs an unscented Kalman filter (UKF) for real-time parameter identification. Based on this model, a novel electrical state-of-charge (SOC) formulation is developed to directly translate the thermodynamic state of a building into an electrical energy metric. Ultimately, this framework enables buildings to be modeled, quantified, and co-optimized as dispatchable assets within any standard energy management system (EMS), thus turning the global building stock into a valuable grid resource.

title: Digital Twin-Based Vehicle to Grid Coordination for Voltage Regulation and Peak Shaving in Unbalanced Distribution Networks
authors: Mohammed A. Mousa
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: The rapid growth of electric vehicles (EVs) presents both challenges and opportunities for distribution networks. This paper proposes a digital twin (DT)-based vehicle to grid (V2G) coordination framework for simultaneous voltage regulation and peak demand management in unbalanced three phase distribution networks. The framework integrates a real time digital twin model of the IEEE 13-bus test feeder, synchronized at 15 minute intervals, with a multi-objective optimization algorithm implemented in MATLAB/Simulink. Four case studies are conducted over a 24 hour simulation horizon: baseline uncoordinated charging, V2G for voltage regulation, V2G for peak shaving, and combined DT-V2G optimization. Results demonstrate that the proposed approach reduces voltage violations by 90.6%, decreases peak demand by 13.7% (470 kW), improves the network load factor by 16.1%, and reduces power losses by 31.0%. The MATLAB/Simulink implementation provides a validated simulation platform for evaluating coordinated V2G optimization strategies in distribution networks with high EV penetration.

title: Proposal of Modular Multi-Active Bridge Converter Analysis as an Interconnected System
authors: Silvana Gamboa, Leonardo Ortega
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: This document presents a proposal for analyzing the Modular Multiactive Bridge Systems under the interconnected system theory. This study verified the feasibility of the proposal, as the matrices describing the relationships between some of the variables in these systems met the requirements to be considered Laplacian matrices. It also verified the possibility of establishing consensus algorithms in MMAB systems, taking advantage of their interconnected nature. Finally, the proposed approaches were validated through simulation on a 5-port system for a typical MMAB operating scenario and their results demonstrated the validity of the proposal.

title: SmartEduNotes: A Retrieval-Augmented Platform for Personalized Note-Taking, Revision, and Career Guidance
authors: Vernika Singh, Tarun Kumar Pradhan, Shreya SS, Kashish Upadhayay, Shah Suraj Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Effective learning in modern education requires tools that not only store information but actively help learners retain knowledge and plan future careers. We present SmartEduNotes, a web-based, retrieval-augmented platform that enhances personal note-taking through dense semantic search, provenance-aware question answering, automated flashcard generation, and concept-graph-driven career guidance. The system integrates Sentence-BERT embeddings, FAISS vector search, and LLM-based generation to provide accurate, context-grounded answers from personal notes, while automatically converting content into cloze-style flashcards scheduled with our novel Adaptive SM-2 algorithm for optimized spaced repetition. Central concepts extracted via a concept graph inform personalized skill recommendations based on real-world job postings. Pilot evaluation on 150 lecture note documents demonstrates up to 87 % QA accuracy, 85% 7-day flashcard recall (compared to 62 % manually), and effective identification of career-relevant skills. The architecture supports modular expansion, multi-modal input (audio, text), on-device privacy, and adaptive learning strategies. This work contributes a reproducible framework for personalized learning, knowledge retention, and career-aligned skill development.

title: Graph Neural Networks for Predicting Urban Traffic Congestion in Smart Cities
authors: B.Mahalakshmi, P N Shiammala, Gowtham M, R.Bagavathi Lakshmi, A.Angel Cerli, S. Sudha
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Urban traffic congestion is a serious issue for all smart cities in terms of sustainability and efficiency. Existing traffic prediction models do not properly account for the spatial and temporal dependencies in roads within the urban road network. This paper proposes a new methodology for modeling urban traffic congestion using a graph neural network (GNN) to design the road network as a defined dynamic graph, with the addition of multi-view traffic data (i.e. speed, volume, and occupancy), temporal sequences, and external contextual factors such as weather conditions and public events. Enhanced GNN architecture is utilized with attention and hierarchical graph pooling to learn local and universal traffic patterns. The model is validated and evaluated on a dataset collected over six months for a medium smart city, alongside three baseline models (LSTM, Xgboost and Static GCN). The proposed GNN model outperformed the three baseline models RMSE is 3.42 km/h MAE is 2.78, and -2 R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score 0.915 with a F1 score of 0.89 in congested hours classification. The results of the proposed methodology confirm the suitability of spatial-temporal graph learning approaches for traffic forecasting methods. To conclude, this methodology demonstrates a strong scalable approach to computing predictive traffic models as part of critical smart city infrastructure.

title: A Dual-Purpose Interpretable Ensemble Framework of Fraud Stratification and Credit Risk
authors: Sujitha S, Suresh Kumar G, Elambarathi E, R Mohan Ram Kumar, Nithish Raj K
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Modern financial institutions are under greater pressure to not only identify a fraudulent action but also to generate transparent credit evaluation, and one in which the regulation is not compromised. Modern pipelines generally approach these as discrete tasks which results in disjointed attribute mapping and black box behaviours not meeting the regulatory requirement of traceability. In this paper, the authors propose a single framework (called Dual-Objective Interpretable Ensemble) that simultaneously fraud stratifies and scores credit using a single interpretable framework. DOIE combines a diversity-optimal ensemble backbone with a hierarchical say layer, resorting to monotonicity based constrained learners, trajectory union between local and global attribution, as well as sample boundary singleness characterization. The outputs of the system are synchronized: a categorical risk of fraud-risk and a continuous credit rating and a report to the regulator consolidating rule-level evidence. The methodology has the benefit of providing every judgment with an equivalent and evidenced analytical rationale. Experimental analysis of multimodal financial data shows that DOIE is able to reach an F1-score of 0.962 with an association rate of 0.011 on fraud detection, as well as an AUC of 0.947 on credit default prediction. Besides, the framework retains over 0.91 accountability scores in all elements. These findings confirm that DOIE is a powerful framework that can reconcile high-performance predictive analytics with the demands of financial compliance that are highly interpretable.

title: Generation of Programmatic Rules for Document Forgery Detection Using Large Language Models
authors: Valentin Schmidberger, Manuel Eberhardinger, Setareh Maghsudi, Johannes Maucher
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Document forgery poses a growing threat to legal, economic, and governmental processes, requiring increasingly sophisticated verification mechanisms. One approach involves the use of plausibility checks, rule-based procedures that assess the correctness and internal consistency of data, to detect anomalies or signs of manipulation. Although these verification procedures are essential for ensuring data integrity, existing plausibility checks are manually implemented by software engineers, which is time-consuming. Recent advances in code generation with large language models (LLMs) offer new potential for automating and scaling the generation of these checks. However, adapting LLMs to the specific requirements of an unknown domain remains a significant challenge. This work investigates the extent to which LLMs, adapted on domain-specific code and data through different fine-tuning strategies, can generate rule-based plausibility checks for forgery detection on constrained hardware resources. We fine-tune open-source LLMs, Llama 3.1 8B and OpenCoder 8B, on structured datasets derived from real-world application scenarios and evaluate the generated plausibility checks on previously unseen forgery patterns. The results demonstrate that the models are capable of generating executable and effective verification procedures. This also highlights the potential of LLMs as scalable tools to support human decision-making in security-sensitive contexts where comprehensibility is required.

title: Explainable Artificial Intelligence in Robotics: A Review of Applications and Methods
authors: Gulsum Alicioglu, Bo Sun
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Since many robots are trained by black-box artificial intelligence (AI) models, their decision-making process is uninterpretable by end-users. Therefore, explainable AI (XAI) methods are crucial in making these systems transparent, trustworthy, and understandable for acceptance. This paper reviews the recent XAI methods and applications in the field of robotics to highlight trends, limitations, and challenges. We review the selected papers across four main robotic tasks: control, navigation, manipulation, and interaction. We discuss XAI applications in terms of explanation granularity, task-decision complexity, and deployment settings. We reveal that most of the robotics research relies on virtual environments with navigation and manipulation tasks. Our findings indicate that the current XAI applications for the reasoning of robotics systems are still emerging and need user-centric multimodal explanations to enhance and repair trust, especially in failure cases.

title: Improving Dysarthria Severity Classification Through Feature Fusion and Ensemble Learning
authors: Prabhmeet Kaur, Jyotindra Narayan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Dysarthria, a motor speech disorder caused by neurological impairment, significantly disrupts communication, yet its clinical assessment remains subjective, time-consuming, and prone to variability, motivating the need for automated and reliable severity classification systems. Existing approaches often struggle with limited datasets, inconsistent feature extraction, and reduced generalization across varying speech patterns. To address these challenges, this study proposes a stacked ensemble framework that integrates comprehensive acoustic, linguistic, and temporal features, including MFCCs, ZCR, RMS, speech rate, word count, and ASR confidence, with SVM, KNN, and XGBoost as base learners and CatBoost as the meta-learner. Using the TORGO database, the model was evaluated through hyperparameter sensitivity analysis and validated using accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, confusion matrices, and ROC/AUC metrics. The proposed ensemble achieved a highest accuracy of 89.2%, outperforming standalone models such as SVM (85.4%) and XGBoost <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(87.1 \%)$</tex>, while also demonstrating strong class separability with an AUC of 0.99 for certain severity categories. These results highlight the model's robustness in managing interspeaker variability and its potential for providing a clinically meaningful, computationally efficient solution for automated assessment of dysarthria severity.

title: LIREX-CAD: A Hybrid Explainable AI Framework with Tree-Based Residual Learning for the Early Prediction of Coronary Artery Disease (CAD)
authors: Monisha S, Jeyachidra J
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Coronary Artery Disease (CAD) is among the leading causes of mortality in the global community, causing an enormous burden on healthcare systems. The timely intervention of the disease and better patient outcomes are possible by ensuring the prediction of CAD early and accurately. Despite the potential of machine learning methods, most existing models are not interpretable, which hinders their use in the clinic. This paper presents LIREX-CAD, a new hybrid Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) platform offered in early CAD forecasting. The framework combines LightGBM as a tree-based learner and residual learning based on a feedforward neural network in order to incorporate complex nonlinear relationships. An evaluation was done on a publicly available CAD dataset containing 14 attributes. Six comparison Machine Learning (ML) models namely, logistic regression, gradient boosting, HistGradient Boosting, XGBoost, LightGBM, and CatBoost, were created for comparison. SHAP and LIME were used for model transparency in order to detect important predictive features. The performance of LIREXCAD was high as it attained 98.7% accuracy, 99.0% precision, 98.5 % recall, 98.7 % F1-score, and 0.987 ROC-AUC. The confusion matrix was used to verify that the classification was strong with low false negatives. The clinically relevant features, like the type of chest pain, cholesterol, and maximum heart rate, were mostly supported by SHAP and LIME analysis as risk factors. LIREX-CAD is a reliable and transparent decisionsupport tool because it allows strong predictive capability and is interpretable. The framework has great clinical integration potential, which will promote early diagnosis and effective management of coronary artery disease.

title: Research on an Improved Global Correlation-Track Association Algorithm
authors: Xiangfeng Liu, Shijie Sun, Runsheng Wang, Xiaojun Li, Feng Luo, Jian Wang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Space measurement system requires real-time tracking, identification, and processing of multiple space targets, and the primary challenge to address is the multi-target association problem. Compared with other association algorithms, the global correlation-track association algorithm offers advantages such as flexible application, strong adaptability, and high association accuracy. However, due to the inherent characteristics of the algorithm, its computational complexity increases geometrically as the number of tracked targets grows. This paper designs a global correlation-track association algorithm based on "association clusters", which can solve the track association problem of dense multi-targets in real-time space measurement system under complex conditions.

title: Design of Cooperative Communication with Data Aggregation and Channel Selection in 6G Wireless Body Area Network based Applications
authors: G. Ramkumar
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Specialized networks known as WBANs use sensors affixed to the body to monitor vital parameters like oxygen saturation, ECG, and movement. These networks are essential for improving healthcare and telemedicine because they enable real-time monitoring and data analysis for disease management and forecasting. Incorporating 6G technology improves WBANs with ultra-low latency, extensive connectivity, and high data transfer rates, setting the stage for more dependable and effective health monitoring systems. Nevertheless, the heightened dependence on wireless communication and public networks raises major concerns about data security, privacy, and system stability. To tackle these issues, Cooperative Communication with Data Aggregation and Channel Selection in 6G WBAN (CDACWB) is developed. The simulation demonstration of the proposed CDACWB is performed in the ns3 software and the parameters which are calculated for the performance analysis are time estimation, computational cost and communication cost analysis. When compared with the baseline methods the proposed CDACWB performed much better in terms of time estimation and cost analysis which helps to maximize the performance.

title: Adaptive Source–Channel Coding for Multi-User Semantic and Data Communications
authors: Kai Yuan, Dongxu Li, Jianhao Huang, Han Zhang, Chuan Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: This paper considers a multi-user semantic and data communication (MU-SemDaCom) system, where a base station (BS) simultaneously serves users with different semantic and data tasks through a downlink multi-user multiple-input single-output (MU-MISO) channel. The coexistence of heterogeneous communication tasks, diverse channel conditions, and the requirements for digital compatibility poses significant challenges to the efficient design of MU-SemDaCom systems. To address these issues, we propose a multi-user adaptive source-channel coding (MU-ASCC) framework that adaptively optimizes deep neural network (DNN)-based source coding, digital channel coding, and superposition broadcasting according to the channel conditions. First, we employ a data-regression method to approximate the end-to-end (E2E) semantic and data distortions, for which no closed-form expressions exist due to the complex coupling between DNN-based source coding and channel codes. The obtained logistic formulas decompose the E2E distortion as the addition of the source and channel distortion terms, in which the logistic parameter variations are task-dependent and jointly determined by both the DNN and channel parameters. Then, based on the derived formulas, we formulate a weighted-sum E2E distortion minimization problem that jointly optimizes the source-channel coding rates, power allocation, and beamforming vectors for both the data and semantic users. Finally, an alternating optimization (AO) framework is developed, where the adaptive rate optimization is solved using the subgradient descent method, while the joint power and beamforming is addressed via the uplink-downlink duality (UDD) technique. Simulation results demonstrate that, compared with the conventional separate source-channel coding (SSCC) and deep joint source-channel coding (DJSCC) schemes that are designed for a single task, the proposed MU-ASCC scheme achieves simultaneous improvements in both the data recovery and semantic task performance.

title: Three-Dimensional UAV Path Planning Using Whale Optimization Algorithm with Lévy Flight and Elite Preservation Strategy
authors: Yanjie Huang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: This paper proposes an enhanced Whale Optimization Algorithm (WOA) integrating Lévy flight initialization and elite preservation strategies for three-dimensional UAV path planning in obstacle-dense environments. The algorithm formulates path planning as a multi-objective optimization problem incorporating path length, smoothness, altitude variation, and trajectory deviation. A hybrid initialization strategy combines Gaussian perturbations with Lévy flight distributions to enhance solution diversity. Comprehensive experiments conducted on a 450×400×50 3D grid map demonstrate superior performance: the proposed WOA achieves the shortest path length of 592.15±9.28m, representing 7.7% improvement over A* (641.72m) and 24.3% over GWO (782.14± 29.83m). The algorithm maintains exceptional smoothness with maximum turning angle of 6.34±1.12° and virtually zero sharp turns (0.1±0.3 occurrences), significantly outperforming A* (348 sharp turns) and PSO (38.42±5.67° maximum angle). Benchmark function tests validate robustness, with WOA surpassing GWO by 57 orders of magnitude on Sphere function (3.48×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−86</sup> vs 5.21×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−29</sup>), confirming its effectiveness for complex 3D navigation tasks.

title: Fine-Grained Monocular Depth Estimation Based on Multi-Scale Recursive Laplacian Fusion Network
authors: Yanhui Xi, Zhen Ding, Wenxin Zhu, Lanlan Liu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Monocular depth estimation is crucial for 3D scene perception, with applications in autonomous driving and robot navigation. Traditional methods often struggle with object boundary delineation and scene layout complexity. This paper introduces a novel Multi-Scale Laplacian-ConvGRU Fusion Network (MSLCF-Net) to address these challenges. By integrating a ResNeXt101 encoder with a Multi-Scale Interpolation and Cross-Layer Aggregation (MICRA) module, and a decoder incorporating a Laplacian-based Convolutional Gated Recurrent Unit (LAP-CGRU), MSLCF-Net enhances feature representation and long-range dependency capture. Experiments on KITTI datasets demonstrate superior performance, with a 5.2% improvement in Abs Rel and a 2.3% reduction in RMSE compared to state-of-the-art models. Our approach offers a significant advancement in monocular depth estimation, providing finer structural details and accurate depth boundaries.

title: Real-Time Game Streaming Enhancement using Deep Learning
authors: Prudhvi Teja Chittiboyina, Nirman Maitra, Karthik GM
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The rise of game streaming platforms has increased the demand for the high-quality streams. The companies that offer streaming as service must provide the video content of good quality for viewer retention and engaging the viewers. However, streaming in high resolution real time needs a significant amount of bandwidth, making it challenging for the users who have limited network bandwidth. As a solution to this issue, we propose a real-time video super-resolution (VSR) technique which uses an Efficient Sub-Pixel Convolutional Neural Network (ESPCN). This deep learning model improves the quality of the stream video, which in turn will reduce the need for high bandwidth streaming from the server side without compromising on the quality. To test the model on the client side we used multi-threaded inference pipeline along with the queues to process the incoming frames asynchronously, The model was finetuned on publicly available streams, curated for non-commercial research use. The experiments show significant improvements over bicubic interpolation it achieves PSNR of 30.4db, SSIM of 0.93 and a VMAF improvement of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{+} \mathbf{7. 6}$</tex> points which confirms both perpetual and quantitative improvement. This work shows that the possibility of the client-side enhancement that can be used for real-time game streaming offering a solution for low-bandwidth media applications.

title: SCD-YOLO: An Improved YOLOv11-Based Detection Algorithm for Rapeseed Sclerotinia Disease in Smart Agriculture
authors: Jiaxin Zhou, Jinghan Sun, Ziyi Chen
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Rapeseed sclerotinia disease is one of the most destructive fungal diseases affecting oilseed rape production, causing severe yield losses and posing a significant threat to agricultural sustainability. Accurate and timely detection of sclerotinia-infected plants under complex field environments is crucial for precision disease management and intelligent crop protection. However, challenges such as irregular lesion morphology, partial occlusion by leaves and branches, and complex background interference significantly degrade the performance of conventional vision-based detection methods. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an improved YOLOv11-based detection algorithm, termed SCD-YOLO (Sclerotinia Crop Detection YOLO). Two key enhancements are introduced. First, a Disease Morphology-Aware Feature Enhancement (DMAFE) module is designed to explicitly model elongated stem lesions and irregular white mycelial regions, improving the representation of partially occluded disease features. Second, a Field Background Suppression Decoupled Head (FBSDH) is proposed to reduce false detections caused by soil, residual straw, and overlapping vegetation by incorporating background-aware suppression into both classification and regression branches. Experiments conducted on a self-constructed Rapeseed Sclerotinia Disease Dataset demonstrate that SCDYOLO achieves an mAP@0.5 of 0.937, outperforming the YOLOv11 baseline by 1.5%. The results verify that the proposed method significantly enhances detection accuracy and robustness in real-world agricultural field scenarios.

title: Design and Performance Evaluation of a Low-Power Highly Sensitive Double-Gate Junctionless FET UV Photodetector
authors: Devendra Rani, Pushpa Raikwal, Koushik Dutta
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This paper presents a double-gate junctionless field effect transistor (DGJLFET) based ultraviolet (UV) photodetector for highly sensitive low-power applications. In the proposed device, two gate electrodes sandwich the channel, increasing the significant current flow and mitigating the short-channel effects. Each gate has a length of 45 nm and an oxide layer thickness of 1.5 nm. For effective absorption of UV light, a thin film of Zinc Oxide is utilized as the gate material. Performance metrics, such as sensitivity, responsivity, and detectivity, are compared with a conventional double-gate metal oxide semiconductor field effect transistor (DGMOSFET). The proposed DGJLFET UV photodetector achieves a sensitivity of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.47 \times 10^{\wedge} 10$</tex>, responsivity of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.30 \times 10^{\wedge} 8 \mathrm{A}/\mathrm{W}$</tex>, and I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</inf>/I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off</inf> of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 10^{\wedge} 10$</tex> under a very low UV light intensity of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$140 \mu W/\text{cm}^{\wedge} 2$</tex>. Overall, the results demonstrate that the DGJLFET-based UV photodetector offers superior performance compared to the DGMOSFET-based UV photodetector at a 350 nm wavelength of incident light.

title: Ways to Increase the Energy and Ecological Efficiency of Intelligent Transport Systems of the Region’s Support Road Network
authors: Roman S. Runets, Vladislav A. Pastushkov, Yuri V. Trofimenko
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: This article examines ways to improve the energy and environmental efficiency of intelligent transport systems being implemented on the region’s core road network. The relevance of the study stems from the increasing energy consumption and environmental impacts associated with the digitalization of road transport infrastructure, particularly in regions with pronounced seasonality in transport demand. Using the Krasnodar region as an example, a systems approach is substantiated based on identifying transport and infrastructure sources of energy losses, developing a rational ITS architecture, and gradually introducing renewable energy sources for autonomous power supply to facilities. An integrated indicator for assessing energy and environmental efficiency is proposed, allowing for the justification of investment decisions and development programs for the region’s core road network.

title: Intrusion Detection and Anomaly Identification in Edge Smart Grid Scenarios
authors: Shuxian Sun, Baohua Cheng, Siwei Li, Yun Li, Huadong Yu, Yiying Zhang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: With the continuous advancement of deep interconnection and edge deployment of smart grids, power communication networks face more complex intrusion and anomaly threats. Traditional detection methods based on rules or shallow learning are susceptible to high-dimensional redundant features and temporal behavioral evolution in dynamic environments, making it difficult to balance accuracy and deployment overhead. To address this, this paper proposes an intrusion detection method for edge smart grid scenarios: First, explicit and size-adaptive particle swarm optimization (ESAPSO) is introduced to reduce feature redundancy and computational cost while retaining key discriminative information; then, a BiLSTM–CNN dual-branch fusion model is constructed to model the long-range temporal dependence and local discriminative patterns of network behavior, and the comprehensive detection capability is improved through feature fusion. Experiments validated the effectiveness of the method on the public dataset CIC-DDoS2019 and a self-built dataset based on OMNeT++: On CIC-DDoS2019, the accuracy was 98.9%, precision was 99.1%, recall was 97.1%, and F1-score was 98.0%; on the self-built dataset, the accuracy reached 97.0%, 98.1%, 96.9%, and 97.8%, respectively. Ablation experiments further demonstrated that ESAPSO feature selection and BiLSTM–CNN co-modeling have complementary gains, maintaining high detection capability while reducing false positives, providing an effective solution for intrusion detection in edge-side smart grids.

title: Performance Improvement of Printed Monopole Antenna using Frequency Selective Surface
authors: Harshit Kaundal, Jasbir Kaur, Arun Kumar Singh, Gourab Das
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: In this paper, the performance of the printed monopole antenna is improved with the help of a Frequency Selective Surface (FSS) based reflector. The antenna used, three reflecting surface planes which are placed at an angle of 45° to each other. The antenna is placed at a certain distance from the reflector. The antenna is radiated at the frequency 5.2 GHz. The radiation is then reflected back by the frequency selective surface (FSS) which leads to increase the unidirectional gain of the antenna. The Simulation of the respective reflection coefficient and transmission coefficient of the antenna and frequency selective surface (FSS) illustrates that an increase in gain of the antenna is achieved by adding Frequency Selective Surface. Integration of the frequency selective surface (FSS) layer improves the antenna gain from 5.43 dB to 10.78 dB and achieved the gain improvement of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 98 \%$</tex>.

title: Design Study of a Fault-Tolerant Fiber-Optic Access Network Based on the Heat Supply Infrastructure
authors: Sergei A. Sidnev, Galina S. Artemeva, Liudmila Y. Krasikova, Vladimir A. Tsarenko
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: The article discusses an approach to designing a fault-tolerant fiber-optic access network using urban heating infrastructure as a platform for laying communication lines. It is shown that heating mains often match the tracing requirements of communications, which supports the principle of “digging the earth once” and allows for faster commissioning of lines with less impact on the urban environment. The methods of optical cable placement during the construction of heating networks, their advantages for operation and access to maintenance are analyzed. The limitations and risks of integration are considered. The key technical challenge in designing such solutions is to align the tree topology of heating networks with the communication requirements for high fault tolerance. Methods of ensuring stability, including route redundancy, hybrid topologies, and monitoring/diagnostics, have been proposed to increase the stability of fiber-optic communication lines. The necessary ratios for estimating capital expenditures in the application of joint construction/use of infrastructure are obtained, the calculation results are presented, and the data obtained are analyzed.

title: End-to-End Behavioral Cloning with Throttle and Speed Fusion: A Multi-Modal Approach Using Udacity Jungle and Lake Tracks
authors: Barshan Mondal, Kartikey Singh, Krishnan V Namboothiri, K. Namitha
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This paper presents an autonomous steering vision based behavioral cloning architecture, which is developed based on data gathered on the Udacity Jungle as well as Lake simulator track. It is an extension of a traditional image-only regression architecture that it uses throttle and speed as auxiliary inputs to an already trained ResNet-18 backbone, thus facilitating the context-based inference of steering. To reduce the severe zero-steering effect, we push the zero-steering effect, which is apparent in 61.8% of the samples, to recalculate selective datacuration strategy 5% of straight-driving matches are simply kept, other straight steering matches are artificially extended by using singular flipping. The result of this procedure is a balanced set of training that contains 5,831 recordings. After 25 training cycles using the Adam optimizer (learning rate= 1e-4), the model achieves a validation mean-squared error equal to 0.0579 and has attained steady convergence. These held out frame real time inferences give a steering estimate that is accurate on held out frames as indicated through an example left turn of a deviation of -6.08 degrees. The findings are relevant to stress that modular sensory-related cues and data-knowledgeable preprocessing play a vital role in increasing the resilience of behavioral cloning frameworks.

title: A Smart Pill That Can Sense, Sample, and Treat: Ingestible Electronics will Deliver Drugs and Take Biopsies
authors: Reza Ghodssi, Justin Stine, Luke Beardslee
publish: IEEE Spectrum
abstract: One day soon, a doctor might prescribe a pill that doesn't just deliver medicine but also reports back on what it finds inside you—and then takes actions based on its findings.

title: Dynamic Incremental Tucker Decomposition for Sparse Tensors on Heterogeneous Platforms
authors: Xiaosong Peng, Laurence T. Yang, Xiaokang Wang, Shijie Lv
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computers
abstract: Tucker decomposition approximates the original tensor using a set of factor matrices and a core tensor, thereby reducing the storage and computational cost. Existing incremental Tucker decomposition techniques have limitations in efficiency and are not applicable to diverse dynamic incremental tensors. This paper proposes a dynamic incremental Tucker decomposition method based on heterogeneous computing, which efficiently handles complex dynamic tensors while preserving computational efficiency. Specifically, the target optimization function of Tucker decomposition is reconstructed to decouple the intrinsic correlation between factor matrix rows and fixed tensor dimensions, and the optimal solutions for the factor matrix rows are derived. This enables the algorithm to adapt to arbitrary forms of incoming incremental data, including time-series tensors, without recalculating existing data, thus enhancing data utilization efficiency. Then, a lightweight precision optimization strategy is introduced, which iteratively updates factor matrix rows using the most element-rich dimensions of each tensor order based on recorded observable data volumes. This strategy improves precision while significantly reducing computational costs. Furthermore, a task-characteristic-based heterogeneous computing scheduling strategy is proposed. By extracting highly parallelizable intermediate operators and leveraging the high throughput of GPUs, the algorithm is accelerated, leading to a 10 to 40 times speedup in incremental tensor decomposition compared to state-of-the-art methods, while maintaining accuracy.

title: Performance Analysis and Evaluation of the Dual Frequency Doppler Consistency Checking based GNSS Spoofing Detection for Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Ziheng Zhou, Feifan Zhou, Yimin Deng, Mingquan Lu, Hong Li
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: With the growing reliance on Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) for navigation and positioning in autonomous systems, the vulnerability to spoofing attacks has emerged as a critical concern. The increasing deployment of dual-frequency GNSS receivers in vehicular applications has drawn attention to their potential for efficient spoofing detection. Dual-Frequency Doppler Consistency Checking (DFDCC) offers an intuitive approach by assessing the Doppler consistency between two frequency signals. This method not only enables the identification of spoofed signals but also exhibits robustness and flexibility in vehicular scenarios. Despite its practical advantages, DFDCC currently lacks a comprehensive theoretical framework and detailed performance analysis, particularly in the context of vehicular applications. To bridge these gaps, this study aims to develop a theoretical foundation and evaluate the real-world applicability and effectiveness of DFDCC in vehicular scenarios. The paper begins by deriving a Doppler observation model for both authentic and spoofing signals, deriving key sources of Doppler bias. These include the deviation between false and real user states, the spoofer clock bias, and the relative velocity between the spoofer and the user. Besides, extensive simulation experiments are conducted to analyze the influence of these factors on DFDCC performance, revealing an offset phenomenon when multiple factors coexist. This method is further validated through field tests under three typical vehicular motion patterns, demonstrating its feasibility across diverse vehicular scenarios. The results confirm that DFDCC is a viable solution for spoofing detection for autonomous vehicles, offering valuable insights for its practical implementation and serving as a reference for future research.

title: Synthetic Bistatic ISAR Imaging for Moving Targets With Prescribed Trajectories and Scattering Regimes: Validation With Passive Radar Data
authors: Sandra Gutiérrez-Serrano, Javier Rosado-Sanz, M. Pilar Jarabo-Amores, David Mata-Moya, Nerea Del-Rey-Maestre
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: The design of a bistatic Inverse Synthetic Aperture Radar (ISAR) images simulator is described, as a response to the need of databases with classification purposes in bistatic radar scenarios characterized by numerous degrees of freedom. Main contributions are related to the use of 3D target models and electromagnetic simulations adapted to the scattering regime defined by the operation frequency and target size. The simulation process is driven by target trajectory and dynamic parameters, being able to mimic the variation of incidence and observation angles in the bistatic planes as the target moves along the trajectory. The simulation approach combines MATLAB and ANSYS HFSS (High Frequency Structure Simulator) tools. Shooting Bouncing Rays (SBR+) and full wave techniques are applied on 3D Computer-Aided Design (CAD) models with specified materials. Passive radar data acquired under different geometries and opportunity illuminators for aerial and maritime targets are used for validation.

title: Three-Dimensional Circumnavigating Control of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles Using Range-Only Measurements_supp1-3681159.mp4
authors: Baoli Ma
publish: N/A
abstract: This letter investigates the three-dimensional (3D) circumnavigating control for a single unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) using only range measurements in GPS-denied environments. The core challenge lies in the unavailability of bearing information, rendering traditional control laws ineffective. To formulate the control design, we propose a hierarchical motion decomposition strategy by introducing a virtual anchor, which conceptualizes the UAV's movement as a superposition of orbital and translational motions. Based on this formulation, a global circumnavigation control law is developed by fusing a nonlinear observer with the system dynamics. A unified stability analysis is presented to guarantee that the circumnavigation errors converge to zero from any initial state, without relying on the separation principle. Numerical simulations and field experiments are performed to validate the theoretical results.

title: GVF-MPC Depth Control Framework Motivated by Restoring-Moment Mechanism Analysis for Bionic Robotic Fish_supp1-3681186.mp4
authors: Zhongjing Ma
publish: N/A
abstract: Longitudinal oscillations are commonly observed in depth control of low-speed underwater platforms such as bio-inspired robotic fish. To investigate this issue, this paper conducts a quantitative analysis of the vertical-plane dynamics. It reveals the speed-dependent coupling between the depth and pitch channels as the swimming speed decreases, and clarifies the underdamped mechanism induced by restoring-moment dominance. A critical speed condition for the onset of low-speed oscillations is also derived. Based on the dynamic analysis, a hierarchical GVF–MPC depth control framework is proposed, which structurally decouples geometric guidance from dynamic execution. The guidance layer employs a guiding vector field (GVF) to generate naturally smooth attitude references from geometric information, with reduced sensitivity to model degradation and pitch disturbances. The inner loop applies model predictive control (MPC) to track the guidance commands while explicitly accounting for dynamic constraints, balancing convergence speed and control-input smoothness to improve stability and safety under low-speed conditions. Simulation and experiments are conducted to evaluate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed method. Simulations consider sinusoidal and V-shaped depth path and compare the proposed approach with LOS, PID, and SMC based controllers. Experiments are performed in still water and under simulated Sea-State 1 disturbances to assess steady state accuracy, disturbance recovery, and overall robustness.

title: Robust Information Bottleneck Guided Non-autoregressive Semantic Communication with Synonymous Mapping
authors: Mingtong Zhang, Haixia Zhang, Dongfeng Yuan, Ping Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Semantic communication emerges as a pivotal technology for realizing the 6G vision. However, existing semantic-aware reconstruction systems rely on syntactic-level loss functions for optimization, failing to focus on the precise recovery of semantic information and resulting in suboptimal semantic fidelity. Meanwhile, the autoregressive decoding architecture adopted in text semantic communication introduces prohibitive high latency. To address these two issues, we propose a robust information bottleneck (RIB) guided non-autoregressive semantic communication (NASC) scheme, named RIB-NASC. First, we pioneer the integration of the RIB criterion into semanticaware reconstruction systems, formulating a direct optimization objective tailored for underlying semantic recovery and establishing an informativeness-robustness trade-off. Second, we derive a tractable variational lower bound for the RIB objective via variational approximation and a novel synonymous mapping-based semantic posterior estimation strategy. Third, we design a lightweight non-autoregressive semantic decoder architecture based on Transformer encoder, enabling high-speed parallel semantic decoding during inference. Extensive simulation results demonstrate that the RIB-NASC scheme significantly outperforms baseline schemes in terms of semantic recovery performance (BLEU score and sentence similarity) and achieves a decoding delay reduction of nearly 96.7% compared to traditional autoregressive decoding.

title: CMOS Current Driver for Electrothermal MEMS Switches With Actuation and Diagnostic Circuitry
authors: Allan Riboullet, Glenn E. R. Cowan, Frederic Nabki
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: This paper presents an integrated CMOS current driver designed for electrothermal micro-electromechanical-systems (MEMS) switches, fabricated using a 0.18 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m Bipolar-CMOS-DMOS (BCD) process. Unlike conventional constant-voltage drivers typically employed for MEMS actuators with positive temperature coefficient of resistance (TCR), this driver adopts a constant-current actuation. This approach benefits series-connected array components, applications seeking enhanced electromigration control, as well as actuators exhibiting a negative TCR. The system includes a 6-bit programmable digital-to-analog converter, current multiplication circuits, and feedback diagnostic capabilities, enabling precise control and monitoring of actuator performance within a core area of 1.5 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. The driver supports pulse-width modulation (PWM) to significantly reduce power consumption compared to DC actuation and implements a double-pulse strategy for accelerating switch activation times. Diagnostic circuits measure actuator resistance within ± 0.5 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {\Omega }$</tex-math> </inline-formula> and switching states to enhance reliability in safety-critical applications. Experimental validation confirmed that the driver successfully provides currents up to 110 mA with a resolution of 1.65 mA. At a minimum output voltage of 1.7 V, it can power electrothermal actuators with up to 775 mW under an 8 V supply voltage, corresponding to a power density of 517 mW/mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. The driver was demonstrated to successfully actuate a chevron-style MEMS switch in 3.5 ms and maintain contact with a 76.7 mA, 50 % PWM waveform at 1 kHz, reducing power consumption by 30.72 %.

title: A Language-enhanced Supervision-reduced Analysis Framework for Multi-branching Vessels
authors: Xi-Yao Ma, Shi-Qi Liu, Xiao-Liang Xie, Xiao-Hu Zhou, Zeng-Guang Hou, Xin-Kai Qu, Wen-Zheng Han, Ming Wang, Meng Song, Chu-Tian Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Artificial Intelligence
abstract: It is essential to quantitatively analyze coronary arterial angiography (CAG) images for coronary interventional procedures as they are the gold reference standard for assessing stenosis. However, mainstream methods cannot extract different vascular branches precisely due to inadequate annotated data by experts and difficulty in uniformly defining vascular branches. To address the issue, this paper presents a novel framework to analyze multi-branching vessels with simple annotation, which localizes the endpoints of the vascular branches first, and then extracts corresponding vessel segments based on the endpoints. Considering it is challenging for current image-based methods to distinguish sparse vessel keypoints, we propose a cross-modal Language-Enhanced Endpoint Detection (LEED) network to achieve better performance by providing linguistic knowledge obtained from clinicians. A medical term corpus is established to convert conversation contents to standard texts. Furthermore, we devise a Twin-Stream Vessel Segmentation (TS-Seg) algorithm to segment various vascular branches based on gray-level features and detected endpoints. Besides, clinicians are allowed to manually add new endpoints and apply the TS-Seg algorithm alone to analyze any variant vascular branch that has not been defined before. Extensive experiments on two in-house and one public datasets consistently demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed framework. Moreover, comprehensive vessel analysis reports are provided based on clinical prior knowledge to assist in preoperative surgery planning and postoperative treatment assessment, showing great potential to improve the efficiency and accuracy of clinical workflow.

title: Making LSM-Tree-based Key-Value Store Practical and Efficient for Multi-Tenant Serverless Cloud Databases
authors: Yingjia Wang, Caixin Gong, Guoyun Zhu, Sheng Wang, Zhengheng Wang, Huan Liu, Junzhi Shi, Wu Qin, Wei Zhang, Feifei Li, Ming-Chang Yang
