title: Accelerating YOLOv8 on General-Purpose CPUs for Object Detection
authors: S. Sasikumar, M Rajmohan, Sivanantham E, C. Ezhilazhagan, Durairaj.M
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: This paper presents a high-speed CPU-based accelerator for the YOLOv8 object detection model. The model is compiled using Open Neural Network Exchange (ONNX) then the design is implemented and evaluated in a simulation environment, aiming to maximize inference speed while efficiently utilizing CPU resources. Experimental results demonstrate significant improvements in processing speed demonstrating the effectiveness of CPU-based acceleration, highlighting the potential of CPU-based deep learning accelerators for real-time applications.

title: Redefining Systolic Efficiency with Pipelining ML Acceleration for High-Throughput AI Inference
authors: Suvitha PS, Aathmanesan T
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: This paper proposes an optimized systolic array architecture tailored for AI acceleration, leveraging hybrid pipelining, Radix-8 encoding, and a CSA–PPA-based accumulation scheme. Distinct from traditional carry-portioned or operand-decomposed approaches, the design integrates a hybrid-pipelined dataflow with asynchronous summation, enabling high-throughput multiply-and-accumulate (MAC) operations with minimal clock-phase dependencies. The hybrid architecture includes delay-balanced logic, parallel prefix adder paths, and lightweight partial product registers that collectively reduce critical path delay and power consumption. The architecture was synthesized and evaluated on an Xilinx FPGA platform, achieving a delay of 13.353 ns and dynamic power of 154.05 mW, with a 26.4% delay improvement and 24.8% energy savings compared to baseline implementations. These results highlight the feasibility of the design for resource-constrained AI edge inference, offering scalable performance while minimizing area overhead and clock distribution complexity.

title: Multi-Channel Dynamic Convolutional Network for Knowledge Graph Completion
authors: Yanni Feng, Haitao Yu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Existing knowledge graph completion models based on convolutional neural networks still face limitations in feature representation. The unified convolutional perspective struggles to fully capture local variations within complex structures. And when feature dimensions increase significantly, redundant information may obscure key features. To address these issues, this paper proposes a knowledge graph completion model based on multi-channel dynamic convolutional network. The model first employs a reshaping combined with a dynamic convolution kernel mechanism to achieve local adaptive modeling and deep latent feature extraction. Subsequently, it introduces alternating reshaping and static convolution kernel structures to extract multi-scale surface features, thereby balancing feature diversity and global consistency. Finally, a self-calibrating attention mechanism selects the most representative features. Experiments on four public benchmark datasets demonstrate that the proposed model achieves significant performance improvements over existing methods in link prediction tasks, validating its effectiveness in modeling complex relationships and feature representation.

title: Energy Efficiency for Automobile A/C Systems Using Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Neha Ananthavaram, Taehyung Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Improving the energy efficiency of vehicle air conditioning (A/C) systems is becoming increasingly important as we work toward more sustainable transportation. While much attention has been given to electric and hybrid vehicles, there is limited research on how A/C systems affect fuel consumption in traditional Internal Combustion Engine (ICE) vehicles. This project focuses on addressing that gap by developing machine learning models that predict and optimize A/C-related energy use using real-world data collected from a 2006 Honda Civic. The study combines OBD-II sensor data with external weather information to create a clean and well-structured dataset. After preprocessing and feature selection, both traditional machine learning and deep learning models were evaluated, including Linear Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, GRU, and a hybrid GRU-XGBoost ensemble. The XGBoost model performed best, showing strong predictive accuracy with low RMSE and high R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> scores. GRU captured sequential patterns effectively, while XGBoost handled non-linear feature interactions. The results demonstrate that machine learning can help identify when A/C usage significantly impacts fuel consumption, offering insights that could support smarter, energy-aware system design. This work lays a foundation for using data-driven techniques to reduce emissions and improve efficiency in conventional vehicles, especially under varied real-world driving and environmental conditions.

title: Robust Prescribed Control of an Electro-hydraulic System with Modified Observer
authors: Jae Ung Yu, Van Chuong Le, Dinh Tu Duong, Phuc Ngoc Nguyen, Quoc Cuong Phan, Van Du Phan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)
abstract: The electro-hydraulic system (ELHS) plays an important role in modern industry. However, various adverse factors—such as disturbances, strict constraints, and nonlinearities—make the tracking control problem more challenging. In this paper, a robust prescribed control (RPC) strategy integrated with a modified sliding mode observer (MSOB) is proposed for the ELHS. Firstly, a prescribed function is employed to ensure the position error reaches to zero with the predefined constraint. Next, the MSOB is designed to handle the influences of lumped disturbances. The simulation trial research demonstrates the efficacy of the suggested control framework.

title: Evaluating the Impact of Compression Techniques on the Robustness of CNNs under Natural Corruptions
authors: Itallo Patrick Castro Alves Da Silva, Emanuel Adler Medeiros Pereira, Erick de Andrade Barboza, Baldoino Fonseca Dos Santos Neto, Márcio de Medeiros Ribeiro
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Compressed deep learning models are crucial for deploying computer vision systems on resource-constrained devices. However, model compression may affect robustness, especially under natural corruption. Therefore, it is important to consider robustness evaluation while validating computer vision systems. This paper presents a comprehensive evaluation of compression techniques—quantization, pruning, and weight clustering—applied individually and in combination to convolutional neural networks (ResNet-50, VGG-19, and MobileNetV2). Using the CIFAR-10-C and CIFAR-100-C datasets, we analyze the trade-offs between robustness, accuracy, and compression ratio. Our results show that certain compression strategies not only preserve but can also improve robustness, particularly on networks with more complex architectures. Utilizing multi-objective assessment, we determine the best configurations, showing that customized technique combinations produce beneficial multi-objective results. This study provides insights into selecting compression methods for robust and efficient deployment of models in corrupted real-world environments.

title: NeuroScan: A Comprehensive Deep Learning Framework for Automated Brain MRI Tumor Detection and Multi-Class Classification
authors: Prathamesh S Vadatille, Dennis Singh Moirangthem, Khoirom Motilal Singh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Brain tumor diagnosis from magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is time-consuming and requires skilled radiologists, which can be a limiting factor in many hospitals. In this work, we present NeuroScan, a deep learning system that automatically classifies brain MRI slices into four classes: pituitary tumor, glioma, meningioma, and no tumor. We finetune a VGG16-based convolutional neural network using transfer learning on a public dataset of 6,891 images, with 5,580 images for training and 1,311 for testing. Standard preprocessing, intensity normalization, and modest data augmentation are applied to improve generalization. On the held-out test set, the model achieves an overall accuracy of 93 % and class-wise precision, recall, and F1-scores close to or above 0.90 for most classes. To make the model easier to use, we integrate it into a simple Streamlit web application that allows users to upload an MRI image and view the predicted class along with confidence scores and basic visualizations. Our results indicate that a relatively compact VGG16-based model, combined with a lightweight web interface, can provide a practical proof-of-concept for automated brain MRI analysis in research and educational settings.

title: Topology of Out-of-Distribution Examples in Deep Neural Networks
authors: Esha Datta, Johanna Hennig, Eva Domschot, Connor Mattes, Michael R. Smith
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: A longstanding problem for deployed deep neural networks (DNNs) is their behavior in the face of unfamiliar inputs; specifically, these models tend to be overconfident and incorrect when encountering out-of-distribution (OOD) examples. In this work, we present a topological approach to characterizing OOD examples based on embeddings from DNNs. Our goal is to identify topological features that distinguish OOD inputs from in-distribution ones. Our experiments on benchmark datasets reveal that well-trained DNNs induce a topological simplification on training data, but not for OOD examples. More specifically, we find that the average lifetime (or persistence) of OOD examples is statistically longer than that of training or test examples. This indicates that DNNs struggle to induce topological simplification on unfamiliar inputs. Our empirical results provide novel evidence of topological simplification in realistic DNNs and lay the groundwork for topologically-informed OOD detection strategies.

title: Deep Feature Fusion: A Hybrid Lightweight CNN and Transfer Learning for Micro-Expression Detection
authors: Anshika Rani, Bam Bahadur Sinha
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Micro-expressions are very quick and involuntary movements of the face that show the true emotions and real feelings of a person in situations where they wish to hide their emotions. The detection of micro-expressions is a very hard and complex task because they last for only a fraction of a second and involve very subtle facial movements. While recent research has used temporal and spatial information, traditional techniques struggle to identify these delicate, hidden emotions. This paper implements an efficient <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$h$</tex> ybrid d eep <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$l$</tex> earning <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$m$</tex> odel <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$f$</tex> or microexpression detection, integrating a Lightweight Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) head with a DenseNet121 transfer learning backbone. The model is trained on a dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$48 \times 48$</tex> pixel grayscale facial images covering six emotion categories: fear, anger, happiness, surprise, disgust, and sadness. Preprocessing includes image resizing, normalization, and grayscale conversion to enhance critical edge and texture features. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed hybrid system achieves a high recognition accuracy of 96.97%, with a final validation accuracy of 85.35%. The lightweight, computationally efficient model is ideal for real-time mental state analysis, deception detection, and human-computer interaction.

title: Design and Evaluation of a Millimeter-Wave MIMO Antenna at 39 GHz for On-Body 5G (n260) Band
authors: Devraj Gautam, Abdul Quaiyum Ansari, Binod Kumar Kanaujia
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Design and performance evaluation of a compact <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \times 2$</tex> MIMO antenna at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 9\ G H z}$</tex> frequency is offered for on-to-off body data transmission in the new radio (n260) band. The design is implemented on a Rogers RO3010 substrate of 0.72 mm thickness, enabling miniaturization and stable resonance. A slotted patch with parallel metallic strips is added to enhance impedance matching and significantly reduce coupling between elements. A bandwidth of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.2 \text{GHz}(37.7-40.9 \text{GHz})$</tex>, a gain of 4.59 dB and excellent port isolation of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-\mathbf{35} \mathbf{dB}$</tex> are achieved. Furthermore, the SAR value remains as low as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.0621\ \mathrm{W} / \text{kg}$</tex> at 1 mW input power, confirming user safety in on-body applications. MIMO performance is validated, and the envelope correlation coefficient value is well below 0.01, confirming low inter-element correlation. It demonstrates the suitability of the antenna for reliable and effective mm-wave body-centric communication systems in healthcare and IoMT (Internet of Medical Things) applications.

title: Edge-Optimized Multimodal Generative Model for Clinical Report Generation
authors: S. Sujanthi, Arnesh R, Arsath Musammil M, Keerthika S
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The medical imaging is critical in the diagnosis of different clinical conditions, and the interpretation of X-rays, CT scans and MRI scans are usually time consuming and involve expert skills. This is especially important in large volume hospitals and resource-limited areas, in which timely reporting may be problematic. This paper introduces an edge optimized multimodal generative artificial intelligence system to design structured clinical reports using medical images automatically. The system incorporates a vision encoder to extract both the global and fine-grained image features with a language model which interprets these features into clinically significant reports such as Findings and Impressions. In order to facilitate accuracy and robustness, the model is trained on various and large-scale datasets like MIMIC-CXR, IU X-Ray, and Chexpert. The framework involves the use of explainability methods, such as attention maps, heatmaps, and 3D visualization to indicate image areas that impact predictions. Its main characteristics are dual-level reporting of physicians and patients, multilingual and speech-enabled reports, and a continuous learning module that integrates the feedback of radiologists. The system is optimized to operate in edge deployment and can work offline, which means it can be used in resource-limited healthcare centers and rural ones. This work can show the potential of multimodal AI to revolutionize clinical report generation by reducing the workload of radiologists, enhancing their accessibility, and supporting medical education in an efficient, interpretable, and inclusive way.

title: Modeling of a GNSS Patch Antenna with a Metamaterial-Based Frequency-Selective Surface
authors: Margarita A. Mashkova, Andrey A. Yelizarov, Nikita O. Bulatov, Denis V. Kolyuzhnov, Oleg V. Arinin
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: This paper investigates the design and performance of a right-handed circularly polarized (RHCP) antenna operating within the 1550-1610 MHz frequency range. The proposed structure uses a square patch with truncated corners on a dielectric substrate as the primary radiating element. To enhance bandwidth, a capacitive ring is integrated at the patch and centered around feed-pin. This design is further enriched by an additional layer of high-density thermoplastic and a frequency-selective surface (FSS) based on a metamaterial (MTM) formed as a periodic array of beveled frames. Antenna simulation demonstrates that it achieves an axial ratio (AR) less than 2.4 dB and a voltage standing wave ratio (VSWR) under 1.3 within the target bandwidth. Furthermore, the integration of the MTM superstrate broadens the operating bandwidth by a factor of 1.6 compared to the original antenna (under the conditions of VSWR ≤ 2 and AR ≤ dB).

title: An Object Recognition and Classification Method for Dexterous Robotic Hands Based on Tactile Perception
authors: Zhen Li, Peng Zhang, Huazhen Chu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: To enhance the tactile perception capability of a robotic dexterous hand, this study constructs a tactile dataset termed NTD-25, which contains contact signals from 25 different objects and covers multi-dimensional tactile information such as pressure and micro-vibrations. To address the noise interference introduced by tactile sensors under no-load conditions, the acquired tactile data are subjected to filtering-based preprocessing. Furthermore, a multi-scale interactive convolutional neural network (MSICNN) is proposed to perform object recognition and classification based on tactile perception, and is trained and evaluated on the constructed dataset. Experimental results demonstrate that the MSICNN achieves accuracies of 98.8% and 98.5% on object hardness recognition and object category recognition tasks, respectively, significantly outperforming comparative models such as ShuffleNet V2 and MobileNet V2, thereby validating the effectiveness of the proposed method for tactile perception in robotic dexterous hands.

title: Noise-Resilient Microwave Skin Tumor Localization Using Hybrid Compressed Sensing and Self-Organizing Maps
authors: Meisam Esfandiari, Majid Amiri, Xiaojing Lv, Yang Yang
publish: 2026 Australasian Symposium on Antennas (ASA)
abstract: Microwave imaging offers a non-invasive alternative for skin cancer detection but traditionally requires dense scanning arrays that increase cost and acquisition time. This study presents a hybrid framework combining compressed sensing (CS) with unsupervised learning techniques, specifically Self-Organizing Maps (SOM), integrated with delay-and-sum (DAS) beamforming for efficient tumor localization. Time-domain reflected data were acquired from a 40 by 40 mm pork skin phantom containing an embedded artificial tumor at a depth of 0.6 mm, using a waveguide-based measurement setup operating in the 25–45 GHz frequency range. The framework was evaluated under additive white Gaussian noise (AWGN) at signal-to-noise ratios (SNR) ranging from 30 to 10 dB. Results demonstrate that reliable tumor detection can be achieved using only 25 scan points. At 20 dB SNR, the method achieved a 2.24 mm localization error, showing high accuracy, while at 10 dB SNR (very high noise), it still indicated the tumor position but with reduced accuracy.

title: Applying Artificial Intelligence in Telecommunication Networks
authors: E. A. Knigin, R. F. Rafikov, Z. M. Afanasyev, E. E. Malikova, V. M. Antonova
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: This article explored the potential of artificial intelligence in telecommunications. Specifically, it described traffic analysis mechanisms and methods for improving energy efficiency in 5 G networks. A study was conducted comparing three variations of the Discontinuous Reception (DRX) energy efficiency algorithm: classic DRX, Q-aware DRX, and AI-DRX based on a convolutional neural network. To ensure the tests were as realistic as possible, real-world IoT traffic data was used. The sample included three types of loads: a constant data stream from industrial Modbus sensors, sudden load spikes from GPS trackers, and rare event signals from a Smart Thermostat. As a result, the AI-DRX algorithm reduced energy consumption by 49-80% compared to other models. At the same time, transmission latency did not increase, and service quality improved. The study revealed that conventional adaptive methods are too slow to respond to bursty traffic. However, the use of AI allows the system to prepare in advance, predicting the load in systems with IoT devices. The implementation of AI in radio interface management is not just a trend, but another way to improve energy efficiency and communication quality in nextgeneration networks.

title: 5G-Driven Telehealth Systems for High-Fidelity Remote Diagnostics
authors: Dal Singh, Chetan Khemraj Lanjewar, Mandeep Kaur Ghumman, Anand Prakash Dube
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: High-fidelity remote diagnostics require ultrareliable, low-latency communications (URLLC), edge intelligence, and compact, diagnostic-preserving compression. This study designed and evaluated a 5G-driven telehealth framework that combined URLLC network slicing, an asymmetric learned image codec, and edge-based inference to preserve clinical utility of ultrasound frames during transport. Two public medical-image datasets were adapted into a paired three-condition protocol (local / URLLC / stressed) and evaluations used Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR), Structural Similarity Index Measure (SSIM), diagnostic AUC, one-way latency, jitter, packet loss, and compute/time metrics (CPU/GPU ms, energy proxy). The proposed pipeline delivered consistent fidelity gains versus classical H. 264 streaming and strong learned-codec baselines, reporting a representative improvement of +4 dB PSNR and an approximate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+8 \%$</tex> relative AUC gain versus the H. 264 baseline under URLLC conditions while meeting E2E latency <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<50 ~\text{ms}$</tex>. Results also showed favourable compute-latency efficiency (AUC per <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0 ~ m s}$</tex> latency) by shifting complexity to an edge decoder. Implications include a deployable URLLC-aware tele-ultrasound architecture for remote screening and procedures, concrete clinical acceptability thresholds (PSNR <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>35 ~\text{dB}$</tex>, SSIM <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>0.90$</tex>, latency <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<50 ~\text{ms}$</tex>), and a reproducible paired-dataset protocol for future research.

title: Reinforcement Learning-Based Dispatching in Flow/Job Shop
authors: I. S. Gavrilov, S. L. Yablochnikov
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: Operational dispatching in discrete manufacturing determines the sequence of operations executed on equipment under queues, setups, breakdowns, and due-date constraints. In complex flow-shop and job-shop systems, even small changes in shop-floor conditions can noticeably degrade on-time performance, order lead time, and work-in-process levels; as a result, fixed priority rules often lose robustness when operating mode shift. The increasing availability of production data and the development of digital shop-floor models create the prerequisites for using machine-learning methods as a decision-support layer at the execution level. This paper provides a structured comparison of dispatching approaches under uncertainty with an emphasis on practical integration into the production control loop: augmenting priority rules with forecasts of processing/remaining times and tardiness risk, training DRL policies to directly select the next operation in a shop-floor simulation. For each group, we identify typical requirements for data and simulation, target metrics (tardiness and on-time delivery, lead/cycle time, WIP, throughput), and operational constraints (decision latency, robustness across modes, and monitoring of performance degradation). The results indicate that, within an MES context, the most readily deployable solutions are those that preserve existing dispatching logic while adding adaptivity through forecasting or ranking, whereas DRL/RL policies offer high improvement potential but require verification on digital shop-floor model and safe-operation procedures.

title: A Knowledge Graph and Credibility Assessment-Based Intelligent Question Answering System for Power Grid Applications
authors: Depin Feng, Qing Xu, Zheng Wang, Xiang Song, Wenlei Xu, Shanzhi Yu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: With the increasing complexity of power grid structures and the diversification of operational data, traditional knowledge management tools have proven inadequate for tasks such as dispatch support, fault diagnosis, and interpretation of regulatory procedures. To address these limitations, this paper proposes KG-CA-QA, an intelligent question answering system for the power grid domain that integrates knowledge graphs and credibility assessment. Centered around grid entities and their relationships, the system constructs a multi-source knowledge graph that enables unified modeling and querying of substations, transmission lines, equipment status, dispatch commands, and other key elements. It incorporates a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) model and a credibility scoring mechanism, combining semantic understanding, knowledge graph reasoning, and source authority to provide fact-based, interpretable, and credibility-scored answers. Furthermore, the system includes a dynamic knowledge updating module to ensure the timeliness of the knowledge base. Experimental results demonstrate that KG-CA-QA significantly outperforms traditional methods in terms of answer accuracy, semantic coherence, and user satisfaction, highlighting its practical engineering value and potential for widespread application.

title: Research on the Performance of Wireless Optical Networks Using System DWDM
authors: A. N. Nabiyeva, B. G. Ibrahimov, V. P. Shuvalov
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: This article discusses a method for transmitting information over a wireless channel other than a radio channel. A wireless optical data transmission system implemented using wavelength division multiplexing technology is considered. The spectral efficiency modulation formats, optical system capacities, and signal-to-noise ratios in multiservice optical telecommunications networks using wavelength division multiplexing technology are analyzed. The purpose of this paper is to explore methods for calculating the performance characteristics wireless optical cellular networks based on a DWDM system. The operating principles wireless optical network nodes and the impact atmospheric factors and turbulence on transmission systems are examined. A study of wireless optical networks proposed a new approach for calculating their performance indicators. The calculation methods considered take into account the efficiency modulation methods, the transmission range optical signals, the received signal power taking into account atmospheric factors, and the frequency efficiency the optical transmission system.

title: Hybrid DNN and SVM: An Automated Camouflaged Weed Detection using UAV Imagery
authors: Kathirvel K, Gopika D S
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Infestation of weed is a serious challenge on agriculture particularly in sugarcane farms where weeds usually pass unrecognized because of the close similarity in the colour and texture of the weeds with that of the crop. This camouflage brings about problems in manual identification, which is always time-consuming. To address this issue, an object detection tool (DL-based), which would automatically detect small and multiple weed patches using high-resolution UAV (drone)-acquired imagery, was created. A DJI Phantom 3 drone was used to take pictures with an altitude of 50 meters to produce a ground resolution of 213 cm per pixel. These images were mapping into detailed ortho mosaic maps through its application such as Drone Deploy and Pix4D. Manual labelling and synthetic data augmentation were employed in marking the weed and non-weed areas, such as lightening, contrast, and saturation levels, to train the model. These annotated pictures were useful towards the development of a large and heterogeneous training set. The model employs the deep neural networks (DNNs) that can learn patterns automatically based on the colour and texture features in the images. Other features such as RGB and HSV colour value, grayscale variations, and texture properties (through gray level co-occurrence matrix) were applied to assist in separating the crop and the weed. The architecture of the network was created so that it had the fully connected structure and learning and enhancing within the layers of information processing as it progressed. The standard performance was used to test the model, and it classified with an accuracy of 92.3%. It spotted even under the various environmental settings small patches of weeds, more than the traditional classifiers such as SVM and Random Forest. The method offers a usable, quick and precise procedure in early detection of the weed in crops. The integration of deep learning with drone technology helps the farmers to maintain the control of the weeds effectively and accurately, without a necessity to monitor the situation manually as well as use polluting chemicals.

title: Enhancing Agricultural Productivity with Machine Learning: A Crop and Fertilizer Recommendation System
authors: Hariom Nabira, Harsh Tiwari, Mayur Kawale, Mudassir Ahmed Siddiqui, Khushboo Khurana
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Agriculture is a critical sector for food security and economic stability; however, conventional farming often depends on intuition, causing suboptimal crop choices and fertilizer use. This study presents an intelligent recommendation system that leverages machine learning to optimize both crop selection and fertilizer application. It uses a Random Forest classifier to predict the most suitable crops based on soil nutrients (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{N}, \mathrm{P}$</tex>, and K), pH, and climate (temperature, humidity, and rainfall). Given the chosen crop, it recommends fertilizers tailored to soil nutrient deficiencies. Importantly, the system integrates economic data, such as Minimum Support Prices (MSP) and relevant government subsidy schemes provided by India, to guide farmers' decisions. Experimental evaluation shows that the Random Forest model achieves 99.32% accuracy, outperforming other classifiers. These results align with those of recent studies demonstrating the high accuracy of ensemble methods in agriculture. By combining agronomic predictions with economic insights and visualizations, our system bridges precision farming and financial sustainability, aiming to empower farmers with data-driven decision-making support.

title: An Intelligent Ultrasound System using YOLO Baby for Real-Time Fetal Growth and Anomaly Detection
authors: M. Jeba Malar, V. Senthil Kumar, A. Nagadeepan, Sudeshna Baliarsingh, N. Rajesh, G. Sajiv
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Timely and accurate assessment of fetal growth and anomalies is essential for optimizing prenatal care. Traditional ultrasound examinations are often dependent on the sonographer’s experience, leading to variability in real-time interpretation. This study introduces a novel intelligent ultrasound platform leveraging a refined YOLO-Baby architecture, specifically engineered for the simultaneous, real-time identification of fetal biometric landmarks and suspected developmental defects. The detector is calibrated on a bespoke collection of annotated ultrasound sequences, allowing precise localization of critical fetal structures—including the cranium, femur, and abdominal contour—throughout the entire gestational spectrum. Quantitative evaluation reveals substantial gains relative to preceding detection pipelines, with the proposed architecture recording a mean Average Precision (mAP) of 91.7%, execution at 47 frames per second, and a 94.2% accuracy for anomaly categorization. These achievements underscore the algorithm’s prospective utility as a collaborative diagnostic aid, reducing the likelihood of operator misinterpretations and sustaining a high standard of fetal surveillance. By marrying rapid processing speed with diagnostic accuracy and clinical interpretability, the YOLO-Baby-based architecture sets a new operational reference for AI-integrated fetal echography.

title: Anatomy-Aware Deep Learning Approach for Schizophrenia Diagnosis Using Structural MRI
authors: L. Reetha, R. Gnanajeyaraman
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Schizophrenia is a persistent mental condition which is characterized by severe structural and functional impairment in particular systems in the brain, which usually results in cognitive, emotional and behavioural disorders. Extensive use of traditional diagnosis is based on the subjective assessment of the symptoms, and it can lead to the late or inaccurate determination of the disorder. To cope with this issue, the current paper will suggest an effective hybrid model of deep learning that incorporates both anatomical segmentation and classification of identifying the presence of schizophrenia on the basis of high-resolution structural MRI data. The pipeline starts off with an intensive preprocessing step that includes skull stripping, bias field correction, normalisation to MNI space, resampling to a uniform <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$128 \times 128 \times 128$</tex> voxel grid, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$z$</tex>-score intensity normalisation, producing standardised and clean input data. An Attention U-Net architecture is then used to divide the important regions of the brain like the prefrontal cortex, hippocampus, and thalamus as well as to apply attention gates to select such features that are of much interest in schizophrenia and eliminate irrelevant business information. To train a fine-tuned ResNet-18 model to binary classification (schizophrenia vs. control) with dropout regularization and gradual unfreezing strategy to optimize learning is then trained using these anatomically segmented outputs. The decisions of the model are also explained with the help of Grad-CAM visualizations to provide an idea about important discriminative areas. The proposed method demonstrates excellent diagnostic accuracy (93.4 %), F1-score <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(93.1 \%)$</tex>, and AUC-ROC (0.961) when evaluated on the OpenNeuro dataset with 176 subjects using 5-fold crossvalidation, and the result is significantly high in comparison with baseline CNN and U-Net models. This integrated assessment methodology shows both diagnostic validity and clinical elucidation and is a promising technique in objective diagnosis of schizophrenia on the basis of the image.

title: A Dual-Branch Multimodal Object Detection Framework Based on Data Fusion
authors: Li-Zi Chen, Zhi-Lin Fan, Yu-Long Wang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Object detection based on visible images is sensitive to illumination changes and complex environments. To address this issue, this paper proposes a dual-branch multimodal object detection framework that integrates visible images and LiDAR point cloud data. By projecting point clouds onto the image plane, a visible and projected cloud data fusion method is constructed to embed geometric depth information into the RGB domain. A six-channel YOLOv11-based detector with channel-level dual-branch fusion is then employed for early multimodal feature interaction, and an enhanced adaptive feature network (EAFN) is incorporated into the multi-scale neck to improve structural representation. Experiments on the KITTI dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a mAP@0.5 of 94.07% and a mAP@0.5:0.95 of 74.59%. The experiments indicate that the proposed method achieves superior performance compared with existing methods.

title: Improving Robustness of Large Language Models Used in Healthcare Through Prompt Engineering
authors: Jonathan O’Berry, Indika Kahanda, Upulee Kanewala
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have been increasingly used in a variety of domains, including the medical field. Although much research has been done on improving the accuracy of medical LLMs, very little research has been done on the improvement of the robustness of medical LLMs. This study attempts to improve the robustness of medical LLMs through prompt engineering. Specifically, this study looks to utilize few-shot prompting, providing both positive and negative examples in prompts, as input into a medical question and answering application. This study found that robustness improved as more examples were provided, though there were diminishing returns. Five-shot prompting (providing five examples) performed the best compared to the prompting with fewer shots.

title: Network Attack Sample Generation and Detection Based on Generative Adversarial Networks
authors: Haohan Xu, Heng Zhang, Jiawei Qi, Jiahong Dai
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Network attack detection is a fundamental research topic in cybersecurity, yet existing methods face challenges including attack sample scarcity and class imbalance. This paper proposes a network attack sample generation and detection method based on Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), constructing an improved Conditional Generative Adversarial Network (CGAN) to synthesize high-quality attack samples integrated with a deep neural network classifier. Experiments on the NSL-KDD dataset demonstrate that generated samples exhibit high distributional consistency with real samples, achieving a Fréchet Inception Distance (FID) of 12.847, significantly outperforming conventional GAN approaches. The augmented detection model attains 94.325% accuracy and 93.178% F1-score, with improvements of 3.892% and 4.561% over baseline methods respectively. This research provides an effective approach for addressing data imbalance in cybersecurity applications.

title: Dual-robot Engine Assembly via Sim-to-Real Reinforcement Learning
authors: Junnan Zhang, Xiumei Yin, Longlong Sun, Ke Wang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: In intelligent robotics, multi-robot coordinated assembly presents complex challenges including motion planning in shared workspace and sim-to-real policy transfer. This paper proposes a multi-robot collaboration method based on learned action masking. An engine cylinder-head assembly platform is built, where two robots with parallel grippers independently install a camshaft and a valve cover. Using deep reinforcement learning, trained policies are successfully transferred to real robots. Comparative experiments demonstrate that our approach completes the dual-robot assembly task effectively and robustly.

title: Design and Development of an Arduino-based Automated Wire Cutting System for Enhanced Industrial Efficiency
authors: Shruti Thapar, Ravi Joshi, Gori Shankar, Akansha Sharma, Yash Kumar Katara, Ravinder Singh Maan
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The industrial sector aims to make processes simpler, faster, more reliable, and highly automated to improve efficiency and productivity. This work focuses on designing and developing an automated wire-cutting system for industrial use, using an Arduino Uno and an ATmega328p microcontroller. The system reduces the need for manual labor by automatically measuring and cutting wires to the required length, saving time and effort. The Arduino Uno controls the entire operation, ensuring accuracy and precision. The system's accuracy was tested, and the mean absolute error before calibration was found to be ±0.73 cm.

title: Problem of Machine-Generated Texts in the Academic Environment of Students
authors: Anastasia V. Ermakova
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: This article examines how students are using texts generated by neural networks in the educational process, and the associated problems of knowledge assessment. It considers statistics on the use of generative models in preparing coursework, laboratory work and tests, and in creating scientific materials. The structure and characteristics of the generated responses are analyzed to identify characteristic linguistic markers and template constructions. A comprehensive approach to identifying machine-generated text has been developed. This includes analyzing style and content, comparing with previous work, verifying facts and sources, using specialized tools and testing knowledge orally. The necessity of a critical approach to the use of neural networks, the importance of maintaining academic integrity and developing independent thinking among students in the context of the active introduction of artificial intelligence are emphasized.

title: Enhancing Secure Data Transmission in IoT-Cloud Systems using Deep Belief Networks
authors: S. Murugesan, N. Ramkumar, P. Usharani, S. Ramalingam, G. Kavitha, V. Sridevi
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The security of IoT networks is becoming more important as the number of connected devices grows and data is exchanged across cloud services. These networks, which are often resource-constrained and scattered, are very susceptible to assaults. Robust security is required to ensure data integrity, confidentiality, and trust in IoT applications. This work proposes a new technique based on Deep Belief Networks (DBN) to forecast safe data transfer in IoT devices using cloud computing. The DBN model, recognized for its capacity for unsupervised feature learning and anomaly detection, is adapt at identifying possible security issues in dynamic IoT settings. The model was assessed using the Edge-IIoTSet dataset, which comprises data from both IoT and Industrial IoT (IIoT) devices and encounters various cyber-attacks, including DDoS and injection assaults. The accuracy of the proposed model was compared with existing approaches that include Artificial Neural Networks, Recurrent Neural Networks, Autoencoders, and Convolutional Neural Networks. As a result, the DBN model achieved 99% accuracy, 95% precision, 92% recall, and a 93% F1 score which is superior to previous techniques. The results show that the DBN model is efficiently used to support secure data transmission for IoT networks with its vast potential for widespread applications in real-time cloud-based IoT systems.

title: Microwave Characterization of Cork as a Sustainable Substrate for Antenna and Sensor Applications
authors: Amir Zahedi, Robert Salama, Karthick Thiyagarajan, Ranjith Liyanapathirana
publish: 2026 Australasian Symposium on Antennas (ASA)
abstract: In this paper, cork material is investigated as a sustainable substrate for antenna and sensor applications. The dielectric properties of cork, including dielectric constant, dielectric loss factor and loss tangent <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\tan \delta)$</tex>, are measured using the Agilent 85070E Dielectric Probe Kit over the frequency range of 250 MHz to 20 GHz. Based on the obtained data, an inset-fed microstrip patch antenna is designed and simulated in CST Studio Suite at 2.45 GHz to demonstrate the feasibility of cork as a sustainable and feasible substrate for antenna and sensor applications. It is found that the cork antenna can achieve electrical performance comparable to a standard FR4 substrate microstrip patch antenna at 2.45 GHz with a reflection coefficient magnitude <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\boldsymbol{S}_{11}\right)$</tex>, of -24 dB, a gain of 8.18 dBi and a radiation efficiency of 93%.

title: Smart Notice Board with Proximity and Cloud Synchronization
authors: A Shanmugapriya, Selvabalakrishnan V, Sridhan KT, Subash K, Sugan MK
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Traditional notice boards in educational institutions, workplaces, and public spaces often require manual updates, lack interactivity, and consume resources such as paper. This work introduces SmartSense, an IoT-enabled notice board system with cloud-based synchronization, smart proximity activation, environmental monitoring, and alert functionalities. Built on the ESP32 microcontroller, the system integrates a TFT display, Firebase Realtime Database, and Firebase Storage for instant content updates via mobile devices. A PIR motion sensor activates the display only when a user is nearby, while a DHT11 sensor continuously measures and displays real-time temperature on the notice board. Additionally, a buzzer module provides audible alerts for high-priority announcements. The system supports both text and image notices, offering a low-maintenance, scalable, and eco-friendly communication platform. Testing demonstrated real-time synchronization within seconds, accurate proximity detection, reliable temperature readings, and effective alerts. SmartSense can be deployed in schools, offices, and public terminals, promoting sustainable and responsive communication infrastructure.

title: AI-Powered Cybersecurity Models for Fraud Detection in IoT-Enabled Smart Healthcare Devices
authors: Nilesh Dnyaneshwar Bhandarwar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The fast expansion of IoT-enabled smart healthcare devices has raised the risk of financial fraud through unusual network transactions that in turn endanger patient data integrity and clinical operations. This research work targets the problem of identifying the most infrequent fraudulent cases in severely imbalanced IoT traffic of the real world. Traditional single models have a poor recall of the minority class, a high number of false positives that disrupt clinical workflows and limited adaptability to new attack patterns in resource-constrained environments. The stacked ensemble of XGBoost, TabTransformer and NGBoost with a logistic regression meta-learner trained on SMOTE-balanced data, as proposed, was able to achieve 96 % accuracy, 96 % macro-F1, and 0.986 AUC. By using the most important network features (DirPkt, DstJitter, Dur) it only allows 22 false positives and 14 false negatives, which makes it possible to deploy it in a lightweight manner at the edge and thus provides a reliable real-time safeguard of medical IoT ecosystems that are sensitive to security breaches.

title: Exploring Predictive Models for Lung Cancer: A Machine Learning Perspective
authors: Sanhita Kar, Debrupa Pal, Meghna Das, Chayantika Roy, Debopriya Dey, Mrinmoy Guria
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)
abstract: Globally, lung cancer is responsible for a significant portion of cancer-associated fatalities, chiefly resulting from late-stage identification and inadequate early detection approaches. This study presents an intelligent lung cancer prediction model leveraging the XGBoost algorithm, trained on a dataset of 1,000 individuals incorporating demographic, lifestyle, and medical attributes such as smoking habits, air pollution exposure, and genetic risk factors. The suggested model obtained a high test accuracy of 98.18%, demonstrating its ability to capture complex, non-linear patterns in the data. Comparative analysis with traditional classifiers like K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) and Support Vector Machine (SVM) reveals the enhanced performance of XGBoost in terms of accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. Additionally, a novel feature importance analysis combining clinical and texture-based features, along with hyperparameter optimization, enhances both interpretability and predictive power. The results highlight the potential of machine learning, particularly ensemble methods, in improving early lung cancer detection and supporting clinical decision-making.

title: Multi-Modal Agricultural Perception: Fusing Deep Optical Networks with Software-Defined Acoustic Sensing
authors: Subham Kumar Beura, Mayank Godara, Mayank Paul, Saurav Kumar, Ravi Kumar, Amanpreet Kaur
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Robots in agriculture have a problem. They cannot easily find where a fruit is in 3D space. Expensive sensors are usually needed. Most systems use LiDAR or RGB-D cameras. These are too costly for small farms. This paper tests a different method. We combined a standard camera with a software-based acoustic sensor. This finds depth without adding new hardware. First, we used YOLOv11 to find the mangoes in the image. Then, we used a smartphone's audio hardware. We used the Phyphox tool to send sound waves. This measured the distance. We found that the phone's internal audio stack was better than an Arduino sensor. The Arduino had too much timing error. Our tests show this hybrid system works. It detects if a fruit is real or a picture. It also calculates the size accurately.

title: Impact of Grid-Forming Inverters equipped with Virtual Inertia on Power System Stability: An Eigenvalue and Dynamic Simulation Study
authors: Maysam Abbasi, Yuchen Zhang, Firuz Zare, Arindam Ghosh, Kevin Paice, Sachin Goyal, Yateendra Mishra
publish: 2025 IEEE Power &amp; Energy Society General Meeting (PESGM)
abstract: In modern power systems, grid-forming (GFM) inverters are increasingly used to enhance stability, especially in low-inertia networks dominated by renewable energy sources. This study investigates the influence of virtual inertia, denoted by the parameter H in virtual synchronous machine (VSM) models, on the dynamic and stability response of a single-machine infinite-bus (SMIB) system. In PowerWorld Simulator environment, the impact of varying H under different grid strengths is precisely examined by performing the steady-state, dynamic, and eigenvalue analyses. According to the obtained results, there is a trade-off between oscillatory behavior and transient recovery, which offers insights into optimal inertia design for GFM inverters.

title: Who’s Who? LLM-assisted Software Traceability with Architecture Entity Recognition
authors: Dominik Fuchss, Haoyu Liu, Sophie Corallo, Tobias Hey, Jan Keim, Johannes von Geisau, Anne Koziolek
publish: ACM Transactions on Autonomous and Adaptive Systems
abstract: None

title: Rateless Deep Joint Source-Channel Coding for Task-Oriented Image Communications
authors: Zijun Qin, Zesong Fei, Jingxuan Huang, Jing Wang, Xianhao Chen, Zhi Zhang, Ming Xiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: The advance of vehicle-to-everything (V2X) networks has led to many emerging data-intensive applications at the network edge. To meet the soaring data rate requirements of these applications, numerous coding schemes has been developed. However, the high heterogeneity of edge users bring challenges to these methods, including adaptation to performance requirements, coping with unknown or varying channels, as well as inefficient multicasting. In this paper, we address those problems by developing a rateless deep joint source-channel coding scheme featuring fine-grained control over rate and informativeness at the user. Towards this end, we first design a novel class of variational information bottleneck (VIB) by employing the multinomial-Gaussian (MG) distribution, to achieve rateless transmission over an erasure channel. We derived important results on the statistical properties of this latent distribution to facilitate efficient training of MG-VIB. Then, we apply this framework to multicasting, proposing MG-VIB-M to enhance adaptability and scalability. Simulations show that our proposed method is more flexible regarding rate-relevance tradeoffs, has greater robustness against channel imperfections, and reduces bandwidth requirements for task-oriented multicasting.

title: Nonlinear Correction Method for Radiometric Consistency in Remote Sensing Images Based on Global Optimisation
authors: Litao Li, Haoqi Liang, Yonghua Jiang, Xin Shen
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Mosaicking multiple remote sensing images is an effective approach to rapidly expand the coverage of remote sensing imagery, and an indispensable process in large-area mapping using remote sensing imagery. Differences in sensor properties, imaging conditions, and processing algorithms cause radiometric variations, requiring consistency correction during mosaicking. However, most existing methods employ linear correction models based on statistical information between images, which are often inadequate for addressing nonlinear discrepancies caused by the above differences. This study proposes a nonlinear correction method for achieving radiometric consistency in multiple remote sensing images, based on global optimisation. A quadratic local constraint model, combined with a global mean-variance preservation constraint, ensures smooth transitions and prevents colour shifts. Validation with multi-temporal high-resolution images shows the method outperforms Wallis filtering, Histogram matching, linear correction, and TRR in handling nonlinear radiance differences. The mosaic products exhibit natural visual quality and smooth colour transitions, achieving colour balance and avoiding excessive radiometric correction. The method reduces the mean of absolute mean differences and the mean of absolute standard deviation differences by 30.5% and 44.5% versus uncorrected images, and by 17.5% and 6.1% versus linear correction. The color distance drops 81.2%, while the gradient loss remains low (0.64), preserving spectral fidelity and structural details. In terms of global radiometric stability, the method, maintaining the overall mean value above 93.4% of all the uncorrected images, an improvement of 10.3% compared to the linear method, achieving effective radiometric balance and consistency, and effectively enhancing the mapping quality of large-area remote sensing imagery.

title: SQLBarber: A System Leveraging Large Language Models to Generate Customized and Realistic SQL Workloads
authors: Jiale Lao, Immanuel Trummer

title: Torque Analysis and Optimization Procedure of Spoke-Array PM Vernier Machines Considering Flux Density Harmonic Contributions
authors: Junzhou Li, Yuting Gao, Xiaoming Zha, Zhaohua Hu, Guifen Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: This paper analyzes the torque sources of the spoke-array permanent magnet vernier machines (SPMVMs) before and after the introductions of asymmetric split-teeth and flux bridges, with emphasis on the role of flux density harmonics. It identifies a fact that their torque enhancements originate from the interaction among specific harmonics. Therefore, to maximize torque density, the key flux density harmonics responsible for torque production are first identified and set as design objectives. Then, a multi-layer high-efficiency optimization procedure that incorporates both the independent and coupled sensitivities of geometric parameters is proposed. By contrast, this method reflects the correlation among torque, key harmonics, and parameters, thereby achieving higher optimization accuracy. Besides, the harmonic products associated with the respective torque components are evaluated before and after optimization, which reveals a clear enhancement of positive-effect components and suppression of those with negative effects. Finally, a SPMVM prototype is built to verify the effectiveness of the proposed theoretical analysis and optimization method.

title: Allocation and Sizing of Distributed Energy Resources and Capacitor Banks Considering Network Reconfiguration through Multi-objective Optimization Algorithms
authors: Vanio F. S. Júnior, Renato S. F. Ferraz, Rafael S. F. Ferraz, Augusto C. Rueda-Medina, Eduardo S. Palmeira
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The incorporation of renewable energies into existing energy infrastructures plays a significant role in advancing sustainable development. One of the tools that can be employed to operate distribution networks efficiently and yield various technical advantages is the integration of distributed energy resources (DERs) based on renewable sources. However, such benefits require their installation in appropriate locations with adequate selection of their dimensions. Another approach that can be employed to ensure efficient network operation is the reconfiguration of the distribution network. Reconfiguration is regarded as an operational approach to minimize power losses and enhance network characteristics. Thus, in this paper, a multi-objective optimization approach is proposed for the efficient allocation and sizing of DERs and capacitor banks (CBs) simultaneously with network reconfiguration to reduce power losses and to minimize investment and operation costs, while ensuring compliance with the physical and operational constraints of the distribution system. The multi-objective optimization problem formulation, which includes aspects such as daily analysis, associated costs, as well as other pertinent variables, is solved using the Non-Dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm II (NSGA-II) and the Strength Pareto Evolutionary Algorithm 2 (SPEA 2), in addition to the Fuzzy Decision-making Method. Finally, the results demonstrate significant improvements in system performance as well as greater overall efficiency in energy supply compared to the original systems.

title: Model-predictive Vibration Reduction of a Suspended Cable-Driven Parallel Robot_supp1-3681158.mp4
authors: Sophie ROUSSEAU
publish: N/A
abstract: This letter focuses on the vibration reduction for Cable-Driven Parallel Robots. A Non-linear Model Predictive Controller (NMPC) is designed and implemented to adapt the control inputs sent to the robot actuators. This NMPC is based on an elasto-dynamic model of the robot, which is discretized and included in a constrained optimal control problem. Two experiments are conducted and the performance of the NMPC is compared with a Computed Torque Controller and a NMPC with an inelastic model. The first one is a trajectory following task and shows that the proposed controller significantly reduces the amplitude of the vibrations of the Moving-Platform along the trajectory and the second one focuses on a sudden braking situation and shows that the proposed control scheme manages to divide the amplitude of the post-braking oscillations by factors ranging from one and a half to twenty-two, demonstrating the interest of this controller for perturbation rejection.

title: Efficient Influential Community Search over Dynamic Graphs
authors: Youran Sun, Yingli Zhou, Yixiang Fang, Cheng Chen, Yongmin Hu, Yingqian Hu

title: A Reconfigurable Metasurface for Real-Time Dynamic Control of EM Wave in Microwave Systems
authors: Patinavalasa Megh Sainadh, Saptarshi Ghosh
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This letter presents a polarization-insensitive reconfigurable metasurface (RMS) that switches among multiple electromagnetic (EM) states using a field-programmable gate array (FPGA), tested in a parallel plate waveguide (PPW). The proposed geometry comprises metallic patterns and p-i-n diodes on the top and bottom of a dielectric substrate, enabling four combinations: <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off–off, off–on, on–off</small>, and <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on–on</small>. In the <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off–off</small> mode, transmission is achieved at 5.07 GHz with an insertion loss of 1.4 dB, whereas an absorption occurs at 5.2 GHz in <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off–on</small> mode. The reflective behavior is observed in <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on–off</small> and <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on–on</small> modes, operating up to 6 and 8 GHz, respectively. The design is analyzed using circuit modeling and evaluated under normal and oblique incidence. A 1-D array is fabricated and integrated with the FPGA to regulate the diodes in different modes. The prototype is measured using the PPW technique, and the experimental results closely align with simulations, demonstrating the effectiveness of the dynamically controlled RMS design in microwave systems.

title: Self-Supervised Multisensory Pretraining for Contact-Rich Robot Reinforcement Learning_supp1-3681156.mp4
authors: Rickmer Krohn
publish: N/A
abstract: Effective contact-rich manipulation requires robots to synergistically leverage vision, force, and proprioception. However, Reinforcement Learning agents struggle to learn in such multisensory settings, especially amidst sensory noise and dynamic changes. We propose MultiSensory Dynamic Pretraining (MSDP), a novel framework for learning expressive multisensory representations tailored for task-oriented policy learning. MSDP is based on masked autoencoding and trains a transformer-based encoder by reconstructing multisensory observations from only a subset of sensor embeddings, leading to cross-modal prediction and sensor fusion. For downstream policy learning, we introduce a novel asymmetric architecture, where a cross-attention mechanism allows the critic to extract dynamic, task-specific features from the frozen embeddings, while the actor receives a stable pooled representation to guide its actions. Our method demonstrates accelerated learning and robust performance under diverse perturbations, including sensor noise, and changes in object dynamics. Evaluations in multiple challenging, contact-rich robot manipulation tasks in simulation and the real world showcase the effectiveness of MSDP. Our approach exhibits strong robustness to perturbations and achieves high success rates on the real robot with as few as 6,000 online interactions, offering a simple yet powerful solution for complex multisensory robotic control.

title: On Weighted Optimality of Excitation Trajectory for Inertial Parameter Identification With Hierarchical Importance
authors: Ben Liu, Wei Zhang, Hua Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
abstract: This brief studies optimal excitation trajectory design for inertial parameter identification of rigid body systems. Despite extensive research on this classical problem, existing works mainly focus on designing a trajectory to ensure the accuracy of the inertial parameter estimation by treating each parameter equally important. In general, however, different tasks require different accuracy levels for different subsets of the inertial parameters. This characteristic, which involves identifying specific sets of parameters with higher accuracy for distinct tasks, has not received sufficient attention in existing works. To address this issue, we propose a novel weighted criterion to generate excitation trajectories that lead to the estimation of the inertial parameters that can adapt to different tasks requiring different accuracy levels. The proposed criterion has a nice coordinate-invariant property, which avoids the potential performance variance due to different selections of coordinate systems. It is also shown that the change of coordinates is equivalent to a coordinate-dependent weight assignment, which explains how the change of coordinates impacts the optimal excitation trajectory. Finally, experiment validations illustrate that, for different tasks, the proposed criterion outperforms other criteria used for the comparison in this brief.

title: Distributed Resource Allocation for Interference Mitigation in Heterogeneous WBANs: a Health-Adaptive Nonuniform Coloring Strategy
authors: Xiangling Peng, Xuxun Liu, Jian Su, Alex X. Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: Interference mitigation is critical for ensuring re liable communication and high quality of service (QoS) in dense and heterogeneous wireless body area networks (WBANs). However, existing approaches often struggle with the scarcity of transmission resources and the heterogeneity of traffic demands. To overcome these challenges, this paper proposes a Health-Adaptive Nonuniform Coloring (HANC) strategy, a novel distributed resource allocation approach. HANC models interference relationships as a graph where structurally central vertices serve as local decision-makers to autonomously assign channel-time resources. This strategy incorporates a token bucket mechanism to quantify traffic demand and a health evaluation model based on vital signs and clinical risks, forming a composite vertex value that determines allocation priority. HANC employs a localized coloring dissemination mechanism to enable conflict-free propagation of allocation results without global synchronization. The key innovations of HANC lie in the prioritization assignment based on real-time patient status, the nonuniform resource allocation proportional to diverse demands, and the decentralized scheduling through local decision-makers. These features collectively enhance transmission reliability, re source utilization fairness, and network scalability in dynamic environments. Extensive simulations demonstrate that HANC outperforms existing schemes in channel utilization, interference reduction, throughput, packet delivery ratio, latency, and energy efficiency, while exhibiting strong robustness in high-density scenarios.

title: NNICE 2026 TOC
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)

title: Crop Pest and Disease Detection Based on a Multi-Dimensional Improvement of the YOLOv11 Model
authors: Hongbing Ren, Meili Zhou, Zongwen Bai
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Crop pest and disease detection constitutes a core technology within precision agriculture, where its efficiency and accuracy directly impact yield and quality. However, robust detection remains challenging due to the multi-scale distribution of targets, severe class imbalance, and complex background interference in field environments. To address these issues, this paper proposes a multidimensional enhancement of the YOLO11 architecture. Specifically, we integrate the BiFPN (Bidirectional Feature Pyramid Network) with the C3k2 convolutional module to optimise feature propagation and maximise extraction efficiency across different scales. Furthermore, we introduce a novel objective function, SILDELoss, explicitly designed to mitigate the adverse effects of class imbalance and hard samples. Experiments conducted on a self-constructed dataset demonstrate that the improved model achieves an mAP@50 of 77.4% while maintaining an inference speed of 1.1 ms per frame, fully meeting real-time detection requirements. Compared to the baseline and single-improvement models, our integrated framework exhibits significant advantages in detecting small targets (e.g., aphids) and ensuring class separability under occlusion, providing an efficient solution for automated field monitoring.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning based Architecture for Cattle Disease Classification
authors: Isan Agniswar Banerjee, Aritra Rana, Shreya Roy, Koyena Paul, Md. Ashifuddin Mondal, Subhram Das, Papri Ghosh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)
abstract: In veterinary research, early and accurate detection of diseases in cattle is important for maintaining animal health and production. Traditional medical tests are often time-consuming and require expertise. This study investigates a method that combines convolutional autoencoders (CAE) and convolutional neural networks (CNN) to identify cattle diseases in order to improve accurate and correct diagnosis. CAE is used for unsupervised feature extraction, thereby reducing noise and highlighting critical patterns in disease datasets. Hierarchical learning is then used by CNN Classifiers to get high classification accuracy. The model is trained and tested on a benchmark dataset that contains images and metadata about common cattle diseases. Experimental results on detailed data of cattle images show that the hybrid model outperforms the traditional CNN model in terms of accuracy, with the hybrid CNN model demonstrating an accuracy of 92% and a RMSE value of approximately 0.35. The hydrid deep learning based architecture has proved to be a useful approach for cattle disease classification.

title: Design and Validation of Low Power High Efficient CMOS Ring Oscillator using CMOS Technology
authors: K. Srilakshmi, Kiran Mannem, K.Jamal, Manchalla.O.V.P.Kumar
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Oscillator circuits enable precise and dependable clock signals, which enable long-distance communication, in devices ranging from basic wristwatches to complex satellites. may construct an oscillator circuit in a number of methods, using either active or passive components. At this stage of mobile communication's development, compatibility and low battery consumption are paramount, yet there are advantages and disadvantages to every potential choice. By fusing the two concepts, the proposed device reaps the benefits of both a negative-skewed delay and a current-starved ring oscillator. To meet the power and frequency requirements of 5G mobile communication, this study presents a five-stage CMOS ring oscillator architecture. The suggested technique will generate a near-frequency in the Ka band. On average, the proposed method consumes 3.9 mW, with 3.92 mW going toward dynamic consumption and 4.3 mW going toward static consumption. The proposed method has the potential to produce a frequency of 30.01 GHz inside the Ka band. This gadget manages to run at high frequencies with little power consumption by combining negative-skewed and current-starved delay methods. This 45GPDK CMOS oscillator is ideal for 5G applications that demand fast, low-power clock signals since it can run on a 5V supply voltage.

title: Human Pose Recognition Algorithm Based on Radar-Visual Data Fusion
authors: Jing Zhang, Jingqi Wang, Xiancun Zhou, Jiaqi Jiang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of intelligent surveillance, smart transportation, and human-computer interaction technologies, the importance of high-precision human pose estimation has become increasingly prominent. Traditional single-sensor recognition methods exhibit significant limitations, whereas multimodal data fusion technology can effectively enhance the accuracy and robustness of pose discrimination. Specifically, LiDAR provides millimeter-level precision in 3D spatial information and depth perception, making it suitable for fine-grained modeling in complex scenarios. Millimeter-wave radar, on the other hand, demonstrates exceptional performance in adverse conditions such as rain, fog, and dust, maintaining stable detection capabilities.By fusing the complementary data from these two sensors, the system’s discriminative accuracy and adaptability in complex environments can be significantly enhanced. This research focuses on radar-vision multimodal data fusion technology. Leveraging millimeter-wave radar point clouds and LiDAR 3D data, we investigate fusion algorithms and human pose discrimination techniques. Experimental results conclusively demonstrate that the performance of multimodal data fusion technology significantly surpasses that of single-sensor methods. The findings provide theoretical support for next-generation intelligent surveillance systems and hold broad application prospects in fields such as security patrols, autonomous driving, human-robot collaboration, and smart elderly care.

title: DeepTime: Meta-Learning Temporal Dynamics for Salesforce Opportunity Forecasting
authors: Sharan Sukesh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: We present DeepTime, a meta-learning framework for forecasting Salesforce sales opportunities. DeepTime employs an inner–outer loop meta-optimization strategy to capture evolving temporal dynamics, enabling accurate prediction of deal closures and revenue projections. To enhance model robustness, auxiliary retrieval-based augmentations (QEXT and TQGEN) enrich input features, but the primary contribution is the forecasting pipeline. Empirical evaluations on Salesforce datasets show superior performance compared to baselines, improving sales resource allocation and strategic planning.

title: Physics-Based AR Framework for Realistic and Interactive Home Furnishing Visualization
authors: Anshuman Dhara, Neha Gode, Gitanjali Gangurde, Anant V. Nimkar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Traditional AR tools often have functional limitations. They typically support placing only one object at a time and do not remember object positions between sessions. These tools usually depend on simple 2D images or poorly optimized models that do not interact realistically with the environment. This paper presents a framework that uses Augmented Reality (AR) and physics based graphic technologies to achieve realistic spatial visualization. The system combines edge detection, plane detection, spatial scanning, and real time rendering to improve physical-digital interaction in augmented environments. Built with Unity 3D and AR Foundation, the framework allows for accurate placement and anchoring of 3D models in a real-world space. A key feature of the system is the support for placing and positioning multiple virtual objects at once while remembering their position and direction. Our framework uses model optimization through level of detail (LOD), physics-based rendering, and lighting techniques like diffuse and specular reflection to enhance realism. The 3D assets created in Blender are optimized for mobile performance without losing visual quality. The framework supports 3D models with realistic interactions using Unity's PhysX engine, all managed through a responsive user interface for manipulating models.

title: Traffic Signal Control Using Reinforcement Learning
authors: Sneha Shrigiri, Shreya Patil, Unnati Lunawat, Sanika Karanjekar, Riddhi R. Mirajkar, Rishikaysh Kaakandikar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive study of intelligent traffic signal control using reinforcement learning (RL). We review the need for adaptive signal control in modern urban environments and propose a system that leverages RL algorithms (Q-learning, SARSA, and PPO) to optimize intersection timing. A block diagram of the system architecture is provided, and each algorithm is described with pseudocode. Experiments in a SUMO simulation environment demonstrate comparative results, showing significant reductions in vehicle delay and queue lengths. We also discuss hardware/software requirements and a conceptual GUI for system interaction. Our findings highlight the potential of RL-based controllers to significantly improve traffic flow compared to traditional fixed-time or actuated systems.

title: Efficient Visual Cyberbullying Detection via Knowledge Distillation from EfficientNet to Lightweight CNNs
authors: Pal Dave, Subhram Dasgupta, Kushal Badal, Xiaohong Yuan, Kaushik Roy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: The rise of social media platforms has resulted in a significant rise in cyberbullying, especially through visual content that can inflict considerable psychological damage on victims. Although current methods have shown effectiveness in identifying cyberbullying via textual analysis, the detection of image-based cyberbullying continues to be a significantly underexplored and important issue. This study introduces a framework that uses knowledge distillation to develop an efficient and interpretable system to detect cyberbullying in images.
