This study employs EfficientNet-B5, which incorporates dual attention mechanisms, as a teacher model to facilitate training of a lightweight enhanced sequential CNN student model. The knowledge distillation process allows the student model to achieve competitive performance while ensuring appropriate computational efficiency for real-time deployment. To improve transparency and trust in automated decision making, we incorporate various explainability techniques such as GradCAM, GradCAM++, and LayerCAM to offer detailed insight into model predictions. The experimental results on 19,300 images indicate that the EfficientNet-B5 teacher model achieves an accuracy of 98.34%. In contrast, the enhanced CNN student model maintains competitive performance with 96.59% accuracy, utilizing significantly fewer computational resources. This reflects a mere 1.75% decrease in performance while making considerable efficiency improvements. The explainability analysis demonstrates that our model effectively identifies pertinent visual cues, including threatening gestures, offensive symbols, and contextual elements associated with cyberbullying behavior. This study addresses the increasing demand for efficient and interpretable AI systems that improve online safety and ensure transparency in automated content management processes.

title: Applying LLMs to the Classification of Cybersecurity Incident Tickets in a Few-Shot Scenario
authors: Sebastião Alves de Jesus Filho, Alvaro Santana Santos, Rafael Dias Araújo, Rodrigo Sanches Miani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: The growing complexity and volume of cybersecurity incidents have increasingly challenged Computer Security Incident Response Teams (CSIRTs), demanding more efficient solutions for alert triage and categorization. In this work, we investigate the use of large language models (LLMs), such as GEMINI 2.0 Flash and LLaMA 3 70B, for classifying real incident tickets based on the CERT.br taxonomy. We explore zero-shot, one-shot, and two-shot learning approaches to compare model performance under different input configurations. Our results demonstrate that incorporating just a single labeled example (one-shot) is sufficient to increase the average F1-score from approximately 45% to over 90%, highlighting the effectiveness of few-shot learning in this classification task. While the performance among models was similar, we find that the approach shows promise in significantly reducing the human effort required for alert triage. We also discuss limitations related to category ambiguity and data representativeness, and suggest future work, including expanding the dataset, refining the taxonomy, and evaluating new language models, potentially through local deployment for enhanced control and parameter tuning.

title: GDFP-Net: A Parallel Feature Fusion-Based Network For Automatic Vessels Segmentation From Fundus Images
authors: Nana Qi, Shenao Zhang, Dongxiang Wang, Jinke Wang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: In clinical diagnosis, the curvature, shape and spatial distribution of retinal vessels are important criteria for diagnosing diabetes. Although deep convolutional neural networks (CNNs) have achieved good results in retinal vessel segmentation, the segmentation accuracy of thin vessels is still limited due to insufficient extraction of detailed features. To alleviate the problem of detail information loss, this paper proposes a global detail feature processing network (GDFP-Net), which consists of a global segmentation network (GSN), a detail enhancement network (DEN), and a feature processing network (FPN). Among them, GSN and DEN work in parallel to enhance the feature extraction capabilities of thick and thin vessels segmentation.

title: Context-Aligned Large Language Models for Situational Awareness in Critical Grid Events
authors: Luciano A. Paniagua, Paras Mandal
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: Power system operators require real-time situational awareness to maintain grid reliability during normal and emergency conditions. During grid emergencies, the operator decision-making process is always high-stakes. Insufficient situational awareness, such as incorrect or untimely responses, can be the cause for major blackouts resulting in adverse impacts on millions of customers as well as huge economic losses. Traditional emergency management systems lack the ability to synthesize multi-source regulatory and operational information during time-critical scenarios. This paper presents a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system for power system operator decision support leveraging a Large Language Model (LLM) with domain-specific knowledge retrieval to address three operational queries: emergency procedure lookup, compliance interpretation, and event response. This involves hybrid retrieval combining sparse and dense searches over local documents, followed by prompting a local quantized LLM. Experimental results indicate that the proposed framework achieves an overall faithfulness of 0.83 and answer relevancy of 0.97, indicating a high degree of potential for improving situational awareness in critical grid operations.

title: Auto Bore Cast – A RNN Driven Intelligent Borewell Drill Estimator
authors: Elizabeth Rani G, Gowjesh P, Sakthimohan M, V. Aruna Janani, Southesh.S, Gowsika P
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Water is essential for life, and in many areas, people depend on borewells as their main source. However, drilling a borewell often feels risky. Some attempts succeed, while others fail, leading to significant financial losses and disappointment. This uncertainty arises because most drilling decisions are made without sufficient reliable information. To address this problem, we created Borewell Mapper, an easy-to-use tool that collects and analyzes data through RNN algorithm. It includes information about groundwater levels, and past borewell results. By processing this data, the system gives users practical advice on where a borewell is more likely to succeed. The goal is to reduce failures, lower costs, and encourage responsible groundwater use. With Borewell agents, farmers and communities can make smarter decisions based on data and feel more confident before they invest in borewell drill.

title: Swin Transformer with Bert Bi-Directional Co-Attention Framework for Visual Question Answering
authors: Smriti Chand, Dinesh Kumar, Manjeet Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The goal of Visual Question Answering (VQA), a cross-disciplinary task that combines computer vision and natural language processing, is to produce precise answers to questions about a particular scene that are presented in natural language. Convolutional neural networks serve as the foundation for visual feature extraction in most previous studies. In this work, we present a Swin-BERT co-attention VQA framework that combines a bidirectional encoder representation from transformers (BERT) model for capturing question semantics with the hierarchical feature extraction capability of the Swin Transformer for images. In order to efficiently integrate and refine visual and textual features, the proposed model uses a bi-directional co-attention mechanism. These features are then aggregated through pooling, combined via fusion, and classified over the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1, 0 0 0}$</tex> most common answer categories. A reduced amount of the VQA-v2 dataset-25,498 Q/A pairs for training and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3,400 \mathrm{Q} / \mathrm{A}$</tex> pairs for validation-was used for the experiments. The strength of integrating hierarchical visual transformers with transformer-based language models via a co-attention strategy is demonstrated by the suggested framework's accuracy of 72.61 %. This synergy significantly enhances cross-modal reasoning and demonstrates the importance of combining textual and visual elements to improve performance when answering visual questions.

title: Automated Fruits and Vegetables Detection: Enhancing Food Quality and Supply Chain Monitoring
authors: Suresh K, S. P. Santhoshkumar, P. Raja Packiam, Krishnamoorthi M, Saurabh Chandra, T. Nalini
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Food safety, sustainability, and quality assurance are seriously threatened by the quick deterioration of fresh produce in supply chains. The automated detection systems that combine cutting-edge sensing and imaging technologies for non-destructive quality monitoring are thoroughly examined in this paper. In order to facilitate data-driven tracing and decision support, emerging strategies that make use of data carriers, intelligent indicators, wireless sensor networks (WSN), and deep learning algorithms are investigated. Additionally, the potential of digital twin frameworks is emphasized as a revolutionary tool for simulating, forecasting, and managing the dynamic behavior of fruits and vegetables in real time. The suggested tactics provide a way to enhance food quality preservation, maximize supply chain effectiveness, and lower post-harvest losses by fusing automation, intelligent analytics, and real-time digital monitoring.

title: Post-training Quantization without BN Statistics: A Data Free Approach
authors: Akash Chavan, Sumit Kumar Jha, Sunny Raj
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Post-training quantization (PTQ) without access to real data is enabling efficient model optimization and deployment in scenarios where privacy or proprietary constraints restrict the use of original datasets. Traditional data free quantization methods rely on Batch Normalization (BN) statistics from the trained full-precision model to generate a calibration dataset for quantization. However, this reliance on BN statistics limits their applicability to deep neural networks (DNNs) without BN layers. In this paper, we propose a calibration dataset generation algorithm that is agnostic to BN statistics, leveraging just the backpropagation to create synthetic images for PTQ. We also demonstrate that it is not necessary to include an image for every target category in the calibration dataset to get the representative activation ranges for quantization. Extensive experiments with both large and lightweight models on large-scale image classification tasks demonstrate that our method consistently improves quantization performance across various DNN architectures, especially in low-bit settings. Notably, in 4-bit quantization, we achieve an improvement of 3.31% in top-1 accuracy for the ResNet18 model and 3.82% for the InceptionV3 model compared to the state-of-the-art (SOTA) DSG method. Importantly, we use very few synthetic images for quantization compared to other methods.

title: MCP-AI: Protocol-Driven Intelligence Framework for Autonomous Reasoning in Healthcare
authors: Zag ElSayed, Craig Erickson, Ernest Pedapati
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Healthcare AI systems have historically faced challenges in merging contextual reasoning, long-term state management, and human-verifiable workflows into a cohesive framework. This paper introduces a completely innovative architecture and concept: combining the Model Context Protocol (MCP) with a specific clinical application, known as MCP-AI. This integration allows intelligent agents to reason over extended periods, collaborate securely, and adhere to authentic clinical logic, representing a significant shift away from traditional Clinical Decision Support Systems (CDSS) and prompt-based Large Language Models (LLMs). As healthcare systems become more complex, the need for autonomous, context-aware clinical reasoning frameworks has become urgent. We present MCP-AI, a novel architecture for explainable medical decision-making built upon the Model Context Protocol (MCP) a modular, executable specification for orchestrating generative and descriptive AI agents in real-time workflows. Each MCP file captures clinical objectives, patient context, reasoning state, and task logic, forming a reusable and auditable memory object. Unlike conventional CDSS or stateless prompt-based AI systems, MCP-AI supports adaptive, longitudinal, and collaborative reasoning across care settings. MCP-AI is validated through two use cases: (1) diagnostic modeling of Fragile X Syndrome with comorbid depression, and (2) remote coordination for Type 2 Diabetes and hypertension. In either scenario, the protocol facilitates physician-in-the-loop validation, streamlines clinical processes, and guarantees secure transitions of AI responsibilities between healthcare providers. The system connects with HL7/FHIR interfaces and adheres to regulatory standards, such as HIPAA and FDA SaMD guidelines. MCP-AI provides a scalable basis for interpretable, composable, and safety-oriented AI within upcoming clinical environments.

title: Effective Cleaning Process of Water Heat Exchangers and Water Jackets of Motors and Generators
authors: Henk de Swardt
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: High-capacity water-cooled electric machines are critical to modern wind energy conversion systems and hydroelectric plants, where superior thermal density and compact design are essential for sustainable power generation. However, the reliability of these renewable assets is compromised by the gradual accumulation of mineral scale, which acts as a thermal insulator. Previous analysis has shown that even a 0.5 mm limestone layer can reduce the theoretical insulation life of a wind generator by up to 30%, threatening the long-term sustainability of the installation. Addressing the maintenance gap where standard hydrostatic pressure testing fails to detect this efficiency-degrading thermal barrier, this paper presents a comprehensive technical protocol for the chemical cleaning and restoration of water jackets and heat exchangers. The methodology compares acid descaling agents – including citric acid, acetic acid, and commercial blends – based on effectiveness, safety, and material compatibility, with specific attention to metallurgy common in wind turbine generators (e.g., mild steel water jackets). Cleaning protocols are proposed for both routine preventative maintenance and severe fouling conditions, incorporating safety protocols and post-cleaning verification. Implementation of these protocols is essential to safeguard the operational lifespan, availability, and energy efficiency of critical renewable infrastructure.

title: AgroBots: Revolutionizing Irrigation with Intelligence
authors: S. Thangalakshmi, M. Shunmugathammal, D. Joseph Jeyakumar, Khaja Mannanuddin, Nakka Lakshmi Srikanth, B.Venkataramanaiah
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Agriculture spends a remarkable amount of freshwater for watering the crops. This work proposes a novel approach for agricultural water management in irrigation by using robotics and the Internet of Things (IoT). Moisture level of the soil is periodically observed by soil moisture sensor which is attached with servo motor. Appropriate time irrigation is promised by the water pump and the relay attached with this soil moisture sensor that detects low moisture. IoT technology used in this system monitors the fitness of the crop and its present status could be monitored from remote location. Hardware of this project is having robot chassis which could move around with the help of motor drivers. This movement will help to navigate designated agricultural areas and irrigate precisely where it needs to be. Reducing water wastage, enhancing agricultural crop output, and reducing human involvement in irrigation are the major objectives of this work. Remote monitoring and control are made possible by the integration of IoT capabilities.

title: A Data-Driven Approach for Sleep Stage Detection Using Machine Learning and Feature Selection Algorithms
authors: Md Nahid Hasan, Md Sultan Mahmud, Clifford Jones, Saroj Baral, Khem Poudel
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: In the United States, more than 50 million individuals are affected by sleep disorders. Accurate detection of sleep stages is crucial for both personal health monitoring and social well-being. Accurate identification of sleep stages is essential for diagnosing sleep disorders and understanding sleep architecture. Traditional approaches based on polysomnography (PSG), while clinically reliable, are often costly, intrusive, and limited to laboratory settings. With the increasing availability of wearable sensors and advances in data-driven methods, machine learning (ML) offers a scalable and automated alternative for sleep stage classification. In this study, we develop and evaluate an ML framework for identifying sleep stages using physiological signals such as photoplethysmography (PPG), PSG, and PPG-PSG feature fusion. Particularly, we utilized nine statistical features with classical ML, stability selection, and PCA on top of the stability selection algorithm. Our analysis demonstrates that the PPG-PSG feature fusion achieved the highest classification accuracy of 97% in detecting sleep stages using LightGBM. Our separate analyses using the PPG and PSG showed that both modalities yielded nearly identical performance. The best classification performance was obtained with LightGBM, with an accuracy of 93.19% and 90.61% using PSG and PPG features, respectively.

title: Fault Isolation in Multi-Cell Series Battery System with Continuous Power Delivery
authors: Harzaan P, Ravishankar G, Gopalakrishnan R, Sri Vidhya D, Indumathi V, Chandrakumar E
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The dependability of multi-cellular series battery structures could be a key difficulty with programs that come equipped with electric car motivators, clean-up strength generation garage and uninterrupted strength supply systems. A single cell failure in series configuration may jeopardize the all percentage of the battery, and cause energy loss and inadequate performances and capability protection. To handle such an assignment, this paper presents a framework of fault isolation to determine stretching the non-stop transport of strengths at the time of cellular fault conditions. The project gadget combines three complementary strategies. First, fast-acting bypass switches are located all through the man or woman cells, to without delay circuit contemporary all through faulty devices, avoiding astonishing interruptions. Second, redundant/ spare cellular modules are integrated to dynamically update failed cells, thus maintaining both voltage and system potential. Third, an AI-enabled monitoring and prediction machine continuously evaluates the fitness fame of every mobile the usage of voltage, temperature and impedance information and enable early () fault detection and proactive isolation. The combination of those techniques presents a robust and scalable solution to increase fault tolerance, safety and increase the operating lifetime of battery systems. Simulation studies prove the feasibility of proposed technique, allowing the demonstration of fast fault isolation, electricity shipping and the correct prediction of cell degradation. The framework is suitable primarily for the performance of undertakings-crucial strength storage programs, in which the reliability and safety cannot be compromised.

title: An Information System Analysis and Design of Private Tutoring at PT eBdesk Teknologi
authors: Durahman Firmansyah Alamsyah, Zalfa Gita Saputri, Angelina Ervina Jeanette Egeten
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Private tutoring is a form of non-formal education that provides personalized learning support to students outside regular school hours. PT eBdesk Teknologi, a software house and big data company with an open culture, welcomes ideas to address ongoing challenges. One major issue is the lack of an integrated system for scheduling, learning material management, attendance tracking, and student progress reporting, which are currently handled manually via WhatsApp. This often leads to scattered information, missed schedules, and miscommunication between tutors and parents. This study aims to analyze and design a mobile application prototype to help private tutors manage learning activities more systematically. The analysis methodology used is the System Development Life Cycle with the prototyping model, while the design methodology applied is the Unified Modeling Language. The resulting prototype includes scheduling, material management, attendance, and reporting features to support a more organized tutoring process and ease the workload of private tutors. The final stage involves evaluation through interviews with stakeholders and users to validate the design. This research also contributes to SDG 4 (Quality Education), as it promotes inclusive and equitable learning opportunities by improving access, structure, and transparency in private tutoring.

title: Audio Feature Based Rotation Speed Extraction of Washing Machine's Dehydration Stage
authors: Binwen Luo, Cuiyun Gao, Yang Zhao, Zengyuan Wu, Mengting Wang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: In the national standard GB/T 4288-2025, measuring a washing machine’s dehydration-stage speed requires attaching sensors to the drum, which necessitates machine disassembly and thus constitutes invasive, inconvenient detection. To address this issue, this paper designs an audio acquisition system with an amplifying circuit and high-order Butterworth low-pass filter, and proposes a non-contact, non-invasive speed measurement method that extracts speed information from dehydration-stage audio signals. Methods: Frequency-domain signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) was used to compare the self-designed low-cost system with high-fidelity recording equipment; Short-Time Fourier Transform (STFT) was then applied to extract signal spectra and calculate dehydration-stage speed curves. Results: Experimental verification on a pulsator washing machine showed that the self-designed system matched the performance of high-fidelity equipment and effectively extracted speed curves, achieving an SNR of 16–17 dB in the 500–2000 Hz band and a speed curve correlation coefficient of 0.9989 with the comparison device. Conclusion: The proposed system and method are expected to be extended to speed measurement of general rotating motor equipment.

title: BRAIN-Net: CNN-based Brain Stroke Detection for Improved Diagnosis
authors: Meriga Kiran Kumar, G. Zayaraz
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Stroke remains a major worldwide cause of death and chronic disability, highlighting the significance of early and accurate detection to improve patient outcomes and reduce healthcare burdens. This paper presents BRAIN-Net, an advanced automated brain stroke detection system utilizing deep learning techniques, specifically a CNN-based approach, for enhanced diagnostic accuracy and reliability. The suggested framework makes use of machine learning algorithms to classify ischemic and hemorrhagic strokes based on imaging data from Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) and Computed Tomography (CT) scans. By leveraging Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), BRAIN-Net efficiently extracts critical features for early stroke detection and precise classification. The model achieves an impressive 98% accuracy, with sensitivity at 97%, specificity at 96.5%, and an F1-score of 97.2%. These findings reveal better diagnostic accuracy than conventional methods, and thus BRAIN-Net is a potential tool to assist clinicians with swift and accurate stroke diagnosis, enabling timely intervention and significantly enhancing prognosis in patients. BRAIN-Net, an optimized CNN-based deep learning framework, detects ischemic and hemorrhagic strokes from CT and MRI scans with superior accuracy, outperforming traditional models.

title: Deep Learning for Automated Lung Nodule Detection in Low-Dose CT Scans
authors: Santosh Reddy P, Geeta L S
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: One of the biggest causes of cancer-related death globally is still lung cancer. Survival chances are greatly increased by early diagnosis of lung nodules with Low-Dose Computed Tomography (LDCT) screening. However, interpreting a hand LDCT scan is subjective, takes a lot of time, and is easily affected by differences between observers, especially when noise as well small tumours are present. It is the goal of this project to make a reliable as well as automated deep learning-based system for accurately identifying lung nodules in LDCT images. The study's goal is to create a 3-dimensional Convolutional Neural Network (3D-CNN)) structure that uses volumetric spatial data to better detect small lumps and handle noise. This is how the network will be built and tested to make sure it can reliably find lung nodules. We test the suggested system using the open-source LIDC-IDRI dataset. The data is enhanced, and a thorough preparation pipeline is implemented, which includes filtering out Gaussian noise, segmenting the lungs, and normalizing the intensity. The Adam optimizer & binary cross-entropy loss are used to train a 3D-CNN model using <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$64 \times 64 \times 64$</tex> voxel volumetric patches. The F1-score, AUCROC, sensitivity, specificity, and accuracy are the performance measures used. The suggested model has a great diagnostic performance, with an AUC of 0.94 as well as excellent sensitivity, especially for small nodules (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\geqslant 3 ~\text{mm}$</tex>). Qualitative study backs up the idea that robust detection works even when LDCT is noisy. The suggested system is a dependable computeraided detection tool that may help radiologists find lung cancer early, cut down on the amount of effort they have to do to get a diagnosis, and help them make better clinical decisions.

title: Collaborative Opportunistic Maintenance Scheduling for Offshore Wind Farms Considering Cognitive Correction
authors: Chengze Rong, Zhenxiang Xiao, Liangwei Xue, Zhenyu Wu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: High operation and maintenance (O&M) costs and harsh marine environments pose significant challenges to offshore wind farms (OWFs). Existing maintenance strategies often rely on imperfect state information, leading to sub-optimal scheduling. To address these issues, this paper proposes a dynamic collaborative O&M scheduling framework utilizing a heterogeneous fleet of Crew Transfer Vessels (CTVs) and Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs). First, a reliability model based on the Weibull distribution is utilized to characterize the degradation process of wind turbines. Second, a cognitive correction mechanism is introduced, where UAV inspections dynamically update belief states and trigger event-driven re-planning when cognitive bias exceeds a safety threshold. Finally, a rolling horizon optimization model based on Deferral Opportunity Cost is established to balance deterministic dispatching costs against probabilistic future risks. Simulation results on a 40-turbine OWF demonstrate that the proposed method reduces total O&M costs significantly compared to blind scheduling and reactive strategies, effectively minimizing downtime while ensuring high reliability.

title: An Imbalance-Aware Machine Learning Approach to Credit Card Fraud Detection Using SMOTE & AdaBoost
authors: Priyanka Kaushik, Atul Kumar Mishra, Anjna Kumari, Rahul Pradhan, Payal Gosai, Mahalakshmi Sampath
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Online banking & transactions have caused a high adoption rate of credit cards, which has made life easier but on the other hand, it has caused the increase of fraudulent practices & financial losses. Conventional rule based fraud detection systems are not able to keep up with the changing trends of fraud. The paper will present a machine learning-based fraud detection model based on Support Vector Machine, Random-Forest, Logistic Regression & Decision Tree models. SMOTE & AdaBoost are used to solve class imbalance & maximum accuracy of detection. The experiments show that the suggested method will greatly enhance the detection of scam credit card transactions. The model demonstrates a high possibility of effective fraud detection in dynamic & complicated web-based transaction setup to improve the safety of online payment systems.

title: On the Task of Detecting Defects in External Structures of Telecommunications Facilities
authors: Kseniya Chepenko, Yuri Leokhin, Timur Fatkhulin
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: This paper examines existing and promising methods for detecting various defects in the external structures of telecommunications facilities. The purpose of this work is to define criteria for selecting appropriate methods for examining and analyzing defects in the external structures of telecommunications facilities. The relevance of this research is determined by the need to promptly identify and prevent potential problems caused by leaks, deformations, and loss of thermal insulation in telecommunications infrastructure facilities. Such phenomena lead to negative consequences, including deterioration in the technical condition of equipment, increased operating costs, and potential risks of emergency situations. The object of this study is the external structures of telecommunications facilities. The subject of this study is the criteria for selecting the appropriate defect detection method. The research methodology consists of methods of theoretical analysis, comparison, description, systematization, and generalization. Finally, conclusions are drawn and recommendations are given for selecting appropriate methods for detecting various defects in the external structures of telecommunications facilities.

title: A Bipolar Stochastic Bitstream Generator Based on Linear Feedback Shift Register
authors: Jian Chen, Yujie Wang, Yi Wang, Yaohua Xu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Stochastic computing (SC) is an emerging approximate computing paradigm that processes data in the form of stochastic bitstreams. The bipolar representation is widely adopted in SC systems to handle data ranges that include negative domains. However, existing designs for converting binary real values to bipolar stochastic bitstreams often suffer from complex circuit structures, high hardware overhead, and low conversion efficiency. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a novel high-accuracy enhanced bipolar stochastic number generator (EB-SNG). By leveraging an optimized comparison logic circuit, the proposed EB-SNG can efficiently map binary values directly into bipolar stochastic bitstreams. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed EB-SNG maintains superior conversion accuracy while significantly reducing hardware resource consumption. Compared with traditional sign-magnitude-based SNG designs, the EB-SNG achieves a better balance between circuit area and power consumption, providing an efficient interface solution for low-power stochastic computing systems.

title: GAIN-Trans: A Hybrid GAN-Transformer Framework for Robust Missing Data Imputation in Stock Market Time Series
authors: Ananya Raj, Mahadev Ajagalla, Vaishnavi Bisen, Mukesh Kumar Bharti
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Missing values in financial data arise from trading suspensions, illiquidity, and data-quality issues, and traditional statistical or purely reconstruction-based deep models often fail to preserve complex temporal dependence and distributional characteristics. GAIN-Trans extends the Generative Adversarial Imputation Networks paradigm by embedding both the generator and discriminator in Transformer encoders, allowing adversarial distribution matching while self-attention layers capture long-range temporal and cross-asset relationships. The generator combines observed entries and noise under a mask-aware Transformer to produce imputations, and the discriminator, equipped with a hint mechanism, learns to distinguish observed from imputed values, with training driven by a joint adversarial and reconstruction loss. Experiments on stock time series with varying missing rates compare GAIN-Trans against statistical, machine learning, GAN-based, and Transformer-only baselines using imputation error and downstream forecasting performance as metrics. Results indicate that GAIN-Trans achieves consistently lower reconstruction error and more realistic volatility and correlation structures, particularly at high missingness levels, demonstrating its effectiveness as a robust imputation tool for financial time series analytics. We further analyze training stability and computational overhead, showing that the proposed hybrid model remains tractable and stable for realistic financial datasets.

title: A Comprehensive Analysis of ML-Based Approaches for IC Yield Prediction
authors: Vishakha Bisouriya, Hrishikesh Das, Sandesh Jain, Manisha Pattanaik, Alok Kumar Kamal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Integrated Circuit (IC) yield prediction has become increasingly challenging due to complex and nonlinear interactions inherent in advanced semiconductor manufacturing processes. Existing deep learning-based approaches often suffer from high computational complexity and are sensitive to choice of various hyperparameters. To circumvent this limitation, we propose two low complexity machine learning (ML) approaches such as random forest and XGBoost for wafer-level yield prediction problem. Simulations performed over real manufacturing data indicate that the proposed random forest and XGBoost algorithms deliver better convergence performance compared to the existing support vector regression algorithm.

title: Deep Hybrid Vision System for Automated Clinical Grading of Diabetic Retinopathy
authors: N. Subhash Chandra, Yerram Sneha, Lakavath Rajitha, Nitesh Gaikwad, Ashok Kumar Vootla, Kokkula Shiva Prasad
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: DR is one of the most significant problems of diabetes and the largest causes of blindness in the world, so the correct assessment of its stages, Mild, Moderate, Severe and Proliferative, is critical in terms of ensuring timely treatment. The word manual grading of fundus images is labor intensive and subject to variability, which provides the need to rely on good automated systems. The proposed project will utilize a hybrid deep learning architecture that is based on a Vision Transformer (ViT) more than a ResNet to identify DR stages. ResNet takes the local features like the microaneurysms and hemorrhases whereas ViT uses the global retinal structure and allows the model to examine both the micro-level and the macro-level of the image. The system was also trained and tested on more than 500 images in one stage and preprocessing and data augmentation were used to improve the performance. The model was trained based on cross-entropy loss and Adam optimizer and had an accuracy of about 100 percent, indicating that there was little misclassification between stages. All these findings suggest that the proposed method can be an efficient automated DR severity grading tool that would assist ophthalmologists in performing early diagnosis, disease progression, and treatment plans.

title: Design and calculation of LLC resonant converter
authors: Anima Kumari, Matta Mani Shankar, Mukhlesur Rahman
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)
abstract: This paper presents a comparison of PID, and Fractional Order PID (FOPID) controllers used in LLC resonant converters designed by Half Bridge (H-B) and Full Bridge (F-B). An evolutionary optimization framework based on Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) and the traditional Ziegler-Nichols (Z-N) method are applied to tune both controller types. Closed-loop simulations based on MATLAB/Simulink are used to analyze dynamic performance considering rise time, settling time, and percentage overshoot as performance metrics. The findings show that PSO-tuned controllers significantly increase response speed and reduce overshoot, especially for FOPID control in Full-Bridge topology. These results support to improving power electronic converter control strategies through the application of soft computing techniques.

title: Digital Pose Trainer for Posture Correction and Rehabilitation in Physiotherapy
authors: Vivek Kumar M, Monika Shree S M, Nandhini S, Nithiya R, Rajashree P
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The goal of the new developed website AI Physio Trainer is to assist doctors in organizing the patient’s physical therapy process by using the benefits of online tools. The use of body movement tracking through a camera in the system also safeguards the patients as the exercises are done correctly to avoid injury. Another essential part of the concept is that doctors would be able to set up personalized exercises for every patient due to employing AI Physio Trainer. The possibility to get feedback and track the results on the chosen treatment course also contributes to the fact that doctors can set measurable goals for the patients, which can be useful for assessment of the patient’s condition change in the course of time. But also, patients can be engaged with the AI Physio Trainer where the program is designed to have his or her own program, and this will keep the patient motivated and relaxed as they recover from their injuries.

title: Junctionless FinFET-based Hydrogen Gas Sensors: A Comprehensive Review of Design, Performance, and Reliability
authors: Deborah Roseline P, Kavitha V P
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: This comprehensive analysis investigates current developments in junctionless FinFET-based hydrogen gas sensors, with an emphasis on design innovations, performance improvements, and reliability challenges. The research explores various methods, including ferroelectric gate stacks, negative capacitance integration, spacer-engineered SOI structures, and strained silicon designs. The study evaluates electrical characterization, sensitivity analysis, and reliability assessments across multiple studies, drawing attention to noise-dependent sensitivity and the impact of gate oxide on device reliability. This review offers a distinctive viewpoint on the present situation and potential future developments of junctionless FinFET-based hydrogen gas sensing technology by combining results from several study undertaking. The focus on junctionless FinFET architectures, a more recent development in gas sensing applications, is what makes this paper distinctive. The applicability of different sensor configurations for specific applications is evaluated, along with associated trade-offs. It provides a comparative analysis of several sensor architecture, such as JL, JLNC, MFIS/MFMIS, and SJL structures, assessing their suitability for particular use cases and performance attributes. The study examines simulation-based sensors, focusing on those that make use of Technology Computer-Aided Design (TCAD) tools. The identification and investigation of reliability challenges in nanoscale gas sensing, such as noise-related problems, aging effects, and gate-induced drain leakage (GIDL), is a major emphasis of this paper. The study investigates how different design factors and material selections affect the sensitivity, reaction time, selectivity, and long-term stability of sensors. For increased sensitivity in applications, the combination of Pt/Pd and SiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> is given particular attention. This review provides engineers and researchers involved in the field of advanced gas-sensing technologies with a comprehensive understanding of the most recent advancements and potential research avenues in junctionless FinFET-based hydrogen gas sensors by combining insights from various research efforts.

title: Revolutionizing Accessibility with Multi-Modal Text-to-Speech Conversion
authors: Malathi.M, P. Nagarajan, N. Ashokkumar, M.Bharathi, Rahul Krishnan, Kavitha Thandapani
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: With the help of OCR features, this article discusses the development of accessibility enhancements with the invention of a new multi-modal text to speech (TTS) system which allows users to easily convert text from images and web articles into audio format. Students, professionals, and just about anyone can access the system and takes full advantage of the features it offers. Using translation and language detection, the system guarantees that the extracted text is coherent and linguistically appropriate. The system addresses two very important problems: first, users who do not speak the language of the content can translate it to the language they prefer, and second, in a dynamic and fast paced society where every second counts, users can transform articles or online newspaper links into audio format designed for easy listening. The clear alterations in OCR accessibility encourage equal opportunities and inclusivity, allowing users to engage with information when needed.

title: AI-Enabled Innovation Management
authors: Aaryan Gupta, Divya Thakur, Kushagra Shekhar, Aditya Kumar Singh, Naman Sharma, Tushar Chahar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This paper provides a detailed examination of the use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) to transform innovation management practices. This paper examines how organizations adopt AI technologies like machine learning, natural language processing, and predictive analytics into their innovation processes to boost efficiency and effectiveness. The paper starts off with explaining why innovation management in modern-day businesses is important and demonstrates how AI is supporting primary phases of the innovation life cycle, such as ideation, product development, and market intelligence. With an inclusion of history, existing patterns, and real-life examples, this research elucidates the game-changing potential of AI to lead innovation. Subsequently, the paper explores the organizational challenges, ethical implications, and technical restrictions on the deployment of AI. The paper concludes by highlighting emerging trends in AI and research gaps, providing useful insights for future AI-enabled innovation management advancements. The results highlight the need for companies to strategically implement AI technologies to remain competitive and promote sustainable innovation.

title: Fake Currency Detection using Image Processing and Machine Learning
authors: Payel V L, S L Saran Kumar, Chayanam Karthik, Bhavana V
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Free circulation of fake money is a significant threat to the financial systems. The present paper contains a multifaceted and fully automated approach to the detection of fake Indian 500 and 2000 notes based on image processing and machine learning. The system derives both structural and visual characteristics i.e. SSIM scores of templates at template areas, Hough Transform based pattern bleed lines, and area based serial number characteristics. A Random Forest classifier is used to train on the features and classify the notes as the real or the fake ones. The model has a total accuracy of 98 per cent on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$500 / 2000$</tex> denominations. Precision, recall, and F1-score equal 0.97, 1.00, 0.99 for fake and 1.00, 0.89, 0.94 for real in case of notes of 500. The scores that the fake yield against the real are respectively <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.97,1.00$</tex> and 0.99 and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.00,0.90$</tex> and 0.95 respectively in the case of Rs. 2000 notes. A real-time detection interface that is based on uploading image and classifying the image is created. The presented technique, which is based on edge finding and object identification in a supervised machine, is better than the classical template matching techniques and operates effectively in both controlled and various situations. Besides, the system is light and can run on low resources which ensures its implementation on mobile devices or in the public kiosks. The transparency in the decision making is provided due to the use of interpretable features that makes the user understand why a note has been labeled as genuine or counterfeit. Compared to the previous counterfeit detection methods, this paper proposes an interpretable pattern of feature movement based on the use of SSIM patterns, bleed-line edge density,and serial-number structure, which is lightweight and can achieve real time deployment without deep learning.

title: Artificial Intelligence and Unemployment in Indonesia with a Panel Data Approach
authors: Nur Azmi Karim
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This study examines the influence of artificial intelligence on unemployment in Indonesia. Comprehensive empirical studies are limited in the literature. Therefore, this study uses a dataset, which covers 34 provinces from 2013 to 2022. Researching the use of computers to explain AI and the unemployment rate. In an empirical approach, we control the dynamic effects of unemployment by using panel data to determine the effect of AI on unemployment. As a variable control, we use economic growth. We use unemployment as a dependent variable and AI as an independent variable. We assume that current unemployment is affected by unemployment in previous periods. All data in this study uses secondary data obtained from the website of the Central Statistics Agency for several years from 2013 to 2022. This study uses panel data which is a combination of cross section data and time series data using the eViews 13 analysis tool. To facilitate cross section data in this study, a sequence number is used to mention the name of the province being analyzed. To facilitate the use of provincial data in Indonesia, the author provides a sequence number code for each province The results of this study are that the unemployment rate of the previous year and economic growth have an effect on the current unemployment rate, while AI has not yet had an effect on the unemployment rate.

title: Bio-Inspired Variance Control: Decoupling Signaling Mode from Power Constraints in Neuromorphic Systems
authors: Rakesh Sengupta
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Advanced intelligent systems and IoT networks face a critical trade-off between signaling flexibility and power efficiency. Traditional rate-coding schemes often require shifting the mean activity to transmit information, incurring high energy costs that threaten system stability. Drawing inspiration from the Log-Normal firing regimes of the cerebral cortex, we propose a novel coding mechanism: Homeostatic Variance Control. We mathematically demonstrate that by modulating the variance of input noise (a proxy for neuromorphic gain), a system can dramatically shift its modal (most frequent) operating point toward sparsity while pinning its median resource consumption to a fixed homeostatic setpoint. We validate this mechanism using a Linear-Nonlinear-Poisson (LNP) simulation, showing that it enables a seamless transition between dense and sparse coding regimes without altering the baseline power budget. This bioinspired architecture offers a rigorous framework for designing resilient, energy-efficient Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) for resource-constrained cyber-physical applications.

title: Quadrifilar Helix Antenna Satellite Receiver Antenna
authors: Himanshu K H, Kanishk R M, Jagadish R, Mohammed Ataur Rahaman, Lakshmi C R, Nandini Kumari B
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The development of a Quadrifilar Helix (QFH) antenna for real-time weather data acquisition from Meteor-M satellites is a challenging area in the satellite industry. Design and Performance of a Quadrifilar Helix (QFH) Antenna for Meteor-M 2-3 LRPT Reception at 1 3 7. 9 MHz is presented in this paper. The Quadrifilar Helix (QFH) antenna optimized for receiving the Low-Resolution Picture Transmission (LRPT) signal from the Meteor-M 2-3 and Meteor-M 2-4 polar orbiting weather satellite is discussed. The system operates at the satellite's primary downlink frequency of 137.9 MHz in the VHF band. The QFH antenna is designed to efficiently receive the satellite's Right Hand Circularly Polarized (RHCP) signal. For the signal reception software defined radio, RTL-SDR is used with software such as SDR++, Sat dump to process satellite signals.

title: Advanced Synergetic and Lyapunov based nonlinear control for PEM Fuel Cell System
authors: Sheraz Ahmad Babar, Eleonora Riva Sanseverino, Rossano Musca, Marco Ferraro
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: Proton Exchange Membrane Fuel Cell System (PEMFCS), working principle is highly nonlinear in nature. The existing literature strategies are mostly based on nonlinear Sliding Mode Control (SMC). Performance is compromised due to the SMC inherent chattering phenomenon in power generation. Although higher versions SMC handled the chattering problem but with much increased computational complexity. This article aims to provide nonlinear based control strategy with moderate mathematical complexity and improved stability for maximum power extraction from PEMFCS. In this paper, the nonlinear Synergetic Controller (SC), Integral Synergetic Controller (ISC) and Lyapunov Redesign Controller (LRC) have been proposed to stabilize partial pressures of hydrogen and oxygen to protect PEMFCS from membrane damage during power generation. The stability of proposed controllers has been proved using Lyapunov theory, while the simulation results in MATLAB/Simulink show the outperformance of the proposed controllers in comparison to the existing SMC approach.

title: Neural Network–Based Temporal Alignment of Heterogeneous Multisensor Signals
authors: Lei Wang, Yuxiang Sun, Jun Bao
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Multi-sensor fusion is a critical foundation for collaborative robot perception and control systems. However, differences in sampling frequency, communication mechanisms, and timestamp accuracy among heterogeneous sensors often lead to temporal misalignment of multi-source data. This paper proposes a time alignment method based on feature-guided interpolation and neural network correction, addressing the time synchronization issue in CAN and serial mixed data acquisition. The method uses high-frequency reference signals to construct a unified time axis and adapts to correct the residual time offset of low-frequency pressure sensors. Experimental results show that the proposed method outperforms traditional alignment methods in terms of time consistency and physical reasoning, improving system robustness and stability.

title: RadixGraph: A Fast, Space-Optimized Data Structure for Dynamic Graph Storage
authors: Haoxuan Xie, Junfeng Liu, Siqiang Luo, Kai Wang
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
abstract: None

title: DI3CL: Contrastive Learning With Dynamic Instances and Contour Consistency for SAR Land-Cover Classification Foundation Model_supp1-3678005.pdf
authors: Kai Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: Although significant advances have been achieved in SAR land-cover classification, recent methods remain predominantly focused on supervised learning, which relies heavily on extensive labeled datasets. This dependency not only limits scalability and generalization but also restricts adaptability to diverse application scenarios. In this paper, a general-purpose foundation model for SAR land-cover classification is developed, serving as a robust cornerstone to accelerate the development and deployment of various downstream models. Specifically, a Dynamic Instance and Contour Consistency Contrastive Learning (DI3CL) pre-training framework is presented, which incorporates a Dynamic Instance (DI) module and a Contour Consistency (CC) module. DI module enhances global contextual awareness by enforcing local consistency across different views of the same region. CC module leverages shallow feature maps to guide the model to focus on the geometric contours of SAR land-cover objects, thereby improving structural discrimination. Additionally, to enhance robustness and generalization during pre-training, a large-scale and diverse dataset named SARSense, comprising 460,532 SAR images, is constructed to enable the model to capture comprehensive and representative features. To evaluate the generalization capability of our foundation model, we conducted extensive experiments across a variety of SAR land-cover classification tasks, including SAR land-cover mapping, water detection, and road extraction. The results consistently demonstrate that the proposed DI3CL outperforms existing methods. Our code and pre-trained weights are publicly available at: https://github.com/SARpre-train/DI3CL.

title: ExplainChain-SE: A Lightweight Benchmark for Explainable Debugging with Reasoning-to-Repair Alignment
authors: Ilia Sedoshkin
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Many automated debugging tools can repair faulty code but often fail to explain why their fixes make sense. This reduces developer trust and limits our ability to study how reasoning connects to repair success. We introduce , a lightweight framework and benchmark that links short natural-language explanations to verified fixes across well-known bug–fix datasets which has three main components: a Bug Analyzer that identifies likely fault locations and suggests possible causes, a Fix Generator that applies small, rule-based edits and records a concise rationale, and a Verifier that checks whether the fix works and measures how closely the explanation matches the result. We evaluate on QuixBugs, Defects4J, and a CodeXGLUE refinement split (Bugs2Fix). The system outputs detailed logs containing the buggy code, the reference fix, the generated fix, the reasoning text, and validation scores. In experiments, generated repairs closely matched reference fixes on Bugs2Fix (mean ≈ 0.97) and Defects4J placeholders (mean ≈ 0.87), while QuixBugs showed moderate variation (mean ≈ 0.80) due to its diverse algorithms. All runs finished quickly and produced reproducible metrics and plots. By combining reasoning with verification, provides a transparent, reproducible foundation for explainable debugging. It is simple to extend with more datasets, richer test cases, or large language model–based fix generators, paving the way for deeper research into reasoning-aware program repair.

title: AI is a Memory Hog: Its Demand Threatens Whole Categories of Electronics
authors: Samuel K. Moore
publish: IEEE Spectrum
abstract: If it feels these days as if everything in technology is about AI, that's because it is. And nowhere is that more true than in the market for computer memory. Demand, and profitability, for the type of DRAM used to feed GPUs and other accelerators in AI data centers is so huge that it's diverting memory away from the supply needed for other uses and causing prices to skyrocket. According to Counter-point Research, DRAM prices rose at least 80 to 90 percent in the first quarter of 2026. • The largest AI hardware companies are likely first in line for new memory, but that leaves everybody else—makers of PCs, smartphones, TVs, and everything else that needs to temporarily store billions of bits—scrambling to deal with scarce supply and inflated prices. • How did the electronics industry get into this mess, and even more important, how will it get out? IEEE Spectrum asked economists and memory experts to explain. Today's situation, they say, is the result of a collision between the DRAM industry's historic boom-and-bust cycle and an AI hardware infrastructure build-out that's without precedent in its scale. And, barring some major collapse in the AI sector, it will take years for new capacity and new technology to bring supply in line with demand. Even then, prices might stay high. • To understand both the supply and the demand ends of this episode, you need to understand its main culprit: high-bandwidth memory, or HBM.

title: News
authors: Ned Potter, Charles Q. Choi, Matthew S. Smith, Lawrence Ulrich
publish: IEEE Spectrum
abstract: Artemis is NASA's bid to reclaim its heritage, to resume the business of human exploration, to take astronauts to the moon and beyond, and to win what's been billed by some U.S. politicians as a “new space race,” with China. Artemis II is planned as the project's first circumlunar test mission with a crew. If it succeeds, and if NASA can deliver on a December executive order from the Trump administration, Artemis astronauts will land near the moon's south pole by 2028 and start building a lunar base by 2030, steps to ensure, as the order says, “American space superiority.”

title: A Bidirectional Integrated Equalizer Based on CLLLC Converter for Series-Connected Supercapacitor String
authors: Lizhou Liu, Jingsheng Shang, Wangxin Zhang, Xueying Yu, Zhipeng Chen, Yi Zhou, Shi Chen, Tianlei Zang, Buxiang Zhou, Kaigui Xie
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Traditional integrated voltage equalization circuits with compact size and low cost. However, each secondary winding can only balance individual cells, and although capable of charge/discharge balancing, cannot achieve autonomous self-balancing functionality. To overcome their shortcomings, an integrated balancing topology based on a CLLLC resonant converter is proposed in this article. First, each secondary winding attains dual-cell balancing capability by incorporating a voltage doubler circuit that capitalizes on the symmetrical voltage ripple characteristics inherent to full-bridge CLLLC resonant converters. This innovative configuration effectively doubles the voltage equalization capacity while maintaining identical transformer winding quantities. Second, the zero-voltage switching (ZVS) across all MOSFETs throughout charge and discharge cycles through meticulous topological optimization of the CLLLC converter topology is guaranteed based on the operational mechanism of the integrated balancer. This inherent soft-switching characteristic enables the system to maintain autonomous self-balancing functionality even under idle operating conditions. Experimental validation was conducted to demontrate that the transferred energy is effectively balanced during charging, discharging, and idle conditions by the proposed integrated balancing circuit on a system with six supercapacitors. Peak efficiencies of 92.47% (18.96 W), 92.97% (12.34 W), and 92.67% (9.87 W) are demonstrated in the charge, discharge, and self-balance modes, respectively.

title: Scalar Precoding for Topological Interference Management with Heterogeneous Erasures
authors: Peixin Chen, Junge Wang, Syed A. Jafar
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: The problem of scalar topological interference management with heterogeneous erasures (STIME) is formalized, by adopting a one-shot model that favors ultra low latency communication, partially connected interference network topologies that are motivated by directional transmission, limited channel knowledge at the transmitters that is restricted to the network connectivity, erasures that model sporadic disruptions that are detectable at the receiver, and a performance metric that is closer to Hamming than Shannon, seeking the shortest multiuser-code that is robust to a set of specified erasure patterns for each user. In the absence of erasures, STIME reduces to the scalar TIM (STIM) problem which, based on prior work, corresponds to scalar index coding, and as such admits a well known minrank optimal solution. By the same token, STIME can be viewed as essentially a scalar index coding problem with heterogeneous erasures at each receiver. The optimal solution to STIME is bounded within a factor of 2 based on its connections to STIM for heterogeneous erasures. The bounds are shown to be tight in several cases, e.g., for topologies where alignment graphs have no internal conflicts, for neighboring interference models, and for arbitrary topologies if the erasure thresholds are identical across users. A mapping from any STIME setting to a corresponding STIM setting is identified such that the two problems have the same solution.

title: RSMA-Enhanced Robust Weighted Secrecy Rate for HRIS-aided Near-Field Communications
authors: Sandeep Singh, Keshav Singh, Sandeep Kumar Singh, Fan-Shuo Tseng, Cunhua Pan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: This study investigates the performance of rate splitting multiple access (RSMA) in a holographic reconfigurable intelligent surface (HRIS)-aided downlink near-field (NF) secure communication system with imperfect channel state information (iCSI) in the presence of an eavesdropper (Eve). In order to provide efficient resource utilization, a weighted sum secrecy rate (WSSR) maximization problem is formulated, where the transmit beamforming vector at the base station (BS) and the HRIS phase profile are jointly optimized under the strict constraint of quality of service (QoS) requirement and total power budget at the BS. Since the optimization problem is non-convex due to the coupling of the variables, we propose an iterative algorithm based on alternating optimization (AO) that jointly optimize transmit beamforming at the BS and beamforming at the HRIS. Various numerical results are shown to validate the effectiveness and convergence of the proposed algorithm. Furthermore, we also discuss the impact of the key system parameters, such as HRIS reflecting elements, minimum QoS constraint, transmit power budget, and number of users. The dominance of RSMA over its counterparts, non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) and space-division multiple access (SDMA), is also demonstrated. It is shown that the use of RSMA can achieve up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$77\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> higher data rate than NOMA and SDMA. It is also highlighted that, under the proposed algorithm with NF assumptions, the achieved average WSSR is around <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$86\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> higher than the hybrid and far-field (FF) assumptions. In addition, we compare the system performance under the assumption of perfect CSI (pCSI) and iCSI.

title: Modeling the Changes in the Current–Voltage Characteristics of Vertical GaN-on-GaN p-i-n Diodes Under Electrical Stress
authors: Ambra Maria Vianello, Simone Leonardo Longato, Manuel Fregolent, Fabiana Rampazzo, Matteo Buffolo, Dawei Wang, Bingcheng Da, Houqiang Fu, Shisong Luo, Cheng Chang, Yuji Zhao, Isabella Rossetto, Carlo De Santi, Gaudenzio Meneghesso, Enrico Zanoni, Matteo Meneghini
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: We analyze and model the changes in the electrical properties of vertical GaN-on-GaN p-i-n diodes submitted to electrical stress. The analyzed devices were treated with hydrogen plasma to minimize surface leakage. Constant-current stress tests were carried out to investigate the stability of the electrical characteristics of the samples. The results indicate the existence of two phases: 1) for short stress times, we detected a lowering in the turn-on voltage (V<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\text {ON}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>), with no significant variation in series resistance, which was ascribed to an increase in injection efficiency at the anode contact and 2) for longer stress times, the turn-on voltage stabilized, but the series resistance showed a measurable decrease (−35%). Based on TCAD simulations, the observed behavior is ascribed to the diffusion of hydrogen from the p<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">+</sup> contact layer toward the junction. A model based on Fick’s second law was developed, finding good agreement with the experimental data.

title: Intent-Driven 6G Communication Framework for RIS and Spectrum Leasing
authors: Zawar Hussain, Naveed Ul Hassan
publish: IEEE Communications Letters
abstract: Intent-Driven Communication (IDC) is emerging as a key paradigm for autonomous 6G networks, where AI and Large Language Models (LLMs) translate high-level user intents into actionable network policies. Meanwhile, Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS) and dynamic spectrum leasing are becoming essential for improving coverage and capacity in resource-constrained environments. This paper extends the IDC framework by integrating RIS and spectrum leasing into AI-assisted intent translation, policy mapping, and orchestration. A leasing-aware architecture is presented, and a Lyapunov-based Decision Support Framework is implemented as an illustrative mechanism for intelligent resource acquisition under time-varying prices and availability. Simulation results validate that the DSF achieves cost-efficient, delay-aware orchestration while exhibiting the expected Lyapunov stability properties. These findings highlight the feasibility of combining IDC with intelligent resource leasing in future 6G systems.

title: Hardware-Friendly Algorithm Optimization and Acceleration for 8K UHD Image and Video Broadcasting
authors: Hang Yuan, Wei Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Broadcasting
abstract: Efficient implementation plays a critical role for real-time 8K Ultra-High-Definition (UHD) video broadcasting with the new generation video coding standards. Specifically, the more complex Coding Unit (CU) partitioning rules in new standards make it much more challenging to design efficient acceleration strategies. This paper proposes a light-weight hardware-friendly acceleration algorithm for CU partitioning, which is the bottleneck to implementing the Versatile Video Coding (VVC) based real-time intra codec for 8K video broadcasting on hardware platform. According to the CU partitioning rule, we first propose an novel One-Pass framework to determine CU split results, which can effectively reduce time complexity and hardware resources incurred by the original iterative decision process. Second, taking into account the characteristics of hardware platform, the efficient calculation methods based on Map-Reduce model and hardware-oriented optimizations are designed for gradient and histogram difference features required by our One-Pass framework. Finally, we design a reference hardware architecture to validate the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm to support real-time 8K video broadcasting. Besides, a light-weight 8K UHD video dataset including diverse content is also provided to more reliably illustrate the coding performance of our algorithm. Experimental results show that our method is capable of the real-time CU division on Xilinx VU440 FPGA at 200 MHz, where the single-path can support 8K videos at 45 frames per second (fps), and 135 fps also can be achieved with multi-path parallelism. As far as we are aware, the proposed algorithm has attained state-of-the-art performance for the hardware-friendly acceleration of CU partitioning with the least loss in VVC standard.

title: Path Planning Considering Artificial Potential Fields Based on Trajectory Prediction
authors: Yining Xing, Yiqian Tu, Yuning Wang, Mengchi Cai, Jianqiang Wang, Lu Geng, Miao Jiang, Kinoshita Masafumi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: With the development of deep learning technologies, the accuracy of trajectory prediction has significantly improved. Leveraging predicted trajectories for motion planning can reduce the risk of collisions between vehicles more effectively. However, prior studies have shown that directly using predicted trajectories in planning often leads to suboptimal performance and may even cause collisions. To address this, we propose a novel framework that integrates trajectory prediction with artificial potential fields (APFs), enabling the effective use of prediction results in planning. In this paper, we introduce a trajectory planning framework that constructs a spatiotemporal risk (STR) field based on deep learning-based trajectory prediction and inter-vehicle interactions. The planning module then employs an optimization-based approach that considers multiple objectives, including safety, comfort, rule compliance, efficiency, and human likeness. Evaluated on a real-world dataset, our method achieves an 8.72% improvement in minimum Anticipated Collision Time (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\text{ACT}\_{\min }$</tex-math></inline-formula>) and a 36.0% gain in efficiency compared to the baseline Driving Safety Field (DSF) method. Compared with other competing approaches, our method improves <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\text{ACT}\_{\min }$</tex-math></inline-formula> by 10.67% while maintaining competitive performance in terms of comfort, efficiency, and human likeness. The results from the NuPlan closed-loop experiments indicate that our method exhibits superior planning capabilities in complex scenarios, surpassing the performance of all baseline methods. Furthermore, closed-loop experiments demonstrate that our approach enables continuous and collision-free planning in highly interactive traffic scenarios.

title: iDiffODE: Learning Continuous-Time Latent Dynamics for Generative Spatiotemporal Modeling
authors: Sai Deepthi Yeddula, Abhijeet Bhattacharya, Chen Jiang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Irregularly sampled time series are prevalent in domains such as healthcare, wearable sensing, and geospatial event monitoring. These datasets pose significant challenges for traditional time-series models, which assume uniform sampling and struggle with missing or delayed observations. In this work, we present iDiffODE, a novel continuous-time generative model that explicitly constructs smooth latent trajectories from irregular observations, enabling both accurate reconstruction and realistic simulation of multivariate system states. We evaluate our method on three representative datasets demonstrating consistent improvements over the existing methods. Our results demonstrate that iDiffODE and it’s model variants effectively learn continuous latent dynamics and generate time series data even under conditions of severe irregular sampling, positioning it as a highly promising solution for handling irregular time series data in the real world with enhanced generalizability. Github: https://github.com/Abhijeet399/iDiffODE

title: Adversary-Aware Synchronization Framework for Entanglement-Assisted QKD: Models, Taxonomy, and Adaptive Windowing
authors: Sanjay Kumar, Neeraj Kumar, Ketan Anand
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Synchronization is a fundamental requirement for the reliability and security of entanglement-assisted quantum key distribution (QKD), where accurate coincidence detection directly impacts both the secret key rate and robustness against adversarial interference. Existing synchronization approaches typically assume benign timing environments, leaving open the possibility of subtle yet impactful adversarial attacks on the temporal domain. In this work, we introduce an adversary-aware synchronization framework for entanglement-assisted QKD that systematically models timing threats and proposes resilient countermeasures. First, we present a formal system model capturing the timing dependencies of entangled-photon-based QKD protocols. We then introduce a taxonomy of timing adversaries, including passive noise amplifiers, delay injectors, jitter inducers, and desynchronization attackers, each characterized by distinct operational footprints and security implications. To mitigate these threats, we propose a preliminary adaptive windowing algorithm that dynamically adjusts coincidence detection thresholds based on realtime statistical estimates of photon arrival distributions. Initial analysis and simulations demonstrate that the proposed adaptive strategy outperforms fixed-window synchronization under both natural noise and adversarial timing manipulations, achieving improved key rates while maintaining security guarantees. These results highlight the need for adversary-aware synchronization as a critical component of next-generation entanglement-assisted QKD systems.

title: Superior Safety Helmet using a Standard Wearing Behaviour Detection Model based on Enhanced YOLOv5
authors: D. Bujji Babu, A. Suneetha
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: This study presents YOLO-ESCA, an advanced standard helmet-wearing detection model designed to address incorrect helmet usage among workers. Based on an improved YOLOv5n model, the approach enables real-time compliance monitoring using UAVs and other methods, reducing detection errors in video streams. Incorporating EIOU-loss, Soft-NMS non-maximal suppression, CBAM, and a short target detection layer (ADL), the model was trained on a custom dataset consisting of 4,400 pictures. Experimental results demonstrate an mAP@0.5 of 94.7%, an FPS of 65.3, and a lightweight model size of 4.47 MB. Detection performance improved by 41.7% and 73% on custom and SHWD datasets, respectively. Extensions include integration with YOLOv5x6, YOLOv8, and YOLOv9 for enhanced accuracy, along with a user-friendly Flask-based front end featuring secure authentication.

title: Tuning of Integrated Terahertz Disk Resonators via Contactless Evanescent Loading
authors: Bryce Chung, Jordan Nedosyko, Withawat Withayachumnankul, Daniel Headland
publish: 2026 Australasian Symposium on Antennas (ASA)
abstract: We introduce a viable mechanical tuning technique for the dispersion of terahertz dielectric waveguide-based passive devices. Evanescent interaction with a movable conductive surface imposes a null on tangential electrical fields, which affects the dispersion of guided waves. Varying the position of this null allows us to tailor the waveguide's dispersion as-desired. As a proof-of-concept of this effect, we demonstrate motorized frequency-tuning of an integrated terahertz micro-disk resonator, yielding a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 2.5 \%$</tex> blue-shift in the resonance frequency. This span is greater than the frequency-spacing between adjacent resonances, meaning that a resonance can be tuned to any specific frequency within the operation bandwidth.

title: A Novel Metamaterial- Integrated Compact Multiband Antenna with Wideband Characteristics Using Intelligent Optimization Technique
authors: Sameer Purohit, Rakesh Singh Rajput
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The innovative metamaterial- Integrated inspired compact multiband antenna with wideband properties is reported in this paper, which was designed with the goal of optimizing the next-generation wireless communication systems using the intelligent Random Forest-based techniques. This is the case with the proposed antenna which will be able to effectively miniaturize through the incorporation of engineered metamaterial components and demonstrate multiband and extended bandwidth of impedance. To optimize the important geometrical parameters of the antenna, a datadriven optization framework based on the random forest is used to provide an incentive in the gain, return loss, and impedance matching at lower complexity. This antenna is modeled and analyzed on CST Microwave Studio and its structure is very compact that can be implemented in latest portable gadgets. It has been shown in simulation that it operates over the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 0 - 2 8 ~ G H z}$</tex> millimeter-wave spectrum with discrete resonant frequencies at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$21.8 \text{GHz}, 24.26 \text{GHz}$</tex>, and 26.53 GHz. The antenna has a voltage standing wave ratio of less than 2 and a better return loss (S11) of less than [?]10 dB in all bands of operation.
