The proposed antenna design has a better overall bandwidth of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5 5 2. 3 4 ~ M H z}$</tex> and stable radiating features compared to traditional designs. The above findings validate that, the proposed metamaterial- Integrated antenna is a potential candidate in the 5 G millimeter-wave and future application of high-data-rate wireless communication.

title: Optimized Framework for Merging Navigable Small-World Graphs: Balancing Efficiency and Search Performance
authors: Priyanka Muppuri
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Navigable small-world graphs have emerged as fundamental structures for efficient a pproximate n earest neighbour (ANN) search in high-dimensional spaces, with applications spanning recommendation systems, image retrieval, and natural language processing. However, the challenge of merging such graphs while preserving their navigability and search efficiency <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r$</tex> emains <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$l$</tex> argely <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$u$</tex> naddressed. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$T$</tex> his <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p$</tex> aper introduces a novel optimisation framework for merging navigable smallworld graphs that strategically balances computational efficiency with search performance. We propose three progressive merging algorithms-Naive Merge, Merge1, and Merge2-that employ localised proximity-based neighbourhood construction and adaptive exploration-exploitation strategies to minimise redundancy and ensure seamless graph integration. Comprehensive experimental evaluation on the Sift1M dataset demonstrates that our advanced merging algorithms reduce computational effort by up to 5x compared to baseline approaches while maintaining competitive search accuracy. The proposed framework provides a scalable solution for dynamic and distributed graph-based systems requiring efficient index maintenance and consolidation.

title: The Effect of Depth of E-Commerce Reviews on Trust through Utilitarian and Hedonic Values
authors: Bima Dwi Putra Widyanto, Ferrel Marcello Jonathan, Muhammad Itsar Zuhdi Afwashiyam, Artha Sejati Ananda
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Online reviews have been important in influencing purchasing decisions on customer-to-customer (C2C) e-commerce. Consumers often perceive these reviews through two main dimensions, utilitarian value and hedonic value. On the other hand, brands in C2C e-commerce often have trust problems as perceived by the customers. This paper has a purpose to investigate the effect of perceived values of the reviews -through utilitarian value and hedonic value- on trust, with depth of review as the antecedent variable, in the context of a fashion store (Erigo) on Shopee Indonesia e-commerce. It adopts a quantitative approach using the stimulus-organismresponse (SOR) framework, where depth of reviews function as stimuli, perceived values as organisms, and trust as a response. Data were collected through convenience sampling from 221 respondents who had shopped at the Shopee Official Store of Erigo. Data analysis was analyzed using the Partial Least Squares-Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM) technique. The results of the study indicate that review depth has a significant positive influence on perceived utilitarian value and perceived hedonic value. Both values also positively influence trust. These findings provide theoretical and practical contributions, especially for online businesses on e-commerce, by emphasizing the importance of encouraging consumers to provide informative and accurate reviews to create long-term trust from future customers. This research represents one of the pioneering studies that explores the linkage between depth of reviews and trust, mediated by reviews' perceived utilitarian and perceived hedonic values within the C2C e-commerce context.

title: Contextualizing Decisions in the Monarchy of Healthcare through Traceable Patient-Level Evidence
authors: Gaurav Sharma, Rahul Kumar, Yashi Tiwari, Sarthak Garg, Mohammad Ammar, Dattatraya Parle
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Clinicians often deal with lengthy, unstructured patient records extending across multiple PDF reports, laboratory findings, diagnostic descriptions, radiology summaries, medication prescriptions, and multi-visit clinical narratives. This type of information review is burdensome to do manually and is cognitively exhausting, especially in high-stakes environments with a short time window to make decisions. While Large Language Models (LLMs) are remarkably good at reasoning and summarising, directly applying them to entire medical documents is expensive, slow, and deprived of the traceability necessary in healthcare settings. We present a patient-personalised RetrievalAugmented Generation framework that incorporates metadataaware ingestion, Milvus-based vector retrieval, hierarchical clustering, and automated PDF evidence highlighting. Unlike generic RAG pipelines, our system retains patient-specific metadata, supports page-level explainability, and generates transparent, auditable responses. Extensive evaluations on multi-page clinical documents show significant improvements in response latency, token cost reduction, and clinician workflow efficiency. The system presents a practical and explainable solution for realworld clinical decision support.

title: Exploring the Viability of Space on Wheels: Trends, Preferences, and Market Factors Shaping Mobile Science Education
authors: Hannah Mae R. Bertulfo, Julianne Grace C. Gomez, Jessica P. Tumambing, Aldrich Ralph C. Divinagracia, Veronica Jones M. Magno, Jaypy T. Tenerife
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: The study is a market feasibility survey to assess the commercial potential of Space on Wheels, a mobile space education vehicle. The goal was to understand trends and factors that could guide the design and marketing of a tech-based, accessible educational museum aligned with user preferences. Using a mixed-method approach, we gathered both qualitative and quantitative data. The survey explored interest in space education, engagement with technology, support for sustainability, and perceptions of affordability and accessibility. Multivariate analysis examined how these factors relate and influence potential participation. Findings confirm the feasibility of the concept in the target market. Respondents showed strong interest in space education and a positive view of tech-enabled learning. Affordability, accessibility, and sustainability also emerged as key concerns. Several consistent and unexpected factors influenced willingness to engage in mobile science programs. Overall, the study points to a high level of market readiness and offers a foundation for expanding similar educational models to rural and underserved areas. Recommendations are provided for future initiatives and research on mobile, inclusive learning platforms.

title: Evaluation of Companding Technique for Signal Integrity
authors: Pranav Arakkal, Rehan George Mathew, Sreyas Sreekumar, Anuraj K, Poorna S S
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Maintaining signal clarity in analog communication systems remains a persistent challenge due to noise, distortion, and limited dynamic range. This work presents the design, simulation, and hardware implementation of a low-cost analog communication system that employs companding and amplitude modulation (AM) to preserve signal integrity. The architecture integrates a logarithmic amplifier for dynamic range compression, a transistor-based AM modulator, a diode-based envelope detector for demodulation, and an antilogarithmic amplifier for signal expansion. By compressing weaker signals prior to transmission and expanding them upon reception, the system improves Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) and reduces the impact of channel noise. Simulations in LTspice and hardware testing using discrete components confirm the system’s ability to reconstruct the original signal with high fidelity. Notably, an input signal with 28.5dB SNR yielded a recovered signal with 27.8dB SNR demonstrating minimal degradation despite the use of minimal circuitry. The simplicity and effectiveness of this design make it ideal for resource-constrained analog applications such as public address systems, wireless microphones, and educational setups, where real-time performance, noise resilience, and hardware feasibility are critical.

title: Research on Indoor Obstacle Avoidance Method of UAV Based on Reinforcement Learning
authors: Juan Ding, Fei Hu, Quan Zhang, Zixi Lin
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Aiming at the complex obstacle avoidance problem of unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) operating in indoor environments without GNSS signals, this paper proposes an indoor UAV obstacle avoidance method based on deep reinforcement learning and the DAC-DPPO (Dynamic-Adaptive Clipping Distributed Proximal Policy Optimization) algorithm. This method adopts a distributed proximal strategy optimization algorithm with a dynamic adaptive clipping mechanism, which can accelerate training speed, enhance the adaptability of the strategy network model under different starting points (environmental conditions),so as to improve the overall obstacle avoidance ability performance of UAV. And an online UAV model parameter identification method is proposed, which can quickly identify the model parameters of the real UAV and import them into the simulation platform. The effectiveness of the method is verified by Simulink simulation experiments and real flight data experiments, which provides excellent technical support for the reinforcement learning training of UAV in indoor environments.

title: Adaptive Multi-Kernel Attention Framework for Multimodal Healthcare Data Integration and Disease Classification
authors: Arun Singh Yadav, Surendra Kumar, Shubham Shukla, Soumya Shrivastava, Jaydip Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Disease subtype classification requires effective integration of heterogeneous medical data such as imaging, genomics, and clinical records. However, existing multimodal fusion methods treat all subtypes uniformly, limiting their ability to capture subtype-specific patterns essential for accurate diagnosis. This paper proposes an adaptive multimodal fusion framework that leverages subtype-aware Gaussian kernel learning and a KernelAdaptive Cross-Modal Attention (KACA) mechanism to model nonlinear, subtype-discriminative relationships across modalities. A Subtype Gating Network dynamically modulates modality relevance, while a multi-task objective with an MMD-based alignment loss reduces cross-modal distribution gaps. Experiments on ADNI, TCGA-BRCA, and MIMIC-III demonstrate an average Macro F1-score of 80.7%, outperforming MMHA (75.0%) and CentralNet (74.1%), including a 12.3% gain for BRCA subtyping. The model also achieves a 49.5% reduction in modality divergence, highlighting its robustness in heterogeneous clinical environments. Overall, the proposed approach enhances subtype-aware representation learning and improves diagnostic precision.

title: YOLO-FM: A Method for Detecting Pine Wilt Disease Infected Timber Based on Optimized YOLOv11
authors: Ye Xie, Jianghua Li
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: To address the challenges of large target scale variations, weakened early disease features, and frequent false and missed detections in complex forest backgrounds in UAV-acquired imagery, this study proposes an improved pine wilt disease–affected tree detection method, termed YOLO-FM, based on an optimized YOLOv11 framework. Taking YOLOv11s as the baseline, the network architecture and loss function are systematically enhanced while maintaining inference efficiency: a Feature Pooling Enhancement Unit (FPEU) is integrated into the C3k2 module to strengthen fine-grained and small-scale target representation, a CBAM attention mechanism is introduced between the backbone and neck to suppress complex background interference, and the ShapeIoU loss is adopted to replace CIoU for improved bounding box regression accuracy and training stability. Experimental results on the PWD dataset demonstrate that YOLO-FM outperforms YOLOv5s, YOLOv8s, and the original YOLOv11s in terms of precision, recall, and mAP, achieving mAP@0.5 and mAP@0.5:0.95 of 85.6% and 39.9%, respectively, with a reduced parameter count of 8.8 M. These results indicate that the proposed method achieves a favorable balance between detection accuracy and model lightweightness in complex forest environments, providing effective technical support for intelligent monitoring and precise control of pine wilt disease.

title: VehicleTracker: A Real-Time ALPR System for Multi-Point Vehicle Tracking
authors: Ishwar Tilkari, Anuj Sarda, Krishna Deokar, Nirmalya Mukhopadhyay, Kunal Kundu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: With the rapid expansion of urban infrastructure and rising concerns over vehicle theft, designing surveillance systems that are not only accurate but also privacy-preserving and computationally efficient has become a critical area of research. Traditional Automatic License Plate Recognition (ALPR) systems often struggle to maintain a balance between real-time performance and environmental robustness, or they rely heavily on expensive cloud-based services that introduce latency and data sovereignty issues. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a novel, end-to-end surveillance framework named VehicleTracker, developed as an edge-native alternative to complex multi-stage pipelines. Our approach integrates the YOLOv5 architecture for rapid object detection with a hybrid Optical Character Recognition (OCR) engine to ensure robust multi-point tracking and geospatial visualization without internet dependency. We conduct a comprehensive comparative analysis by evaluating system latency, feature coverage, tracking continuity, and resource efficiency against state-of-the-art baselines, including GRU-based and multi-stage YOLO systems. Extensive simulations and realworld benchmarks validate the superiority of VehicleTracker, which achieves a recognition accuracy of 88.9 % and a low processing latency of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 35 \text{ms}$</tex>, outperforming traditional architectures in resource-constrained environments. The experimental results demonstrate that our system provides a scalable, cost-effective, and secure solution suitable for modern Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS).

title: WindFLOW: A Wind Foundation Model for Generative Temporal Super-Resolution
authors: Cong Feng, Rui Yang, Pranav Sharma
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: Wind power plants are increasingly expected to provide grid services such as operating reserves, ramping support, and headroom-based dispatch, all of which depend critically on accurate wind variability at minute-level time scales. However, most available wind datasets and operational forecasts are limited to coarser resolutions, creating a fundamental data gap for grid service quantification. To address this challenge, this paper introduces a novel temporal wind super-resolution framework based on time series foundation models, named WindFLOW. We leverage the pretrained TimesFM Transformer with a three-layer fully-connected neural network head to downscale 5-minute wind speed into 1-minute sequences. The WindFLOW method is evaluated using over 58 million minutes of wind observations from seven wind sites. Results demonstrate that WindFLOW consistently outperforms state-of-the-art deep learning models and classical interpolation methods across all sites, with a 0.39 m/s average mean absolute error. The WindFLOW model achieves superior pointwise accuracy while uniquely preserving empirical distributions and high-frequency spectral energy. These findings indicate that time series foundation models can reliably reconstruct minute-scale wind dynamics from coarse inputs and enable scalable high-resolution wind data generation to enhance grid reliability through grid services.

title: A Hybrid AI-Powered Framework for Accurate Urban Flood Prediction and Real-Time Early Warning Systems
authors: Prashantha Kumar Kemmannu, M. Udayakumar, V. Tamilselvan, Sree Lakshmi Lingineni, Ankit Kumar, P. Mano Paul
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Urban flooding presents increasing threats to densely populated cities as a result of climate change and urbanisation. Current flood prediction models often show deficiencies in real-time accuracy and do not deliver timely notifications, hence constraining disaster preparedness. This proposed study presents a hybrid AI system that combines Gradient Boosted Decision Trees with a CNN-BiLSTM ensemble to analyse spatial-temporal patterns from different datasets. The system, employing rainfall and water-level data from Kaggle, attains exceptional performance metrics: 97.83% accuracy, 98.15% recall, and an AUC-ROC of 0.993, markedly exceeding baseline models such as Random Forest and LSTM. The framework's real-time alarm distribution improves responsiveness, potentially minimising flood-related damages and fatalities. This methodology enhances flood prediction through the integration of explainability, accuracy, and operational security, providing it an essential instrument for urban disaster management.

title: Uncertainty-Aware Federated Learning for UAV Predictive Maintenance: Confidence-Based Aggregation and Risk-Aware Scheduling
authors: Tanisha Pal Singh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Reliable health monitoring is essential for modern UAV fleets, yet real operational data are often heterogeneous, noisy, and difficult to centralise due to bandwidth and privacy constraints. This paper presents an uncertainty-aware federated learning framework for remaining useful life (RUL) prediction, combining LSTM-based temporal modelling with Monte-Carlo dropout, confidence-based aggregation, and risk-aware client scheduling. To emulate a distributed UAV environment, the NASA C-MAPSS turbofan dataset is partitioned into four clients per subset, introducing realistic non-IID behavior. Experimental results across all four datasets show stable improvements in global model performance, with RMSE and MAE consistent with dataset complexity and confidence-interval coverage ranging from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 3}-\mathbf{8 9} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex>. Analysis of uncertainty indicates that aleatoric components dominate long-horizon predictions, while the risk-aware scheduler successfully down-weights low-quality clients. These findings demonstrate that the proposed framework can support distributed predictive-maintenance workflows and provide reliable uncertainty estimates even without centralised data. Future extensions will explore communication-efficient FL updates and transfer to real UAV propulsion datasets.

title: Enhancing Crop Disease Identification Using an Ensemble of Pretrained CNN Feature and SVM
authors: Nadeem Akhtar, Manish Mandloi, Rehan Ahmad, Rashmi Bhattad
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Accurate and timely detection of crop diseases is essential for enhancing yield and achieving food security, for smooth grains like Millet and Pearl Millet (Bajra). Many traditional image analysis techniques tend to be subjective, time-consuming and prone to inconsistencies due to the overlapping disease symptoms. Though there have been encouraging instances of the successful use of deep learning algorithms for the detection of crop diseases, these tend to pose issues related to their computational complexity and intra-class variability. This article uses five pre-trained architectures are used for feature extraction and an SVM is used for classification. The proposed approach is validated on two publicly available datasets of Millet and Bajra. The achieved result indicate that the Dense Net and Efficient Net outperform all other individual feature extractors with an accuracy of up to 99.41% on Bajra and 98.23% on Millet datasets. Moreover, the ensemble approach with the majority voting rule greatly increases the classification performance up to 100% on Bajra and 96.05% on the Millet dataset, decreasing the false positive rate. These experiments validate that combining these varied deep feature learning results using an ensemble approach has a significant enhancement effect on both the generalization capability and class-specific robustness, especially when diagnosing disease classes considered more challenging. The proposed approach provides a practical, time- and resource-efficient strategy for automated disease diagnosis on crops.

title: Synthetic Lipid Liquid Coolant Ratio Algorithm based on Multi-Objective Optimization and its Application in GPU Cluster Cooling
authors: Ruichen Bai, Zijie Lin
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: For the liquid cooling system of high-performance GPU clusters, this study proposed a new dynamic multi-objective optimization algorithm D-MOOSA based on Pareto front evolution. The algorithm is used for adaptive optimization of the ratio and flow distribution of synthetic lipid liquid coolants. The study first established a fluid mechanics and heat transfer coupling model of the liquid cooling system, and constructed an equivalent thermophysical property model of the synthetic lipid coolant. This model includes specific heat capacity, viscosity and thermal conductivity. Based on this, this study designed a multi-objective optimization function. This function covers three levels: minimizing thermal resistance, minimizing pump power consumption, and minimizing environmental impact indicators. D-MOOSA achieves dynamic response through sub-population co-evolution, boundary change criteria and "big blood transfusion" mechanism. This optimization can cope with GPU cluster load fluctuations and local hot-spot migration. The experimental platform was a four-node GPU training cluster (architecture: 8 GPUs per node, total power consumption approximately 11.2 kW). Tests were conducted under long-term full load (W1), peak load (W2), and memory-limited (W3) workloads. Static ratio screening results showed that optimizing the coolant ratio can achieve the best compromise between temperature rise and pump power. Under a 6-hour comprehensive workload, the proposed D-MOOSA method achieved Tmax=65.6°C and T99=67.4°C. This result is a reduction of 3.8°C and 4.5°C, respectively, compared to the fixed ratio strategy. At the same time, the thermal resistance Rth was reduced to 0.0150 K•W⁻¹, and the cooling energy consumption Ecool was reduced to 6.63 kWh. During the 72-hour continuous reliability test, the system only received two pump cavitation warnings during low-load switching. The research results show that D-MOOSA can achieve efficient thermal management of GPU cluster liquid cooling systems under the multi-objective constraints.

title: A Correlation-Aware and Dynamic Multi-Level Fusion Approach for Multimodal Relation Extraction
authors: Jing Liu, Jie Wang, Huili Zhang, Haifang Mo
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Multimodal relation extraction integrates text and visual information to identify relationships between entities. However, in real-world scenarios like social media, many images or objects are irrelevant to the text, creating noisy interference that limits model generalization. To address this core challenge, this study proposed a Correlation-Aware Dynamic Multi-Level Fusion framework. The framework incorporated three key designs for effective noise suppression and feature fusion. First, a lightweight module assessed the semantic correlation between textual and visual features. Second, a dynamic weighting mechanism adjusted the fusion weight for visual features based on the correlation score. Third, a multi-level fusion architecture performed cross-modal interaction within intermediate Transformer layers, balancing modal heterogeneity and feature complementarity. Experiments on the MNRE dataset demonstrated that the proposed method significantly improved relation extraction performance across diverse scenarios. Ablation studies confirmed the collaborative effectiveness of each component.

title: A Machine Learning Framework for Identifying and Modeling Speed Governing Deficiencies in Hydropower Units
authors: Soumyadeep Nag, Richard Tapia, S M Shafiul Alam
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: A common notion is that all hydropower units have active speed governing capabilities. However, this capability can be deactivated due to varying reasons. To uncover and quantify the existence of such units in the Western Electricity Coordination Council (WECC), this paper performs a data-driven analysis utilizing open-source and restricted datasets. A decision tree ensemble-based machine learning algorithm is designed to estimate governor models for the identified units. After updating the WECC power system files with estimated governors the impact on frequency profile is quantified. Results indicate that there are 182 hydropower units in the WECC PSS/E format power system dynamic data file that do not have governor models (representing their in-field speed regulating incapability). 74% of these units are less than or equal to 20MW. It was observed that an additional 150MW to 200MW of response could be added which improved frequency nadir and frequency sensitivity.

title: YOLOv8-Nano Driven Foot Stampede Prediction with Homography Mapping
authors: J Umamageswaran, CH. Gowtham Sri Satya, C. Karthik Reddy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Stampedes in crowded places may lead to serious injuries and deaths, and hence early detection and prevention are major concerns. This paper proposes a computer visionbased real-time foot stampede prediction system using YOLOv8 Nano and homography transformation. The system first uses YOLOv8-Nano: A Lightweight Yet Powerful Object Detection model to identify and track people in video streams. To account for perspective distortion, homography is used to project the camera view to a top-view representation of the scene, allowing spatial crowd distribution is to be estimated accurately. From the reconfigured viewpoint, the parameters include crowd density, velocity, and density-velocity ratio are computed to determine crowd dynamics. A possible stampede is anticipated when high density and low mobility are both present simultaneously, which indicates critical pressure build-up. The system is not using any other sensors or prior knowledge of the environment like traditional methods; on the contrary, it calibrates via homography every video stream and adapts to it. The system is scalable, lightweight, and it can be incorporated into real-time crowd monitoring systems, which will give authorities pre-warnings and enable them to take proactive measures to prevent stampedes.

title: AI-Enhanced Soil Microbial Diversity Analysis for Sustainable Agriculture Practices
authors: R. Hema, S Murugan, Santosh Kumar Sahoo, R. Jagadeesh Kannan, Dennis Surendar, Lokeshwaran N
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Investigating soil microbial diversity is crucial for improving agricultural sustainability, given that soil microbes significantly contribute to nutrient cycling, pest management, and plant development. Traditional techniques for analyzing microbial populations are often arduous and protracted. Recent breakthroughs in artificial intelligence (AI), particularly Graph Neural Networks (GNNs), provide a viable method for enhancing the efficiency and precision of microbial diversity investigation. GNNs are engineered to represent complicated connections in data by modeling things, such as microorganisms, and their interactions as graphs, effectively capturing microbial communities' complex networks. This paper proposes using AI-augmented soil microbial diversity analysis using GNN to promote sustainable farming practices. Utilizing GNNs to create a framework that predicts microbial behavior, identifies changes in microbial communities, and provides insights into effective soil management practices. This system enables real-time monitoring and personal recommendations for soil health management. Incorporating AI-driven microbiological analysis may enhance crop yields, reduce environmental impact, and provide more effective resource utilization, fostering a sustainable and resilient agricultural future.

title: Post Fault Stability Assessment in Distribution System Using Sliding RMS and Phase Angle Methods
authors: Nimai Charan Patel, Bhabasis Mohapatra, Ritesh Dash
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Post fault stability assessment is a critical function in modern power distribution system more particularly in the presence of variable loads and increasing penetration of renewable energy sources. This research paper proposes a simple effective methodology to assess the stability of a single-phase distribution system during the fault condition. The approach is based on, two core signal analysis such as sliding root mean square voltage calculation and phase angle tracking by using dynamic phasor analysis. In order to showcase the realtime application line to ground fault is introduced in a single-phase system between 0.06 seconds and 0.11 second. Voltage waveforms are generated and analyzed in three different stages i.e. Pre-Fault, During Fault and Post Fault. The sliding root mean square method is applied over a moving window to detect sudden voltages dip and to monitor the recovery trend after the fault is cleared. Similarly, the phase angle variation is computed from the Fourier Spectrum to capture the dynamic behavior of the voltage signal. Cross correlation is applied to estimate the delay, which can indirectly indicate the location of the fault.

title: Knowledge-Injected Optimization Framework for Heavily-Constrained SPMT Layout of Offshore Decks
authors: Xiaorui Zhai, Shengli Wang, Qianran Zhang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: In modern offshore engineering, the layout planning of Self-Propelled Modular Transporters (SPMTs) for large deck modules is an extremely complex system engineering task, characterized by high dimensionality, heavy constraints, and computationally expensive "black-box" optimization features. Traditional trial-and-error methods struggle to achieve an optimal trade-off among multiple conflicting objectives—such as structural safety, load balance, and economic cost—and face the dual challenges of an extremely sparse feasible region and expensive Finite Element Analysis (FEA) costs. Therefore, this paper explores the innovative application of intelligent algorithms in the field of large-scale offshore transportation, and proposes an integrated intelligent framework based on "SACS-in-the-loop Simulation-Driven Design"—KI-NSGA-III. With a parametric knowledge base serving as the digital foundation, this framework constructs an intelligent optimization system featuring "One Architecture and Three Core Mechanisms": First, a two-stage hybrid optimization architecture is adopted to decouple feasibility exploration from performance optimization, effectively overcoming the "cold start" bottleneck caused by the sparse feasible region ; second, an evaluation and repair mechanism is established, maximizing the value of each simulation through a geometric firewall, layout fingerprint caching, and data-driven post-repair ; meanwhile, by integrating an evolutionary operator system with a dynamic control mechanism, full-process deep knowledge injection is achieved. Experimental results demonstrate that this framework not only overcomes the non-convergence defect of standard evolutionary algorithms but also automatically generates a Pareto-optimal set that significantly outperforms human expert baselines across the three dimensions of safety, stability, and cost , realizing a paradigm shift from passive verification to active generation.

title: A Wavelet-Enhanced Convolutional TimesNet for Interval Forecasting of Thermal Power Generation
authors: Qingkai Yang, Xiaoliang Zheng, Hualiang Chen
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: With the increasing penetration of renewable energy sources, thermal power units are compelled to operate under frequent start–stop cycles and deep peak-shaving conditions, which substantially exacerbate the nonlinear, non-stationary, and multi-scale characteristics of power generation time series. Under such highly volatile operating regimes, conventional forecasting approaches often exhibit degraded predictive accuracy and insufficient adaptability to rapid dynamic variations. To address these challenges, this study proposes WTConv-TimesNet-QR, a short-term thermal power interval forecasting model that integrates learnable wavelet transform convolution with the TimesNet architecture. The model employs wavelet convolution to learn and extract multi-scale dynamic features, and further enhances sensitivity to local disturbances and high-frequency variations by building upon TimesNet’s periodic-domain modeling framework. Meanwhile, quantile regression is incorporated to construct predictive intervals, thereby improving the reliability and credibility of the forecasting results. Experiments based on real operational data from a thermal power plant demonstrate that the proposed model outperforms LSTM, GRU, TCN, and Transformer in MAE, RMSE, and MAPE, and achieves high PICP with narrow MPIW. These results confirm the superior accuracy, robustness, and uncertainty modeling capability of WTConv-TimesNet-QR under complex and highly dynamic thermal power operating conditions.

title: Closed Form Expressions for 2D Localization Problem in Electromagnetic Near-Field
authors: Georgii Reznev
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: Recent results on line-of-sight user localization with a uniform linear array (ULA) have shown that, as the number of antennas increases, the estimation error does not decrease as rapidly as predicted by the Cramér–Rao bound (CRB). It was conjectured in that this gap arises because the localization procedure does not exploit the full set of available estimators. In this paper, we confirm this conjecture. We propose a localization approach for ULAs that first generates a large collection of candidate estimates and then fuses them into a single final estimate using a median-based rule. Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm achieves an estimation error that is extremely close to the CRB and decreases with the same scaling as the CRB as the array size grows.

title: A-MARL: Attention-Enabled Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning For Scalable Cloud Load Balancing
authors: Swetha M, Suruthi K, Raja Lavanya L, Indira K
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Task scheduling inefficiency continues to hinder performance in heterogeneous cloud infrastructures. This paper solves this problem by introducing a novel scheduler built on a multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) framework. Its core innovation is an attention mechanism that directs scheduling agents to dynamically filter system state, focusing computational resources on high-priority tasks while accounting for real-time resource constraints. To transition from reactive to proactive management, we incorporate a lightweight predictive model for short-term demand forecasting. The output of this predictor directly governs a dynamic scaling module, which performs live migrations of tasks across the virtual machine (VM) pool to mitigate resource fragmentation and maximize parallel execution. We simulated a heterogeneous cloud environment to test our scheduler. The model achieved promising experimental outcomes such as 35 percent improvement in load distribution fairness, a substantial 61 percent boost in overall system performance, and an 8 percent reduction in average job response time compared to standard heuristic and rule-based baselines.

title: Examining Source Credibility and Parasocial Interaction as Drivers of Purchase Intention in Beauty Vlogger Content
authors: Finola Amanda Handritartha, Damalia Afiani, Naila Almira, Ridho Bramulya Ikhsan, Hartiwi Prabowo, Warniancy Ariesty
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Businesses use beauty vloggers to increase purchases of local cosmetic products, but their effectiveness is hampered by low consumer trust and minimal parasocial interaction with beauty vloggers. This study aims to analyze the influence of source credibility on consumer trust, parasocial interaction, and purchase intention, as well as the relationship between consumer trust and parasocial interaction on the purchase intention of local cosmetic products. Research data was collected through a closed questionnaire and distributed online to 387 respondents residing in nine major cities in Indonesia who follow beauty blogger accounts. Therefore, purposive sampling and data analysis techniques were applied using PLS-SEM. The results showed that source credibility significantly and positively influenced consumer trust, parasocial interaction, and purchase intention. In addition, consumer trust and parasocial interaction also significantly and positively influenced purchase intention. This finding demonstrates how beauty vloggers are vital in creating consumer trust and parasocial interaction, affecting local cosmetic brands' purchase intention. Also, this analysis emphasizes the importance of parasocial interaction, where followers develop personal connections with the vloggers. Ultimately, these findings confirm that leveraging the influence of beauty vloggers is beneficial and essential for driving purchase intent and enhancing the status of local cosmetic brands in a competitive market.

title: Calibration of a Six-Axis Force Sensor Based on a Bayesian Neural Network
authors: Jun Bao, Yuxiang Sun, Xuefei Du
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Six-axis force sensors are core components of robotic perception and control systems, and their measurement accuracy directly affects force-control performance. Traditional calibration methods based on linear least squares struggle to address inherent inter-axis coupling and nonlinear effects such as hysteresis. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes a decoupling calibration method based on a backpropagation (BP) neural network with Bayesian regularization (BR). First, a comprehensive data preprocessing pipeline is established, including data cleaning, denoising, and bidirectional normalization. A conventional linear calibration model is constructed as a baseline for comparison, followed by the training of a dual-hidden-layer BP neural network. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms traditional linear calibration and existing decoupling data in terms of root mean square error (RMSE) across the force components (F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</inf>,F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">y</inf>,F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">z</inf>) and torque dimensions. The coefficient of determination (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) approaches 1.0, effectively improving both decoupling accuracy and measurement stability of the sensor.

title: A Distributed Matching Framework for Low-Latency Cooperative Data Uploading in Smart Construction Sites
authors: Yiyi Zhang, Weiguang Jiang, Peng Guo, Cheng Zhou, Ke You, Chen An
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Smart construction sites are characterized by spatiotemporal data burstiness and heterogeneous uplink capabilities, which frequently result in network congestion and high data upload latency. To reduce this latency, the idle uplink resources of peripheral terminals can be utilized to assist in data uploading via Device-to-Device (D2D) communication. However, this collaborative scheme faces a complex resource contention problem where multiple source nodes may compete for limited helper resources. This transforms the scheduling challenge into a distributed many-to-one matching problem. To solve it, in this paper we propose the Distributed Preference-based Matching Algorithm (DPMA). DPMA leverages matching theory to construct comprehensive utility functions based on channel state, queue backlog, and uplink capability, enabling terminals to autonomously form stable source-helper pairs through a distributed proposal-acceptance mechanism. Extensive simulation experiments evaluate DPMA against Nearest, Greedy, and Centralized Optimal benchmarks. The results demonstrate that DPMA significantly outperforms heuristic baselines, reducing the average critical data completion latency by 28% compared to the Greedy algorithm and 56% compared to the Nearest scheme, while achieving performance within 4% of the theoretical centralized optimum. Additionally, DPMA reduces the overall makespan by 29% and 54% respectively, proving its effectiveness in time-sensitive industrial IoT applications.

title: Confusion and Diffusion Reinforced Image Encryption with Henon-Osprey Optimization
authors: Roopkamal K. Thind, Ashima Anand, Anamika Sharma
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Due to the proliferation of digital information technology, the people used the internet to communicate their data. This data is vulnerable to several types of attacks on the internet, and attackers utilize different strategies to steal the sensitive and confidential information of the people. The encryption technique applied adds a security layer for safeguarding the data. In this research, the focus is on data that is transmitted in the form of images. The image contains a large amount of information in the form of pixels, and the correlation between them is high. Therefore, in this paper, encryption method for images is proposed that utilizes the chaotic Henon map and employs the metaheuristic osprey optimization (OO) algorithm to hyper-tune its parameters. The <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{O O}$</tex> algorithm hyper-tuned the parameters by exploring the solution space of the parameter built on the entropy objective function and determining the best value. Further, in the encryption method, two random sequences are produced utilizing the chaotic Henon map. After that, these sequences are employed to achieve the confusion and diffusion in the proposed method. The simulation results showcase that the histogram of the encrypted images is equally distributed, entropy with a high value near to the ideal value is required in the encryption, and a low value of CC, SSIM, and PSNR is achieved by the proposed method.

title: A Streamlined Deep Learning Framework for Vineyard Yield Estimation
authors: P. Santhi, Aisha Suleiman Shaik, Yemineni Meenakshi, Komali Kadiyala, Suman Pandipati, V. Lakshmi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Accurate vineyard yield estimation is crucial mainly for harvesting, logistics, and production planning. Manual estimation of yield is time-consuming, labour-intensive, and error-prone. Many vision-based systems were proposed to overcome the limitations due to the manual mechanisms; therefore, complex systems that depend on costly sensors have been adapted. However, despite the use of expensive hardware, these systems failed to deliver promising results, also limiting their practical use. This paper proposes a pipeline that focuses on maintaining simplicity, eliminating the use of overpriced hardware, and also succeeds in delivering accurate yield estimation. This was achieved using the Wine Grape Instance Segmentation Dataset (WGISD). The WGISD dataset undergoes proper data pre-processing using CLAHE and denoising to improve image quality under varying illumination, which is then followed by grape bunch detection using the Faster R-CNN, where berry counts were refined using a CNN-based regression model optimized with Huber loss, achieving an accuracy of 94.7%. A YOLOv8 was also evaluated, which achieved an mAP@50 of 89.1%, showing reduced robustness under occlusion. In addition, a MINN model was introduced, combining visual and cluster-level features, which achieved the best overall performance, gaining an accuracy of 95.8%, proving that a simplified model, with proper data pre-processing, can deliver reliable yield estimation for real-world vineyard applications.

title: Miniaturized Wide Band Dual L-Slot on-Chip Antenna with Enhanced Gain for 5G Applications
authors: Harshavardhan Singh, Vipin Singh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Miniaturized on-chip antenna is an essential component in a compact and truly wireless Radio Frequency Integrated Circuit (RFIC). This paper presents a design of a CMOS based wide band on-chip patch antenna designed at 23 GHz band for 5G applications. The proposed antenna incorporates two identical L-shaped slots on the patch to achieve miniaturization with a wider bandwidth. Moreover, these two slots help to excite higher-order mode of operation i.e. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{TM}_{02}$</tex>. The antenna achieves a wide operational bandwidth of 7.55 GHz, spanning from 20.92 GHz to 28.47 GHz, along with a 54 % peak radiation efficiency. It also displays a gain of −6.8 dBi at 23 GHz. Moreover, the design also shows that the antenna's gain is increased by 6.9 dB after employing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{HfO}_{2}$</tex> layer. The simulated results show its potential for high-frequency, short-range applications, including fifth-generation (5G) technologies.

title: Data Imputation and Performance Prediction of xEV Batteries Integrated with DC Microgrids
authors: Saleha Tabassum, Pathan Adam Shafiullakhan, Sama Praneeth, Palukuri Gopichand, Shaik Imran Basha, M. Bharathi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Whenever merging electric vehicles (xEVs) with DC microgrids, accurate battery parameter monitoring and prediction are essential for efficient use of energy. However, real-time measurements are often impacted by missing data, leading to inaccurate control decisions. This work addresses data imputation and prediction in the absence of data in xEV integrated DC microgrids. The k -Nearest Neighbors (k-NN) approach and linear regression (LR) are used to recover incomplete battery properties, including voltage, current, temperature, and state of charge (SoC). MATLAB/Simulink is used to model and simulate the entire system in order to evaluate end-to-end performance. Simulation results show that both LR and k-NN effectively replace lost data, with k -NN showing superior prediction accuracy. According to quantitative study, k-NN obtains a coefficient of determination <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(R^{2}\right)$</tex> of 0.988, a mean absolute error (MAE) of 2.04 %, and a root mean square error (RMSE) of 0.028. In EV-integrated DC microgrids, the suggested framework promotes intelligent energy management, increases system dependability, and improves parameter estimate accuracy.

title: Design and Implementation of a Hybrid IoT-Enabled Solar-Powered Wheelchair and Patient Monitoring System for Physically Challenged Individuals
authors: M. Vijayakumar, R. Arul, A. Divakar, N. Naveen, A. Jaya Prakash, R. Mathavan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Disability is a major challenge to completing the day-to-day activities. The majority of these disorders are acquired at birth or accidentally. To provide a smart solution with the help of digital technology development will help them for a better life cycle. This proposed work is to develop the hybrid wheelchair with the help of the internet of things (IoT). This hybrid wheelchair consists of mobile and gesture control and biomedical sensors that help to monitor the disabled person's health conditions. In this work a solar-powered gesture control wheelchair is developed. The customized smart wheelchair has the ability to move forward, right, left and stop based on the hand motion. The prototype model is completely tested both indoors and outdoors, and the obtained output results are significantly improved. This proposed work covers the two most relevant Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) are SDG 3 - Good Health and Well-Being and SDG 9 - Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.

title: DuET-Shape: A Dual-Stream Evolutionary Topology Framework with Native Shapelets for Time Series Clustering
authors: Tianhao Hu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Balancing morphological interpretability with temporal dependency modeling presents a fundamental challenge in time series clustering. Traditional shapelet-based methods offer transparency yet often reduce data to a static "bag of patterns", discarding underlying chronological causality. On the other hand, deep generative models can capture these dependencies but often suffer from unstable training and "feature drift," where the semantic alignment of features shifts continuously during training. To address this, we propose DuET-Shape (Dual-Stream Evolutionary Topology), an end-to-end framework integrating static shape matching with dynamic logic inference. Subsequently, an Evolution Graph Builder converts unstructured sequences into structured Prototype Transition Matrices (PTM) using a Null-Class noise filtering mechanism and temporal pooling. Finally, a dual-stream co-clustering module jointly optimizes both the static shape similarity and the dynamic evolutionary logic. Extensive experiments on 7 UCR datasets demonstrate that DuET-Shape outperforms leading baselines, validating its efficacy in capturing non-linear process dynamics.

title: Semantic Segmentation of Drone Orthophoto Images
authors: Nikhileswar Reddy Ramireddygari, Hemanth Kumar Reddy Marappareddy, Deepak Krishnamoorthy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The problem that is discussed in this paper is the difficulty in segmenting high-resolution SVAMITVA drone orthophotos (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5-10\ \text{cm} /$</tex> pixel) in rural areas of India, where incomplete labels and a variety of land objects prevent the use of conventional geospatial techniques to analyze the image. The goal is the creation of an AI-controlled pipeline that will preprocess and segment these orthophotos, which will further improve the identification of rural features to better plan and manage resources. The methodology transforms ECW raster and manually prepared shape files to GeoTIFF format with GDAL, inpainting to repair missing pixels, and creates ground-truth masks of five classes, which are background, buildings/houses, roads, agricultural land, and waterbodies, in six villages (Suragpur, Uncha, Uplarshi, Lalpur, Chhattisgarh, Amora). Two deep learning models, Land-Unet and DeepLabV3+, using ResNet50 as a backbone, take <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$256 \times 256$</tex> tiled patches, resulting in about 44,082 training and 7,371 validation samples of a dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3,566 \text{MB}$</tex>. On the Amora test set, the results of the Land-Unet are 90.67% accuracy and 80.05% mean Intersection over Union (IoU) with per-class IoUs of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 8. 9 9 \%,\ } \mathbf{8 6. 7 4 \%,\ } \mathbf{6 2. 4 2 \%,\ } \mathbf{7 9. 3 0 \%,\ } \mathbf{9 2. 7 8 \%}$</tex>. DeepLabV3+ results are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 1. 0 2 \%}$</tex> accuracy and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 0. 8 6 \%}$</tex> mean IoU with per-class IoUs of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 9. 5 3 \%}, \mathbf{8 7. 0 2 \%}, \mathbf{6 4. 2 5 \%}, \mathbf{8 0. 2 7 \%}$</tex>, and 93.23%. These two models are better than the traditional ones, and DeepLabV3+ performs slightly better, especially on roads, but Land-Unet is considered more suitable for practical deployment due to its lower computational requirements. The conclusion provides a solid segmentation framework in an effort to assist in the development of rural areas in India.

title: Lithium-Ion Battery Degradation in Coastal Climates
authors: Alex O’Sullivan, Ted K.A. Brekken
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: A battery health experiment was conducted to determine the effect of marine temperatures on battery degradation and lifetime. Battery health was measured by conducting a cycle life test and comparing the capacity of the battery with the nominal capacity. The internal resistance and open circuit voltage of the battery was also measured to serve as secondary health metrics. A variable temperature chamber was utilized to help maintain ambient temperatures at three different test conditions: room (77 °F, 25 °C), cold (41 °F, 5 °C), and hot (95 °F, 35 °C). It was found that the battery capacity degrades faster as temperature increases. In hot conditions, warm, and cold conditions, the battery degraded at 0.11 % loss of capacity per cycle, 0.08 % loss of capacity per cycle, and 0.06 % loss of capacity per cycle, respectively. These initial results suggest that, although the overall battery capacity is slightly reduced in cold conditions, its degradation is less than warm conditions, and thus the lifetime of batteries in cold and coastal conditions is not expected to be derated.

title: Low-Latency Network Task Scheduling via Graph-Aware Reinforcement Learning
authors: Ziyi Huang, Yuzhe Wang, Na Pang, Xiangkun Huang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Edge computing can effectively support low-latency intelligent applications, but the heterogeneity of nodes in terms of storage, computing, and link bandwidth, as well as the dynamic changes of resources with task execution, make task routing and scheduling extremely challenging. The traditional shortest path and heuristic strategies are difficult to take into account both the topological characteristics of the network and multi-resource coupling simultaneously, resulting in limited scheduling performance in complex dynamic environments. To this end, this paper proposes an intelligent edge task scheduling method for GNN-PPO based on graph neural networks and proximal policy optimization. It models the correlation between nodes and link resources in the topology through graph convolution, and combines task locations and resource consumption processes to achieve globally aware next-hop adaptive decision-making. To approach the real system, the scheduling environment explicitly simulates the transmission, propagation and computing overheads, and adopts a delay-driven reward promotion strategy for optimization. The experimental results show that this method significantly improves the end-to-end delay performance in 8×8 heterogeneous networks compared with the MLP-DRL method, verifying its effectiveness in dynamic resource scenarios.

title: Impact of Deep-Level Traps on Carrier Mobility in β -Ga 2 O 3 MOSFETs
authors: O. Maimon, N. A. Moser, P. R. Shrestha, M.-Y. Kim, S.-M. Koo, K. J. Liddy, A. J. Green, K. D. Chabak, Sujitra Pookpanratana, Qiliang Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: Beta-phase gallium oxide (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\beta $</tex-math> </inline-formula>-Ga<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{{3}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> is a promising semiconductor for high-power and high-frequency electronics due to its ultra-wide bandgap (UWBG) and high breakdown field. However, the carrier mobility in <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\beta $</tex-math> </inline-formula>-Ga<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>-based metal-oxide-semiconductor field-effect transistors (MOSFETs) remains significantly lower than its theoretical limit, often attributed to the presence of deep-level traps. In this work, an electro-optical measurement technique is used to study ionized impurity scattering by deep-level traps in lateral depletion-mode <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\beta $</tex-math> </inline-formula>-Ga<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> field-effect transistors (FETs). Using the conductance method and photo-assisted capacitance-voltage (PCV) measurements with sub-bandgap illumination from 730 nm (1.7 eV) to 280 nm (4.4 eV), the density of interface trap states (D<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\text {it}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> in <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\beta $</tex-math> </inline-formula>-Ga<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> FETs was determined between 0.4 and 4.4 eV below the conduction band (E<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\text {C}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\beta $</tex-math> </inline-formula>-Ga<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>. D<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">it</sub> peaks near the band edges, similar to conventional material systems. Based on the results, traps located between E<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">C</sub>–4.0 eV and E<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">C</sub>–3.4 eV are primarily responsible for carrier scattering, reducing the mobility by 50%–75% in these devices with increasing distance from the interface. The increased mobility degradation further from the interface is attributed to traps located in the bulk.

title: Non-Dominated Sorting Dynamic Programming-Based Multi-Objective Driving Path Planning in Closed Parks
authors: Kegang Zhao, Ziqian Huang, Jie Ye, Haiyan Dai, Qing Liu, Chijun Wang, Xiaolin Tang, Yanwei Liu, Wei Zhong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: In increasingly complex parking lot environments, driving path planning needs to balance multiple objectives such as efficiency and safety. Aiming at the problems of difficult weight determination and single solution in traditional weighted sum multi-objective optimization, this paper proposes a multi-objective driving path planning method based on the Non-Dominated Sorting Dynamic Programming (NSDP) algorithm. In grid map path planning, NSDP has more effective solutions compared with other algorithms, providing a wider selection space for path planning. In parking lot path planning, first, the A* algorithm is used to generate path guide points and discretize them. Variable-curvature Dubins curves are adopted to connect state points. After calculating the cost of each optimization objective, NSDP is used to screen optimal solutions. Then, the entropy weight-Topsis method is applied to recommend paths, and finally the conjugate gradient method is used to smooth the paths. The results show that the proposed method is verified in the parking lot scenario: the recommended driving paths effectively balance multiple objectives, and the curvature is second-order continuous.

title: A 22.7–30.6/35.8–43.2GHz Linear Power Amplifier With Transformer-Based Band-Selective Network
authors: Qin Chen, Yue Liang, Depeng Cheng, Haoxiang Wang, Lianming Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article presents a frequency-reconfigurable dual-band linear power amplifier (PA) using the transformer-based band-selective network (TFBN) for 5G millimeter-wave (mm-Wave) applications. The TFBN features large frequency shifting, wide instantaneous bandwidth, and enhanced out-of-band rejection ability, but suffers from high design complexity due to a large number of design parameters. To tackle this issue, the frequency-dependent equivalent circuit models of the TFBN are proposed, indicating that the TFBN can be approximated as magnetically coupled resonator (MCR) networks in low-frequency and high-frequency (LF and HF) modes. Leveraging these models, the TFBN reconfigurable frequency range, instantaneous fractional bandwidth (FBW), and gain flatness can be analyzed theoretically, and a TFBN design flow for the PA interstage matching network is provided. To enhance the PA amplitude-to-amplitude (AM-AM) performance, a capacitively coupled cold-FET linearizer is adopted and analyzed with a theoretical model. As a proof-of-concept, a frequency-reconfigurable dual-band PA is implemented in a 65-nm CMOS process, which can support the LF and HF bands of 22.7–30.6and 35.8–43.2GHz, respectively. Across LF/HF bands, the PA can achieve 13.2–14.5/13.3–14.85dBm OP<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1dB</sub>, 15.8–17.2/15.1–16.6dBm P<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">sat</sub> with 17.7%–25.9%/15%–21% peak power-added efficiency (PAE). With 400-MHz 64-QAM signals, the PA can achieve 10.7–11.6/10–11.7dBm average output power with −25dB EVM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">rms</sub> and the corresponding ACPR of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${\lt }{-}29$</tex-math> </inline-formula>/−27dBc over 24–30/37–44GHz, respectively.

title: SWMT: A Sliding Window Merkle Tree with Delayed Writes for Scalable Blockchain State Management
authors: Nianzu Sheng, Tong Zhou, He Zhao, Xiaofeng Li, Jinlin Xu
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
abstract: None

title: Ku-band Tile-based Metal-only Mechanically Controlled Two-dimensional Beam-Scanning Circularly Polarized Reflectarray
authors: Yi-An Lin, Zhi-An Zhou, Tzyh-Ghuang Ma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This communication presents a Ku-band metal-only, mechanically controlled, two-dimensional beam-scanning circularly polarized reflectarray (CPRA) based on tile-based unit cells (UCs). Each UC (or tile), laser-cut on a piece of stainless steel, is designed with two pairs of orthogonally placed semi-annular slots to ensure stable reflection response under oblique incidence. A metal frame with high mechanical strength is designed to secure all tiles with plastic screws, while the rotation of the UCs for phase compensation can be conveniently controlled with a pair of tweezers. The experimental results demonstrate that the CPRA, centered at 15 GHz, provides a broadside peak gain of 29.8 dBic, an aperture efficiency of 52.3%, and 1-dB/3-dB bandwidths of 12.7% and 19.3%. The main beam can be steered two-dimensionally to 50o and 60o with a scanning loss of 2.4 and 4.8 dB, respectively. A comparison table is provided to highlight the uniqueness and advantages of the proposed design over existing state-of-the-art counterparts.

title: A 0.4V 1.9-nW Bulk-Driven Cascode OTA Achieving 75-dB DC Gain With Replica-Bias Assisted PVT Robustness
authors: Riccardo Della Sala, Giovanni Nicolini, Alessandro Trifiletti, Giuseppe Scotti
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: This work presents a bulk-driven operational transconductance amplifier (OTA) designed to operate from a 400mV supply while maintaining nanowatt-level power consumption. The proposed single-stage cascode topology achieves a differential gain close to 75dB, combining a body-driven input pair with output-resistance enhancement through a positive-feedback mechanism. Areplica-bias loop is incorporated to improve robustness to PVT variations without increasing current consumption. The OTA was implemented in a 180nm TSMC CMOS technology, and post-layout simulations demonstrate strong resilience to PVT and device mismatch variations. Afabricated prototype was experimentally characterized, showing a measured DC gain consistent with simulations, a CMRR of 44.5dB and PSRR of 42.5dB at 1Hz, and a voltage offset of approximately 4.5mV, while consuming 1.9 nW of power. Time-domain tests further confirm the OTA’s dynamic performance, including a measured slew rate of 1.02 V/ms for a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$200~\mathrm {mV_{pp}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> step input. Overall, the results validate the proposed architecture as an energy-efficient and compact solution suitable for ultra-low-power analog front-ends in biomedical and autonomous sensing applications.

title: A Novel PID Design Method via Model-Based Reinforcement Learning Algorithms
authors: Hozefa Jesawada, Amol Yerudkar, Yang Liu, Navdeep Singh, Carmen Del Vecchio
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper introduces a novel framework that bridges advanced reinforcement learning (RL) with traditional PID control by converting model-based RL policies into interpretable PID gains. By combining inverse reinforcement learning (IRL) with Kullback–Leibler divergence minimization, our method aligns sophisticated control strategies with the simplicity and robustness of PID controllers. In doing so, the proposed approach maintains the transparency and simplicity of PID controllers while incorporating the adaptability, data-driven optimization, and long-horizon planning capabilities of RL. Compatible with both model-based and model-free RL algorithms, the approach has been validated through extensive simulations on benchmark systems and real-world experiments on the Robotarium platform, demonstrating resilience against disturbances, parameter uncertainties, and noise. By blending the strengths of reinforcement learning with the practical familiarity of PID control, the proposed framework offers a data-efficient, scalable, and transparent solution for enhancing PID controller design in complex and dynamic environments.

title: MUFNet: A Mamba-Based Uncertainty-Aware Fusion Framework for Fine-Grained Satellite Hyperspectral-LiDAR Tree Species Mapping
authors: Xueqin He, Kaijian Xu, Shuzhou Wang, Ping Zhao, Yuqi Zhang, Juanjuan Bi
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Satellite hyperspectral imagery (HSI) fused with LiDAR enables large-area, fine-grained tree-species mapping in subtropical forests. However, current satellite HSI-LiDAR fusion remains limited because non-task-oriented spectral reduction may weaken discriminative narrowband and inter-band cues, while weakly coupled fusion cannot adequately address cross-modal inconsistency in satellite observations. To address these issues, we propose MUFNet, a mamba-based uncertainty-aware fusion framework that integrates multi-stage cross-modal interactions with uncertainty-guided prediction aggregation under end-to-end task-driven representation learning. We validate MUFNet in a representative subtropical region and benchmark it against six state-of-the-art (SOTA) models. Experimental results demonstrate that MUFNet achieves the best mapping performance, with an overall accuracy (kappa) of 93.34% (91.17%). Moreover, MUFNet significantly outperforms all competing methods, improving overall accuracy (kappa) by 2.85%–11.45% (6.45%–15.49%). In addition, MUFNet exhibits good predictive reliability, achieving an ECE of 0.0073, and maintains a favorable performance–efficiency trade-off with only 0.400 M parameters and 0.086 GFLOPs. Ablation studies further confirm the complementarity of the three fusion stages and the contributions of the proposed task-driven representation learning, cross-modal interaction, and uncertainty-aware routing components. This work provides a transferable technical pathway for fine-grained tree species mapping under multi-source remote sensing, offering methodological support for biodiversity conservation and forest resource management.

title: Engineering AIOps Controllers for High-Performance Software Operations: an Action Design Research Study
authors: Fernando Piris Font, Stefano Fossati, Marco Tonnarelli, Damian A. Tamburri, Roel Engelen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cloud Computing
abstract: Efficient memory allocation is critical for controlling and optimizing High-Performance Computing (HPC) infrastructures. This action design research study aims to identify the best principles, practices, and patterns for improving the precision and efficiency of control and optimization mechanisms in large-scale HPC infrastructure. To achieve this, we explore the use of Artificial Intelligence Operations (AIOps), which applies artificial intelligence to manage software infrastructure operations. In this context, we propose Job Prophet (JP), a machine-learning-based application. JP is designed to regulate and continuously optimize HPC resource management, enhancing operational efficiency and performance. The tool integrates continuous training, inference, monitoring, and CI/CD pipelines, ensuring adaptability to dynamic workloads. By leveraging historical execution data from heterogeneous sources, JP enhances scheduling efficiency while maintaining model reliability through continuous retraining and monitoring. We evaluate JP on real-world HPC workloads, demonstrating significant improvements in resource utilization and job performance. Our findings highlight the benefits of AI-driven memory allocation and provide insights into scalable, automated machine-learning integration within HPC environments.

title: Inverse-Designed Broadband and Compact $TE\_{N}$ to $TE\_{0}$ Mode Converters via Optimized Bezier Curves
authors: Ziyin Wei, J. Stewart Aitchison
publish: IEEE Photonics Journal
abstract: We present a scalable design strategy for compact, broadband mode converters capable of transforming arbitrary high-order transverse electric (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$TE\_{N}$</tex-math></inline-formula>) modes into the fundamental <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$TE\_{0}$</tex-math></inline-formula> mode on a silicon-on-insulator platform. The architecture employs a “split-phase-combine” mechanism: the higher-order mode is spatially separated into fundamental mode components, phase-synchronized, and subsequently recombined. The device comprises a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1\times (N+1)$</tex-math></inline-formula> input splitter, an adiabatic phase shifter, and a multimode interference (MMI) combiner. By parametrizing the device geometry with optimized cubic Bezier curves, we achieve a compact footprint with large operating bandwidth, while effectively maintain low losses. We validate the scalability of this approach by designing converters for <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$TE\_{1}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$TE\_{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$TE\_{3}$</tex-math></inline-formula> modes. The optimized devices exhibit mode conversion efficiencies of 98.3%, 95.8%, and 86.8%, respectively, across a broad 100nm bandwidth (1500–1600nm). Tolerance analysis further confirms the robustness of the designs against systematic fabrication deviations of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm 10$</tex-math></inline-formula> nm, suggesting their suitability for high-density mode-division multiplexing in next-generation optical interconnects.

title: GenTouchVR: Generating a Touchable Virtual Reality Environment from a Single Image
authors: Jaejun Park, Soyeon Nam, Jeongwoo Kim, Uison Ju, Seungmoon Choi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Creating interactive VR content typically requires extensive modeling effort, making scalable production difficult. We present an automated computational pipeline that converts a single image into an interactive 3D scene with plausible kinesthetic feedback. Our approach leverages a Large Language Model (LLM) to detect objects in the image and infer haptic properties from visual and textual cues. These extracted results are then utilized to synthesize 3D models and optimize haptic properties for perceptual distinguishability, while combining them into a complete interactive environment. A user study shows that the generated VR scenes provide compelling visuo-haptic experiences, highlighting the potential of our method for scalable multisensory world generation. To our knowledge, this is the first system to automatically produce VR scenes with force feedback from a single image, pointing toward a practical direction for lowering the barriers to creating haptically enriched VR content.
