title: Adaptive Robust GNSS/INS Tightly-Coupled Navigation and Positioning for Off-Road Hovercrafts Using Extended Kalman Filtering
authors: Shaosong Zhang, Xiuping Wang, Jinghao Chen
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Off-road hovercrafts exhibit unique non-contact motion characteristics and high sensitivity to environmental disturbances, posing significant challenges for navigation and positioning in complex terrains. This paper presents an adaptive robust GNSS/INS tightly-coupled navigation method using Extended Kalman Filtering (EKF) specifically designed for off-road hovercrafts. The proposed approach directly processes raw GNSS pseudorange and pseudorange rate observations integrated with Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU) data through a tightly-coupled framework. A kinematic model incorporating drift angle compensation and wind-wave disturbance correction is developed to address hovercraft-specific motion characteristics. An adaptive robust estimation mechanism is introduced to dynamically adjust filtering parameters and suppress multipath errors under degraded satellite signal conditions. Experimental validation in off-road slalom scenarios demonstrates that the proposed tightly-coupled algorithm achieves positioning accuracy improvements of over 25.2% compared to existing open-source navigation and positioning methods. The algorithm maintains real-time performance with 9.9 Hz output frequency, providing a high-precision navigation and positioning solution for off-road hovercraft applications.

title: Comparative Study of Tree-Based Ensembles, CNN–LSTM, and State Space Model for Photovoltaic Power Forecasting
authors: Karan Veer Singh, Aditya Sachdeva, Drishti Agarwal, Divyansh Sharma, Abhilasha Tiwari, Amanpreet Kaur, Sandeep Mandia
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Photovoltaic (PV) power forecasting is crucial for maintaining grid stability and optimizing energy management. A comparative analysis of machine learning and deep learning models for short-term and long-term power forecasting using the NIST PV dataset for the year 2017 is presented in this study. There are a total of four models, including the Random Forest Regressor, XG-Boost, hybrid 1D CNN–LSTM, and the recently proposed Mamba State Space Model (Mamba SSM), that were evaluated. The results show that XG-Boost achieved the best performance in both short-term and long-term forecasting with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> of 0.9515 and MSE of 175.7043. This demonstrates the strength of XG-Boost in modeling non-linear relationships. Random forest had a lower R2 score of 0.9504, which meant that it did not perform as well. However, it did have a lower MAE of 3.0962 in short-term forecasting. This means that Random Forest can handle small changes well. In contrast, CNN–LSTM successfully captured local and temporal dynamics but exhibited inferior performance relative to tree-based ensemble models in error metrics. The new Mamba SSM has done well, especially when it comes to long-term forecasting. The ReLu activation function was utilized to mitigate negative value predictions in the Mamba SSM model.

title: Design of an on-Chip Microring Resonator Based Silicon Photonic Sensor
authors: Jenila C, Aarthi V.P.M. B, Sai Kiriti V, Sri Durga Prasad B, Ganesh Y
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This work proposes the design and numerical analysis of a silicon photonic microring-resonator-based biosensor for the detection of the Human epidermal growth factor 2 (HER2) cancer biomarker in an aqueous environment. The sensor is implemented on a standard Silicon on Insulator (SOI) platform using a silicon bus waveguide <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.45 \mu ~\mathrm{m} \times 0.22 \mu ~\mathrm{m}$</tex>) and an 8 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>-radius ring resonator coupled through a 200-nm gap, with water acting as the upper cladding. A thin biolayer (10 nm) representing HER2 binding is modeled on the ring surface to evaluate refractive-index-induced resonance shifts. Using 3D Finite-Difference Time-Domain (FDTD) simulations, the sensor demonstrates a measurable resonance shift of 11 nm for an index change from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n=1.38$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n=1.45$</tex>, corresponding to a sensitivity of 110 nm/RIU (nanometers per refractive index unit). The resonance around 1524 nm exhibits a Full width at half maximum (FWHM) of 17 nm, resulting in a simulated Q-factor of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim$</tex> 89.6. Comparative E-field and H-field distributions confirm stronger modal confinement and enhanced perturbation at higher HER2 refractive indices. These results validate the proposed microring architecture as a compact, Complementary Metal-Oxide-Semiconductor (CMOS)-compatible platform for labelfree cancer biomarker sensing, demonstrating clear resonance signatures suitable for future optimization toward high quality (high-Q) and ultra-sensitive biomedical applications.

title: An Autoencoder-based Feature Selection with Optimized Reconstruction Loss and Feature Scoring
authors: Monitha Davuluri, Manish Vikrama, Rashida Hasan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Supervised feature selection aims to identify a subset of relevant features from high-dimensional data to enhance model accuracy and efficiency. Traditional feature selection techniques often produce suboptimal feature subsets due to limitations in capturing nonlinear interactions and complex feature relationships, poor algorithmic stability, and susceptibility to the curse of dimensionality. To tackle these challenges, we proposed two algorithms for feature selection in supervised learning: the Autoencoder with Enhanced Reconstruction Loss (AERL) and an advanced version, AERL with Feature Scoring (AERL-FS). AERL employs an autoencoder with a custom loss function to address the complexities of high-dimensional data more comprehensively than traditional methods. AERL-FS builds on this by adding a feature importance mechanism for effective feature selection. Comparative evaluations show that our proposed methods outperform state-of-the-art algorithms in terms of model accuracy.

title: Boosting Fine-Grained Aircraft Detection with Fine-Grained Language Knowledge
authors: Xueru Xu, Zhong Chen
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: The insufficient visual differences pose a great challenge to identifying fine-grained objects in remote sensing scenes. Since the language modality enables the description of all visual concepts in an open world, it serves as a preferred supplement for the limited visual signals. In this paper, we explore how to construct language signals for fine-grained categories that can aid in the fine-grained detection task; consequently, we propose constructing learnable fine-grained text prompts by introducing several specific text tokens that are responsible for learning discriminative content for fine-grained categories, and then finetuning a pretrained vision-language model on an auxiliary fine-grained classification task to optimize the learnable text tokens, thus generating fine-grained text embeddings that carry discriminative clues. Several detectors are employed to analyze the effectiveness of the proposed language supplemental signals. The experimental findings indicate that all comparative detectors embrace pronounced performance gains when integrating the learned fine-grained text embeddings into the visual feature space output by the detection network, demonstrating that the learning- based strategy enables the generation of fine-grained language knowledge with abundant discriminative clues in a manner free from meticulous manual design to aid in fine-grained detection tasks.

title: Proactive Make-Before-Break Handover Using Signal Degradation Prediction for Robust Urban Mobile Sensing Systems
authors: Tharaniya R, D. Kiruthika, Rajeshkumar J, Joy Christina J, Janardan M, Ananthi G
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Mobile sensor networks in urban settings play a vital role in enabling autonomous vehicles and public safety initiatives, yet they face frequent data disruptions from node movement and signal blackouts. This study proposes and assesses a forwardlooking handover protocol to deliver uninterrupted sensing coverage. The approach employs a low-overhead algorithm for forecasting signal decline, initiating a “make-before-break” switch that shifts sensing duties from a weakening node to a reliable alternative ahead of failure. Through extensive simulations across diverse network densities and mobility levels, we examine core metrics such as service availability and handover occurrences via Monte Carlo methods. Findings reveal that performance improves markedly with higher node counts, reaching virtually perfect uptime (nearly 100 %) in setups with eight or more nodes. The protocol also handles elevated speeds effectively by dynamically adjusting handover rates, underscoring its reliability for critical urban deployments prone to motion-related outages.

title: Deep Learning for Third Molar Segmentation: An Empirical Analysis on Dental Radiographs
authors: Munim Ahmed, M. M. Mahbubul Syeed, Jahid Hasan Rony, Razib Hayat Khan, Mohammad Faisal Uddin, Safiqul Islam, Mohammad Khursheed Alam
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Third molar segmentation on panoramic dental radiographs is crucial for oral diagnosis and effective treatment planning. However, it remains a challenging task due to anatomical complexity, variability, and frequent image artifacts. This work presents a comprehensive benchmarking of state-ofthe-art semantic segmentation models from three architecture families: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Transformer-based models, and Hybrid architectures. We introduce a novel dataset of 871 annotated panoramic radiographs, labeled with four third molar classes based on the FDI numbering system. The study evaluates U-Net (with MobileNetV2, ResNet34, ResNet50), DeepLabV3, SegFormer, SETR, DETR, and Mask2Former models. All models were trained and compared under a consistent experimental setup with extensive hyperparameter tuning. Performance was assessed using multiple metrics: Mean IoU, Dice Score, Precision, Recall, and Pixel Accuracy. U-Net with ResNet34 achieved the highest segmentation performance (Mean IoU: 0.7771, Dice: 0.8691), closely followed by SegFormer. These results highlight the superior spatial localization of CNNs and the contextual modeling strength of Transformers. In contrast, hybrid models such as DETR and Mask2Former ranked lower, indicating limitations in capturing smaller and irregular anatomical structures in dental radiographs. The outcome of this study offer important insight into the challenges of applying various deep learning paradigms to dental image analysis and underscore the significance of architectural choices and dataset properties in achieving clinically relevant outcomes.

title: Cervical Cancer Prediction using Deep Learning Algorithms
authors: Jansi Sophia Mary C, Mariya Nithiya P, Soundarya S, Mahalakshmi V, Sathya Swetha V, Thilagavathi S
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Cervical cancer is still an important women's wellness problem worldwide, and early diagnosis is essential for its prognosis. Classification of cervical cell abnormalities is complex due to variations in cell morphology and similar overlapping structures of Pap smear images. Segmentation accuracy and classification performance are significant challenges for most existing approaches. The cervical cancer detection system using deep learning suggests a new automated system by merging advanced image preprocessing. The Pap smear images are initially subjected to the Wiener filtering to minimize the uncleared area. Subsequently, watershed transform is handy to discrete the cervical area from the background, which assists in precisely determining cell areas. The filtered images are then run through a CNN, and VGG-19 classifies them into one of five categories: normal, metaplastic, dyskeratotic, parabasal, and superficial. This categorization helps to differentiate the various stages of abnormal cervical cells, ranging from normal to precancerous and cancerous. Experimental outputs show the system's effectiveness, reaching a classification accuracy of 95.68% and following precision, recall, and F1-score are 94.8%, 96.2%, and 95.5%, correspondingly. These results validate the model's effectiveness in correctly calculated cervical cell abnormalities and helping in the early finding and analysis of cervical cancer. This method provides early intervention and a better prognosis for the patient with cervical cancer.

title: Influence of Stratified Variable Encoding on Quality of Mortality Prediction in Systolic Heart Failure
authors: Bhumi Patel, Lemba Priscille Ngana, Atefehsadat Haghighathoseini, Janusz Wojtusiak
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: This study investigates how stratified variable encoding strategies affect the performance of machine learning (ML) models in predicting in-hospital mortality among ICU patients with systolic heart failure (HF). Effective data preprocessing, particularly encoding of clinical variables, is essential for transforming raw data into formats suitable for analysis. Despite its significance, optimal encoding methods remain poorly defined, with implications for model accuracy and robustness. Data were sourced from the MIMIC-IV dataset, focusing on 1,037 adult ICU patients within the first 24 hours of admission. Forty-three patient characteristics, including 39 laboratory test results and demographic data, were encoded using four approaches: binary (present/not present), mean/maximum/minimum, first/last, and a combined method integrating all of the encodings. Gradient boosting, random forest, and logistic regression models were trained and evaluated using the area under the curve (AUC), accuracy, precision, and recall metrics. Feature importance was measured using the random forest method with stratified analyses to evaluate optimal encoding techniques. Model performance varied by the encoding method and algorithm. Random forest achieved the highest AUC with binary (0.833) and first/last (0.920), while gradient boosting achieved the highest AUC with average/minimum/maximum (0.911) and combination (0.931). In stratified evaluation, the highest average AUC further improved performance (AUC = 0.939) in comparison to the highest minimum (AUC = 0.930) and the highest maximum-minimum (AUC = 0.907). These findings emphasize the importance of encoding selection in ML model development. Tailored encoding strategies can enhance model performance, providing critical insights for clinical decision-making and improved patient outcomes.

title: FractureNet: Hybrid YOLOv11 and Vision Transformer Framework for Micro-Fracture Detection
authors: Rontala Manasa, Kokkula Shiva Prasad, Pothuganti Mithinesh, Thakur Koushul Singh, Voggu Ravi, Muntha Raju
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Short lines of fractures are very fine and subtle in nature, making the detection of hairline fractures in medical imaging very difficult. Although some deep-learning models, including CNNs and the previous version of YOLO, demonstrate the potential of detecting the presence of general bone fracture, in many images, they fail to recognize fine hairline features. The current literature points out the advantages of CNNs in spatial learning and YOLO in efficient detection, but both of them fail to detect fine fractures when applied separately. A solution to these facets is the FractureFinder, which is a hybrid architecture that uses the bounding-box identification (YOLOv11) method and localizes specific features, followed by fine-tuning through Vision Transformers (ViT), which can also capture global contextual features, using Grad-CAM heatmaps as a way of visualizing these results. The publicly available dataset Hairline Fracture Detection v2 was experimented with and the accuracy was 94 %. Comparative analysis has shown that FractureFinder has better sensitivity to detect fine fractures with reduced false negatives, thus bridging a serious gap in medical diagnosis. The findings are that FractureFinder can lead to better clinical use due to a greater sensitivity of detecting small fractures and a greater level of diagnostic transparency provided by visual explanations. Finally, hybrid object detection and transformer-based refined feature deep learning models demonstrate promising perspectives on medical imaging. The future will expand this strategy and model with multi-modal imaging, larger datasets, privacy federated learning, and clinical real-time implementation that will continue developing AI-assisted diagnostics.

title: SHAP-Optimized Explainable Computational Intelligence Models for Interpretable Chemotherapy Adverse Event Prediction
authors: M Kathiravan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The negative effects of chemotherapy can be varied among patients, which will greatly reduce the continuity of the treatment and the clinical outcomes. The existing risk assessment approaches are mainly based on generalized statistical techniques or opaque models, which would tend to be susceptible to incomplete clinical evidence and would not deliver the explanations required to offer clinical faith. To address these limitations, this paper proposes an explicable artificial intelligence-based model of forecasting adverse extreme-type chemotherapy side effects using XG-Boost, random forest, and decision tree models, and using SHAP-based interpretability. The clinical, demographic, and treatment-related data were processed in an orderly manner, and the models that were trained using the data were processed with missing values. The experiments indicate that XG-Boost is more powerful in prediction and robust and generalization, as it has a reduced cross-validation error and improved confusion matrix performance with a reduced number of false negatives and false positives. Random forests and decision tree models proved to have higher misclassification errors, in particular, false negatives, and limit clinical validity. SHAP analysis provided patient-specific and globalized explanations of the risk factors that made predictions. In general, the proposed explainable AI architecture is better than the existing approaches regarding its capacity to be precise, resilient, and interpretable with reliable and tailored clinical decision support in cancer treatment.

title: Deep Learning based HAR for Monitoring Physical Activity of Patients
authors: Shashikiran S, Ramanamma Parepalli, Srinivas Babu N, Sudhanva Lokesh Manne, Vasanth Kumar D P, Suraj M
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Human Activity Recognition (HAR), which tracks the amount of physical activity to prevent inappropriate physical activity or to enable prompt medical care, is a crucial component of monitoring the patient’s rehabilitation process. In this study, time-series data is used to build a Convolutional neural network (CNN) - based deep learning (DL) model for HAR. The dataset is made up of CSV files with sensor data from patient-attached biosensors. The CNN model created with different input layers and output layer with Colab assistance provides the accuracy of 87% in classification of human activities and it further increased to 94% buy hyper tuning the model. The model’s timely performance in activity identification tasks demonstrates its high ability to identify walking, jogging, standing, and sitting. This makes it possible to incorporate tactics that allow for continuous physical activity monitoring for a medical facility, which can improve patient care and rehabilitation services. HAR models using deep learning can be effectively incorporated along the surveillance cameras for continuous monitoring of patients for their fast recovery.

title: A Hybrid GWO-CatBoost Model for Predicting Electricity Consumption in Commercial and Residential Buildings
authors: S. Archana, Reena Prashant Shinde, Sayed Mohammed Imdadullah, K. Prakash, J. A. Baskar, M. Sreedhar
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Lots of the world's energy comes from homes and businesses. With the proliferation of smart technology and sensors, it is crucial to have reliable hourly consumption of electricity estimates for operations, demand response, and distributed generation decisions. These forecasts should cover the medium to long term. Energy management and the reduction of greenhouse gas emissions in business and residential buildings are being addressed by smart grids and smart buildings, which are becoming more practical solutions. This research lays forth a system for making predictions in three stages: cleaning up the data, choosing the features, and training the model. To standardize data, the min-max approach works fine. The selection of features, the amount of training data, and the frequency of model updates are all well thought out. To enhance CatBoost parameter adjustment, the IDBO use centroid opposition-based learning rather than random initialization. The optimized IDBO improves the accuracy of building energy consumption forecasts. According to the experimental data, the suggested strategy outperforms the current models by a margin of 95.60 percent. Commercial and residential sustainability are both aided by its capacity to increase the efficiency of smart building systems.

title: NLD-LLM: A systematic framework for evaluating small language transformer models on natural language description
authors: Hamed Jelodar, Mohammad Meymani, Parisa Hamedi, Tochukwu Emmanuel Nwankwo, Samita Bai, Roozbeh Razavi-Far, Ali A. Ghorbani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Natural Language Description (NLD) is a Natural Language Processing (NLP) task that requires models to generate structured and meaningful outputs from natural language inputs. In this work, we propose NLD-LLM, a systematic NLP framework to evaluate the performance of language models to generate accurate and concise source code descriptions. This framework incorporates a diverse set of transformer models, including Qwen, DeepSeek, Phi, LLaMA, and Mistral, spanning various sizes, architectures, and training approaches. Central to NLD-LLM is a comprehensive prompt design strategy that includes standardized formatting, clear task guidance, and NLD prompting, ensuring fair and consistent evaluation. Additionally, we apply an iterative refinement process to improve output’s quality and assess the model’s adaptability. Using semantic and structural metrics, our analysis demonstrates that prompt engineering significantly impacts the effectiveness of the model such that smaller models often performing competitively when supported by well-crafted prompts.

title: Research on Bill Image Text Detection Based on Adaptive Scale Fusion and Feature Enhancement
authors: Bo Zhu, Fengxiang Zhang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Bill images often contain text with variable scales, complex background interference, and diverse morphologies, which bring great challenges to accurate text detection. To solve these problems, this paper proposes an improved PP-OCRv4-based bill text detection model (EPDv4-B). Aiming to verify the effectiveness of the core modules in the model, ablation experiments are designed, focusing on analyzing the performance contributions of the Hierarchical Feature Coding (HPC) module and Spatial Feature Refinement (SPR) module. The experimental results show that both modules can significantly improve the detection performance, and their combination achieves the optimal comprehensive effect. This study provides experimental basis for the optimization and practical application of bill image text detection technology.(Abstract)

title: Enhancing Smart Grid Observability through Optimal PMU Placement using Nonlinear Programming
authors: Anand Kumar, Srikant Prasad, Shravan Kumar Yadav, Shubhranshu Kumar Tiwary, Alok Priyadarshi
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The current work primarily depicts a Smart Grid enhancement based on Non-linear Programming and Optimal Phasor Measurement Unit (OPP) placement. PMUs (phasor measurement units) can generate synchronised voltage and current phasors, increasing the accuracy of state estimation results. The optimal PMU placement (OPP) method reduces the total number of PMUs required to fully observe a system. The proposed algorithm has two goals: finding the fewest number of Phasor Measurement Units (PMUs) and increasing SG redundancy across all buses. The accuracy of the state estimation results in a synchronised current and voltage phasor; using fewer PMUs results in substation communication capability. Failures of PMUs result in unobservable buses at the SG. During the design process, PMU losses and communication constraints must be considered. As a result, the proposed PMU formulation includes LCC, conventional measurement, and Zero Injection Bus (ZIB) measurement parameters. To ensure its effectiveness, the proposed algorithm is tested in MATLAB using the IEEE 14-,30-,57-,118-, and 300-bus test methods. To demonstrate the efficacy and authenticity of the presented algorithm, the results are compared with Simplex Linear Programming (SLP) and Mixed Linear Programming (MLP) methods. It demonstrates that the NLP algorithm generates roughly the same number of PMUs as other methods.

title: Intelligent Bank Customer Churn Prediction: An AI Ensemble Approach Using Neural Networks and TabPFN Foundation Models
authors: Nauman Pathan, Vishwajeet Patil, Ayushi Pahadia, Smita Bharne
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Banking institutions need customer churn prediction to ensure their profits remain secure and to increase customer retention. The proposed paper introduces a new AI ensemble method that integrates Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs) with TabPFN base models to improve customer churn prediction in banks. We use a domain-specific learning approach that leverages the potential of a custom neural network, combined with the novel and highly effective generalization capabilities of TabPFN, a foundation model for tabular data introduced in recent work. With an ensemble system, the accuracy on the standard bank customer churn data stands at 91.3%, whereas with a single model method, the accuracy is at 68.8%. The system comprises processes for large-scale data preprocessing, weighted ensemble methods, and a professional dashboard for real-time analytics. The experimental findings demonstrate better performance than current techniques, with practical results of this achievement evident in banking customer retention processes.

title: Deep Learning-Enhanced Edge-AI Architecture for Autonomous Drone-based Traffic Flow Prediction and Congestion Management
authors: Yashini P, R.Renugadevi, Berlin Magthalin R
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The rapid growth of urban populations has intensified traffic congestion, making real-time monitoring and predictive management essential for smart city infrastructure. This paper proposes a deep learning-driven Edge-AI framework deployed on autonomous unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) to perform real-time traffic analysis, incident detection, and congestion prediction in dynamic urban environments. The system integrates high-resolution aerial video capture with on-board deep convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and transformer-based architectures optimized for edge inference, eliminating reliance on constant cloud connectivity. A multi-stage processing pipeline enables vehicle detection, trajectory extraction, and traffic flow estimation directly on the UAV, followed by edge-to-edge coordination between drones for large-scale coverage. Experiments conducted on publicly available drone-traffic datasets, enhanced with custom aerial recordings, demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves over 94% detection accuracy with sub second inference latency, while significantly reducing bandwidth usage compared to cloud-based methods. The framework’s adaptability to varying traffic densities and lighting conditions highlights its potential for deployment in Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS), urban planning, and emergency response. This work contributes a scalable, privacy-preserving, and latency-optimized solution for next-generation traffic management in smart cities.

title: An Attention-Guided RFCN Framework for False Positive Reduction in Lung Nodule Detection
authors: Mallepula Sai Teja Goud, Sabbu Jagan, Askani Harish Sagar, Nikhila Kathirisetty
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The strong visual similarity between malignant pulmonary nodules and vascular cross-sections poses a significant challenge for accurate nodule detection in computed tomography (CT) scans, often leading to a high number of false positives in automated systems. Despite high sensitivity performance, existing deep learning based detectors remain inadequate in removing vessel-like feature responses in the initial steps of feature extraction. In light of these considerations, an attention-guided region based Fully Convolutional Network, namely GLAM-RFCN, is proposed in this study to emphasize false-positive reduction at the feature map level. The proposed model consists of a multilayer fusion region proposal network (mLRPN) for retaining high definition semantics without iterative refinement. A Global-Local Attention Module (GLAM) introduced before region proposal, assists in modifying feature maps through channel-wise feature recalibration, thus promoting vascular smoothing and modularity feature enhancement. In addition, the model adopts a focal loss function in training for handling an imbalance issue between the foreground and background. Based on the LUNA16 dataset, the proposed strategy demonstrates high sensitivity and achieves approximately a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 7 \%}$</tex> reduction in false-positive values compared with existing models.

title: DL-TPS: Fast Invertible 3D Thin-Plate Spline Deformation Method Based on Deep Learning
authors: Mingjun Zhu, Yuanyuan Li, Lei Qu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Thin-Plate Spline (TPS) image registration based on control points is one of the commonly used methods for nonrigid medical image registration and anatomical structure shape analysis. By minimizing bending energy, TPS achieves smooth and globally consistent deformation. However, TPS has notable limitations: its computational complexity is cubic in the number of control points, leading to inefficiency with large-scale data and difficulty in meeting real-time requirements. Additionally, TPS deformations are non-invertible—in regions far from the landmarks, it fails to establish unique correspondences between two images. This paper proposes a deep learning-based method for fast and invertible 3D Thin-Plate Spline deformation. It transforms the computationally intensive process of matrix inversion and kernel function operations in traditional TPS into an end-to-end trainable single forward propagation process. This approach significantly reduces computational resource requirements and time costs while ensuring the diffeomorphic property of the deformation, thereby enabling the generation of smooth and invertible displacement fields.

title: A YOLO and Transformer-based Smart Scan Framework for Lung Disease Identification in Radiographic Imaging
authors: Sharda Thete, S. Jency, Vijay Bhagawan Nerkar, L.R. Sujithra, Harshali Chaudhari, G. Sajiv
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Accurate and timely diagnosis of lung disease using radiographic imaging continues to pose a formidable obstacle for contemporary healthcare systems, especially in low-resource settings where radiologist personnel are in limited supply. This study introduces a hybrid Smart Scan Framework, integrating a real-time, YOLO-based object detector with Transformer-driven attention modelling to achieve robust lung disease classification. The architecture first employs a custom fine-tuned YOLOv8 backbone to precisely delineate lung boundaries and to localize suspected lesions, after which a Vision Transformer module quantifies long-range spatial dependencies that refine diagnostic determination. To enhance feature observability in sub-optimal chest X-rays, the pipeline employs preprocessing techniques including Contrast-Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization and Gaussian blur suppression. Rigorous experimentation on the CheXpert benchmark shows that the integrated system delivers a detection mean Average Precision of 96.4% and a classification accuracy of 94.7%, exceeding the performance of conventional convolutional neural networks, standalone YOLO, or Transformer approaches. The architecture further accelerates inference, processing 42 frames per second, and lowers the false-positive rate by 18% relative to the baselines, facilitating rapid and dependable triage within clinical workflows. These performance metrics position the Smart Scan Framework as a leading solution for automated lung disease screening, with compelling applicability across hospital settings and portable diagnostic units.

title: A Multi-Layer Blockchain Architecture with Diffie-Hellman Key Exchange for Secured IoT Communication
authors: Anguraju Krishnan, Rajesh Arunachalam
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The proliferation of the Internet of Things (IoT) has increased pressure on the development of secure, scalable, and real-time communication schemes due to the inability of traditional security system to keep up with the changing levels of cyber attacks. Due to this, the conduct of this research proposes multi-layer blockchain structure with incorporated Diffie-Hellman (DH) key exchange protocol to provide confidentiality, integrity, and authentication during sciences in IoT data transport. The given model is based on the DH exchange to agree on the secure session key and the lightweight AES-GCM encrypt hence providing minimal latency and are CPU-light. Proof-of-Authority (PoA) - Multi-layer blockchain framework with secure storage of hashed fingerprints of data digests using SHA3-256 hash algorithm will guarantee that verification cannot be tampered with. The implementation of the methodology is in the Python language, experimental testing is carried out on the datasets of IoT networks with heterogeneous sensor data that is under diverse loads. Experimental results show that the architecture has an average encryption time of 3.68 ms when encrypted payloads with 512 bytes, blockchain write latency of 42.3 ms at 250 transactions per block as well as greater than 98 percent accuracy in integrity verification tests, even in simulated tampering attacks and replay attacks. The multi-layer topology achieves improved scalability due to isolation of Microsoft and clusters of IoT devices as well as global trust synchronization. The present contribution establishes that blockchain-backed storage can be adopted to solidify low-latency and verifiable security in a next-generation IoT network when combined with DH key exchange.

title: IPD-YOLO: Resource-Efficient Real-Time Detection Framework for Cotton Pest Management
authors: Fengjiao Ge, Jin-Whan Kim
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: As a major cash crop, cotton contributes substantially to the economy, yet effective pest detection is still challenging. In response, we introduce the IPD-YOLOv8 algorithm to improve early pest identification during the cotton bud stage. To optimize model efficiency, we introduced the Lightweight Down-sampling Extraction (LDE) method, which dynamically adjusts weights based on input feature maps to reduce dimensionality. Additionally, the backbone network adopts FasterNet as its core feature extractor, replacing traditional bottlenecks to enhance feature extraction capacity. The model also incorporates VoV-GSCSPC and SimAm in the Neck network, boosting its robustness and accuracy in varied environments. The experiments demonstrate that IPD-YOLOv8 reaches 92% mAP using only 4.6M parameters, and runs at 7.8 ms. Against the baseline, it delivers a 2.2% gain in mAP and reduces parameters, model size, and GFLOPs by 26.5%, 25.8%, and 25.6%, respectively.

title: Towards Interpretable Monitoring in Agriculture: a Multi-Task Collaborative Deep Network for Crop Disease Diagnosis
authors: Rongchang Lu, Jiayu Wang, Zhenhe Deng, Ludan Zhang, Yuhui Liu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: This study addresses the challenges of traditional and existing deep learning-based crop disease diagnostics by proposing an intelligent system that integrates multi-task collaborative learning and an interpretable deep network. The framework uses a shared backbone for feature extraction alongside task-specific decoders for classification, severity grading, and lesion localization. It incorporates a meta-learning module for few-shot adaptation and an explanation component to provide visual and semantic evidence for predictions. Through dynamic loss weighting, cross-task attention, and lightweight neural architecture search, the system achieves efficient knowledge sharing, robustness in field conditions, and transparent decision-making. Experimental results on multiple datasets demonstrate the method’s superiority over state-of-the-art approaches in accuracy, generalization, and practicality, offering a deployable solution for agricultural disease management.

title: Evaluating Machine Learning and Deep Learning Approaches for Binary Sentiment Classification
authors: V Sumalatha, Parwati Kumawat, Neha Rani, Ashok Bhansali, Aryan Mekde, Rachna Rathore
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: One of the essential functions of the Natural Language Processing is the sentiment analysis that has been grown and improved through the utilization of traditional machine learning (ML) and contemporary deep learning (DL) networks. This paper will contrast five classical Random Forest, ML algorithms, Simple RNN, Logistic Regression, SVM and k-Nearest Neighbors to five deep learning models, NAIVE Bayes, LSTM, Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), Bidirectional LSTM and Stacked LSTM on the Sentiment Labelled Sentences dataset. The research results from the experiment show that classical ML techniques provide competitive performance at low computational costs. In particular, Naive Bayes and Logistic Regression had an accuracy of 80.91% and 80.00, respectively, and their training time is less than 0.01 seconds, which indicates that they are suitable to small datasets. On the other hand, deep learning methods performed inconsistently and were also significantly more demanding in terms of computing resources. Even though the CNN model gained a similar accuracy of 80.55, it took a considerably longer time to be trained. As well, the Simple RNN and Stacked LSTM models did not perform well with accuracy at about 50 percent indicating that they are not effective in the situation where there is limited availability of data. Comprehensively, the findings emphasize that when the sentiment classification problem is of small scale, conventional machine learning based algorithms are giving a more effective and convenient answer, but when the problem is of large scale, the deep learning models tend to require larger scale datasets to perform optimal.

title: Object Identification Under Known Dynamics: A PIRNN Approach for UAV Classification
authors: Nyi Nyi Aung, Neil Muralles, Adrian Stein
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: This work addresses object identification under known dynamics in unmanned aerial vehicle applications, where learning and classification are combined through a physics-informed residual neural network. The proposed framework leverages physics-informed learning for state mapping and state-derivative prediction, while a softmax layer enables multi-class confidence estimation. Quadcopter, fixed-wing, and helicopter aerial vehicles are considered as case studies. The results demonstrate high classification accuracy with reduced training time, offering a promising solution for system identification problems in domains where the underlying dynamics are well understood.

title: Adaptive Clustering and Cluster Head Selection in VANETs Using Semantic Embeddings and Weighted CRITIC
authors: Ritu Kumari, Kusum Dalal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Vehicular Ad Hoc Networks (VANETs) have been at the centre of the Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) to support safety-critical applications, including collision avoidance, congestion detection, and real-time route optimisation. Nevertheless, they have dynamic topology and high mobility of nodes, which make it difficult to have stable clustering, as well as, to select Cluster Head (CH) with reliability. The current research presents a new framework, which uses semantic relation-based embeddings to build clusters and an adaptive selection mechanism of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{C H}$</tex> based on the Weighted CRITIC approach. This model combines both connectivity and feature data to create stable clusters and also to select CHs based on various stability parameters such as Relative Cooperative Speed, Driver impulse value and neighbourhood connectivity and objective weights calculated by CRITIC. Results with SUMO-based vehicular traces show significant levels of improvement: the lifetime of clusters is increased to 54, the stability of the CH is 84, packet delay is reduced to 0.00148 s and throughput is up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.42 ~\text{Kb} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>. These results verify that the suggested framework has a crucial impact on the stability of the clusters, the decrease of the re-election frequency, and the high efficiency of communication in the dense and dynamic VANET environment.

title: Methodological Aspects of Financial Competence Formation in the Structure of Supra-Professional Skills of Engineering Personnel
authors: Tatyana Y. Salutina, Yulia V. Gnezdova, Asiya R. Kaberova, Elena E. Volodina, Polina A. Zholtikova
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: The relevance of developing financial competence among future engineering personnel is determined by fundamental shifts in the global economy and industry. The purpose of this article is to develop and test the methodological foundations of financial competence formation as a structural element of the supra-professional skills of future engineers. To achieve this goal, the following tasks were solved in the study: to diagnose the demands of employers and the initial level of financial competencies of engineering students.; to develop a structural and substantive model of an engineer’s financial competence; to propose and experimentally test a methodological construct based on the principle of contextually embedded learning. The methodological basis of the study was based on systematic and competence-based approaches. The work used a set of theoretical (analysis of scientific literature, regulatory documents, modeling) and empirical (questionnaires, pedagogical experiment, methods of mathematical statistics) methods.

title: Single Event Upset Characterization in the Control Path of RISC-V Processor Core
authors: Shreyas R, Subir Kumar Roy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Functional Safety has emerged as a critical design requirement for modern-day SoCs operating in safety-critical environments. Single-event upsets (SEU) induced in hardware due to radiation or other transient disturbances pose a serious threat to the normal functionality of the design. Several SEU faults on the memories and data-path have been extensively analyzed and handled using both architectural and micro-architectural techniques. However, the control path is equally vulnerable and can potentially cause cascading failures, ultimately manifesting as a hardware malfunction. This paper focuses on extensively analyzing the SEU faults on the Open Source PULPino RISC-V core's control path, specifically for a motor control algorithm running on the CPU core. A specific methodology of fault analysis is introduced, and a vulnerability metric called Transition Architecture Vulnerability Factor (T-AVF), which maximizes the fault coverage on the control path for a given application running on the core. The results of Fault injection simulation on the core's control machines and the resultant T-AVFs are highlighted in this paper. This provides a foundation for designing future efficient, low-power, minimal area, and performance overhead-based mitigation techniques.

title: One-shot 3D Affordance Learning for Multi-stage Robotic Manipulation
authors: Hyunseo Kim, Yeon-Ji Song, Minsu Lee, Byoung-Tak Zhang
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Human-assistant robots must understand human-object interactions to execute collaborative manipulation tasks described in natural language. While affordance learning addresses this need, current approaches face a fundamental trade-off: 2D methods capture action-relevant object semantics but lack robust 3D geometric reasoning, whereas 3D methods demand labor-intensive point cloud datasets. To bridge this gap, we propose AffoRo-GS, a one-shot 3D affordance learning framework that grounds action verbs directly into 3D Gaussian Splatting representations. Our key insight is that verb-centric affordances from sparse 2D signals can be lifted into view-consistent 3D representations without explicit 3D annotations. Specifically, AffoRo-GS requires only a single affordance-labeled image per scene during training, and zero reference images at inference. Operating seamlessly with natural language, this approach eliminates the need for explicit object- or part-level queries, enabling rapid inference crucial for multi-stage tasks. Extensive real-world experiments demonstrate that our approach outperforms baselines reliant on 2D affordances or part-level reasoning, particularly in challenging long-horizon multi-stage settings.

title: TechRxiv: Share Your Preprint Research with the World!
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Human-Machine Systems

title: l 2 - l ∞ Proportional-Integral State Estimation for Stochastic Nonlinear Systems With State Saturations Under Decode-and-Forward Relay
authors: Xueyang Meng, Zidong Wang, Fan Wang, Yun Chen
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: This paper investigates the problem of l2-l∞ proportional-integral state estimation for stochastic nonlinear systems over decode-and-forward relay networks subject to packet losses. The system model is formulated to capture the effects of stochastic nonlinearities and state saturations, thereby reflecting practical engineering conditions. To alleviate transmission distance limitations, a decode-and-forward relay is introduced into the wireless channel to support communication from the sensor to the remote estimator, which enhances the reliability of long-distance data transmission. The objective is to design a proportional-integral state estimator that guarantees a prescribed finite-horizon l2-l∞ performance level for the estimation error dynamics, despite the presence of stochastic nonlinearities, state saturations, packet losses, and decoding errors. A sufficient condition for the existence of such an estimator is established, and the corresponding estimator gains are obtained by solving a set of matrix equalities. Finally, a simulation example is provided to demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed state estimation scheme.

title: An Efficient Data Generation Method Based on Flow Matching for Bearing Fault Diagnosis Under Imbalanced Data Conditions
authors: Mohammad Hatami Kakesh, Akbar Rahideh, Gholam Reza Agah, Mohammad Reza Peyghan, Shahin Hedayati Kia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Energy Conversion
abstract: The process of fault diagnosis is greatly constrained by imbalanced datasets, as a lack of fault data results in models with poor diagnostic performance. To overcome this issue, generative adversarial networks (GANs) have been utilized. However, they often suffer from training instability and are prone to mode collapse, where this critical issue causes the generator to produce a very limited variety of outputs. Consequently, this article proposes an efficient data augmentation framework based on flow matching for the domain of bearing fault diagnosis under imbalanced data. First, the continuous wavelet transform of vibration signals is employed to train a conditional flow matching model. After training, the model can generate new synthetic data by transforming a random Gaussian noise vector into a high-fidelity time–frequency spectrum. A key advantage of this approach is its computational efficiency, where the model produces diverse and authentic samples with as few as 5-15 generation steps, significantly reducing the generation time compared to traditional diffusion models. Furthermore, to comprehensively evaluate the diversity and fidelity of synthesized samples, various evaluation techniques, such as kernel inception distance (KID), the GAN-test/GAN-train metric and t-distributed stochastic neighbor embedding (t-SNE) visualizations of the learned feature space, are employed. Ultimately, a series of imbalanced fault diagnosis experiments was conducted to validate the practical effectiveness of the proposed method, resulting in final diagnosis accuracies of 95.90% and 97.31% on two widely used datasets and 98.81% on an actual test bed.

title: Lateral Anti-Misalignment Performance Improvement of the Single-Transmitter/Dual-Receiver WPT System with a Single Load
authors: Xu Liu, Dezhen Chen, Mingrui Yu, Hui Qian, Cancan Rong, Chenyang Xia, Hui Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: With the rapid advancement of wireless power transfer technology, its application scenarios have expanded significantly. However, the misalignment issue during power transmission, primarily due to the difficulty in precisely aligning charging devices, exerts a non-negligible impact on system performance. Given that automated guided vehicle misalignment predominantly manifests as lateral displacement, this study proposes an improved compensation topology and coil structure based on a single-transmitter and dual-receiver coil configuration. Meanwhile, this research further develops algorithmic approaches for parameter design criteria and optimization strategies to effectively enhance the system's lateral misalignment tolerance. The proposed compensation topology and parameter optimization methodology are validated through a 1 kW experimental prototype. And the experiment result shows that when the lateral alignment remains within 1/4 of the coil's maximum side length, the system's output power fluctuation can be controlled within 4.7%, and the maximum DC-DC efficiency of the system can reach 90.87%, with the efficiency fluctuation being within 1.6%.

authors: N/A

title: MOFM: A Multiple-in-One Flow Mamba for Unregistered Multi-Modal Image Fusion
authors: Bo Yang, Zhaohui Jiang, Dong Pan, Zhiping Lin, Weihua Gui
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Multi-modal image fusion aims to integrate complementary cues from different modalities into a single image, facilitating downstream tasks such as object detection. However, input image pairs are often misaligned due to rigid or non-rigid deformations in practical scenarios. Such misregistration introduces structural distortions and visual artifacts, reducing the reliability of the fused results and limiting their effectiveness for downstream vision applications. While existing methods demonstrate satisfactory results under specific deformation scenarios, they exhibit limited generalization to diverse and severe misregistrations. To this end, this study proposes a unified multiple-in-one flow Mamba framework for registering various image deformations and generating high-quality fused results. Specifically, a hierarchical flow Mamba is designed to model rigid and non-rigid flow fields and enhance adaptability to complex deformations by progressively refining misaligned features. To better distinguish between rigid and non-rigid types, a flow field classifier predicts rigid/non-rigid categories and provides prompts for high-level feature modulation. Furthermore, a flow-guided fusion Mamba module is developed to aggregate aligned multi-level modality features and generate fused images, while an iterative training strategy enables collaborative optimization by using fusion outputs to refine flow estimation. Experiments across three representative modality tasks demonstrate that the proposed method delivers superior fusion performance while maintaining applicability to object detection. The code will be available at: https://github.com/BOYang-pro/MOFM.

title: Stacked Intelligent Metasurfaces-enabled Transceiver: Functional Coding and Data-Enhanced Deep Unfolding Detection
authors: Shuyi Chen, Yingzhe Hui, Yifan Qin, Chang Su, Jiancheng An, Weixiao Meng, Chau Yuen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Recent studies have shown that the stacked intelligent metasurfaces (SIM) can exploit inter-layer electromagnetic (EM) wave transmission for wave-domain signal processing. This enables over-the-air computing with characteristics such as high-speed operation, low energy consumption, and support for multitasking parallel processing. By leveraging these advantages, SIM show great potential in substituting or augmenting customized communication functions in conventional wireless systems. To support various transmitter-customized communication functions, we design a novel multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) transmitter architecture based on SIM and develop corresponding detection algorithms. Initially, we present a SIM-enabled MIMO transceiver model. Unlike conventional wireless communication, the SIM-enabled transmitter automatically performs customized functions, such as precoding and channel encoding, as the EM waves propagate within the layers. At the receiver side, minimum mean square error (MMSE) and maximum likelihood (ML) detectors are adopted as benchmarks, and their theoretical performance bounds are derived accordingly. Furthermore, we propose a data-enhanced deep unfolding algorithm, namely orthogonal approximate message passing causal dilated convolutional transformer (OAMP-CDT), to demonstrate the performance of customized functions based on SIM. Simulation results verify that the proposed SIM-enabled MIMO transmitter supports customized functions via over-the-air signal processing, and the proposed OAMP-CDT algorithm outperforms the same series of algorithms, such as orthogonal approximate message passing (OAMP) and OAMP-Net2.

title: A Multi-AAV Assisted Mobile Edge Computing Task Offloading Based on Dynamic Priority and Two Stage Multi-Agent Collaborative Optimization
authors: Jiaxin Yan, Zhiang Gao, Junling Shi
publish: IEEE Networking Letters
abstract: In emergency and other dynamic scenarios, although autonomous aerial vehicle (AAV)-assisted mobile edge computing (MEC) alleviates the computational limitations of ground mobile devices (GMDs), its performance remains constrained in complex environments due to insufficient consideration of priority and fairness, coarse grained resource scheduling, and the strong coupling between discrete and continuous decision variables. The innovation of this paper lies in the proposal of a hierarchical cooperative task offloading framework, which incorporates a dynamic priority mechanism, multi-level geographic task grouping, and auction-based resource allocation strategy to address the issues of task priority and resource scheduling in task offloading. Additionally, a two stage cooperative optimization framework is employed, utilizing offline deep Q-network (DQN) for optimal AAV quantity selection and multi-agent deep deterministic policy gradient (MADDPG) for optimizing trajectory and service strategies. Simulation results show significant improvements in system utility, high priority task completion, and energy efficiency in dynamic environments.

title: USIGAN: Unbalanced Self-Information Feature Transport for Weakly Paired Image IHC Virtual Staining
authors: Yue Peng, Bing Xiong, Fuqiang Chen, Deboch Eybo Abera, RanRan Zhang, Wanming Hu, Jing Cai, Wenjian Qin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: Immunohistochemical (IHC) virtual staining is a task that generates virtual IHC images from H&E images while maintaining pathological semantic consistency with adjacent slices. This task aims to achieve cross-domain mapping between morphological structures and staining patterns through generative models, providing an efficient and cost-effective solution for pathological analysis. However, under weakly paired conditions, spatial heterogeneity between adjacent slices presents significant challenges. This can lead to inaccurate one-to-many mappings and generate results that are inconsistent with the pathological semantics of adjacent slices. To address this issue, we propose a novel unbalanced self-information feature transport for IHC virtual staining, named USIGAN, which extracts global morphological semantics without relying on positional correspondence. By removing weakly paired terms in the joint marginal distribution, we effectively mitigate the impact of weak pairing on joint distributions, thereby significantly improving the content consistency and pathological semantic consistency of the generated results. Moreover, we design the Unbalanced Optimal Transport Consistency Mining (UOT-CTM) mechanism and the Pathology Self-Correspondence Mining (PC-SCM) mechanism to construct correlation matrices between H&E and generated IHC in image-level and real IHC and generated IHC image sets in intra-group level. Experiments conducted on two publicly available datasets demonstrate that our method achieves superior performance across multiple clinically significant metrics, such as IoD and Pearson-R correlation, demonstrating better clinical relevance. The code is available at: https://github.com/MIXAILAB/USIGAN.

title: ISAC-enabled Beam Tracking for U2U Communication Systems under Jittering Effects
authors: Wei Liu, Yuhang Tang, Jinkun Zhu, Peng Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: A novel jitter-robust integrated sensing and communication (ISAC)-enabled beam tracking framework is proposed for unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV)-to-UAV (U2U) communication systems. In particular, a tethered UAV base station (UAV-B) serves a maneuvering UAV user (UAV-U), where jitter disrupts beam alignment and degrades communication rates. To enable beam alignment, a UAV-U angle tracking method is first proposed to estimate the relative angles between UAV-B and UAV-U. Specifically, the tethered UAV-B leverages sensing echo signals to obtain motion information of UAV-U, and integrates them with UAV-U’s prior motion models to enhance the accuracy of angle prediction. Meanwhile, through the communication link, UAV-U can receive the direction of UAV-B. Building on the obtained angle information, a jitter compensation algorithm is further developed to maximize the achievable rate of the U2U communication systems. In particular, UAV-B employs adaptive beamwidth control (ABC) to adjust its beamwidth and thus ensures robust coverage for UAV-U. Meanwhile, UAV-U employs beam steering refinement (BSR) to optimize the direction based on predicted angle information, enhancing the received signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) and reducing beam misalignment caused by jitter. Finally, our numerical results unveil that: 1) higher angle prediction accuracy is achieved by the proposed UAV-U angle tracking scheme compared to conventional Kalman filter-based angle tracking methods; and 2) a higher achievable rate in the U2U communication systems is achieved by the proposed jitter compensation scheme compared with traditional beamwidth control-based methods.

title: Improving Robustness of MRI-Based Brain Tumor Classification Via Multi-View Preprocessing and Hybrid VIT-CNN Learning
authors: Sabuj Maity, Jyoti Bharti, Vasudev Dehalwar, Anuj Khandelwal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Although magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is a critical tool in the clinical assessment of patients with suspected or confirmed <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}$</tex> rimary <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{b}$</tex> rain <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{t}$</tex> umors, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{c}$</tex> omputerized i mage interpretation for automatic classification ofb rain t umors has remained a significant <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{c}$</tex> hallenge. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{T}$</tex> he <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{c}$</tex> hallenges <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{i}$</tex> nclude the availability of sufficient a nnotated i mages, d ifferences i n image acquisition between scanners, variations in image quality associated with acquisition parameters, and others. Convolutional neural networks (CNNs) have demonstrated excellent capabilities for extracting localized anatomical features from MRI images. However, CNNs are limited in their ability to capture long-range spatial relationships because they have small receptive fields. On the other hand, Vision Transformers (ViTs) can capture longrange spatial relationships using self-attention mechanisms but they are less efficient in terms of the amount of data required to train them and may be unstable in training on the heterogeneity of medical images. Therefore, this study presents a clinically motivated hybrid CNN-Transformer model combined with a targeted multi-view preprocessing method. Unlike many previous studies that relied on large sets of augmentation operations to enhance generalization to different acquisition protocols, the preprocessing module produces three complementary representations of each MRI slice, which are created through baseline intensity normalization, brightness normalization, and leastvariant filtering. The goal of these transformations is to account for the variability caused by intensity shifts due to different MRI scanners, variability in backgrounds, and non-informative homogeneous regions that are common in many routine clinical MRI exams, and therefore to improve the robustness of the model to variations in appearance due to the acquisition protocol. The proposed architecture uses a CNN as the backbone to extract discriminative local features from MRI slices. A lightweight Transformer encoder is then used to aggregate convolutionally embedded patches to model global contextual relationships while preserving spatial coherence. An experimental evaluation was performed on a four-class brain tumor MRI dataset (glioma, meningioma, pituitary tumor, and non-tumor) that demonstrates the proposed approach achieves an accuracy of 96.42 %. The computational cost of the proposed architecture is also low and it exhibits stability across preprocessing views. Attention response maps demonstrate that the model focuses its attention on clinically relevant tumor regions, providing evidence for the model's interpretability. Overall, the results provide support for the feasibility and effectiveness of combining selective and clinically grounded preprocessing methods with hybrid CNN-Transformer modeling for brain tumor classification in heterogeneous MRI environments.

title: Cloud-IoT and AI-based Intelligent Waste Monitoring and Collection System for Sustainable Urban Development
authors: Suganya S, Priyadharsini N. K, Kommabatla Mahender, Rajesh Kumar A, Naitik S. T., A. Kathiravan
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Urban waste management has increasing problems due to rapid population increase and ineffective collection processes, resulting in overflow, environmental contamination, and decreasing resources. The proposed study handles these issues by offering an intelligent Cloud-IoT and AI-driven garbage monitoring and collecting system aimed at sustainable urban growth. The system features intelligent bins outfitted with sensors that relay real-time data to cloud platforms, where AI algorithms categorise trash types, predict waste generation, and dynamically optimise collection routes. The suggested framework utilises deep learning techniques, including CNNs and LSTMs, for enhanced accuracy and adaptability, in addition to previous study that depends on traditional machine learning algorithms. Experimental results indicate a classification accuracy of 97.8%, precision of 97.4%, and a R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score of 0.94 in trash forecasting, outperforming baseline methods such as SVM and Random Forest. Route optimisation decreases collecting time by 28%, so improving operating efficiency and reducing carbon emissions. This integrated strategy enhances trash management efficiency while advancing environmental sustainability objectives, providing a scalable and economical solution for smart cities. The research's practical consequences highlight its capacity to revolutionise municipal waste management, promoting cleaner and healthier urban settings.

title: Edu-EmotionNet: Cross-Modality Attention Alignment with Temporal Feedback Loops
authors: S M Rafiuddin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Understanding learner emotions in online education is critical for improving engagement and personalized instruction. While prior work in emotion recognition has explored multimodal fusion and temporal modeling, existing methods often rely on static fusion strategies and assume that modality inputs are consistently reliable, which is rarely the case in real-world learning environments. We introduce Edu-EmotionNet, a novel framework that jointly models temporal emotion evolution and modality reliability for robust affect recognition. Our model incorporates three key components- a Cross-Modality Attention Alignment (CMAA) module for dynamic cross-modal context sharing, a Modality Importance Estimator (MIE) that assigns confidence-based weights to each modality at every time step, and a Temporal Feedback Loop (TFL) that leverages previous predictions to enforce temporal consistency. Evaluated on educational subsets of IEMOCAP and MOSEI, re-annotated for confusion, curiosity, boredom, and frustration, Edu-EmotionNet achieves state-of-the-art performance and demonstrates strong robustness to missing or noisy modalities. Visualizations confirm its ability to capture emotional transitions and adaptively prioritize reliable signals, making it well suited for deployment in real-time learning systems.

title: Enhancing NFT Ownership and Trading with Blockchain based Decentralized Marketplaces
authors: Inbamalar T M, Abarna S, Abinaya G, Dhanalakshmi R, Dhanushiya E
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: A decentralized Non-Fungible Token (NFT) marketplace website powered by block chain technology enables secure and transparent trading of digital assets has been proposed. Unlike traditional platforms, this system eliminates the central authority and provides full control over their NFTs to the users. It incorporates smart contracts to automate transactions, ensuring efficiency and security. As there is no limit to crypto currency, the marketplace works exclusively through crypto currencies, enhancing global access and minimizing transaction fees. The platform also features a bargaining system, allowing buyers and sellers to negotiate prices directly. By utilizing decentralized storage solutions like IPFS, the project ensures secure, immutable storage of NFT metadata and assets. This website enables users to buy or sell their NFTs. The process involves selecting the desired NFT from the list of NFTs. The next step is the "make offer" system, where the buyer and seller negotiate the price. After that, the price is fixed, and the transaction is processed with the crypto currency in their wallet. Finally, the ownership is updated by the smart contract on the block chain and the database. This website sets a new standard for efficient, secure, and innovative NFT trading. Thus, it motivates people to buy assets from the internet as they have ownership over their assets

title: Automatic Dual-Battery Switching System for E-Vehicles using Solar Integration
authors: Barkavi M, Kodieswaran M, Gopalakrishnan R, Sri Vidhya D, Jaividhya S, Prabha K
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The growing worldwide call for sustainable transportation has elevated the adoption of electric automobiles (EVs). However, limitations which includes quick driving range, lengthy charging times, and reliance on charging infrastructure restrict their considerable application, especially in lengthy-distance or remote journey situations. This paper proposes a wise, twin-battery switching device to beautify the operational efficiency and variety of EVs by means of integrating sun electricity into the electricity architecture. The gadget is prepared with unbiased power sources: a number one battery charged through a traditional AC power deliver and an auxiliary battery charged through a rooftop solar panel. A microcontroller-primarily based control unit (ATmega328) is utilized to display the voltage degrees of each batteries and the sun panel in actual-time through committed voltage sensors. When the primary battery's voltage drops beneath a vital threshold, the controller routinely triggers a relay circuit to interchange the energy source to the auxiliary battery, ensuring uninterrupted motor operation. A 16x2 LCD offers actual-time voltage display and supply repute, even as an IoT module permits cloud-based tracking of machine parameters consisting of voltage degrees and battery switching activities. The device's conduct is verified using Proteus simulation, confirming the accuracy of switching logic and real-time overall performance. By intelligently combining solar electricity harvesting with computerized strength management, the proposed answer reduces dependency on grid electricity, helps longer tour distances, and promotes the adoption of renewable power in EV systems.

title:
Performance Enhancement of Rice Bran Oil–based Transformer Fluid Enhanced with TiO
                    <sub>₂</sub>
                    Nanoparticles as Dielectric Additives
authors: Bharanisree V, Abirami V, Dr.B.Vigneshwaran, Aarthi M
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: This study evaluates the dielectric properties and physicochemical strength of used transformer oil combined through blending with rice bran oil and enhanced with titanium dioxide (TiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">₂</inf>) nanoparticles. Different samples were taken in varying blend ratios, starting from 100% used transformer oil to 100% rice bran oil. In this, 0.1 g of TiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">₂</inf> nanoparticles was added to each 500 ml of blended oil sample. The key properties, such as breakdown voltage, viscosity, flash point, fire point, and acidity were evaluated and statistically analyzed. Results indicate that the incorporation of rice bran oil significantly improved the thermal and chemical properties of the used mineral oil, while TiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">₂</inf> nanoparticles improved its dielectric strength. This method demonstrates a sustainable and an effective approach for used transformer oil reclamation process in extending the life time of mineral oil and reducing environmental impact. To evaluate, a paired t-test was conducted using a data analysis tool to compare values before and after nanoparticle addition across all samples to determine whether the sample has a significant impact.

title: Deep Learning-Based Direction Prediction: A Comparative Study of Neural Architectures on XAU/USD 30-Minute Data with Win-Big-Lose-Small Strategy
authors: Ankit Kumar Singh, Ram Chandra Barik, Niranjan Behera, Surendra Kumar Panda
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: In this paper, Deep neural networks have been used to predict FOREX commodity price directions. After comparing seven neural network models on 60 days of 30-minute gold trading data (XAU/USD), with 1,897 trading records. The Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) was identified as the best-performing model, achieving a price direction accuracy of 53.41 %. As this accuracy is only 3.41 percentage points above random guessing, it has been demonstrated through real trading that this small edge when combined with a smart risk management strategy, generates a substantial amount of profit. It was implemented with a trading strategy based on the principle of “winning big while losing small”. After backtesting the model on TradingView Strategy Tester, it shows a profit of 3,752.91 USD across 94 trades with a profit factor of 2.518 and maximum drawdown of only 0.04 %. This good performance was achieved after winning only 39.36 % of our trades. The main factor in trading is that trading success depends more on managing risk in a trade rather than on prediction accuracy. This paper suggests that machine learning trading works best in real life market conditions when combined with proper position sizing and risk control.This paper also explains that the 30-minute timeframe represents the optimal timeframe for short-term trading. While profit factors generally increase with longer timeframes, 30-minute intervals always allows for faster capital accumulation by increasing the number of trades taken. Our approach is validated by using time-series methodology including stationarity analysis, feature engineering with 46 features extracted from the raw price data, and proper train-test splitting.

title: Reconstruction-Free Classification for Lensless Imaging Systems
authors: Pramil Paudel, Fengjun Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Lensless imaging offers a promising, privacy-preserving alternative to traditional cameras by capturing unintelligible diffraction patterns instead of direct scene images. This enables compact, lightweight, and energy-efficient imaging systems suitable for resource-constrained surveillance applications such as UAVs and wearable devices. However, conventional lensless systems require computationally intensive image reconstruction, which compromises both privacy and efficiency. In this work, we propose a robust, reconstruction-free lensless image classification framework based on a modified ResNet18 architecture. To address the lack of structure in lensless data, we introduce a frequency-domain learning strategy using block-DCT applied to non-overlapping spatial blocks. Our method achieves 97.61% accuracy on a real-world lensless face recognition dataset and 93.98% on a simulated remote sensing benchmark, outperforming prior reconstruction-free approaches. These results demonstrate the feasibility of high-accuracy lensless recognition without reconstruction and pave the way for practical, privacy-aware deployment in real-world surveillance scenarios.

title: Transformer Bushing Health Monitoring via Embedded Nanoparticle-Coated Micro-Sensors
authors: Naveen Garg
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: Transformer bushings are vital insulation materials in high voltage power transformers, and their failure may have devastating effects of fires, transformer outages, and grid-wide crashes.
