title: Transporting the Cross-modal Prototypes for Unsupervised Visible-Infrared Person Re-identification
authors: Zhizhong Zhang, Jiaqi Chen, Xin Tan, Yuan Xie, Yan Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: Unsupervised visible infrared person reidentification (USVI-ReID) is a challenging retrieval task that retrieves cross-modality pedestrian images without using any label information. In this task, the large cross-modality variance makes it difficult to generate reliable cross-modality labels, and the lack of annotations also provides additional difficulties for learning modality-invariant features. To facilitate this unsupervised cross-modal learning, we begin by leveraging the information contained in the cross-modality input and its predicted label. Aiming to minimize information loss, we optimize the model by incorporating entropy minimization, uniform label distribution, and cross-modality matching. In our approach, we design a loop iterative training strategy alternating between model training and cross-modality matching, where a uniform prior guided optimal transport assignment is proposed to select matched visible and infrared prototypes. This matching information is then utilized to minimize the intra- and cross-modality entropy. As a result, our model can gradually self-learn useful information, enabling it to generate discriminative representations for unlabeled cross-modal data. Extensive experimental results on benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness of our method, e.g., 69.4% and 89.4% of Rank-1 accuracy on SYSU-MM01 and RegDB without any annotations. The code will be released soon.

title: Analysis of Brittle Fracture Characteristics Under Static/Dynamic Loads in Silicon-Based MEMS Cantilevers
authors: Mo Yang, Baolin Cheng, Weirong Nie, Mingyuyi Shi, Shenghong Lei, He Wang, Shijuan Dai, Yun Cao
publish: Journal of Microelectromechanical Systems
abstract: Silicon-based MEMS cantilevers are widely used in high-reliability microsystems. Addressing the unclear relationship between the fracture strength of silicon-based MEMS cantilevers under static loads and high-strain-rate dynamic loads, this study investigates the typical cantilever-type setback safety device in fuze MEMS safety and arming devices. A dynamic model of a cantilever with a concentrated mass at the end was established. Through material characterization, static and dynamic loading experiments, and numerical simulations, the fracture response characteristics were systematically examined. The crystal orientation and mechanical parameters of (111)-oriented single-crystal silicon were verified through X-ray diffraction and nanoindentation testing. The critical fracture stress of cantilevers was obtained under quasi-static loading and half-sine acceleration impact loading. The results indicate that the fracture stress under dynamic load is significantly lower than that under static load. The load factor <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$K$</tex-math> </inline-formula> was introduced to effectively describe the relationship, with an average value of approximately 3.0620. Fracture section analysis further revealed the fundamental differences in crack propagation patterns under varying load conditions. The findings can serve as guidance for the engineering design of silicon-based cantilever.[2026-0027]

title: Tilt-Angle Tuning in mmWave Base Stations and Non-terrestrial IAB for Mission-Critical Communications
authors: Inam Ullah, Hesham El-Sayed, Alexis Dowhuszko, Manzoor Ahmed Khan, Jyri Hämäläinen
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: The rollout of Fifth-Generation (5G) technologies has highlighted the need for robust, high-reliability communications to support mission-critical applications. This new kind of service requires that future mobile networks ensure seamless connectivity, high bandwidth, and stringent quality of service, particularly in emergency-response scenarios. To meet these stringent demands, the use of Integrated-Access-and-Backhaul (IAB) nodes mounted on unmanned aerial vehicles offers a promising solution by not only enhancing data rates via improved line-of-sight propagation conditions to the donor Base Station (BS) via backhaul link, but also enabling on-demand coverage in mission-critical scenarios requiring temporary cellular coverage. This paper investigates the performance gain that is feasible in a Millimeter Wave (mmWave) terrestrial network that uses non-terrestrial IAB nodes to enhance coverage in mission-critical applications, with a focus on optimizing the antenna down-tilt angles of both donor BS and IAB node to maximize the End-to-End (E2E) spectral efficiency (SE) for ground mobile users. The proposed framework aims to improve the backhaul link quality and ensure reliable, high E2E SE connectivity in urban environments characterized by non-line-of-sight conditions when donor BSs and mobile users are placed on different streets. Simulation results show that the joint optimization of donor BS and non-terrestrial IAB node antenna down-tilt angles, combined with best path selection, enhanced the E2E SE of mobile users by 105% at IAB down-tilt angles of −45° (3.9 bps/Hz), compared to the baseline −90° configuration (1.9 bps/Hz).

title: Application of Machine Learning Approach for Medicine Recommendation in Healthcare System
authors: Siya Mandal, Namrata Dey, Sanjeev Patel
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Healthcare is a very crucial domain that should evolve to meet the growing demands of the population and unpredictable global crisis. In challenging situations, such as pandemics, natural disasters, or other medical emergencies, patients may not receive proper treatment due to high demand. Recognising this shift, we propose a machine learning-based medicine recommendation system that allows users to input symptoms to detect possible diseases, receive basic descriptions, and obtain corresponding medicine suggestions. In cases where direct medication is not available, the system provides essential instructions to manage the condition. This model aims to serve as a trustworthy platform for early diagnosis, especially when traditional healthcare access is limited. By bridging the gap between online health inquiries and clinical insight. Specifically, it focuses on improving healthcare accessibility and reliability in both everyday and emergencies. The results support the use of Random Forest, which achieved perfect classification performance, closely followed by K-Nearest Neighbour. In contrast, SVM underperformed due to its sensitivity to high-dimensional data. The system also incorporates safety features, such as precautionary measures to consult a physician when the prediction confidence is low, ensuring ethical reliability.

title: Triple-Stream Ensemble Fusion for Resilient Multi-Modal Human Action Recognition: Addressing Generalization in Co-Located Sensing
authors: Suraj Shandilya, Mahendra Siyag, Muskan Kumari, Rajkishor Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Human Action Recognition (HAR) with high accuracy in private areas is a hard task. Even though camera-based systems are accurate, they create serious privacy issues. On the other hand, RF sensing (WiFi) is passive and protects privacy, but it suffers from signal noise and environmental changes. In this paper, we introduce the Triple-Stream Multi-Modal Fusion Framework, which uses the combined benefits of skeletal vision data and WiFi Channel State Information (CSI). We study three different architectures: a lightweight Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) for spatial data, an Adaptive Graph Convolutional Network (GCN) for structure, and a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network for time sequences. Experiments on the MMFi dataset show an unexpected result: although complex GCNs train well, they fail to generalize to new people. The simpler MLP works better on unseen subjects <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(72.25 {\%}$</tex> vs <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$70.62 {\%})$</tex>. To fix this, we use a Probabilistic Ensemble Fusion approach. By optimizing weights on a validation set <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.5 W_{M L P}+0.4 W_{G C N}+0.1 W_{L S T M})$</tex>, we raised the final accuracy to 75.64%, beating the single best model by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+3.4 {\%}$</tex>. These results show that combining models can reduce overfitting and offers a practical way to do privacypreserving sensing on edge devices.

title: Bio-RV: Low-Power Resource-Efficient RISC-V Processor for Biomedical Applications
authors: Vijay Pratap Sharma, Annu Kumar, Mohd Faisal Khan, Mukul Lokhande, Santosh Kumar Vishvakarma
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This work presents Bio-RV, a compact and resourceefficient RISC-V processor intended for biomedical control applications, such as accelerator-based biomedical SoCs and implantable pacemaker systems. The proposed Bio-RV is a multi-cycle RV32I core that provides explicit execution control and external instruction loading with capabilities that enable controlled firmware deployment, ASIC bring-up, and post-silicon testing. In addition to coordinating accelerator configuration and data transmission in heterogeneous systems, Bio-RV is designed to function as a lightweight host controller, handling interfaces with pacing, sensing, electrogram (EGM), telemetry, and battery management modules. With 708 LUTs and 235 flip-flops on FPGA prototypes, Bio-RV, implemented in a 180 nm CMOS technology, operates at 50 MHz and features compact hardware footprint. According to post-layout results, the proposed architectural decisions align with minimal energy use. Ultimately, Bio-RV prioritises deterministic execution, minimal hardware complexity, and integration flexibility over peak computing speed to meet the demands of ultra-low-power, safety-critical biomedical systems.

title: Micro-defect Detection Based on Application of Mixing Frequency Ultrasound
authors: Ganlu Zeng, Shengzi Lu, Xufeng Li, Lei Wang, Pengan Zhu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: The insufficient detection capability of micro defects limits the research and application of high-strength metal materials in China. Due to the energy attenuation and low signal-to-noise ratio of ultrasound signals, it is difficult to detect micro defects in thick components using conventional ultrasound pulse reflection methods. Compared with conventional linear ultrasonic testing methods, nonlinear ultrasonic testing methods are more sensitive to micro defects and have become a new research hotspot in the non-destructive testing industry in recent years. In order to realize the identification and location of micro defects in thick components, the collinear anisotropic longitudinal wave mixing method is used to identify micro defects through sum and difference frequency signal characteristics. The influence of the change of defect buried depth on the mixing effect is studied. The depth location of micro defects is realized by measuring the amplitude change of differential frequency signal. The results show that this method can effectively identify the transverse hole micro defects with a buried depth of 80mm in 7075 aluminum alloy, and can realize the depth location of micro defects.

title: Learning from Execution Graphs: A Graph Neural Network Approach to Malware Detection
authors: Hossein Shokouhinejad, Mackenzie Chase, Roozbeh Razavi-Far
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: The growing sophistication and scale of malicious software demand detection methods capable of capturing both the behavioral complexity and structural relationships in program execution. Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have shown strong potential for malware detection by leveraging graph-structured representations such as Control Flow Graphs (CFGs), which preserve execution semantics and interdependencies between program components. When extracted dynamically, CFGs reflect actual runtime behavior, offering richer and more reliable information than static analysis alone. At the same time, GNN performance is sensitive to hyperparameter configurations, where suboptimal settings can lead to over-smoothing, reduced accuracy, or excessive computational cost. This paper presents a systematic study that combines the development of a GNN-based malware detection framework using dynamically extracted CFGs with a detailed analysis of hyperparameter sensitivity. We investigate the influence of model depth, hidden dimensions, learning rate, weight decay, and training epochs using two representative architectures, Graph Convolutional Networks (GCN) and GraphSAGE. The findings provide empirical insights and practical guidelines for building powerful, efficient, and well-tuned GNN-based malware detection systems, with applicability to other graph classification domains.

title: Smart Fall Detection for Elderly using Wearable Sensors and Machine Learning Analytics
authors: M Arun Kumar, M.Kalimuthu, Jayasurya J, Kavinshankar K, John Sham A
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The Fall Detection System for Elderly Patients is a cutting-edge technology designed to improve the security and health of senior citizens, especially those who live alone or in assisted living facilities. In order to precisely identify falls and track patient movement patterns in real time, this system combines wearable sensors with intelligent algorithms. The primary feature is the combination of accelerometer and gyroscope data, which allows for the accurate detection of abrupt impacts, odd postures, and inactivity after a possible fall. In order to ensure prompt action, the system instantly notifies emergency contacts or caregivers via cloud-based platforms or mobile notifications upon detection. Advanced features like motion trend analysis, posture classification, and ambient condition monitoring help reduce false positives and improve situational awareness. The gadget is perfect for continuous monitoring because it is comfortable, lightweight, and made to be worn for extended periods of time without causing discomfort. The system provides smooth data transfer to healthcare providers for continuous evaluation and proactive care planning thanks to its wireless communication integration and battery-efficient operation. This technology dramatically lowers the risks of fall-related injuries, hospitalizations, and long-term health decline by tackling the crucial problem of unreported or delayed responses to falls. In the end, the Fall Detection System gives older patients more autonomy while bringing comfort to their families and caregivers.

title: Enhancing Image Classification Performance Using Data Augmentation and Adaptive Training Strategies
authors: Murali Mohan Reddy M, S. Masood Ahamed
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Real-world Indian image collections exhibit large intra-class variance, severe class imbalance, and localized discriminative cues (for example rare traffic signs, regionspecific crop lesions, and food items), which reduce the effectiveness of generic augmentation policies and standard training schedules. The objective of this study is to evaluate a multi-scale, structure-aware augmentation pipeline, combined with adaptive training strategies, for improving classification accuracy, calibration, and robustness on Indian image data. This work proposes SAIA, a multi-scale augmentation module that produces global, regional, and feature-level variants, and then uses an attention-based patch scorer to rank and sparsify augmented candidates. SAIA is complemented by an adaptive training suite that applies class-aware learning rates, samplehardness weighting, curriculum scheduling, and jump-fusion of complementary backbone embeddings. Experiments compare the approach to strong baselines (ResNet variants with MixUp/CutMix and RandAugment) on curated India-focused datasets, with ablation and robustness tests under noise, occlusion, and illumination shifts. Across multiple tasks, SAIA and adaptive training yields consistent improvements with relative Top-1 accuracy gains of about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3-6 \%$</tex> over strong augmentation baselines, macro F1 gains of 0.04-0.10, and notably smaller robustness degradations. Minority-class recall improves by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 - 1 0}$</tex> points. Structure-aware augmentation, with adaptive optimization, produces more informative, augmented views, and fairer learning with extensions to semi-supervised and cross-region transfer as future directions.

title: System and Method for Automated Redaction of Personal Identifiable Information Using Responsible AI and Computer Vision
authors: Nowrin Begum R, Jazila Begum B, Syed Aejaz Ahmed A, Sam V George
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This paper is about deep learning method for automatically redacting sensitive personal identifiable information, or PII from high resolution Aadhaar card images. It gives some top-notch natural language understanding for text. We ran a bunch of experiments putting this against the latest computer vision setups like YOLO models for spotting sensitive information like ID cards and contracts. Those hold names, addresses, Aadhaar number, father's name and date of birth. We zeroed in on the newest YOLO versions, YOLOV5, YOLOV8, and YOLOV11, tweaking them on our own PII dataset. Trained them end-to-end from those high-resolution scans, which really bumped up detection accuracy and precision way beyond the basic YOLO setup. To check reliability, we looked at metrics like precision, recall, accuracy, F1 score for the classifier itself. We add OCR for multilingual docs plus contextual checks and made redaction solid even in noisy spots. The whole approach delivers accuracy and runs efficiently, generalizing better than baselines. Our work drives home that in privacy-heavy fields with high detection rates with interpretability and real personal information safeguard.

title: Assessing Operational Autonomy in Decentralized Energy Systems
authors: Ailén Kreizer, Julius Frerk, Michael Popp, Markus Zdrallek
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: Energy autonomy in decentralized, inverter- dominated power systems is commonly assessed using annual, energy-based metrics. Existing autonomy metrics remain largely energy-centric and focus on annual balances such as self-sufficiency ratios. While useful, these metrics fail to reflect operational independence during real-time operation. Systems can therefore appear highly autonomous on an annual basis while remaining dependent on upstream grids for significant portions of time. This paper introduces an operational autonomy framework that combines extended metrics with an optimized battery energy storage system operation. In addition to temporal self-sufficiency measured as continuous hours without upstream grid support, the framework enforces reserve-aware battery constraints to guarantee stability services. Crucially, the proposed control strategy optimizes BESS charging not only from local surplus but also from the upstream grid in anticipation of future deficits. Results show that operational BESS optimization is essential to achieve meaningful autonomy beyond annual energy balances.

title: A Novel Strategy for Boosting Performance and Power Efficiency of Multicore WNoC Systems
authors: Abu Asaduzzaman, Koteswara R. Pandi, Md Raihan Uddin, Christian C. Thompson
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: Wireless network-on-chip (WNoC) has emerged as a promising solution to address the growing demands for both performance and power efficiency of multicore systems. However, subnet-based WNoC architecture often faces issues with inefficient core utilization, leading to decreased performance and localized overheating. This paper introduces a novel approach that combines dynamic core selection and job scheduling to improve the performance and power efficiency of WNoC systems. The proposed method selects nodes for wireless routers based on hop count, uses the shortest remaining job first (SRJF) algorithm to prioritize jobs for execution, and assigns cores with the lowest heat indicators to execute the selected jobs. We simulate a 49-core WNoC with 1-hop (where a core requires only one hop to reach the nearest wireless router), 2-hop, and 3-hop configurations. The simulation programs are run using a representative workload of 92 jobs to assess the effectiveness of the proposed method. Simulation results show that the 2-hop WNoC configuration performs optimally, reducing wireless routers by 57% compared to a 1-hop configuration, and reducing latency and power consumption by 17% and 20%, respectively, compared to a 3-hop configuration. The 2-hop WNoC achieves an average latency and power consumption reduction up to 31% and 34%, respectively, compared to subnet-based WNoC architecture. Furthermore, the proposed strategy effectively balances heat distribution across the WNoC chip without creating any high heat zones.

title: Joint Nested-Array and Frequency Offset Design for FDA-MIMO Radar Under Quasi-Static Interference
authors: Yimeng Zhang, Wenxing Li, Bin Yang, Chuanji Zhu, Kai Dong
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Frequency diverse array (FDA) MIMO radar achieves range–angle dependent beampatterns by introducing frequency offsets across transmit elements, offering additional degrees of freedom for interference suppression. However, its performance is highly sensitive to interference uncertainty, particularly in quasi-static scenarios with slowly varying interference ranges. This paper presents a joint nested transmit array and frequency offset design for FDA-MIMO radar under quasi-static interference. A low-dimensional parametric frequency offset model is optimized via a robustness-oriented differential evolution strategy, effectively mitigating interference range uncertainty without resorting to high-dimensional optimization. The nested transmit array further enhances spatial degrees of freedom, complementing the frequency-domain design and improving overall robustness. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves more stable beampattern focusing and higher output SINR than conventional FDA schemes and representative sparse array designs. In addition, the proposed approach exhibits strong robustness against array position errors and gain–phase mismatches, highlighting its suitability for practical implementations.

title: Development of a Hybrid Piezoelectric and Solar Rainfall Energy Harvesting System with Water Collection Storage and Python-Based Performance
authors: Joshua Bryant B. Anilao, Gene Gabriel B. Payumo, Noel A. Salalila, Carlos C. Hortinela
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This study presents the development and validation of a novel, integrated hybrid system designed to enhance resource sustainability by simultaneously harvesting solar energy, converting raindrop kinetic energy via a piezoelectric array, and collecting rainwater through an integrated storage mechanism. The system was constructed as a compact, multifunctional prototype suitable for locations characterized by highly variable weather conditions. The methodology involved designing and fabricating the hardware, implementing smart control algorithms for power and water flow management, and evaluating the overall system performance through controlled experimental testing. Results from the piezoelectric subsystem confirmed a clear correlation between rainfall intensity (Light Rainfall at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.56 \text{mL} / \mathrm{s}$</tex> and Heavy Rainfall at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.25 \text{mL} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>) and instantaneous voltage generation, with peak outputs observed under high intensity conditions. Concurrently, the photovoltaic (PV) subsystem analysis characterized thermal and irradiancedriven derating effects on power output. Hybrid power flow measurements verified that the combined system can reliably operate despite the contrasting electrical profiles of the transient piezoelectric and steady-state solar sources. This research demonstrates the technical feasibility of a unified platform that enhances energy availability and environmental adaptability through the strategic integration of multiple ambient resources.

title: RetCareNet: Real-Time Precision Healthcare through IoT–VANET Integration and RetNet-based Analytics
authors: Jaganathan J, Mohin Kumar V S, Prasanna Sai Kumar R, A. Bhagya Lakshmi
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Emergency healthcare services require quick diagnostics and effective use of medical resources. However, many current systems based on the Internet of Things (IoT) and Vehicular Ad Hoc Networks (VANET) face issues like delays, problems with scaling, and limited flexibility. To tackle these challenges, this paper presents RetCareNet, a framework that combines IoT devices, VANETs, and Retentive Networks (RetNet) to enable real-time healthcare with precision. Health indicators from patients are collected through wearable IoT sensors and sent via VANET-enabled ambulances. These indicators are then assessed using RetNet, which effectively detects long sequences of anomalies while keeping computational demands low. At the same time, Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MADDPG) manages crucial hospital resources dynamically, ensuring fair use during emergencies. Experiments show that RetCareNet reaches a diagnostic accuracy of 97%, cuts response time to 45 minutes, lowers network latency to 120 milliseconds, and improves hospital resource use to 95%, consistently outpacing traditional systems. The innovative aspect of RetCareNet lies in its combination of effective sequence modeling and flexible resource management. With strong scalability, reliable communication protocols, and quick performance, RetCareNet offers a promising solution for intelligent emergency healthcare.

title: AI-Driven Personalization in the Classroom: Impacts on Intrinsic Motivation and Academic Performance
authors: Parul Dubey, Abhinesh Kumar Sahu, Anshul Sharma, Om Balram Sharma, Saurabh Mishra, Manjushree Nayak
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Primary school mathematics education is an important area where learner motivation and engagement play a significant role in academic achievement in the long run. Recent progress in artificial intelligence has raised the possibility of creating adaptive and personalized learning systems. Yet, conventional teaching methods are based on common sources that do not take into consideration the variety of interests present among students leading to lack of interest and reduced achievement. This gap can be bridged by scaling methods that dynamically accommodate different learner profiles. Data were derived from 114 German elementary school students who provided pre-test/post-test performance, as well as measures regarding intrinsic motivation and self-reported personal interest. It included both the measurable test scores and qualitative interest data, which created a broad base for examination. The design was <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times 2$</tex> mixed factorials (learning content: personalized vs. standard between subjects; learning type: personalized vs. standard within subject). Educational resources and exercises were as generated using a Generative AI implemented over school mathematics curriculum. The contribution of our study is to use the free-form nature of student interests for fine-grained personalization, instead of predened categories. Performance was assessed by test accuracy, intrinsic motivation self-reports and engagement indicators. Results indicate that AI-based personalization significantly increased intrinsic motivation, situational interest, learning performance and reengagement intention than the traditional materials and thus shows potential in scalable adoption.

title: Passivity-Based Analysis and Control of Low-Inertia Islanded Microgrids
authors: Hyun Cheol Cho, M. Sami Fadali, Abdelrahman Abdelkader, Mohammed Ben-Idris
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: Islanded microgrids (MGs) are susceptible to unfolding collapses. Low-inertia inverter-based resources (IBRs) struggle to maintain normal operation even with small disturbances such as load variations. This paper proposes a passivity-based approach for distributed stability analysis and control system design of IBRs that enhances the stability of islanded MGs. A dynamic nonlinear MG model is utilized to demonstrate passivity conditions for the nonlinear microgrid model and its reliance on $P - f/Q - \dot V$ droop control parameters. The concept of local passivity is utilized to analyze the linearized models to determine passivity conditions for the nonlinear model. The passivity conditions are then used to determine the control parameters for the $P - f/Q - \dot V$ droop control systems that ensure the stability of the MG. The proposed model and control systems for an islanded MG with IBRs are simulated to demonstrate the passivity property of the nonlinear models of MGs and the stability of the system under disturbance. The results show that the system closely tracks control signals with fast response times.

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks in Low-Data Regimes: A Comparative Study with Multilayer Perceptrons
authors: Farhad Pourkamali-Anaraki
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Multilayer Perceptrons (MLPs) have long been a cornerstone in deep learning, known for their capacity to model complex relationships. Recently, Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs) have emerged as a compelling alternative, utilizing highly flexible learnable activation functions directly on network edges, a departure from the neuron-centric approach of MLPs. However, KANs significantly increase the number of learnable parameters, raising concerns about their effectiveness in data-scarce environments. Hence, this paper presents a comprehensive comparative study of MLPs and KANs from both algorithmic and experimental perspectives, with a focus on low-data regimes. We introduce an effective technique for designing MLPs with unique, parameterized activation functions for each neuron, enabling a more balanced comparison with KANs. Using empirical evaluations on two real-world data sets from medicine and engineering, we explore the trade-offs between model complexity and accuracy. Our findings show that MLPs with individualized activation functions achieve higher predictive accuracy with only a modest increase in parameters, especially when the sample size is limited to around one hundred. For example, in a classification problem within additive manufacturing, MLPs achieve a median accuracy of 0.91, significantly outperforming KANs, which only reach a median accuracy of 0.53. In addition, we analyze key factors affecting KAN performance, including the polynomial order and grid size of spline-based activation functions.

title: Enhancing Distribution Grid Resilience: A Data-Driven Calibration of Physics-Based Fragility Models Using Historical Outage Records
authors: Hanbyeol Shin, Abdollah Shafieezadeh, Salman Mohagheghi, Mohamed Illafe
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: This study presents a computational framework for calibrating physics-based fragility models of power distribution systems using verifiable system-level outage data. Bridging the gap between component-level mechanics and aggregated system performance constitutes a complex inverse problem. To address this, we implement a direct Bayesian calibration framework that couples a topology-aware physics simulator with a Poisson-based likelihood function. This approach allows for the rigorous inference of structural parameters directly from historical outage records. When applied to a distribution feeder, the framework successfully identifies latent structural degradation. The calibrated system captures the ground truth outage count within its predictive bounds, validating the method’s ability to transform generalized fragility curves into site-specific digital twins that better quantify the risks of aging infrastructure.

title: Comparative Analysis of LoRaWAN and NB-IoT for Wide-Area IoT Deployments: Performance, Scalability, and Cost Considerations
authors: T Suvarna Kumari, Lendale Venkateswarlu, Naveen Kumar Pedada, Bandi Rambabu, Satyanarayana Nimmala, M Archana
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Wide-Area Internet of Things (WA-IoT) technologies have become foundational for applications requiring long-range, low-power connectivity, such as smart metering, agriculture, asset tracking, and smart city infrastructure. Among these, LoRaWAN and NB-IoT have emerged as leading contenders, each offering distinct advantages and trade-offs. This paper presents a comprehensive comparative analysis of LoRaWAN and NB-IoT in terms of performance (range, data rate, latency, power consumption, and interference), scalability (network capacity, density handling), and cost (device, infrastructure, connectivity, and total cost of ownership). The findings reveal that LoRaWAN is more suitable for rural, low-power, delay-tolerant deployments due to its long-range capabilities and minimal infrastructure cost, while NB-IoT is better aligned with urban, real-time, and carrier-managed scenarios due to its reliability, lower latency, and integration with existing LTE infrastructure. Through tabulated comparisons and real-world deployment use cases, this study provides practical guidelines for technology selection and explores future trends, including hybrid models and evolving standards such as LoRaWAN 2.0 and 5G NB-IoT.

title: Enhancing Island Power Systems Operational Flexibility with Hybrid Power Plants
authors: Yuxin Deng, Xin Fang, Weijia Liu, Jin Tan
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: Hybrid power plants (HPPs) have emerged as a promising approach to enhance operational flexibility in modern power systems. However, effectively leveraging the operational flexibility of HPPs remains a significant challenge, as existing studies lack a comprehensive, generic modeling approach suitable for investigating flexibility-driven dispatch strategies. To address this gap, this paper develops an enhanced generic modeling framework for HPPs. A generic HPP identification formulation based on incidence matrices is proposed to integrate plant-level and component-level models. In addition, a unified HPP capacity formulation applicable to both AC-coupled and DC-coupled HPP configurations is developed, and the system operator’s operational preference is explicitly modeled. Different flexibility-driven dispatch strategies are investigated on a realistic island power system. The results show that enabling HPP net charging is the most effective measure to enhance operational flexibility.

title: A Classification based on Salp Swarm Algorithm for Incomplete Imbalanced Data
authors: Yi Liu, Yangsen Zhou, Qibin Zheng, Guoli Yang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Data classification is significant important for artificial intelligence applications, and its performance rely on the characteristics of data. Many useful methods are advanced to resolve it. However, some general reasons lead to the occurrence of incomplete imbalanced data, and deteriorate capability of conventional approaches. A novel approach called Salp swarm algorithm for Incomplete Imbalanced Data (SIID) is developed in this paper. It adopts feature selection to improve classification accuracy, uses multiple data imputation methods to fill in missing positions, and applies undersampling and oversampling simultaneously to balance data. Besides, it employs salp swarm algorithm to optimize corresponding parameters for obtaining high accurate classification outcomes. Five representative datasets and four outstanding algorithms are taken to evaluate our way. The results on four indicators demonstrate that SIID is superior to compared methods and the proposed components are effective.

title: DistilBERT-Powered Optimized Sentiment Analysis for Next-Generation Natural Language Processing
authors: S.R.Menaka, S.Mohanasundaram, P.Sukumar, S.M.Ramesh, Ravikumar Gurusamy, S.Hemalatha
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: This proposed system aims to assess the effectiveness and performance of DistilBERT, a streamlined transformer-based model, in sentiment classification tasks, in comparison to BERT. It centers on investigating whether DistilBERT can deliver comparable sentiment analysis results while minimizing computational resource demands, thereby make it suitable for real-time scenario and settings with limited resources. This study involves two distinct groups. Group 1 pertains to sentiment classification utilizing BERT, also with 26 samples. The power analysis is set at 80%, and the confidence interval at 95% the significance level at 0.05%, pertains to sentiment classification utilizing DistilBERT, which comprises 26 samples, while Group 2 pertains to sentiment classification utilizing DistilBERT, which comprises 26 samples. The DistilBERT model offers impressive accuracy while requiring much less computational power than BERT. DistilBERT's accuracy ranges between 91.34% to 97.89%, in contrast to BERT, which reaches an accuracy range of 88.12% and 96.45%. DistilBERT offers an effective approach to sentiment analysis, achieving impressive accuracy while requiring less computational power.

title: Scheduling and Optimization of EV Fleet Charging using Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
authors: Puja Mandavkar, Kunal Sawalakhe, Ganesh Wakte, Pritesh Mhaiskar, Akansha Sontakke, Grishma Bahadure
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Electric vehicles (EVs) are becoming more popular every year, not only among individuals but also within large organizations like delivery services, taxi fleets, and bus companies. This growing number of EVs presents a serious challenge for electricity providers and fleet managers, especially when multiple vehicles need to be charged at the same time. If charging is not properly scheduled, it can lead to high electricity costs, grid overload, and unavailability of vehicles when they are needed. To address this issue, this paper proposes a smart charging strategy using Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL). In this approach, each charging station or EV is controlled by an intelligent software agent that can learn the best time and speed to charge. These agents make decisions by learning from past experiences and by communicating with each other, similar to a team working together. They continuously adapt their strategies based on electricity prices, vehicle schedules, and real-time conditions. Our experimental results show that the proposed system can reduce electricity costs by up to 23% compared to traditional rule-based methods, while maintaining 99.2% vehicle readiness. The system also shows strong adaptability when faced with dynamic changes in pricing and vehicle return times. This research demonstrates that MARL-based charging strategies can significantly improve the efficiency, cost-effectiveness, and scalability of EV fleet operations, supporting the broader adoption of clean and smart transportation systems.

title: Clustering and Explainable AI for Supporting Energy Contracting in the Brazilian Free Energy Market
authors: Sérgio B. Júnior, Gabriel S. Matz, Marcos B. S. Paula, Ewerton Guarnier, Donato S. Filho, Raquel L. Melo, Lucas B. Picarelli, Victor C. V. Rosa, Zhao Liang, Renato Tinós
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: As Brazil transitions toward a liberalized electricity market, energy portfolio managers face increasing complexity when evaluating new contracts. The growing presence of non-controllable renewable sources, alongside consumers with diverse and uncertain demand patterns, intensifies volume and shape risks in energy trading. This study proposes an Explainable Artificial Intelligence-based methodology to help decision-makers incorporate new power generators and consumers into energy portfolios. The approach begins by clustering historical time series data from wind and solar generation units and consumers to identify typical behavioral profiles. These clusters become labels for training supervised classification models that predict a new participant’s likely profile based on spatial and temporal features. Of the tested models, the Multilayer Perceptron achieved the highest performance. To address the opacity of the black-box model, we apply the Local Rule-based Explainer, which produces interpretable, rule-based explanations and counterfactual examples. It enables stakeholders to understand the rationale behind each classification and to assess how minor feature changes impact predictions. By combining high-accuracy prediction with transparent model interpretation, the proposed methodology improves shape and volume risk assessment in energy contracting. It also gives actionable insights to support strategic decisions in a dynamic, decentralized energy market.

title: Synthetic Data Generation with GANs for Rareevent Detection in Medical and Traffic Vision
authors: Bhanu Prakash Battula, P Sumana, R Lavanya, Juhisha J, Subha Balamurugan, Navyatha Ravi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Rare-event detection in safety-critical vision systems such as video capsule endoscopy (VCE) and traffic surveillance for vulnerable road users (VRU) remains challenging due to extreme class imbalance and limited annotated examples. Class imbalance and high intra-class variability caused supervised detectors to miss uncommon but critical events and to produce costly false alarms. A conditional, diversity-preserving generative adversarial network (cD-GAN) was developed to synthesize plausible rare-event images and short clips and to augment training of a transformer-based detector (ViT-D) with temperature-scaled calibration. Experiments used contemporary 2024-25 testbeds-Galar VCE and Capsule Vision 2024 in the medical domain, and Waymo VRU resources plus TU-DAT and TAD in traffic-and evaluated exclusively on real hold-outs. Compared with a strong pretrained ViT baseline, the augmented pipeline increased recall by +18 percentage points in the medical setting and +10 percentage points in traffic, and improved AUPRC by +0.27 (medical) and +0.17 (traffic), while reducing expected calibration error (ECE) by roughly 44%. The approach also reduced false positive rate at operational TPRs. The results suggested that carefully constrained, diversity-aware synthetic augmentation could improve detection sensitivity and reliability; future work will extend temporal coherence, multisite validation, and uncertainty-aware decision policies for deployment.

title: Evolution and Future of Decision Support Systems: Frameworks, Applications, and Emerging Trends
authors: Siva Subramanian R, K. Ramesh, P.Ruba Sudha, Nagarajan S, D.Lekha, S.Deepa
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Decision Support Systems (DSS) are of strategic, tactical and operational importance for facilitating strategic, tactical and operational decisions in a range of disciplines. This survey focuses on the development of DSS from the initial basic structural models up to the recent systems that include artificial intelligence, big data, and cloud computing. Model driven, data driven, knowledge driven and hybrid DSS frameworks are used to solve problems as wide-ranging as mathematical models to decide resource allocation and real time decision making in volatile conditions. Healthcare, business, environmental planning, and smart city sectors demonstrate the effectiveness of DSS for improving diagnostic, financial, disaster, and urban planning solutions. The new trends include; Artificial intelligence integrated DSS, real time analysis, and ethical issues that are transforming DSS roles, stressing on issues of openness, expandability and user orientation. As for the recent developments in the DSS field, we observed that there are still some issues with data quality, system size, adaptation to use, and adherent to ethical standards. Solving these problems implies the use of more sophisticated approaches including cognitive DSS, reinforcement learning, quantum computation to provide faster, accurate and sustainable decision support. This survey clearly points to the centrality of DSS in increasing organizational performance, creativity and providing timely, relevant solutions in a world that is turning into a maze.

title: Interpretable Gender Classification from Malayalam Speech
authors: Suprajha Baskaran, V. Sidharrth, Sirisha Tadepalli
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Speech serves as a primary medium of human communication and conveys important paralinguistic information such as speaker gender. This work investigates acoustic featurebased gender classification for Malayalam, a low resource Dravidian language that remains underexplored in speech processing research. A multi-speaker Malayalam speech corpus has been used in this study, from which a comprehensive feature set comprising of spectral, energy based and voice based characteristics of speech such as Mel Frequency Cepstral Coefficients, Jitter, Shimmer etc. have been extracted. Multiple machine learning classifiers are compared, among which Logistic Regression achieves the highest accuracy of 94.65 % and a weighted <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F}$</tex>-measure of 94.6 % on unseen speakers under speaker independent evaluation. Statistical significance tests, including McNemar, paired t-test, Wilcoxon, and Friedman tests, confirm meaningful performance differences across models. Robustness analysis under additive white noise demonstrates that Logistic Regression is the model most robust to noise with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 0. 8 4 \%}$</tex> accuracy at 20dB SNR, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 4. 9 0 \%}$</tex> at 15dB SNR and 82.88 % at 10 dB SNR. Feature-level interpretability using SHAP on the best model further highlights the dominant role of MFCC-derived and spectral features.

title: Real-Time Accident Prevention, Detection and Alert System using Federated Learning: A Decentralized Framework for Road Safety
authors: K. Maharajan, Mymoon Sulthana K, Jai Praveena Sai J, Sai Ganka R
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Road traffic accidents are a serious worldwide problem, resulting in numerous deaths and financial losses. Federated learning provides a privacy-preserving and decentralized solution for accident detection without the need for centralized data storage. This paper proposes a real-time accident prevention, detection and alert system that combines deep learning and federated learning for improved road safety. The model proposed applies CNN, ResNet, and EfficientNet to detecting accidents from real-time traffic videos and Random Forest for identifying high-risk areas from dashboard metrics to warn drivers. Federated learning enables accident detection models to be trained on distributed data sources without data sharing, promoting privacy and scalability, unlike in conventional models. The system also automates emergency response by sending real-time alerts through Email and Whatsapp, which reduces delay in emergencies. Impressionally, the suggested federated approach accurately detects accidents with 95.8% accuracy when compared to centralized methods. The accident prevention model is 96% accurate. These results show the capability of federated learning in transforming realtime accident forecasting, providing a scalable, privacyoriented, and effective framework for enhancing road safety.

title: Bridging Machine Learning and Generative AI for Intelligent Vulnerability Detection and Remediation in Source Code
authors: Atharva Deokar, Mayank Chauhan, Saur Deshmukh, Paarth Mahadik, Debjit Ghosal, Mahadev Balla, Megh Doshi, Shrey Dedhia, Sujal Chaudhari, Aniket Atole, Anant V. Nimkar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Software vulnerabilities remain a major concern in modern development, where increasing code complexity and rapid release cycles make manual reviews and traditional static analysis insufficient. Existing automated approaches primarily focus on detecting vulnerabilities but rarely provide actionable, context-aware remediation, resulting in delayed patching and increased developer effort. To address this gap, this research proposes an AI-driven architecture that combines traditional machine learning with large language models (LLMs) for both precise detection and guided repair. The system employs an LSTM-based pipeline for accurate vulnerability localization and an LLM-based recommender module capable of generating targeted fixes and explanations. By constraining the LLM's operational scope, hallucinations are minimized and the reliability of recommendations improves. This hybrid architecture ultimately supports faster, more intelligent, and developer-centric vulnerability mitigation during the development phase.

title: Leveraging U-Net and Convnext for Accurate Plant Disease Prediction with Hyperparameter Tuning
authors: Deepa Bhadana, Thanjaivadivel M, R. Lakshmana Kumar, Venkatesan S, Vinoth Kumar G, Rakesh Podaralla
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Early and immediate detection of plant diseases is essential for crop protection and food security. Usually, these may be delayed and adversely impact agriculture because inspections are subjective and time-consuming. This research proposes a deep learning-based framework consisting of the combination of U-Net with Attention Gates for an accurate image segmentation process that hands over the task of disease classification to ConvNeXt. Image preprocessing using CLAHE and augmentations are introduced to tackle some dataset problems, such as noisy data and variations within plant species. Hyperparameter optimization using Optuna is set in place for fine-tuning along with learning rate and batch size, which results in even further improvement in performance. The performance of the proposed model is found better than those of baseline models like VGG-19, MobileNetV2, and EfficientNet with accuracies of 99.16%, 98.48% precision, 98.22% recall, and 98.33% F1-score. Despite its high success rate, the analysis exposes the limitations of the proposed system through ROC curve and confusion matrix analysis, indicating further avenues for improvements. Even then are good levels of robustness and generalization present in the model across different plant species and diseases, thus offering a good discriminatory power for disease diagnosis in actual-farm environments. This study paves the way for developing smart, fully automated systems for early disease detection, which can play a significant role in enhancing global food security and improving crop yields.

title: Radix-8 Booth Encoded Approximate Multiplier Design for Energy-Efficient Image Processing Applications using a 5:3 Compressor
authors: Manju I Kollannur, Oudaya Coumar S
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Low-power design of the Arithmetic Logic Unit heavily relies on approximate computing. Applications involving image processing, like multimedia and signal processing, are its best fits. However, approximate computing is less accurate with area and power consumption. To overcome this, a novel high-performance multiplier architecture is proposed using Radix-8 Booth Encoding, and an approximate 5:3 compressor has been proposed. The proposed Radix-8 Booth encoding reduces the number of partial products. Signed bits of the multiplication are generated by grouping four multiplier bits using Radix-8 Booth encoding. Three bits are new bits and one is an overlapping bit at a time, and encodes them. The proposed 5:3 compressor efficiently compresses the partial product, leading to a reduced critical path and area. The proposed 5:3 compressor efficiently compresses five bits into three outputs, resulting in improved speed and reduced power consumption. By attaining high PSNR and MSSIM values, the proposed method is verified through image sharpening. Excellent image quality and structural similarity are provided by the high PSNR and MSSIM values. Using Xilinx ISE, the proposed multiplier is implemented on a 28 nm FPGA and assessed in terms of area, power, and delay. The proposed structure achieves up to 57% area, 51% power, and 40% delay reduction in comparison to existing approximate compressors and multipliers. Therefore, the proposed design is highly efficient for image-sharpening applications.

title: A Comparative Analysis of Deep Learning and Transformer Models for Emoji-Aware Sarcasm Detection in Code-Mixed Text
authors: Archana Acharya, Rajeev Goyal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Sarcasm detection of code-mixed multilingual text, especially in Hindi-English social media communication is a major natural language processing (NLP) problem. This study entails a thorough comparative method of deep learning models in identifying sarcasm in code-mixed Hinglish (Hindi-English mixture) text to which emoji are added. This paper consider six main models such as LSTM, Bidirectional LSTM (Bi-LSTM), GRU, Bidirectional GRU (Bi-GRU) and transformer-based BERT models, with word-emoji embeddings based on GloVe and Word2Vec representations. Experiment findings show that Bi-GRU with an F1-score of 87.9 and 89.6% accuracy and BERT-based models with 90.3% accuracy and 92.1% accuracy are respectively. Bidirectional architectures are much more effective than unidirectional ones, and paralinguistic attributes of social media discourse can be better accommodated through the addition of emoji embeddings. The proposed study will advance the field of sarcasm detection methods on low-resource, code-mixed languages, and offer an empirical conclusion about the most suitable choice of the model in multilingual applications of NLP.

title: Exploring the Potential of Oil Concentration Estimation From Single-Polarization SAR Data
authors: Honglei Zheng, Yunhua Wang, Peng Ren, Weimin Huang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Quantitative estimation of oil concentration in marine oil spills is critical for effective emergency response. Previous studies on oil concentration retrieval have primarily relied on multi-polarization Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) imagery. In this work, we explore the potential of using single-polarization SAR imagery to estimate oil concentration. Oil spills appear as low backscatter areas in SAR images due to two mechanisms: (i) damping of short Bragg waves and (ii) reduction of effective permittivity when the oil spill is sufficiently thick. To explore the feasibility of single-polarization SAR for oil concentration estimation, we assume that the damping effect on short waves is independent of oil concentration. Accordingly, the normalized radar cross section (NRCS) attenuation caused by wave damping can be separated from the total attenuation induced by the oil spill. The remaining NRCS reduction is then attributed to variations in effective permittivity associated with oil concentration. Based on this principle, oil concentration can be estimated from a single-polarization SAR image. The proposed method was applied to the Deepwater Horizon oil spill, and the retrieved concentrations showed good agreement with results from dual-polarized SAR when the oil concentration is sufficiently high (e.g., greater than 0.6 in our experiments). This study demonstrates that single-polarization SAR is applicable not only for oil spill detection but also for rapid estimation of oil concentration in thick, high-concentration marine oil spills.

title: UAV-Based Edge Inference for Insulator Defect Detection on Transmission Lines via Convolutional Feature Reuse
authors: Yulong Zhang, Xianghong Xue, Lingxia Mu, Jing Xin, Jing Huang, Youmin Zhang
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) equipped with onboard processors enable edge intelligence for smart-grid Internet of Things (IoT) inspection, yet insulator defect detection from aerial imagery remains challenging due to cluttered backgrounds, small targets, and strict latency constraints. Compared with handcrafted methods that rely on manual feature design and parameter tuning, this paper presents an end-to-end improved Faster R-CNN detector whose feature extractor is redesigned via convolutional feature reuse and a two-layer multi-scale fusion scheme with a deeper extraction path, preserving fine-grained cues while limiting model complexity. Experiments on the Chinese Power Line Insulator Dataset (CPLID) and a newly constructed Glass Insulator Dataset (GID) achieve mAP@0.5 values of 97.75% and 96.79%, respectively, which is 7.1% higher than a standard Faster R-CNN (VGG16) baseline on CPLID while reducing the model size to 58 MB. On a UAV system (DJI M300 + H20T + Jetson Xavier NX), TensorRT acceleration runs at 8.5 FPS, outperforming Faster R-CNN (3.2 FPS) and YOLOv11 (5.3 FPS) under the same edge setting. Outdoor flight tests on 330 kV transmission lines further validate field readiness, supporting low-latency and bandwidth-efficient UAV inspection for smart-grid IoT maintenance.

title: Automatic Registration of Urban Scene Point Clouds Based on Semantic Structure Matching
authors: Wenpeng Zong, Minglei Li, Xueyong Xiang, Rengui Ruan, Yue Mao
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Point cloud based 3D reconstruction is a critical technique for urban digitalization. Traditional marker-based methods involve deploying targets, which are time-consuming, inefficient, and sometimes fail to ensure accurate registration. Given the abundance of linear features in urban environments, leveraging line features for automatic registration of urban point clouds offers a promising solution. However, individual linear features lack sufficient information to support robust correspondence establishment. Therefore, we propose a Semantic Structure Based Registration (SSBR) method that utilizes line segment tuples as semantic structures. The method first extracts line features from point clouds using edge line detection algorithms, yielding a set of line segments along with their endpoints and support regions. It then identifies semantic structures from building facades and extracts their semantic attributes. Next, semantic structures are matched pairwise based on similarity measurements, and corresponding transformations are computed. The result with the highest number of common line segments is selected as the initial registration outcome. Finally, registration refinement is achieved through alignment of common line segments. Experimental results on three urban point cloud datasets demonstrate that the proposed SSBR method enables efficient and automatic registration of large-scale urban point clouds with centimeter-level accuracy. Compared with baseline approaches, the SSBR method shows superior overall performance across different datasets.

title: Automating Root Cause Analysis in Semiconductor Manufacturing Using Transformer Models with Explainable AI
authors: Youssef Alothman, Mohamed Bader-El-Den, Lalit Maurya, Eslam Amer
publish: IEEE Transactions on Semiconductor Manufacturing
abstract: Root Cause Analysis (RCA) is a critical step in diagnosing defects and preventing them in complex software and hardware systems. In practice, however, RCA is cumbersome and unstable, especially for high volumes of unstructured defect reports. This research considers the usage of transformer-based language models to automate and enhance RCA. Trained models, such as ELECTRA and RoBERTa, surpass traditional machine learning and deep learning methods in accuracy, enabling them to detect a wider range of defect types. Interpretability is achieved by post-hoc explanation of model predictions with SHAP, which consistently identifies key technical variables such as ”architecture,” ”memory leak,” and ”API failure.” These explanations increase confidence levels in model output decisions and make them more appropriate in real-world engineering applications. Overall, transformer models provide an open and scalable approach to automating RCA in high-volume, high-speed programs, overcoming challenges such as class imbalance and computational cost.

title: Residual-Driven Voltage Forecasting and Power Quality State Classification in Distribution Systems
authors: Fatih Serttas
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Voltage quality problems in electrical distribution systems, especially voltage sags and irregular voltage fluctuations, are critical for grid operation safety and the continuity of sensitive loads. This study aims to predict voltage behavior in the short term and classify operating states in a meaningful manner using long-term hourly three-phase voltage and active power measurement data obtained from a real distribution network in Türkiye. In this context, an architecture called Deep-Fusion-VS has been developed that performs voltage estimation and related power quality classification. In this architecture, the interphase mean voltage estimation is performed using a CNN-LSTM-based deep learning model, and the estimation performance obtained for different substations is found to be in the range of MAE = 0.0025–0.0044 pu, RMSE = 0.0031–0.0073 pu, and R² = 0.71–0.82. After the estimation phase, a holistic approach is proposed in which the estimation residuals are evaluated together with the voltage time series. In this method, system behavior is defined under three classes as “Normal”, “Fluctuating”, and “Sag”. Statistical and dynamic features are extracted using a sliding-window approach. The developed multi-class deep learning-based classifier achieves an overall accuracy of 83.2% on the test data, with an F1-score of 0.90 for the fluctuating state. ROC and Precision–Recall analyses present that even rare sag events can be distinguished with confidence. The results obtained reveal that the proposed approach offers a viable solution for voltage quality monitoring and early warning-based decision support systems.

title: Nonlinear almost contractions via digraphs and applications to boundary value problems
authors: Salma Aljawi, Mohammad Akram, Izhar Uddin
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper outlines a few findings that assure the fixed-point of a nonlinear almost contraction employing a transitive and reflexive digraph G. To showcase the profitability of our conclusions, we impart a couple of instances. The insights we found allow ourselves to accommodate the unique solution of a first-order boundary value problem. Numerous existing theorems are sharpened, subsumed, united, improved, enriched, and compiled by the outcomes reported here.

title: Utilizing Everyday Textures as Concept-Based Explanations for Deep Learning Model Prediction of Glioma Biomarker Status
authors: Rayyan Akhand, Farzad Alizadeh, Thanh Binh Nguyen, Nick D. James, Sreeraman Rajan, Rebecca E. Thornhill.
publish: IEEE Transactions on Artificial Intelligence
abstract: This study aims to bridge the gap between artificial intelligence (AI) model prediction and human comprehension of higher-level imaging concepts. The specific application involves magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) of gliomas, focusing on AI classification of isocitrate dehydrogenase (IDH) biomarker status, a key indicator of glioma aggressiveness. Methods: Four convolutional neural network models, based on the ResNet50 architecture, were trained using MRI of gliomas to differentiate between gliomas with “wildtype” and “mutated” forms of the IDH biomarker. The models were evaluated using 5- fold cross-validation. Additionally, the Testing Concept Activation Vectors framework (TCAV) was employed to assess the impact of ‘everyday’ image texture concepts on model decision-making. Sensitivity scores ranging from 0 to 1 were calculated for 47 concepts across three layers of each model for both forms of IDH. Results: The analysis revealed varying levels of sensitivity to different image texture concepts when classifying IDH forms. These sensitivity scores indicate the extent to which the model is influenced by these concepts when making decisions about the IDH type. Conclusion: This study provides a preliminary evaluation of how concept-based explanations can elucidate the internal workings of black-box neural networks, particularly in a medical context. Significance: The findings offer valuable insights into how concept-based explanations can improve understanding and trust in AI models, which is crucial for deploying AI models in clinical settings.

title: CL-ERDA: Certificateless Recoverable Data Auditing with Corruption Localization in Edge Computing_supp1-3675881.pdf
authors: Dengzhi Liu
publish: N/A
abstract: Edge computing paradigm deploys infrastructures on users' edge side, enabling data to be stored in edge storage systems. Thus edge computing finds a balance between remote cloud storage with high latency and local storage with limited resources. However, the frequent update of edge data increases the probability of data corrupting. To ensure availability and integrity of edge data without affecting future use, this paper proposes a certificateLess edge recoverable data auditing (CL-ERDA) approach with corruption localization. First, the certificateless network coding signature in CL-ERDA is utilized to generate the homomorphic authenticator, addressing public key certificate management issues while avoiding insecure key escrow problems. Then, CL-ERDA designs a two-phase auditing method, where only file-level aggregation proofs are respond in first phase of external check and the second phase of self-check is launched only when damaged data is detected, reducing communication overhead of the auditing. Moreover, CL-ERDA combines hierarchical check method with batch localization method to achieve locating efficiently all the corrupted data at one time rather than locating only one corrupted data at once. Finally, security analysis shows that the proposed scheme resists various attacks and supports the unforgeability of authenticators. Experimental results demonstrate that CL-ERDA is efficient in auditing and corruption localization.

title: An Area- and Energy-Efficient Point-Based 3-D Object Detection Processor With Feature-Decoupled PointNet for Autonomous Driving
authors: Changyu Seong, Sunwoo Lee, Beomseok Kim, Dongsuk Jeon
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: Recent point-based neural networks have shown promising performance in 3D object detection by directly processing raw point clouds without converting them into voxels. However, they exhibit stage-specific computational bottlenecks due to the varying computational demands of sampling, grouping, and neural network operations. This paper presents an area- and energy-efficient processor that accelerates point-based neural networks with low latency through algorithm-hardware co-optimization. The proposed feature-decoupled PointNet enables reusing point features by separating MLPs, reducing MAC operations by up to 67.6% without accuracy loss. In addition, density-aware adaptive block pruning eliminates redundant computations in overly dense regions, significantly lowering the computational costs of sampling and grouping. The proposed 3D object detection processor efficiently parallelizes the grouping process by combining a dual-core architecture with a lightweight prioritized shift register, effectively mitigating the workload imbalance. It also adopts a dual-center fused-layer dataflow that maximizes PE utilization while significantly reducing external memory access. Furthermore, the masked max pooling unit eliminates the need for a separate gathering unit by integrating the gathering step into the pooling logic for higher area and power efficiency. Experimental results show that the design provides real-time 3D object detection with a high throughput of 77.9 frames/s, also exhibiting a high accuracy of 83.11 mAP on the KITTI dataset, surpassing prior arts in both accuracy and throughput. In addition, its low energy consumption of 2.5 mJ/frame and high area efficiency of 24.0 frames/s/mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> make it particularly well-suited for embedded systems for autonomous driving.

title: Analysis and Design of Dual-Injection Injection-Locked Frequency Triplers With Wide Locking Range and High Harmonic Rejection
authors: Qingfan Zeng, Jingzhi Zhang, Yiming Yu, Huihua Liu, Yunqiu Wu, Chenxi Zhao, Kai Kang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article presents a dual-injection injection-locked frequency tripler (ILFT) achieving a wide locking range (LR), high harmonic rejection, and low power consumption. The dual-injection ILFT consists of a tail injection path and a coupling injection path. The tail injection path leverages a hard-switching behavior to reshape the injection current into a pseudo-square waveform, which enhances the strength of the third harmonic to extend the LR. In addition, the coupling injection path utilizes both electric and magnetic coupling of the injection-current-boosting (ICB) transformer to further enhance the injection current. By combining the two injection paths, the third harmonic currents are combined in phase, while the other undesired harmonics are suppressed out of phase. Additionally, an injection-locked buffer (ILB) is cascaded to improve the flatness of the output power while further suppressing undesirable harmonics. Fabricated in a 65-nm CMOS process, the ILFT operates from 17.4 to 26.4 GHz with 41.1% LR. The harmonic rejection ratio (HRR) is above 40 dBc from 17.4 to 24 GHz, and the power consumption of the ILFT core is 3.2 mW.

title: Time-Varying Acceleration Constraint-Based Admittance Control for Robot-Aided Bilateral Human-Compliant Ankle Rehabilitation
authors: Jianfeng Li, Cong Wang, Shiping Zuo, Mingjie Dong, Yifeng Chen, Yongzhen Li, Mingming Zhang
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: Robot-assisted bilateral rehabilitation has demonstrated efficacy in facilitating ankle function recovery. This article develops a control framework to ensure safe bilateral human-compliant ankle rehabilitation training. First, a bilateral ankle-coupled rehabilitation training mode (BACRTM) is established, which dynamically couples the human–robot interaction (HRI) torques of both ankles. When one ankle performs active training, resistive or assistive torques are synchronously applied to the contralateral side, forming a coordinated bilateral movement mechanism. Second, a time-varying acceleration constraint (TVAC) strategy integrating error feedback is designed based on Lyapunov stability theory. By limiting the upper and lower bounds of reference acceleration, system stability is ensured during high-dynamic HRI. Theoretical analysis proves that the TVAC strategy can produce stable and bounded tracking errors. Experimental results demonstrate that under the BACRTM, the TVAC strategy can reduce the bilaterally rendered inertia by an average of 99.05%, while maintaining robustness to varying subject inputs and dynamics. Furthermore, the strategy features adjustable acceleration boundary effects, enabling adaptation to patients with ankle injuries at different rehabilitation stages.
