title: A Robust Control for Alleviating Harmonic Distortion in SPVA Integrated BES Systems
authors: Ravi Kumar Gupta, Ambuj Kumar Dwivedi, Sushma Gupta, Shailendra Jain
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This work addresses the synchronized control of a solar photovoltaic array (SPVA) and a battery energy storage system (BESS) connected to the utility grid through a voltagesource converter (VSC). A Synchronous Reference-Frame Phase-Locked Loop (SRF-PLL) theory is utilized to track the utility grid frequency and phase with high accuracy, which helps the converter remain synchronized and sustain a stable reference voltage even when grid conditions violate. The control scheme manages the flow of energy among the SPVA, BESS, and utility grid, reducing current harmonics and ensuring that the voltage at the point of common coupling (PCC) stays almost sinusoidal. When energy generation from SPVA ceases, the BESS discharges energy to meet the load demands, whereas surplus energy is fed back to the utility to maintain a stable and smooth connection. MATLAB/Simulink environment showing that the proposed scheme responses quickly to transients and is tolerant against utility disturbances and demonstrates total harmonic distortion (THD) remains below the 5% threshold defined in IEEE-519 standards.

title: Deployment of Attention-based VGG19 with Deep SVM for Soil Moisture and Vegetation Index Prediction in Agriculture Field using Satellite Images
authors: M. Ashok Nagasai, S.Maruthupermal
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Variation in soil moisture content can greatly affect crop growth and agricultural productivity. While soil moisture provides needed water for plants, excessive soil moisture can hinder crop growth. Therefore, it is necessary to develop effective methods for analyzing normal soil moisture levels before the harvesting period of crops to boost agricultural output. It helps predict and analyze water cycles, climate crises, water vapour transport, drought, water management, and overflow during rainfall. In particular, the conventional technique for measuring habitat structure has difficulty in understanding variation among habitat categories due to the complex patterns. In agricultural settings, the integration of aerial and satellite imagery techniques has improved the ability to monitor the yields. In this study, an effective attention-based deep learning method is proposed for the prediction of soil moisture and vegetation index analysis. Firstly, the required samples of satellite images are garnered from the benchmark datasets. Consequently, the input images are processed under Attention-based Visual Geometry Group 19 with Deep Support Vector Machine (A-VGG19-DSVM) for predicting the soil moisture and vegetation index level. Lastly, the experimentation of the proposed model is assessed with distinct evaluation metrics. On the contrary, the results are obtained by the proposed method to prove the efficacy.

title: Machine Learning Techniques for Voice-Based Parkinson's Disease Detection
authors: Nishvika Teja Reddy, Niharika. N, Jayashree K.R, J. Pallavi, Abdul Haq Nalband, Trupthi Rao
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Parkinson's Disease represents a progressively debilitating neurological condition that fundamentally disrupts motor control, vocal production, and patient quality of life. The integration of artificial intelligence within modern healthcare paradigms have catalyzed innovative approaches for the objective disease identification. This investigation presents a comparative evaluation of machine learning architectures, including Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, Support Vector Machines, Logistic Regression, and Multilayer Perceptron Neural Networks. Our methodology analyzes biomedical vocal recordings to establish discriminative patterns between healthy subjects and individuals with Parkinsonian symptoms. Performance assessment encompasses diverse statistical indicators, while model transparency is achieved through SHAP-driven feature analysis. Experimental outcomes demonstrate that computational intelligence applied to vocal pattern recognition offers a dependable, minimally invasive pathway for early-stage identification, with Random Forest achieving 94.87% accuracy. The framework exhibits substantial promise for augmenting clinical judgment and supporting therapeutic intervention.

title: Data-Driven Energy Optimization of a CPA and Gain-Managed-Nonlinearity Mamyshev Oscillator
authors: Ya Lin, Qiurun He, Peng Liu, Zhongxing Jiao, Yan Zhang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Mamyshev oscillators are well known for generating high-energy ultrashort pulses. However, further energy scaling is fundamentally constrained by stimulated Raman scattering, intracavity nonlinear accumulation, and gain-induced spectral narrowing. Conventional optimization based on generalized nonlinear Schrodinger equation simulations is extremely time-consuming and strongly dependent on manual parameter tuning in a multi-parameter cavity space, making it inefficient for systematic energy optimization. To address these challenges, a data-driven optimization framework is developed to enhance the pulse energy of a chirped-pulse-amplification and gain-managed-nonlinearity Mamyshev oscillator. The framework integrates a long short-term memory (LSTM) model with a particle swarm optimization (PSO) algorithm. The LSTM model reproduces intracavity pulse evolution with sub-percent accuracy and over three orders of magnitude speed-up relative to full simulations for generalized nonlinear Schrodinger equations. The PSO algorithm explores the multi-parameter cavity space efficiently. The PSO-LSTM framework identifies an optimal configuration yielding 558.9 nJ, representing a ~70% increase over the experimental result, demonstrating an effective approach for high-energy ultrafast fiber laser optimization.

title: Integrating the Principles of Sustainable Development Into the Training of Electronics and Electrical Engineers
authors: Olga I. Sharavova, Tatyana A. Kuzovkova, Maria M. Sharavova
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: The article is devoted to the urgent problem of integrating the principles of sustainable development into the training of electronics and electrical engineers. Attention is drawn to the serious challenges posed by rapid changes in technology and the impact of human activities on the environment. The key aspects of changing engineering education in the context of the strategy of sustainable economic development are formulated: efficient energy consumption, responsible management of electronic waste and the use of environmentally friendly materials. The necessity of including disciplines in the curricula of training electronics and electrical engineers, which form students’ holistic vision of the environmental and social consequences of their future activities, is argued. A list of mandatory disciplines is proposed, such as fundamentals of sustainable development, programming of energy-efficient systems, materials science and social responsibility. The tools that allow teachers to take into account the goals of sustainable development in the educational process are presented. The future problems and prospects of engineering education in the context of a sustainable development economy are discussed, and the need for a deep awareness of the upcoming changes and the readiness of both teachers and students for them is emphasized.

title: AI based Chatbot for Students using RAG in Large Language Models
authors: Monica R, Mukhesh S, Mahalakshmi M, Madhumathi R
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: This research paper presents a novel AI-powered educational platform designed to address the significant barriers faced by Indian school dropouts, particularly those from regional language-speaking backgrounds. The platform's core functionality provides personalized learning resources and scholarship recommendations. To optimize the system's retrieval and conversational capabilities, a performance evaluation is conducted on three open-source language models namely all-MiniLM-L6-v2, DistilGPT-2, and Mistral AI. The system incorporates a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) chatbot, implemented with the Gemini API, to deliver interactive, context-aware support. The platform's recommendation accuracy is enhanced by a custom-curated dataset, and PyPDF integration ensures seamless access to digital learning materials. The efficacy of the system is demonstrated through strong performance metrics, including a response relevance of 87%, a retrieval accuracy of 89%, and a low latency of 1 second. These results highlight the platform's potential to empower students to resume their education and access vital financial aid.

title: DrawBench: A Benchmark for High-Level Intent Multi-Format Creative Outputs
authors: Anshu Raj
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The current multimodal generation systems will be excellent at creating visually appealing pictures, however, they fail to meet the requirement of the actual design process. Designers do not merely wish to see beautiful pictures but instead, they require icons, infographics, assets done in vectors, and content that is editable through several steps. The fact that the existing models do not often satisfy these practical needs implies that they can be used only minimally in the professional field, in those cases, when the accuracy, clarity, and compatibility of the formats are important. Current benchmarks largely are visual quality benchmarks or single-format benchmarks. They have little to say about whether models are capable of learning high-level creative intent, or structured editing instructions, or create assets that will fit readily into down-stream design tools. They seldom also compare the layout structure and compositional constraints across formats of models, either. To bridging this existing gap, we propose DrawBench, which is a benchmark that are coupled with detailed creative intents and professionally-created raster images, vector SVGs and editable infographic files. DrawBench offers multi-format targets, step-by-step editing sequences and metrics which are specifically tailored to the actual design utility.

title: Digital Semantic Communication with Channel Adaptation and Vector Quantization
authors: Yuyang Zheng, Weihong Lin
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Semantic communication has demonstrated the potential to surpass the Shannon limit through joint source-channel coding. However, existing systems suffer from two critical limitations: the lack of adaptability to dynamic channels and insufficient compatibility with digital communication systems due to reliance on analog modulation. This paper proposes a signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) adaptive digital semantic communication framework that achieves high-quality transmission under dynamic channels and compatibility with digital systems by integrating a channel-aware semantic codec with a learnable semantic feature quantization module. The system employs lightweight residual convolutional modules to extract semantic features, enables a single model to adapt to a wide SNR range through an SNR-adaptive attention mechanism, and utilizes a learnable codebook to map continuous features to discrete binary sequences. A three-stage progressive training strategy is adopted to optimize system performance. Experimental results on the test set demonstrate that the proposed system outperforms the traditional JPEG+LDPC scheme, validating the effectiveness and practical value of this framework.

title: Zero-DiDCE: Dual-Illumination Curve Estimation For Zero-Reference Low-Light Image Enhancement
authors: Yashwitha Reddy Chapala, Praveen Kumar Kalangi, Sriram Bodla, Aishwarya Tota, Nitisha Sharma, Bollipelly Pruthviraj Goud, G. L. Anand Babu, Gudepu Sai Krishna
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)
abstract: The challenge of improving images captured in insufficient light settings continues to be a difficult problem in computer vision, posing challenges in fields like surveillance, autonomous driving, and medical imaging. Many traditional improvement methods have problems with areas that are either underexposed or overexposed, with excessive noise, requiring paired ground-truth data, and other concerns. In this study, we introduce Zero-DiDCE: a reference-free deep curve estimation method leveraging dual illumination for boosting image quality captured in low-illumination environments via unsupervised learning. It expands the scope of Zero DCE by utilizing both the original and its inversion illumination versions to enhance the illumination range of the image. Our algorithms design and train a spatially-extensive pixel-wise formulation of enhancement curves to enhance the compact convolutional neural networks. These curves are applied in an iterative manner to improve exposure and contrast without any referenced images. The resulting image exhibits structure-preserving smoothing, accurately refining visibility in thoroughly dark conditions. The experimental results demonstrate that the method delivers impressive improvements across a wide range of low-light conditions, showcasing its robustness and adaptability for real-time applications.

title: Stability of Control Systems with Nonlinear Elements
authors: S. L. Yablochnikov, I. O. Yablochnikova, V. B. Dzobelova, D. I. Shlychkov, A. P. Altukhova
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: A set of analytical methods for the synthesis of control systems, which contain nonlinear elements developed and justified by the authors of the article, provide a sufficiently high degree of formalization of the solution. These methods are designed for the analysis and synthesis of stable linear or nonlinear deterministic systems. In particular, nonlinear control systems are considered as piecewise linear, which, in turn, makes it possible to formalize the dynamics of their functioning within finite or semi-infinite time intervals using a set of differential equations with constant coefficients. In addition, the above coefficients can change abruptly at the time of various switching modes of operation. And a special case of piecewise linear control systems are linear systems with a prehistory, that is, with nonzero values of coordinates, external disturbing influences and their derivatives, characteristic of earlier time periods than those considered by the researcher.

title: Retinal Disease Classification Using a Multi-Stage Deep Learning Model on OCT C8 Dataset with Grad-CAM Support
authors: T. G. Ramnadh Babu, Bathula Timothi, Kannekanti Teja Vardhana Chari, Kurra Vamsi Krishna, Sireesha Moturi, Y. Sri Lalitha, Y. Sravani Devi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Early diagnosis of retinal-related disorders is paramount in ensuring from causing significant vision loss, especially where access is limited specialist medical treatment. The following study offers a short, multi-step procedure. Convolutional model assisting in the identification of different disorders of the retina on the Retinal OCT C8 dataset. This includes eight prominent categories that are widely employed by physicians in real scenarios. The model consists of a sequence of convolutional layers with special modules which assist in recognizing fine textures as well as large ones. These modules consist of different filters which are signs for potential retinal problems. The data set was were thoroughly cleaned and divided, and the model was trained only on these samples to ensure the results are reliable in a given real medical setting. On the test section of the data, The accuracy achieved by the model was 95.07%, with precision, recall, and F1-score all tend to be very close to each other, which is better than models such as it. The model has been designed to be lightweight, so that it A model such as it can perform well even on system with low computing capabilities. To in order to make the model more trustworthy, it also includes visual tools those that emphasize what aspects of the retina were most significant in arriving at a diagnosis, assisting physicians in employing it effectively in their practice.

title: Gearbox fault diagnosis based on multi-feature description method and adaptive neighbor selection LTSA : Fault diagnosis of gearbox based on improved LTSA
authors: Yue Zhu, Haibin Zhang, Mingyu Wang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Gear transmissions are widely used in machinery and play a decisive role in the overall performance of the equipment. Therefore, online monitoring and fault diagnosis of gearboxes are critical issues. This paper proposes an Adaptive Neighborhood Selection (ANS) method, which dynamically compresses or expands sample neighborhoods using parameters such as local curvature, sampling density, and local tangent space approximation angles. Furthermore, the ANS method is integrated with Local Tangent Space Alignment (LTSA) to enhance the algorithm's dimensionality reduction capability. The fault diagnosis framework begins with signal processing. First, the original signals are denoised using Empirical Mode Decomposition (EMD) and Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT) to generate low-noise reconstructed signals. Next, the Random Forest algorithm is used to calculate the importance of each feature and select the feature subset most sensitive to faults. Finally, the proposed ANSLTSA algorithm performs dimensionality reduction on the features, and the results are input into a Support Vector Machine (SVM) for multi-class fault identification and prediction. Experimental results demonstrate that this method outperforms other methods, achieving significant improvements in fault classification accuracy and demonstrating strong robustness. Consequently, this method provides an effective technical approach for gearbox fault diagnosis.

title: Real-Time Remaining Useful Life Prediction with LSTM Priority Resampling
authors: Tori Wright, Bulent Soykan, Sean Mondesire
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: With the advancement of digital twins for real-time predictive analytics, there is a growing demand for continual learning systems that can adapt dynamically and be retrained efficiently. In this context, we focus on a deep learning approach using a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) model to predict Remaining Useful Life (RUL). RUL, defined as the time or operational cycles remaining before a system fails, is central to predictive maintenance and digital twin applications, enabling proactive scheduling of repairs, minimizing downtime, and improving system reliability. However, as these real-time models are retrained, performance degrades due to an imbalance of prioritizing new, incoming data over previously learned knowledge. This presents a challenge in digital twin environments, where historical data often remains relevant for predicting future outcomes. Therefore, it is critical to adopt retraining strategies that are both efficient and mitigate forgetting.This work introduces a novel deep learning LSTM-based retraining framework that uses a priority resampling strategy to enhance forward learning while preserving past knowledge. Rather than focusing solely on mitigating forgetting, we evaluate how the inclusion of strategically selected historical data improves learning metrics such as convergence speed and prediction error. Our experiments on the NASA C-MAPSS jet engine dataset show that this approach achieves faster convergence on new data, yields lower prediction error, and demonstrates better generalization to unseen data. These results confirm that relevance-guided resampling is effective in continual learning settings, particularly for real-time, adaptive digital twin systems that require fast and reliable RUL predictions.

title: AQUA-LLM: Evaluating Accuracy, Quantization, and Adversarial Robustness Trade-offs in LLMs for Cybersecurity Question Answering
authors: Onat Gungor, Roshan Sood, Harold Wang, Tajana Rosing
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have recently demonstrated strong potential for cybersecurity question answering (QA), supporting decision-making in real-time threat detection and response workflows. However, their substantial computational demands pose significant challenges for deployment on resource-constrained edge devices. Quantization, a widely adopted model compression technique, can alleviate these constraints. Nevertheless, quantization may degrade model accuracy and increase susceptibility to adversarial attacks. Fine-tuning offers a potential means to mitigate these limitations, but its effectiveness when combined with quantization remains insufficiently explored. Hence, it is essential to understand the trade-offs among accuracy, efficiency, and robustness. We propose AQUA-LLM, an evaluation framework designed to benchmark several state-of-the-art small LLMs under four distinct configurations: base, quantized-only, fine-tuned, and fine-tuned combined with quantization, specifically for cybersecurity QA. Our results demonstrate that quantization alone yields the lowest accuracy and robustness despite improving efficiency. In contrast, combining quantization with fine-tuning enhances both LLM robustness and predictive performance, achieving an optimal balance of accuracy, robustness, and efficiency. These findings highlight the critical need for quantization-aware, robustness-preserving fine-tuning methodologies to enable the robust and efficient deployment of LLMs for cybersecurity QA.

title: A Multi-Feature Fusion Approach to Learner Engagement Detection
authors: Eman Almotairi, Danda B. Rawat
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Recognizing inattentive students can be particularly challenging on online learning platforms such as Zoom. The rapid growth of virtual education has created an urgent need for reliable and interpretable methods to detect learner engagement. Although several models have been proposed, many struggle to capture engagement accurately and often misclassify inattentive students as engaged. To overcome this limitation, we propose a predictive framework that detects engagement by combining visual behavioral cues, such as head movement and facial expressions. The proposed approach combines the Swin Transformer architecture with head pose estimation through a feature-level fusion strategy to capture global context and directional attention cues. We validate our framework using the publicly available dataset named ‘Student Engagement Dataset’ and benchmark performance gains across standard metrics, including accuracy. The findings demonstrate that our approach outperforms current state-of-the-art methods, increasing accuracy from 94% to 99%. This study highlights the Capability of transformer-based models to enhance engagement detection systems in virtual education environments.

title: Dynamic Adaptive Decision Control System for Lightning Protection of Energy Infrastructure Based on Federated Reinforcement Learning
authors: Ting Su, Jie Xu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Energy infrastructure is the cornerstone of socio-economic development, and hence safety measures, such as lightning protection measures, become an imperative during the design and construction of the infrasturcture. Currently, the inspection of lightning protection measures and facilities relies on manual work and suffers a high cost of maintenance. To address challenges in the lightning protection of energy infrastructure, this study proposes a dynamic adaptive decision control system for lightning protection of energy infrastructure based on federated reinforcement learning (FRL). At the IoT device end of the system, grounding resistance monitoring devices are introduced to detect the resistance and other sensors are deployed to collect and transmit information, including terrain, climate, soil, time, and lightning-protection materials to the edge end; at the edge end, the current lightning protection status of the device is identified in real time through threshold comparison and AI model inference; the edge nodes perform local training with the collected data, refine the early-warning model through reinforcement learning, and transmit the locally trained model to the cloud; the cloud server aggregates the local model parameters transmitted from multiple edge nodes through federated learning, produces a better-performing early-warning model and delivers the results to the edge end, thereby realizing adaptive decision control.

title: An Improved Plateau Tibetan Brown Bear Object Detection Algorithm Based on Multi-Scale Feature Fusion
authors: Kaiqiang Zhang, Qing Qi, Jiahao Wang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Aiming at the limitations of existing object detection algorithms in monitoring plateau wildlife, particularly in terms of detection accuracy for multi-scale targets in Tibetan brown bear images, this paper proposes an improved YOLOv8 detection framework that incorporates species-specific visual features. The framework introduces a Bidirectional Feature Pyramid Network (BiFPN). Through bidirectional cross-scale connections, it enables efficient interaction between features at different scales, thereby enhancing the model's ability to capture multi-scale information. To further optimize multi-scale feature fusion, a fast normalized fusion method is proposed. By learning the importance of input features, it dynamically adjusts their contribution to the fusion result, making full use of the advantages of features at different resolutions. This improves the accuracy of Tibetan brown bear detection on the plateau. Experimental results on the self-built dataset show that compared to the original YOLOv8, the proposed YOLOv8-BiFPN achieves improvements of 1.2% in mAP50, 4.8% in mAP50-95, and 2.9% in Recall. These results validate the effectiveness of the BiFPN network in enhancing multi-scale object detection performance.

title: Unified Adversarial Robustness: Combining Adversarial Training with Certified Defense
authors: Haijia Zhong, Fucai Xie, Shuanglei Wang, Xianpeng Wang, Silalai Phadboke
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Adversarial examples pose critical security threats to deep neural networks, yet existing defenses face a fundamental dilemma: adversarial training provides strong empirical robustness but lacks provable guarantees, while certified defenses offer formal guarantees but sacrifice practical performance. We propose a unified framework that bridges this gap through balanced adversarial training combined with randomized smoothing certification. By introducing a tunable clean/adversarial mix ratio α, our approach achieves simultaneous improvements on both fronts: 72.20% robust accuracy under AutoAttack and 92.19% certified accuracy at r = 0.5 on CIFAR-10, surpassing prior certified defenses by 23.8% while maintaining 93.93% clean accuracy. Through systematic ablation studies, we discover a remarkable negative gap phenomenon (−42.34%) where pure adversarial training achieves robust accuracy exceeding clean accuracy, revealing critical insights into training distribution mismatch and validating the necessity of balanced training. Our work demonstrates that empirical and certified robustness are complementary rather than conflicting objectives, providing a practical foundation for trustworthy AI deployment in safety-critical applications.

title: Design and Analysis of Wide Band Terahertz Antenna
authors: Khushi Sachan, Shubham Kumar, Jayant Kumar Rai, Shriram Gupta, Pinku Ranjan, Rakesh Chowdhury
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The proposed 6 G THz antenna is designed on a Silicon Dioxide (SiO2) substrate with a relative permittivity of er <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=3.9$</tex> and physical dimensions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$50 \times 40 \mu^{2}$</tex>, backed by a copper ground plane of thickness <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.035 \mu ~\mathrm{m}$</tex>. The radiating patch are implemented using a circular patch integrated with a hexagonal ring and elliptical resonators to enhance the impedance bandwidth. The operating frequency of the proposed antenna 3.21 to 5.62 THz with an impedance bandwidth of 2.41 THz. The antenna maintains a VSWR below 2 throughout the entire operating band and achieves a peak gain of 6.59 dBi. Hence, it is suitable for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 G ~ T H z}$</tex> communication systems.

title: Comparative Power Analysis of Adder Circuits for Low-Power Digital Design
authors: Gitika, Prof. Greeshma Arya
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: As VLSI technology keeps getting smaller over time, adders have become a crucial part of practically every modern integrated circuit. An adder must be fast, with efficiency in terms of power consumption and chip area coming in second. As more compact digital devices with the lowest power consumption continue to emerge and replace one another, the competition to design the most optimal device structure is becoming more intense and quicker. For modern digital systems to improve performance, the ideal adder architecture is essential. This study examines five 4-bit adder architectures from the wide field of adders: Ripple Carry Adder (RCA), Carry Look Ahead Adder (CLA), Carry Select Adder (CSA), Carry Save Adder (CSaA), and Carry Skip Adder (CSkA). The study assesses adder designs' performance characteristics in terms of total on-chip power consumption using Xilinx VIVADO software. The study shows that the Ripple Carry Adder (RCA) consumes the highest power at 5.072 W, primarily due to its sequential carry propagation. The Carry Save Adder (CSA) consumes less power at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4. 4 9 4 ~ W}$</tex> but is still higher than the more advanced adder designs. The Carry Select Adder (CSA), Carry Lookahead Adder (CLA), and Carry Skip Adder (CKA) demonstrate significantly lower power consumption, with CLA and CKA being almost identical at 2.955 W, reflecting their optimized architectures. Analysis of static and dynamic power indicates that RCA has higher leakage currents, while CLA and CKA minimize dynamic power through reduced switching activity. Overall, for high-performance and low-power applications, CLA or CKA are the preferred choices, whereas RCA, despite its simplicity, is less suitable for energy-sensitive designs.

title: An Ultra-Wideband Slot-Coupled Circularly Polarized Antenna Array Using Sequential Rotated Feeding for Millimeter Wave Applications
authors: Zihuan Lu, Zhichao Sun, Xiaojing Lv, Yang Yang
publish: 2026 Australasian Symposium on Antennas (ASA)
abstract: An ultra-wideband (UWB) slot-coupled circularly polarized (CP) antenna array using sequential rotated feeding (SRF) for millimeter wave applications is proposed in this paper. By using an SRF network in an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8 \times 8$</tex> antenna array, both the impedance bandwidth and the axial-ratio (AR) bandwidth are enhanced. Simulation results show that the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8 \times 8$</tex> array achieves an overlapped bandwidth <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\left\vert\mathrm{S}_{11}\right\vert<-10 ~\text{dB}, \text{AR}<3 ~\text{dB}\right)$</tex> of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$56.5 \%(26.56- 47.5$</tex> GHz), with a peak realized gain of 22.66 dBic. The proposed antenna maintains stable high realized gain and a wide overlapped bandwidth across the operating band, making it well suited for millimeter wave applications.

title: Integrating BRP and Aggregator Roles for Imbalance Management and Frequency Regulation using Demand-side Flexibility
authors: Arqum Shahid, Roya Ahmadiahangar, Argo Rosin, Tarmo Korõtko
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: Energy balancing costs significantly impact the operational efficiency of Balance Responsible Parties (BRPs), particularly in the context of increasing renewable energy integration and varying weather conditions that amplify forecasting uncertainties and frequency deviations in community-level energy systems. Imbalance settlement mechanisms often penalize deviations, exacerbating BRP operational costs. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a dual-role methodological framework for optimizing community-level flexibility resources when a balance BRP simultaneously acts as an aggregator providing frequency containment reserve for normal operation (FCR-N) services. The optimization is performed by minimizing the net operational costs, balancing day-ahead procurement costs, imbalance penalties, and maximizing FCR-N capacity and activation revenues simultaneously. Three distinct operational scenarios are analyzed: a baseline scenario without flexibility usage, flexibility dedicated solely to imbalance reduction, and flexibility fully committed to the FCR-N market. The analysis demonstrates that leveraging community-level flexibility for reserve services significantly reduces overall economic costs. Specifically, committing the same flexibility solely to FCR-N results in substantial economic benefits, achieving a 4.75% cost reduction compared to the baseline. These savings primarily stemmed from substantial FCR-N capacity and activation payments, despite occasional increases in imbalance volumes due to reserve activations. The research highlights important trade-offs and operational considerations regarding frequency-dependent activations can influence BRP imbalance costs.

title: Troll Detection in Reddit Using Combined Machine Learning classifiers, Deep Learning, and Human-in-the-Loop Analysis
authors: Rafeef Baamer, Mihai Boicu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Detecting troll accounts in Online Social Networks (OSN) has become increasingly critical for maintaining reliable online interactions. This study presents a comprehensive analysis of machine learning methods for troll detection on Reddit platform, evaluating seven classifiers, Support Vector Machine, Logistic Regression, Naive Bayes, K-Nearest Neighbors, Random Forest, AdaBoost, and XGBoost, along with hard and soft voting classifiers. Aggregating the outputs of the last three classifiers yielded the best results, achieving 94% accuracy with an F1-score of 94% for hard voting and 96% accuracy with an F1-score of 95% for soft voting, both surpassing prior works. Furthermore, a deep learning model (Deep Neural Network) was implemented and achieved high performance, reaching up to 95% accuracy and F1-score of 93%. In addition to this automated approach, this paper emphasizes the importance of Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) analysis for improving model performance through hyperparameter tuning, feature engineering, and misclassification analysis. Additionally, we propose a hybrid model that integrates automated analysis with a cognitive assistant trained by human expertise. This system facilitates continuous learning of troll patterns and Sockpuppet behaviors broadly. Future work will focus on implementing and validating this framework across larger datasets, aiming to develop a more accurate, adaptive, and resilient troll detection system.

title: Impact of the TensorFlow Library on Neural Network Performance for Harnessing Hardware Parallelism
authors: Abu Asaduzzaman, Fairuz Nawar, Duncan Campbell, Fadi N. Sibai
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: Neural networks constitute a well-established and extensively studied area of modern computational technology. Among available frameworks, TensorFlow remains one of the most prevalent platforms for implementing and experimenting with neural network architectures. TensorFlow abstracts the process of constructing and training neural networks, and supports several parallel computing interfaces, including Compute Unified Device Architecture (CUDA) and Direct Machine Learning (DirectML). However, the structure and training process may not be able to take full advantage of the underlying hardware due to how TensorFlow parallelizes computations. In this paper, we investigate the impact of the TensorFlow library on the performance of a sample Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) by varying two training parameters: batch size and the number of epochs. We use CNN to classify images from the Modified National Institute of Standards and Technology (MNIST) database of handwritten digits. We run the CNN on a desktop with an eight-core central processing unit (CPU) plus 4608-core graphics processing unit (GPU) and a high-performance computing (HPC) cluster with 2x18 CPU cores plus 5120 GPU cores. According to the experimental results, a larger batch size may reduce training time with negligible losses in accuracy for certain CNN parameters. Results suggest that optimization should be taken on a case-by-case basis to determine the best parameters.

title: Heliostat Field Optimization for a Solar Power Tower Using Differential Evolution and Multi-Stage Configuration Search
authors: Jiale Xu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: This paper studies the layout and configuration optimization of a circular heliostat field for a solar power tower system at 98.5 °E, 39.4 °N and 3 km altitude. A physics-informed annual performance evaluation model is established by combining solar-position astronomy, direct normal irradiance (DNI) estimation, and a composite optical-efficiency model including cosine loss, atmospheric attenuation, truncation loss, and mirror reflectivity. For uniform heliostats, a nonlinear constrained optimization is solved via differential evolution to jointly determine tower location, heliostat size, and installation height under spacing, boundary, and exclusion-zone constraints. For heterogeneous heliostats, a mixed discrete–continuous configuration is solved by a distance-layered specification policy and a multi-stage random search. The baseline field achieves an annual mean optical efficiency of 0.5805 and an annual mean thermal power of 18.65 MW. The optimized uniform configuration yields a best solution at tower location (24. 30, −0. 75) with 8m×8m heliostats at 4.22 m height, producing 20.75 MW. A multi-spec configuration deploys 1824 heliostats of seven sizes, producing 16.93 MW with comparable specific power.

title: Artificial Intelligence Powered Autonomous Surveillance Robot using Blockchain Security
authors: Safira Begum A, P Shobana, Devareddy Vedha, S Pavithra
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The device is a surveillance robot controlled by AI via internet commands while incorporating threat detection technology from AI and blockchain encryption for unhackable video storage. Within the overall security system, V2V communication facilitates collaboration of multiple robots so they can perform surveillance and at the same time establish extended connectivity and real-time monitoring features. Such an invention constitutes a smart monitoring device for secure places that can easily detect a breach and find the best path for it and a secure data management system at the same time. The novel security system will in addition bring night vision capabilities to aid in low-light observations and will also be the recipient of the automated threat predictions through the AI systems it is going to operate as a solar-powered long-term autonomous surveillance system.

title: Designing Secure and Compliant MLOps Pipelines for Financial Services using AWS SageMaker and Terraform
authors: Sandeep Reddy Bobbala
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: However, MLOps pipelines are particularly challenging to implement in highly regulated environments, which bring significant challenges related to security, provisioning and governance, and infrastructure automation. This paper provides a framework to build secure and compliant MLOps pipelines using AWS SageMaker managed ML pipelines and Terraform Infrastructure as Code (IaC). The methodology combines automated provisioning, secure model training and deployment, model monitoring and standards-based compliance validation (e.g. GDPR, PCI-DSS, and SOC 2). Results include increased pipeline reproducibility, compliance auditing and operational efficiency with reduced manual intervention. However, the dependencies with AWS ecosystem services, cost implications and regulatory interpretations will shift regularly and need to be adjusted. Financial institutions are challenged with unique challenges in the introduction of machine learning where strict security, compliance and governance requirements are to be met. This paper is all about the design of secure and compliant MLOps pipelines using AWS SageMaker & Terraform. The framework provides for scalability, reproducibility and operational efficiency financial services for powering innovation at scale, and for providing compliance and trust. The use of machine learning in the financial services is a balancing act as it involves combining innovation with highly regulated and security standards. This paper introduces the end-of-end framework of designing secure, compliant and scalable MLOps pipelines using AWS SageMaker and Terraform. SageMaker: Managed model development training and deployment capabilities Terraform: Reproducible infrastructure provisioning with infrastructure as code The proposed architecture includes the combination of encryption, fine-grained access control, network isolation, and automated audit logging for financial compliance needs.

title: SONAR: A Hybrid Explainable Emotion-Aware Music Recommendation Framework
authors: Harinarayanan U, Oneesa Faizal, Juben George John, Akash JP, K. Namitha
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Music influences emotional control, cultural identity, and individual well-being significantly. But today's music recommendation systems lack cultural awareness, contextual intelligence, and transparency in decision making; they rely on collaborative filtering, audio similarity, or simple mood classification. This paper presents SONAR, a novel Explainable Hybrid Emotion-Aware Music Recommendation System that integrates contextual intelligence, audio feature psychology, cultural modeling, text emotion recognition, speech emotion analysis, and safety-aware mental health filtering. SONAR proposes a threepillar hybrid architecture that considers (1) Cultural Context, (2) Audio Feature Science based on music psychology, and (3) Real-world Contextual Intelligence together for personalized genre recommendations. To produce transparent playlist choices, weighted reasoning is applied using a multilayer scoring framework with adaptability to user preferences. An Explainable AI layer is integrated at every stage of the pipeline to generate human-understandable explanations for mood detection, genre selection, safety judgments, and ordering of playlists. Balancing technological intelligence with psychological and cultural realism, this work presents a strong, ethically sound, culturally sensitive system that raises the bar above conventional models in user trust and emotional fit.

title: Event Detection in Dark Web and Surface Web Forums using Machine Learning
authors: Rodrigo Bernabé Silveira, Rodrigo Sanches Miani, Paulo Henrique Ribeiro Gabriel
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Detecting malicious posts on the Dark Web and Surface Web forums is crucial for cybersecurity. Analyzing these posts can enhance intrusion detection systems and aid in identifying cyberattacks. This analysis also reveals new threats and provides other benefits. By investigating forums on cybersecurity topics, various types of attacks can be proactively prevented. Therefore, it is essential to identify emerging events in posts with relevant cybersecurity keywords. This work aims to analyze forum posts from both sources over time to detect new events – specifically, emerging topics of cybersecurity interest. To evaluate the significance of detected events, a malicious post classifier categorized them as high, medium, or low relevance. The proposed algorithm proved promising. It identified 767 new events from a dataset of 7,643 analyzed posts. Approximately 0.9% of the detected events were classified as highly relevant.

title: Binarization-Guided K-Means Clustering for Fingerprint Image Compression
authors: Vimi Patel, Dhruv Trivedi, Jignesh Thaker
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The concept of the intended research is based on developing a fingerprint image compression and recognition pipeline that is intended to enhance storage and processing efficiency without endangering structural information of the fingerprint images. The compression algorithm includes implementing adaptive binarization through Otsu's thresholding algorithm and K-Means clustering algorithm to greatly down-sample the fingerprint image without compromising on the underlying structures. This is followed by a fingerprint recognition pipeline which performs normalization, region of interest (ROI) segmentation, skeletonization and minutiae extraction. Tests conducted on DB2 database of FVC 2002 revealed a significant reduction in file size with the extracted minutiae being stable enough for analysis even after the extreme compression. The quality assessment measures which include the Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR), Structural Similarity Index (SSIM) and False Minutiae Rate (FMR) indicate the trade-off between rates of compression of the fingerprint images and structural preservation of the same. These findings support the idea that extracting meaningful features of fingerprints is possible in the face of strongly compressed images, and it is a theoretically promising fact in the context of resource-constrained biometrics systems and databases of fingerprint images.

title: Weakly Supervised Lesion Localization Through Classification Based on U-Shaped Backbone
authors: Qiye Chen, Bingtian Qiao, Yiwen Wang, Qifeng Lin
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Although deep learning-based segmentation provides significant contributions in medical imaging, traditional supervised methods still rely heavily on pixel-level annotations, which are both scarce and costly to obtain. To solve this problem, we propose Weakly Supervised U-Shaped Architecture (WSUSA), which integrates a Cascaded Fusion Module (CFM) into a U-shaped backbone to perform image-level lesion classification. Moreover, the outputs from the backbone decoder provide an interpretable analysis. We train WSUSA using image-level labels from the COVID-19 Radiography Database. Experimental results demonstrate that WSUSA balances model performance with interpretability while reducing reliance on pixel-level annotations, offering potential for segmentation-limited scenarios.

title: NeuCODEX: Edge–Cloud Co-Inference with Spike-Driven Compression and Dynamic Early-Exit
authors: Maruf Hassan, Steven Davy, Muhammad Zawish, Owais Bin Zuber, Nouman Ashraf
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) offer significant potential for enabling energy-efficient intelligence at the edge. However, performing full SNN inference at the edge can be challenging due to the latency and energy constraints arising from fixed and high timestep overheads. Edge-cloud co-inference systems present a promising solution, but their deployment is often hindered by high latency and feature transmission costs. To address these issues, we introduce NeuCODEX, a neuromorphic co-inference architecture that jointly optimizes both spatial and temporal redundancy. NeuCODEX incorporates a learned spike-driven compression module to reduce data transmission and employs a dynamic early-exit mechanism to adaptively terminate inference based on output confidence. We evaluated NeuCODEX on both static images (CIFAR10 and Caltech) and neuromorphic event streams (CIFAR10-DVS and N-Caltech). To demonstrate practicality, we prototyped NeuCODEX on ResNet-18 and VGG-16 backbones in a real edge-to-cloud testbed. Our proposed system reduces data transfer by up to 2048x and edge energy consumption by over 90%, while reducing end-to-end latency by up to 3× compared to edge-only inference, all with a negligible accuracy drop of less than 2%. In doing so, NeuCODEX enables practical, high-performance SNN deployment in resource-constrained environments.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Mobile RSU Orchestration in Urban V2X Networks
authors: Gleb I. Tambovtsev, Pavel V. Plotnikov, Andrei G. Vladyko
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: This paper investigates the use of Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) for mobile Roadside Unit (mRSU) orchestration within a heterogeneous OBU–Fog–Cloud architecture. The system is formalized as a Markov Decision Process, where an agent learns to control mRSU movement based on network state observations, including spatial coordinates, task load, and delay metrics. A multi-component reward function is designed to balance task completion, deadline violations, and movement cost. The training process is conducted using Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) within a custom simulation environment (simV2X) that incorporates realistic radio channel models, including SNR-dependent modulation and airtime estimation under Urban Canyon conditions. Experimental evaluation compares the learned DRL policy with a baseline reactive strategy that relocates mRSUs in response to detected load increases. Results show that the DRL-based approach achieves delay distributions comparable to the reactive model while generating qualitatively different movement trajectories. Although consistent proactive performance gains were not observed, the learned policy demonstrates the ability to construct alternative spatial strategies under equivalent QoS constraints. The analysis suggests that reward shaping and state representation play a critical role in enabling truly anticipatory behavior in mobile V2X infrastructure control.

title: Automated Diagnostic System for Leukaemia Using CNN-Based Image Analysis
authors: Gaurav Tuteja, Kanegonda Ravi Chythanya, Neha Sharma, Preeti Badhani, Garima Jaitly
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Leukemia, a severe hematological malignancy, presents significant diagnostic challenges due to the subtle morphological differences among blood cell subtypes. This research proposes a convolutional neural network (CNN)-based framework for the automated classification of blood cells into four categories: benign, malignant pre-B, malignant pro-B, and malignant early pre-B. Utilizing a carefully curated dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3, 2 4 2}$</tex> microscopic blood cell images, the model architecture incorporates convolutional layers, batch normalization, max pooling, and dropout layers to enhance feature extraction and reduce overfitting. The model was trained over 30 epochs with a batch size of 32, employing real-time data augmentation and dynamic learning rate scheduling. Experimental evaluation demonstrated outstanding performance, achieving an overall accuracy of 97.9%, with class-specific precision, recall, and F1-scores consistently exceeding 0.94. Accuracy and loss plots confirmed the model's stable convergence, while the confusion matrix revealed minimal misclassification across classes. This study contributes to the advancement of automated hematological diagnostics, demonstrating the potential of deep learning to support early and accurate leukemia detection. The findings suggest promising applications in clinical workflows, enhancing diagnostic precision, reducing manual workload, and improving patient outcomes. Future work may explore larger datasets, alternative model architectures, and real-world deployment strategies to further strengthen diagnostic capabilities.

title: Java Code Generation Method Based on UML Class Diagrams and Large Language Model
authors: Linyun Cai, Yuandan Deng, Weiqin Chen, Bo Chen
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: We propose an automatic Java code generation method that integrates UML class diagrams with large language models. First, we employ DOM4J parsing and FreeMarker templates to transform complex, tool-exported UML XML into a unified and concise XML representation. We then construct umltocode, a task-specific dataset of aligned UML XML and Java pairs, and fine-tune CodeLlama to perform direct UML-to-Java generation. Finally, we evaluate generation quality using a structural consistency-oriented scoring strategy. Experimental results indicate that fine-tuning substantially improves both code structure fidelity and semantic accuracy, enabling fast and reliable synthesis of high-quality Java source code from class diagram specifications.

title: A Design and Evaluation of Intelligent Graph-based Learning Model for Unmanned Aerial Networks Guided Vehicular Communication
authors: G. Ramkumar
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: In this article, Unmanned Aerial Network based vehicular communication is developed to maximize the throughput and reduce the delay occurrences of the communication system. The inter-communication among the UAVs need betterment in a frequent manner to reduce the redundant message transmission. So that we need an effective vehicle grouping process to maximize the performance of UAVs. This proposed model is the combination of cluster coverage and intelligent graph-based learning process to maximize the UAVs efficiency (IGLUAV). These modules are constructed in the software called NS3 and the parameters which are calculated to analyses the performance of this model are data delivery ratio, delivery delay, data accuracy and loss calculation. When compared with the baseline methods the proposed IGLUAV obtained maximum accuracy which greatly increases the overall efficiency of the network.

title: DeepTamperNet Identification: A Hybrid Preprocessing and Feature Extraction Fusion Pipeline for Robust Deepfake Detection
authors: Sharon, N. Shyamala Devi
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The proliferation of crimes especially with the misuse of digital content embedded with advanced technology to manipulate individuals has largely accelerated in neoteric times. Simulated data has established trust amongst individuals, thereby making them vulnerable to several frauds that manipulate their identity. This manipulation has led to severe loss interms of finances and in also risking several lives. Deepfakes are one such cybercrimes that has increased gargantuanly, thereby necessitating the impeccable technological mechanisms to agnize fakes, tampering and manipulation. Although existing research identifies deepfakes, the approach of static identification makes it impossible to identify the tampered data dynamically while in the process of manipulation. This determination overcomes the gap evinced in the existing studies by incorporating tampering levels in a dynamic data. The tampered heatmap levels are shown dynamically to comprehend the level of tampering, thereby enabling users to distinguish between manipulated and genuine data. The paper entails a detection pipeline through the processes of pre-processing and feature unsheathing using deep heuristic techniques. The Multi-Task Cascaded Convolutional Neural Network (MTCNN) is used for face detection embedded with different pre-processing techniques. The study analyses the tamper score which accelerates the evaluative quantification of manipulated data. A hybrid deep learning model such as the ResNet18, MobileNetV2 and EfficientNetB0 fused with hybrid heuristic edge algorithm is combined to form the "DeepTamperNet" that aids in stratifying the dynamic data into fake and genuine. This research aids in curbing cybercrimes through efficient tampering cognizance deliverables that can be used as video tampering detectors. The results for this study are successfully procured, and show that the tamper score obtained from different combination of algorithms and pre-processing methods, and enable the user to explicitly identify manipulated data.

title: The Impact of Artificial Intelligence on the Model of Higher Engineering Education
authors: Tatyana A. Kuzovkova, Olga I. Sharavova, Ivan M. Sharavov
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: The high rates of development and application of artificial intelligence (AI) and neural networks in industry, transport, economics and social life are characterized by a significant impact on cognitive, educational and industrial processes, which actualizes the need to transform the system of higher engineering education. The article substantiates the role and place of AI in the digital transformation of the state, building a data economy and implementing its federal projects, provides a statistical description of the use of AI in the economy by types of technologies and the composition of products and services sold, and reveals the methods, tools and tools of big data analytics. The importance of artificial intelligence in engineering higher education is considered, its advantages, opportunities and emerging risks are substantiated in four aspects: academic, technological, socio-personal and intellectual. Based on the conclusion that it is necessary to transform the educational model, the required competencies of artificial intelligence specialists are revealed, and the principles and conditions for using AI in higher education are formulated.

title: Digital Leadership Practices and Their Influence on Employee Productivity in IT-Enabled Workplaces
authors: Nirmala Gandla, M.Rajya Laxmi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The digital environment becomes meaningful in the information technology of today due to companies' desire for greater efficiency, innovation and collaboration. This shift is all about digital leadership practices in which employees can navigate this change in technology without sacrificing productivity. The authors of this study examined the possible impacts on individual productivity in the workplace and considered the role of digital culture as a moderator of the relationship between digital leadership practices and individual productivity. The survey data comprised 200 participants employed in IT-enabled work environments across different sectors and was compiled using a quantitative research design. The data was analyzed using multiple regression analysis to identify the relationships between digital leadership practices, digital culture and employee productivity. The analysis determined that digital leadership practices can have a positive and significant effect on employee productivity, and that digital culture is a moderator, based on its complementary effect to that of digital leadership practices. These discoveries affirmed a need to promote positive digital leadership behaviors and fostering digital cultures in order to improve employee performance in IT-enabled organizations.

title: Spectral and Spatial Graph Learning for Multispectral Solar Image Compression
authors: Prasiddha Siwakoti, Atefeh Khoshkhahtinat, Piyush M. Mehta, Barbara J. Thompson, Michael S. F. Kirk, Daniel Da Silva
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: High-fidelity compression of multispectral solar imagery remains challenging for space missions, where limited bandwidth must be balanced against preserving fine spectral and spatial details. We present a learned image compression framework tailored to solar observations, leveraging two complementary modules: (1) the Inter-Spectral Windowed Graph Embedding (iSWGE), which explicitly models inter-band relationships by representing spectral channels as graph nodes with learned edge features; and (2) the Windowed Spatial Graph Attention and Convolutional Block Attention (WSGA-C), which combines sparse graph attention with convolutional attention to reduce spatial redundancy and emphasize fine-scale structures. Evaluations on the SDOML dataset across six extreme ultraviolet (EUV) channels show that our approach achieves a 20.15% reduction in Mean Spectral Information Divergence (MSID), up to 1.09% PSNR improvement, and a 1.62% log-transformed MS-SSIM gain over strong learned baselines, delivering sharper and spectrally faithful reconstructions at comparable bits-per-pixel rates. The code is publicly available at https://github.com/agyat4/sgraph.

title: Multi-Modal Virtual Input using Hand and Voice Commands
authors: V.M.Jemin, J Sherine Glory, Mosses A, N. Ramshankar, Manikandan K, Saravanan K
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The field of human-computer interaction has under- gone significant transformation with the integration of machine learning, paving the way for touchless control systems that enhance user experiences. This study introduces a gesture- driven virtual interface that enables users to operate digital devices through hand movements. The system identifies and recognizes hand gestures in real time through the use of machine learning technology, enabling the user to navigate the cursor, perform a click, or input data into the keyboard without having to touch the screen. The invention can serve as a substitute for conventional input devices, which give physically disabled individuals greater accessibility and introduce a new level of interaction into the virtual and augmented reality realms. The system is easily compatible with contemporary computing environments and is effective, user-friendly, and adaptable to various environments. Experimental work demonstrates high error margin and responsiveness, which support its prospects as a reliable hands-free interaction device to use in assistive technology, game design, virtual reality, and smart home control.

title: Optimizing Reverse Logistics Capacity for Sustainable Supply Chain of Electronics: A Multi-Objective Framework
authors: Jeevan Mohapatra, Vijender Yadav, Prashant Ashiwal, Chandra Shekhar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This study presents a capacity-constrained, multi-objective optimization frame-work for reverse logistics in electronic product supply chains, illustrated through a detailed case study of Apple's iPhone operations. The framework employs a three-state deterministic model, capturing serviceable inventory, refurbishment, and recycling, formulated using a system of ordinary differential equations. To evaluate the inherent trade-offs between cost efficiency and environmental performance, a genetic algorithm–driven Pareto optimization approach is used to map the sustainability frontier. The results reveal that refurbishment capacity is the most critical driver influencing both cost and emissions, demonstrating substantially higher sensitivity than production capacity. Expanding refurbishment capability delivers concurrent reductions in operational expenditure and carbon footprint, underscoring the strategic value of circular economy pathways. The identified knee-point solution offers a well-balanced and operationally realistic compromise for decision-makers. Overall, the proposed framework provides actionable guidance for capacity investment and sustainable supply chain planning, contributing to more resilient and environmentally responsible electronics logistics systems.

title: Sleep Disorder Classification Using Optimized Ensemble Machine Learning and Genetic Algorithms
authors: Kakkera Prasanthi, Kosana Venkata Navadheer, Jayanth Devisetty, Kalupukuri Santhosh Kumar, U.D. Prasan, K. Adilakshmi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The rise in the number of cases of insomnia and sleep apnea in contemporary society has a negative impact on the physical and mental health of the individuals. Timely and correct diagnosis of such a disorder is important in healthcare and medical diagnosis. It is in the environment of multi-class machine learning, where the algorithm is applied to the Sleep Health and Lifestyle data, where the author identifies different sleep disorders based on demographic and lifestyle variables. The number of samples is 400 which are the variables such as age, gender, occupation, sleep duration, sleep quality, body mass index (BMI) and blood pressure. The learning techniques used in this research are Decision Tree (DT), Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Machine (SVM), Artificial Neural Network (ANN) and k-Nearest Neighbour (KNN). Performance measures (accuracy, precision, recall and F1-score) are used to evaluate it. One can assume that, according to the obtained results of the performed experiments, the assessed ensemble and deep learning methods turn out to be more efficient than the conventional machine learning algorithms in the classification of sleep disorders.

title: Integrating Physiological Signals for Enhanced Sleep Apnea Detection Using Deep Learning Techniques
authors: Rudra Vyas, Parva Lunawat, Santosh Kumar Satapathy, Nibedita Das, Suren Kumar Sahu, Rajanikanta Mohanty
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Sleep apnea (SA) holds substantial clinical importance, as it markedly elevates the risk of cardiovascular and cerebrovascular complications. However, its diagnosis through full polysomnography (PSG) presents several drawbacks, including high costs and the use of numerous sensors that can cause patient discomfort and disrupt normal sleep. To address the above problems, this study proposes using single-channel photoplethysmography (PPG) to detect pediatric sleep apnea (SA) in an automated manner. One of the main issues lies in identifying small, non-steady-state apneic events in raw PPG records, while simultaneously reducing the large class imbalance exhibited by clinical data. These aims were achieved through the analysis of two deep-learning pipelines: (1) a one-dimensional convolutional neural network (1D-CNN) that directly takes raw PPG timeseries data as input, and (2) a two-dimensional convolutional neural network (2D-CNN) that accepts time-frequency data (scalograms) created using continuous wavelet transform (CWT) as input. The Childhood Adenotonsillectomy Trial (CHAT) dataset, including 246 subjects, was used, and class imbalance was handled using controlled oversampling and class-weighted loss functions. The baseline 1D-CNN reached an overall accuracy of 80.0 %, but with a sensitivity of only 3.6 % to apneic events, exemplifying the accuracy paradox. Conversely, the optimised 2D-CNN using complex Morlet wavelets demonstrated significantly increased subject-independent performance: in a leave-one-subject-out (LOSO) study on a twelve-subject sample, the average accuracy was 86.8 %, the area under the AUC-ROC curve was 0.893, and the macro F 1 -score was 79.7 %. Future research can explore multimodal fusion, temporal aggregation, real-time signal quality, and clinical support. Overall, 2D-CNN of CWT scalograms from single-channel PPG is a promising wearable-based pediatric SA screening method, outperforming 1D-CNN, though temporal modelling and artefact robustness need refinement before clinical use.

authors: Xiongfei Wu, Mingfei Cheng, Xiaoning Ren, Qiang Hu, Jianlang Chen, Yuheng Huang, Maxime Cordy, Yao Zhang, Xiaofei Xie, Lei Ma, Yves Le Traon

title: Supervised Contrastive Learning for Uncertainty-Aware Wireless Signal Analysis: A Case Study for Modulation Classification
authors: Han Zhang, Mohammad Farzanullah, Akram Bin Sediq, Ali Afana, Melike Erol-Kantarci
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI), and more specifically deep learning techniques, have demonstrated strong capabilities in processing wireless signals, enabling automatic modulation classification (AMC). However, existing AI-based AMC methods often produce unreliable predictions and lack robustness to out-of-distribution (OOD) inputs, which limits their deployment in real-world scenarios. This study aims to address the gap in simulations to real deployments by fitting predictions to OOD scenarios. We propose an uncertainty-aware AMC framework based on supervised contrastive learning (SupCon). The proposed framework aims to enhance classification reliability, particularly under OOD conditions. In this framework, a ResNet-based representation learning model that consists of a feature extractor and a projection head is first trained using a combination of SupCon loss and cross-entropy (CE) loss to produce class-discriminative embeddings. By decoupling the shared representation learning model from task-specific classifiers, the framework enables a modular and computationally efficient uncertainty estimation strategy, avoiding redundant computation during inference. The learned embeddings are then processed by a set of class-wise binary classifiers that provide both classification and sample-wise uncertainty estimates. A rejection mechanism is incorporated to improve decision reliability by discarding uncertain predictions. We evaluate the proposed framework on the RadioML 2018 dataset. Experimental results show that our approach significantly improves representation quality and classification reliability compared to conventional supervised learning. Specifically, the classification accuracy increases from 63.7% to 93.6% under in-distribution conditions, and from 30.8% to 92.5% under 50% OOD contamination, while maintaining over 85% recall on accepted predictions.

authors: Ningyi Liao, Haoyu Liu, Zulun Zhu, Siqiang Luo, Laks V.S. Lakshmanan

title: CDNet: Consistency-Driven Semi-Supervised Object Detection for Remote Sensing
authors: Yuhan Sun, Shengyang Li, Jian Yang, Han Wang, Yuxuan Liu, Zhuang Zhou, Qi Wen, Jianing You, Kailun Zhang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Remote sensing images exhibit arbitrary orientations, uneven sample distributions, and large scale variations. Existing semi-supervised detectors mainly handle rotation yet neglect how distribution and scale impair pseudo-label quality and positive–negative sample assignment. These limitations manifest in three ways: (1) Inconsistent features. Classification and regression features often mismatch, leading pseudo-labels selected based on classification scores to poorly align with ground truths. (2) Inconsistent sample distributions. A fixed selection threshold yields unstable pseudo-labels due to uneven object distributions, injecting noise into training. (3) Inconsistent discriminative regions. Objects with extreme aspect ratios often have key features at the periphery, causing them to be mislabeled as background. To tackle these issues, we propose CDNet, a novel Consistency-Driven semi-supervised object detection network for remote sensing with four designs: (i) A task-feature alignment module align classification and regression features, allowing classification scores to better reflect pseudo-label quality. (ii) A GMM-based adaptive thresholding strategy that adjusts pseudo-label selection criteria across training stages to cope with uneven distributions. (iii) A sample assignment consistency constraint method and (iv) a scale consistency loss to regularize high-aspect-ratio and scale-varied objects. Extensive experiments on DOTAv1.5 and DIOR-R demonstrate CDNet's superiority over state-of-the-art methods.

title: Counteracting the Active Area Reduction Caused by Explicit Guard Rings in High-Voltage CMOS SPADs
authors: Ivan Berdalović, Borna Požar, Paula Bartulović, Patrik Sočković, Tomislav Suligoj
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: Novel structures of circular single-photon avalanche diodes (SPADs) with different active junction radii (9 and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\text{4}~{\mu }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>m) have been fabricated in a commercial 180-nm high-voltage (HV) CMOS process. The devices were connected to an active quenching and recharge circuit (AQRC) fabricated in the same technology, capable of operating at excess voltages up to 20 V, and characterized in terms of dark count rate (DCR), afterpulsing probability (AP), and position-dependent photon detection probability (PDP). The device with the larger radius exhibits state-of-the-art noise performance with a DCR per unit area below 0.2 Hz/<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$ {\mu }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>m<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{\text {2}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\textit {V}_{\textbf {EX}}=\text {5}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> V, but the device with the smaller radius shows a further improvement in DCR by more than a factor of 10, which clearly does not scale with area. Technology computer-aided design (TCAD) simulations have been performed to explain the differences in breakdown voltage and DCR between the two structures, and the record-low DCR of the smaller device was linked to a significant decrease in effective active area, caused by the electric field decrease due to charge sharing between the explicit guard ring (GR) and the multiplication region, as confirmed by position-dependent PDP measurements over the entire device. Nevertheless, it is demonstrated that the active area can be increased by applying a higher excess voltage while achieving a central PDP above 20% at a wavelength of 635 nm, making the device an attractive candidate for ultralow-noise applications where a high fill factor is not a critical requirement.

title: Seems like a Touch? Comparing Emotional Experience and Illusion of Touch of Visual and Haptic Presentations of Mediated Social Touch in VR
authors: Julius Tietenberg, Timo Fürtges, Maic Masuch
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Social virtual reality (VR) applications enable users to experience highly immersive mediated interactions and social experiences. Especially embodied nonverbal communication, like mediated social touch, is mostly unique to social VR and potentially a major factor in enabling fulfilling emotional experiences at a distance. In our within-subjects study (N = 28), we compared feedback from wearable vibrotactile haptic devices with two established visual presentation forms of social touch: clipping and pseudo-haptics. Our findings suggest that the addition of haptic feedback can elicit significantly higher emotional arousal and result in a stronger illusion of touch than visual presentations alone. In contrast to existing literature, there was no evidence of a difference between the immersive and emotional experience of clipping and pseudo-haptics in our sample. In addition, we also investigated whether simply wearing haptic feedback devices during a social touch interaction, i.e., without active haptic feedback, affects the illusion of touch and user experience. However, there was no evidence of a significant effect in the sample studied. A free copy of this paper, including the appendix, is available at https://doi.org/10.17605/OSF.IO/4FYKQ.

title: Occlusion-Free Conformal Lensing for Spatiotemporal Visualization in 3D Urban Analytics
authors: Roberta Mota, Julio D. Silva, Fabio Miranda, Usman Alim, Ehud Sharlin, Nivan Ferreira
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: The visualization of temporal data on urban buildings, such as shadows, noise, and solar potential, plays a critical role in the analysis of dynamic urban phenomena. However, in dense and geographically constrained 3D urban environments, visual representations of time-varying building data often suffer from occlusion and visual clutter.
