abstract: This paper investigates a Cuk-derived bridgeless power factor correction (PFC) converter for single-phase 220 V AC input supply, modeled and evaluated using MATLAB/Simulink. By doing away with the requirement for a front-end diode bridge, the bridgeless construction reduces conduction losses and boosts efficiency. The converter functions in discontinuous conduction mode (DCM), which intrinsically facilitates input current shaping while minimizing sensor needs. An Integral Type-2 Fuzzy Logic Controller (IT2FLC) is utilized to control the output DC voltage while improving dynamic response across a broad range of load and source conditions. In comparison to traditional PI control, the suggested controller exhibits greater resilience, providing faster transient recovery and less steady-state error.According to simulation results, the input current minimizes total harmonic distortion (THD) and achieves near-unity power factor by closely following the source voltage waveform. The measured THD values are well within the limits prescribed by IEC 61000-3-2 standards, ensuring compliance for practical deployment. The overall findings highlight that the combination of Cuk-derived bridgeless topology and intelligent fuzzy control provides a reliable and efficient solution for modern AC–DC conversion applications such as electric vehicle chargers and distributed renewable energy systems.

title: Assessing Ethical Risks of Data-Driven Prediction Models in Healthcare AI
authors: Rachna Rathore, Rajendra Kumar Vairagi, Raghavaiah Bonam, Deepika, Rajesh Kumar Tripathi, Minakshi Poonia
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The use of artificial intelligence (AI) to improve predictive modeling of healthcare has increased disease detection, planning of treatment, and risk assessment, but it also raises ethical issues concerning bias, fairness, transparency, and privacy of data. This paper analyses these concerns by comparing a traditional machine learning model with an ethical-enhanced model on a diabetes data. The model that was created with no bias-reducing measures had the accuracy of 75.76%. Comparatively, the ethically enhanced model, which contained the fairness-oriented technique, including the Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE), achieved a precision of 7 5. 3 2%. Even though the improved model demonstrated slight decrease in the overall accuracy, it performed greatly improvements in recall among diabetic patients (74% vs. 66%), which minimized the risk of false diagnosis. Analysis of feature importance was done to enhance model transparency. The findings emphasize the need to incorporate fairness-based concepts of healthcare AI to reduce bias and improve ethical reliability and competitive predictive accuracy.

title: NORA: A Nephrology-Oriented Representation Learning Approach Towards Chronic Kidney Disease Classification
authors: Mohammad Abdul Hafeez Khan, Twisha Bhattacharyya, Omar Khan, Noorah Khan, Alina Aziz Fatima Khan, Mohammed Qutub Khan, Sujoy Ghosh Hajra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) affects millions of people worldwide, yet its early detection remains challenging, especially in outpatient settings where laboratory-based renal biomarkers are often unavailable. In this work, we investigate the predictive potential of routinely collected non-renal clinical variables for CKD classification, including sociodemographic factors, comorbid conditions, and urinalysis findings. We introduce the Nephrology-Oriented Representation leArning (NORA) approach, which combines supervised contrastive learning with a nonlinear Random Forest classifier. NORA first derives discriminative patient representations from tabular EHR data, which are then used for downstream CKD classification. We evaluated NORA on a clinic-based EHR dataset from Riverside Nephrology Physicians. Our results demonstrated that NORA improves class separability and overall classification performance, particularly enhancing the F1-score for early-stage CKD. Additionally, we assessed the generalizability of NORA on the UCI CKD dataset, demonstrating its effectiveness for CKD risk stratification across distinct patient cohorts.

title: Laboratory Evaluation of Federated, Hierarchical Controls for Distribution Power System Management
authors: Annabelle Pratt, Venkateswara Reddy Motakatla, Ismael Mendoza, Harsha Padullaparti, Jiyu Wang, Andrew Ingram
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: The connection of more flexible loads, generation and storage to the distribution power system brings challenges, such as increased variability in net load profiles, and opportunities to system operators. There are opportunities to aggregate flexible loads, generation and storage to provide transmission grid services, but the coordinated actions of these distribution assets being managed by independent, third-party aggregators to support transmission system operations can present challenges to distribution system operations. We developed a federated management architecture and control framework that aims to manage heterogeneous assets to deliver reliable transmission grid services while respecting distribution system constraints. The controls include stochastic day-ahead optimization, model predictive control, and a simple real-time management scheme. We present simulation results obtained from a realistic laboratory test bed of federated controls managing distribution assets within a substation service area to make the substation net power follow the optimal net power determined by the day-ahead optimization.

title: Quantifying Grid Resilience Under Wind Events: A Multi-Utility Analysis of Outage Risk
authors: Wenqi Zhang, Reiko Matsuda-Dunn, Vincent Westfallen, Abodh Poudyal
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: Understanding how wind events affect electric grid infrastructure is critical for developing risk-informed resilience strategies. This paper presents a quantitative assessment of wind-driven outages across multiple anonymized utilities operating in diverse climatic and topographic regions of the United States. Using multi-year outage records integrated with bias-corrected wind data, the study develops metrics that capture how distribution system performance responds to wind intensity, duration, and spatial exposure. Outage frequency, affected customers, and restoration duration are aggregated to derive resilience indicators such as cumulative impact curves and wind-outage response thresholds. Comparative analyses highlight how local exposure conditions influence outage behavior across regions. The results provide actionable insights into the sensitivity of electric distribution networks to wind stress and demonstrate a scalable, data-driven framework for quantifying outage risk. The approach supports the development of consistent resilience metrics that can inform future utility planning and risk-informed investment decisions.

title: Hybrid SVM and K-Means Clustering Model for Anomaly Detection in Wireless Sensor Network Environments
authors: Ashitha V Naik, Gayathri V, S. Menaka, J. Mrudula, Amarnath Prabhakaran A, M Sreedhar
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Detecting anomalies in wireless sensor networks (WSNs) is essential for ensuring secure and dependable connectivity, particularly in applications related to critical infrastructures and real-time monitoring. Conventional detection techniques frequently have increased false-positive rates, restricted scalability, and challenges in managing unlabelled or partially labelled datasets. This research presents a hybrid machine learning framework that integrates K-Means clustering with Support Vector Machine (SVM) classification to overcome these limitations. The model first employs K-Means to identify behavioural patterns and create structural clusters, which are subsequently enhanced using SVM to classify anomalies with more accuracy. This dual-phase methodology improves both sensitivity and specificity in identifying malicious or abnormal actions. Experiments performed on benchmark WSN datasets demonstrate exceptional performance, attaining 98.4% accuracy, 98.1% precision, 97.9% recall, 98.0% F1 score, and a 99.2% ROC-AUC score. The suggested system demonstrates markedly enhanced classification performance relative to baseline models like Naïve Bayes, KNN, and standalone SVM, especially in high-dimensional and noisy contexts. The suggested approach is both theoretically efficient and practically deployable in real-world wireless sensor network applications, including smart agriculture, healthcare, and industrial control systems. The hybrid design provides a scalable and intelligent solution to a significant difficulty in WSN security.

title: Mathematical Modeling of the Propagation of Decameter Radio Waves in Magnetoactive Cold Plasma Based on the Bicharacteristic System and Spatio-Temporal Geometric Diffraction Theory
authors: Andrew S. Kryukovsky, Dmitry V. Rastyagaev
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: An asymptotic approach, alternative to the Fourier method, for description of the radio pulse propagation in ionospheric plasma - the method of space-time geometric theory of diffraction-and the possibility of its numerical implementation are considered. Mathematical modeling of the amplitude structure of a radio pulse propagation in homogeneous ionospheric plasma was carried out. A numerical comparison of nonuniform and uniform asymptotics containing Fresnel functions is performed.

title: Comparative Analysis of Machine Learning and Deep Learning Models for Mental Health Classification: A Comprehensive Study
authors: Sameer Barwal, Shiv K. Sharma
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The classification of mental health has become a prominent issue in the areas of digital healthcare and preventive medicine. This research is in line with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals, especially SDG 3 (Good Health and Well-being) by supporting the early detection, and SDG 10 (Reduced Inequalities) by pointing out the need for lightweight and interpretable models that are suitable for resourcepoor environments. The paper introduces such a wide-ranging comparative study of machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) techniques for mental health risk classification. The experiments were carried out on a dataset containing 2,000 individuals with 11 demographic, lifestyle, and psychological characteristics, and eight algorithms were evaluated: Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, Support Vector Machine, Logistic Regression, Naive Bayes, K-Nearest Neighbors, Decision Tree, and Neural Networks. Logistic Regression model triumphed over all models with 81.25 % accuracy, 81.33 % precision, 81.25 % recall, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$81.11 \% ~\mathrm{F} 1$</tex> score. The dominance analysis of the features revealed that stress level (26.76 %), mental health history (14.42 %), and sleep hours (14.27 %) were the most prominent indicators of the risk of mental health. The findings indicate the need for a balance between the predictive performance and the interpretability of the model, showing that simpler models can outperform complex ones based on structured mental health data. This research encourages the use of medical screening tools based on transparent ML in clinical settings and demonstrates the possibility of incorporating AI-driven mental health risk classifiers into electronic healthcare systems for better patient monitoring and availability of psychological support services.

title: Advancing Environmental Disaster Classification using Deep Learning Models
authors: Varshitha Dodla, Sai Sreepad Punith Chindukuru, Rajesh Boppana, Srinivasa Rao Vankdoth
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Weather uncertainty is realized in the form of a major threat to regions like agriculture, transportation, and public safety, which makes effective disaster classification systems inevitable. The paper suggests using a deep learning approach in the Environmental Disaster Management Identifier for the classification of weather events using image data. Architectures were tested between CNN, AlexNet, VGG16, GoogleNet, ResNet, AutoEncoders, and a tailored Encoder+ResNet architecture, each exposed to optimization methods like SGD, Adam, and RMSprop. GoogleNet and ResNet kept the validation accuracy at the highest of 96.15%, while VGG16 was very poor at a rate of 30.77%, and AlexNet followed at 69.23%, therefore validating that the accommodating of deeper architectures is beneficial when learning intricate disaster patterns. The hybrid Encoder+ResNet model offers additional improvement to feature reconstruction and classification capability, hence promising towards scalable real-time disaster detection. These results highlight the need for accurate selection of optimizers and architectural inference and demonstrate that such models are viable for deployment in intelligent early warning systems. Through the combination of strong deep learning models and real-time image feeds, this strategy presents promising avenues for enhancing disaster response, risk reduction, and decision-making in dynamic environmental contexts.

title: Distribution Phasor Measurement Unit with Edge Computing for Enhanced Distribution Grid Monitoring and Power Quality Applications
authors: Brijesh P, Arya G Lal, Anoop P, Ajeesh A, Manju A S, Aby Joseph
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: Distribution systems were historically lagged behind transmission system on its observability, measurement and monitoring technologies. The economics of monitoring system is much welcoming in transmission system due to its relatively less nodes for monitoring, high value infrastructure and quantity of power transfer. The measurements required for a distribution system needs to be more precise and faster when compared with that of a transmission line. Per node infrastructure investment in distribution system will be very less when compared to transmission system. Distribution infrastructure is generally the major contributor of power system outages. Also, penetration of large number of renewable energy sources made the distribution power flow into a bi-directional from its legacy unidirectional and predictable nature. The urge for green transportation system introduced an unpredictable and variable energy demand in addition to the 'masked' loads created by RES. The Distribution Phasor Measurement Unit (D-PMU) is a promising technology for distribution grid monitoring. This paper presents a comprehensive framework for a D-PMU integrated with edge computing capabilities, specifically designed for power quality monitoring and analysis applications.

title: A Multimodal Model for Analyzing Publications of Copyrighted Works
authors: S. A. Palibza, A. A. Rodina, D. V. Gadasin, D. V. Rulev
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: Currently, authors have the opportunity to publish their works on a large number of electronic resources. To perform a quick search, each publication is provided with a tag. Based on the tags, publications belong to a specific genre. The correlation process is quite complex and requires a lot of resources. One of the possible classification methods is the multimodal model, which is considered in this paper. This model should take into account the existing ONIX publication classification standards and the automatic document parsing algorithm with GROBID data extraction. The model is based on the principal component method. The paper considers a working example. The developed model can be used not only for working with text data, but also in machine learning and image recognition systems.

title: ViLT and Customized Classification Block for Remote Sensing Visual Question Answering
authors: Jaswanth Polarowthu, Bala Venkateswarlu Isunuri
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Transformer models have been greatly improved recently, which has led to their extensive use in the process of captions for images, responding to questions regarding images, and the processing of both images and words. The current models use extensive computational power, time, and many configurable parameters for handling inquiries about remote working and detecting images. In this paper, we introduced an efficient and rapid visual question answering (VQA) system for satellite images using the Vision-and-Language Transformer framework (ViLT). Compared to the past models of remote sensing VQA, our approach improves the efficiency of the training process while maintaining robust image understanding. Our model utilizes a ViLT backbone consisting of immobilized transformer components. A customized block using dense layers with ReLU6 activation has been developed for categorization and the generation of answers from low-resolution images. This approach makes it easier to adapt to VQA tasks that involve several tunable parameters. Testing of the conventional remote sensing VQA (RSVQA) dataset shows that our system performs well with an overall accuracy of 86.26%. Moreover, this proposed model has lower operational expenses than the alternatives that require comprehensive retraining.

title: Toward Unified Moderation of Cyberbullying Across Social Media and Video Games
authors: David Cong, Keyan Guo, Hongxin Hu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: The growing integration of social media and video games in adolescents’ daily lives has heightened the risk of cyberbullying, contributing to severe mental health challenges such as depression and suicidal ideation. Traditional moderation techniques, including manual review and rule-based filters, often fail to capture contextual nuances. Existing machine learning models also face limitations due to their reliance on large labeled datasets and lack of cross-platform adaptability. Our experiments further reveal that models trained on social media content perform poorly on video game data and vice versa, leading to inefficient moderation. To address these challenges, we propose a novel approach that leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) with Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning for cyberbullying detection across both domains. This method eliminates the need for extensive training data while significantly improving accuracy. It achieves 90.3% and 91.1% accuracy on social media and video game datasets, respectively, offering a scalable and resource-efficient solution for cyberbullying moderation.

title: Research on Fault Diagnosis Algorithm of Liquid Permeability Measurement Equipment Based on CNN-LSTM
authors: Shuaishuai Teng, Zhejun Pan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Shale liquid permeability measurement equipment is a core measurement device in shale gas development, and its operating status directly determines the accuracy of permeability data and the rationality of development plans. When the equipment operates under complex operating conditions, it generates a large amount of time-series operating data with spatiotemporal coupling characteristics, and there are strong nonlinear relationships between the data, which increases the difficulty of fault diagnosis. To this end, a fault detection method based on the Convolutional Neural Network - Long Short-Term Memory (CNN-LSTM) hybrid algorithm is proposed for SMP-200 shale permeability meters (GRI-95/0496) and GB/T34533-2017-compliant equipment. Firstly, 5 typical faults (e.g., sensor drift) are defined per industrial standards, and key parameters like injection pressure are selected as diagnostic indicators; then, key parameters such as injection pressure, instantaneous flow rate, and pressure difference at both ends of the core are selected as fault diagnosis indicators, and the variation laws of characteristic parameters under various faults are calculated; finally, the preprocessed time-series characteristic parameters are used as input to construct a CNN-LSTM hybrid fault detection model. The CNN layer extracts the spatial correlation characteristics of the data, the LSTM layer captures the temporal dependence, and the cross-entropy loss function is introduced to optimize the model parameters, realizing accurate fault detection of the equipment. Experimental results show that the algorithm has an average recognition accuracy of 98.7% for five typical faults (e.g., pressure sensor drift), outperforming LSTM-CNN, LightGBM, and other models. The recognition rate of early minor faults is improved by 15.3 percentage points compared with a single algorithm, and the maximum diagnosis delay is ≤0.8 seconds, confirming accurate and reliable fault detection.

title: Accurate Classification of Soft Tissue Sarcoma using Hybrid Models
authors: Vineela Madireddy, HariKrishna Bommala
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Soft Tissue Sarcoma is an aggressive malignant tumor with more than 50 histological subtypes early detection of these tumors is also very crucial. Most of these tumors are undetected until it is progressed to a more advanced stage significantly Affecting treatment outcomes. The goal of the research is to utilize both the DL and ML algorithms to analyze metastasis with the help of MRI, X-Rays and CT-scan. This study focuses on classifying the image into viable tumor or nonviable tumor at the initial stages using CNN, LSTM, BiLSTM and compared with proposed model sarcomaNet. A Novel sarcomaNet integrates CNN, attention mechanism and sequential mechanism to enhance diagnostic performance. The proposed model Sarcoma Net achieves classification with accuracy of 87.5% which demonstrates superior performance when compared with traditional models which aids clinicians in early diagnosis.

title: Multimodal Sentiment Analysis Based on Dynamic Language Enhancement and Synergistic Cross-Modal Transformer
authors: Linqin Cai, Daohong Liu, Lanrui Liu, Renyun Mou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: Multimodal sentiment analysis (MSA) is a challenging task that utilizes verbal, visual, and acoustic cues to infer human sentiment and has garnered substantial research attention in recent years. However, due to the diversity of multimodal data, current MSA methods often fail to adequately leverage the rich semantic knowledge present in the linguistic modality while also overlooking the issue of informational redundancy within the visual and auditory modalities. In addition, the intermodal heterogeneity and spurious cross-modal interactions also pose huge challenges for effective multimodal fusion. To address these issues, we propose an MSA approach based on dynamic linguistic enhancement and synergistic cross-modal Transformer (LESCT). Our LESCT constructs a dynamic language enhancement network (LEN) for feature extraction. The proposed LEN enables visual and auditory features to dynamically capture contextual cues from multigranularity language representations via guided attention mechanism, thereby mitigating intramodal redundancy and noise interference. On this basis, the LESCT builds a new synergistic cross-modal Transformer (SCT) and local-to-composite multimodal fusion strategy. The SCT network employs a bimodal generator to produce composite features for each pair of modalities, transferring the composite information from the bimodal features to complementary unimodal features to facilitate rich intermodel and intramodal interaction. Extensive experiments were performed on three popular MSA benchmark datasets CMU-MOSI, CMU-MOSEI, and CH-SIMS. The overall accuracy of our LESCT is 86.43% on CMU-MOSI, 86.38% on CMU-MOSEI, and 81.35% on CH-SIMS. Experimental results demonstrate that our proposed LESCT is superior to the state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods. The code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/jhwvh/LESCT</uri>

title: Enhancing Local Differential Privacy Accuracy by Exploiting Inherent Uncertainty
authors: Peng Tang, Xiya Shao, Rui Chen, Ning Wang, Shanqing Guo
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
abstract: None

title: Temperature-Dependent Modeling and Optimal Design of LLC Resonant Converters Considering High-Voltage Field-Stop IGBTs Turn-Off Transient
authors: Shuiyuan He, Ruiqi Ma, Chongshan Xie, Yunhao Guo, Jiepin Zhang, Yingtao Ma, Lijun Diao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: In high-voltage high-power rail transit auxiliary power supply (APS) systems, ensuring zero-voltage switching (ZVS) under discontinuous conduction mode (DCM) is critical for LLC resonant converters (LLC RCs). However, the temperature sensitivity of the turn-off transient in high-voltage field-stop insulated gate bipolar transistors (FS IGBTs) is often neglected, posing a risk of ZVS failure under thermal stress. In response to this challenge, this article proposes a temperature dependent analytical model and an optimal design method. Specifically, an analytical model is derived to capture the characteristic two-stage dVCE/dt of FS IGBTs during the near zero-current turn-off (near-ZCT) transient. Based on this model, a parameter design methodology for LLC RCs is developed, incorporating constraints on junction temperature and turn-off current. Unlike conventional approaches, the proposed method optimizes dead-time selection to ensure ZVS and DCM operation across the full operating temperature range. The approach is validated on a 150-kW prototype, achieving a peak efficiency of 98.97% at full load.

title: Multimodal Population Graph Networks with Self-Attention Mechanisms for Autism Spectrum Disorder Identification
authors: Yaning Liu, Weiyang Chen, Yi Pan
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) identification is challenged by data heterogeneity across sites, unclear population-level inter-subject relationship modeling, and a lack of biologically meaningful brain-region analysis. To address these issues, this paper proposes GPGATNet, a cross-site ASD identification framework that introduces learnable mechanisms at three levels—individual representation alignment, population graph construction, and stable propagation learning—to achieve robust classification while enabling biomarker discovery. A three-layer self-attention unsupervised graph pooling module, SAGNet, is designed to adaptively extract brain-feature subgraphs via node-importance scoring and hierarchical Top-k selection. This strategy alleviates cross-site scale discrepancies and simultaneously provides interpretable estimates of regional contributions. In addition, a population-graph modeling scheme based on a “phenotypic skeleton + functional-connectivity edge-weight updating” paradigm is employed to enhance the robustness of inter-subject relationship modeling. Furthermore, a graph convolutional network, MH-GCAT, is constructed by integrating multi-branch convolutions with multi-head attention fusion to strengthen population-level discriminative capability. Experiments on the ABIDE dataset demonstrate that GPGATNet outperforms existing methods. Moreover, by combining attention-based localization with analyses of BOLD signal fluctuations, we observe functional abnormalities and activity instability in key regions such as the thalamus and hippocampus in ASD, providing neuroimaging evidence for understanding impairments in emotion regulation and social behavior.

title: Radargrammetric 3D Reconstruction of Multiple Moving Ship Targets via Multi-angle Spaceborne SAR Imagery
authors: Bin Liu, Yicheng Jiang, Zitao Liu, Jin Wei, Yong Wang, Yun Zhang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: The increasing complexity of the marine environments imposes higher demands on the surveillance of maritime ships. Radargrammetry can extract the height information of targets from the amplitude of multi-angle synthetic aperture radar (SAR) imagery, which provides a more specific and accurate description of the ship targets to efficiently enhance maritime surveillance capabilities. However, existing radargrammetric methods are limited to stationary targets, which cannot meet the demands for 3D reconstruction of multiple moving ships in maritime surveillance. To address this problem, a radargrammetric three-dimensional (3D) reconstruction principle and a detailed reconstruction procedure for multiple moving ships via multi-angle spaceborne SAR imagery are proposed in this paper. First, a radargrammetric 3D reconstruction principle for moving targets based on the multi-angle moving-target projection (MAMTP) model is proposed, and its theoretical height reconstruction accuracy is derived. Based on this principle, a multi-angle spaceborne SAR image registration algorithm for moving targets is proposed. Then, a whole radargrammetric 3D reconstruction procedure for multiple moving targets via multi-angle spaceborne SAR imagery based on the proposed principle is developed. This procedure includes the following steps: (i) division and refocusing of received signals from multiple moving targets; (ii) estimation of targets' velocities; (iii) multi-angle spaceborne SAR image registration; (iv) radargrammetric 3D reconstruction. This procedure can achieve accurate 3D reconstruction of multiple moving ships. Simulation results including both scatterer targets and electromagnetic targets verify the correctness of the proposed radargrammetric 3D reconstruction principle and the effectiveness of the proposed reconstruction procedure.

title: IEEECONF 2026 Author Index
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications

title: Real-time Balancing Control of Reconfigurable Battery Packs using Reinforcement Learning
authors: Ali Irshayyid, Wanqun Yang, Jun Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: This paper proposes a reinforcement learning (RL)-based control framework for real-time state-of-charge (SOC) balancing in reconfigurable lithium-ion battery packs. Traditional battery management strategies, such as passive and active balancing, suffer from energy inefficiency and limited adaptability. Reconfigurable battery systems offer enhanced flexibility through dynamic switchable topologies. However, to fully exploit the potential of the battery pack, an intelligent control policy to determine the optimal battery configuration in real-time is needed, which is a difficult task due to the large optimization space. To address this challenge, the reconfiguration problem is formulated as a Markov Decision Process, and a policy optimization framework is proposed to guide switch decisions based on observed cell states, such as the SOC of the individual cells in addition to the current pack configuration. The proposed RL agent operates on discrete action space and is guided by a multi-objective reward function tailored for battery management application. To avoid the high cost associated with physical battery training, we construct a linear surrogate model for efficient simulation-based learning. The proposed RL controller is evaluated against rule-based and greedy-based baselines, achieving superior SOC balancing performance with reduced switching frequency and improved runtime. In addition, comparison to true optimal obtained by dynamic programming demonstrates that the proposed RL controller achieves competitive results with significantly lower computational cost.

title: TemporalFED: A Software Module for Detecting Cyberattacks in Industry 4.0 Time-Series Data Using Decentralized Federated Learning
authors: Ángel Luis Perales Gómez, Enrique Tomás Martínez Beltrán, Pedro Miguel Sánchez Sánchez, Alberto Huertas Celdrán
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Industry 4.0 has brought numerous advantages, such as increasing productivity through automation. However, it also presents major cybersecurity issues, such as cyberattacks affecting industrial processes. Federated Learning (FL) combined with time-series analysis is a promising cyberattack detection mechanism proposed in the literature. However, having a single point of failure and network bottleneck are critical challenges that need to be tackled. Thus, this article explores the benefits of the Decentralized Federated Learning (DFL) in terms of cyberattack detection and resource consumption. The work presents TemporalFED, a software module for detecting cyberattacks in industrial environments using FL paradigms and time series. TemporalFED incorporates three components: time series conversion, feature engineering, and stationary conversion. To evaluate TemporalFED, itwas deployed on Fedstellar, a DFL framework. Then, a pool of experiments measured the detection performance and resource consumption in a chemical gas industrial environment with different time-series configurations, FL paradigms, and topologies. The results showcase the superiority of the configuration utilizing DFL and Semi-Decentralized Federated Learning (SDFL) paradigms, along with a fully connected topology, which achieved the best performance in anomaly detection. Regarding resource consumption, the configuration without feature engineering employed less bandwidth, less usage of the Central Processing Unit (CPU), and less usage of the Random Access Memory (RAM) than other configurations.

title: Hybrid Hierarchical Federated Learning over 5G/NextG Wireless Networking
authors: Haiyun Liu, Jiahao Xue, Jie Xu, Yao Liu, Zhuo Lu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: Today's 5G and NextG wireless networks are moving toward using the coordinated multi-point (CoMP) transmission and reception technique, where a client can be simultaneously served by multiple base stations (BSs) for better communication performance. However, traditional hierarchical federated learning (HFL) architectures impose the constraint that each client can be associated with only one edge server (ES) at a time. If we keep using the traditional HFL architectures in modern hierarchical networks for model training, the benefits of the CoMP technique would remain unexploited and leave room for further improvements in training efficiency. To address this issue, we propose hybrid hierarchical federated learning (HHFL), which allows clients in overlapping regions to simultaneously communicate with multiple edge servers (ESs) for model aggregation. HHFL is able to enhance inter-ES knowledge sharing, thereby mitigating model divergence and improving training efficiency. We provide a rigorous theoretical convergence analysis with a convergence upper bound to validate its effectiveness. Experimental results show that HHFL outperforms traditional HFL, particularly when the data across different ESs is not independent and identically distributed (non-IID). For example, when each ES is dominated by only two of the ten classes and 15 out of the 57 clients can connect to multiple ESs, HHFL achieves up to 2× faster convergence under the same configuration. These results demonstrate that HHFL provides a scalable and efficient solution for FL model training in today's and NextG wireless networks.

title: Masthead
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE MultiMedia

title: A Practical On-state Resistance Estimation Scheme of SiC MOSFETs in High-frequency Resonant Converters
authors: Quanjie Wang, Yingzhou Peng, Wei Wang, Xiaoliang Yu, Yi Zhang, Zhikang Shuai
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Industrial Electronics
abstract: The on-state resistance (Rdson) of silicon carbide (SiC) MOSFETs serves as a critical indicator of device degradation and performance. Traditional estimation methods based on Ohm's law face challenges in high-frequency applications due to phase shifts caused by parasitic inductance and sampling delays. To address this problem, this paper proposes a power-loss-based Rdson estimation method for SiC MOSFETs in high-frequency LLC resonant converters. It is immune to the phase shift issue and can obtain the Rdson in real-time. Validated across four typical control modes in an LLC converter, the proposed approach demonstrates <5.0% estimation error, significantly outperforming conventional solutions.

title: New Constructions of Binary Cyclic Codes with Both Relatively Large Minimum Distance and Dual Distance
authors: Lingqi Zheng, Weijun Fang, Rongxing Qiu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Theory
abstract: Binary cyclic codes are worth studying due to their applications and theoretical importance. It is an important problem to construct an infinite family of cyclic codes with large minimum distance d and dual distance d⊥. In recent years, much research has been devoted to improving the lower bound on d, some of which have exceeded the square-root bound. The constructions presented recently seem to indicate that when the minimum distance increases, the minimum distance of its dual code decreases. In this paper, we focus on the new constructions of binary cyclic codes with length n = 2m − 1, dimension near n/2 and both relatively large minimum distance and dual distance. When m is even, we construct a family of binary cyclic codes with parameters [2m − 1, 2m−1 ± 1, d], where d ≥ 2m/2 − 1 and d⊥ ≥ 2m/2. Both the minimum distance and the dual distance are significantly better than the previous results. When m is the product of two distinct primes, we construct some cyclic codes with dimensions k = (n+1)/2 and d > n/log2 n , where the lower bound on the minimum distance is much larger than the square-root bound. When m is odd, we present two families of binary [2m−1, 2m−1, d] cyclic codes with d ≥ 2(m+1)/2−1, d⊥ ≥ 2(m+1)/2 and d ≥ 2(m+3)/2 − 15, d⊥ ≥ 2(m−1)/2 respectively, which leads that d · d⊥ can reach 2n asymptotically. To the best of our knowledge, except for the punctured binary Reed-Muller codes, there is no other construction of binary cyclic codes that reaches this bound.

title: Efficient Inversion of Multiple Magnetic Dipole Models Based on Deep Learning Prior Analysis and Penalty Function Optimization
authors: Yiding Wang, Shengxin Lin, Donghua Pan, Chongyu Jin, Yitao Chen, Yuxiao Zhang, Liyi Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: The inversion of multiple magnetic dipoles plays a critical role in applications such as geological exploration and spacecraft magnetic modeling. The primary challenges include an unknown number of magnetic sources, high sensitivity to noise, and low inversion accuracy. In this paper, we propose a novel three-dimensional inversion method for multiple magnetic dipole models that integrates deep-learning-based prior analysis with a penalty function optimization strategy. A lightweight convolutional attention network, termed MGTI-net, is developed to extract prior information from the invariants of the magnetic gradient tensor, thereby enabling accurate identification of the dipole count and providing preliminary estimates of their positions. By incorporating a penalty function into an improved least squares estimation framework, the proposed method robustly inverts the spatial positions and magnetic moments of the dipoles. Simulation results demonstrate that MGTI-net can reliably determine the number of dipoles in a three-dimensional space and maintain a classification accuracy above 75% even under 50% relative random noise interference. Moreover, the improved least squares estimation can constrain the position and magnetic moment errors within 0.01 m and 0.1 A·m², respectively, under 15% relative noise, reducing the error by more than one order of magnitude compared with conventional least squares methods. Experimental findings corroborate the simulation analysis, confirming that the new approach can accurately recover magnetic source parameters.

title: GAT-Based Energy-Efficient UAV Trajectory Planning for Logistics Service in 6G Edge Networks
authors: Defeng Sheng, Peng Yu, Can Tan, Chaochao Li, Wenjing Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV)-assisted logistics is a key application scenario in 6G edge networks. However, the stringent low-latency requirements of logistics services impose significant computational pressure on edge. Consequently, the inherent resource limitations of edge servers restrict real-time trajectory optimization, which directly exacerbates UAV energy consumption and operational instability, particularly in large-scale service areas. To address these challenges, this paper proposed a UAV-assisted logistics service in the 6G edge network with the goal of achieving high energy efficiency and rapid response. Specifically, we propose a deep graph matching method based on a graph attention network(GAT) for UAV trajectory planning, which is applicable to dynamic and large-scale scenarios and demonstrates superior energy efficiency and rapid execution speed. Compared to baseline algorithms, the proposed method reduces energy consumption by at least 20%, while maintaining a high service completion rate and system stability.

title: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
authors: N/A
publish: N/A

title: A Mixed Platoon Guided Control Model for Efficient and Safer Merging in Mixed Traffic With Low Penetration of Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Zhihao Zhang, Minh Kieu, Yunlong Wang, Prakash Ranjitkar, Hai L. Vu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Traditional ramp merging relies on drivers' observation and cooperation, which creates efficiency bottlenecks and accident hotspots. Existing mixed-traffic strategies offer improvements, many remain limited by requirements for high AV penetration and strong driver cooperation. This paper introduces the Mixed Platoon Guided Control (MPGC) model, a hierarchical AV-guided merging framework that combines protocol, prediction, and optimisation within a rolling MPC structure. MPGC applies a platoon-interleaving mechanism where AV leaders manage most merging actions, allowing human-driven followers to simply follow without complex decision-making. The framework integrates a dual-rule protocol for dynamic traffic, a data-driven prediction layer accounting for human driving heterogeneity, and a trajectory optimisation layer based on merging time window search. SUMO simulations demonstrate that at 10% AV penetration, MPGC increases throughput by up to 33% and 12% compared to FIFO and RM, respectively. For safety, MPGC achieves an 86% reduction in risky time-to-collision (TTC) events compared to FIFO, reaching a safety level comparable to RM. The system also remains robust under human driving heterogeneity, incomplete platoon formation, and V2X failures, showing good scalability in low-penetration scenarios.

title: MADQI: A Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning with Human Intervention in an Uncontrolled Intersection
authors: Guoxi Chen, Shan Liu, Ya Zhang, Xinde Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: An uncontrolled intersection, which lacks traffic signals or signs, is a key scenario in autonomous driving, where multi-agent deep reinforcement learning (MARL) offers end-to end control without relying on preset rules or precise models. Unfortunately, within the centralized training with decentralized execution (CTDE) framework, classical MARL algorithms are limited in their ability to represent non-monotonic cooperative strategies, leading to poor performance in traffic scenarios. This paper studies the coordinated control of multiple vehicles at an uncontrolled intersection. First, the limitations of the QMIX algorithm when applied to an uncontrolled intersection are discussed. Second, the conditions of the joint action-value matrix that cause QMIX to fail are identified to highlight the shortcomings of existing classical algorithms at an uncontrolled intersection. Then, a novel lane-based observation is proposed, utilizing prior knowledge of traffic scenarios to satisfy permutation invariance. Next, inspired by the role of traffic police at an intersection, a novel MARL algorithm is proposed based on human intervention. The proposed algorithm allows agents to learn from demonstrated actions while avoiding taking dangerous actions. The theoretical analysis shows that the proposed algorithm significantly expands the original expression space by integrating the human-provided demonstrative cooperative actions into the solution space. The effectiveness of the proposed algorithm is validated in an open simulation environment. The proposed algorithm improves by 6.88% over the MARL baseline and by 5.61% over the human-guided baseline. By tracking how the model's decisions adjust in response to varying observations, it is confirmed that the agents can learn to cooperate and optimize traffic efficiency while ensuring safety. The code is available at https://github.com/SEUCGX/MADQI.

title: University in the Age of AI: Vectors of Transformation
authors: Evgeny V. Kunts
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: The article examines the outcomes of employing generative artificial intelligence in university education. The ongoing technological revolution around AI adoption has triggered a crisis in higher education, caused by the declining relevance of key elements of the traditional learning process. At the same time, the rapid development of technology and growing global instability are increasing public demand for high-quality higher education. Based on the study of eighty publications, the article examines the reasons for and the content of educational transformation brought about by the spread of generative AI. It also explores promising teaching practices that involve AI in the learning process. The author concludes that generative AI technology opens up new opportunities to enrich both educational theory and practice in higher education. The article contributes to the discussion on how AI affects higher education, on developing a comprehensive set of measures that universities can take in response to the spread of this technology, and on ways to use AI effectively in the teaching process.

title: Machine Learning-Based Analysis of Multi-Source Network Packet Captures
authors: Kirubavathi G, Karthika Biju M, M A Niranjani Nair, Sreedhanu Surendran, Sanjana S
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: In this research paper, we introduce an unsupervised network traffic labeling method based on K-means clustering to distinguish between benign and malicious flows. The CTU-55 malware dataset consists of 55 malicious traffic samples and 9 benign samples. Following preprocessing and feature extraction, KMeans clustering is utilized to generate unsupervised labels with integrated outlier detection. Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) with correlation matrices to understand feature relationships and inform feature selection. Subsequently, supervised machine learning classifiers, including Random Forest, XGBoost, KNN, and Naive Bayes, are trained on the labeled dataset to evaluate malware detection performance. Experiments demonstrate that cost-sensitive classifiers such as Random Forest trained with Gini impurity achieve the highest classification accuracy; XGBoost trained with binary cross-entropy loss, Naive Bayes optimized for negative log-likelihood, and KNN minimizes distance in feature space, whereas cross-model error agreement analysis reveals common and complementary misclassification trends are crucial for robust network traffic malware detection.

title: Breaking and Securing Vision-Language Models: An Adversarial Robustness Study
authors: Md Zarif Hossain, Abdur R. Shahid, Ahmed Imteaj
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have achieved remarkable performance in various downstream tasks, such as image captioning and visual question answering (VQA), by learning joint representations from paired image-text data. However, the robustness of VLMs against adversarial attacks is a critical concern as these models become widely adopted in real-world applications. This study provides a comprehensive overview of the current state of research on the adversarial robustness of VLMs. We introduce a novel taxonomy that categorizes the diverse landscape of adversarial attacks on VLMs, considering factors such as the modality of the attack, the level of model access, and the attack objective. We explore various types of attacks, including jailbreak, backdoor, data poisoning, gradient-based, and patch-based attacks, and discuss their underlying principles and assumptions. Furthermore, we present a comparative analysis of existing defense strategies and their strengths and limitations. By identifying open challenges and future research directions, we aim to stimulate further exploration of this crucial area and contribute to the development of robust and trustworthy VLMs for real-world applications.

title: MeshCentral-FogHealth: A Hybrid Supervision Architecture for Distributed Telehealth Systems
authors: Mamadou Ba, Massamba Seck, Alie Ndiaye, Ibrahima Ngom, Samuel Ouya
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Faced with Senegal’s epidemiological transition, where non-communicable diseases account for 45% of deaths and hypertension affects 24.5% of the adult population, this research proposes an innovative centralized supervision architecture for distributed telehealth systems. The integration of MeshCentral into the FogHealth ecosystem addresses the operational challenges of national-scale deployment, particularly the coordination of a distributed network of connected care points in a context of variable connectivity. Our contribution lies in the design of a hybrid solution combining distributed fog computing and centralized supervision, enabling remote maintenance, proactive monitoring, and secure management of remote access. The results demonstrate operational stability with 271,993 continuous readings, validating the effectiveness of this approach in reducing hospital burden while maintaining an optimal level of medical surveillance. This architecture paves the way for a hybrid care model adapted to the constraints of developing countries, aligned with the vision of Senegal Strategy 2050.

title: IoT based Automated Irrigation System for Aquaponics and Land Farming
authors: M. Kavitha, C. H. Hussaian Basha, B. Janani, B.S. Gopika, S. Sivamani, S. Senthilkumar
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: To monitor essential environmental parameters in real time, an IoT based smart irrigation system is proposed. pH and TDS sensors in aquaponics systems ensure that the water quality maintains itself at ideal levels for fish and plants, encouraging effective nutrient absorption and water recirculation. In land farming, soil moisture and NPK sensors provide precise data to enable precision irrigation and fertilization. Based on threshold-based decision-making and predictive analytics, the system dynamically triggers water pumps and actuators to regulate irrigation cycles. Cloud integration stores and analyzes historical data, enhancing system reliability and optimizing performance through data-driven insights. Real-time remote monitoring enables farmers to control and manage the system from anywhere, ensuring timely intervention and improved operational efficiency. The system’s performance is enhanced by predictive analytics models that forecast soil moisture and plant growth, enabling adaptive irrigation schedules. Fault detection mechanisms ensure system reliability by preventing sensor drift, communication failures, and power disruptions. By addressing challenges such as sensor calibration, power consumption, and system redundancy, this IOT-based irrigation system ensures consistent and accurate operation. It promotes sustainable agriculture by reducing water waste, lowering energy consumption, and improving overall crop health. The integration of IOT technologies in Aquaponics and land farming offers a scalable, efficient, and environmentally friendly solution for modern agricultural practices.

title: Analysis and Control of Multi-Input Zeta Converter fed from Renewable Sources for Grid-Tied Inverters
authors: Sayantan Chowdhury, Sujoy Bhowmik, Arkendu Mitra, Amritesh Kumar, Chayan Bhattacharjee
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)
abstract: Now-a-days, the world is shifting towards the use of emission-free power sources. The installation of Photovoltaics (PVs) has become a necessity in order to utilize green power as well as to mitigate the increasing power demand at cheaper costs. However, the power available from PVs is primarily influenced by weather conditions, results in a major challenge in ensuring reliable power delivery.To overcome this issue, this work proposes a multi-input DC-DC converter that uses PV as the primary source of power and a battery for back-up purposes. The back-up power is activated when the power available from PVs fails to meet the desired power demand. The proposed topology is based on DC-DC Zeta converter comprising of an additional power source. The work focuses on the analysis of the different modes of operation of the proposed Multi-Input Zeta Converter (MIZC). A control strategy emphasizes harnessing power from both sources at the same time to meet a maximum load power demand of 2.5 kW at 400 V accordingly. Various results obtained from the simulations are depicted to validate the proposed work.

title: Southwest Power Pool Adoption of IEEE Std 2800-2022 Reactive/Active Power and Consecutive Voltage Ride-Through Standards
authors: Mostafa Sedighizadeh, Steve Purdy, Nick Parker, Sunny Raheem
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: This paper summarizes Southwest Power Pool (SPP)’s 2025 efforts to implement IEEE Std 2800-2022 requirements, specifically Clause 5 (Reactive Power–Voltage Control), Clause 6 (Active Power–Frequency Response), and Subclause 7.2.2.4 (Consecutive Voltage Ride-Through). Stakeholder feedback emphasized the complexity of implementation and associated concerns, leading to a decision to prioritize near-term voltage and frequency control capabilities. These initiatives enhance grid reliability and establish consistent performance expectations amid increasing inverter-based resource penetration. To support compliance with these new requirements, a PSCAD simulation was performed for an IBR plant proposed for connection to the SPP grid. The simulation results demonstrate the capability of this new SPP interconnection customer to meet the SPP-adopted IEEE Std 2800 requirements.

title: Monte Carlo-Type Neural Operator for One-Dimensional Differential Equations
authors: Salah Eddine Choutri, Prajwal Chauhan, Othmane Mazhar, Saif Eddin Jabari
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: The Monte Carlo-type Neural Operator (MCNO) introduces a framework for learning solution operators of one-dimensional partial differential equations (PDEs) by directly learning the kernel function and approximating the associated integral operator using a Monte Carlo-type approach. Unlike Fourier Neural Operators (FNOs), which rely on spectral representations and assume translation-invariant kernels, MCNO makes no such assumptions. The kernel is represented as a learnable tensor acting on the latent features at sampled points, with sampling performed once uniformly at random from the discretized grid. This design enables generalization across multiple grid resolutions without relying on fixed global basis functions or repeated sampling during training, while an interpolation step maps between arbitrary input and output grids to further enhance flexibility. Experiments on standard 1D PDE benchmarks show that MCNO achieves competitive accuracy with efficient computational cost. We also provide a theoretical analysis proving that the Monte Carlo estimator yields a bounded bias and variance under mild regularity assumptions. This result holds in any spatial dimension, suggesting that MCNO may extend naturally beyond one-dimensional problems. More broadly, this work explores how Monte Carlo-type integration can be incorporated into neural operator frameworks for continuous-domain PDEs, providing a theoretically supported alternative to spectral methods (such as FNO) and to graph-based Monte Carlo approaches (such as the Graph Kernel Neural Operator, GNO).

title: AgriCloud Framework: Improving Crop Production by Integrating Cloud and Machine Learning
authors: K Vijayalakshmi, G. Ramesh, Saroo Raj R. B, Rajeswari Rajesh Immanuel, P.Nirmala
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Agri Cloud is a cloud-based system that combines cloud computing, machine learning algorithms, and Internet of Things (IoT) sensors to improve agricultural management. The technology gathers data in real time from a variety of agricultural sensors, analyzes it in the cloud, and gives farmers insights they can use. Agri Cloud seeks to maximize resource use, boost agricultural yields, and promote sustainable farming methods by utilizing scalable cloud infrastructure. For contemporary agriculture, Agri Cloud is a dependable, accurate, and expandable solution. By giving farmers access to real-time insights, it improves crop management, lowers waste, and boosts yields. For more thorough decision-making, this basis can be extended to incorporate drone integration, satellite imaging, and sophisticated analytics. integrating cloud computing, machine learning, and the Internet of Things into a single framework for agriculture; facilitating real-time decision support by giving farmers data-driven, timely advice to maximize crop management; facilitating scalability by utilizing cloud infrastructure to manage massive data sets and assist with extensive agricultural operations; and emphasizing user-centric design to make it accessible to farmers with different levels of technological expertise. The simulated data is used to train a Decision Tree Classifier. Data is divided into test and training sets. Assesses model accuracy in this instance, 98%, which shows that crop conditions were well predicted.

title: Machine Learning Based Approaches in the Education Sector to Impart the Quality Among Students
authors: Rajalaxmi Hegde, Sandeep Kumar Hegde, Thangavel Murugan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The concept of Machine learning (ML) algorithms can be significantly applied in the area of academic research, which has the capability to impact teaching and learning processes, and research by offering a platform for individualized learning, responsive evaluation, and also providing information on student achievement, advancement, and learning practices. ML is transforming the way in which educational institutions work, communicate knowledge, and educate students. In the present era, young minds are being exposed to a wide range of global concepts. This would help the students to tune their mindset to the technologically advanced world of tomorrow. Due to the breakthrough in technology, machine learning has influenced the education sector by enabling facilities such as transcription, text-to-speech, and personalized learning. By applying ML, it is possible to determine whether a student is at risk of failing out or not graduating on time. These kinds of issues can be framed as binary classification problems, where the solution has two possible outcomes: the student either graduated on time or not. The proposed ML algorithms aim to find students who are at risk of not graduating in time by applying the concept of prediction from a set of training data. In this paper, the performance of supervised machine learning algorithms such as SVM, RBF, and Naïve Bayes algorithms is analysed by applying adaptive learning patterns to predict the students who are at risk by dynamically monitoring the progress of students. The proposed machine algorithms aim at identifying the learning gaps and also to create a personalized learning path that every student receives instruction tailored to their individual needs. The experimental investigation revealed that the SVM algorithm in combination with the RBF kernel attained a higher accuracy rate of 96 % compared to other ML models. It can also be analysed from the experiment that the proposed algorithm also improved the learnability of the students by 10 %.

title: Joint Optimization and Leakage-Proof Strategies for Imbalanced Data
authors: Guoqing Meng, Qingqing Cao, Liang Sheng, Yihua Wang, Yuling Jiang, Yumeng Li, Lijun Deng, Xianjun Yang, Lisheng Gao
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: In disease risk prediction using real-world health examination data, class imbalance and information leakage are critical challenges often exacerbated by traditional stepwise optimization that overlooks synergistic interactions among modeling stages. To address this, we propose a Bayesian-based joint optimization framework. This approach constructs a unified parameter search space to simultaneously optimize preprocessing parameters—such as feature selection size and SMOTE neighbors—alongside XGBoost hyperparameters, guided by a weighted composite metric within a rigorous leakage-proof pipeline. Applied to hyperuricemia risk prediction on 18,164 records, the optimized model achieved an ROC-AUC of 0.8512 and PR-AUC of 0.5950, significantly outperforming an SVM-RBF baseline (ROC-AUC: 0.8182). Notably, comparative analysis revealed that neglecting strict train-validation segregation could lead to a systematic performance overestimation of approximately 0.20. These results demonstrate the method’s robustness and reliability for clinical modeling applications.

title: Edge-Scale Coranking: Azure-Ready Dataset Ranking with DevOps Discipline
authors: Uday Kumar Gannu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This paper implements coranking and tensor-based multiranking over heterogeneous author–paper–journal graphs as a production-minded service, emphasizing reproducible pipelines, scalable computation, and operational rigor. Data ingestion, graph/tensor construction, and iterative solvers are packaged as containerized stages with CI/CD gates to ensure deterministic builds and safe promotion. Deployment targets a managed Kubernetes footprint on Azure for elastic execution of convergence-heavy jobs, while environment configuration is codified to keep preprocessing and ranking runs consistent across rings. Telemetry and release automation are integrated from the outset, enabling canary rollouts of ranking logic, fast rollback on drift, and continuous tracking of runtime efficiency and result stability. The result is a robust, cloud-oriented ranking system that couples network-theoretic models with DevOps practices—turning algorithm prototypes into reliable, maintainable, and scalable services suitable for Azure-backed research compute.

title: Operational Efficiency Assessment of Wind Power Generation Across Indian States: An SBM-DEA Approach
authors: Jyoti Luhaniwal, Shivi Agarwal, Trilok Mathur
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Earth's average temperature has increased by approximately 0.06° Celcius per decade since 1850, amounting to a total rise of about 1.1° C. Electricity generation is a major source of greenhouse gas emissions in India, which has increased the focus on low-emission renewable energy options. Wind power is the second-largest source of renewable electricity generation in the country. Therefore, this study employs a non-oriented slacksbased measure data envelopment analysis (SBM-DEA) model to evaluate the relative efficiency of wind power generation across nine Indian states for the years 2022 and 2023. Wind power density and installed capacity are used as inputs, while capacity factor and power generation are considered as outputs. The results reveal notable variations in efficiency across states and time, with Rajasthan, Kerala, Madhya Pradesh, and Telangana achieving full efficiency throughout the analysis period. A Tobit regression analysis further shows that site elevation positively affects efficiency, whereas wind curtailment significantly reduces performance. The findings offer practical insights for improving wind power plant operations and policy planning.

title: Quantum Resilient Cryptography for Secure Ultra Dense 6G Terahertz Networks
authors: S. Aghalya
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The advent of 6G terahertz (THz) communication networks offers unparalleled data rates and ultra-dense device connectivity but introduces tremendous vulnerabilities to quantum computing attacks. Conventional cryptographic techniques such as RSA and ECC are becoming increasingly vulnerable to quantum attacks and are thus a serious threat to network integrity and data confidentiality. The research here addresses the challenge of protecting 6G-THz networks from quantum adversaries by designing a quantum-resilient cryptographic protocol for ultra-dense network scenarios. The main objective is to offer resilient, scalable, and low-latency encryption and authentication to enable high-throughput THz communications. The system leverages lattice-based post-quantum cryptography combined with a light-weight key distribution protocol and hierarchical identity management to support massive device connectivity. The novelty lies in the adaptive blending of post-quantum primitives optimized to the bandwidth and latency limitations inherent in THz systems, combined with context-aware key negotiation to remove quantum threats in real time. The protocol significantly enhances future network resilience in quantum-enabled adversarial networks.

title: Research on Forest Fire Detection Algorithm Based on Improved YOLOv8
authors: Jian Huang, Tutu Sun, Rui Wang, Shanshan Chen
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of low efficiency of manual patrol, insufficient recognition accuracy of small targets, and poor adaptability to low-light environments in traditional forest fire detection, an improved YOLOv8 forest fire detection algorithm is proposed. A four-scale detection architecture is constructed by adding a 4× downsampling detection head to enhance the capture ability of tiny fire and smoke; the CBAM attention mechanism is fused with the improved HSV image enhancement algorithm to improve the feature extraction accuracy in low-light environments; meanwhile, a high-robustness training set covering multi-angle, multi-distance, multi-landform and low-light scenarios is constructed to further enhance the generalization ability of the model; the anchor box configuration is optimized to adapt to the characteristics of forest fire targets. Experimental results show that the mAP value of the improved algorithm reaches 92.7% on the public forest fire dataset and the field-collected robust dataset, which is 8.3 percentage points higher than the original YOLOv8. Among them, the detection accuracy of small targets is increased by 12.6%, and the detection accuracy in low-light environments is improved by 15.2%, which can meet the practical needs of early and rapid warning of forest fires.

title: Efficient Ensemble Machine Learning Framework for Early Prediction of Thyroid Cancer Using Clinical Data
authors: Sandeep Kumar Hegde, Rajalaxmi Hegde, Thangavel Murugan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Thyroid cancer is considered as one of the global health burden that occur due to the abnormal growth of cells in the thyroid gland. One of the remedies to improve survival rates and provide successful treatment is to diagnose the disease at an early stage. The traditional diagnostic approach depends on the clinical findings and imaging-based techniques. Hence, it is recommended to adopt a data-driven approach by incorporating Machine Learning (ML) for the early prediction of diseases. According to the literature findings, machine learning applied to predict thyroid cancer using clinical data has demonstrated high precision in predicting the disease and has also improved personalized treatments. The XGBoost is an ML model that can handle complex features in the dataset by analyzing the feature interaction, and LightGBM will work efficiently on an imbalanced dataset. In the proposed research work, an ensemble-based machine learning framework has been implemented by combining the Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) and Light Gradient Boosting (LightGBM). The main objective of the proposed work is to predict thyroid cancer with higher accuracy by applying an ensemble-based machine learning approach. The proposed work also aims to improve the model bias and generalization by utilizing the capabilities of two powerful ensemble algorithms. The experimental results illustrated that the proposed ensemble framework achieved greater accuracy, precision, recall, and AUC score compared to traditional ML algorithms. Hence, the proposed ensemble framework can be effectively used to predict thyroid cancer disease early.

title: Reduction in Motor and Generator Life due to Buildup of Residue in Water Heat-exchangers
authors: Henk De Swardt
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: The reliability and efficiency of large electric machines are vital to sustainable energy systems, particularly in wind and hydroelectric power generation. While water cooling offers superior thermal performance over air cooling, the buildup of mineral residue in heat exchangers and water jackets poses a critical risk to machine longevity. This paper investigates the thermodynamic impact of fouling on generator performance using thermal circuit modeling and Arrhenius life-cycle analysis. Results show that even a 0.5 mm layer of residue can elevate internal temperatures enough to reduce insulation life by up to 30%. These findings reveal that conventional maintenance practices overlook hidden degradation mechanisms. The study provides a quantitative foundation for adopting more rigorous, cleaning protocols to preserve the operational lifespan of renewable energy generation assets.

title: Detecting Fake Job Advertisements Using CNN, RNN, DNN, and Multilingual Transformers
authors: K. Sireesha, Dharani Dasari, Reshma Shaik, Pavani Marripalli, Bondala Ramakrishna, N. Srihitha Gunapriya
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Fraudulent job advertisements are increasingly reported on online recruitment platforms, posing both financial and emotional risks to applicants. This study employs the publicly available Fake Job Postings dataset, which contains around 18,000 labeled records, to develop a hybrid detection framework. The preprocessing pipeline addressed missing values, cleaned textual content, and balanced classes using SMOTE. Text was represented using TF–IDF features alongside tokenisation. We used multiple neural architectures, including fully connected deep networks, convolution-based models (CNN), and recurrent models (RNN). In addition, a multilingual transformer, XLM-RoBERTa, is used to evaluate its capacity for cross-lingual generalisation. Experimental findings reveal that DNN and CNN architectures achieved the highest accuracy and F1-scores, while XLM-RoBERTa showed notable cross-lingual adaptability. The proposed framework can be integrated into recruitment systems to strengthen fraud detection, improve reliability, and build greater trust among platform users.

title: Event-Driven IoT Node: A Low-Power Asynchronous Microcontroller Architecture
authors: Amrutha, Kolluru Taraka Rama Shriya, Rajesh Kannan Megalingam
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The Event-Driven IoT(Internet of Things) Node presented in this project was created for ultra-low power sensing applications which uses asynchronous microcontroller concept. In general traditional clock-driven microcontrollers run continuously which results in wastage of energy so, to over come this drawback our proposed system operates on an event-driven concept, where the node stays in sleep state and only activates when triggered by major sensor events. Software based implementation is done using python for our system. In the proposed system Temperature, Humidity, and soil moisture values are monitored by the node, which only transmits data under unusual or threshold-crossing circumstances, this results in significant energy saving due to this selective activation which cuts downs significantly cuts down on unnecessary processing and transmission. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach effectively extends system longevity and power efficiency, offering a scalable and sustainable solution for IoT based smart agriculture and other remoting applications.

title: The Pandemic Paradigm Shift: Analyzing Pre-and Post-COVID Effects on Life Insurance
authors: Amrin Samar Sultana, Aishwarya V. Kamble, Rekha R Nair
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The COVID-19 pandemic is the core research issue of the present study, and the major shifts occurring in the life insurance industry are explored before and after March 2020. It examines changes in new consumer perspectives, underwriting and economic positions, products and service portfolios, and insurers' financial status. Before the pandemic, the industry growing with precise risk assessment parameters in place. The sudden outburst early this year led to several impacts such as; a higher mortality rate, economic fluctuations in investments, and the advancement of digitalization throughout the insurance value chain. Specific mortal risks were more visible to the consumers due to the pandemic if this is a growth driver for the life insurance segment. Thus, it also raised price-sensitive levels as a result of economic volatility. This has led to the change of underwriting and pricing models, and the repositioning of products to address issues of long-term morbidity and mortality, in future pandemics. Information major of the work also outlines a growing trend of products that blur the lines between life and health insurance; instead of calling them life or health insurance products, several companies are labeling their products as life and wellness or health. Drawing data from India Stats, this research applies regression analysis to analyze demographic factors affecting insurance in the backdrop of the COVID-19 pandemic. In light of this, the study establishes that insurance markets for the employed population are largely in urban centers while in rural areas expansion is mostly by government initiative. Thus, this research identifies the following prospects as the key to the industry's evolution: the creation of new, customer-centered solutions, increasing financial awareness, and the integration of innovative technologies and behavioral changes.

title: Task-Aware Facial Micro-Dynamics Modeling for Dementia Detection using Face-Mesh Motion Signatures
authors: Hemantha Kumar H S, Tanuja R, S H Manjula
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Finding dementia early and correctly is still hard because cognitive decline is so subtle and different in different people. Recently, facial motion analysis during tasks that require a lot of thinking has been shown to be a promising non-invasive sign of dementia-related impairment. This paper suggests a new way to use facial mesh dynamics to find dementia called task-aware facial motion degradation modeling framework (TA-FMDM). The suggested method uses a Facial Motion Degradation Index to track how facial motion changes in different regions and combines task-aware attention with cross-task consistency learning to make it more robust across a range of cognitive tasks. We did a lot of tests on the PROMPT dataset using deep learning models like 1D convolutional neural networks, long short-term memory networks, and Transformers. A systematic evaluation of the effects of several data augmentation strategies showed that shifting-based augmentation gives the most consistent performance improvements. The suggested TA-FMDM framework did better than strong baseline models on all evaluation metrics, with an accuracy of 80.3%, a recall of 70.4%, and an area under the ROC curve of 0.821. Ablation and task-wise analyses showed that facial motion degradation modeling and task-aware learning worked well. These results show that task-aware facial motion analysis could be a useful and understandable way to screen for dementia. It could also be a non-invasive and scalable way to assess cognitive function early on.

title: BoP-X: A New XAI Approach to Explain Reinforcement Learning for Autonomous Driving
authors: Gulsum Alicioglu, Bo Sun
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) is commonly used in artificial intelligence (AI) applications. Explaining how a DRL agent learns and makes decisions is crucial in gaining the trust of end users. In this paper, we introduce a new explanation technique, Bag-of-Pattern for eXplanations (BoP-X), supporting lightweight policy summary. The proposed approach adopts a multivariate bag-of-pattern method to extract and summarize the learned behaviors and strategies of the DRL.
