title: A Thermoacoustic Transducer for Precise Sound Field Focusing and Its Model
authors: Siwei Sun, Ye Zheng, Debo Wang
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: To achieve the precise focusing and directional control capabilities of the sound field, a parabolic structure thermoacoustic (TA) transducer is designed and fabricated in this work. Theoretical models for electro-thermo-acoustic transducer and sound field focusing are established and validated through finite element analysis, which derive the relationship of sound pressure level (SPL) with driving voltage, operating frequency, and spatial distance, and accurately predict the acoustic field distribution and the frequency-dependent focal shift phenomenon. The microstructure of the material is characterized by SEM, XRD, and Raman spectrum. Acoustic characteristics are tested for the parabolic TA transducer, with results showing that its SPL is enhanced by 5.5 dB in the focal region compared with the planar structure. The transducer effectively concentrates sound wave energy along the main axis, achieving high directionality (with a -3 dB main-lobe beamwidth of < 30°), which is attributed to the leveraging of geometric gain and constructive interference. Within the full frequency range of 0.2 kHz to 20 kHz, the transducer shows excellent operational stability (with a maximum error of only 0.7%) and achieves the high SPL of 74.7 dB at a driving voltage of 4.5 V and an operating frequency of 20 kHz, indicating that the transducer exhibits excellent acoustic characteristics within a wide frequency band. Furthermore, the measurement results were compared with the theoretical values and found to be basically consistent. The MXene/GO TA transducer fabricated in this work has greatly improved acoustic performance through geometric structure design and is capable of sound field control, which provides a solution for fields that require precise sound field manipulation, such as directional communication and spatial audio applications.

title: Smart Vehicle Ignition System with Multi-Factor Authentication using Raspberry Pi Pico
authors: Madhusudhana M, Mohammed Sameer, Kiran R, Ammar Shibli, Shivapanchakshari T G
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This paper presents a vehicle ignition control system that combines fingerprint and RFID-based authentication, implemented on the Raspberry Pi Pico platform. The purpose of this system is to enhance vehicle security by allowing the engine to start only when the identity of an authorized individual is verified through either fingerprint or RFID scanning. The system connects a biometric sensor and an RFID reader to the Pico microcontroller, which compares the captured data with the stored records to authenticate the user. Upon successful verification, the microcontroller activates a relay to start the engine. In the event of a failed verification, the system promptly triggers an alert to indicate an unauthorized access attempt. This prototype offers a cost-effective, hands-free solution for vehicle access, ensuring increased security and preventing unauthorized usage.

title: SAM2LoRA: Composite Loss-Guided, Parameter-Efficient Finetuning of SAM2 for Retinal Fundus Segmentation
authors: Sayan Mandal, Divyadarshini Karthikeyan, Manas Paldhe
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: We propose SAM2LoRA, a parameter-efficient fine-tuning strategy that adapts the Segment Anything Model 2 (SAM2) for fundus image segmentation. SAM2 employs a masked autoencoder-pretrained Hierarchical Vision Transformer for multi-scale feature decoding, enabling rapid inference in low-resource settings; however, fine-tuning remains challenging. To address this, SAM2LoRA integrates a low-rank adapter into both the image encoder and mask decoder, requiring fewer than 5% of the original trainable parameters. Our analysis indicates that for cross-dataset fundus segmentation tasks, a composite loss function combining segmentationBCE, SoftDice, and FocalTversky losses is essential for optimal network tuning. Evaluated on 11 challenging fundus segmentation datasets, SAM2LoRA demonstrates high performance in both blood vessel and optic disc segmentation under cross-dataset training conditions. It achieves Dice scores of up to 0.86 and 0.93 for blood vessel and optic disc segmentation, respectively, and AUC values of up to 0.98 and 0.99, achieving state-of-the-art performance while substantially reducing training overhead. <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup> <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>

title: Genomics Big Data Analytics for Disease Prediction Using Transformer Based Deep Learning with Explainable Insights on the TCGA Dataset
authors: Poovarasan P, Prabakaran K, Radhakrishnan K R
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Predicting disease outcomes from genomic data is challenging because gene expression profiles have a lot of dimensions, and the biological interactions involved in disease mechanisms are complex. In lung cancer, these issues are even greater due to differences at the molecular level and variability among patients. Traditional bioinformatics and machine learning methods often find it hard to capture long-range relationships among genes and usually lack interpretability. To address these issues, this paper introduces a Transformer-based deep learning framework along with Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) networks for predicting lung cancer using data from The Cancer Genome Atlas (TCGA). The Transformer uses selfattention to identify global interactions between genes, while the BiLSTM layer captures the contextual relationships within gene expression sequences. To improve transparency and build clinical trust, we include Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) techniques, such as attention visualization and SHAP-based feature attribution, in the framework. Experimental results show that our approach outperforms traditional bioinformatics methods like Elastic Net and XGBoost when tested on the same TCGA lung cancer group.

title: Forecasting Electric Vehicle Charging Demand in Urban Areas using Time-Series ML Models
authors: Shubham Umate, Ganesh Wakte, Kushal Masarkar, Akanksha. Sontakke, Ritesh Banpurkar
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The number of electric vehicles (EVs) on city roads increases, it is necessary to predict the charging demand accurately with urgency. Precise forecasting helps in planning the charging infrastructure, avoiding power deficits, and ensuring smooth traffic and energy flow. Charging demand for electric vehicles in urban areas is forecasted in this research using several time-varying machine learning approaches. In this study, we employed real data with historical charge history, weather, and traffic information of three major cities. We deployed and compared four of the most used prediction models namely Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), Auto Regressive Integrated Moving Average (ARIMA), Random Forest, and Support Vector Regression (SVR). We used each of the models to see how well it could predict demand for the future. Out of all the models, LSTM gave the best results with the least error rate (MAPE of 8.2%), followed by Random Forest with 10.7%. These findings suggest that advanced time-series models can examine and document more intricate patterns of demand. The outcome of this research can be utilized to support government departments, urban planners, and power suppliers to make better decisions regarding EV infrastructure. Using accurate predictions, they can avoid power loading issues, conserve energy, and assist in constructing smart and green cities.

title: Data Storage Method Based on Unary Code
authors: Alexey N. Terekhov
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: During the presentation of the article materials, it is intended to evaluate in detail the strengths and weaknesses of the proposed data storage method. To disclose in more detail the encoding and decoding mechanism of the proposed data storage method based on unary code. Assess the possibility of using additional methods and techniques of information compression to increase entropy. The received data is encoded using Modified Run Length Encoding (MRLE). It is planned to abandon the use of prefix codes of the Huffman algorithm at the final stage of coding by introducing a separator symbol and to use all possible binary code combinations corresponding to the number of information bits used. It is proposed to use the development of modern technologies, in particular, multi-level memory cells -Multi-Level Cell (MLC), which allow storing information dibits for storing symbols for separating and ending data. Due to what it is expected to provide a higher degree of compression of the proposed method of storing information. Consider the possibility of assessing the change in noise immunity when switching to coding using the proposed algorithm. Assess the strengths and weaknesses of the proposed information storage method.

title: Circularly Polarized DRA Antenna with Double Split-Ring Resonator Filtering for 5G Applications
authors: Akhilesh Kumar, Vinay Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: A compact DRA antenna with integrated passive Filtering is presented for cognitive radio applications in the sub6 GHz band. A planar ultra-wideband radiator is employed as the baseline antenna, and frequency selectivity is achieved using a double split-ring resonator (DSRR) integrated along the feedline. A defected ground structure (DGS) is introduced to improve impedance matching, stabilize resonance, and enhance circular polarization performance. The simulation results demonstrate bandpass behavior around 4 GHz with good impedance matching, stable radiation characteristics, and circular polarization, making the proposed antenna suitable for 5G cognitive radio systems.

title: Modulation-Controlled DAB Converter Design and Performance Evaluation for EV On-Board Charging
authors: Rohit Raikwar, Pankaj Swarnkar, Punjan Dohare
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The increasing requirement for efficient and compact power conversion system in electric vehicles has accelerated the advancement of sophisticated topologies for On-Board Chargers (OBCs). A paper presents comprehensive simulation-based study of DAB DC-DC converter, an innovative solution for high-frequency isolation and bidirectional power conversion in OBC application. DAB architecture is ideal for EV charging system that enable Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) capabilities, and it has many other benefits as well, including as galvanic separation, high power density and the ability flow power bidirectionally. In this research work, the simulation of the DAB converter is performed using MATLAB/Simulink to examine the essential parameters such as voltage and current. phase shift control behavior, and the impact of load variations. Result validate that optimized phase-shift modulation and transformer design can significantly enhance converter performance, reduce switching losses, relevance to low voltage EV system. This study contributes valuable insights into the practical design and control of DAB-based OBCs and represents a crucial step towards the establishment of high-efficiency bidirectional EV charging infrastructure.

title: Federated Sparse Principal Component Analysis for Collaborative Condition Monitoring of Multi-Wind-Farm Systems
authors: Kemeng Wei, Housheng Wang, Wenyi Zhang, Zhenyu Wu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: As wind power becomes a cornerstone of renewable energy, its large-scale deployment creates an urgent need for collaborative monitoring across multiple wind farms. However, traditional condition monitoring methods for individual wind farms encounter significant limitations when applied to multi-wind-farm scenarios, primarily due to concerns about data privacy. To overcome this challenge, a federated learning-enabled distributed monitoring strategy for multi-wind-farm systems is proposed, effectively improving the accuracy and efficiency of condition monitoring while ensuring data privacy security. Specifically, we develop a distributed sparse principal component analysis method for dimensionality reduction. The algorithm operates within a federated learning framework, only requiring the transmission of low-dimensional model data (the principal components projection matrix) instead of raw SCADA data. On this basis, a multivariate exponentially weighted moving average (MEWMA) control chart is constructed for joint anomaly detection over multiple wind farms. The effectiveness and superiority of the proposed approach are demonstrated on both simulated fault cases and real wind farm datasets.

title: Compact Wideband Filtering Antenna for Wireless and 5G Application
authors: Ashwanth Ravi Kumar, Sathish Krishna, Tharan Srinivasan, Dilip Kumar Choudhary
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This research presents a miniaturized wideband filtering antenna (FA) to improve the bandwidth and gain having inbuilt filtering mechanism. In order to realize filtering features, the antenna combines a bow-tie radiator with a symmetrical Y-shaped branch and a semicircular slot carved in the ground plane. Combining the aforementioned components results in controllable radiation nulls in the antenna gain profile. A frequency selective surface (FSS) is placed above the antenna structure to further increase the gain. A large impedance bandwidth is made possible by the bow-tie topology, and the FSS significantly boosts gain without sacrificing compactness. In the operating frequency band, which spans from 4.5 to 6.2 GHz with a central frequency of 5.35 GHz, the suggested FA has a total gain of 4.10 dBi with a bandwidth of 31.8%. The suggested design will be suitable for 5G and modern wireless communication. It then uses integrated filtering mechanisms to achieve wideband operation while keeping compact sizes.

title: IT-Enabled Management Frameworks for Driving Sustainable Social Change
authors: Sonali Sharma, Gulnozakhon Muydinova, B. Charwak, Pooja Nagpal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Digital technologies are changing how public services are delivered in India, but evidence is scarce on which combinations of technology, management capacity, and institutional context produce sustained social gains. This study investigates which Technology–Organization–Environment (TOE) configurations of IT investment, local IT management, and governance context lead to improvements in service delivery, health, and livelihood indicators across Indian districts. A two-stage mixed-method design is used: fuzzy-set qualitative comparative analysis (fsQCA) first uncovers multiple sufficient TOE configurations, then panel fixed-effects regressions and difference-in-differences tests estimate effect sizes and test robustness. District-level panels (2010–2023 where available) are built from NFHS, MGNREGA MIS, Data.gov.in indicators, DBT dashboards, TRAI/BharatNet reports, and Census/SECC variables. Calibration uses percentile-based anchors and robustness checks include alternative thresholds, spatial models, placebo tests, and propensity-score matching. Three recurrent solution paths appear. A Technology – Organization (TO) synergy high IT investment combined with active local IT management is associated with improved DBT success and timeliness (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 2-4$</tex> percentage points). An Environment-led path strong institutional quality and governance efficiency aligns with higher immunization coverage (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 2-3$</tex> percentage points). An Organization-plus-human-capital path program uptake together with staff education links to modest poverty reduction (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 1-2$</tex> percentage points). Findings hold across calibration and spatial checks. The paper provides actionable, configuration-sensitive guidance for targeting infrastructure, management, and institutional reforms to maximize inclusive social gains in India.

title: Landscape Semantic Segmentation: A Method Based on Transformer with Residual Refinement
authors: Kaijie Luan, Mingqi Yang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Semantic segmentation of landscape architecture images is pivotal for automated planning and design, facilitating precise identification of elements like trees, water, grass, and mountains. Conventional CNNs face challenges in capturing global context in complex landscapes, resulting in boundary inaccuracies and texture misrecognition. We propose an enhanced SegFormer model with a novel residual refinement module, integrating lightweight convolutions, batch normalization, and ReLU to refine logits, boosting edge precision and segmentation accuracy with minimal computational cost. The hierarchical Transformer encoder of SegFormer enables efficient multi-scale feature extraction, combined with the refinement head for superior landscape performance. Evaluations on the Kaggle Landscape Segmentation dataset show our model achieves 0.792 mIoU, surpassing baseline SegFormer by 11.9% with comparable parameters and speed. Visualizations highlight improved detail handling and error reduction. Ablation confirms component efficacy, and noise tests affirm resilience. This advances computer vision in landscape architecture for urban planning and environmental design, offering a robust, efficient tool.

title: A Comparative Study and Technical Analysis of Photonic Amplifier Architectures for High-Speed Optical Systems
authors: Priya Singh, Indu K, Mallikarjuna Hampali
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Photonic amplifiers have aided the high speed and long distance optical communication systems. In this paper author has reviewed three popular technical amplifier–Semiconductor Optical Amplifiers (SOAs), Erbium-Doped Fiber Amplifiers (EDFAs) and Raman Amplifiers–and their performance analysis is done. The comparison is done on the basis of noise figure, gain, bandwidth, integration potential, and application compatibility. Further discussion on future trends for small and energy efficient solutions are highlighted related to recent developments in silicon photonics and hybrid integration. This work can assist in deciding the appropriate photonic amplifier topologies for a range of different applications in communication, sensing, and neuromorphic photonic systems.

title: Improving the Performance of Switched Reluctance Motor by Changing Rotor Pole Modifications for Low Power Traction Applications
authors: Deepak M, Karuniya M, Nithya Sri B, Anto Joel RF
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)
abstract: Switched Reluctance Motors (SRMs) are a good choice for electric vehicles (EVs) because they do not use magnets and have strong speed-torque characteristics. However, a major issue with SRMs is torque ripple, which causes noise and vibrations. This Paper aims to reduce torque ripple by changing the rotor design and testing different stator and rotor pole combinations. In this investigation, 8/6 and 6/4 pole combinations with optimal geometry parameters are selected based on the finite element analysis method. The proposed design model is developed using MAGNET Infolytica and Ansys. The simulation results validate the flux linkage, phase current, electromagnetic torque and torque ripple analysis. Results showed that the interior rotor with an 8/6 configuration gave the lowest torque ripple, making it the best option for high-speed EV applications. In future, this model will help to interface with the advanced control techniques, further reducing torque ripples.

title: The Evolution of hhe Ideas of Pedagogical Cybernetics and their Application in the Educational Processes of Training IT Specialists
authors: I. O. Yablochnikova, K. A. Makhiboroda, S. L. Yablochnikov
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: Modern information and communication technologies (ICTs) have a significant impact on the evolution of education in general and higher education in particular. It is quite natural to actively use ICT to manage a hierarchical complex of educational processes, including those related to the training of future IT specialists. A large number of scientific studies conducted over the past 50 years have been devoted to numerous aspects of the effective implementation of these technologies in practical educational activities. Both the technologies themselves and the corresponding means of achieving pedagogical goals have gradually evolved. The ideas of cybernetic pedagogy and pedagogical cybernetics, which were formulated by B. Skinner, G. Pask, F. Kube, H. Frank, A. Berg, were supported by the international psychological and pedagogical community. They received a positive response and were successfully applied in pedagogical practice, including in higher educational institutions. At the same time, the relevant theoretical foundations for the successful use of ICT in higher school pedagogy were actively developed and improved. This article is devoted to the analysis of a set of positive aspects, as well as existing problems related to the above processes.

title: Empowering IoT with Federated Learning: A Cross-Domain Survey from Healthcare to Industry
authors: Shobhit Aggarwal, Arnab Purkayastha, Jay Tharwani
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) generates massive amounts of data from a multitude of interconnected devices, offering valuable insights into various sectors. However, effectively leveraging this data for machine learning (ML) faces significant hurdles. These include data quality issues, resource constraints on IoT devices, and concerns regarding data privacy and security. This survey paper explores the synergistic potential of combining IoT and federated learning (FL) to address these challenges. We examine how FL can mitigate the limitations of traditional ML approaches in IoT contexts. Specifically, we investigate the application of FL in diverse domains like healthcare, smart cities, and industrial automation, highlighting its ability to train robust models collaboratively without compromising data privacy. Furthermore, we analyze the key challenges that impede the widespread adoption of FL in IoT, such as device heterogeneity, communication latency, and resilience to device failures, outlining potential avenues for future research.

title: Background-Aware Instance Segmentation for Early Detection of E. coli and Salmonella in Time-Stamped Microscopy Images
authors: Bibek Koirala, Anas M. Alsobeh, Namariq Dhahir, Amer AbuGhazaleh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Rapid detection of bacterial contamination in food supply chains is essential to prevent outbreaks and protect public health. Traditional microbiological methods, such as culture and biochemical testing, are slow and can delay necessary interventions. This study presents deep learning–based approaches for the early detection and classification of Escherichia coli and Salmonella Typhimurium in microscopy images captured during incubation periods ranging from 1.5 hours to 4 hours, with a step size of 30 min, under two different conditions: a plain background and an onion mixture background. We annotated a dataset of 2,200 high-resolution (2160×1620) images, collected under 60× magnification from the time frame 1.5 h to 4 h, using a combination of manual and semi-automated techniques with Mask R-CNN. For manual annotation, dataset was annotated using tkinter framework in python. For model training, only pure culture data were used. The initial pipeline employed Cellpose for segmentation and a ViT for classification across bacterial growth stages. However, Cellpose was unable to segment all instances in an image due to its requirement for a diameter parameter, which, under variable colony sizes, resulted in partial segmentation. To overcome these challenges, we adopted an end-to-end Mask R-CNN model for instance segmentation. Mask R-CNN achieved consistently strong mean Intersection over Union (mIoU) scores between 0.91 and 0.98 across growth stages. Over the full incubation period, the mean Average Precision (AP) and Average Recall (AR) at IoU threshold 0.5 were 0.93 and 0.96 for E. coli, and 0.945 and 0.975 for Salmonella, respectively, indicating robust detection performance across bacterial types and bacterial growth period. Using fine-tuned Mask R-CNN trained on background aware time-stamped microscopy datasets, our method achieves an mAP@0.5 of 0.95 for colonies cultured at 2 hours timepoint. This work focuses on proactive food safety monitoring in agricultural pipelines.

title: Energy-Sensitive Anomaly Detection Models for IoT Devices in Limited Power Environments
authors: Pathan Firoze Khan, Maddi Venkata Pavan Kumar, Meriga Kiran Kumar, M. Sitha Ram, Venkata Srinivasu Veesam, Repudi Pitchiah
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The propagation of IoT devices in healthcare, environmental monitoring, and smart cities has created the demand for robust anomaly detection mechanisms for security against failure and cyber-attacks. Most of these devices, however, must run within power-constrained environments, and energy efficiency takes the center stage. Conventional anomaly detection techniques, relying on power-intensive operations and periodic transmission of data, have the potential to drain IoT devices' short power supplies in no time, rendering them inappropriate for continuous use. To overcome this, this paper suggests a new energy-aware anomaly detection framework tailored to IoT systems with short power supplies. Our approach uses lightweight machine learning with adaptive sampling schemes to facilitate continuous monitoring without wasting power. The model makes use of in-device feature selection and edge computing models to lessen data transmission requirements and processing requirements, with a hierarchical detection framework where initial screening is done at the device level and only high-priority anomalies are passed on to edge processors for in-depth analysis. Results show significant energy saving without compromising accuracy, proving the applicability of the model to real-world IoT environments with power limitations.

title: Phase-Change Material Enabled Metamaterials and Metasurfaces
authors: Shirish Tripathi, Vibhu Srivastava, Ritesh Kumar Mishra
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Phase-change materials (PCMs) like <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Ge}_{2} \text{Sb}_{2} \text{Te}_{5}$</tex> (GST) have become essential for tunable nanophotonic structures. GST shows notable changes in its electrical and optical properties when it switches between crystalline and amorphous phases. These changes in GST properties enable the manipulation of light. The static optical properties of conventional metamaterials and metasurfaces limit their use in dynamic applications. When GST is added to these nanostructures, it changes their behavior and makes them nonvolatile. This feature lets us control the optical and thermal reactions in real time. This review talks about the latest progress made in GST-based metamaterials and metasurfaces, such as switches, beam-steering devices, sensors, and tunable absorbers. We describe GST's crystal structure along with the phase transition mechanisms.

title: Design and Analysis of a High-Performance Ultra-Wide Band Antenna for Advanced Wireless Communication
authors: D Ramesh Varma, P Narasimha Rao, G Naga Poojitha, J Vyshnavi, G Pujitha Devi, K Chandra Sravanthi
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: This paper presents the design and analysis of a compact ultra-wide band microstrip patch antenna for advanced wireless communication systems. The antenna is developed using an RT 5880 substrate with dimensions of 32 mm × 24 mm, selected for its low dielectric constant and minimal loss tangent, which enhance radiation efficiency and reduce signal loss. To achieve UWB performance and multi-band resonance, various slot shapes including circular, square, hexagonal, octagonal, hexagonal, and pentagonal slots are utilized and analysis is made for each slot. It is observed that, the antenna performed well for all different shapes of slots. were introduced into the patch design. The antenna resonates at 4 GHz, 8.3 GHz, 19.8 GHz, and 28.3 GHz, covering C-band, X-band and milli meter-wave frequencies. A comparative study was conducted using different patch shapes to evaluate parameters such as return loss, VSWR, efficiency, gain and surface current. It is observed that among all the tested configurations, the octagonal-shaped patch antenna delivered the best performance, offering the highest gain of 4.6 dB and lowest surface current, resulting in efficient radiation and minimal losses. With a VSWR of 1.8, it ensures good impedance matching. The proposed design provides a compact, efficient, and reliable solution for high-performance wireless communication system.

title: Ventilator Performance Improvement using K-Means Clustering for Effective Data Pattern Recognition
authors: T. S. Balaji Damodhar, E. Sivanantham, R. Leela Jyothi, Viyyapu Lokeshwari Vinya, K Umapathy, S Sujatha
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The efficacy of ventilators is essential for patient management, especially in emergencies necessitating immediate modifications. This work presents a way to improve ventilator performance using K-Means clustering for efficient data pattern detection. Data from ventilators, including pressure, volume, and flow rates, are gathered over time to predict performance patterns and abnormalities. Using K-Means clustering, the data can be classified into discrete groups according to the similarity of ventilation patterns, enabling enhanced monitoring and intervention techniques. The program classifies the ventilator's operating states, preventing underperformance or deviations from predicted parameters. By promptly identifying these trends, healthcare personnel may proactively modify ventilator settings, enhancing patient outcomes. The clustered data offers insights into ventilator performance under many settings, facilitating the creation of more efficient and responsive ventilator models. These research findings indicate that K-Means clustering may significantly increase the accuracy of ventilator performance monitoring and improve decision-making in clinical environments. This methodology facilitates the development of more advanced, data-informed systems in critical care, where quick actions are essential for patient life and recuperation.

title: AI-Powered Leukemia Detection: A VGG16 Transfer Learning Approach for Blood Smear Image Classification
authors: Shanvi Chauhan, Nagendar Yamsani, Neha Sharma, Ritika Badhani, Ashwani Kharola
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia (ALL) is a fast-evolving Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia (ALL) is a rapid developing blood and bone marrow cancer and has the greatest direct impact on the white blood cells hence early and accurate diagnosis is more than essential to improving patient survival. Traditional methods of diagnosis, such as microscopic analysis of blood smears, are time-consuming and greatly dependent on the experience of expertise, which may cause variation in diagnosis. To this end, this study will propose an automated deep transfer learning model of VGG16 based classification to detect ALL. The pre-trained VGG16 model was fine-tuned, with the removal of its top layers, by adding custom fully connected layers to allow adapting to a given task, namely to classify leukemic cells into four classes, i. e., Benign, Pre-B Malignant, Pro-B Malignant and Early. The characteristics of the data utilized in the research are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3, 2 5 6}$</tex> microscopic images divided into <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1, 9 5 4}$</tex> training, 650 validation and 652 testing. The test accuracy of the proposed model was 97% indicating that it was highly reliable and robust in its ability to separate the subtypes of the disease. These findings point to the potential of deep learning-grounded techniques to significantly enhance the diagnostic accuracy, reduce manual workload, and facilitate faster and more consistent diagnosis of the ALL, therefore, contributing to clinical decision-making and increasing the outcome of treatment.

title: Concerns and Redefining Load Modeling of Large Data Centers in Future Power Systems—A Review
authors: Sachinth Viththarachchige, Demy Alexander, Sarangan Rajendran, Arun Manoharan, Visvakumar Aravinthan
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: The exponential rise of generative artificial intelligence is driving an unprecedented surge in hyperscale data center capacity, creating novel stability challenges for future power systems. Traditional load modeling approaches, largely based on induction motor and static load counterparts, are inadequate for capturing the complex, control-driven dynamics of modern rectifier-interfaced data center facilities. This paper discusses data center behavior from a system perspective, identifying critical risks such as transient instability caused by weak fault ride-through capabilities and oscillatory instability driven by periodic artificial intelligence workload patterns. We critique existing static and composite load models and suggest the requirement of a decision framework for selecting appropriate modeling domains, based on the characteristics of the phenomena under study. Finally, the paper outlines urgent industry needs, emphasizing standardized blackbox modeling to overcome intellectual property barriers and the necessity of high-resolution field data for model validation to ensure power system integrity.

title: Research on Citrus Fruit Disease Identification Based on Improved YOLOv8
authors: Xiaofang Wu, Yunying Luo, Shiyu Zhang, Tiansong Zhai
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: China is a major citrus-producing country, but citrus fruits are vulnerable to diseases such as blackspot, canker, and yellowspot during growth and storage, leading to significant economic losses. To address the inefficiency and low accuracy of manual identification, this paper proposes an improved YOLOv8-based citrus fruit disease identification method. By integrating the GAM (Global Attention Module) and SPPF (Spatial Pyramid Pooling - Fast) module into the YOLOv8-cls network, the model's ability to perceive multi-scale targets and aggregate cross-scale features is enhanced. A dataset containing 1,359 images of three citrus diseases and healthy fruits is constructed for model training and validation. Experimental results show that the improved model achieves an identification accuracy of 99.80%, which is 0.4% higher than the original YOLOv8-cls model. The method has fast inference speed and strong robustness, providing an effective technical solution for rapid citrus disease identification.

title: Resource Allocation in UAV-Enabled Wireless Networks: A Unified Survey of Modeling and Algorithms
authors: Haihan Qiu, Ziwei Chen
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) provide a flexible platform for enhancing performance in wireless networks through dynamic allocation and three-dimensional mobility. This paper presents a concise and unified review of resource allocation in UAV-enabled wireless networks. Representative optimization objectives, including energy efficiency, spectral efficiency, utility maximization, and trajectory-related design, are systematically summarized. Then commonly adopted algorithmic approaches ranging from model-based optimization to learning-driven and hybrid methods are summarized. By comparing different solution paradigms, this paper highlights their respective applicability and limitations. Finally, key challenges and future research directions are outlined for efficient and scalable UAV-enabled wireless systems.

title: Guest Editorial:Beyond Classic Deep Learning: Algorithms for Dealing With Real-World Applications in Industrial Automation
authors: Gian Antonio Susto, Olga Fink, Seokho Kang, Lars Mönch, Davide Dalle Pezze
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering

title: Explainable AI Based DL Framework for Brain Tumor Discovery, Classification Using Densenet-121 and Grad-CAM
authors: C Alamelu, Matan P, Krishna Priya K
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Brain tumors pose a significant neurological concern, where timely and precise identification is vital for improving treatment outcomes and patient survival. The integration of AI with medical imaging has transformed diagnostic efficiency and interpretation in recent years. Despite these advances, many DL systems continue to face limited adoption in clinical settings because their decision-making process often lacks transparency. To address this issue, the present study proposes an XAI-enabled deep learning framework for brain tumor classification, employing the DenseNet-121 architecture to achieve both reliability and interpretability. The framework was trained on MRI scans from the publicly available Kaggle Brain Tumor dataset using optimized hyperparameters and extensive data augmentation to ensure robustness. Visualization tools such as Grad-CAM and SHAP were used to identify tumor-specific regions and clarify the reasoning behind classification outputs. Experimental evaluation showed that the model achieved an overall accuracy around 98%, with precision exceeding 0.95, recall exceeding 0.95, and F1-scores exceeding 0.95 across four categories. Comparative analysis confirmed that the proposed method outperformed standard CNN and hybrid baselines in both two and severalclass evaluations. The incorporation of XAI not only strengthened interpretability but also increased clinical trust by providing clear visual evidence for each prediction. These findings highlight the potential of combining DenseNet-121 with explainable modeling techniques to enable dependable brain tumor classification, early diagnosis, and informed decision support in modern healthcare environments.

title: Pixel-Level Crack Segmentation with a Lightweight MobileNet and Transformer-based U-Net Hybrid Model
authors: S.Jency, G Ramkumar
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Cracks are among the most critical defects threatening the structural integrity of bridges and other infrastructure. To address the challenges of detecting cracks in various environments with high precision, we propose a pixel-level crack segmentation model based on a hybrid deep learning architecture named MobileNet TransUNet. This model integrates the lightweight feature extraction capabilities of MobileNet as the encoder with the global context modelling power of a transformer-based decoder derived from TransUNet. The encoder efficiently captures hierarchical features, while the transformer decoder enhances segmentation accuracy by modelling long-range dependencies. Skip connections between the encoder and decoder improve spatial resolution and detail. The model is trained on a custom dataset containing cracks from diverse real-world scenes. Experimental results demonstrate that our hybrid model achieves superior segmentation performance compared to existing methods, offering a robust, efficient solution for automated crack detection in structural health monitoring applications.

title: Sustainable Inventory Management for Dual Warehouses with Reverse Logistics and Green Optimization Policies
authors: Prashant Ashiwal, Vijender Yadav, Chandra Shekhar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This research presents an inventory model for perishable items within a dual-warehouse framework, where products are initially supplied from rented storage and subsequently from owned storage, assuming immediate deterioration. The demand is influenced by factors such as price, time, and the greenness of the product, addressing both economic and sustainability considerations. A reverse logistics system is included, enabling full refunds for dissatisfied customers, with returned items being refurbished and sold in the secondary market at a lower price to minimize waste. The model also incorporates controls for carbon emissions and green technologies aimed at fulfilling environmental goals. Metaheuristic algorithms are utilized to optimize the complex profit function, yielding effective solutions to the non-linear model. Numerical tests and visual representations illustrate the model's efficacy and provide managerial insights into achieving a balance between profitability, sustainability, and customer satisfaction. This study adds to the existing literature by offering a thorough, environmentally focused inventory framework that combines dual-warehouse management, reverse logistics, and emission controls within a single optimization strategy.

title: Chaotic Behaviour of Primary Pump Motor in Water Cooled Screw Chiller
authors: Susmita Adhikary, Sumit Bandopadhyay, Ambarnath Banerji, Ranabir Sen, Asis Majumder, Priyabrata Adhikary
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)
abstract: This paper investigates the effect of chaotic behavior of the primary pump motor in a water-cooled screw chiller system, focusing on the bifurcation phenomena and their implications for system stability in the context of two-phase refrigerant flow occurring inside the evaporator coil of a water-cooled screw chiller plant. Using the principles of bifurcation theory, the study analyzes how varying system parameters, such as motor speed and load, influence the operational dynamics of the pump motor. The paper also introduces the Minimal Stable Signal (MSS) line theory as a novel method for parameter selection, providing insight into the most critical operating conditions that lead to chaotic regimes. Through simulations and analysis, the paper highlights the sensitivity of the system to parameter changes, with particular emphasis on how minor alterations can lead to drastic shifts in performance, potentially resulting in instability. The findings underscore the importance of careful parameter tuning in preventing chaotic behavior and ensuring reliable operation of water-cooled screw chillers in industrial applications.

title: Improving Internet Traffic Matrix Prediction via Time Series Clustering
authors: Martha Cash, Alexander Wyglinski
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: We present a novel framework that leverages time series clustering to improve internet traffic matrix (TM) prediction using deep learning (DL) models. Traffic flows within a TM often exhibit diverse temporal behaviors, which can hinder prediction accuracy when training a single model across all flows. To address this, we propose two clustering strategies —source clustering and histogram clustering —that group flows with similar temporal patterns prior to model training. Clustering creates more homogeneous data subsets, enabling models to capture underlying patterns more effectively and generalize better than global prediction approaches that fit a single model to the entire TM. Compared to existing TM prediction methods, our method reduces RMSE by up to 92% for Abilene and 75% for GÉANT. In routing scenarios, our clustered predictions also reduce maximum link utilization (MLU) bias by 18% and 21%, respectively, demonstrating the practical benefits of clustering when TMs are used for network optimization.

title: Localized Uncertainty Quantification in Random Forests via Proximities
authors: Jake S. Rhodes, Scott D. Brown, J. Riley Wilkinson
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: In machine learning, uncertainty quantification helps assess the reliability of model predictions, which is important in high-stakes scenarios. Traditional approaches often emphasize predictive accuracy, but there is a growing focus on incorporating uncertainty measures. This paper addresses localized uncertainty quantification in random forests. While current methods often rely on quantile regression or Monte Carlo techniques, we propose a new approach using naturally occurring test sets and similarity measures (proximities) typically viewed as byproducts of random forests. Specifically, we form localized distributions of OOB errors around nearby points, defined using the proximities, to create prediction intervals for regression and trust scores for classification. By varying the number of nearby points, our intervals can be adjusted to achieve the desired coverage while retaining the flexibility that reflects the certainty of individual predictions. For classification, excluding points identified as unclassifiable by our method generally enhances the accuracy of the model and provides higher accuracy-rejection AUC scores than competing methods.

title: I-Powered Sustainable Consumer Behavior Analysis in FMCG using Smart Electronics and Communication Platforms
authors: J.Jayashree, N.Sushmitha, S. Mekala, Mithra Rivin, Jinto Sebastian, T.Aswathinath
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Eco-friendly products are produced, used, recycled, and disposed of in ways that are not harmful to the environment. The environment encompasses everything surrounding us, including natural elements such as water, air, land, animals, plants, and climate. The environment is our life support system; it is difficult for people to survive without a healthy environment. Therefore, a healthy environment is a must for leading a healthy life. Consumers and manufacturers are increasingly aware of the importance of producing and purchasing eco-friendly products that do not harm the environment. Every individual is actively seeking products made from these types of materials. This research creates a model to classify eco-friendly products. The analysis specifically targets FMCG, or Fast-Moving Consumer Goods, a category of low-cost, fast-moving products. This classification includes personal care products, household products, food and beverages, and some medicines. This research focuses particularly on classifying the customer-purchased items as non-circular or circular based on important sustainability attributes, such as eco-friendliness and recyclability. The sustainable fashion trends dataset from Kaggle is used for this purpose. This dataset includes features with missing attribute values and mixed types, including both numerical and categorical values. The dataset contains values with wide ranges that require normalization. Additionally, it includes several features that are not relevant to the classification process. Therefore, before the algorithm implements the dataset, preprocessing methods, such as K-NN imputation, modes for missing value attributes, min-max scaling for normalization, and mutual information for feature selection, are used to increase the quality of the data. The preprocessing step generates a new feature that exhibits circular behavior. Then, the dataset is processed using the proposed DCKSVM-FA (Divide and Conquer Kernel Support Vector Machine learning algorithms with Firefly) model. The algorithm is implemented with the dataset, classifying each sample as either circular or non-circular. We evaluate and compare the algorithm's performance with existing algorithms using performance metrics such as accuracy, precision, and recall. The comparison results demonstrate the superiority of the proposed model, achieving the highest accuracy of 98%.

title: Analyzing Cyber Attack Patterns and Detection Strategies Through T-Pot Honeypot Deployment
authors: Aditya Suresh Nair, Joel Stephen Mathew, Arman Singh Bhati, Mohana, Abhilash Chakkan
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: This paper presents an analysis of T-Pot honeypot deployments for threat intelligence gathering and analysis. Through extensive experimentation and data collection spanning multiple months, the study evaluates attack trends, geographic distribution patterns, targeted service ports, and sophisticated attacker methodologies. The research demonstrates the crucial role of honeypots in identifying and mitigating emerging security threats while providing valuable insights into attacker behavior patterns and attack vectors. The analysis encompasses multiple honeypot types, including Cowrie, Tanner, H0neytr4p, and CiscoASA, covering over 690,000 attacks to derive patterns and conclusions. Furthermore, the study explores recent advancements in honeypot models and their integration into modern cybersecurity frameworks, while proposing potential enhancements for future deployments, including machine learning integration and automated response mechanisms.

title: Blockchain-Based Hybrid Academic Certificate Management System
authors: Anurag De, Gautam Pal, Ananya Mukherjee, Shubham Das, Venkata Sridhara Reddy Dodda
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: In this paper, a decentralized certificate management system leveraging blockchain technology to ensure the authenticity and immutability of academic credentials is proposed. The proposed academic certificate management system utilizes Ethereum's Sepolia testnet for storing certificate hashes, IPFS for decentralized document storage, and a Node.js web application for user interaction. The system adopts a hybrid storage approach, temporarily maintaining certificate requests in a Post-greSQL database until university approval, after which validated certificates are permanently recorded on the blockchain. This design optimizes resource utilization while preserving security. The verification process is streamlined, allowing third parties to authenticate certificates using a unique certificate ID. By integrating blockchain immutability, decentralized storage, and a user-friendly interface, this system mitigates certificate fraud, simplifies verification, and enhances credential portability while reducing administrative overhead.

title: FSVT-SL: Few-Shot Vision Transformer with Synthetic Lesion Augmentation for Robust Plant Disease Diagnosis Under Field Conditions
authors: Bhoomeshwara Bala, G. Mahendraswaroop, Bochu Ramasree, Yerram Sneha, G. Swapna, Kokkula Shiva Prasad
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Quick and efficient diagnosis of plant diseases is paramount to viable agriculture, but frequently the models based on deep learning fail in the real field conditions because of domain shifts and due to limited labeled data. The given work demonstrates a Few-Shot Vision Transformer (ViT) framework which has been improved using the concept of conditional diffusion synthesis of lesions and the fusion of RGB and NIR/hyperspectral imagery with conditional diffusion. The hypothesis incorporated in the proposed way focuses on utilizing the supervised contrastive pre-training, synthetic realistic lesion and lightweight domain adaptation with an aim of enhancing the generalization between lab-collected samples and actual farm settings. Experiments of PlantVillage, hyperspectral, and custom in-field images demonstrate the resilience of the model yielding accuracy of 99.9, precision of 0.999 and recall of 0.999 achieving almost perfect AUC of all disease classes. Comparative studies demonstrate that the approach is much superior to ResNet, EfficientNet, and standard ViT baselines. The findings legitimize that a combination of few-shot learning, a generative augmentation model, and multimodal sensing would effectively diagnose and forecast plant diseases in the field on a reliable basis.

title: Adaptive Maritime Vessel Detection System Under Reduced Visibility Conditions
authors: Maxim Fionin, Ksenia Polyantseva
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid architecture for maritime vessel detection combining image quality enhancement via MPRNet with parallel YOLO-family detectors. The YOLO_COCO model handles standard visibility, while specialized YOLO_Fog addresses fog, precipitation, and spray under extreme low-contrast regimes. Analytical synthesis using average confidence metrics selects the most reliable detection. Multi-stage filtering ensures real-time processing. Experimental validation demonstrates robustness across three scenarios: heavy rainfall, dense fog, and intense sea spray. The specialized model successfully identifies vessels where the general-purpose detector fails, achieving automatic weather-based adaptation. The system integrates preprocessing, parallel detection with dynamic model selection, and reporting subsystem via Flask web service, enabling applications in navigation support, port surveillance, and environmental monitoring.

title: Semi-Automatic Generation of Drivable Road Networks from Satellite Imagery for CARLA
authors: Marina Moseva, Vadim Borzov
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: We present a lightweight SAM-to-OpenDRIVE pipeline for generating simulator-ready road networks for CARLA from overhead imagery. Road masks obtained via prompt-based segmentation are converted into vector graphs and compiled into geometrically consistent ASAM OpenDRIVE maps with valid lanes and junction topology. The modular design enables scalable and rapid map prototyping while explicitly targeting simulator ingestion and structural compliance.

title: Researching Algorithms Used to Forecast Sales Volumes of Telecommunications Equipment
authors: Yuri Leokhin, Timur Fatkhulin, Svetlana S. Dymkova, Irina Zozulya
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: This article presents a study of the problems of forecasting sales volumes of telecommunications equipment. The objective of the work is to determine the most effective algorithm used for forecasting sales volumes of various types of telecommunications equipment. The relevance of this work lies in the rapid growth of information and data transmitted by telecommunications equipment, as well as the need for IT companies to keep up with the times and be able to promptly adjust business strategies for the purchase and sale of such equipment. Traditional methods based on probability theory and mathematical statistics cannot effectively generate forecasts given the above trends in global business processes. The application of machine learning methods focused on forecasting this type of product can significantly improve the efficiency of solving this problem. The object of the study is sales volumes of telecommunications equipment. The subject of the research is metrics for assessing the quality of algorithms and machine learning models built on them, designed to forecast sales volumes of telecommunications equipment. The article draws conclusions on the practical application of each algorithm and indicates areas for promising research.

title: Application of FTN signals in UAVs Communication Systems
authors: D. S. Chirov, E. O. Kandaurova, A. A. Frolov, A. A. Tolasova
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: Developing highly efficient UAVs communication systems is a pressing and in-demand task today. This is driven by the rapid development of unmanned aircraft and their use in various sectors of the economy. The key requirements for UAV communication systems include high reliability and noise immunity, high data transfer rates, electromagnetic compatibility with existing electronic equipment, and so on. Various complex signal-code structures are used to meet these requirements. Analysis of sources shows that the most commonly used technology is the proven OFDM technology, which, in addition to its obvious advantages, has several disadvantages: to combat multipath, a cyclic prefix must be inserted between OFDM signal symbols, resulting in a 10-25% reduction in data throughput; OFDM signals have a high peak-to-average power ratio (crest factor), forcing transmitter power amplifiers to operate in linear mode, which has a low efficiency. Another way to improve information transmission efficiency is to use signal spreading techniques, which increases the signal base. Currently, three main methods of spread spectrum are used: frequency-hopping spread spectrum, direct spread spectrum, and linear spread spectrum. The authors of this article have previously examined the use of OFDM and DSSS technologies for building UAVs communication systems. This article presents the results of a study of FTN (Faster than Nyquist) technology for solving this problem. The results of modeling and field studies are presented. It is shown that these technologies allow for the efficient construction of UAVs communication systems based on commercially available components at low cost.

title: Comparative Evaluation of Embedding Representations for Financial News Sentiment Analysis
authors: Joyjit Roy, Samaresh Kumar Singh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Financial sentiment analysis enhances market understanding. However, standard Natural Language Processing (NLP) approaches encounter significant challenges when applied to small datasets. This study presents a comparative evaluation of embedding-based techniques for financial news sentiment classification in resource-constrained environments. Word2Vec, GloVe, and sentence transformer representations are evaluated in combination with gradient boosting on a manually labeled dataset of 349 financial news headlines. Experimental results identify a substantial gap between validation and test performance. Despite strong validation metrics, models underperform relative to trivial baselines. The analysis indicates that pretrained embeddings yield diminishing returns below a critical data sufficiency threshold. Small validation sets contribute to overfitting during model selection. Practical application is illustrated through weekly sentiment aggregation and narrative summarization for market monitoring. Overall, the findings indicate that embedding quality alone cannot address fundamental data scarcity in sentiment classification. Practitioners with limited labeled data should consider alternative strategies, including few-shot learning, data augmentation, or lexicon-enhanced hybrid methods.

title: HIRE: A Hybrid Learned Index for Robust and Efficient Performance under Mixed Workloads
authors: Xinyi Zhang, Liang Liang, Anastasia Ailamaki, Jianliang Xu

title: Exploring Freehand Selection Techniques of Polyhedron Faces in VR Environments_supp1-3680697.pdf
authors: Xuning Hu
publish: N/A
abstract: Virtual reality (VR) allows users to observe and manipulate 3D geometry from multiple viewpoints. Most VR selection work, however, optimizes techniques for selecting entire objects. Selecting a single face on a polyhedron remains underexplored and is more challenging because the interaction must act on a local component while preserving the object's global structure. We introduce a design space tailored to this task with three dimensions: viewing strategy, disambiguation consistency, and interaction metaphor. Guided by this space, we design eight freehand techniques for polyhedral face selection. A within-subjects study with 16 participants evaluates these techniques across polyhedral complexity (two radii; face counts 4, 6, and 12). The results identify three complementary top techniques, reveal tradeoffs between viewing choices and geometric preservation, and yield concrete guidelines for matching techniques to target geometry and task demands. A follow-up study with complex, realistic models confirms the robust ness and practical usability of the three techniques. Together, these contributions shift attention from whole object selection to precise component selection in VR and provide actionable methods for 3D modeling, assembly, and texturing.

title: Deep Learning-Based Rain Rate Classification Using 5G Cellular Network Signals
authors: Yanning Mao, Jian Wang, Fiallos Manuel, Soon-Thiam Khu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: In recent years, the frequency and intensity of extreme rainfall events have underscored the need for innovative technologies that provide rapid and accurate meteorological measurements. Given the high spatial and temporal variability of rainfall, there is an urgent demand for real-time monitoring methods with adequate spatiotemporal resolution. This study proposes a novel deep learning-based classifier model to estimate rainfall based on the signal variation of the 5G cellular network received by mobile phone terminals. The proposed method included four essential characteristics: (1) employing a 5G mobile phone to collect reference signal strength with the variation of rainfall; (2) record data at one second intervals using a 50 meters short distance as the signal transmission path; (3) meteorological variables were integrated to enhance classification accuracy across varying rainfall rates; and (4) optimizing the classifier model with the Whale Optimization Algorithm; Results showed that the binary classification task achieved an overall accuracy of 99.42% with a 0.1mm/min threshold. The multi class classification accuracy of the five levels rainfall intensity classification reached 98.54%, which is at least 2% higher than other studies in terms of accuracy. As a result, combining signal data with meteorological variables improved classification performance, where atmospheric pressure exhibited the most significant impact, followed by air humidity and temperature. This approach proposes a cost-effective solution for capturing uneven rainfall dynamics, particularly in remote areas lacking traditional meteorological stations. By enhancing the spatiotemporal resolution of rainfall data, the proposed method supports the development of robust weather prediction systems, achieving wide area refinement and fine-grained monitoring and bridging gaps in existing systems.

title: AdaEndoGS: an Adaptive Enlightening Model for Endoscopy Based on 3D Gaussian Splatting
authors: Fei Xia, Yiding Wen, Yuanfan Liu, Huanmei Guan, Fei Luo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Endoscopic reconstruction and rendering technology are crucial for minimally invasive diagnosis and treatment. Due to physical constraints such as limited light source placement and narrow operational space, endoscopic scenes often contain some dark regions that compromise clinical observation and diagnostic accuracy. To address this issue, we propose AdaEndoGS for endoscopic 3D reconstruction with physically consistent dark region illumination enhancement. Specifically, built upon 3D Gaussian Splatting, AdaEndoGS enriches each Gaussian with surface attributes including normal, roughness, and reflectance, thereby constructing an illuminatable 3D scene representation. AdaEndoGS automatically identifies the dark region via ray tracing, leveraging Gaussian opacity, surface normal, and viewpoint information to adaptively plan the placement of a virtual light source. Supervised by a carefully designed loss function incorporating multiple illumination-related terms, AdaEndoGS optimizes light source intensity and attenuation parameters to generate harmonious illumination enhancement in the dark regions, while avoiding overexposure in originally bright areas. We comprehensively evaluate our method on the public dataset C3VD and a novel endoscopy simulation dataset created by us in Unity3D, which includes paired low-lit and well-lit images to facilitate quantitative evaluation. Experimental results show that AdaEndoGS more accurately simulates light-matter interactions compared to existing methods. It significantly improves visual quality and enhances detail visibility, offering an effective technical solution for advancing endoscopic image-based reconstruction and rendering. Project repository: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://webstermorton.github.io/adaendogs-website</uri>.

title: Electrical Life Assessment of Hybrid Circuit Breakers Based on Segmented Electrical Wear Model
authors: Yunhong Zhou, Lei Xu, Kaiyi Wang, Chenbin Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Components, Packaging and Manufacturing Technology
abstract: With the widespread application of DC distribution systems, traditional mechanical DC circuit breakers are increasingly unable to meet high-performance requirements due to problems such as difficulty in arc extinguishing, limited electrical life, and slow response speed. Hybrid circuit breakers, which innovatively integrate mechanical switches with power electronic devices, possess stronger breaking capabilities and longer electrical life, and have become a research hotspot. Because existing electrical life evaluation methods are difficult to accurately apply to hybrid circuit breakers, a segmented electrical wear model is proposed in this article. This model is suitable for hybrid circuit breakers which are composed of molded case circuit breakers and electronic branches connected in parallel. The parameters of this model are determined through experiments, and the inhibitory effect of the power electronic branch on contact wear is quantitatively analyzed. The influence of IGBT intervention time on electrical life is revealed, enabling accurate evaluation of the electrical life of hybrid circuit breakers, and providing a theoretical basis for optimizing control strategies and improving service life of hybrid circuit breakers.

title: GLINS: GNSS-LiDAR-INS Integrated Navigation System
authors: Jiahui Liu, Cheng Chi, Xin Zhang, Binlin Zhang, Dongen Li, Xingqun Zhan, Marcelo H. Ang Ang
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Reliable navigation in complex urban environments is challenging due to frequent GNSS signal outages and multipath interference. A practical mitigation is to incorporate LiDAR-inertial odometry (LIO) to bound drift during GNSS gaps and maintain local accuracy. However, commonly adopted scan-to-map constraints may yield inconsistencies when global corrections conflict with a static map in a tightly coupled framework. To address these issues, this letter presents GLINS, a measurement-level tightly coupled GNSS-LiDAR-INS system that features a consistent estimator design with explicit landmark states. GLINS extracts stable landmarks from the LiDAR voxel map and treats them as explicit variables in the state estimator, allowing the map representation to co-evolve with global corrections. The proposed estimator is implemented as a keyframe-based sliding-window factor graph that tightly fuses raw GNSS pseudorange, carrier-phase, and Doppler measurements with IMU preintegration and LiDAR landmark factors. A robust GNSS module performs fault detection and exclusion and carrier-phase ambiguity resolution, further improving reliability. Experiments on a public benchmark and proprietary urban driving sequences demonstrate that GLINS achieves improved estimator consistency compared to representative baselines and delivers high-precision, drift-free trajectories across diverse urban scenarios.

title: A High-Order Fast Boundary Element Method as a Benchmarking Computational Framework for RCS Analysis of PEC Targets
authors: Omid Babazadeh, Emrah Sever, Ian Jeffrey, Constantine Sideris, Vladimir Okhmatovski
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Antennas and Propagation
abstract: A fast and accurate boundary element method framework for benchmarking the Radar Cross Section (RCS) computations for various Perfect Electric Conductor (PEC) targets, including sharp-edge models with electrically large sizes, is proposed. The approach employs a high-order Chebyshev-based Boundary Integral Equation (CBIE) formulation of the Magnetic Field Integral Equation (MFIE). Acceleration is achieved using both the H-matrix framework and distributed parallelism for filling inadmissible blocks, substantially improving computational efficiency in both time and memory usage. We refer to the resulting solver as H-CBIE and demonstrate its O(hp) error convergence, scalability, and accuracy through extensive numerical results, h being the mesh element size and p the order of discretization. Localized hp-refinement is incorporated for sharp-edge problems to maintain accuracy near edges and junctions. We include a comprehensive complexity analysis of the high-order accelerated framework, detailing the error-dependent wall time scaling for each stage of the solution process. The resulting scaling dependencies show that the total solution wall time grows quadratically in N with the target accuracy when high-order discretizations are used, whereas it rises exponentially for traditional low-order methods, underscoring the substantial efficiency gains of the proposed approach and its suitability for benchmarking.

title: Heuristic-Supervised-DRL: A Unified Optimization Framework With Convergence Analysis
authors: Wei Zhao, Kai Wang, Xiangyu Liu, Zhi Liu, Nei Kato
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: Many real-world problems involve hierarchical multi-objective optimization over coupled sequential decisions. A common strategy is to combine heuristic planning with deep reinforcement learning (DRL). While existing hybrid methods often show strong empirical performance, the coupled closed-loop learning dynamics between upper-tier planning and lower-tier control are typically analyzed only empirically, and explicit convergence guarantees for the integrated scheme remain limited. To address this gap, we propose a heuristic-supervised-DRL (HSD) framework that tightly couples (i) a heuristic planner for upper-tier decision-making, (ii) a DRL agent for lower-tier execution, and (iii) an online supervised predictor that serves as an adaptive bridge between planning and execution. The key novelty of HSD lies in this closed-loop architecture and its accompanying theoretical treatment. By formulating the coupled updates as a two-timescale stochastic approximation process, we show that, under standard conditions, the supervised predictor tracks a quasi-stationary regression target and the overall joint process converges almost surely to an asymptotically stable equilibrium. We further analyze robustness under approximate planning errors. As a case study, we instantiate HSD in a multi-UAV-assisted mobile edge computing system. Experimental results show that the proposed framework consistently outperforms representative baselines in key performance metrics, demonstrating both its practical effectiveness and its value as a principled framework for hierarchical optimization in dynamic environments.

title: Optimized multi-step hydroxide catalysis bonding method of an all-glass optical fiber coupler for space precision interferometry
authors: Guotao Pan, Xiaoqin Deng, Jianzhao Wang, Dongxue Zhang, Chi Sun, Wei Sha, Xudong Niu, Hongwei Lou, Heshan Liu, Ziren Luo
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Conventional optical fiber couplers with metal–glass structures cause additional optical path noise because of thermal expansion mismatch between different materials. This effect limits the stability of the ultra-stable optical measurement bench. An all-glass optical fiber coupler was developed for ultra-stable optical measurement benches used in precision interferometry for space gravitational-wave detection. An optimized multi-step hydroxide catalysis bonding method was proposed to improve bonding strength and stability over different bonding-surface error ranges. Two bonding solutions were used for different surface error conditions: NaOH:H₂O at a molar ratio of 1:300 for surface RMS ≤ 0.1 λ (λ = 632.8 nm), and NaOH:SiO₂:H₂O at a mass ratio of 14:27:59 for 0.5 λ ≤ RMS ≤ 0.66 λ. The maximum bonding strength of both formulations exceeded 10 MPa. Supported by theoretical analysis and finite-element simulation, the all-glass coupler was integrated and validated through ground-based testing to assess the stability of the proposed method. Experimental results showed that the spot centroid jitter was no more than 10 μm and the pointing drift was no more than 20 μrad during the bonding-stability test. Under thermal cycling conditions of 20±10°C, the spot drift stability reached 2.25 μrad/°C. System-level noise testing of the ultra-stable optical bench showed an approximately one order of magnitude improvement in noise stability. These results indicate that the proposed multi-step hydroxide catalysis bonding method effectively improved thermal stability and reduced bench noise, providing a viable fabrication solution for key components in high-stability interferometry.

title: A Side-viewing Fluorescence Needle Probe for Brain Biopsy Guidance
authors: Mingze Yang, Vasily Lebedev, Vasiliki Staikopoulos, Bryden C. Quirk, Sarah Saxon, Yunle Wei, Heike Ebendorff-Heidepriem, Amal Abou-Hamden, Adam Wells, Jiawen Li, Robert A. McLaughlin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biomedical Engineering
abstract: Objective: This study presents a fiber-optic needle probe to detect malignant tissue prior to removal from the brain. Methods: The probe comprises separate multimode optical fibers for excitation and detection of fluorescence. A higher numerical aperture was used in the collection fiber to provide a larger acceptance angle for fluorescence emission light. Using a dual-bore ferrule to simplify fabrication and ensure optical alignment, the fibers were angle-polished and silver-coated using Tollens' reagent to produce a side-facing needle probe, which was integrated into a 16-gauge needle (outer diameter 1.65 mm). Sensitivity of the probe was assessed using a tissue-mimicking fluorescent brain phantom, with optical scattering, absorption and fluorescence properties comparable to brain tissue. To demonstrate feasibility in a clinical environment, experiments were performed on fresh brain tissue obtained from patients undergoing surgical resection for glioblastoma. Results: When the tissue-mimicking fluorescent phantom was obscured by a thin layer of non-fluorescent material, sensitivity was found to be comparable to a free-space fluorescence surgical microscope. Fluorescence was detectable through an obscuring thickness of 0.4 mm of low absorption, non-fluorescent material, and 0.3 mm of high absorption, non-fluorescent material. When evaluated on fresh human brain tissue, the probe detected the characteristic fluorescence signal of protoporphyrin IX, with a stronger signal detected in tissue with an increased density of malignant cells. Conclusion: These findings show feasibility of using a dual-fiber fluorescence needle probe to identify malignant brain tissue. Significance: This work contributes to advancing the use of optical methods for surgical guidance.

title: Analysis and Design of Irregular-Mapped BICM-ID System with Non-Uniform Sources
authors: Chen Chen, Jiaofan Cai, Qiwang Chen, Jia Zhan, Yi Fang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: Bit-interleaved coded modulation with iterative decoding (BICM-ID) is a key technology enabling communication systems to realize high-throughput and high-reliability. In contrast to previous studies focused exclusively on uniformly distributed sources, this work investigates irregular mapping (IM)-based BICM-ID systems for sources with non-uniform distributions. First, a density evolution (DE) algorithm based on Gaussian weighted mixture approximation (GWMA) is put forward to estimate the convergence performance of the IM-BICM-ID systems with non-uniform sources, where the probability density function of the log-likelihood ratio messages output from the bit-level channels in this system is neither Gaussian nor symmetric. Then, by employing the GWMA-DE algorithm and the achievable system rates analysis, a design strategy for optimal IM labelings is established, which utilizes the inherent redundancy in non-uniform sources to achieve shaping gains. Furthermore, channel codes tailored to the proposed IM labelings are designed to further improve the bit error rate performance. Both analytical results and simulations indicate that the proposed labelings and codes outperform existing counterparts in IM-BICM-ID systems with non-uniform sources.

title: Practical Parameterized Query Optimization via Efficient Plan Reuse and List-wise Ranking
authors: Hai Lan, Yang Yu, Zhifeng Bao, Zi Huang, Yuwei Peng
