title: Actor–Critic-Based Dynamic Event-Triggered Control for Hypersonic Flight Vehicles
authors: Lei Shi, Xuesong Wang, C. L. Philip Chen, Maolong Lv, Yuhu Cheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: Hypersonic flight vehicles (HFVs) exhibit complex nonlinear dynamics and time-varying uncertainties, placing stringent demands on the adaptive learning capabilities of flight control systems. Meanwhile, the limited onboard communication and computational resources further require efficient resource utilization. To address these issues, this article proposes a dynamic event-triggered control (DETC) scheme based on actor–critic framework for HFVs, achieving synergistic optimization of control performance and resource conservation. First, an optimal event-triggered controller is developed within actor–critic framework to regulate the frequency of control input updates. A static event-triggered condition is formulated, and the stability of the closed-loop system is analyzed, with Zeno behavior eliminated, ensuring both stability and feasibility. Building on this, a dynamic variable is introduced, leading to a dynamic event-triggered condition that adaptively adjusts the trigger threshold based on real-time system states. Unlike static event-triggered mechanisms with fixed thresholds, this mechanism further optimizes the frequency of control input updates, achieving more efficient resource utilization while maintaining the desired control performance. Moreover, rigorous theoretical analysis demonstrates that the closed-loop system is stable and Zeno behavior is eliminated under the DETC scheme. Finally, the effectiveness of our proposed DETC scheme is verified through a simulation example.

title: Event-Based Adaptive Consensus Control of Multiple Flexible Manipulator Systems With Input-Output Constraints
authors: Wei Zhao, Xiangqian Yao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: The article investigates the vibration suppression and bipartite consensus control of networked flexible manipulators with input-output constraints. Neural networks (NNs) are utilized to address system uncertainties. The disturbance-like terms generated by backlash and input quantization decomposition, and the time-varying disturbance, are collectively regarded as disturbance terms, and their upper bounds are estimated using adaptive techniques. The integral barrier Lyapunov function (IBLF) is employed to ensure time-varying constraints on both boundary displacement and angle positions. Since the IBLF imposes constraints directly on the system states rather than on the errors, it relaxes the conservative constraints of the traditional BLF control on the state constraints. In addition, to implement input quantization, a hysteresis quantizer is introduced, and the quantized input is decomposed accordingly. An event-triggered mechanism with a relative threshold strategy is designed to reduce the controller update frequency and save network resources.

title: A Hybrid Dynamic Restart MPPT Algorithm with Voltage-Segmented Initialization Mechanism for Photovoltaic Power Optimizers
authors: Yanxuan Zheng, Yan Li, Zhihao Jiang, Xiaochun Fang
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: Under partial shading conditions (PSCs), the photovoltaic (PV) curves exhibit multi-peak characteristics, using power optimizers to extract maximum power of PV modules is crucial for safe PV generation. Although global maximum power point tracking (GMPPT) algorithms have achieved multi-peak optimization of the P-V curve, research has found that GMPPT algorithms triggered solely by power variation thresholds restart indiscriminately and frequently, causing additional transient oscillations under complex PSCs and even tracking failure. This issue is particularly pronounced in metaheuristic optimization-based MPPT algorithms. However, current GMPPT restart strategy rarely considers dynamic PSCs. This article proposes a hybrid dynamic restart MPPT algorithm with voltage-segmented initialization mechanism. A unified voltage-segmented sampling framework is adopted for both startup initialization and reinitialization. With adaptive scanning directions and termination criteria, the proposed algorithm identifies GMPP region under different PSCs. When irradiance changes, a steady-state conductance boundary is used to detect the change and trigger a restart, then the dynamic restart strategy adapts to reinitialization based on irradiance influence factor and current variation index. Simulation results show that the proposed algorithm improves transient power tracking smoothness by 10% compared with PSO-based algorithm, and the restart strategy increases the average dynamic tracking efficiency by 5% compared with the traditional power-threshold method. Experiments validate the effectiveness under multi-peak PSCs, achieving the average MPPT efficiency of 99.3%.

title: Content-Aware Gan for Selfsupervised Video Summarization
authors: V S Saiprashanth, Karri Dhruthi, G. Anitha
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Given that video content comprises 80 % of online data, it is imperative to develop scalable and efficient summarization methods. The most significant hurdle of video summarization is the expensive need for human-annotated data, and this paper proposes SAVS, a self-supervised adversarial video summarization framework using context-aware generative adversarial networks (GANs) to overcome the necessity of labeled data. SAVS employs latent sequence generator networks and temporalcontextual discriminator networks to learn discriminative frame representations that are semantically aligned and temporally consistent. The new loss-functions works in conjunction to include the clip consistency loss, hybrid feature refinement loss, and separation loss for making frame-importance predictions without any external priors and without any additional fine-tuning. Evaluating SAVS on the SumMe and TVSum benchmark datasets indicated it achieved supervised-level performance and surpassed and established a baseline to state-of-the-art unsupervised and weakly supervised baselines. The label-free and scalable features of SAVS makes it well-situated for real-world use, for example, in e-learning, video surveillance, indexing or for a multi-media application, such as YouTube or Netflix, needed to address the growing video content management phenomenon.

title: Adversarially-Refined VQ-GAN with Dense Motion Tokenization for Spatio-Temporal Heatmaps
authors: Gabriel Maldonado, Narges Rashvand, Armin Danesh Pazho, Ghazal Alinezhad Noghre, Vinit Katariya, Hamed Tabkhi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Continuous human motion understanding remains a core challenge in computer vision due to its high dimensionality and inherent redundancy. Efficient compression and representation are crucial for analyzing complex motion dynamics. In this work, we introduce an adversarially-refined VQ-GAN framework with dense motion tokenization for compressing spatio-temporal heatmaps while preserving the fine-grained traces of human motion. Our approach combines dense motion tokenization with adversarial refinement, which eliminates reconstruction artifacts like motion smearing and temporal misalignment observed in non-adversarial baselines. Our experiments on the CMU Panoptic dataset [7] provide conclusive evidence of our method’s superiority, outperforming the dVAE baseline by 9.31% SSIM and reducing temporal instability by 37.1%. Furthermore, our dense tokenization strategy enables a novel analysis of motion complexity, revealing that 2D motion can be optimally represented with a compact 128-token vocabulary, while 3D motion’s complexity demands a much larger 1024-token codebook for faithful reconstruction. These results establish practical deployment feasibility across diverse motion analysis applications. The code base for this work is available at https://github.com/TeCSAR-UNCC/Pose-Quantization.

title: Design Methods of Outphasing Transmitter Exciter for Lab Prototyping
authors: Nikolay S. Bekushev, Quoc Fung Ngo, Oleg V. Varlamov
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: The paper presents a prototype digital signal component separator (SCS), for a power amplifier, built using the outphasing technique. An analysis of the shortcomings of the previously developed analog-digital SCS is provided, which led to the selection of a fully digital implementation based on a personal computer and a multichannel sound card. Algorithms for generating amplitude-phase, amplitude, and frequency modulated signals are developed using a digital signal processor (DSP), which offers high flexibility in configuration and precise signal synthesis. The structure of both software and hardware components of the SCS is described, including the implementation of a Hilbert transformer, quadrature modulators, and the interface for communication with analog blocks. Experimental studies confirm a low level of nonlinear distortions (down to $\boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{7 0} \mathbf{~ d B}$) and showed the practical applicability of the proposed solution in the development of outphasing transmitters. The results demonstrate the potential of digital outphased signals generation techniques to enhance the efficiency and stability of outphasing radio transmitters.

title: Research on Intelligent Cancer Diagnosis via Raman Spectroscopy Using Convolutional Neural Networks and TimesNet Ensemble Learning
authors: Jingtian Hu, Xiang An, Shi Bai, Ying Ma
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: This study proposes a deep learning method that integrates with data preprocessing and ensemble learning to address challenges in early cancer diagnosis using Raman spectroscopy. The method includes Babbitt filter denoising, down sampling, normalization, and employs a multi-model comparison framework with convolutional neural networks, TimesNet, and ensemble learning. The ensemble learning model achieved a classification accuracy of 0.969 and a loss value of 0.272 on the test set. This approach effectively extracts deep biomarker information, enhances diagnostic accuracy, and advances early cancer screening technologies.

title: Adaptive von Mises–Fisher Likelihood Loss for Supervised Deep Time Series Hashing
authors: Juan Manuel Perez, Kevin Garcia, Brooklyn Berry, Dongjin Song, Yifeng Gao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Indexing time series by creating compact binary representations is a fundamental task in time series data mining. Recently, deep learning-based hashing methods have proven effective for indexing time series based on semantic meaning rather than just raw similarity. The purpose of deep hashing is to map samples with the same semantic meaning to identical binary hash codes, enabling more efficient search and retrieval. Unlike other supervised representation learning methods, supervised deep hashing requires a discretization step to convert real-valued representations into binary codes, but this can induce significant information loss. In this paper, we propose a von Mises–Fisher (vMF) hashing loss. The proposed deep hashing model maps data to an M-dimensional hyperspherical space to effectively reduce information loss and models each data class as points following distinct vMF distributions. The designed loss aims to maximize the separation between each modeled vMF distribution to provide a better way to maximize the margin between each semantically different data sample. Experimental results show that our method outperforms existing baselines. The implementation is publicly available at https://github.com/jmpq97/vmf-hashing

title: Reflection Coefficient for Wireless Applications in Reconfigurable Antennas on RT Duroid and FR4 Substrates
authors: Suresh Kumar Muthuvel, Suresh Subramanian
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: This study investigates the reflection coefficient and performance characteristics of concentric circle antennas fabricated on two different substrates—FR4 and RT Duroid—at a resonant frequency of 2.4 GHz. The antennas were simulated over a sweep range of frequency of 1.2 to 2.95 GHz to assess their reflection coefficients and overall performance. The concentric circle antenna, characterized by a circular patch design, is widely applicable in Dire ction of Arrival (DOA) systems, offering the advantage of 2D and 3D scanning capabilities with reduced element counts. Additionally, these antennas can provide omnidirectional radiation patterns, enabling 360-degree coverage, which is particularly valuable in modern wireless communications. The results demonstrate that the concentric circle antenna on the RT Duroid substrate exhibited a significantly higher reflection coefficient (-39.0948 dB) than the FR4 substrate, which showed a reflection coefficient of (-28.500 dB). These findings indicate that the RT Duroid substrate provides superior performance of impedance matching and signal reflection. At frequency of 2.45 GHz, the RT Duroid-based concentric circle antenna achieved a high gain of 7.59 dB, which further underscores its potential for efficient radiation and enhanced signal strength. Overall, the study highlights, when compared to FR4 substrate, the effectiveness of RT Duroid in improving antenna performance, good efficiency and low signal loss. Also the RT Duroid exhibits excellent reflection characteristics and high gain of 7.59dB, making it suitable for various wireless communication applications, particularly in DOA systems.

title: Phonocardiogram Signal Processing with Heart Sound Classification Using Transfer Learning
authors: Birendra Kumar Pal, Laxmi Rajani, Pragati Agrawal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Phonocardiogram (PCG) signals provide a low cost, non-invasive clinical means for early screening of cardiovascular disorders, especially in regions with limited diagnostic infrastructure. However, conventional manual auscultation suffers from significant subjectivity and variability between clinicians, while classical machine learning pipelines struggle are sensitive to noise, inter-patient variability, and limited annotated data. To overcome these limitations, this work proposes a deep learning-based heart sound classification architecture that integrates advanced preprocessing with transfer learning on time-frequency representations. The PhysioNet/CinC 2016 dataset, containing 3240 PCG recordings resampled to 2000 Hz, is used to evaluate the proposed approach. Raw PCG recordings undergo wavelet denoising and are transformed into Mel-spectrograms, enabling the extraction of discriminative temporal-spectral patterns. Six state-of-the-art pretrained convolutional neural networks VGG16, VGG19, ResNet50, InceptionV3, EfficientNetB0, and MobileNetV2 are fine tuned and benchmarked. Performance is assessed using accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, specificity, sensitivity, and AUROC metrics. Experimental results demonstrate that MobileNetV2 achieves the best trade-off between computational efficiency and model performance, reaching 97 % accuracy and high robustness against class imbalance and noise. The findings suggest that transfer learning applied to spectrogram-based PCG representations can provide clinically viable, scalable solutions suitable for embedded and mobile diagnostic systems. Future extensions include classyifying of multi-class murmur, domain adaptation, and integrating of multimodal biomedical signals.

title: Design and Implementation of a Machine Learning-Based Legal Drafting Assistant
authors: Aditya Pisal, Keshavsingh Thakur, Madhav Khurana, Vidya Dhamdhere
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: For millions of Indians in under-developed and under-privileged areas, legal services are difficult to access and this poses a serious obstacle to justice. In order to overcome this obstacle and to provide legal assistance to everyone, this paper introduces an AI-powered legal drafting assistant that uses machine learning models as well as natural language processing (NLP) models with multi-agent system. This system generates structured legal documents such as FIRs, notices and complaints, it retrieves relevant legal precedents, and classifies user queries into proper IPC sections using hybrid similarity scoring to verify the output quality. The system uses Sentence-BERT embeddings, Chroma Vector databases and Crew AI multi-agent architecture to achieve a validation pass for generated documents and an average IPC classification accuracy of minimum <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 5 \%}$</tex>.

title: A Method for Reducing the Dimensionality of Data Used for Machine Learning Using the Principal Component Analysis
authors: D. V. Gadasin, S. V. Shevelev, L. A. Tremasova, V. V. Elina
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: Despite the constant growth in computational capabilities of modern intelligent data analysis systems, the volume of information being processed grows exponentially and, in some cases, becomes a bottleneck in analytical processes. Consequently, the task of optimizing the dimensionality of the original information space without compromising the analytical value of the obtained results acquires critical importance. The principal component method (PCA) represents one of the most effective tools for solving the data dimensionality reduction problem. This research focuses on the application of this method in analyzing the characteristics of node elements in wireless sensor networks. The key advantages of the method are as follows: first, the method requires no prior assumptions about the nature of the investigated object; second, the linear structure and iterative nature of the algorithm ensure efficient computational implementation on modern computers. As the empirical foundation, we use a dataset characterizing the operational performance of a wireless sensor network comprising one thousand nodes. For each node, samples of observations are compiled for key Quality of Service (QoS) metrics that enable the assessment of the system’s operational characteristics. The research procedure consisted of sequential stages: preliminary cleaning and normalization of the raw observations; construction of the covariance matrix; computation of the eigenvalue spectrum and corresponding eigenvectors; and a linear mapping of the data into the principal component space. The principal components were obtained via an orthogonal transformation of the covariance matrix’s eigenvectors and were used as the basis for subsequent analysis and interpretation of the structure of feature variability. The practical effect of the approach is a substantial reduction of the feature space dimensionality while preserving the representativeness of the original data and the informativeness of the results.

title: Multimodal Transportation Model for Real-Time Prediction and Adaptive Optimization Using Advanced Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Ms. Sweta Kahurke, KTV Reddy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: As cities grow, traffic congestion increases, necessitating efficient and reliable multimodal transportation networks. Lack of real-time prediction and optimization hindered transportation management. Advanced machine learning is used to create a multimodal transportation model with real-time, high-accuracy forecasts and adaptive optimization. Start using Cross-Modal Joint Embedding Networks (CJEN) to merge data sources into a cohesive feature space. A network of ST-GCNs captures spatial and temporal dependency needed to characterize urban transport complexity. TCN and GRU enable robust shortterm forecasting across all transport modalities. We optimize dynamic routes based on real-time conditions using Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (DDPG) in a multi-agent reinforcement learning framework to maximize flexibility. Timestamp Varying Dynamic Bayesian Networks (TVDBNs) refine results and reduce travel timestamp and congestion forecasting uncertainty using probability-based forecasting. This complete approach improves forecast accuracy by 89 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 2 \%}$</tex>, optimizes journey durations by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5 - 2 0 \%}$</tex>, and decreases expenses by 30 % through transfer learning. We improve urban transportation by providing actionable and trustworthy predictions and adaptive route improvements for sustainable and efficient multimodal networks.

title: A Sleep Schedule-Pairing based Efficient Routing in WSNs
authors: Shashank Singh, Rohit Sharma, Bharti Sharma
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Energy efficiency is an open research challenge in Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs). Nodes are generally powered by battery and deployed in unfavorable settings; therefore, available energy has a direct impact on lifetime of the network in WSNs. Energy efficiency is the most important when designing routing protocols in WSNs. Our approach implements new routing mechanism that systematically deploys nodes in order to save energy, and therefore realizes an efficient data transmission with higher lifetime of the network. A node-node and node-group classifiers, which are defined according to the near nodes and sensing types of nodes is able to establish an efficient chain structure topology. The ability to toggle paired nodes between active and sleep states reduces redundant transmissions, thereby optimizing energy utilization. Furthermore, the traditional Prim's algorithm is utilized to forward data from active nodes to the base station, which reduces both transmission overhead and energy consumption. The simulation result of proposed scheme compared existing schemes i.e. IEEWSN and EBPT-CRA and it shows that our novel scheme is superior on various QoS parameters like in energy efficiency <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0}-\mathbf{1 5 \%}$</tex>, network lifespan <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 0}-\mathbf{2 5 \%}$</tex>, and stability improves by approximately 10%.

title: Explainable AI for Personalized Diagnosis and Treatment Planning in Multi-Modal Healthcare Data
authors: Raghu Dhumpati, M. Ayyappa Chakravarthi, G Bhavani, J Jesintha Princy, Arulananth T S, Subhashini Peneti
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Clinical decision-making increasingly relies on heterogeneous data from electronic health records, medical imaging, and clinical text. Yet many existing healthcare AI models fail to effectively integrate these modalities that remain explainable and clinically reliable. This limitation reduces trust, impairs calibration, and restricts the translation of predictive accuracy into actionable clinical benefit. This study aims to develop and evaluate an explainable multimodal AI system that can be used to determine personalized diagnosis and treatment plans. This can be done with simultaneous optimization of predictive efficiency, calibration, interpretability, and usefulness to the clinical profession. We conducted an experimental study, based on publicly available multimodal healthcare data that comprised of structured EHR data, medical images, and clinical text. A novel multimodal medical foundation model (MMFM-X) based on Perceiver-type fusion, biomedical knowledge-graph grounding, and intrinsic concept bottlenecks was created and contrasted with an EHR-only XGBoost base, and a hybrid cross-attention fusion framework. Discrimination, calibration, treatment ranking, explainability, and decision curves were used as measures to assess models and computational efficiency. MMFM-X fitted better (with an AUROC of 0.913 and AUPRC of 0.524) than the hybrid (0.864 / 0.391) and EHR-only (0.812 / 0.284) models. Calibration was also greatly enhanced, whereby there was an Expected Calibration Error of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.7 \%, 6.1 \%$</tex>, and 9.6 % respectively. MMFM-X (NDCG@5 0.712, Top-3 accuracy 0.84) had the highest personalized treatment planning performance, and decision curve analysis showed higher net clinical benefit (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx$</tex> 0.16) at the relevant thresholds. These results suggest that explainable multimodal foundation models are capable of providing more accurate, reliable, and clinically useful decision support compared to other methods. The proposed model provides a reliable AI pilot project in personalized diagnosis and treatment planning, in practical healthcare.

title: An Ensemble Learning Framework for Heart Disease Risk Stratification Using Population-Based Health Survey Data
authors: Amarjeet Raj, Vasudev Dehalwar, Deepak Singh Tomar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Cardiovascular disease (CVD) is still one of the main causes of death around the world, which means we need scalable and smart systems for early risk stratification. This study builds a high-performance machine learning framework using the 2023 Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System (BRFSS) dataset, which has a huge number of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 2 6, 3 5 3}$</tex> instances. A structured preprocessing and feature engineering pipeline was implemented to address significant class imbalance and ensure data integrity. We conducted a comprehensive benchmark of eight architectures, including traditional tree-based methods and advanced Transformer-based models like SAINT and FTTransformer. Experimental results demonstrate that the SoftVoting Ensemble and LightGBM achieve state-of-the-art discriminatory performance with a peak AUC of 0.8826. The Ensemble model demonstrated strong clinical utility by achieving a high precision of 80.00 % at a controlled recall level, reflecting a deliberate design choice to minimize false positives in large-scale population screening. Feature importance analysis via SHAP identified age, stroke history, and hypertension as the most influential predictors, aligning the model with established clinical pathophysiology. This framework establishes a reliable, lowcost proxy for heart disease risk assessment in resource-limited settings.

title: Basin of Attraction for Chaotic Oscillations of HVAC Centrifugal Chiller
authors: Susmita Adhikary, Sumit Bandopadhyay, Ambarnath Banerji, Ranabir Sen, Asis Mazumder, Priyabrata Adhikary
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)
abstract: This study explores regions in the parameter space of occupant density in a commercial space provided with the facility of a centrifugal chiller as the HVAC system and difference between its condensing and evaporating pressure, such that compressor motor of the HVAC unit for operating conditions set inside the identified region exhibits chaotic speed. The purpose of this study is therefore to avoid the identified parameter range or the basin of attraction for minimizing the chances of occurrence of chaotic speed fluctuations and thereby to maintain rated cooling capacity and consistent refrigerant flow throughout the operational hours in a commercial building ensuring energy saving. The study uses Gordon-Ng universal chiller model to explore bifurcation behavior of rotor speed for the compressor motor against variation in occupant density and difference between condensing and evaporating pressure of the chiller plant to obtain the information on avoidable range of the parameters.

title: Energy-Proportional Load Balancing for Sustainable AI Workload Performance in Cloud Infrastructure
authors: Kiran Bhat, Sowmya Chintakindi, Bharath Kumar Maganti
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: As AI technology continues to evolve in size and complexity, the number of inferences that AI services process is in the billions. This intensified the use of resources and energy consumption, leading to challenges in energy efficiency. Traditional load balancing algorithms focus on optimal performance rather than on energy profiles. The increase in demand for AI and cloud computing has led to an increase in energy consumption, creating an urgent need for intelligent energy management that adapts dynamically to workload intensity. Addressing this gap forms the core objective of the present study. This study compares four widely used load balancing algorithms and presents an Energy-Proportional Load Balancing (EPLB) algorithm that intelligently distributes AI inference tasks according to energy utilization patterns and workload intensity, achieving energy savings without degrading performance. Results demonstrate that on an average, for both sinusoidal and spiky workload types, EPLB consumed 41.6 kWh, significantly lower than Round Robin (83.08 kWh), Least Connections (82.04 kWh), Thermal-Aware (145.24 kWh), and Path-Based (78.95 kWh). This results in energy savings of 51-62% compared to most algorithms.

title: FDA-DETR: An Enhanced Algorithm for Object Detection in UAV Aerial Imagery
authors: Chenhao Song, Wen Ge
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: To address the challenges of blurred object boundaries, complex background interference, and low-resolution, hard-to-recognize small targets in UAV aerial images, this paper proposes an improved RT-DETR–based UAV object detection method, referred to as FDA-DETR. First, a new backbone network is constructed by incorporating the Cross Stage Partial Network (CSPNet) architecture with the Fourier Dynamic Aggregation Module (FDAM), enhancing the ability to model global contextual information during feature extraction. Second, Dynamic Tanh (DyT) is employed to replace Layer Normalization in the AIFI module. Finally, Adaptive Detail–Context Fusion (ADCF) is used in place of the original concatenation operation to improve multi-scale feature fusion. Experimental results demonstrate that FDA-DETR achieves a 2.2% improvement in mAP@0.5 compared with the RT-DETR-R18 baseline model.

title: Highly Sensitive Metamaterial-Inspired Biosensor Using Meander Split Ring Resonator
authors: Donald Dong Zhang, Youhong Tang, Samuel Drake, Shengjian Jammy Chen
publish: 2026 Australasian Symposium on Antennas (ASA)
abstract: A highly sensitive metamaterial-inspired biosensor based on a meander split ring resonator (SRR) is proposed. The proposed biosensor has high sensitivity by introducing a meander structure into the SRR, where a stronger electromagnetic (EM) field confinement is achieved. Experimental characterizations of the proposed biosensor using water-ethanol mixtures successfully validate the concept. This compact, simple and high-sensitivity microwave biosensor can provide pathways to applications such as microfluidic assays and multiplexed lab-on-a-chip read-out.

title: Urban Sound Classification for Smart City Noise Mitigation Using a Deep Learning Approach
authors: Ramisa Hossain, Md. Imam Uddin Forkan, Tasmia Binte Arefin, Mirza Raquib, Mohammed Minhaj Uddin, Meraz Ahmed Ohi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Sound pollution has emerged as an escalating challenge with rapid urbanization and industrialization, particularly in urban, suburban, and industrial areas. It contributes to adverse health issues and reduces the quality of life for millions in developing countries, where such problems often remain unaddressed due to the high cost of potential solutions. Existing noise monitoring systems are limited to measuring noise levels and can't trace the source of pollution, thereby hindering authorities from implementing targeted control measures. This study aims to develop an effective sound classification model for the accurate identification of sources, which also allows noise pollution analysis and strategic mitigation. The performances of Random Forest, DNN, LSTM, and CNN models were evaluated on the UrbanSound8K dataset, which contains ten urban sound classes. Among the tested models, CNN demonstrated superior performance, achieving 99.31 % training accuracy and 94.58 % validation accuracy. The CNN model was trained using melspectrogram inputs resized to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$128 \times 173$</tex> over 30 epochs with the Adam optimizer. This study introduces a CNN framework enhanced by melspectrogram preprocessing and tailored training procedures, delivering a dependable and adaptable method for classifying urban sounds. The approach supports real-time noise monitoring and provides a foundation for effective noise reduction strategies in smart city development.

title: Spiking Neural Network Based Framework for Breast Cancer Detection
authors: Juhi Faridi, Mohd. Hasan, Omar Farooq
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Breast cancer classification problem has been extensively studied with machine learning and deep learning models. However, existing solutions with competitive accuracies rely on computationally intensive models, limiting their feasibility for energy-efficient <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$h$</tex> ardware d eployment. T his w ork proposes a single-layer Spiking Neural Network (SNN) with approximately 3000 parameters, trained using surrogate gradient-based Backpropagation Through Time (BPTT) for breast cancer classification. T he p roposed m odel a chieves 99.11 % classification accuracy, surpassing recent state-of-the-art methods, while remaining lightweight and suitable for hardware deployment. This demonstrates the potential of SNNs as a biologically realistic alternative for breast cancer detection.

title: Design and Performance Optimization of an Infrasonic Recording System for Elephant Call Detection
authors: Guruprasath Jeevajothi, Sanjoy Deb, Sannasi Chakravarthy S R, Palanisamy G, Srinivasan S, Thambiran T S
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Many animals, such as elephants, whales, crocodiles, big cats like tigers and lions, use infrasonic sound to communicate. This low-frequency channel is mostly invisible to human senses. Studying this range helps us understand animal behavior and tackles the increasing issue of human-wildlife conflict. However, current infrasonic recording systems tend to be costly and are not meant for ecological studies. This limits their use in conservation efforts. This paper introduces a new, low-cost infrasonic recording system that turns a geophone into a microphone. It includes special amplification, filtering circuits, and software modules for signal analysis. The system was tested with indoor trials using HG-24HS and HG-07 sensor setups. The HG-24HS geophone has shown the best results for sensitivity, signal-to-noise ratio, and classification accuracy. Signal processing has been enhanced with Kalman filtering, the Enhanced Empirical Denoising Method (EEDM), and machine learning algorithms. A Support Vector Machine classifier attained superior Accuracy and Precision in separating infrasonic calls from background noise. Field validation at Theppakadu Elephant Camp proved that the system could successfully record elephant infrasonic signals in controlled tests. Analyzing these recordings demonstrated clear communication patterns and reduced false detections. The results indicate that this system offers an affordable, portable, and scalable option for wildlife monitoring. Besides contributing to behavioral research, it has practical uses in early warning systems for reducing human-elephant conflicts and opens new paths for bioacoustics research in low-frequency communication.

title: A Cloud-based Hybrid Learning System for Remote Monitoring and Optimization of E-Waste Recycling Operations
authors: Nidhi Srivastav, Saikumari N, S. Pragadeeswaran, Gayatri Niphadkar, Dinesh S, Monika Gulati
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Due to shorter product lifecycles and rising demand, e-waste, particularly WEEE, is the fastest-growing solid waste category globally, growing 3-5% yearly. Despite international limitations, illegal trading and informal recycling still take place, particularly in low-income neighbourhoods where people dispose of hazardous rubbish for profit. To get over these problems, this research proposes a system that uses the Trans-CNBiGRU paradigm to remotely monitor e-waste recycling activities. Data cleansing, feature extraction, and model training are all part of the process. After data normalisation guarantees quality, dimensionality is reduced using Principal Component Analysis (PCA). This results in a two-dimensional fusion vector that contains information at the level of both sentences and keywords. Instead, then relying on word embedding and position encoding, Trans-CNBiGRU utilises a CNN-BiGRU architecture to capture latent properties and relative positioning. The proposed model achieves a classification accuracy of 95.81%, which is higher than current techniques, according to the experimental results. Ethical e-waste recycling, detection of unauthorised disposal, and short-term remote monitoring are all aided by high accuracy. By using the model, operational control and monitoring can be enhanced in global e-waste recycling systems.

title: Microgrid Zero-Game Frequency Control Based on Integral Reinforcement Learning
authors: Desheng Ling, Weiliang Hong, Xuesong Tu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: The fluctuating nature of renewable generation together with uncertain demand makes microgrid frequency restoration increasingly challenging and calls for control policies with strong robustness. To enhance regulation capability, this study proposes an online adaptive frequency-control framework that explicitly coordinates governor action with an energy storage system. The microgrid frequency dynamics in the presence of external disturbances are formulated as a zero-sum differential game, which enables the synthesis of a robust optimal policy. In addition, to cope with operating-condition-dependent parameter variations, we develop an improved integral reinforcement learning scheme where a critic network is updated online to approximate the value function and refine the policy without relying on an accurate plant model. Numerical studies demonstrate that the proposed controller suppresses frequency deviations effectively and maintains favorable robustness and adaptability under combined disturbances and parametric uncertainties when compared with representative baselines.

title: Two-Layer Hierarchical Predictive Control of a Fuel Cell–Battery Hybrid System for Electric-Vehicle Fast-Charging Applications
authors: Masood Shahverdi, Jacob Rangel, Jinalben Chaudhary, Ayaan Gulwani
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: This paper develops a hierarchical predictive control framework for a fuel cell–battery hybrid system supplying electric vehicle (EV) fast-charging demand. The proposed two-layer strategy integrates long-term planning with short-term adaptation to minimize hydrogen consumption while mitigating battery degradation. The upper layer employs dynamic programming over a six-hour predicted load horizon to evaluate the impact of forecast-driven operation, and the framework is designed to be extendable to longer horizons—such as daily or weekly scheduling—in future work. The lower layer refines decisions in real time over a 30-minute window, minimizing hydrogen consumption while meeting state-of-charge (SOC) targets. Simulation results show that the framework reduces hydrogen cost by approximately 5% compared with a baseline fuel-cell on/off strategy, while maintaining smoother SOC trajectories and alleviating battery-cycling stress. With forecasting integration now completed, future work will focus on enhancing prediction accuracy in both layers and validating the controller through hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) testing.

title: Ensemble Learning and Behavioral Analysis for Advanced Cybersecurity in Evolving Digital and IoT Ecosystems
authors: Ketan Rathor, Sujit Kumar Sadhukhan, Mohammad Shahid Kamal, Prasanthi Vallurupalli, Manu Vasudevan Unni, Gourav Kalra
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The exponential growth of digital and IoT ecosystems has made cybersecurity an urgent matter of concern. Despite the many advantages, there are also serious security risks associated with the coming together of cloud computing, artificial intelligence, and networked gadgets. Operations are disrupted, sensitive data is compromised, and user trust is eroded by threats including data breaches, ransomware, and vulnerabilities targeting the IoT. The protection of these intricate digital infrastructures is becoming more difficult as the attack surface is being widened by the increasing number of IoT devices. This work addresses these challenges by assessing scalar and normalization function preprocessing procedures and applying a univariate chi-squared test for effective feature selection. They provide an EML strategy that combines the best features of EML with static analysis to tackle cybersecurity issues in the IoT ecosystem. This approach takes advantage of a large feature space to improve cybersecurity systems' detection capabilities. The experimental results show that the suggested method successfully identifies threats in large-scale, dynamic digital and IoT contexts with detection accuracies ranging from 97.3% to 99%. These results show that sophisticated machine learning methods may greatly enhance cybersecurity resilience and performance, making them crucial for protecting contemporary digital and IoT ecosystems.

title: Hybrid LSTM-CNN Model for Real-Time Network Intrusion Detection in Cybersecurity
authors: Sajiv G, N. Meenakshisundaram
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The escalating complexity and increasing number of cyber threats render conventional intrusion detection approaches insufficient for delivering real-time and precise protection. This study presents a hybrid deep learning model that integrates Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks with Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) for real-time intrusion detection. LSTM networks are effective in capturing sequential dependencies and temporal patterns in network traffic, making them well-suited for detecting spatiotemporal anomalies. CNNs, on the other hand, excel at recognizing spatial characteristics and distinctive attack signatures. By combining these complementary strengths, the proposed LSTM-CNN model ensures efficient detection in real-time environments. Experimental results demonstrate that the hybrid model consistently outperforms traditional intrusion detection methods with respect to detection accuracy, false positive rate, and processing efficiency. This research underscores the potential of deep learning-based hybrid architectures to enhance the accuracy, interpretability, and responsiveness of modern intrusion detection systems, thereby offering a promising direction for proactive and reliable cybersecurity defence.

title: Ultra-Wideband Metal-Dielectric Additively Manufactured Planar Slot-Coupled Antenna Array
authors: Zhichao Sun, Xiaojing Lv, Yang Yang
publish: 2026 Australasian Symposium on Antennas (ASA)
abstract: This paper proposed a novel ultra-wideband (UWB) metal-dielectric additively manufactured planar slot-coupled antenna array for millimeter-wave (mm-Wave) applications. Different from conventional multilayer PCB-based antenna arrays, this design integrates a novel metal-dielectric additive manufacturing approach in which the exponential-tapered radiation part is co-printed layer-by-layer together with the dielectric substrate. A separate feeding network is implemented on a PCB substrate and couples to the radiation part, ensuring high radiation efficiency. The simulated results of the 8×8 array demonstrate a −10 dB impedance bandwidth (IMBW) of 82.1% from 16.3 GHz to 39 GHz with a peak gain of 24.05 dBi. By integrating conventional PCB fabrication with advanced additive manufacturing techniques, it enables structural customization and enhanced performance, paving a new path toward next-generation high-performance mm-Wave applications.

title: Heterogeneous Time Series Anomaly Detection in Cyber-Physical Systems with VAEM-LSTMs
authors: Lekha Patel, Shamina Hossain-McKenzie
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Anomaly detection in Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) is challenging due to the heterogeneous nature of the data, which includes count variables (e.g., packet counts), continuous measurements (e.g., voltages), and complex temporal dependencies. Existing deep learning approaches often apply uniform Gaussian assumptions across all features, leading to poor modeling of count data and inadequate capturing of inter-feature relationships. We propose VAEM-LSTM, a novel two-stage architecture that extends Variational Autoencoders for Mixed data (VAEM) with Long Short-Term Memory networks to address these challenges. Our approach uses appropriate statistical distributions for different data types including, Poisson/Negative Binomial for count data and Gaussian for continuous measurements, while capturing temporal dependencies through LSTM layers. We evaluate our method on a real-world CPS dataset containing both network traffic metrics and power system measurements under normal operations and three types of attacks (cyber, physical, and cyber-physical). Results demonstrate that overall, VAEM-LSTM achieves superior anomaly detection performance that outperforms traditional approaches that assume homogeneous distributions.

title: Reducing Exposure Bias in Deep Learning Models for GPU Temperature Prediction in Data Centers
authors: Mark Fullton, Xiaojun Ruan, Xunfei Jiang, Mahdi Ebrahimi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Dynamic thermal-aware load balancing addresses rising data center cooling costs by relying on accurate GPU temperature prediction. Due to the complexity of GPU temperature, deep learning models are widely used, but exposure bias–differences between training and inference environments–often degrades performance in autoregressive settings. While methods like scheduled sampling and attention forcing have been proposed to mitigate exposure bias, their effectiveness for hardware temperature forecasting is not well understood. This study systematically evaluates these mitigation techniques along with patching and modern deep learning architectures for GPU temperature prediction, considering accuracy, robustness, speed, and energy efficiency. Results show that scheduled sampling significantly worsens autoregressive RMSE and exposure bias (p < 0.001) for CNN-BiLSTM and Transformer models, whereas scheduled attention forcing reduces exposure bias but increases RMSE. The classic Transformer achieved the best overall balance with an AR RMSE of 4.34, median exposure bias of 2.4, median inference time of 257 µs, and the lowest training power usage (0.42 kWh), resulting in 22–84% lower carbon emissions. Patch-based models like PatchTST excelled in non-autoregressive settings but only the smallest patch sizes approached the Transformer’s autoregressive robustness at higher computational cost.

title: Optimizing Resource Utilization through Virtualization Approaches in Cyber-Physical Systems
authors: G Nagaleela, V.S.N. Murthy, D Arthur Jebastine Sunderraj, Gulipalli Venkata Pradeep Kumar, Lakshmeelavanya Alluri, Bendalam Vijaya
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: In today's computing environments, virtualisation technologies are essential for improving performance, scalability, and reliability, especially for important systems that need to be available all the time. This study assesses and contrasts Hypervisor, Container, and Para-virtualization methodologies by employing performance indicators, including average CPU utilisation, response time, fault recovery time, and system uptime, under simulated workloads and fault scenarios. Experimental outcomes demonstrate that Containers regularly surpassed alternative techniques, attaining the lowest average CPU utilisation (45.6%), the swiftest reaction times (152 ms), and the highest uptime (98.9%) during network failures. Para-virtualization had a balanced performance with modest resource use. Hypervisors, on the other hand, had slightly higher resource overhead and longer recovery, but they did provide strong isolation. The results show the trade-offs between efficiency, fault tolerance, and system resilience. This information can help to choose the best virtualisation solutions for specific needs in high-performance and fault-sensitive contexts.

title: Enhancing 3D Semi-Supervised Teeth Segmentation with Voxel Entropy-Guided Self-Learning
authors: Elbadry Elbadry, Mahmoud Gamal, Marwa Baraka, Marwan Torki
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Teeth segmentation is crucial for the analysis of maxillofacial diseases, with Cone Beam Computed Tomography (CBCT) scans offering superior 3D imaging compared to conventional Computed Tomography (CT). We propose a semi-supervised framework based on a multi-iterative self-learning approach, introducing a novel ranking technique for pseudo-label selection and a post-processing algorithm to eliminate false positives and ensure mask consistency. Pseudo-label ranking is guided by average voxel-level entropy, which quantifies uncertainty between foreground and background classes. High-confidence labels are then selected to refine training in subsequent iterations. Our post-processing algorithm enhances segmentation fidelity by identifying connected components, retaining the largest within a defined radius, and filtering out extraneous voxels. Additionally, expert-refined pseudo-labels are incorporated to assess their impact on performance. To further validate our approach, we established a baseline model and conducted a comprehensive evaluation on both the private test set of the STS-3D Challenge and our self-collected out-of-set data. The framework employs nnUNetV2 with the ResEnc M preset, optimizing feature extraction and dataset adaptation. Our approach achieves a Dice score of 0.8244 and an IoU of 0.851 on the private test set of the STS-3D Challenge, outperforming previous published methods on the same benchmark and demonstrating the effectiveness of our semi-supervised teeth segmentation strategy.

title: Oilfield Multi-Scenario Energy Storage Optimization: A Method Based on Improved Genetic Algorithm
authors: Xinyu Zhang, Yanduo Xia, Yuhui Lu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: This paper introduces an improved genetic algorithm tailored for optimizing capacity configuration and operational strategies in oilfield energy storage systems across multiple scenarios. By integrating adaptive crossover probabilities and diversity-threshold-based mutation adjustments, the method effectively tackles issues such as premature convergence and suboptimal solutions in complex environments involving oil price fluctuations, peak demands, and extreme weather conditions. The optimization framework employs a weighted multi-objective approach (weights: 0.7 cost, 0.2 efficiency, 0.1 reliability) to minimize total costs while improving efficiency and system reliability. Comparative experiments against standard GA and PSO, utilizing simulated datasets, reveal notable advancements: average cost reductions of 17-21%, efficiency improvements of 4-6%, and accelerated convergence rates. Contributions encompass a versatile optimization architecture, detailed sensitivity analyses, and practical guidelines for hybrid renewable-storage deployments in oilfields. The approach not only addresses current limitations in energy management but also paves the way for sustainable practices in remote industrial settings. Potential extensions include incorporating predictive analytics for real-time adaptations.

title: Fractional Programming Based Optimization within ISAC Frameworks Leveraging Intelligent Reflecting Surface Technology
authors: Xinyi Guo, Liping Luo
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: This research addresses the collaborative design of active and passive beams for Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC), which incorporates a Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (RIS). To maximize the user sum-rate, we formulate an optimization problem under radar Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR), Base Station (BS) power, and RIS constant modulus constraints. Addressing the prohibitive complexity of existing methods, we propose an efficient fractional programming (FP)based joint optimization scheme. To circumvent non-convexity, a synergistic framework integrating FP, Riemannian Conjugate Gradient (RCG), Lagrange Multiplier Method (LMM), and Penalty Function Method (PFM) is developed for an alternating iterative solution, effectively managing unit-modulus constraints and variable coupling. Numerical evidence indicates that our suggested framework surpasses conventional baselines in total transmission rate while minimizing processing cost. These findings offer a robust resource allocation solution for future RIS-enhanced ISAC networks.

title: Landscape Architecture Virtual Design: An Interactive Aesthetic Optimization System Based on ControlNet Conditional Generation Model
authors: Kaijie Luan, Mingqi Yang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: This paper presents an innovative interactive virtual design and aesthetic optimization system for landscape architecture, leveraging the ControlNet conditional generation model integrated with Stable Diffusion. The system enables users to generate high-fidelity landscape designs from simple inputs such as sketches or text prompts, incorporating depth maps and semantic labels for precise control. By fusing multiple conditions through a lightweight attention layer, the model ensures enhanced detail and coherence in generated images, addressing limitations in traditional design processes. Experimental evaluations on a diverse landscape dataset demonstrate superior performance, with PSNR values exceeding 29 and SSIM above 0.88, indicating high visual quality and structural similarity. Ablation studies confirm the efficacy of hybrid fusion and attention mechanisms in improving generation accuracy and aesthetic scores. The system supports iterative optimization, allowing real-time user feedback integration, which enhances sustainability and user satisfaction in urban green space planning. This approach not only accelerates design iterations but also promotes creative exploration, making it a valuable tool for landscape architects in achieving sustainable and aesthetically pleasing environments. Furthermore, the integration of advanced AI techniques allows for the simulation of environmental impacts, such as biodiversity enhancement and carbon sequestration potential, directly within the design workflow. This added layer of analysis empowers designers to make data-driven decisions that align with global sustainability goals, like the UN Sustainable Development Goals. By incorporating user preferences through interactive loops, the system reduces design revisions by up to 30%, as evidenced by preliminary user studies conducted with 50 landscape professionals. Overall, this framework bridges the gap between computational efficiency and artistic intuition, fostering a new paradigm in landscape architecture where technology amplifies human creativity rather than replacing it.

title: Adiabatic Quantum Computing and Global Solution of NP-Hard QUBO Problems: A General Approach
authors: Sanjay Sharma, Ravi Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: QUBO problems are NP-hard and have wide applications in optimization, including finance, logistics, machine learning, and combinatorial problems. However, classical algorithms employed for solving them can often get stuck in local minima. This paper presents an Adiabatic Quantum Computing (AQC) approach towards finding an optimal solution for QUBO problems. The QUBO problem has been first encoded into Hamiltonians. The adiabatic theorem has been used to achieve success of an AQC solution with a rate which is found to be dependent on relative gap between the ground state and first exited state of the Hamiltonian.

title: Bio-Cybersecurity with AI
authors: Samit S. Shivadekar
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: A study on bio-cybersecurity is augmented by an artificial intelligence (AI) methodology. This study advances the field of bio-cybersecurity by integrating artificial intelligence (AI) methodologies into an innovative defense framework that combines Community Search Resilience–Graph Neural Network (CSR-GNN) with Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL). This pioneering approach is specifically engineered to protect interconnected healthcare systems from the rising threats of cyber-bio attacks. The CSR-GNN enhances system resilience by accurately detecting adversarial perturbations, identifying targeted nodes, and restoring compromised data, which significantly strengthens the reliability of security analytics in hostile environments. Additionally, the refined datasets produced by CSR-GNN enable the DRL agent to develop optimal, adaptive defense strategies that dynamically respond to evolving threats. This integrated methodology addresses the shortcomings of traditional AI-based cybersecurity, which often overlook the complexities inherent in biological systems and rely on basic bioinformatics. As cyber–physical–social–biological systems become increasingly complex and interconnected, our CSR-GNN + DRL architecture stands out as a robust, scalable, and adaptive solution. It is meticulously designed to safeguard critical biosciences, biotechnologies, and biomanufacturing infrastructures from sophisticated cyber-bio attacks. By ensuring continuous and reliable operation within smart healthcare ecosystems, this framework is not merely a defensive tool; it is an essential investment in the future of secure healthcare technology. Embracing this innovative approach will protect the integrity of our vital systems in a rapidly evolving digital landscape.

title: Grid-Responsive Control of Aggregated Smart Loads in the Emerging Power Ecosystem
authors: V S K V Harish, Avijit Routh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)
abstract: The transformation of modern power systems, characterized by high penetration of variable renewable energy sources and declining synchronous inertia, necessitates robust demand-side frequency regulation frameworks. This paper presents a digitalization-driven control strategy leveraging aggregated inverter air-conditioners (IACs) integrated with a single-area reheat steam turbine power system (IAC–SAPS). A generalized small-signal model is formulated that explicitly incorporates nonlinear thermal dynamics, ±30% parametric uncertainties in turbine–governor and load parameters, and communication latency represented through Padé approximation. Structured singular value analysis (μ-analysis) is employed to quantify robust stability margins, and the most destabilizing parameter realization is identified as the worst-case system. Balanced truncation is subsequently used to derive a fourth-order reduced-order worst-case model that preserves dominant oscillatory modes while ensuring computational tractability for controller design. A robust proportional–integral (PI) controller is synthesized for the reduced-order worst-case model by solving a linear–quadratic cost function that penalizes both frequency deviation energy and control effort. The optimized gains are demonstrated to relocate eigenvalues leftward in the complex plane, improving damping ratios and guaranteeing phase/gain margins under delay and uncertainty. Comparative validation against benchmark TCL-based control frameworks highlights the superior resilience of the proposed design, with frequency nadir improved to −1.23 Hz and settling time reduced to ≈399 s. Simulation results confirm that robust, delay-aware PI control of aggregated IACs can significantly enhance transient stability and provide synthetic inertia, establishing demand-side flexibility as a reliable ancillary service in low-inertia grids.

title: Analyzing Code Injection Attacks on LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems in Software Development
authors: Brian Bowers, Smita Khapre, Jugal Kalita
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Agentic AI and Multi-Agent Systems are poised to dominate industry and society imminently. Powered by goal-driven autonomy, they represent a powerful form of generative AI, marking a transition from reactive content generation into proactive multitasking capabilities. As an exemplar, we propose an architecture of a multi-agent system for the implementation phase of the software engineering process. We also present a comprehensive threat model for the proposed system. We demonstrate that while such systems can generate code quite accurately, they are vulnerable to attacks, including code injection. Due to their autonomous design and lack of humans in the loop, these systems cannot identify and respond to attacks by themselves. This paper analyzes the vulnerability of multi-agent systems and concludes that the coder-reviewer-tester architecture is more resilient than both the coder and coder-tester architectures, but is less efficient at writing code. We find that by adding a security analysis agent, we mitigate the loss in efficiency while achieving even better resiliency. We conclude by demonstrating that the security analysis agent is vulnerable to advanced code injection attacks, showing that embedding poisonous few-shot examples in the injected code can increase the attack success rate from 0% to 71.95%.

title: Extending Quantum Annealing to Continuous Domains: a Hybrid Method for Quadratic Programming
authors: Hristo Djidjev
publish: ACM Transactions on Quantum Computing
abstract: None

title: Performance Analysis of Combined PD-SCMA System
authors: Julia Timoschuk, Taoufik Ben Rejeb
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: This paper investigates the integration of two distinct non-orthogonal multiple access paradigms: power-domain NOMA and sparse code multiple access. The respective system models for both techniques are developed, and a combined PD-SCMA system model is proposed with the aim of improving spectral efficiency and enhancing flexibility in radio resource allocation. Particular attention is paid to performance analysis issues arising from the joint application of these technologies, including the increased complexity of reception and multiuser detection algorithms, optimal power and codebook allocation, as well as the impact of channel estimation errors and inter-user interference. The main limitations of the combined approach are discussed, and potential directions for further research on non-orthogonal multiple access techniques for next-generation wireless communication systems are outlined.

title: A Machine Learning Biosignal Monitoring System for Pre-Anaphylactic Risk Prediction and Autonomous Intervention
authors: Pranali Chavhan, Vijayshri Khedkar, Sameera Jape, Sanchita Desai, Ramya Purohit, Sarvesh Rathod
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Severe allergic reactions, including anaphylaxis, can escalate in a matter of minutes and often occur so rapidly that the administration of lifesaving epinephrine may be impossible. This work describes a machine learning-driven biosignal monitoring approach for the prediction of pre-anaphylactic risk before critical deterioration sets in. The method relies on a minimal set of physiological indicators-heart rate, blood oxygen saturation, and accelerometer-based motion data-to detect subtle changes associated with the early stages of anaphylaxis. An XGBoost classifier forms the core predictive component, enabling reliable risk estimation even in low-resource or continuouswear scenarios. When elevated risk is detected, the framework can initiate automated actions such as issuing alerts, recording events for model refinement, and supporting the feasibility of controlled microdose epinephrine delivery along with emergency notifications. Experiments using simulated datasets demonstrate sensitivity above 90 percent, showing that machine learningenabled physiological monitoring can provide timely, low-cost, and proactive support for individuals who are at risk of anaphylactic episodes.

title: Computational Identification of Differentially Expressed Genes for Biomarker Discovery in Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis
authors: Sanskriti Yadav, Pravir Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This study aims to find out the transcriptomic landscape of ALS and identify biomarker candidates through a comprehensive in silico analysis. We used the Gene Expression Omnibus (GEO) microarray dataset GSE10953. DEGs analysis on GEO2R. This identified 1216 DEGs, including 613 upregulated and 603 downregulated genes. These DEGs are involved in biological processes, neuroinflammation, immune response, protein degradation, and epigenetic regulation. Protein-protein interaction networks were created using the STRING database in order to comprehend the connections between these genes. Topological analysis of these networks pinpointed 5 key hub genes. These hub gene findings present a distinct molecular signature of ALS. The present study thus differed from earlier transcriptomic studies, focused mainly on DEGs identification, by incorporating differential expression profiling with protein-protein interaction network topology to prioritize regulatory hub genes. The identification of epigenetic and ribosomal-associated hubs thus offers novel mechanistic insights into ALS pathogenesis and highlights potential biomarkers for further experimental validation.

title: Semantics-Preserving Contrastive Representation Learning for Encrypted Traffic Classification
authors: Yihao Chen, Shihao Peng, Jinchuan Liu, Zehua Zhang, Daojing He
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: As network traffic encryption becomes increasingly prevalent, traditional content-based traffic classification methods face severe challenges. Self-supervised learning offers a new paradigm for learning general representations from unlabeled traffic, yet existing methods still exhibit significant shortcomings. First, data augmentation strategies are often directly transferred from other domains, which disrupts the inherent temporal logic and protocol structure of network traffic, making it difficult to generate semantically consistent positive and negative sample pairs. Second, existing contrastive learning frameworks are insufficiently adaptive to network dynamics, often treating transport-layer fluctuations as noise to be suppressed, resulting in learned representations that are sensitive to network environmental changes and have limited generalization capabilities. To address these issues, this paper proposes a self-supervised learning framework based on the TCP reliable transmission mechanism, aiming to learn semantically preserved general representations from the intrinsic dynamics of traffic. The framework first designs a domain-consistent data augmentation paradigm by algorithmically simulating TCP timeout retransmission and fast retransmission mechanisms to generate traffic variants that preserve the original semantics, thereby constructing high-quality positive sample pairs. On this basis, a general representation learning framework based on momentum contrast is constructed, leveraging dynamic negative sample queues and a momentum encoder to perform pre-training on large-scale unlabeled traffic, efficiently learning discriminative features that are resistant to network fluctuations and focused on application-layer semantics. Experiments show that, after fine-tuning, our method outperforms other state-of-the-art network traffic classification methods on multiple encrypted traffic classification benchmark datasets, validating the effectiveness of the proposed augmentation strategy and learning framework.

title: A Cooperative Steering Control Strategy for Human–Machine Co-Driving Based on Stackelberg Game and Reinforcement Learning
authors: Yisheng An, Haijing Ning, Herong Zhu, Yaxin Wei, Naiqi Wu, Yang Bai, Xiangmo Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: Human–machine co-driving is expected to be a long-term driving mode. However, existing cooperative steering control strategies often struggle to effectively handle human–machine interaction conflicts and dynamically allocate driving authority, making it difficult to balance safety and driver’s comfort. To address these issues, this article establishes a Stackelberg game-based model predictive control (MPC) framework and derives a human–machine optimal control strategy under the equilibrium conditions. Furthermore, a two-layer adaptive authority allocation model is developed using the deep deterministic policy gradient (DDPG) method, which comprehensively considers environmental risks, human–machine conflict, and driver’s states. This model prevents a vehicle from entering an unstable state by dynamically allocating the driving authority to both the human driver and the autonomous driving system. Results from driver-in-the-loop experiments indicate that, in obstacle avoidance scenarios, the proposed control strategy enhances vehicle driving stability by 31.49%, improves control stability by 48.11%, and reduces the driver’s burden by 42.87%, demonstrating that the proposed strategy can assist the driver in completing obstacle avoidance tasks and ensure driving safety in high-risk scenarios. In low-risk scenarios, it maintains the driver’s freedom, prevents excessive intervention from the autonomous driving system that could lead to significant human–machine conflicts, and alleviates the driver’s operational burden.

title: Learning-Based Robust Jamming Suppression with Movable Antenna Arrays
authors: Jing Su, Kaiquan Cai, Jingjing Zhao, Quan Zhou, Xidong Mu
publish: IEEE Communications Letters
abstract: This letter investigates movable antenna arrays for jamming suppression in a multi-jammer scenario. We formulate the problem as maximizing the beamforming gain of the legitimate signal and null-steering the jamming signals. Due to the non-convexity of the optimization problem and the involvement of complex exponential functions, the problem is analytically challenging. Additionally, obtaining accurate jamming angles of arrival (AoAs) is difficult in practice. To address these issues, we propose a learning-based neural network framework to jointly optimize the antenna positions vector (APV) and antenna weights vector (AWV) with imperfect jamming AoAs. Specifically, the AWV optimization is addressed through a zero-forcing beamformer, while fully-connected layers are employed to learn the satisfactory APV. The simulation results show that the proposed approach outperforms other benchmarks in terms of desired signal gain, with much reduced computational time and complexity. Moreover, the proposed scheme demonstrates strong robustness and effectiveness under imperfect jamming AoAs.

title: Knowledge Distillation enhanced Lightweight Pruned Graph Neural Network for Urban Scenario Sensing using Consumer-Grade LiDAR Devices
authors: Jinhao Zhou, Huaiqing Zhang, Kai Fang, Ruisheng Wang, Thippa Reddy Gadekallu, Ting Yun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: With the growing availability of consumer-grade LiDAR, large-scale crowdsourced urban point-cloud sensing has become feasible. To enable high-accuracy point-cloud segmentation on resource-limited consumer-grade devices, we propose a knowledge-distillation based lightweight enhanced graph neural network. We first convert raw point clouds into supervoxels with Fast Point Feature Histogram (FPFH) descriptors and construct a pre-pruned graph using a Laplacian kernel function. Based on this graph, we design a high-capacity teacher model of pruned graph neural network equipped with a Semantic Feature Aggregation Module (SFAM) and Progressive Pruning Graph Convolution (PPGC), and obtain a compressed student model by compressing the teacher model. The student then learns from the teacher through soft-label distillation and feature-based distillation. It reduces network parameters and inference time by approximately 70% and 60%, respectively, while maintaining an mIoU gap of only about 0.3% relative to the teacher. The model is deployable on NVIDIA Jetson-class embedded chips with practical throughput and low memory usage, demonstrating its significance for scalable urban sensing and cost-effective deployment in large-scale smart-city applications. Both the implementation of our method and the collected point cloud datasets are publicly available at: https://github.com/njyunting/zhoujinhao_SDGCNN.

authors: Christoph Mayer, Haozhe Zhang, Mahmoud Abo Khamis, Kyle Deeds, Dan Olteanu, Dan Suciu

title: Improving Range Scan Performance in LSM-trees with Group Caching
authors: Hengrui Wang, Jiaoyi Zhang, Jiansheng Qiu, Fangzhou Yuan, Huanchen Zhang
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
abstract: None

title: Year-Long Measurement of Glucose Oxidase Deactivation in Electrochemical Glucose Sensors
authors: Sven Ernst, Justin Buckland, Sophie Meredith, Mark A. Tapsak
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: The long-term in-vitro performance and stability of an electrochemical glucose sensor based on glucose oxidase were evaluated over one year. Aging experiments designed to maintain the glucose oxidase in an oxidised state demonstrated a sensor response which decayed exponentially, with a half-life of 75 days. As the sensor aged, the rise-time in response to a glucose step was found to progressively increase, which is attributed to the glucose needing to diffuse further into the sensor’s polymer membranes to reach active enzyme. These results represent the first measurement of the decay of glucose oxidase enzyme for a year-long period. The results provide updated and higher-confidence glucose oxidase deactivation rates, support the feasibility of long-lifetime implantable continuous glucose monitors, and highlight design considerations for enabling the next generation of accurate and low-burden glucose monitoring technologies.

title: Fast Photometric Stereo by Time- and Spectral-Multiplexing with Crosstalk Handling
authors: Xiaoyao Wei, Lingfeng Shen, Li Shuai, Zhijie Xu, Yanlong Cao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: Photometric stereo is widely used to recover detailed surface normals. However, previous methods fail to balance the accuracy and efficiency. Conventional photometric stereo achieves high accuracy but suffers from low efficiency due to spectral-multiplexing and inefficient algorithms. In contrast, multispectral photometric stereo captures images efficiently with spectral-multiplexing, but its accuracy is harmed by crosstalk. In this paper, we aim to resolve the crosstalk issue to achieve fast photometric stereo (FPS) at low cost. First, we analyze the formulation and impact of crosstalk, showing that it significantly affects normal estimation, with external factors being primary contributors to crosstalk and internal factors being the secondary. Subsequently, we propose the FPS framework with a fast data capture scheme that combines time- and spectral-multiplexing to introduce constraints on crosstalk regarding both internal and external factors, along with a lightweight network, FPS-Net, to remove crosstalk caused by those factors based on constraints under such scheme. Finally, we build a real-world crosstalk-affected FPS dataset to evaluate the performance in handling crosstalk for normal estimation. Experimental results show the superior accuracy and efficiency of our method. The code and dataset are available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/FPS-Net.

title: Nearest Neighbor Projection Removal Adversarial Training
authors: Himanshu Singh, A. V. Subramanyam, Shivank Rajput, Mohan Kankanhalli
publish: IEEE Transactions on Artificial Intelligence
abstract: Deep neural networks have exhibited impressive performance in image classification tasks but remain vulnerable to adversarial examples. Standard adversarial training enhances robustness but typically fails to explicitly address inter-class feature overlap, a significant contributor to adversarial susceptibility. In this work, we introduce a novel adversarial training framework that actively mitigates inter-class proximity by projecting out inter-class dependencies from adversarial and clean samples in the feature space.
