Specifically, our approach first identifies the nearest inter-class neighbors for each adversarial sample and subsequently removes projections onto these neighbors to enforce stronger feature separability. Theoretically, we demonstrate that our proposed logits correction reduces the Lipschitz constant of neural networks, thereby lowering the Rademacher complexity, which directly contributes to improved generalization and robustness. Extensive experiments across standard benchmarks including CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, SVHN, and TinyImagenet show that our method demonstrates strong performance that is competitive with leading adversarial training techniques, highlighting significant achievements in both robust and clean accuracy. Our findings reveal the importance of addressing inter-class feature proximity explicitly to bolster adversarial robustness in DNNs. The code is available in the supplementary material.

title: Boosting the Stealthiness of Backdoor Attack against Data-Free Detection
authors: Tao Jiang, Rui Su, Luyao Chen, Zhiquan Liu, Yinbin Miao, Peihan Qi, Robert H. Deng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: The proliferation of model-sharing platforms has intensified the need for data-free backdoor detection, as deployed models are often accessed without accompanying clean validation data. This constraint renders traditional, data-dependent detection methods ineffective. However, existing strategies to evade data-free detection are inadequate; they frequently fail to circumvent the multi-faceted discriminative criteria of modern detectors that analyze model output behavior, and they often compromise the backdoor model's primary task performance, thus failing to balance attack stealth with functionality. To evade these detections, this paper introduces a Stealthy Backdoor Attack (SBdA) based on label smoothing. Our method dynamically adjusts the training labels for backdoor samples by leveraging the feature similarity between each class and the attacker's target class. This optimization shapes the backdoored model's output distributions to closely mimic those of a benign model, thereby evading detection mechanisms that rely on outlier characterization and posterior distribution analysis. Extensive experiments demonstrate that SBdA maintains a high attack success rate (exceeding 91% under all tested conditions, with 75% of models surpassing 96%) while significantly reducing its detectability. On CIFAR-10, the average outlier score for our models was 0.554-merely 0.050 higher than benign models-compared to a 25.517 deviation for conventional attacks. On GTSRB, SBdA reduced the outlier score gap by 82.86% compared to the average-label technique. Furthermore, by carefully calibrating the posterior distribution, SBdA effectively avoids detection by posterior matrix-based methods across all four tested datasets.

title: Message Passing-Based Robust Asynchronous Multistatic Localization Using Differential Time Delays with Unknown Transmitter Position
authors: Wu Hong, Ming Jin, Qinghua Guo, Wenjuan Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Radar Systems
abstract: This paper addresses the problem of target localization in mixed line-of-sight (LOS) and non-line-of-sight (NLOS) environments using differential time delay (DTD) measurements from signals of opportunity emitted by a non-cooperative transmitter with unknown position. The system operates without time synchronization among the transmitter and receivers, making it suitable for asynchronous multistatic localization. To mitigate the impact of outliers caused by NLOS propagation, the localization task is formulated as a least absolute deviation (LAD) optimization problem, and we then develop an efficient iterative message passing algorithm to solve it. Different from conventional methods, our proposed method circumvents the coupling issue between measurements and noises, making it suitable for dealing with outliers. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art approaches under mixed LOS/NLOS conditions, exhibiting strong robustness and high accuracy even at high noise levels and with a limited number of sensors.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks for Soil Moisture Modeling and Landslide Assessment
authors: Shelly Gupta, Jovan Andjelkovic, Abhudaya Shrivastava, Atsuhiro Muto, Zoran Obradovic
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Landslides triggered by heavy rainfall threaten infrastructure and public safety, especially in regions with complex terrain and variable soils. Existing satellite-based methods often overlook the physics of landslide processes. We propose a ground-sensor-based framework that couples physics-informed machine learning with soil hydrology for improved landslide modeling. Our pedotransfer function (PTF)-enhanced physics-informed neural network estimates spatiotemporal soil moisture using Richards’ equation, operating under partially observed conditions without explicit boundary inputs, enabling scalable deployment. From modeled soil moisture, we derive physically consistent daily features to train a downstream landslide classifier. Across 43 sites in six US states, our approach achieves strong soil moisture prediction (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.77) and accurate landslide classification (Macro F1 = 0.88). These results highlight the value of integrating physics-based modeling with machine learning for practical geohazard prediction. Data and code are here.

title: Privacy-Preserving Multimodal Stress Detection from Wearables with Attention Fusion and Federated Learning
authors: Mohd Farhan Israk Soumik, Hussein Zangoti, Ebrahim Maghsoudlou, Awal Ahmed Fime, Ahmed Imteaj, Abdur R. Shahid
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Stress is a major factor affecting mental health and long-term well-being, making its early detection essential for effective intervention. Wearable sensors have emerged as a promising solution for continuous stress monitoring, but current systems face two major limitations. First, they are single-dimensional, relying on either chest- or wrist-based sensors alone, which limits their ability to capture diverse physiological signals. Second, they typically require centralized data collection, raising significant privacy concerns since stress-related information is highly sensitive. This work introduces a distributed multimodal stress monitoring framework that addresses both challenges. The framework integrates synchronized signals from both chest and wrist sensors, providing a richer and more reliable representation of stress patterns. An attention-based fusion mechanism is introduced to adaptively weigh complementary information across modalities, while two deep learning architectures, BiLSTM and TS-BERT, are employed for temporal modeling. To ensure scalability and protect user privacy, we adopt Federated Learning (FL) for decentralized training across simulated edge devices. Experimental results demonstrate that TS-BERT achieves superior performance, reaching 99.93% test accuracy in FL, closely matching centralized outcomes.

title: Energy-Efficient Driving via Model-Agnostic Meta-Learning and Trust-Region Policy Optimization
authors: Shaheen, Nalin Kumar Sharma, Rahul Kala, G. C. Nandi, Surya Prakash, Chengyuan Ma, Xiaopeng Li
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This paper proposes an adaptive driving framework for an autonomous following vehicle to compute the energyoptimal time headway that minimizes energy consumption while ensuring safety and aerodynamic efficiency in autonomous vehicles. The proposed method integrates Model-Agnostic MetaLearning (MAML) with Trust Region Policy Optimization (TRPO) to enable rapid policy adaptation across varying driving tasks. The Intelligent Driver Model (IDM) is employed to compute the ego-vehicle's acceleration for the energy-optimal headway. Simulation results show that the meta-learned policy achieves up to 23.19 % reduction in fuel consumption and 50.6% reduction in aerodrag compared to manual driving.

title: Survival Analysis for Employee Retention Prediction in Retail and Trade Sector Organizations
authors: Ariel Gonzalez Batista, Xingquan Zhu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: This paper proposes to study employee retention prediction in a multi-sector organization running retail operations, sales and service centers, and corporate support functions. We examine the uniqueness of the Retail and Trade Sector (RTS) industry and propose to use survival analysis to predict employee tenure and identify retention risks. Using a data set of 4,953 employees in multiple business sectors, we evaluated regression models vs. survival analysis models, and show that our survival analysis approach successfully distinguishes between employees who leave within two years versus those who stay 6+ years. Our methodology employs rate-based temporal feature engineering to capture time-dependent patterns while preventing data leakage, and demonstrates that regression-and classification-based approaches have performance limitations when making accurate predictions for active employees. Survival models enable proactive identification of high-risk employees, providing actionable insights for talent retention strategies, making them particularly useful for dealing with incomplete observations inherent in human resources data.

title: Difficulty-Driven Fine Training for WisdomNet
authors: Ayomide Afolabi, Ramazan Aygun, Truong X. Tran
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Machine learning models, despite achieving low error rate, continue to encounter trust issues, particularly in domains where a single incorrect prediction can be costly or disastrous. WisdomNet architecture facilitates to achieve a zero error rate if certain conditions are met by rejecting data instances it is uncertain about and delegating those cases to a human expert. However, WisdomNet still faces several challenges, such as a high rejection rate and determining the appropriate point to stop fine-training. In this paper, we propose a novel technique called Difficulty-Driven Fine Training (DDFT), which not only determines when to stop fine-training but also minimizes the rejection rate. This technique focuses on excluding difficult data from the validation set, and fine-training WisdomNet with misclassified samples until the new validation set achieves a zero error rate. We conducted experiments to identify factors contributing to increased rejection rates in certain datasets. Our experimental results show that our method reduces the rejection rate from 39.90% to 30.53%, 96.62% to 23.25%, 89.09% to 9.80%, 5.24% to 1.65%, 94.4% to 20.55% in drybeans(Sira-Dermasons), banana quality, FashionMNIST(Dress-Shirt), MNIST(2-7) and CIFAR10(Cats-Dogs) datasets, respectively, compared to an ideal WisdomNet.

title: A Multimodal Machine Learning Approach for Solar Flare Prediction Using Polarity Inversion Lines Data with Low-Rank Fusion
authors: Ziba Khani, Reza Mansouri, Rafal A. Angryk, Berkay Aydin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Predicting solar flares is a central problem in space weather forecasting that has drawn increased research interest due to advances in data-driven learning models and novel datasets integrated from heterogeneous data sources. This paper marks a pioneering effort in leveraging a rich dataset of magnetic polarity inversion lines (MPILs) derived from the Solar Dynamics Observatory’s Helioseismic and Magnetic Imager (SDO/HMI). We utilize rasters of MPILs and the convex hull of MPILs as two distinct spatial modalities, along with a multivariate time series of physical and shape-based characteristics of MPILs. We introduce a new multimodal prediction model that aims to predict the occurrence of ≥M1.0 class flares within the next 24 hours. This model utilizes Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks for the time series data and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) for the spatial data, extracting features from each modality in a self-supervised manner and combining them by employing a low-rank fusion method for the prediction task. We employ a chronological partitioning strategy to evaluate the predictive skill of our model, using the established True Skill Statistic (TSS) and Heidke Skill Score (HSS) metrics. Evaluation results show that the proposed multimodal model achieves a True Skill Statistic (TSS) of 0.44, Heidke Skill Score (HSS) of 0.30, outperforming unimodal baselines on the same dataset. The main contributions of this study include: (1) innovative multimodal data integration using magnetic polarity inversion lines from active regions, (2) effective implementation of a multimodal flare prediction model utilizing the MPIL dataset, and (3) empirical validation that multimodal integration enhances predictive performance. This work lays the groundwork for advancing solar flare forecasting through multimodal machine learning.

title: Supervised Contrastive Disentanglement for Classification
authors: Joshua Luo, Luke Zhong, Feiyang Cai, Long Cheng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Both supervised contrastive learning and traditional cross entropy based supervised learning methods have demonstrated strong performance in classification tasks. The combination of those two methods has the potential to achieve better performance for classification tasks. In this paper, we introduce Supervised Contrastive Disentanglement for Classification (CoDiC), a novel method that combines supervised contrastive learning and cross entropy to explicitly disentangle latent features into class logits and instance-specific embeddings. This disentanglement enables CoDiC to isolate class information while suppressing spurious or instance-dependent variations, leading to enhanced performance. We evaluate CoDiC across a diverse set of datasets, including CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and the histopathology dataset CAMELYON16. CoDiC consistently outperforms baseline methods, including cross-entropy, SimCLR, and SupCon. Notably, on CIFAR-100, CoDiC achieves a top-1 accuracy of 80.1%, outperforming the next-best method by 3.6%, and demonstrating the effectiveness of disentanglement for robust classification in different domains.

title: Renewable Energy Output Prediction using Machine Learning and Logistic Regression – A Framework
authors: Sanjay Goswami, Barnali Goswami Banerjee, Tapabrata Roy, Joshit Naik, Sabyasachi Kar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)
abstract: This work attempts to explore the improved applicability of a logistic regression framework to predict the renewable energy output of a place based on natural and engineered weather condition data, such as temperature, wind speed, humidity, air pressure, solar radiation, and wind power index. After cleaning and processing the dataset, the machine learning model was trained to classify the energy output of that place into high or low energy categories with 92.88% accuracy. The Confusion Matrix approach was taken to test the other aspects of the model performance, such as Precision, Recall, and F1 Score, and all came out to be 0.93 on a scale of 0 – 1 (weighted mean). A feature engineering approach for creating a Wind Power Index helped improve the results, leading to the observation that the classifier is highly precise in identifying high-energy output locations (a precision score of 0.98) after proper training. Though the dataset lacks temporal features such as the time of the day/week/month/year, the energy will be high; future implementations can certainly include these features.

title: A Real-Time Online Monitoring System for Assessing Front- and Rear-Side Soiling Effects on Bifacial PV Performance in the Middle East
authors: M. Saad Khalil Khan, Habibur Rehman, Rached Dhaouadi
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: Bifacial photovoltaic (PV) technology offers significant potential in the Middle East, leveraging vast desert expanses and high surface albedo. However, performance is severely hindered by significant soiling. To optimize yield and profitability, cost-effective Operation and Maintenance (O&M) strategies must balance mitigation expenses against energy losses. This requires understanding of asymmetric soiling characteristics on both front and rear surfaces and impact on bifacial PV performance, yet existing literature provides limited insight into side-specific impacts within arid Middle Eastern environments. This paper presents the design and implementation of a real-time online monitoring system designed to quantify separate front- and rear-side soiling losses developed for an upcoming six-month field study at American University Of Sharjah (AUS) to study the side-specific impacts of soiling on bifacial PV performance in the Middle East.

title: YOLOv11-CSFCN: A Multi-Category Target Detection Method for Chili Pests and Diseases
authors: Tianlv Liu, Zongwen Bai, Meili Zhou, Zhijiang Zhang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Automated chili pest and disease detection faces significant challenges due to extreme scale variations and complex field backgrounds. To address these issues, this paper proposes an enhanced YOLOv11-based object detector incorporating a novel Cross-Scale Feature Fusion Channel Attention Network (CSFCN). A dual-calibration mechanism is introduced to resolve two fundamental bottlenecks in conventional convolutional neural networks: Context Feature Calibration, which mitigates pixel–context mismatch in multi-scale scenarios, and Spatial Feature Calibration, which rectifies feature misalignment through learnable spatial sampling. The coordinated operation of these modules enables precise context modeling for targets of varying scales while effectively suppressing background interference. Experimental results on a challenging dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a mean average precision of 81.0% at an IoU threshold of 0.5, outperforming the baseline by 3.4%. Notably, detection accuracy for small targets is significantly improved, highlighting the robustness and practical potential of the proposed approach for precision agriculture applications.

title: Future Leaders: A Comparative Analysis of Personality Traits in Defence Officers and Their Strategic Relevance to Corporate Leadership
authors: Ripunjay Kumar, Ramesh Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The combination of organized military background and dynamic business issues provides a special perspective of leadership development. This is a review paper examining the personality characteristics of defence officers and their strategic usefulness in leadership of the corporation. Using a mixed-method study, information was gathered using 80 respondents, 40 defense officers and 40 corporate leaders, in a structured questionnaire. Besides surveys, both the defence and corporate respondents were interviewed to provide rich qualitative information. The quantitative data analysis using SPSS, supplemented by the qualitative evaluation in terms of thematic analysis, gave some insights into the leadership characteristics, crisis response, adaptability, and decisionmaking. Findings indicate a strong overlap in such values as emotional intelligence, teamwork, and ethical behavior and a strong divergence in such as discipline, resilience, and strategic foresight. The leaders of the defense focused on integrity and operational rigor, their corporate counterparts focused on communication and innovation. Other practices in the area of military leadership identified by the study to be very transferable into business environments include ethical grounding and crisis simulations. The demographic analysis displays diverse backgrounds but the complementary strengths, which support the use of cross-domain leadership models. The implications of results include the possibility of applying the characteristics of military leadership to business spheres in order to develop resilient, ethically oriented, and futureoriented leaders.

title: Single-Step Reconstruction-Free Anomaly Detection and Segmentation via Diffusion Models
authors: Mehrdad Moradi, Marco Grasso, Bianca Maria Colosimo, Kamran Paynabar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Generative models have demonstrated significant success in anomaly detection and segmentation over the past decade. Recently, diffusion models have emerged as a strong alternative, outperforming previous approaches such as GANs and VAEs. In typical diffusion-based anomaly detection, a model is trained on normal data, and during inference, anomalous images are perturbed to a predefined intermediate step in the forward diffusion process. The corresponding normal image is then reconstructed through iterative reverse sampling.However, reconstruction-based approaches present three major challenges: (1) the reconstruction process is computationally expensive due to multiple sampling steps, making real-time applications impractical; (2) for complex or subtle patterns, the reconstructed image may correspond to a different normal pattern rather than the original input; and (3) choosing an appropriate intermediate noise level is challenging because it is application-dependent and often assumes prior knowledge of anomalies, an assumption that does not hold in unsupervised settings.We introduce Reconstruction-free Anomaly Detection with Attention-based diffusion models in Real-time (RADAR), which overcomes the limitations of reconstruction-based anomaly detection. Unlike current state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods that reconstruct the input image, RADAR directly produces anomaly maps from the diffusion model, improving both detection accuracy and computational efficiency. We evaluate RADAR on real-world 3D-printed material and the MVTec-AD dataset. Our approach surpasses state-of-the-art diffusion-based and statistical machine learning models across all key metrics, including accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 score. Specifically, RADAR improves F1 score by 7% on MVTec-AD and 13% on the 3D-printed material dataset compared to the next best model.Code: https://github.com/mehrdadmoradi124/RADAR

title: Generalization of the Modified Method of Discrete Sources to the Problem of Wave Diffraction by an Arbitrary Three-Dimensional Compact Scatterer
authors: S. A. Manenkov, D. V. Krysanov
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: In this paper, a new version of the modified method of discrete sources for solving the three-dimensional problem of plane wave diffraction by a compact perfectly reflecting body is proposed. The results of calculation of the scattering patterns for two scatterer shapes are compared with those obtained using other numerical methods. The advantages of the proposed approach over existing methods are demonstrated.

title: Immunemesh: a Hybrid Bio-Inspired Cyber Defense System
authors: Shivangi Chauhan, Arvind K. Sharma
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The accelerating sophistication of cyberattacks has highlighted the limitations of static, rule-based defense mechanisms and signature-dependent intrusion detection systems (IDS). Such systems lack adaptability against polymorphic, zero-day, and AI-driven threats. The paper proposes ImmuneMesh, a bio-inspired hybrid cyber defense framework modeled on the functional dynamics of the human immune system.ImmuneMesh introduces a distributed architecture of autonomous nodes acting as immune agents for detection, isolation, and recovery.. Each node operates both independently and collaboratively, exchanging immune tokens to propagate adaptive intelligence across the network. The architecture integrates innate detection, adaptive response, and neural immunological memory, ensuring high resilience, scalability, and self-healing capability. Comparative theoretical evaluations reveal that ImmuneMesh can outperform conventional IDS and Zero-Trust systems in adaptability, detection accuracy, and automated recovery.

title: RespFormer: A Motion-Guided Temporal-Frequency Multimodal Fusion Transformer for Contactless Respiratory Monitoring
authors: Shadman Sakib, Gaurav Shinde, Snehalraj Chugh, Mohammad Saeid Anwar, Nirmalya Roy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Monitoring respiratory rate (RR) is essential for early identification of respiratory and metabolic abnormalities. However, the limitations of contact-based sensors and the lack of reliability in many contactless methods make continuous and accurate monitoring difficult in non-clinical settings. To address these challenges, we introduce RespFormer, an edge-optimized, motion-guided temporal-frequency multimodal fusion transformer framework for real-time, contactless RR estimation and breathing pattern classification. RespFormer integrates dense optical flow analysis with temporal, statistical, and frequency-domain features derived from video sequences and enhances them through a multi-stage signal processing pipeline. These features are modeled using an ensemble of three time series transformer architectures (ETSformer, Temporal Fusion Transformer, and Informer) to capture distinct aspects of temporal dynamics. A shared attention-based refinement module enhances the feature representations, and final predictions are fused using a stacking-based meta-learner. We validate RespFormer on a multimodal dataset comprising synchronized RGB, NIR, and IR video data, including a custom in-house dataset captured under various conditions. Experimental results demonstrate that RespFormer achieves a mean absolute error (MAE) ≈ 0.98 bpm, improving prediction accuracy by ≈ 11% and reducing memory usage by ≈ 26%, while maintaining real-time inference (≈ 1.22 seconds) on resource-constrained devices. Furthermore, RespFormer accurately classifies breathing patterns (normal, bradypnea, tachypnea, and apnea) with 95% accuracy, underscoring it’s potential for practical application in telemedicine, clinical screening, and low-resource healthcare settings.

title: Robust Autism Spectrum Disorder Classification via Optimized 15-Channel Feature Mapping Through Multi-Domain Features Analysis
authors: Kazi Mahatir Mohammad Samir, Md. Imam Uddin Forkan, Sawrav Das, Adnan Sami Sarker, Md Mahbubur Rahman, Mohammad Abu Bakar, Maksuda Islam, Tasnim Nahar Raisa, Sanzida Nishat
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The paper describes a powerful machine learning algorithm to classify the Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) correctly through electroencephalography (EEG) activities. Our solution combines an important channel choice according to the statistical spectral analysis with an exhaustive multi-domain feature extraction. EEG data of 40 children (20 ASD, 20 typically developing) underwent bandpass filtering (1-45 Hz) along with Fz re-referencing, which resulted in 14,349 epochs. A statistical analysis was used to reveal 11 significant channels with differing spectral power (p<0.05). We used relative spectral power in 5 frequency bands, Hjorth parameters, sample entropy, and statistical moments to create a 494-dimensional feature matrix. There were 4 machine learning classifiers that were tested on 5-fold cross-validation. XGBoost had better performance in terms of 96.11 % accuracy, F1- Score of 0.9567, and AUC of 0.9932, which was much better than the performance of the Random Forest, SVM, and Logistic Regression. The suggested framework offers a clinically attractive ASD diagnosis methodology with the capability to deliver state-of-the-art performance with the help of the channel selection and ensemble learning.

title: Strategy Masking: A Method for Guardrails in Value-based Reinforcement Learning Agents
authors: Jonathan Keane, Sam Keyser, Jeremy Kedziora
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: The use of reward functions to structure AI learning and decision making is core to the current reinforcement learning paradigm; however, without thorough understanding of the incentives a reward function creates, it can be difficult to impose principled yet general control mechanisms over its behavior. In this paper, we study methods for constructing guardrails for AI agents that use reward functions to learn decision making. We introduce a novel approach, which we call strategy masking, to explicitly learn and then suppress undesirable AI agent behavior. We apply our method to study lying in AI agents and show that it can be used to effectively modify agent behavior by suppressing lying post-training without compromising agent ability to perform effectively.

title: AI-Driven Prediction of Material Deformation: Stress-Strain Curves Faster Than Crystal Plasticity Finite Element Simulation
authors: Sayak Chakrabarty, Shahriyar Keshavarz, Yuwei Mao, Andrew Reid, Alok Choudhary, Ankit Agrawal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Stress–strain curves capture the mechanical behavior of materials but are computationally expensive to generate using crystal-plasticity finite-element (CPFE) models, due to the profound nonlinearity of the response, and its relationship to crystallographic orientation. We propose an AI-driven framework for predicting bilinear approximate stress–strain curves of metallic alloys using supervised machine learning models. We evaluate its performance on three representative materials: aluminum (Al), nickel (Ni), and copper (Cu), commonly used in aerospace engineering. Trained on just 100 fully resolved CPFE curves per material, our model accurately reconstructs entire curves using features extracted after only a single CPFE step, effectively leading to orders of magnitude speedup with respect to CPFE simulation for predicting stress-strain curves of new orientations unseen by the AI model. The resulting predictions achieve a mean absolute error fraction of around 1.53% for nickel, 1.43% for aluminum, and 2.86% for copper, while producing orientation-specific stress–strain curves several times faster than conventional CPFE simulations.

title: Web-based Wireless Peer-to-Peer Projector Connectivity using Raspberry Pi
authors: E. Anna Devi, P. Josephin Shermila, Thanigai Murugan S, Venkateshaa T D, M. Eugine Prince, P. Sujatha Therese
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: This project aims to develop a wireless peer-to-peer (P2P) communication system using Web-Sockets, enabling seamless file sharing and screen sharing across any IP address within a local network. Unlike traditional LAN-based systems that rely on wired connections and centralized servers, our approach provides a fully decentralized and wireless solution. By leveraging Web-Sockets, the system ensures real-time, bidirectional communication with minimal latency. Users can upload and access files dynamically, irrespective of their IP address within the network. Security measures are implemented to ensure data privacy and integrity. Performance analysis demonstrates the efficiency of the system in terms of speed, stability, and scalability, making it ideal for collaborative and remote work environments.

title: Depth Estimation for Autonomous Driverless Vehicles in Industrial Intralogistics
authors: Keerthi Vandakudari, Jayalaxmi G. Nargund, Vishwanath P. Baligar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The growing implementation of Industry 4.0 has also generated a need for intelligent and autonomous intralogistics systems that can handle materials safely and efficiently. Depth estimation is a critical enabler of such systems, as it endows vehicles with the means to understand their environment and map out complex industrial spaces. This paper introduces a computer vision-oriented depth estimation method for autonomous driverless cars without the use of costly 3D sensors. such as LiDAR. The system in question utilizes rectified stereo image pairs to generate disparity maps, which are then converted to depth using the SemiGlobal Block Matching (SGBM) algorithm. The experiments are carried out in Python with support from OpenCV and NumPy libraries and tested on bench-mark datasets such as KITTI, Driving Stereo, and Argoverse. Experimental results indicate that the KITTI dataset yields the best and most precise results, while Driving Stereo and Argoverse have more error rates owing to the environment. The technology enables strong detection of obstacles, path planning, and safe navigation in real-time, providing an industrial intralogistics solution that is affordable and scalable. This article demonstrates that computer vision algorithms based on stereo vision can be employed to efficiently facilitate autonomous transportation of material and support the development of intelligent, automated industrial environments.

title: A Knowledge Graph-Based Data Provenance Framework for Risk Indicators in Intelligent Auditing
authors: Zhichao Jiang, Linfan Xia, Zexin Qu, Bo Han, Yifan Wu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: In response to prevalent issues in traditional auditing—specifically obscure indicator origins, fragmented data lineage, and the underutilization of unstructured evidence—this study presents a novel data provenance framework for risk indicators in intelligent auditing. We construct a comprehensive indicator-data mapping model that systematically delineates data lineage, including sources, schema structures, and processing pipelines. By integrating metadata management, field-level semantic alignment, and knowledge graph modeling, our approach ensures verifiable traceability of structured data throughout its lifecycle. Furthermore, we leverage NLP techniques, such as entity extraction and vectorization, to convert unstructured assets like audit reports and rectification records into an accessible semantic knowledge base, offering robust supplementary evidence. Empirical evaluations demonstrate that this framework significantly improves source transparency, data consistency, and query efficiency. Ultimately, it establishes a solid data foundation for risk identification, model explainability, and audit decision-making.

title: Real-Time Detection Algorithm for Field Weeds Based on Improved YOLOv12
authors: Guanglu Lu, Ruoming Lan, Tianzeng Chu, Weiwei Yue
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Real-time detection and recognition of weeds in fields using computer vision technology is a rapidly emerging key research direction in the field of smart agriculture. The YOLO series is one of the most representative algorithms in object detection. Significant progress has been made by improving these algorithms for field weed detection. As the latest member of the YOLO series, YOLOv12 represents a major breakthrough in real-time object detection, and its application to field weed detection is of practical significance. To enhance the model’s weed detection capability, improvements were made to the original YOLOv12 architecture by incorporating the weighted Bidirectional Feature Pyramid Network(BiFPN) and MobileNetV4 network module, called BM-YOLO. Experimental results on the DATASET Weeds dataset show that the mAP50 and mAP50-95 of the improved model increased by 3.1% and 1.2%, respectively. Additionally, the recall and precision of the improved model are 0.871 and 0.936, which are 1.2% and 4.0% higher than the original, providing higher accuracy and stronger stability for real-time field weed detection. The research results also indicate that integrating the MobileNetV4 network module into the backbone of YOLOv12 not only effectively reduces the computational complexity of the model but also achieves faster inference speed than the basic structure of YOLOv12.

title: Stochastic Modeling of Fast HVAC Demand Response Derived from Aerial Imagery and 3D District Models
authors: Yoshifumi Aoki, Shota Imichi, Hiroka Akazawa, Chuzo Ninagawa
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: The increasing penetration of renewable energy sources has enhanced the importance of Demand Response (DR) as a flexibility resource at the urban district scale for achieving Positive Energy Districts (PEDs). However, evaluating the DR potential of building air-conditioning systems remains challenging due to the lack of detailed building information and measured operational data. This study proposes a stochastic modeling framework for assessing district-scale air-conditioning DR potential without relying on measured data. The total rated air-conditioning power of a building is estimated as a probability distribution using Bayesian linear regression based on aerial imagery and a 3D district model. By integrating multiple estimation sources, uncertainty is reduced, enabling evaluation of DR duration and cumulative energy reduction under realistic constraints.

title: Synthesis of Android Application Network Traffic Using the TabDDPM Diffusion Model
authors: O. I. Sheluhin, F. A. Matorin
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: Purpose: To conduct a comprehensive quality analysis of the Tabular Denoising Diffusion (Tab-DDPM) model for generating tabular features of realistic synthetic Android application network traffic using a consistent set of metrics and machine-learning evaluation. Approach: Model performance is assessed using statistical analysis methods and a network-session dataset with numerical features and class labels. Findings: The results demonstrate the feasibility of creating controlled synthetic datasets that preserve characteristic traffic patterns and are suitable for modeling cyberattacks. Practical implications: The model is used for data augmentation by generating realistic synthetic traffic and can be adapted to any tabular dataset. It is suitable for machine learning and for validating intrusion detection systems (IDS), including zero-day and stress-testing scenarios. Originality: A reproducible generation pipeline and a unified metric suite are proposed for evaluating diffusion-based tabular data models.

title: Assessment of Electric Vehicle Accident Risks in Indian States: An Empirical Investigation
authors: Kallakuri Nitya Srikari, Vishnu C Rajan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Electric vehicle (EV) adoption in India has gained substantial momentum in recent years, driven by both environmental considerations and economic incentives. Given this rapid growth, it is now crucial to assess the road safety performance of EVs across regions. Accordingly, this research presents a comparative analysis of EV accidents reported across Indian states and union territories, based on three normalized safety indicators: the Accident Risk Index (ARI), the Fatality Risk Index (FRI), and the Risk Exposure Index (REI). Individual regions are further classified into high, medium, and low-risk categories to enable comparative EV safety assessments. Furthermore, Correspondence Analysis is employed to examine patterns of interstate accidents. The results obtained from these analyses are cross-checked with those of conventional Internal Combustion Engine Vehicles (ICEVs) to identify any EV-specific factors. The relationship between EV adoption levels and the safety indices is analyzed to derive risk-adjusted policy implications. Interestingly, both EVs and ICEVs exhibit different accident patterns across the regions. The union territory of Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu (DNHDD) exhibit a significant deviation, indicating a disproportionately strong association with EV accident incidence compared to other regions. Additionally, the results suggest that the ARI between EVs and ICEVs differs statistically across regions, whereas the FRI and REI between EVs and ICEVs do not differ statistically. The findings provide a data-driven basis for identifying accident-prone regions, enhancing road safety strategies, and supporting evidencebased decision-making for safer EV deployment within India's transportation ecosystem.

title: Gyroscope and Accelerometer based Assistive Spoon for Motor Disorder Patients
authors: C.Selvarathi, P.Pandiaraja, C.Ushapriya, Senthilkumar R, B.T.Annapoorani, R. Deebika
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Parkinson's disease is a neurodegenerative disorder that affects motor function, leading to hand tremors during simple tasks such as eating. In this project, it is proposed to create an AI augmented self-stabilizing spoon for Parkinson's patients to help them regain autonomy while eating. The device is fitted with inertial measurement sensors, such as gyroscopes and accelerometers, which sense real time movement of the hand. The sensor data is processed by a machine learning model to distinguish between voluntary hand movements and involuntary tremors. When tremors are identified, the built in micro actuators control the spoon head position to stabilize it and prevent spills while facilitating smoother meal consumption. The proposed research work mainly focused three base classifiers, namely Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest (RF), and 1D Convolutional Neural Network (1D-CNN), The AI system learns users distinct tremor patterns for individualized support. Ergonomically and user friendly in design, the spoon is light, transportable, and can be recharged. It also has the possibility of wireless capability, with data logging and monitoring by carers or health professionals. The final aim is to improve the quality of life for Parkinson's sufferers by enabling autonomy and minimizing frustration at mealtimes. Future enhancements will be aimed at improving the AI model, enhancing battery life, and linking the gadget with mobile health applications for ongoing assistance and monitoring.

title: Demand Response Orchestration Using Incentivecompatible Mechanism Design and IoT Gateways
authors: M Harinath Reddy, Gunavardini V, N.Baby Rani, Venkatesan M, J Jesintha Princy, Chunduri Raghavendra
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Demand-response (DR) mechanisms traditionally assume centralized, high-fidelity reporting and unconstrained communication. Assumptions that breakdown in IoT-mediated deployments where hierarchical gateways impose bandwidth, latency, and partial-verification limitations. This study aimed to design a practically implementable DR mechanism that is incentive-compatible, near-efficient, and robust to gateway constraints and strategic manipulation. A novel two-stage, gateway-aware mechanism MG was formulated that integrates lossy gateway compression <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\operatorname{Qg}(\cdot)$</tex>, probabilistic local verification, and ex-post payment reconciliation. Formal analysis derived sufficient conditions for expected incentive compatibility and bounded welfare loss, under finite gateway capacity; an online orchestration algorithm with provable computational and communication complexity was presented. Empirical evaluation used synthetic residential traces, calibrated to Indian diurnal patterns and Monte-Carlo parameter sweeps. Theoretical results established that for nonzero verification probability, truthful reporting maximized expected utility and allocative inefficiency, scaled with quantization error and verification stochasticity. Empirically, MG recovered <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx \mathbf{9 0 - 9 5 \%}$</tex> of centralized (VCG) welfare at moderate verification rates, while reducing communicated bits by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 60 \%$</tex> and producing lower price volatility than dynamic pricing baselines. Gateway-aware mechanism design reconciles economic rigor with deployment constraints, and informs verification and gateway standards for scalable DR programs.

title: VISION AID: Device for Obstacle Detection and Text-To-Speech Conversion
authors: Milind Rane, Rohan Shrirame, Shreya Sonawane, Shivam Sawant, Ashish Shadija
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: This paper describes an integrated assistive technology that can be used to enable users with visual impairments to be more independent and safer by using two components. The first component is an ultra affordable obstacle detection system that consists of an Arduino UNO, an HC-SR04 ultrasonic sensor and a piezoelectric buzzer, that allows for detecting obstacles in real time within a 0.2-2 meter distance and verbally alerting the user when an obstacle is detected. A user can comfortably navigate through unknown environments while feeling safe and preventing collisions. The second component is an interactive text-to-speech (TTS) desktop application featuring a working prototype developed in Python using libraries to convert document formats such as .txt, .pdf and .docx into audible speech using customizale parameters. The text-to-speech application has controls for the user to change voice settings, a dark mode for better usability, and the ability for users to save their audible documents as .mp3 files. Autosyn and Niasu are an affordable, energy efficient, and capable assistive technology that create an all in one platform that can help individually tailor solutions to a user’s mobility and access to information barriers. This project aims to promote accessibility in inclusive education, communication and navigation. Ultimately improving the quality of life for users with visual impairments.

title: Edge-Forged Resilience: A Zero-Trust Security Architecture for Autonomous Cyber-Physical Systems in Industry 5.0
authors: Valiveti Dattatreya, Mallareddy Adudhodla, S Anupkant, Vasavi Bande, Ch G V N Prasad, Pavan Kumar Reddy Manellore
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Industry 5.0 introduces autonomous cyber-physical systems (CPS) that work closely with humans, significantly expanding the attack surface and rendering traditional perimeter-based security models inadequate. This study proposes an edge-native Zero-Trust Architecture (ZTA) to secure these decentralized environments. The architecture enforces security at the network edge, near operational devices, using AI-driven continuous authentication, strict micro-segmentation, and dynamic least-privilege access via an Edge Policy Enforcement Point (PEP). Simulated testing against a traditional firewall model showed the proposed ZTA mitigated 98.5% of attacks, compared to 35% with the baseline. Despite handling 50 active devices, it maintained low latency at 48.2 ms and reduced unnecessary core network traffic by 40%, with only a 15% CPU increase at the edge. These results demonstrate that edge-forged ZTA delivers robust, low-latency, and adaptive security, meeting the needs of Industry 5.0 by enabling resilient, secure, and efficient human-machine collaboration in autonomous manufacturing systems.

title: Safety Auto Brake System for Hilly Regions
authors: Geetla Abhinav, Patil Mounica, Natarajan Karuppiah, Tellapati Anuradha Devi, Ravivarman S, Vinod Reddy S
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The "Safety Auto Brake System for Hilly Region" is intended to increase vehicle safety in hilly terrains with steep hills and instability. The system incorporates a MEMS based accelerometer and gyroscope connected to a NodeMCU microcontroller to manage real-time monitoring of the vehicle's orientation and movement. In the event of abnormal tilting, acceleration or a potential collision, the system will deploy the braking system using an L293D motor driver and 300 RPM DC motors. Additionally, an LED will illuminate to notify the user of engagement of the braking system. The intention of this fully automated braking system intends to lessen accidents in hilly terrains with a timely and accurate response to risk. The whole system is powered using a stable power supply circuit with a 9V battery, bridge rectifier, filter capacitor and distributor.

title: Implementation of Wireless and Contactless IoT-based Water Level Monitoring and Control System
authors: F. Infant Raj, Muniraj C, Kamatchi Kannan V, B.V. Sai Thrinath, Ramesh S, Ponmurugan P
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: This research presents an advanced contactless wireless water level monitoring system for diverse applications. Water overflow incidents in residential, commercial, educational, and agricultural facilities cause significant damage and losses. This study develops an intelligent automated water level controller that reduces human intervention through ultrasonic sensing and wireless communication technologies. A comparative evaluation between wireless and wired approaches for automatic water level control was conducted. Results demonstrate superior performance of the wireless implementation over wired methods across multiple assessment criteria. The wireless solution provides enhanced reliability, simplified installation, and improved scalability for water management applications. This autonomous system offers real-time monitoring and remote control capabilities, validating wireless technology's effectiveness in water level regulation.

title: A Defensive Mechanism Against Rubber Ducky Devices and Keystroke Injection Attacks
authors: Subramanian R, Udhayakumar S
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Malicious USB devices, such as Rubber Ducky and BadUSB, abuse the implicit trust of operating systems in Human Interface Devices (HIDs) to perform keystroke injection attacks without users' knowledge. These devices masquerade as normal peripherals, sending commands at high rates and completing payloads in seconds, far quicker than manual execution, which takes at least a minute. Because of the speed of execution, these devices are effective in privilege escalation, data exfiltration, credential theft, and system compromise with minimal user interaction. In this paper, we introduce a defensive tool that detects and blocks malicious HID-based attacks in real time. Our tool provides layers of defense, including behavior-based keystroke tracking, program blacklisting, and AutoType integration, to distinguish between authorized and unauthorized inputs. The tool enforces security policies to limit suspicious USB activity and block unauthorized essential system commands. This paper dis- cusses the architecture, implementation, and performance of the tool in addressing real-world scenarios. Additionally, this work discusses flexibility to evolving attack strategies and suggests future improvements in the context of USB security. Given this strong defense against keystroke injections, this work strengthens endpoint security and reduces the threat from malicious USB devices. Evaluations on 50 simulated attacks yielded a detection rate of more than 90 %, false positives of less than 5 %, detection latency of less than 15 ms, and blocking success of the payload above 85 %, with minimal overhead less than 3.5 % CPU.

title: A Systematic Comparison of Deep Learning Models for Single-Trial P300 Detection in Autism Spectrum Disorder
authors: Anupam Das, Prasant Kumar Pattnaik, Pritam Debnath, Kamruzzaman Mondal, Anjan Bandyopadhyay, Anirban Mitra
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The reliable detection of the P300 event-related potential (ERP) from single-trial Electroencephalography (EEG) data is a critical challenge in Brain-Computer Interface (BCI) research, particularly for clinical populations such as individuals with autism spectrum disorder (ASD). While complex deep learning architectures have been proposed, a systematic comparison of their efficacy a gainst s impler <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$m$</tex> odels i so ften <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$l$</tex> acking. This paper presents a rigorous comparative analysis of multiple classic and deep learning architectures on the public BCIAUT_P300 dataset. We evaluated classic machine learning models (Logistic Regression, Random Forest) and a suite of deep learning models (Multi-Layer Perceptron, 1D CNN, CNN with self-attention, and a 2D CNN on spectrogram representations). Using a robust persession, chronological evaluation protocol over multiple seeds, we demonstrate that a relatively simple 1D CNN achieves the best and most stable performance, with a mean F1-Score of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$67.9 \% \pm 0.21$</tex>. Our key finding is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$t$</tex> hat <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$m$</tex> ore c omplex <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$m$</tex> odels, including the attention-based variant and the 2D CNN, consistently failed to converge, exhibiting model collapse and performing identically to a naive majority-class baseline. This cautionary result highlights that for this task, a direct 1D convolutional approach is superior, and increasing architectural complexity or altering data representation can be detrimental. Our work provides a clear benchmark and a critical insight into model selection for EEG-based P300 detection. Fig. 1 depicts the graphical abstract of the work.

title: Research on Weld Defect Detection of Industrial Robot Based on Deep Learning
authors: Cuicui Wang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Weld defect detection of industrial robots is a key link to ensure the quality and safety of manufacturing industry, and traditional detection methods are facing challenges such as low efficiency and poor adaptability in complex industrial scenarios. Aiming at the problems of YOLOv7 model in weld defect detection, such as large number of parameters and insufficient sensitivity to small defects, an improved algorithm EMO-LSK-YOLOv7 is proposed, which integrates lightweight EMO backbone network and LSK attention mechanism. By introducing the multi-scale dynamic receptive field mechanism and the feature recalibration strategy, the model achieves 96.8% mAP on the open weld data set, reduces the number of parameters by 66%, and improves the inference speed to 78 FPS. The experimental results show that the proposed method significantly optimizes the computational efficiency while maintaining high precision, and provides a feasible scheme for real-time detection in industrial field.

title: Research on zero-training-sample infrared ship target recognition method based on meta-learning
authors: Mingrui Zhang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Infrared imaging technology is irreplaceable in fields such as target recognition and target monitoring due to its advantages such as all-weather operation capability and strong resistance to electromagnetic interference. However, the development of this technology faces a bottleneck, as publicly available annotated datasets specifically targeting infrared ship targets are extremely scarce. The high cost and low efficiency of infrared image annotation further exacerbate the difficulty of data acquisition. The root cause of these issues lies in the requirement of traditional supervised learning methods that datasets must cover a wide range of scenarios to ensure model generalization ability. Even if original ship image data can be obtained, tedious annotation work is still required. Ships come in a wide variety of types and shapes, necessitating a significant amount of manpower and professional knowledge for annotation. When faced with "unseen categories" that have not appeared during the training phase, models trained based on transfer learning lose performance due to the lack of prior knowledge of categories. Meta-learning constructs a multi-task learning framework, learning general optimization strategies across tasks during the meta-training phase, and directly generalizing to unseen categories during the meta-testing phase, achieving classification of the sample without fine-tuning. Therefore, it is of great significance to research zero-shot infrared ship target recognition methods based on meta-learning, exploring how to achieve high-performance recognition of typical ship targets under conditions of limited or missing samples.

title: Optical Character Recognition for Pre-Digital Historical Documents using Large Language Models
authors: Chreston Miller, Bipasha Banerjee
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Multi-modal large language models (LLMs) with vision capabilities have been shown to be a promising and effective approach to extracting text from images or optical character recognition (OCR). However, text extraction using OCR is challenging when applied to scanned pre-digital historical documents. Factors such as poor scan quality due to the age of the documents, various levels of fading, scan skew (left or right), and possible abnormal or poor background-to-text contrast can contribute to incorrect OCR results. Performing OCR on scanned pre-digital historical documents makes them machine-readable, thus enabling computational analysis and preservation. This can lead to a better understanding of the past, especially for significant events and time periods. Given the text extraction capabilities of LLMs with vision, we posit that they are a viable option for performing robust OCR on scanned pre-digital historical documents. We chose a set of foundational and capable OCR technologies to compare to LLMs with vision. To accomplish this, we curated a ground truth dataset comprising scanned pre-digital historical documents from the early twentieth century for comparing the chosen OCR technologies. Our experiments showed that LLMs with vision, specifically Mistral AI’s Mistral-Small-3.1-24B-Instruct-2503 model and Allen AI’s olmOCR-7B-0225-preview, are able to perform OCR on our dataset very well. We evaluated using the character error rate (CER), BLEU score, multiple ROUGE scores, and the Normalized Levenshtein Distance (NLD) for each OCR technology. Mistral had better results but the potential for large error (2 cases out of 359 from our dataset), while olmOCR performed almost as well but was more consistent in mitigating high error. These results support the use LLMs with vision to perform OCR on scans of pre-digital historical documents with challenging characteristics.

title: UrbanFlow: Pothole Detection using YOLOv8 and Alert Notification
authors: Rajesh Raikwar, Sanket Botre, Sanika Brahmankar, Suhani Buche, Chaitanya Kulkarni, Krushnamegh Chakke
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: In intelligent cities, the problem of maintaining urban infrastructure, especially road safety, is the most critical one. Potholes are critical safety hazards and lead to traffic congestion. To solve this problem, we propose a geolocation and computer vision-based intelligent web-based pothole detection and reporting system. With an easy web interface, one can upload pothole images or videos, giving permission for location access for appropriate mapping. The system employs YOLOv8 for real-time detection and severity grading in terms of height, width, and depth. Potholes are tagged into three alert categories: minor (yellow), moderate (orange), and severe (red) to ensure prioritized repair. The platform has role-based dashboards for users, corporators, and municipal departments with differentiated functionalities. Automatic email triggers on detection—user confirmation messages and location-based messages to officials. This scalable solution combines AI-driven detection, real-time alerting, and severity-based classification to enhance road safety, promote civic engagement, and enable seamless urban mobility. Through improved road upkeep procedures and citizen engagement, the system enables faster, data-driven decision-making to ensure that the city's infrastructure is better managed.

title: Equivariant Graph Neural Networks with Self-Supervised Learning for Accelerated Solid-State Electrolyte Conductivity Prediction
authors: Tiantian Gao, Yongliang Wu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Solid-state electrolytes (SSEs) are pivotal for next-generation energy storage, yet their design is hindered by the arduous process of identifying materials with superior ionic conductivity. Traditional methods, including experimental synthesis and density functional theory (DFT) calculations, are resource-intensive and time-consuming. Machine learning (ML) offers a promising avenue to accelerate this discovery by predicting material properties directly from structural information. However, conventional graph neural networks (GNNs) often struggle to fully capture the intricate three-dimensional (3D) geometric and symmetry properties inherent in crystalline materials. Here, we introduce a novel framework leveraging 3D equivariant graph neural networks (EGNNs), specifically SchNet, combined with a self-supervised contrastive learning paradigm, for accurate and efficient prediction of SSE ionic conductivity. Our approach intrinsically respects the rotational and translational symmetries of crystal structures, allowing the model to learn more physically meaningful and robust representations. We show that our EGNN-based framework not only achieves greate accuracy in predicting conductivity but also offers improved interpretability by focusing on local atomic environments.

title: Interpretable Weighted Averaging Ensemble Model for Chronic Kidney Disease Detection
authors: Kanishka Kumar Shaw, Rohini Nandi, Debaleena Nandy, Debopratim Biswas, Md Ashifuddin Mondal, Subhram Das, Papri Ghosh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)
abstract: CKD is a chronic disease that impacts millions of patients all over the world and imposes a great burden on health systems. Early diagnosis and appropriate intervention can delay the advancement of the disease and enhance patient survival. This paper suggests a system for diagnosing CKD based on an Ensemble machine learning method that couples XGBoost and LGBM using Weighted Averaging method. Explainable AI techniques are used to clarify which clinical parameters have the most influence on the prediction. The proposed method is simulated and compared with other models Decision tree, XGboost and LGBM. Results from the simulations show that the ensemble model performs better than individual algorithms, with an accuracy of 97%, emphasizing the effectiveness of ensemble methods in medical diagnostics. The results show high accuracy and reliability in identifying CKD at an early stage, highlighting the potential of ML models as useful tools in clinical decision-making.

title: Offline Reinforcement Learning Benchmark for Variable Speed Limit Control with Real-World Dataset
authors: Yuhang Zhang, Zhiyao Zhang, Marcos Quinones-Grueiro, William Barbour, Gautam Biswas, Daniel Work
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Offline reinforcement learning (RL) enables learning decision-making policies directly from historical data, which is advantageous for safety-critical domains like traffic control. However, existing offline RL benchmarks in transportation systems typically rely on simulated data, which may not fully capture the complexities of real-world environments. In this paper, we introduce the first offline RL benchmark for variable speed limit (VSL) control, built from approximately 100 million transitions of real-world interaction data collected from a field-deployed, multi-agent RL-based VSL system on a major freeway. We evaluate five state-of-the-art offline RL algorithms under multiple dataset conditions defined by varying sizes and action noise levels. Through traffic microsimulation experiments, we analyze algorithm performance and generalization, providing insights into the challenges and opportunities of offline RL for intelligent transportation systems. The dataset and benchmark are released at https://github.com/Lab-Work/i24-vsl-orl.

title: Estimation of the Complex Transmission Coefficient of Acoustic Systems Based on a Real Signal
authors: O. B. Popov, T. V. Chernysheva, K. V. Orlov, P. S. Sapronov
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: Objectively assessing the sound quality of acoustic systems (AS) remains an unsolved problem. AS specifications are typically based on the emission of a single-frequency harmonic signal, which precludes adequate assessment. An AS is a mechanical system with multiple natural resonances that emits simple and complex signals in different ways. A developed spectral analysis method with an accuracy and resolution approaching the capabilities of an auditory analyzer allows for the formation of a complex transmission coefficient for an AS based on a real signal. It is shown that the obtained estimates differ significantly from the specifications and allow for the development of approaches to objectively predictive assessment of AS sound quality.

title: Localized LoRA: A Structured Low-Rank Approximation for Efficient Fine-Tuning
authors: Babak Barazandeh, Subhabrata Majumdar, Om Rajyaguru, George Michailidis
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) methods, such as LoRA, offer compact and effective alternatives to full model fine-tuning by introducing low-rank updates to pretrained weights. However, most existing approaches rely on global low-rank structures, which can overlook spatial patterns spread across the parameter space. In this work, we propose Localized LoRA, a generalized framework that models weight updates as a composition of low-rank matrices applied to structured blocks of the weight matrix. This formulation enables dense, localized updates throughout the parameter space—without increasing the total number of trainable parameters. We provide a formal comparison between global, diagonal-local, and fully localized low-rank approximations, and show that our method consistently achieves lower approximation error under matched parameter budgets. Experiments on both synthetic and practical settings demonstrate that Localized LoRA offers a more expressive and adaptable alternative to existing methods, enabling efficient fine-tuning with improved performance.

title: Attention-Guided Feature Fusion for Cylindrical Label Segmentation and Flattening
authors: Siwei Liu, Wenju Zhou, Xianqi Bao, Yiyou Gao
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Flattening cylindrical labels is a critical preprocessing step in modern industrial visual inspection. The existing geometric unwrapping methods are difficult to adapt to the complex and changeable industrial environment, and the manual calibration efficiency is low. Therefore, this paper introduces an image expansion method based on deep learning. We use the U-Net improved by multi-scale context extraction and dual attention mechanism to achieve accurate segmentation of tags, and then use a novel cylindrical back-projection algorithm to carry out geometric perception expansion on the segmented mask image. The deformed and bent label image can be corrected and unfolded without preset camera or surface parameters. Experimental results prove that our method can generate stable and high-quality unwrapped images, and provide a reliable solution for subsequent information acquisition and defect detection.

title: Workly: An AI-Powered Platform for Hyperlocal Micro Job Finding
authors: Aarman Kadri, Hanif Jamadar, Vedant Bachhav, Vidya Dhamdhere
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Workly is a mobile-based job platform designed to connect people who need help with small tasks like cleaning, delivery, home repairs, or tutoring with nearby individuals looking for short-term work. The app offers two types of users: Clients, who post jobs, and Workers, who apply to complete them. By simplifying this connection, Workly helps create flexible job opportunities for students, part-time workers, and freelancers, while allowing Clients to find quick and trusted help in their local area. A key feature of Workly is its smart worker ranking system, powered by a machine learning algorithm that sorts applicants based on skills, ratings, job completion history, and availability. This helps Clients choose the best person for the job with confidence. Each Worker has a profile showcasing their experience, reviews, and ratings promoting trust and transparency across the platform. The mobile application is developed using React Native with Expo, allowing it to run smoothly on both Android and iOS platforms. For the backend, it uses Appwrite, which handles tasks like user login, data storage, file uploads, and real-time updates all within a secure and scalable environment. It also includes a chatbot system to guide users, along with in-app messaging and job status updates to support smooth interaction. By combining intelligent matching, real-time location filters, and a clean user experience, Workly aims to make micro-job hiring easier, faster, and more reliable for everyone involved. The system supports role-based access, secure data handling, and future integration of advanced AI features making it a future-ready solution for the growing gig economy.

title: Design and Implementation of a Dual Arm Imitation Learning System for Fine Manipulation Tasks
authors: Deyu Kong, Lianke Shi, Heng Jiang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Dual-arm fine manipulation via imitation learning is challenged by long horizons, error accumulation, and limited robustness. We propose FCE-ACT, a structured framework that improves long-horizon stability by integrating automatic way-point compression, kernel-based latent modeling, and channel–spatial visual attention within the Action Chunking Transformer. Experiments on five dual-arm manipulation tasks demonstrate that FCE-ACT consistently outperforms ACT, achieving higher success rates and more stable execution.

title: Smart Contracts and Legal Implications Security Vulnerabilities and Regulatory Challenges in Blockchain-based Systems
authors: Saurabh Chandra, Rishiraj Kohli, Sai Krishna Akula, Vishvanatha Raju, Chandini Chowdary R, Siva Koteswara Rao Katta
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The emergence of blockchain technology has led to the development of smart contracts, which are considered the most significant due to their ability to support automated agreements within the system without the need for third parties. Although it might have its advantages, smart contracts do not address all the issues as they face various security vulnerabilities and legal issues and regulatory conditions. The study explores the current role of smart contracts by examining the vulnerability that threaten the integrity of these contracts like programming errors along with reentrancy hacking and architectural flaws of the blockchain systems. In this paper, the relationship between enforceability and jurisdictional issues and fault-based issues that pose legal challenges to smart contracts will be examined. The paper explores system regulations which arise out of these systems through evaluation of legal frameworks which are aimed at providing blockchain security within the existing legal economic frameworks. Studies on comprehensive smart contract risk have made researchers come up with recommendations that enhance security by way of blockchain system controls.

title: QSeg: Quantum - Classical Segmentation Model for Breast Cancer Images
authors: Nadine Matondo-Mvula, Khaled Elleithy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: We present QSeg, a hybrid quantum-classical encoder-decoder for breast ultrasound image segmentation. The model integrates a quantum encoder with a classical decoder to enhance feature representation. We trained on the Breast-Lesions- USG dataset and fine-tuned on the BUS-UCLM dataset. Evaluation with standard metrics (Dice, IoU, precision, recall, pixel accuracy) showed that QSeg outperformed a purely classical baseline and a state-of-the-art model, demonstrating the potential of quantum encoders to improve segmentation accuracy in medical imaging.

title: IT-Enabled Data Analytics for Modeling Energy Conservation Behaviour in Industrial Demographics
authors: Gunjan Dwivedi, Pooja Jain, Ajit Kumar Singh, Rachit Jain, Anuradha Kushwah
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The conservation of energy is critical for sustainable development and environmental safety. We are using different energy sources in our day-to-day lives to meet diverse requirements. But it's time to analyze how energy use alters the ecological system and whether we are doing anything to preserve our surroundings. This paper is an effort to understand the socio-demographic and behavioral determinants of energy-saving behaviour specifically in the context of industries. We applied a predictive model by using R and SPSS Software to evaluate major determinants including environmental awareness, economic incentives, social norms, education level, income and industry characteristics. The results stated targeted policy interventions to improve energy efficiency.

title: Joint Brain Signal Enhancement and Classification via Dual-Stream Generative Adversarial-Convolutional Networks (DS-GACN)
authors: Hulin Jin, Zhiran Jin, Yong-Guk Kim, Yutong Zhou, Kaisen He, Kun Li
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The acquisition and analysis of brain signals have widespread applications in fields such as the brain-computer interfaces and cognitive monitoring. However, the nonlinear, nonstationary, and multi-scale characteristics raise challenges to accurate signal processing. To address this, this study proposes a dual-stream generative adversarial-convolutional network (DS-GACN) to achieve adaptive enhancement and efficient classification of brain signals tasks. This model applies the generator to denoise and enhance raw EEG signals, and a discriminator enhances the performance of the generated signals. During the feature extraction stage, the DS-GACN constructs both time-domain and frequency-domain streams. This will capture dynamic and spectral information respectively. An attention mechanism is then introduced during the fusion phase to highlight key patterns. Subsequently, an improved non-negative matrix factorization (NMF) is introduced to achieve sparse representation. This will make the feature distribution more discriminative. Experiments are conducted on both publicly available and self-collected EEG datasets. Comparisons are conducted with the traditional convolutional neural networks (CNNs), multi-scale convolutional neural networks (MS-CNNs), and horizontal-vertical brain region feature fusion networks (HVF2N-DBRs). Results show that the proposed method improves the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) by over 7 dB compared to traditional methods. This significantly enhances signal quality. Regarding complexity metrics, both the generalized fractal dimension and permutation entropy are improved. This proves that the multi-scale dynamic characteristics of the signal are better preserved. In terms of classification performance, DS-GACN achieves an average 6.8% improvement in accuracy and an F1-score of 0.07 over the control method in the four-category emotion classification task. After introducing the improved NMF, the classification accuracy further improved to 92.4% and the F1-score to 0.91.

title: Pedagogical Conversation Agents in Developing Technical Students’ Speaking Skills
authors: Nataliya V. Solovyeva, Gleb I. Pryakhin
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: The study aimed to assess the efficacy of integrating chatbots into developing communicative competence. The novelty of the research lies in testing a methodology for incremental development of both interactive and expressive speaking skills with the help of chatbots. The conducted literature review revealed a number of benefits resulting from chatbot integration, among which are increased speaking opportunities, flexibility, real-time feedback and low-pressure educational environment. However, when resorting to communication with a chatbot, students’ young age and a low level of language proficiency have to be taken into account as they may hinder interaction. For the purpose of the study a pedagogical conversation agent was developed. To evaluate its contribution to improving students’ communicative competence, the experimental method was employed.
