The findings demonstrated a significant improvement in interactive aspects of communication among the experimental group participants. English language tutors seeking supplementary methods to refine their students’ speaking skills may find the methodology useful.

title: Realistic Modeling of Communication Latency in a Digital Substation With GOOSE and Sampled Values Based on IEC 61850
authors: A. N. Praveen, K. Shanti Swarup, O. D. Naidu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Sampled values and generic object-oriented substation events from merging unit and intelligent electronic devices use communication interface in a digital substation for both control and protection applications. Communication latency additionally influences the performance and availability of the control and protection functions. This article proposes a conditional probability-based method to estimate communication network latency in a digital substation based on IEC 61850. Proposed method in the current work use correct data size, communication path, and characteristics of communication service between a publisher and subscriber as defined in a substation configuration language. Communication latency under both steady state traffic and event-driven traffic is calculated using the proposed method. Results from the set-up based on commercially available merging units and intelligent electronic devices validate the effectiveness of the proposed method in estimating communication network latency accurately for a medium sized substation. Proposed method can be extended to any communication architecture and substation size.

title: CPS-SST: A Single-Stage AC-DC Solid-State Transformer (SST) Based on the Converter PWM Switch (CPS) Concept
authors: Chen Chen, Cheolhwan Kim, Mafu Zhang, Alex Q. Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: This paper proposes a new single-stage topology for medium-voltage AC (MVAC) to low-voltage DC (LVDC) solid-state transformers (SSTs) based on the recently introduced Converter PWM Switch (CPS) concept and the input-series output-parallel (ISOP) architecture. The proposed CPS-SST features a wide ZVS range, low turn-off loss, high efficiency, a reduced number of active switches, and simple PWM control. A module-rotation multilevel modulation strategy is introduced to reduce the required inductance value, while ensuring balanced power sharing among modules in the CPS-SST. The ZVS performance and turn-off current characteristics are analyzed in detail, and a comprehensive loss model is presented and compared with that of a conventional two-stage ISOP SST. A 10 kW CPS-SST module is constructed and experimentally validated, achieving a peak efficiency of 98% at 5.8-kW. The experimental results closely match the analytical predictions, confirming the strong potential of the proposed topology for MV power conversion applications.

title: GreenTech 2026 TOC
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)

title: Automated Counting for Large-Scale Bird Flocks_supp1-3681129.mp4
authors: Tianjiang Hu
publish: N/A
abstract: Automated bird counting is crucial for ecological assessment, yet current methods often lack accuracy with large-scale flocks. This paper proposes and develops FlockCounter, a novel approach for end-to-end flock counting that relies solely on synthetic training data. Our method consists of three stages. Labeled synthetic data of large-scale flocks is automatically generated by using a collective behavioral model with only a limited set of real-world bird flock trajectories. Each annotated image is sequentially enhanced into the visual realism via style transfer. Finally, we train a customized deep learning network exclusively on synthetic data to achieve accurate bird flock counting. As demonstrated in the testing results, FlockCounter achieves state-of-the-art cross-domain performance on real-world datasets, attaining 91.3% accuracy on ground-based images and 92.3% on aerial-view images, substantially outperforming existing methods. This work provides a scalable, annotation-free solution for accurate large-scale bird flock counting, supporting efficient ecological monitoring and conservation efforts.

title: Marking Underwater Infrastructure: A Crab-Like Robot for Traversing Pipes_supp1-3681155.mp4
authors: Kathryn A Daltorio
publish: N/A
abstract: Underwater infrastructure projects are increasingly seeking robots to inspect, repair, maintain, and salvage in the ocean with minimal human diver time. To perform tasks that would otherwise require human workers, most remotely operated vehicles (ROVs) are designed to operate either while resting on bottom surfaces (which provides stability) or while suspended neutrally buoyant in the water column (which expands workspace, but requires careful thrust control for large forces or precise motion). Here, our hypothesis is that by using the legs to locomote on and grasp infrastructure substrates, stable toolpaths are generated while walking, without relying on localization or thrust actuators. To our knowledge, the robot presented here is the first legged, underwater robot capable of walking along pipes. To show repeatable, consistent toolpaths, we attach a marker that leaves continuous paths on the cylindrical surface of the pipe. Specifically, we demonstrate that our half meter-wide hexapod walks horizontally or climbs vertically on both 25.4 cm and 45.7 cm diameter pipes. Full (360°) circumferences on both pipes are completed in 75 s, and a 3.5 cm × 2.5 cm square is drawn in 3 s. Individually, these demonstrations parallel future application-specific tasks such as cutting a piling into sections for salvage, cleaning patches of pipeline for ultrasonic testing, or dislodging invasive species. Taken together, our work suggests that bio-inspired legs can provide robust robotic solutions to support ambitious undersea construction projects. Future work is expected to demonstrate value of combining closed-loop model-based control; adaptive behaviors for addressing biofouling or other pipe irregularities; underwater thrusters for initial positioning.

title: Accelerating Wireless Network Optimization with Topology-Aware Machine Learning: Exploring and Exploiting the Scheduling Structure
authors: Oluwaseun T. Ajayi, Suyang Wang, Yu Cheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: The classic multi-hop wireless network optimization problem has recently re-attracted many attentions due to some emerging applications such as wireless mesh network, space-air ground integrated networks, and 5G/6G integrated access and backhaul systems. The key issue in multi-hop wireless network optimization is the interference management through scheduling. The fundamental NP-hardness of this problem is that there are exponentially many possible independent sets (ISs), but only a small number of them will be scheduled in the optimal solution (termed as the scheduling structure). The existing literature on approximation algorithms are mainly along the direction of searching the ISs in a heuristic manner. With the capability of machine learning (ML) algorithms in supporting big data analytics, in this paper, we propose a two-stage self-supervised learning framework that can explore the scheduling structure from historical optimization instances, and such knowledge is then exploited to solve a new instance with greatly reduced computational overhead. At the exploring stage, we develop dimension reduction techniques for effective scheduling structure classification in a high-dimensional vector space. At the exploiting stage, we design an innovative structure criticalness indicator (SCI)-based IS selection algorithm that can robustly lead to close-to-optimal approximation of the average achievable throughput with roughly constant complexity. Furthermore, we also contribute a geometric canonical representation (GCR) system to equip the ML-assisted optimization framework with a topology-aware applicability. The proposed topology-aware ML (TAML) framework exhibits good generalizability across different network topologies and flow demands.

title: Feeling in Sync: Reducing the Impact of Network Latency on Joint Rowing
authors: Laura Simon, Lina Klass, Anton Lammert, Bernd Fröhlich, Jan Ehlers, Eva Hornecker
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Engaging in rhythmic synchronized activities such as dancing or coordinated exercises is a powerful driver of social connection, as interpersonal entrainment promotes feelings of closeness, empathy, and cooperation. However, in media-based settings such as Social Virtual Reality (SVR), maintaining synchrony is technically challenging. Even modest latency can disrupt the temporal coordination needed for shared rhythm, thereby diminishing social presence, user engagement and the quality of interaction. Our work explores whether synchrony-reinforcing feedback can mitigate latency effects in a dyadic VR rowing task. Participants received real-time visual, audio, or audio-visual feedback: visual feedback was provided through particle effects, and audio feedback through music, both of which intensified with stronger synchronization and diminished as it weakened. Latency was systematically varied. Our findings show that latency, especially higher delays, significantly disrupts coordination and a sense of togetherness. Participants often misattribute these disruptions to personal or interpersonal factors. Audio-visual feedback reduces these effects, supporting smoother interaction and masking symptoms of latency. The task load remains stable across latency levels, but decreases when clear, multimodal feedback is provided. Notably, while closeness remains robust, togetherness deteriorates in the absence of timely, multimodal cues. Our results suggest that latency effects can be partially addressed through perceptual design. Providing multimodal feedback is a key strategy for maintaining synchrony, reducing cognitive effort, and sustaining meaningful social interaction in latency-prone environments.

title: Real-Time Charging Control for Electric Roadways
authors: Yifan Dong, Ge Chen, Junjie Qin, S. Sivaranjani, Xiaonan Lu, Dionysios Aliprantis, David Love
publish: IEEE Transactions on Smart Grid
abstract: This paper studies the optimal wireless charging for electric vehicles (EVs) driving through an electric roadway (ER). We first develop a clairvoyant formulation to model and optimize the minute-level energy transmission between the ER and EVs, which incorporates ER microgrid operation, EV-side power balance, battery dynamics, and wireless power transfer limits as constraints. However, this formulation requires future information regarding how EVs will be driven, which is often unavailable during real-time operation. To circumvent the dependence on future information, we design a causal algorithm by leveraging the optimality of valley-filling charging profiles of the clairvoyant formulation. We theoretically establish the optimality of the proposed algorithm in an ideal setting without considering losses. The algorithm is then extended to account for practicalities such as estimation errors, losses, and incorporation of a stationary battery co-located with the ER. Finally, for distinct traffic conditions, we numerically validate the performance of the algorithm under realistic settings (incorporating losses) with different information patterns (with and without lookahead).

title: Evaluating Preattentive Features for Detecting Changes in Virtual Environments
authors: DongHoon Kim, Isaac Cho
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Visual perception plays a critical role in detecting changes within immersive Virtual Reality (VR) environments. However, as visual complexity increases, perceptual performance declines, making it more difficult to detect changes quickly and accurately. This study examines how visual features, known for facilitating preattentive processing, impact a change detection task in immersive 3D environments, with a focus on visual complexity, object attributes, and spatial proximity. Our results demonstrate that preattentive processing enhances change detection, particularly when the altered object is spatially isolated and not perceptually grouped with similar surrounding objects. Changes to isolated objects were detected more reliably, suggesting that perceptual isolation reduces cognitive load and draws more attention. Conversely, when a changed object was surrounded by visually similar elements, participants were less likely to detect the change, indicating that perceptual grouping hinders individual object recognition in complex scenes. These results provide guidelines for designing VR applications that strategically utilize spatial isolation and visual features to improve the user experience.

title: ICOSEC 2025 TOC
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)

title: HiRe: A Hierarchical Reconfigurable Architecture for Large-Scale Multichiplet DNN Accelerators
authors: Zhihong Chen, Jinming Zhang, Dongxu Lyu, Hao Chen, Jianfei Jiang, Weiguang Sheng, Chen Zhang, Guanghui He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: Multichiplet deep neural network (DNN) accelerators have evolved as promising modular solutions, offering enhanced performance, scalability, and cost-effectiveness. These architectures, however, suffer from the escalating communication bottleneck with increasing scale, primarily stemming from rising hop count and worsening link underutilization. The bottleneck is exacerbated by conventional fixed interconnection networks’ nonadaptability to diverse DNN dataflows. Moreover, an efficient routing tailored for large-scale networks with deadlock-freedom is needed for performance. To address these scalability challenges, leveraging the low-latency links and abundant interconnection resources with reconfigurability in the active interposer, we propose HiRe, a hierarchical reconfigurable network-routing co-design architecture for large-scale multichiplet DNN accelerators. The architecture introduces reconfigurable nodes (RNs) across on-chip and interchiplet hierarchical networks, enabling dynamic bypassing and network reconfiguration. Based on the network, it incorporates an efficient deadlock-free routing that combines simulated annealing (SA)-based communication scheduling with greedy path selection. Through the network-routing co-design, HiRe reduces the hop count and enhances the link utilization. The HiRe architecture is implemented and synthesized in a 55-nm CMOS process. Experimental results demonstrate that HiRe achieves a 14.3%–45.2% EDP reduction and a 14.1%–35.0% latency reduction compared to state-of-the-art (SOTA) innovations, effectively mitigating the large-scale communication bottleneck.

title: Firewall Configuration Review Tool with Secure Data Transmission
authors: Gujjarlamudi Mahesh Babu, Akhil Challapalli, S. Vijay Kumar, Pujitha Nandipati, Jayanth Bonthu, Arathi Choppakatla
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Firewall misconfigurations pose a significant threat to network security. Organizations rely heavily on firewalls to regulate traffic, enforce security policies, and block unauthorized access. However, over time, rule sets often become cluttered, redundant, or mismanaged, which weakens the effectiveness of the firewall and exposes vulnerabilities. This paper introduces a Firewall Configuration Review Tool designed to automate the analysis, validation, and optimization of firewall rules while ensuring secure data handling. The tool identifies redundant, shadowed, and conflicting rules, detects security gaps such as open ports and weak protocols, and recommends corrective actions. It supports configurations from multiple platforms, including Cisco ASA, iptables, Palo Alto, and cloud- native systems. By combining static and dynamic analysis with assisted rule resolution and simulation features, the tool helps administrators maintain consistent security policies and improve operational efficiency. Secure report generation using AES/RSA encryption and transfer through SFTP or TLS ensures data confidentiality and compliance with international standards. The system effectively bridges automation with administrator oversight, thereby strengthening trust, reducing manual effort, and enabling scalable governance in network security.

title: Performance Evaluation of Pre-Trained Networks in Face Recognition
authors: Santosh Reddy P, Ashwini R Malipatil
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Face recognition systems based on pre-trained deep networks have achieved high accuracy on benchmark datasets; however, their performance often degrades under multimodal, heterogeneous, and unconstrained conditions, and concerns related to robustness, fairness, and computational efficiency remain insufficiently explored. Addressing these gaps is critical for deploying reliable and equitable face recognition systems in real-world, security-sensitive applications. This study aimed to evaluate the performance of the pre-trained face recognition models in multimodal and heterogeneous conditions and to explore whether a foundation-based multimodal architecture can enhance the accuracy, robustness and fairness. The publicly available multimodal measurements of facial images, video frames and audio signals were used to build an experimental study. A baseline ArcFace model, hybrid CNN + Audio fusion model and a proposed foundation-based model (FaceFoundation-XL) were tested with the help of verification and identification protocols. Measures of performance were based on factors such as TAR at low FARs, Equal Error Rate, ability to perform in difficult conditions, demographic fairness and computational cost. The proposed model had 99.91 % accuracy on LFW, TAR <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=97.8 \%$</tex> with FAR <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=1 \mathrm{e}-4$</tex> and TAR <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$= 89.4 \%$</tex> with FAR <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=1 \mathrm{e}-6$</tex>, which was higher than that of the baseline (93.4 % / 76.1%) and hybrid (94.6 % / 79.3%) models. The robustness increased by up to 11.7 percent when using occlusion and cross-spectral conditions and demographic FNMR gaps dropped to 2.3-3.1 percent. Equal Error Rate has been reduced to 0.52 % with the false positives and false negatives respectively. These findings indicate that foundationbased multimodal face recognition is more accurate, robust, and fair, but at a higher cost of computation. The results offer practical solutions in the design and implementation of the next generation face recognition systems in the unconstrained settings.

title: InsightMeet: A Multilingual Meeting Summarization and Recap System
authors: Madhuri H. Badole, Himanshu R. Chougule, Shruti A. Jadhav, Shashank S. Khengre, Amey N. Mule
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The proposed system, InsightMeet: A Multilingual Meeting Summarization and Recap System, aims to intelligently manage hybrid and multilingual meetings through automation. It focuses on generating accurate transcriptions, concise summaries, and contextual recaps to enhance communication, collaboration, and decision-making efficiency. Existing meeting management solutions face challenges such as limited multilingual support, lack of contextual continuity between sessions, and high dependency on costly cloudbased APIs, leading to incomplete insights and reduced accessibility for diverse teams. To overcome these limitations, InsightMeet integrates advanced machine learning and natural language processing techniques. It utilizes Whisper for multilingual transcription, pyannote-audio for speaker diarization to distinguish participants, and transformer-based models such as BART, T5, and mBERT for high-quality summarization and task extraction. Additionally, Sentence-BERT embeddings enable contextual recap generation by linking unresolved discussion points across multiple meetings. The system provides structured outputs in formats like PDF and CSV, ensuring enhanced inclusivity, efficiency, and accountability in distributed work environments.

title: HierMamba: Multi-stage State-space Modeling for Multi-channel Speech Separation
authors: Jinwu Yin, Erhua Zhang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Multi-channel speech separation in complex acoustic environments necessitates the effective capture of both fine-grained temporal nuances and global spatial correlations. While CNN-based models are restricted by local receptive fields, Transformer-based architectures face efficiency bottlenecks due to their quadratic computational complexity O(L<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) and high memory consumption during long-duration audio processing. To address these challenges, this paper introduces HierMamba, a hierarchical multi-stage architecture that re-imagines neural beamforming through the lens of State Space Models (SSMs). Specifically, HierMamba first employs a Latent Mamba Layer following the encoder to refine initial feature representations. It then partitions the input into intra-chunk and inter-chunk stages, where Bidirectional Mamba (BiMamba) modules capture long-range temporal dependencies with linear complexity O(L). Furthermore, we introduce Spatial Mamba within the Transform-Average-Concatenate (TAC) module, combined with a concat-linear fusion strategy, to effectively model cross-channel spatial relationships. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that Hier-Mamba significantly outperforms the FaSNet-TAC baseline and achieves competitive performance compared to state-of-the-art Transformer models. Notably, HierMamba maintains superior computational efficiency and a smaller memory footprint, providing a scalable solution for low-latency audio processing in resource-constrained environments.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning Framework for Automated Plant Leaf Disease Detection
authors: Nipa Rani Das, Mirza Raquib, Farida Siddiqi Prity, Md. Imam Uddin Forkan, Afif Hossain Irfan, Md Jiabul Hoque, Mohammad Amzad Hossain
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The production of crops is a major beneficent supporter to the national economy in every country. Maintaining a country's agricultural growth demands the rapid and accurate diagnosis of plant diseases. Farmers can take alternative from deep learning since it can detect diseases in plant leaves at an early stage, which bolsters them in sidestepping crop negligence. Crop expansion and agricultural output can only be ensured with early disease disclosure. The foremost factor for effective crop supervision and reducing pecuniary losses is early and accurate disease diagnosis. This research comes across how well several deep learning models can ascertain diseases in plant leaves. The VGG19 model, RNNs, and CNNs are the three types of neural networks that are alluded to and evaluated in this study, harnessing plant leaf diseases as a case study. The CNN model landed 94.52% training accuracy and 90.59% validation accuracy. AlexNet captured 92.80% accuracy on the validation set and 90.68% accuracy on the training set. When it came to feature extraction, VGG-19 upheld solidity with training and validation accuracy of 79.14% and 87.16%, respectively. A validation accuracy of 97.49%, a training accuracy of 96.57%, and a validation loss of 0.5485 were all achieved by this hybrid approach, which eclipsed all single models. The results announce that hybrid models using deep learning are the best adjunct for automatically diagnosing illnesses in plant leaves.

title: Jump-Attentive Graph Learning for Camouflaged Financial Fraud in Transaction Networks
authors: Priyanka Muppuri
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This paper presents a finance-centric graph learning approach that embeds fraud detection directly into transaction networks through a two-pronged design: a similarity-aware neighbourhood sampler and a jump-attentive GNN. The sampler leverages relationship-specific covariance structure and mutualneighbor weighting to surface look-alike anchors while injecting controlled randomness to preserve rare but decisive financial risk signals. The proposed network combines multi-relational attention over sampled neighborhoods with depth-bounded jump connections that retain distant indicators such as chargebacks without succumbing to over-smoothing. Evaluated on a largescale, highly imbalanced e-commerce transaction graph and an external review-fraud benchmark, the method improves AUC and recall over strong baselines, demonstrating robust identification of camouflaged financial fraud in production-style settings. The results highlight a practical CS-for-finance pathway that unifies graph representation learning, imbalanced risk modeling, and deployable data engineering for modern payment ecosystems.

title: Labeling an Arabic Sockpuppet Dataset Using a Human-in-the-Loop Approach – Short Paper
authors: Rafeef Baamer, Ahmed S. Alghamdi, Mihai Boicu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Sockpuppet accounts pose significant threats to the integrity of online platform, but research on their detection in Arabic-language content remains limited due to the lack of suitable datasets. This paper introduces the first publicly available Arabic sockpuppet dataset, addressing a critical gap in the current research. The dataset comprises approximately 3,000 distinct accounts labeled as normal, Sockpuppet, or personal spammer. A Human-in-the-Loop labeling approach was adopted, involving 12 trained students as labelers over a six-week period. The labeling process included structured training, batch creation and scheduling, dataset labeling and peer review, and coordinator oversight. Additionally, students feedback was collected through a survey to evaluate the clarity and effectiveness of the labeling framework and instruction. Future work will involve labeling additional accounts, validating the labeling with another cohort and refining the labeling instructions based on the feedback received.

title: Predicting Heart and Lung Diseases using IoT and Machine Learning
authors: P Jayasri Lakshmi, N S Mohitha, Y Sangeetha, D Hemakalyani
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Predictive system for heart and lung disease risk assessment using real-time data provided by the Internet of Things through web application. A disease presence prediction facility is operational utilizing machine learning techniques via a random forest classifier. Factors tracked include the patient's beats per minute and oxygen saturation (SpO2) levels detected via the MAX30100 sensor integrated with an Arduino and supporting peripheral devices. This approach resulted with 99.8% accuracy in detecting heart disease and lung disease symptoms prediction. The lung disease risk, however, stems from the risk factors of its symptoms and includes smoking, physical inactivity, etc. Apart from predictions, the system aims to provide optimum health support to users with appropriate advanced technologies including precautionary advice and drug recommendations as part of the treatment plan. This model has the potential to help both healthcare providers and patients in the effective management of the respective health risks.

title: Impact Study of EV Grid Integration on a Nanogrid Scale Network
authors: Ismail Mustafa, Morgan Benner, Miguel Rivera, Ken King-Man Siu, Hassan Qandil, Naser Abusaad
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive impact study of electric vehicle (EV) grid integration on a residential-scale Nanogrid network through physical, on-site experimentation. A 100 kVA Nanogrid testbed was constructed and operated with real EV connections to evaluate the practical impacts of EV charging on power quality and system behavior. Continuous, high-resolution operational data were collected over a six-month period to capture realistic usage patterns and dynamic interactions. Based on the Nanogrid architecture, multiple operating scenarios were systematically examined, including: (i) traditional utility– load operation, (ii) renewable integration with photovoltaic (PV) resources, (iii) storage-supported operation with battery systems, and (iv) EV-integrated operation with EV chargers. Key power quality metrics, including apparent power, power factor, total harmonic distortion (THD), voltage, and frequency—were monitored and evaluated across all scenarios to quantify the physical impact of EV integration. Experimental results demonstrate trends consistent with theoretical expectations and highlight the distinct influence of EV charging behavior on residential-scale network performance, providing practical insights for Nanogrid planning and EV-ready infrastructure design. A clear understanding of EV grid integration strategy and metrics is identified from this.

title: Robust Adaptive Prescribed-Time Consensus in Directed Networks
authors: Sanjoy Mondal, Madhumita Pal, Santosh Sonar, Arghya Roy, Debabrata Bhattacharya
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive mathematical analysis of a adaptive prescribed-time sliding-mode consensus tracking scheme. The system under consideration consists of second-order agents required to track a reference trajectory un-der a directed communication graph and bounded disturbances. The controller design integrates three essential components: a prescribed-time scaling term that guarantees convergence within a user-defined horizon, consensus coupling in both position and velocity to account for inter-agent interactions, and a robust sliding-mode element with a boundary layer to mitigate the effect of disturbances. The resulting closed-loop error dynamics are analyzed using a Lyapunov framework to establish finite-time convergence of the agents’ tracking errors to a boundary layer before the prescribed time. An adaptive gain law is also introduced to enhance robustness. Finally, the correspondence between the MATLAB implementation and the underlying mathematical expressions is explained to highlight the reproducibility of the results.

title: Implementation of A Hybrid TRNG-PRNG Architecture for Efficient Random Number Generator
authors: Nandini H S, Shashi Raj K.
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Modern cryptography relies heavily on random numbers to facilitates safe procedures like key generation, encryption and authentication. The random numbers that are used must be both efficient and unpredictable in order to maintain the security of this system. This project presents a 32-bit hybrid random number generator that combines the advantages of a polynomial-based pseudo-random number generator (PRNG) and a true random number generator (TRNG). To create randomness, the TRNG employs hardware-level variations like carry-chain logic and tiny timing variations. The PRNG expands on this by continuously producing a stream of random values that are periodically updated with fresh seeds from the TRNG. This combination uses fewer digital resources while improving output quality and reliability. For safe embedded applications that requires a constant flow of random data, the design is a lightweight and sensible option because it lowers slice register usage by 36.9% and slice LUT usage by 47.5%.

title: A Novel Approach for Automatic Prediction of Canine Impaction using Deep Learning Algorithms
authors: M. Faritha Begum, A. Umamageswari
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Canine impaction is a common dental anomaly with significant implications for orthodontic treatment planning and patient outcomes. Early and accurate prediction of impacted canines is essential for minimizing complications such as root resorption, prolonged treatment duration, and surgical interventions. This paper proposes a novel deep learning-based framework for the automatic prediction of canine impaction using panoramic radiographs (orthopantomograms, OPGs). A convolutional neural network (CNN) architecture enhanced with transfer learning and attention mechanisms was developed to detect morphological and positional anomalies of the maxillary canines. The proposed model was trained on a curated dataset of annotated OPG images and evaluated against conventional diagnostic approaches and baseline machine learning methods. Experimental results demonstrate that the model achieves superior performance with accuracy of 95.4%, sensitivity of 94.2%, and specificity of 96.1%. Furthermore, the integration of Grad-CAM attention visualization provides interpretability, enabling clinicians to understand the model’s diagnostic focus. This study highlights the potential of deep learning algorithms in dental radiology for assisting orthodontists in early, precise, and automated canine impaction prediction.

title: Bi-objective AGV Routing Optimization Problem with Quantity Constraints and Workload Balancing
authors: Huihong Huang, Hong Zhang, Jingbo Zhou, Mengtao Zhang, Ruihan Guo
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Automated guided vehicle (AGV) scheduling is critical in automated logistics and manufacturing systems, especially under limited vehicle availability and unbalanced task allocation. This paper studies a bi-objective AGV routing problem with quantity constraints and workload balancing (BO-AGVRP-QB), aiming to minimize both total AGV traveling distance and the difference between maximum and minimum AGV distances. To solve this problem, a population-based multi-objective adaptive neighborhood search algorithm (PMANS) is proposed. A two-stage greedy initialization generates high-quality initial solutions, followed by an adaptive perturbation mechanism with local search to escape local optima and enhance exploration. Five problem-specific neighborhood operators improve search efficiency and solution quality. Comparative experiments with MOPSO and NSGA-II show that PMANS consistently outperforms benchmarks in solution quality and computational efficiency.

title: Design and Implement Machine Learning Based Predictive Analytics for Road Safety and Accident Prevention
authors: Hemant K Yadav
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Coded traffic accident analysis is critical for improving road safety, optimizing emergency response, and enabling smart transport systems due to the rapid rate of urbanization and the persistent increase in traffic quantities. This study delves into the use of ML models to forecast the severity of accidents using a massive dataset of US occurrences from 2016 to 2023. An ordered preprocessing pipeline was adopted, with missing value management, outlier elimination, categorical encoding, designed temporal and environmental variables and Synthetic Minority Over-Sampling Technique (SMOTE) to resolve the imbalance of classes. The AdaBoost and random Forest (RF) models were trained and optimized using 5-fold cross-validation to make them more generalizable and resilient. RF model was best with the greatest accuracy of 97.82% and the greatest precision of 99%, but AdaBoost also gives good performance of 95.67% accuracy and 96.56% recall. Both models performed better than the simple models like XGBoost, CatBoost, and Support Vector Machine (SVM). The analysis of feature significance revealed that variables linked to location, time, and weather were the most relevant predictions for policymakers to understand. The findings prove the relevance of ensemble learning to proactive road safety management, infrastructure planning and accident prevention. This paper connects machine learning with real-world traffic dynamics, which can be used in the future to integrate into intelligent transportation systems.

title: Scenario Based Assessment of Renewable Energy Growth Using LEAP Modeling for Sustainable Power Planning
authors: Arti Uikey, Ravinder Kumar, Sakhi Katre, Arvind Mittal, Archana Soni, Gaurav Dwivedi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: With the rising demand for electricity and growing worries about the environment, the need for sustainable electricity planning is imperative. The current paper proposes a scenario analysis on electricity generation in the state of Madhya Pradesh using the Long - range Energy Alternatives Planning (LEAP) model. The historical electricity generation pattern from the year 2004-05 to 2024-25 is studied and classified into the share of thermal, hydro and renewable electricity generation. The Business-as-usual (BAU) approach assumes the projection of electricity generation in the coming years using the linear trend approach from the period 2026-30. At the same time, an alternative approach is also designed in the research paper to demonstrate the effect of the rising share of renewable electricity on future electricity generation. The performance of the models is checked using statistical parameters like Root Mean Square Error (RMSE), Mean Absolute Error (MAE), the coefficient of determination (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>), and the correlation coefficient. The results in the paper fetched from the model suggest that despite the rise in the shares of other sources, the share of thermal electricity will continue to dominate the overall electricity generation in the state, while the fastest growth will emerge in the renewable electricity shell in the coming years, thus underlining the prospects of renewable electricity in ensuring sustainable electricity planning. The results in the paper confirm that the rise in the share of renewable electricity will reduce the share of thermal electricity in the electricity mix.

title: Automated Cervical Cancer Screening Using Pap Smears Image for Remote Diagnostic
authors: Hiruthika M, Dharshini V, Harini R, D Prema
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Cervical cancer is a major worldwide health problem many for underprivileged areas with limited access to diagnostic services. Improving patient outcomes requires early detection, but traditional screening techniques like Pap smears are time-consuming and highly dependent on the examiner's expertise. In order to address this issue, we propose a deep learning framework that uses ResNet-50 for cervical cell classification. We have refined this framework on the Herlev dataset of cervical cells. To increase its resilience to variations in the smear images, our classification with preprocessing techniques like resizing, normalization, and different augmentations. Test accuracy of 98.08 %, the optimized model performs well across all seven types of cervical cells. Additional metrics-precision, recall, Fl-score, and confusion matrix-are used to establish the diagnostic reliability of the model. The visual interpretability is further established through Grad-CAM heatmaps showing the discriminative regions influencing the classification outcome. Our proposed method can be used to enable fast, accurate, automated cervical cancer screening that can easily be employed in clinical practice and remote diagnostics.

title: SLA-MORL: SLA-Aware Multi-Objective Reinforcement Learning for HPC Resource Optimization
authors: Seraj Al Mahmud Mostafa, Aravind Mohan, Jianwu Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Dynamic resource allocation for machine learning workloads in cloud environments remains challenging due to competing objectives of minimizing training time and operational costs while meeting Service Level Agreement (SLA) constraints. Traditional approaches employ static resource allocation or single-objective optimization, leading to either SLA violations or resource waste. We present SLA-MORL, an adaptive multi-objective reinforcement learning framework that intelligently allocates GPU and CPU resources based on user-defined preferences (time, cost, or balanced) while ensuring SLA compliance. Our approach introduces two key innovations: (1) intelligent initialization through historical learning or efficient baseline runs that eliminates cold-start problems, reducing initial exploration overhead by 60%, and (2) dynamic weight adaptation that automatically adjusts optimization priorities based on real-time SLA violation severity, creating a self-correcting system. SLA-MORL constructs a 21-dimensional state representation capturing resource utilization, training progress, and SLA compliance, enabling an actor-critic network to make informed allocation decisions across 9 possible actions. Extensive evaluation on 13 diverse ML workloads using production HPC infrastructure demonstrates that SLA-MORL achieves 67.2% reduction in training time for deadline-critical jobs, 68.8% reduction in costs for budget-constrained workloads, and 73.4% improvement in overall SLA compliance compared to static baselines. By addressing both cold-start inefficiency and dynamic adaptation challenges, SLA-MORL provides a practical solution for cloud resource management that balances performance, cost, and reliability in modern ML training environments. Our code is open-source and available at https://github.com/big-data-lab-umbc/SLA-MORL.

title: Data-Driven Air Quality Classification: A Performance Analysis of K-Nearest Neighbors and Naive Bayes Algorithms
authors: Joven J. Beloy, Loravel A. Amizona, Jerson N. Maasin, Rhoderick D. Malangsa
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: This study compares the performance of K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) and Naïve Bayes in classifying air pollution levels using a dataset from Kaggle. Traditional air quality assessment methods are time-consuming, whereas machine learning provides a faster and more precise alternative. Data preprocessing involved format conversion, normalization, and feature selection before splitting into training and testing sets. Both models were implemented in WEKA, and results showed that Naïve Bayes outperformed KNN in accuracy, precision, recall, and ROC Area (0.989 vs. 0.944), demonstrating better classification ability. Given its efficiency and accuracy, Naïve Bayes is recommended for air pollution classification, making it a reliable choice for real-time air quality monitoring.

title: Artificial Intelligence Algorithms for Retransmission-based Secured MQTT Protocol in Internet of Things Environment
authors: G. Ramkumar
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The growing prevalence of the Internet of Things (IoT) has prompted the adoption of efficient communication protocols like Message Queuing Telemetry Transport (MQTT) across various sectors, including industrial, healthcare, and smart cities. MQTT's emphasis on energy and bandwidth efficiency introduces security vulnerabilities such as Denial-of-Service (DoS) attacks and message spoofing. To address these concerns, the paper presents Artificial Intelligence for Secured MQTT Retransmission in IoT Environment (AIRSMI), a security model that enhances MQTT with sealed communication, deep learning traffic classification, and a mechanized delay estimation. AIRSMI combines Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks for classifying network traffic, implementing techniques to boost detection accuracy while reducing latency. A probabilistic delay estimation model for real-time responsiveness in IoT applications. AIRSMI's performance in identifying sophisticated attacks while minimizing latency, thus promising integration of security and responsiveness suitable for critical IoT sectors like industrial automation and smart healthcare. The following performance metrics of the AIRSMI model with accuracy and loss calculation, comparative accuracy calculation, and comparative ROC calculation.

title: Neuro-Cybernetic Architecture for Enhancing Human-Robot Team Performance in Adaptive Environments
authors: K Sathish
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The architecture of neuro-cybernetics combines the brain-inspired adaptive control and intelligent signal processing in applying the human-robot cooperation in variable and unpredictable situations. It facilitates natural collaboration and intelligent operation among human users and their robotic companions using modular systems and bio-inspired frameworks and using real-time modifications. The distributed adaptive control (DAC) architecture has a cognitive module that provides task planning, safety monitoring, modeling of the worker, and management of the interaction. Modern methods, such as neural networks with genetic algorithm back-propagation and reinforcement learning, as well as deep neural networks make it possible to perform the precise interpretation of neural signals, and to cause an adaptable reaction of the robots. Live calibration and constant feedback enhance the smoothness of navigation by 23%, increase the level of user satisfaction to 87%, and promote teamwork collaboration efficiency by 18 percent higher. There is an over 95% accuracy in intent recognition, the cognitive load is reduced by 15%, and the response time falls below 250 ms, making the process highly safe and reliable with a relatively high compliance, over 92%, in both industrial and service settings since human and robot interaction with each other is safer, more adjustable, and effective.

title: The VGG16-Based Deep Learning Framework for Multi-Class Classification for Medicinal Leaf Diseases
authors: Seerat Singla, Sripelli Jagadish, Neha Sharma, Manisha Aeri, Aanchal Rawat
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Disease diagnosis in plant is a highly important process within the agricultural and medicinal plant studies, because the earlier the disease is diagnosed the more the direct influence on crop yield, quality and therapeutic use. The computer vision and deep learning technologies have become effective tools that can be used to automate this process and achieve high accuracy and efficiency. The dataset used in the current study consist of the AI-MedLeafX dataset, which consists of 10,858 original and 65,178 augmented images in 13 classes, such as Camphor (Healthy, Bacterial Spot, Shot Hole), Haritaki (Healthy, Bacterial Spot, Shot Hole), Neem (Healthy, Powdery Mildew, Shot Hole, Yellow Leaf) and Sojina (Healthy, Bacterial Spot, Yellow Leaf). The suggested methodology combines transfer learning based on VGG16 architecture as a feature extractor, which is augmented with dedicated layers (dense, batch normalization, and dropout) to enhance the generalization and minimize overfitting. The preprocessing involved image resizing, image normalization, dataset division and will use five diversity augmentation techniques. The model was trained over 50 epochs with a batch size of 8 and Adam optimizer and a learning rate of 0.001. The analysis of experimental evaluation showed good performance in all classes since the classification metrics, learning curves and confusion matrix analysis proved it. Finally, the framework provides a good scaled and robust system of automated medicinal leaf disease classification, which has potential to be successful in the real world of agriculture.

title: DunhuangMamba: A State Space Model-based Method for Dunhuang Mural Super Resolution
authors: Xin Li, Lei Yang, Diao Zhou
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Super resolution (SR) Dunhuang murals require models that simultaneously preserve long-range iconographic structure and recover fine-grained pigment textures and crisp contour lines from low-resolution captures. Prior CNN-based SR methods often struggle to capture global dependencies, while Transformer SR models can be computationally heavy when operating on high-resolution feature maps. We propose DunhuangMamba, a state space model (SSM)-based super-resolution framework designed for Dunhuang mural restoration. We introduce a Super-Resolution Mamba Block (SRMB) that integrates a Mamba-style selective SSM for efficient long-context modeling with an Inverted Residual Feed-Forward Network (IRFFN) to enhance local detail synthesis and stabilize feature refinement. Experimental results demonstrate that DunhuangMamba delivers consistently improved fidelity and perceptual quality, producing sharper edges, more coherent decorative patterns, and fewer texture artifacts.

title: Load-Dependent Modeling and Simulation of Piezoelectric Fast Steering Mirror and Fine Tracking System
authors: Yuxin Wang, Wen Zhang, Yashuai Han, Fan Wang, Yingwei Xia
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of model parameter mismatch caused by different load weights of piezoelectric fast steering mirrors (FSM) and the difficulty in carrying out long-distance fine tracking experiments, this paper proposes a load-dependent modeling and simulation method. Taking the Core-Morrow P33.T6S piezoelectric FSM as the research object, and targeting the actual condition equipped with a 5g mirror, the modified equivalent mass, stiffness, and damping parameters of the system are derived, and a refined electromechanical coupling dynamic model is established in Simulink. On this basis, a closed-loop optoelectronic fine tracking control system oriented to a 200m working distance is constructed, considering physical constraints such as system latency, geometric mapping, and angle multiplication. Simulation results show that the corrected model can accurately reflect the dynamic characteristics of the system, steady-state tracking precision at the micro-radian level is achieved, verifying the effectiveness of this modeling method in the design of long-distance optoelectronic tracking and pointing systems.

title: State-of-Charge Estimation for Lithium-Ion Batteries using CatBoost Regressor
authors: Surasi Sharan Kumar, Vinod Kumar D.M., Ram Deshmukh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Accurate State-of-Charge estimation is required for lithium-ion batteries to work effectively in a Battery Management System environment. The conventional models lack capturing complex nonlinear electrochemical dynamics under variant operating conditions. In this paper, the performance of advanced ensemble machine learning-based techniques is presented: CatBoost Regressor, AdaBoost Regressor, and Support Vector Regressor, each contributing to the SoC prediction of Li-ion batteries. In this work, the models were trained and tested by simulated discharge data with features like voltage, current, and temperature. The proposed CatBoost Regressor performed very well and achieved 0.0098 MAE with an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score of 98.29%, while the minimum MAE reached by AdaBoost and SVR was 0.0125 and 0.0382, respectively. The results confirm that CatBoost presents state-of-the-art accuracy in mapping the complex behavior of the battery. The obtained high-precision model is very suitable for real-time deployment in the BMS and promises enhanced reliability and longevity.

title: Semantic Segmentation of Kale Plant Images Using Deep Learning for Phenotypic Feature Analysis
authors: Halima Audu, Varun Kumar Reddy Dodda, Ruth Agada, Jie Yan, Anne Osano, George Ude, Supriyo Ray
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Agriculture is integral to the development and sustainability of human civilization. Its impact is far-reaching, affecting the economy in addition to supplying food. The agricultural industry requires a lot of resources. However, the limited availability of resources and continued environmental deterioration are no longer exclusive occurrences in specific regions. This presents alternatives to traditional farming. In areas where regular interaction is difficult, non-contact, highly efficient methods for monitoring systems can be utilized to observe the health of plants and other organisms. Through this observation, farmers can remotely inspect crop yields, specifically plant leaves, fruits, etc., for diseases during cultivation. This study aims to investigate the application of deep learning models in recognizing the parameters associated with hydroponically grown kale. These parameters include the leaves, root system, fruit color, length, width, shape, and weight of the crop yield as they relate to the healthiness of the kale being grown. To address the limited data set availability, in this study, image data will be collected from the remote sensing system, which shows the various growth stages of kale and its corresponding state of health.In this paper, we study several deep learning models to identify key kale morphologies as they correspond to relevant plant phenotypes. The main challenge with the data collection method in the study is the impact of illumination and background exposure as the kale sample grows. To that end, two sets of experiments were conducted analyze the performance traditional and deep learning methods under the complex environmental conditions. In this study, we used U-Net, DeepLabv3 + ResNet-50 backbone, Mixed transformer model, and Swin+ConvTrans2d to segment kale phenotypes for further analysis.

title: Neural Network-based Techniques for Classifying IoT-Enabled Smart Irrigation Data in Agriculture
authors: Radha Mahendran, Priyanka Ravindra Dhumal, Nanthini L, Shantanu Datta, Mohaideen A, M. S. Mohamed Mallick
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Conventional farming has supported human advancement for a long time, but humanity will need to start using smarter, more effective farming techniques because of rising population constraints and water scarcity. Agriculture 4.0 has arisen in this setting, improving resource management through the use of cutting-edge technology like crop health monitoring and remote sensing. Using SIS, or Smart Irrigation Data in Agriculture, to maximise water efficiency is a crucial part of this transition. In order to enhance SIS prediction and classification, this work suggests a hybrid model called CNRN-AHB. There are two parts to the model's operation: preprocessing, which uses data refinement techniques to make sure the input is high-quality, and training, which uses a combination of CNN and RNN networks optimised by the Adaptive Honey Badger algorithm. The model's prediction capabilities are improved by the hybrid technique, which allows it to successfully distinguish between exudates and non-exudates. The CNRN-AHB model outperforms current state-of-the-art methods, according to analytical data, which demonstrate an accuracy of about 95.24 percent in classification and prediction. Contributing to sustainable farming and efficient water management within modern agriculture, this research highlights the significance of intelligent data-driven strategies in smart irrigation.

title: Scientific Crosstalk: Analyzing Sentiment-Topic Alignment in Personal and Scientific Discourse
authors: Praveshika Bhandari, Connor Z. Young, Jan P. Springer, Aryabrata Basu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Scientific discovery is often portrayed as a purely rational process, yet personal writing of scientists frequently reveal rich emotional narratives interwoven with their intellectual journeys. We present Scientific Crosstalk, a computational framework leveraging topic modeling, sentiment analysis, and similarity metrics that allows to explore the alignment between emotional expression in personal correspondences and formal scientific publications. Using a curated corpus of Albert Einstein’s personal letters and research papers, we apply transformer-based sentiment classifiers and BERTopic to extract topic-specific sentiment distributions. Cosine similarity and Jaccard index measures are used to quantify emotional alignment across matched thematic regions. To visualize these dynamics over time, we construct a temporal knowledge graph linking emotional states to stages of scientific development. Our preliminary results reveal measurable emotional correspondences during key periods of discovery, suggesting that affective language in informal texts may reflect or even anticipate formal scientific reasoning. Our work offers a novel methodological contribution to affective computing, science-of-science studies, and interdisciplinary application of machine-learning techniques, opening new directions for analyzing cognitive and emotional processes that shape innovation.

title: A Miniaturized Circularly-Polarized Patch Antenna with a 3D-Printed Heterogeneous Substrate
authors: Patrick Bartley, Nghia Nguyen-Trong, Nicholas Lawrence, Christophe Fumeaux
publish: 2026 Australasian Symposium on Antennas (ASA)
abstract: Volumetric density-based modulation of relative permittivity enabled by high-permittivity 3D-printable dielectric filaments offers engineers significantly greater design freedom than that offered by pre-fabricated homogeneous dielectric substrates. Through the addition of an asymmetrically oriented high permittivity region, a single layer miniaturized, circularlypolarized microstrip patch antenna is introduced for operation at 3 GHz. The miniaturized patch is fabricated with the filament Preperm ABS1000 and has a realized gain of 6.7 dBic, an axial ratio of 2.1 dB, and an operating bandwidth of 1.99%. To demonstrate the effectiveness of the miniaturization technique, a model is compared in simulation to a conventional homogeneous substrate circularly-polarized antenna.

title: Hybrid Res with ANFIS-Based Control for Efficient Power Management
authors: Prakriti Deepak, Adeeb Uddin Ahmad, Arunima Verma
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This study introduces an innovative Hybrid Renewable Energy System (RES) which combines solar and wind power generation, optimized by Adaptive Neuro-Fuzzy Inference System (ANFIS) controller for efficient power management and storage. The integration of solar and wind energy sources addresses irregular nature of renewable power generation, thereby giving a more stable and reliable supply of energy to the battery storage system. The proposed ANFISbased controller adapts in real-time to variations in the input energy from both sources, dynamically managing power flow to the battery while mitigating fluctuations. The performance of ANFIS controller was compared with conventional methods, including systems utilizing only Fuzzy Logic Controllers (FLC) and those without wind power integration. The results demonstrate that the ANFIS controller significantly enhances system efficiency by minimizing settling time and minimizing power fluctuations. By effectively handling the fluctuations inherent in RESs, the system improves the overall stability of the energy storage process, ensuring that the battery receives a more consistent charge. Moreover, the hybrid integration of solar and wind power further optimizes energy utilization, improving the system's resilience and efficiency. Our analysis shows that the ANFIS-based control strategy outperforms the conventional FLC-based approach, with a notable reduction in both transient fluctuations and the time required to reach a stable state. This work illustrates the potential of ANFIS in managing complex hybrid RES, offering a more adaptive and reliable solution for renewable energy integration in energy storage systems. The proposed system not only enhances performance but also offers a practical solution for sustainable energy management in real-world applications.

title: A Comparative Analysis of City Traffic Congestion Prediction Using SARIMA, LSTM and XGBoost
authors: Prashant Yadav, Rahul Kumar Verma, Jay Katre, Shubhra Jain, Varun Sharma, Neelu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: In order for smart cities to effectively handle traffic congestion, short-term traffic predictions must be precise. Using a year-long simulated urban traffic dataset, this study compares three common forecasting techniques: Seasonal ARIMA (SARIMA), LSTM networks, and eXtreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost). LSTM represents non-linear temporal behaviour but is susceptible to tiny amounts of data, XGBoost leverages engineered temporal characteristics for powerful tabular learning, and SARIMA provides a clear statistical baseline that indicates weekly seasonality. Based on the experimental results, XGBoost has the best MAPE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(11.21 \%)$</tex> and SARIMA has the lowest RMSE (7.34) when utilised independently. Additionally, we examine basic convex ensembles and discover that merging models consistently yields superior outcomes. To illustrate, an LSTM-XGBoost ensemble achieves the lowest MAPE of 10.89 % and a SARIMA-LSTM ensemble reduces RMSE to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7. 0 5 \%}$</tex>. Based on these findings, lightweight hybrid ensembles are an effective and practical method for predicting urban traffic delays in the short term, outperforming individual models.

title: Design of a Dynamic Tracking and Control System for Environmental Emergency Supplies Based on RFID and GIS
authors: Shiyuan Jia, Nanhua Cao, Kandi Yang, Chuang Wang, Longji Sun
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: To address the technical bottlenecks in dynamic tracking of environmental emergency supplies, such as low positioning accuracy, lagging multi-source data fusion, and weak anti-interference capabilities, a dynamic tracking and control system based on RFID and GIS is designed, and an Adaptive Weighted RFID-GIS Spatial Collaborative Positioning Optimization Algorithm (AW-RGCL) is proposed. The system adopts a three-layer architecture of hardware-software-data, achieving full-process coverage of material data collection, real-time positioning, and visualized control. The AW-RGCL algorithm overcomes the limitations of traditional algorithms, such as fixed weights and insufficient anti-interference capabilities, through adaptive weight allocation, outlier filtering, and coordinate iterative optimization. A simulation platform based on MATLAB and ArcGIS is built, and comparative experiments are conducted in an emergency scenario in a chemical industrial park. The results show that the average positioning error of the algorithm is 0.32m, which is 72.6% higher than the traditional RFID triangulation algorithm and 53.6% higher than the fixed weight fusion algorithm; the data update delay is 0.21s, the tag recognition rate is 99.4%, and the running time is 0.08s. The system has undergone functional testing and field verification, enabling precise control of supplies and providing reliable technical support for environmental emergency response.

title: A Location-based Support Vector Regression Approach for Wet-Bulb Temperature Prediction
authors: Epifelward Niño O. Amora, Jose C. Agoylo, Jorton A. Tagud, Jimson A. Olaybar, Keano Nikko L. Sy, Alex C. Bacalla
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Wet-bulb temperature is a critical metric for assessing human heat stress, integrating both air temperature and humidity to reflect the threshold of physiological tolerance. Traditional estimation methods typically rely on empirical formulas that lack spatial adaptability and often fail under extreme or non-standard conditions. This study presents a location-based modeling framework for wet-bulb temperature prediction using Support Vector Regression (SVR). A dataset containing over 75,000 weather observations, enriched with geospatial coordinates and meteorological variables, was utilized to train and evaluate several machine learning models. The SVR model achieved superior performance, with a mean absolute error (MAE) of 0.234 °C and an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score of 0.9923, outperforming both linear regression and K-nearest neighbors, while avoiding the overfitting observed in random forest regressors. The model’s integration of spatial inputs enables accurate estimation across diverse climatic regions. These results demonstrate SVR’s potential as a robust and scalable solution for wet-bulb temperature forecasting, with applications in heat hazard mapping, public health planning, and climate resilience initiatives.

title: A Hybrid Graph Neural Network and Deep Reinforcement Learning Framework for Adaptive Network Optimization
authors: Shashipurna Kurapati, Subramanya Bharathvamsi Koneti, Mohanraju Muppala
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The escalating complexity and scale of modern communication networks, driven by the proliferation of IoT devices, 5G/6G technologies, and stringent low-latency application requirements, necessitate a paradigm shift from traditional, often heuristic, optimization methods. Artificial Intelligence (AI), primarly Machine Learning (ML), offers a potential avenue to automating and enhancing network management. This paper proposes innovative hybrid framework that synergistically integrates Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) and Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) for adaptive and robust network optimization. The GNN component learns rich representations of the network's topological structure and dynamic state, effectively capturing spatial dependencies. This learned representation is then leveraged by a DRL agent to make informed control decisions, such as adaptive routing and resource allocation, enabling it on generalize across different network topologies and traffic patterns. We conceptualize the network as a graph and formulate the optimization obstacle as a Markov Decision Process (MDP). Simulations conducted on a variety of network topologies demonstrate that the proposed framework significantly outperforms conventional OSPF-based routing and a standalone DRL approach in key performance metrics, encompassing packet delivery ratio, end-to-end delay, and network throughput, especially under dynamic and high-load conditions. Our findings indicate that the fusion of structural learning (via GNNs) and sequential decision-making (via DRL) provides a powerful and generalizable foundation for autonomous network management.

title: Design of Fuzzy based Linearizer to eliminate the effect of Swelling and Shrinkage for accurate level measurement in Industrial Boiler
authors: Abir Das, Bansari Deb Majumder, Moumita Ghosh, Tanmay Sinha Roy, Joyanta Kumar Roy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)
abstract: Boiler drum is an important process of any thermal power plant. For the safe operation of boiler drum accurate level measurement is the key factor. Researchers have developed many level sensors for continuous level measurement in boiler drum. However, the measurement is significantly affected because of the cross sensitivity effect of various influencing factors. To linearize these cross sensitivity of level sensor, various soft computing based methods have been reported in the literature. Fuzzy Based linearizer has been successfully used for the said purpose. Swelling and Shrinkage of boiler drum is found to be one of the factor which affects the accuracy of the level measurement. An attempt has been made to develop a Fuzzy based linearizer which is capable to generate corrected level with the variation of errors in real-time basis.

title: Identifying Network Traffic Patterns Associated with Ransomware Targeting Data in Transit
authors: Kadali Sai Vinesh, Kavita Devi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Ransomware has evolved beyond endpoint-based file encryption attacks into elaborate campaigns that exploit network communication channels and target data in transit. The paper provides a systematic overview of the behavioral aspects of ransomware that can be detected using network traffic profiling and how these indicators can be used to detect malware infections at an early stage before extensive data loss or encryption takes place. The paper reviews current methods for detecting abnormal communication patterns related to ransomware, based on machine learning, deep learning, and software-defined networking (SDN). Existing detection frameworks have been comparatively analyzed and strengths, weaknesses, and difficulties handling encrypted traffic with high throughputs are highlighted. The paper also presents the critical research gaps connected to encrypted traffic visibility, insufficient availability of datasets, scalability, and practical implementation. In general, the survey will aid the creation of sound, efficient real-time ransomware detection systems that will add greater control in terms of data protection during transit.

title: NoiseVis: A Visualized Robustness Analysis System for Heterogeneous Graph Neural Networks
authors: Zhiwen He, Lihua Zhou
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Heterogeneous Graph Neural Networks (HGNNs) have garnered significant attention in recent years as an effective approach for heterogeneous graph representation learning. However, existing HGNNs typically assume that training data is independent and identically distributed (i.i.d.), without considering the impact of noise interference and distribution shifts, limiting their robustness in practical applications. In this work, we develop a visualization system called NoiseVis, which integrates multiple heterogeneous graph representation learning models, aimed at exploring the robustness of different models against noise interference. Through this system, users can intuitively assess the performance degradation of different models under various noise conditions and select the most suitable model for real-world applications.

title: Active Learning Pipelines for Efficient Annotation of Large-Scale IOT Video Streams
authors: Kumaresh Sheelavant, Sanjay Kumar Suman, G Sravanthi, L. Bhagyalakshmi, J Jesintha Princy, D. Sravanthi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Manual frame-level annotation for large-scale IoT video streams in Indian urban deployments imposes prohibitive cost and latency, undermining timely analytics for traffic management and incident response. This work presents an edgeaware active-learning pipeline that fuses epistemic uncertainty sampling with geometric/topological diversity through persistence-based summaries of short embedding windows and temporal-consistency regularizers. The pipeline is engineered for low-latency on-device operation and amortized topological computation. Evaluation uses an India urban traffic video corpus and a ResNet-18 / video-embedding backbone as a pragmatic baseline (ResNet-18 edge pipeline and dataset split reported in the reference). Empirically, the hybrid acquisition reduced annotation expenditure by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 40-60 \%$</tex> to reach operational F1/AUC comparable to full supervision, and with 50 % of labels achieved AUC up to 0.998 (marginally exceeding the baseline AUC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\approx} \mathbf{0. 9 9 7}$</tex>) while preserving sub-second decision windows through model compression and batch amortization. The results indicate that topologically-aware, temporally-aware acquisition materially increases per-label utility and enables practical, privacy-preserving municipal rollouts that minimize human-in-the-loop burden.

title: Research on Compatibility Testing of Deep Learning Frameworks on Heterogeneous Computing Power
authors: Chaosheng Yao, Lei Cui, Zheheng Liang, Jinbo Zhang, Jiaxin Lin
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: This paper aims to systematically evaluate the compatibility performance of deep learning frameworks on Huawei Ascend's heterogeneous computing platform. By constructing a three-tier testing framework that includes basic environment verification, functional correctness validation, and performance bottleneck probing, along with a specialized testing method for dynamic operator scheduling efficiency and an automated test data pipeline, a comprehensive assessment of compatibility is achieved. The experimental results show that the Ascend platform performs excellently in terms of functional correctness, with only a 0.2% accuracy difference in the ResNet-50 model, and the training throughput of the Vision Transformer model reaches 85% of the A100. However, the performance gap for the BERT model is 28%. Root cause analysis reveals that the initial iteration compilation overhead for the LSTM model is five times that of subsequent iterations, while operator fusion optimization can improve the performance of specific computational segments in the ViT model by 35%. The research indicates that the Ascend platform has good foundational compatibility but still requires optimization in dynamic graph support and operator fusion. Corresponding improvement suggestions are made, providing technical support for the development of the heterogeneous computing ecosystem.

title: Micro-motion Parameter Estimation for Rotary-wing targets Using Scintillation envelope
authors: Xing Wang, Degui Yang, Jin Li
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Rotary-wing targets, as integral components of modern aerial vehicles, play a crucial role in ensuring airborne safety through detection and identification. The estimation of micro-motion parameters of rotary-wing targets is paramount in this regard. In this paper, we address the issue of estimating micro-motion parameters of rotary-wing targets and propose a feature extraction method based on the sinusoidal envelope of scintillation bands. This method, grounded in gradient correction, involves computing the first-order partial derivative information of the mathematical expression of the time-frequency analysis method to eliminate scintillation bands and extract the sinusoidal envelope from the Doppler characteristics of rotary-wing targets. Finally, an improved inverse Radon transform is employed to extract features from the sinusoidal envelope. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of this method in accurately estimating micro-motion parameters and blade count of rotary-wing targets in the absence of prior information.

title: MARVEL: Multi-modal Analysis and Reasoning for Violence Explanation with Large Language Models
authors: Kushal Badal, Subhram Dasgupta, Md Tasnim Alam, Xiaohong Yuan, Kaushik Roy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Violence detection in surveillance footage remains a critical challenge for public safety; however, existing approaches suffer from a fundamental limitation: their black-box nature prevents security personnel from understanding the rationale behind automated alerts. This paper introduces MARVEL (Multimodal Analysis and Reasoning for Violence Explanation with Large Language Models), a novel framework that addresses this interpretability gap by combining advanced violence detection with comprehensive explainability. Our approach integrates three complementary modalities, pose dynamics, motion patterns, and scene context, through an attention-based fusion mechanism, while employing hierarchical explainability through SHAP and LIME to identify violence-indicative regions at both feature and frame levels. The distinguishing contribution of MARVEL lies in its ability to generate human-readable security narratives through Large Language Model integration, transforming technical detection outputs into actionable intelligence reports. Extensive evaluation on the RWF-2000 and RLVS datasets demonstrates robust performance, achieving 92. 4% overall precision with 92. 8% F1 score on the combined dataset. In particular, the system exhibits strong generalization capabilities, attaining 86.7% accuracy on RWF-2000 surveillance footage and an exceptional 98. 9% accuracy in various scenarios from RLVS. Beyond quantitative metrics, MARVEL generates detailed incident reports that articulate specific behavioral indicators, temporal patterns, and risk assessments. This research work represents a novel comprehensive solution to explainable violence detection, bridging the gap between automated surveillance technology and practical security operations through interpretable multimodal analysis coupled with natural language explanation generation.

title: Data Imbalance in Binary Outcomes: A Comparative Study of Classifiers on Healthcare Datasets
authors: Joshua Remlalliana, Parthiban S, Vidya P
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
