title: Adaptive TCP Congestion Control using Deep Reinforcement Learning for TCP CUBIC Parameter Optimization
authors: Deepanshu Sharma, Varun Sharma, Madhur Bakshi, Vivek Mehta
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Transmission Control Protocol congestion control algorithm tells the rate at which a sender injects data traffic into the network, while balancing throughput, delay and packets lost. Although TCP CUBIC is the default algorithm in modern world deployments, its fixed configuration makes it difficult to work well with highly variable network conditions such as wireless loss, long range communication, and persistent queues. Our work introduces a hybrid and adaptive variant of TCP CUBIC in which a deep reinforcement learning (DRL) agent is trained using Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) and it continuously adjusts two internal parameters: the multiplicative window reduction factor (beta) and the cubic growth coefficient (C). To ensure stability of TCP CUBIC algorithm, updates are restricted to safe ranges. Through experiments in five NS-3 network scenarios, the learned agent demonstrates consistent gains in environments with loss or delay, by showing improved queue behaviour under bufferbloat, and maintaining fairness when competing with standard CUBIC flows, it indicates that selective and bounded learning-based adjustments can enhance TCP behaviour without altering the core protocol.

title: The Role of Self-Efficacy and Technological Readiness in Student Reliance on Generative AI
authors: Joni Suhartono, I Gusti Made Karmawan, Drajad Wiryawan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The rapid, unprecedented adoption of Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) in higher education presents a dualism of revolutionary potential and radical disruption, raising fundamental questions about academic integrity and over-reliance. Indonesian university students, in particular, demonstrate this global phenomenon, where their high digital fluency aligns with the country's national strategic focus on digitalising education. While existing technology adoption models often overlook the factors that foster deep dependence, this study addresses that gap by developing and validating an inclusive model that identifies the determinants of Generative AI Dependency (GAD) among this key demographic. Utilising Structural Equation Modeling (SEM) on a sample of 453 Indonesian university students, the model incorporates constructs such as Academic Self-Efficacy (ASE), Technological Readiness (TR), Perceived Usefulness (PU), Perceived Ease of Use (PEU), Effort Expectancy (EE), Personal Innovativeness (PI), Performance Expectation (PX), and Positive Experiences (PE). The results indicate that ASE, PU, and PE all have a statistically significant, positive impact on GAD. Notably, we found that Performance Expectation (PX) has no substantial direct influence on GAD. Conversely, Technological Readiness (TR) has a significant impact on Academic Self-Efficacy (ASE), which, in turn, influences GAD. Overall, the model explains a meaningful portion of the variance in the endogenous constructs, confirming its relevance for predicting generative AI usage and student behavioural outcomes. These findings provide valuable insights for educators and policymakers seeking to promote a balanced and healthy integration of AI in higher education.

title: CORDIC-Accelerated FPGA Design for Direct Kinematics of Omniwheeled Mobile Robots
authors: Tarang Srivas, Deepak Kumar Dubey, Harshit Manktala, Aaditya Prakash Yadav, Nalin Kumar Sharma, Padmini Singh, Chandan Yadav
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This paper presents a high-throughput, low-latency FPGA-based architecture for computing the direct kinematics of a 3-wheeled omnidirectional wheeled mobile robot (OWMR) using the Coordinate Rotation Digital Computer (CORDIC) algorithm. Conventional software-based techniques suffer from significant processing delays due to their sequential execution, making them unsuitable for real-time robotic control. The proposed design calculates the sine and cosine values needed to convert wheel angular velocities into global linear and angular velocities by utilizing the shift-add-based nature of the CORDIC algorithm and the inherent parallelism and pipelining of FPGAs. The design is synthesised on an Artix-7 FPGA that shows a computation latency of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.41 \mu ~\mathrm{s}$</tex> and a peak frequency of 141.2 MHz. Simulation shows that the design offers a scalable and resource-efficient solution with substantially lower logic resource requirements than multiplier-based alternatives.

title: SmartComply-RL+: An AI and ML-Driven Framework for Compliant, Cost-Optimal, and Carbon-Conscious Workload Placement Across Multi-Cloud Environments
authors: Ankit Dubey, Shubham Sharma, Naween Kumar
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: SmartComply-RL+ is a unified AI and ML framework for compliant, cost-efficient, and carbon-conscious workload placement across multi-cloud environments. The framework integrates deterministic compliance filtering, supervised predictive governance, deep reinforcement learning (DQN), and automated policy enforcement. In experiments with 1,500 synthetic deployment records, SmartComply-RL+ reduced costs by an average of 35% (up to 47%), improved response times by 28%, raised availability to 99.97%, and lowered carbon footprint by 31% while maintaining 100% compliance. Compared to static policies and genetic algorithms, SmartComply-RL+ consistently achieved higher cost savings, better performance, and stronger sustainability. These findings validate its effectiveness as a scalable and interpretable decision-making system for multi-cloud workload orchestration.

title: Intelligent Control of WSN-based Monitoring Systems using Neuro-Fuzzy Models
authors: Sayan Nath, Bidyutmala Saha, Arijit Saha, Niloy Das, Kushal Bhattacharya, Granthik Saha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)
abstract: Real time data monitoring systems would greatly recognize the flexibility and cost benefits offered by Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs). As for WSNs monitoring systems, they would face major problems to data energy efficiency, data reliability, and environmental reliability, which would necessitate an advanced control system. This study discusses about adaptive control systems based on Neuro-Fuzzy Inference Systems to WSN based systems, which stand to gain from such adaptive control systems. The neuro-fuzzy model combines the language knowledge representation of fuzzy systems with the learning power of neural networks to make decisions in real time and with an understanding of the situation. Our model in simulations shows that the new methods are more robust, accurate, and energy-efficient than the old ones.

title: Bird sound recognition based on photoelectric residual reservoir computing
authors: Siyu Li, Dianzuo Yue, Keke Yan, Yushuang Hou, Xiumei Cao, Junsheng Dong
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: To tackle the challenges of low accuracy and high model complexity in noisy bird sound recognition, this work proposes an efficient scheme based on photoelectric residual reservoir computing (RC). The system extracts and preprocesses audio segments, employs an adaptive Mel-frequency cepstral coefficient method for feature extraction, and maps features into a time series via masking. A residual structure with two parallel photoelectric reservoirs is constructed, where the second reservoir learns the residual errors of the first to enhance recognition. Experimental results on 12 bird species show that the residual system achieves 86.5% accuracy, outperforming a single reservoir. Notably, when the first reservoir operates suboptimally (74.1% accuracy), the residual system improves performance by about 10%. This approach maintains low energy consumption and complexity, offering a practical solution for lightweight bioacoustic monitoring.

title: Neurophysiological Assessment of Human Stress Levels via Electroencephalographic Signal Analysis in Reaction to Musical Stimuli
authors: Pallav Dutta, Rupa Bhattacharya, Aparjita Paul, Anilesh Dey, Aurpan Majumder, Banshibadan Maji
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)
abstract: Most of us have dealt with stress at some point in our lives. Accurately measuring stress enables to determine how much it is burdening a person and opens the door to actions to reduce it and enhance wellbeing. This study examines how English and Bengali songs impact stress levels by examining brain activity. As they listened to various musical tracks, their brain impulses were recorded using a four-channel MUSE headband. A questionnaire about state and trait anxiety was also used to gauge participants' stress levels. Whereas the Bengali songs encompassed five genres popular, patriotic, melodic, classical, and Rabindra sangeet the English songs were separated into four categories: rock, metal, electronic, and rap. Researchers used five distinct feature types from the EEG data absolute power, relative power, coherence, phase lag, and amplitude asymmetry to categorize stress levels. Five frequency bands and four channels were used to extract these. Four machine learning algorithms logistic regression, stochastic gradient descent, multilayer perceptron, and sequential minimum optimization were used to partition stress into two or three categories. Logistic regression performed best, achieving 98.76% accuracy for two-level classification and 95.06% for three-level classification. Bengali music was found to be more helpful than English music for reducing stress. Bengali music was found to be more effective than English music at reducing stress. Within each language, there were no discernible differences between genres. However, compared to men, women reported more substantial changes in stress levels, indicating that women might react more strongly to the calming effects of music.

title: FINNET: A Hierarchical Graph Learning Framework for Adaptive Cross-Market Financial Risk Prediction
authors: Anandu P S, Vyshali J Gogi, Azarudheen S
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Systemic financial risk emerges from complex multi-scale interactions among entities, sectors, and markets. We introduce FINNET, a hierarchical graph neural network framework that models these vertical dependencies through volatility-aware adaptive pooling. Our approach features: (1) a tri-scale graph structure capturing entity, sector, and market dynamics; (2) dynamic embeddings combining static features with time-varying signals; (3) transfer learning for emerging markets; and (4) transparent risk decomposition for regulatory compliance. Validated on 58,432 financial entities across three continents, FINNET achieves 0.891 AUC with only 3.8% performance degradation during crises, while providing early warnings 15 days before failures.

title: Hybrid CNN-LSTM and Random Forest Framework for Predicting the Severity of Traffic Accidents
authors: Shaik Mohammad Jany, Kattula Sasidharsai, Marella Gopi Krishna, Prathipati Ananda Raju, Mallikarjuna Rao Gundavarapu, B. Srinivasarao
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: One of the biggest issues in the world today is road traffic accidents and the number of accidents taking place in an urban set up is on the rise due to congestion and poor road driving conditions. To minimise the scale of accidents, there is need to maximise emergency response and road safety by trying to predict accidents in real time to aid the decision-making process. The current paper proposes a hybrid predictive model of accident severity based on conventional ensemble learning and deep learning approaches. The framework is based on Convolutional Neural Network and Long Short-Term Memory (CNN-LSTM) to sequentially learn features and a control based on a Random Forest. Categoric encoding, normalization, and class dissimilarity were pre-processed with the Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE). As a result, the Random Forest model has achieved a moderate performance of 83.77 percent and fairly good performance (Precision: 0.2792, Recall: 0.3333, F1-score: 0.3039), and the CNN-LSTM model has achieved a much higher performance of 91.98 percent and good balanced performance The given framework can be deployed

title: Applying Machine Learning to Classify Automobile Repossession Success
authors: Preston Billion-Polak, Andy Sinclair, Taghi Khoshgoftaar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Automobile repossession and impoundment is crucial to enforcing loan repayments and the law, but remains unpredictable and challenging. A wide range of factors can prevent a successful repossession attempt, such as miscommunication on vehicle location or lien status, or debtor hostility. Automated risk estimation models may provide a valuable resource in allowing repossession companies to evaluate different jobs and allocate resources effectively; however, we find no existing work investigating statistical or machine-learning-based models in this field. To this end, we investigate the application of machine learning classification techniques to a private auto repossession dataset containing information on 200,000 distinct repossession attempts, including location, license plate status, and address type. We train a logistic regression classifier and two Gradient Boosted Decision Tree (GBDT) algorithms (CatBoost and XGBoost) to predict whether any given repo attempt will be a success or failure. We find that all three models, when optimized, give moderate classification performance (almost 80% accuracy and >0.50 F1 score), indicating that they provide valuable information for repossession companies and may support more effective decision-making and resource allocation. This is supported by extracted feature importance values from our GBDT models, which may provide additional insights to practitioners when prioritizing assignments. However, the application domain of auto repossession remains largely unexplored, and we highly encourage future work to explore other machine learning approaches or data collection techniques within this field.

title: Research on Intelligent Detection System of Construction Site Safety Equipment Based on Improved YOLOv11
authors: Bin Li, Yan Wang, Haoyang Jia
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: To address challenges in construction site safety equipment detection such as significant target size variations, severe occlusion, and difficulties in small object recognition, this study proposes an improved YOLOv11-based detection model called YOLOv11-HCE. The proposed framework introduces three key innovations: 1) A high-resolution Small Object Detection Head (SODH) enhances small object feature extraction; 2) A Channel Feature Fusion with Involution (CFFI) module bridges the backbone and neck networks, mitigating semantic loss from 1×1 dimensionality reduction while strengthening cross-channel information exchange; 3) An efficient channel attention mechanism (ECA) at the backbone network end emphasizes safety equipment region features through spatial and channel dual weighting. Ablation experiments demonstrate that the proposed method achieves 81.3% mAP@50,54.6% mAP@50:95, and 89.55% P, 75.74% R. YOLOv11-HCE demonstrates superior accuracy and stability in low-light, heavily occluded, and densely small-object detection scenarios.

title: Robust Minimum Logarithm Criterion Subband Filtering for Impulsive Noise with the EIV Model
authors: Zhan Jin, Tingting Wang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Traditional subband adaptive filter algorithms encounter significant challenges in errors-in-variables (EIV) models affected by impulsive noise, such as slow convergence, high steady-state error, and reduced robustness. To address these issues, we propose a bias-compensated LC-NSAF algorithm (BC-LC-NSAF), which incorporates a bias compensation (BC) term derived from an unbiased criterion to improve coefficient estimation accuracy. Although the BC-LC-NSAF algorithm exhibits enhanced performance, its reliance on a fixed step size constrains its capacity to optimally balance convergence speed with steady-state accuracy. To overcome this limitation, we introduce a convex combination BC-LC-NSAF algorithm (BC-LC-CNSAF), which blends two step sizes to achieve a better trade-off. System identification simulations with Speech input signals demonstrate that BC-LC-CNSAF is robust against impulsive disturbances, achieves low steady-state error, and maintains effective tracking under noisy conditions.

title: AI-Driven Disease Risk Prediction and Personalized Nutrition through ML, XAI, and Q-Learning
authors: Puvvada Vamsidhar, Lokesh Settypalli, Kancharapu Suvarna, M Bhanu Prathap Reddy, Ravishankar P Desai
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The increased prevalence of chronic lifestyle diseases highlights the need for early risk assessment and individualized preventive therapy. Data-driven healthcare solutions have been made possible by recent developments in artificial intelligence (AI); yet, clinical adoption is still hampered by a lack of useful guidance and restricted interpretability. This paper offers an AI-powered system for illness risk prediction and individualized food planning, encompassing the following components: (i) Three prevalent chronic diseases–diabetes, heart disease, and stroke–are predicted by a Random Forest (RF) algorithm that was trained using machine learning (ML). (ii) Explainable AI (XAI) methods, including SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations), offer clear insights into how various traits affect predictions. (iii) The system incorporates the Nutritionix API to provide diet programs that are medically relevant and customized based on individual lifestyle characteristics and anticipated health concerns. Recommendations are improved by a Q-learning-based reinforcement learning (RL) system in response to user input. (iv) Nutritionix API ensures individualized dietary advice aligned with health risk profiles and personal preferences. The proposed framework is assessed against multiple ML algorithms using clinically validated datasets. Experimental results reveal that the system not only provides accurate and interpretable disease risk estimates but also offers actionable preventative healthcare suggestions. This emphasizes its potential as an intelligent clinical decision-support tool and a digital health companion.

title: Corporate Credit Rating Assessment for Financial Risk and Regulatory Compliance
authors: Manjunatha M K, Harinadh Karimikonda, Yogita Dwivedi, Dhanalakshmi Komatiguntala
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Accurate corporate credit rating is crucial to financial risk management and regulation but the current models tend to use narrow data modalities, fail to consider time and relational relationships and have weak probabilistic calibration. These constraints make them less effective in detecting the risk of default and under pinning decision-making that is in line with the regulator. The objective of this study was to formulate and test a multimodal model with a time-dependent credit rating system to incorporate financial, textual, market and relational information. The publicly available corporate financial statements, market time series data, text disclosures and inter-firm relational information were used to conduct an experimental study. Baseline logistic regression, a hybrid XGBoost with FinBERT embeddings model, and a proposed Temporal Heterogeneous Graph Transformer with cross-modal fusion were implemented and compared using discrimination, calibration, and computational efficiency metrics. The model proposed had the best predictive performance up to a ROC-AUC of 0.903 and PR-AUC of 0.482 which is better than the baseline (0.761) and hybrid (0.842) models. Calibration analysis revealed more correspondence with observed default frequencies, and confusion matrices revealed that the number of true default detection improved as 64 (baseline) to 158. Ablation and Pareto analysis was used to verify that multimodal fusion and temporal graph modelling were the major sources of performance improvements. These findings indicate that the combination of multimodal, temporal, and relational data has a significant positive effect on the accuracy and reliability of credit ratings and provides an institutional and supervisory-appropriate credit risk evaluation framework to the regulator.

title: Trust-Aware Minimal-Intervention Shared Control via Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Kangjie Huang, Wen Zhang, Ruoshan Wang, Xiaoran Jin, Yun-Bo Zhao, Pengfei Li
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: In shared control systems, the arbitration mechanism determines the allocation of control authority to optimize human-machine collaboration. However, current methods mainly use fixed parameters or intention inference confidence to assign authority, which often overlooks the critical psycho-cognitive factor of human-machine trust, leading to potential conflicts or loss of human control when intervention mismatches the operator’s expectations. To address this problem, we propose a Trust-Aware Minimal Intervention Shared Control method that organically integrates dynamic trust modeling with the principle of minimal intervention. We construct a performance-driven trust dynamics model to estimate the operator’s trust level in real-time and utilize it as a dynamic regulator for action selection thresholds within a Deep Reinforcement Learning framework. Finally, we verify the effectiveness of the proposed method through experiments in the Lunar Lander simulation environment, demonstrating significant improvements in task success rates and reducing human workload compared to traditional approaches.

title: Adaptive Intelligent Control of Six-Phase Induction Motors for High-Performance EV Drives
authors: Sanjay S, Saranyadevi R, Balamurugan R
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The increasing demand for reliable and high-efficiency propulsion systems in electric vehicles has brought multiphase induction motors into focus. Six-phase induction machines offer distinct advantages over their three-phase counterparts, including reduced torque ripple, higher fault tolerance, and improved load distribution. However, controlling such drives under dynamic conditions remains a challenging task due to nonlinearities and parameter variations. This work presents a hybrid control strategy that integrates Direct Torque Control (DTC) with an Adaptive Neuro-Fuzzy Inference System (ANFIS). The proposed ANFIS-DTC scheme continuously adjusts inverter switching states using real-time feedback of speed and torque. By doing so, it significantly minimizes torque ripple and enhances transient response. MATLAB/Simulink models of a six-phase induction motor drive were developed to validate the method. Comparative studies with conventional DTC confirm smoother torque profiles, faster speed tracking, and improved efficiency. The results highlight the potential of intelligent control in advancing multiphase motor drives for next-generation electric vehicle applications.

title: MeshCentral-based Remote Laboratories for SDR Education: Application at UN-CHK
authors: Mamadou Moustapha Diagne, Mamadou Ba, Massamba Seck, Samuel Ouya
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The digital transformation of higher education in Africa faces challenges of inclusion and accessibility, particularly for practical work requiring demanding software such as GNU Radio. This study proposes an innovative solution based on MeshCentral to allow the 73,797 students of Cheikh Hamidou Kane Digital University (UN-CHK) to remotely access high-performance computers hosted at the Diamniadio data center via SENUM’s SDN/VXLAN infrastructure. Unlike existing approaches that rely on complex multi-component architectures, MeshCentral provides a lightweight solution (70 MB) with built-in encryption (AES-256-GCM, SHA-384, RSA-3072) and latency below 10 ms. The implementation of a practical exercise on amplitude modulation demonstrates the technical feasibility of this approach, enabling students with low-capacity computers to perform GNU Radio simulations in real time. This solution contributes to educational digital inclusion in Africa by democratizing access to advanced SDR technologies, with a target of 80% student participation by 2026. Its integration with Senegal’s national infrastructure represents a major innovation not explored in existing literature, offering a scalable and secure alternative for remote practical teaching in resource-constrained African contexts.

title: Calculation of Speed Indicators of Optical Transmission Systems with Multichannel Fibers
authors: Vladimir N. Korshunov, Nikolay I. Likhachev, Nadezhda A. Shishova
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: Technical and economic aspects of application of multi-core and low-mode optical fibers in transmission systems on transport networks are considered. The task is to optimize the fiber in terms of achieving extreme results in the process of step-by-step improvement of quality indicators. A two-criteria approach is used to optimize fiber design while minimizing interference between spatial channels and additional loss attenuation at the optical shell boundary. Numerical values of spectral efficiency, spatial efficiency, spatial-velocity efficiency, spectral-spatial efficiency are calculated. A quality metric is provided - a cumulative signal-to-noise reflected interference ratio the effect of crosstalk in a multichannel fiber on the aggregated light path throughput. A set of technical and economic indicators is presented, which can serve as a tool for choosing technical solutions to increase line capacity and transmission systems in transport networks.

title: Real-Time AI-Driven Cross-Market Trading System for Retail Traders Using XGBoost-LightGBM Ensembles
authors: Vidhya R, Kavimani S, Chandra Mohan R, Guhan C, Harithaa T, Parimala Devi M
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Algorithmic trading systems can have considerable monthly costs associated with market data and can face issues with downtime if they only use one source of data. We propose a zero-cost, multi-market trading system that delivers 99.90% uptime by coordinating multiple free-tier APIs using a three-level fallback data process with Twelve Data, Finnhub, and Alpaca. Automated TOTP authentication was included in the system so it could continuously run without human intervention. The system can also collate trading behavior so that five cognitive biases can be detected in real time. The machine learning sub-module combines XGBoost and LightGBM algorithms, achieving <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$56.14 \% \pm 3.63 \%$</tex> accuracy in statistical validation (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0}$</tex> trials, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p=0.018$</tex>) and 64.60% accuracy in 4 -year backtest (111 trades, 65.63% win rate, 1.76 Sharpe ratio, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+5.47 \%$</tex> total return, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+1.37 \%$</tex> annualized) after transaction costs over 4 years. The trading decisions for the system are based on sentiment analysis (25% of the signal), technical indicators (60% of signal), and optional LSTM forecasts <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(15 \%)$</tex>. Testing of the system has been completed across 19 suites with complete coverage indicating readiness for deployment. The system has been deployed on both US (both NASDAQ and NYSE) and Indian markets (both NSE and BSE) simultaneously, enabling inter-market arbitrage within one (1) second in six (6) pairs of ADRs. The average data retrieval time is an efficient 85 ms with no operational expenses. This enables the next generation of algorithmic trading for individual users.

title: Mockhire: Adaptive Interview Training and Recruitment Automation Through Objective AI Assessment
authors: Reshma Totare, Sneha Gonjari, Siddhi Uttekar, Ishwari Abuj
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The rapid growth of competitive hiring environments has increased the need for effective interview preparation and objective candidate evaluation methods. Traditional mock interview practices often depend on human evaluators, which limits scalability, provides subjective feedback, and leads to inconsistent assessment standards. To tackle these issues, this paper introduces MockHire, an AI-driven platform for interview training and recruitment support. It aims to improve candidate readiness and recruiter efficiency. The system allows candidates to take adaptive mock interviews, receive structured, data-driven feedback, and monitor their progress through performance analytics. At the same time, recruiters get tools to manage job postings, track applications, and assess candidate responses using standardized AI-generated insights. MockHire combines resume analysis, dynamic question changes, automated scoring, and visual feedback dashboards to support decision-making based on evidence. By merging interview simulation, assessment, and application tracking into a single framework, this platform reduces evaluation bias, saves recruiter time, and provides a scalable solution for modern recruitment processes.

title: Short-term Electricity Price Forecasting with Constrained Regressors
authors: Mucun Sun, Li Zhang, Yifeng Gao
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: The volatility of electricity price presents a challenge to market participants as their decision-making process are highly depend on the accuracy of price forecasts. However, there is growing empirical evidence of increasing price volatility and price spikes in electricity markets as a result of variable renewable energy generation, extreme weather events, and other factors. The distribution shift caused by spikes in electricity price data differentiates the forecasting tasks from other renewable energy sources. Moreover, the observations may be compromised by cyberattacks and thus not available in the testing phase. To this end, we propose a Similarity-Enhanced Electricity Decomposition Forecasting model (SEED-Forecaster) to address the missing response problem and spikes capturing in short-term electricity price forecasting. The effectiveness of the proposed framework is tested on real-world electricity price data from California Independent System Operator (CAISO). Numerical results of case studies show that the proposed SEED-Forecsater can enhance forecasting performance, particularly in capturing electricity spikes, even under conditions without regressors during testing stage.

title: Twin Nail Clock Triggering D-Flipflop using CNTFET Technology for Power Efficient VLSI Systems
authors: P.Nagarajan, N.Ashokkumar, Kavitha Thandapani, G.Arthy, M.Malathi, R. Sinthia
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The rapid advancement of digital circuits necessitates innovative approaches to improve performance while minimizing power consumption. This paper presents the 5CNTB-TNAFF, a dual-edge triggered flip-flop design utilizing Carbon Nanotube Field-Effect Transistors (CNFETs) for enhanced efficiency in very-large-scale integration (VLSI) applications. This design leverages the unique electrical properties of CNFETs, which enable lower threshold voltages and reduced power requirements compared to conventional MOSFET technologies. The 5CNTB-TNAFF architecture is optimized for both speed and power efficiency, utilizing dual-edge triggering to capture data on both the growing and dropping nails of the clock signal, thereby increasing operational responsiveness. The design incorporates advanced clock gating mechanisms that significantly lower power consumption during idle states, an essential feature for modern digital circuits where energy efficiency is paramount. Empirical analysis demonstrates that the 5CNTB-TNAFF achieves a power consumption of approximately 5.7nW, a substantial improvement over traditional MOSFET designs, while exhibiting a propagation delay of just 70 ps. Furthermore, the layout area of the 5CNTB-TNAFF is estimated to be around 120 µm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, highlighting its compactness and suitability for integration into larger systems. Comparative evaluations against existing flip-flop designs, including the 5DC-DETFF, illustrate the superior performance and effectiveness of the 5CNTB-TNAFF, making this a capable candidate for next-generation digital applications. The findings underscore the transformative potential of CNFET technology in reshaping VLSI design paradigms, facilitating the development of high-performance, low-power circuits crucial for the evolving landscape of electronic devices.

title: Explainable Machine Learning Models for Dual Diagnosis of Osteoporosis and Osteoarthritis from Knee X-Rays
authors: Sanika M. Rangayyan, Divya K., Ishaan Shettigar, Jimish Mrug, Rajesh R., Gowri Srinivasa
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This study presents an explainable machine learningbased framework for the dual diagnosis of osteoporosis and osteoarthritis in knee X-ray images by identifying specific visual indicators, such as trabecular bone patterns, osteophyte formation, and joint space reduction, providing an accessible and inexpensive assessment option. A unified pipeline integrating image processing and deep learning techniques is developed, where each component corresponds to a specific radiographic feature. The framework links each diagnosis to the appropriate radiographic markers and facilitates the identification of instances in which osteoporosis and osteoarthritis coexist within the same radiograph. By emphasizing visual explanation, the proposed approach enhances interpretability and supports clinically meaningful decision-making.

title: Intelligent Mri-Based Framework for Alzheimer's Diagnosis With Deep Learning
authors: Polavarapu Neelima, Navya Adapala, Saheda Shaik, Aveela Jangala, V. Chittibabu, R. Aruna Flarence
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Alzheimer disease (AD) is a degenerative disorder in the brain that causes a slow process of deterioration of memory and thinking or cognition. AD is an important issue that requires early and accurate diagnosis in order to control the disease and enhance the general wellbeing of the patient. One of the most popular methods to view and assess the changes in the brain structure is Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) that is utilized on regular basis, but MRI scan analysis is time-consuming and variable. We propose a computer-diagnosis system based on deep learning (DL) algorithms that can automatically decide whether a patient has AD from MRI scans. Various model architectures like CNN, VGG16, ResNet50, BiLSTM, and The architectures of DenseNets were implemented and their performances were compared on a highly controlled experimental environment.The idea of image preprocessing has also been explored in the development of clarity and power models of improvement, which may include the removal of noise or image augmentation. VGG16 with an accuracy rate of 98.39% performed better than other models and has affirmed the reliability of the proposed model in determining the different phases of dementia.The proposed system can assist clinicians in the practical diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease.

title: Automated Forensic Approaches for SaaS and PaaS Platforms
authors: S. R. Seenivasan, D. Prem Raja
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Cloud computing is used as a large-scale platform that has become a foundation of contemporary information systems, to everyday services. Services such as Software as a Service (SaaS) and Platform as a Service (PaaS) have become a one of the leading models offered for scalability and reduced infrastructure costs. These services give a benefit to create a unique challenge for digital forensics. Cloud Environments is one of the important suggestions to distribute across multiple tenants, altered in very short timeframes, or restricted by diverse legal jurisdictions. That suggestion makes the preservation and collection of forensic data more complex than in traditional systems. This paper can be described about the survey effort to automate the forensic tasks in SaaS and PaaS settings. Both academic studies and industry tools are review with the emphasis on logging mechanisms, automated evidence acquisition, and readiness of the frameworks. The above progress has been made with the some of more important limitations to remain the limited interoperability, uneven support for real-time data collection and the back of standardized practices. Based on the broader worries it includes the data volatility, legal admissibility, and cross-border governance. Achievements are mapped and gaps are identified for this survey and it highlights the what are the steps needed to establish reliable, scalable, and legally credible forensic readiness for cloud ecosystems.

title: Researching Algorithms Used to Predict Defects in the Software Code of Telecommunications Equipment
authors: Timur Fatkhulin, Yuri Leokhin, Svetlana S. Dymkova, Albina Myalicheva
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: This article examines the problem of predicting defects in telecommunications equipment software. The goal of this work is to identify the most effective machine learning (ML) algorithm for accurately predicting defects in the source code of telecommunications equipment. The relevance of this work lies in the fact that traditional statistical algorithms and models do not provide a sufficiently accurate forecast of the quality of software used in various telecommunications equipment (including in space in Earth orbit), and static code analysis tools produce a large number of false positives and false negatives. Furthermore, given the high demands placed on the operation of telecommunications equipment in space in Earth orbit, it is especially important to identify defects in software code at all stages of its development. Therefore, the need for machine learning algorithms to predict code errors is increasing. The object of this study is the growing demand for high-quality software. The subject of this study is metrics for assessing the quality of machine learning algorithms and models designed to predict defects in source code. As a result of the experiments, conclusions were drawn about the effectiveness of each algorithm, and the choice of the most suitable algorithm for solving a specific applied problem of telecommunications equipment was determined.

title: Attention-Based Offline Reinforcement Learning and Clustering for Interpretable Sepsis Treatment
authors: Punit Kumar, Vaibhav Saran, Divyesh Patel, Nitin Kulkarni, Alina Vereshchaka
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Sepsis remains one of the leading causes of mortality in intensive care units, where timely and accurate treatment decisions can significantly impact patient outcomes. In this work, we propose an interpretable decision support framework. Our system integrates four core components: (1) a clustering-based stratification module that categorizes patients into low, intermediate, and high-risk groups upon ICU admission, using clustering with statistical validation; (2) a synthetic data augmentation pipeline leveraging variational autoencoders (VAE) and diffusion models to enrich underrepresented trajectories such as fluid or vasopressor administration; (3) an offline reinforcement learning (RL) agent trained using Advantage Weighted Regression (AWR) with a lightweight attention encoder and supported by an ensemble models for conservative, safety-aware treatment recommendations; and (4) a rationale generation module powered by a multi-modal large language model (LLM), which produces natural-language justifications grounded in clinical context and retrieved expert knowledge. Evaluated on the MIMIC-III and eICU datasets, our approach achieves high treatment accuracy while providing clinicians with interpretable and robust policy recommendations.

title: Mental Multi-class Classification on Social Media: Benchmarking Transformer Architectures against LSTM Models
authors: Khalid Hasan, Jamil Saquer, Yifan Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Millions of people openly share mental health struggles on social media, providing rich data for early detection of conditions such as depression, bipolar disorder, etc. However, most prior Natural Language Processing (NLP) research has focused on single-disorder identification, leaving a gap in understanding the efficacy of advanced NLP techniques for distinguishing among multiple mental health conditions. In this work, we present a large-scale comparative study of state-of-the-art transformer versus Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM)-based models to classify mental health posts into exclusive categories of mental health conditions. We first curate a large dataset of Reddit posts spanning six mental health conditions and a control group, using rigorous filtering and statistical exploratory analysis to ensure annotation quality. We then evaluate five transformer architectures (BERT, RoBERTa, DistilBERT, ALBERT, and ELECTRA) against several LSTM variants (with or without attention, using contextual or static embeddings) under identical conditions. Experimental results show that transformer models consistently outperform the alternatives, with RoBERTa achieving 91-99% F1-scores and accuracies across all classes. Notably, attention-augmented LSTMs with BERT embeddings approach transformer performance (up to 97% F1-score) while training 2-3.5 times faster, whereas LSTMs using static embeddings fail to learn useful signals. These findings represent the first comprehensive benchmark for multi-class mental health detection, offering practical guidance on model selection and highlighting an accuracy–efficiency trade-off for real-world deployment of mental health NLP systems.

title: Enhancing Multi-Agent Collaboration with Attention-Based Actor-Critic Policies
authors: Hugo Garrido-Lestache Belinchon, Jeremy Kedziora
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: This paper introduces Team-Attention-Actor-Critic (TAAC), a reinforcement learning algorithm designed to enhance multi-agent collaboration in cooperative environments. TAAC employs a Centralized Training/Centralized Execution scheme incorporating multi-headed attention mechanisms in both the actor and critic. This design facilitates dynamic, inter-agent communication, allowing agents to explicitly query teammates, thereby efficiently managing the exponential growth of joint-action spaces while ensuring a high degree of collaboration. We further introduce a penalized loss function which promotes diverse yet complementary roles among agents. We evaluate TAAC in a simulated soccer environment against benchmark algorithms representing other multi-agent paradigms, including Proximal Policy Optimization and Multi-Agent Actor-Attention-Critic. We find that TAAC exhibits superior performance and enhanced collaborative behaviors across a variety of metrics.

title: Bandwidth-Efficient Federated Learning: A Preprocessing and Compression-based Approach using MNIST
authors: Sayed Muhammed Fazil P P, Bharathi.A
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) allows training a multi-client model without centralizing raw data, which makes it an excellent choice to apply on privacy-sensitive tasks such as digit recognition using the MNIST dataset. But the issue is non-IID data distributions and communications overhead are a major challenge that can adversely impact model accuracy and efficiency. The study introduces a streamlined FL framework with the addition of data preprocessing, proximal regularization (FedProx), gradient quantization and top-k sparsification. CNN was trained over 100 clients under IID and non-IID (Dirichlet =80alpha:0.5) partitioning with rounds capped at 10. Experimental results demonstrate that whereas baseline FedAvg obtained 98.2% accuracy on IID data, 85.4% on non-IID data, the proposed optimizations have the significant contributions to stability and efficiency. In particular and specifically, FedProx boosted non-IID accuracy to 88.6 percent, 8-bit quantization reduced bandwidth by 75 percent at the cost of less than one percent, and top-10% sparsification conserved 90 percent of communication incurring only ~3 percent accuracy loss. In addition, FedProx combined with the gradient compression provided a practical trade-off between accuracy and communication efficiency, and performed better than the baseline methods. These results show that proximal-regularized communication-efficient FL can address non-IID hurdles and make federated training resource-aware and scalable without considerable performance loss.

title: Fast & Power Efficient Voltage Translators for IO/Memory Sub-Systems
authors: Mainak Ghosh, Poleboyina Manideep, Viraj Panchal, Priyanka Nirakari, Ramesh Karmungi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: In this paper, a fast and low-power unidirectional voltage translator with isolation cell. The design incorporates a conventional cross-coupled pull-up network that significantly enhances switching speed while greatly reducing dynamic power consumption. The unidirectional level shifter allows data transfer between the core logic and pad drivers in uni directional while reducing power consumption and delay. The proposed voltage translator can translate input signals with amplitudes up-to the MOSFET threshold voltage to higher, nominal supply levels. Its compact structure, achieved through a minimal number of components, results in a small silicon footprint and extremely low power usage, making it well-suited for energy-constrained applications. Cadence tool have been used for simulation purpose. Simulations carried out in a Global Foundry's Low Power enhance <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$65-\text{nm}$</tex> CMOS process demonstrate that the circuit can successfully convert input voltages as low as 600 mV. For supply voltages of 0.75 V (low) and 1.2 V (high) at an input frequency of 1 MHz, the proposed voltage translator achieves a power dissipation of 545.5 nW, a propagation delay of 19.1 ns and it consumes a silicon area of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$176 \mu \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex>.

title: Multimodal Medical Image Fusion Using Dilated ResNet with Region Attention Module
authors: Harmanpreet Kaur, Swati Katwal, Anjna Kumari, Kushangi Pathak
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Multimodal medical image fusion is a vital process of integrating complementary information from various medical imaging sources into a single representation which helps to increase the accuracy of diagnoses and treatment planning. Although deep learning approaches, especially Convolutional Neural Networks and Transformers, have greatly improved the results of fusion, but they still have limitations in capturing fine-grained multi-scale and edge features, which results in less optimal feature integration. Also, some of the models are computationally intensive amid training as well as at the fusion stage, making them unsuitable for real-time usage in a clinical environment. This research presented a multimodal imaging fusion approach based on a deep learning framework to overcome the challenges discussed previously by proposing a Dilated ResNet along with a Region Attention module. The encoder is responsible for effectively extracting multi-scale features. It also employs a hippopotamus optimization technique, which draws inspiration from nature, to improve the efficiency of weighted feature fusion. The final fused image is produced by passing the combined features to the decoder. Extensive experiments show that the proposed approach outperforms numerous baseline methods in terms of texture retention, fusion time, and visual quality; hence, it may be a feasible option for clinical real-world applications.

title: BERT-ViT Cross-Attention Fusion for Detecting Fake Content in Social Media
authors: Gauri Kitukale, Navneet Pratap Singh, Sidharth Quamara
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The rapid spreading of fake content on social media platforms poses serious challenges to information integrity. In this study, we suggest a cross-attention fusion-based framework for multimodal fake content identification that jointly leverages textual and visual information. Our approach employs pretrained encoders in order to extract features from text and images, followed by a cross-attention fusion mechanism to capture inter-modal relationships. Extensive experiments on benchmark multimodal fake news datasets, such as Fakeddit and Weibo, demonstrate that the proposed framework outperforms unimodal baselines and simple fusion methods, achieving F1-scores of 84 % and 81 %, respectively. These results indicate that modelling cross-modal interactions is crucial for effective fake content detection in real-world social media scenarios.

title: SAFE-T: Edge-Aware Multi-Sensor Fusion Framework for Real-Time Tsunami Detection in IoT-Enabled Early Warning Systems
authors: Priyanka Mane, Vinita Edlabadkar
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: As tsunami occurrence is a persistent threat in the coastal areas, the rapid and reliable detection of tsunamis is critical for minimizing loss of life and property through early warning systems. This paper proposes SAFE-T (Sensor-fusion and Adaptive Framework for Edge-based Tsunami detection), an integrated framework for real-time tsunami detection leveraging multi-sensor data fusion and advanced signal-processing algorithms within IoT-enabled early warning architectures. By combining data streams from heterogeneous oceanographic sensors—such as ocean-bottom pressure gauges, GNSS buoys, coastal tide gauges, underwater acoustic sensors, and distributed IoT wireless nodes—SAFE-T deploys state-of-the-art decomposition techniques (empirical mode decomposition and fast iterative filtering) and machine learning-based classifiers to enhance the detection of tsunami waveforms amidst noisy, variable marine environments. The framework addresses challenges in synchronization, data reliability, and computational efficiency, outperforming conventional single-sensor methods in key detection metrics. Comparative evaluations using real-world records and simulated testbeds demonstrate significant improvements in detection accuracy, false alarm reduction, and timely characterization of tsunami amplitude and propagation. Furthermore, SAFE-T incorporates edge-aware computing strategies to enable real-time deployment on power- and bandwidth-constrained IoT platforms, highlighting its robustness and scalability for next-generation tsunami early warning systems.

title: Coverage Enhancement Mechanism for WSNs Based on Improved Ant Lion Optimizer and Hungarian Algorithm
authors: Saurabh Kumar Srivastava, Shaik Wasim Akram, Prasenjit Chanak
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: In the current era, the Internet of Things (IoT) has become an important technology with it's applications in smart agriculture, smart cities, battlefields etc. Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) form the backbone of these IoT-based systems. In challenging environments such as battlefields or disaster zones, Sensor Nodes (SNs) are often dropped randomly from aircraft. The SNs rarely land in ideal positions, which leaves parts of the monitoring area uncovered and creates coverage holes, due to random deployment. To address this problem, this paper proposes an improved Ant Lion Optimizer combined with the Hungarian algorithm (H-ALO) to enhance the coverage of the monitoring field. The proposed H-ALO method also optimizes the movement of SNs to reduce unnecessary overlap between sensing areas and improve overall coverage. This approach has been compared with other state-of-the-art approaches in terms of coverage rate, average energy consumed, and average moving distance of SNs. The results show that the proposed approach outperforms existing methods.

title: Perceived Authenticity in Influencer Marketing: Effects on Indonesian Generation Z Purchase Behavior in Social Commerce Platforms
authors: Sabar Aritonang Rajagukguk
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The rapid growth of social commerce in Indonesia has fundamentally transformed digital marketing landscapes, particularly among Generation Z consumers who represent the first truly digital-native generation. This study examines how perceived influencer authenticity affects purchase behavior among Indonesian Generation Z consumers across social commerce platforms. Using a cross-sectional survey design with behavioral tracking data, we collected responses from 500 Indonesian Generation Z consumers aged 18-26 years across major Indonesian cities. Structural Equation Modeling revealed that perceived authenticity significantly influences purchase intention (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\beta=\text{0.67}, \mathrm{p}<0.001$</tex>) and actual purchase behavior (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\beta=\text{0.54}, \mathrm{p}<0.001$</tex>), with trust in influencer and parasocial relationships serving as significant mediators. The study found that micro-influencers demonstrated higher authenticity perceptions compared to macro-influencers, while product category and social media usage intensity moderated these relationships. Content quality and influencer-product fit emerged as critical antecedents to authenticity perception. These findings provide theoretical contributions to social commerce literature and practical insights for marketers developing authentic influencer strategies targeting Indonesian Generation Z consumers.

title: From Concrete to Carbon Smart: Advancing Green Affordable Housing in India
authors: Arindam Dutta, Bishaljit Paul, Susovan Dutta, Nirmalya Roy, Arindam Hazra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)
abstract: India faces a dual imperative: bridging a 25-million-unit affordable housing deficit by 2030 while achieving 86% carbon emission reductions in its rapidly expanding built environment. This study demonstrates how low-carbon materials, policy innovation, and life cycle thinking can converge to deliver green and affordable housing for economically weaker sections (EWS) and low-income groups (LIG). With buildings accounting for 21% of global emissions, scaling sustainable construction represents both a national necessity and a global climate priority. Adopting a multi-method approach combining policy analysis, secondary Life Cycle Assessment (LCA), and comparative case study evaluation, this research identifies critical barriers: weak policy enforcement, fragmented institutional coordination, limited access to green finance, and underutilization of context-appropriate materials such as bamboo and fly ash bricks. The study reveals that bamboo housing demonstrates 85-90% lower embodied energy and carbon emissions compared to conventional brick cement alternatives, with a payback period of 2-3 years through operational savings. To address these gaps, this paper proposes seven strategic interventions, including localized material codes, decentralized green governance, vernacular construction mainstreaming, certification reform aligned with affordability, and financial instruments like carbon-linked incentives and green microloans. The research offers a replicable framework that integrates material innovation with housing policy to reduce carbon intensity while enhancing social inclusion.

title: CPB-GA: A Critical Path and Bottleneck Guided Genetic Algorithm for Flexible Job Shop Scheduling
authors: Wenbo Li, Xiangyu Chen
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: The Flexible Job Shop Scheduling Problem (FJSP) presents a significant combinatorial optimization challenge, primarily due to the close interdependence between operation sequencing and machine assignment. To effectively tackle this issue, this study introduces CPB-GA, a genetic algorithm that is explicitly guided by critical paths and bottlenecks, incorporating domain-specific scheduling knowledge into the evolutionary search. The algorithm employs a dual-layer chromosome representation, combined with a serial schedule generation scheme, to ensure feasibility. To enhance robustness, a bottleneck aware machine assignment repair mechanism is implemented, which dynamically balances machine loads by reallocating operations based on their processing times. Additionally, a lightweight neighborhood search focused on critical paths is integrated to selectively refine elite offspring. Experiments conducted using Brandimarte’s instances demonstrate that CPB-GA consistently produces high-quality schedules, showcasing competitive computational efficiency and stability. These findings indicate that incorporating critical path and bottleneck strategies within genetic algorithms provides a highly effective framework for addressing complex flexible scheduling problems.

title: Intrusion Graph Topology-Aware Sampling Technique for Balanced Intrusion Detection in IoT Networks
authors: Anju, Navdeep Bohra, Nripendra Narayan Das
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The complexity that Internet of Things (IoT) networks have increasingly posed has made it harder to accurately detect various intrusion attacks. According to the traditional intrusion detection systems (IDS), the imbalance of data is usually high with a minority type of attack being underrepresented leading to biased classification and low recall. In this paper, the author introduces an Intrusion Graph TopologyAware Sampling (IGTAS) method that scales up the intrusion detection by distributing classes in the graph based IDS data. The method combines minority attack nodes in the graph embedding space and recreates their topological edges to maintain structural integrity. IGTAS outperforms numerous benchmarks in its detection with a Graph Convolutional Network (GCN)-based classifier. Experimental findings indicate that overall accuracy increases by 1.3 on average, minority attacks retrieve and F1-score are improved by 3-5% and AUCROC increases by 1.2%. Assessments on four benchmark datasets, BoT-IoT, ToN-IoT, CIC-IDS2018, and NF-UQ-NIDS, show that IGTAS can deliver balanced and structurally consistent intrusion detection and is best than the existing graph-based approaches at the same time preserving topological coherence.

title: An Automated VAPT Framework for Smart Home IoT Environments: Protocol-Aware Vulnerability Detection and Mitigation
authors: Shivaswamy D. S., Arun Kumar B. R., Prakash B R, Shreyas A
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The proliferation of Internet of Things (IoT) devices in smart homes has enhanced automation, comfort, and energy efficiency while simultaneously introducing significant security challenges arising from protocol vulnerabilities, weak firmware protections, and fragmented standards. This research presents ProVAPT SmartHome, an automated and protocol-aware Vulnerability Assessment and Penetration Testing (VAPT) framework specifically designed to secure smart home IoT ecosystems. Unlike traditional VAPT tools, ProVAPT combines dynamic protocol analysis, CVE-based vulnerability detection, and lightweight machine learning– driven behavioural anomaly identification within a Windows-based environment suitable for practical deployment in Indian households. The framework was evaluated using commonly available smart home devices such as Wi-Fi and ZigBee enabled smart bulbs, DLink IP cameras, and Xiaomi smart plugs, covering protocols including MQTT, CoAP, UPnP, and ZigBee. ProVAPT identified 17 vulnerabilities comprising 14 known CVEs and 3 previously undocumented zero-day flaws, achieving a true positive rate of 96.1 percent, a false positive rate of 3.8 percent, and an average detection latency of 6.2 seconds per device. Behavioral anomaly detection using an Isolation Forest model reached a precision of 93.7 percent and an F1-score of 93.1 percent, while real-time mitigation was performed through Windows native firewall rules with an 81 percent user confirmation rate. Through its modular architecture, native Windows compatibility, and user-centric mitigation dashboard, ProVAPT SmartHome provides a robust, scalable, and low-overhead solution for IoT threat detection and response, eliminating the need for specialized hardware or Linux systems and establishing a new direction in IoT cybersecurity by integrating protocol awareness, behavioural intelligence, and automated enforcement in a single deployable solution.

title: Smart Charging Station Powered by Speed Breaker-based Generation
authors: Sasivarman C, Yokesh S, Karthik P
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: In order to power A vital urban utilities, this paper presents a novel renewable energy system that uses a gear-type speed breaker to capture the mechanical energy from traffic and transform it into electrical energy. Before being sent to the battery for storage, the harvested energy is processed via a DC boost converter to raise its voltage level. This collected energy is efficiently used for two mainly purposes: powering automated street lighting and the wirelessly charging electric vehicles (EVs). It is stored in a rechargeable battery. An Arduino uno is a microcontroller, which facilitates it as smooth automation and Internet of Things-based monitoring and is used to control the system. The system recognizes the presence of an EV and automatically starts the wireless power transmission when it approaches and enabling contactless energy transfer for easy and secure charging. A relay module is used to intelligently it operates the street lighting system, saving energy by turning lights on or off in the response to a time-based logic or environmental conditions. For real-time monitoring of important parameters like battery charge, voltage, current, and power status, the system has digital display. This integrated solution, which can be intended for smart city applications, uses wireless power transfer, kinetic energy harvesting, and Internet of Things-based control to learn about grid reliance, cut carbon emissions, and encourage the use of the clean energy in urban transportation and infrastructure.

title: An LLM-Enabled Multimodal Agentic AI Framework for the Medical Internet of Things (MIoT)
authors: M. A. Rahman, Syed Usman Jamil, M. Shamim Hossain, Muhammad Arif Khan, Tanveer Zia, Muhammad Ali Paracha, Mubarak Alrashoud, Min Chen, Selwa A. F. Al-Hazzaa
publish: ACM Transactions on Multimedia Computing, Communications, and Applications
abstract: None

title: Should the retailer invest in supplier development under asymmetric demand information?
authors: Xiubang Li, Jianxiong Zhang, Jiajun Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Engineering Management
abstract: Implementing supplier development to reduce production costs has become a critical strategic choice for firms aiming to improve efficiency. Under asymmetric demand information, investing in supplier development by a retailer who holds private demand information serves two intertwined roles: enhancing supplier capability while conveying private market demand information. To capture this tension, we develop a signaling game framework that characterizes the interaction between supplier development and demand information asymmetry. Our analysis shows that equilibrium outcomes are governed by the prior belief about high-demand potential, underscoring the central role of information asymmetry when the downstream retailer independently determines supplier development. We further demonstrate that supply chain members may coordinate on supplier development under certain conditions, achieving a win-win outcome that enhances operational efficiency. However, when the prior belief of high demand becomes sufficiently high, supplier development may instead yield a lose-win outcome, benefiting the retailer but harming the supplier. This result highlights that, despite the retailer's good intentions, supplier development can inadvertently reduce the supplier's welfare. An extension is conducted to further verify the robustness of our main conclusions. Overall, the study provides novel insights into when supplier development enhances supply chain efficiency and offers actionable guidance for firms facing asymmetric demand information.

authors: Yuvaraj Chesetti, Navid Eslami, Huanchen Zhang, Niv Dayan, Prashant Pandey

title: Securing RIS-Assisted Vehicular Networks in the Presence of Obstructing Vehicle
authors: Mohamed G. Abd El Ghafour, Ahmed H. Abd El-Malek, Ali Arshad Nasir, Anas M. Salhab
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: This research paper investigates the secrecy performance in a vehicular network. A legitimate vehicular source aims to transmit confidential information to a legitimate vehicular destination, while a passive vehicular eavesdropper attempts to intercept this transmission. To enhance the practical relevance of the system model, the probabilistic existence of an obstructing vehicle is considered. This obstructing vehicle can potentially disrupt communication links between the source and the destination, the source and the eavesdropper, or both. To mitigate the adverse effects of obstruction and eavesdropping, a reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) is employed. Additionally, the source employs a power-splitting scheme to generate artificial noise (AN), a physical-layer security technique to degrade the wiretap channel. In this power-splitting scheme, the source’s transmission power is divided into two portions: confidential transmission and generating the AN signal. Closed-form expressions are derived for the outage probability, upper bound intercept probability, and upper bound secrecy outage probability (SOP), along with their asymptotic expressions. The influence of power-splitting ratio, transmission power, number of reflecting elements of the RIS, density of the obstructing vehicle, and the channel conditions on the performance metrics is investigated through numerical results. The results are extended to encompass practical impairments, specifically examining the impacts of node mobility, channel state information feedback delay, and AN leakage at the destination. It has been observed that an increase in the density of large obstructing vehicles leads to a reduction in the SOP. However, this degradation can be mitigated by increasing the number of passive reflectors of RIS. Additionally, based on the closed-form upper bound expression of the SOP, a power allocation optimization problem is solved to minimize the SOP in terms of the power-splitting ratio. The accuracy of the derived closed-form expressions has been verified through Monte Carlo simulations.

title: Knowledge Integration for Multilingual Fake News Detection: LLM Rationales and Adapter-based Deep Fusion
authors: Gyeongmin Kim, Yeongwook Yang
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Detecting fake news in multilingual settings is especially challenging for low-resource languages, where pre-trained language models often struggle to incorporate external knowledge effectively. We propose a Knowledge Integration (KI) framework for Multilingual Fake News Detection (MFND) that leverages LLM-generated rationales through three integration routes: Dual Segmentation (DS), Knowledge Fusion (KF), and their combination (DSKF). KF employs a lightweight projection-residual adapter to inject rationale semantics into intermediate encoder representations. Experiments on the six-language TALLIP benchmark indicate that KF achieves state-of-the-art performance in five languages while maintaining minimal parameter overhead. Ablation results further underscore that the integration mechanism is as important as the knowledge itself: KF is the most stable strategy, DS exhibits higher variance, and DSKF reveals interaction-driven interference. Controlled perturbation experiments demonstrate that KF is more robust to corrupted rationales than DS or DSKF, whereas qualitative evidence suggests that rationale clarity remains an important limitation. Overall, these findings position representation-level deep fusion as a parameter-efficient and reliable approach for knowledge-enhanced MFND.

title: Galvanically Isolated Coreless Peak Current Detector for Switch Mode Power Supplies
authors: Hossein Niakan, Ali Farahani, Babak Parkhideh
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: Reliable current sensing has become a fundamental requirement in modern power conversion systems, where operation demands precise current information. In this work, an all-new coil-based current sensing solution is proposed to measure the peak value of switch-type current waveforms, having bidirectional signal detection capability as well as system-level scalability. A prototype of the concept has been designed that includes a coil network with a wide detection bandwidth, followed by a dual-path signal processing system. The main path integrates the induced voltage of its designated coil and uses an analog compensator to reconstruct the waveform unidirectionally. The alternate path includes a novel polarity estimator, which decouples the coil from the processing circuit during undesired intervals. As such, the switch-current waveform peak can be reconstructed without the need for external control or full-scale waveform acquisition. The peak sensitivity value of approximately <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$75~\text{mV/A}$</tex-math></inline-formula> was measured for waveforms up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\text{1}~\text{MHz}$</tex-math></inline-formula> nominal switching frequency, presenting a maximum of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1.91\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> deviation across the tested frequency range. Furthermore, the sensor shows a maximum <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1.35\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> linearity error measuring device current peaks up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\text{66.7}~\text{A}$</tex-math></inline-formula>. The proposed solution offers accurate peak current measurement together with a compact form factor, establishing an alternative to traditional isolated current sensing used in switch mode power supplies.

title: A Multi-Head Attention ResCNN–BiGRU Model for Robust SOC Estimation in EVs Lithium-Ion Batteries Using Real-World Driving Data
authors: N. Vigneswar, R. Manivannan, Chala Merga Abdissa
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Precise assessment of the state of charge (SOC) is essential for ensuring the safety, efficiency, and dependability of electric vehicles (EVs). However, achieving high accuracy remains challenging due to the complex nonlinear characteristics of lithium-ion batteries (LIBs), which are strongly influenced by ambient temperature fluctuations, dynamic load variations, and aging effects. To address these challenges, this study proposes a sophisticated hybrid deep learning (DL) framework that combines convolutional neural networks (CNNs) with residual connections (ResCNN), bidirectional gated recurrent units (Bi-GRU), and a multi-head attention (MHA) mechanism. In the proposed ResCNN-Bi-GRU-MHA model, the ResCNN layer implements residual connections to strengthen CNN-based spatial feature extraction, allowing for more stable training and richer feature representations, while the Bi-GRU layer captures temporal dependencies; subsequently, both features are dynamically integrated through theMHAto improve prediction accuracy. The framework is validated using real-world field data gathered from BMW i3 EV driving trips outfitted with a 60 Ah LIB pack. A comparative analysis is performed to determine the influence of the MHA by comparing the hybrid ResCNN-Bi-GRU model with and without the MHA. The established results demonstrate that the ResCNN-Bi-GRU-MHA model achieves superior predictive accuracy, with a root mean squared error (RMSE) of 6.0476% and a mean absolute error (MAE) of 4.5550%. Furthermore, the proposed framework outperforms conventional approaches, including feedforward neural networks (FFNN), long short-term memory (LSTM), gated recurrent units (GRU), and extreme learning machines (ELM). These findings point out the ResCNN-Bi-GRU-MHA model’s robustness for real-time SOC estimation in battery management systems (BMS).
